PT P
PN CN111440762-A; CN111440762-B
TI New fully suspended Madin-Darby canine kidney (MDCK) cells D11-F11-D11, useful for culturing swine influenza virus.
AU QIAO C
   CHEN H
   YANG H
   CHEN Y
AE HARBIN VETERINARY RES INST CAAS (CAGS-C)
GA 2020717124
AB    NOVELTY - Fully suspended Madin-Darby canine kidney (MDCK) cell D11-F11-D11 having preservation of CCTCCNO: C2019211, is new.
   USE - The fully suspended MDCK cell is useful for culturing swine influenza virus.
   ADVANTAGE - The cell: can multiply swine influenza virus with high titer; provides excellent culture medium for development and production of scaffold-implantable (SI) vaccines; and lays a foundation for the development of cell culture technology for SI vaccine production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for culturing swine influenza virus by using the fully suspended MDCK cells, comprising mixing the fully suspended MDCK cells with swine influenza virus and L-1-p-tosylamino-2-phenylethyl chloromethyl ketone (TPCK)-pancreatin to obtain a mixed solution, and culturing the mixed solution at 33-37 degrees C in presence of 5% carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The mixed solution is cultured in a cell shaking flask. The culturing time is 30-72 hours, preferably 48 hours, and culturing temperature is 35 degrees C. Preferred Components: The content of fully suspended MDCK cells in the mixed solution is 1x 106-1.2x 107 cells/ml. The number of swine influenza virus is calculated by using the formula (PFU is equal to multiplicity of infection (MOI)x number of fully suspended MDCK cells, where the MOI is 0.001-0.01. The concentration of TPCK-pancreatin in the mixed solution is 1-10 mu g/ml, preferably 2-8 mu g/ml.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); C06 (Biotechnology - including plant genetics and veterinary vaccines.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)
MC B04-F01; B04-F11B; B04-L04C; B10-A08; B11-A04A; B14-S11; C04-F01; C04-F11B; C04-L04C; C10-A08; C11-A04A; C14-S11; D05-H08A

PT P
PN WO2020150174-A1; CN113302340-A; US2021388512-A1
TI Synthesizing alkane by providing electrolyte            formulation comprising carboxylic acid and alkali            carboxylate into pressure vessel having electrode cell            or stack, applying electrical current to electrode cell            or stack, and recovering gas.
AU OMALLEY J
   MONTGOMERY D
AE GRIDTHINK INC (GRID-Non-standard)
   GRIDTHINK INC (GRID-Non-standard)
GA 202069092H
AB    NOVELTY - Process for synthesizing 2-8C alkane, involves                (a) providing an electrolyte formulation comprising                3-6 N 2-5C carboxylic acid and 2-4 M alkali 2-5C                carboxylate into a pressure vessel having an                electrode cell or stack, where the carboxylate and                carboxylic acid have the same carbon alkyl length,                (b) applying electrical current to the electrode                cell or stack, (c) pressurizing the pressure                vessel, and (d) recovering a gas stream from the                pressure vessel comprising 2-8C alkane, carbon                dioxide and hydrogen.
   USE - The process is useful for synthesizing 2-8C                alkane.
   ADVANTAGE - The process improves energy efficiency, and                hinders ionic conduction between the                electrodes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The electrodes are in                  a stack configuration comprising alternating                  anodes and cathodes, and have a smooth platinum                  surface, where the electrical current provided to                  the electrode cell or stack has a current density                  of 20-300 mA/cm2, preferably 60-100 mA/cm2.                  Preferred Device: The pressure vessel is                  pressurized by providing a back-pressure                  regulator to a gaseous outlet of the pressure                  vessel or adding the electrolyte with a                  high-pressure electrolyte pump, or both.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The carboxylic acid is                  acetic acid. The alkane is ethane.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).)

PN TW598887-U
TI Vehicle exhaust filter regeneration equipment, has air blowing unit provided with air pipe whose end is provided with air outlet, housing unit provided with hollow inside space, and heating unit arranged below inner side of housing unit.
AU DENGYONG H
AE RUISHENG PRECISION IND CO LTD (RUIS-Non-standard)
GA 2021650277
AB    NOVELTY - This creation is about a regeneration design of vehicle exhaust filter The device mainly includes a blower unit, a housing unit and a heating unit. The air blowing unit is connected with an air inlet pipe to the bottom edge of the shell unit, and the heating unit is arranged below the inside of the shell unit. The regeneration equipment can heat and increase the temperature of the inserted filter element, and cooperate with the rotating flow of the hot air current, which can convert the carbon particles on the filter into carbon dioxide to obtain the regeneration function.

TI Greenhouse planting platform, has storage platform provided with soil surface and soil separator, where soil clapboard and adjacent soil surface are formed with planting groove that is matched with step height along opening direction.
AU ZHOU J
   LI Y
   CHEN H
   CAO J
   LU J
   YIN G
   LIU X
   ZHAO Y
AE UNIV HUBEI (UYUB-C)
   UNIV HANJIANG NORMAL (UYHA-Non-standard)
GA 202061368F
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a novel greenhouse planting platform, comprising a storage platform, storage platform comprises a plurality of soil surface and soil separator, a plurality of soil storing plane are arranged in stepped, soil clapboard and the adjacent soil surface formed with a planting groove, the planting groove is provided along the opening in the height direction of the step. Through reasonable structure using, the plant can grow on the horizontal plane with different heights by the mutual isolation of the step height difference and a planting groove, the plant height do not shade the light, ensuring each plant are acceptable to sufficient carbon dioxide and other resources, reduces the human intervention of cost.

PT P
PN CN111203072-A
TI Filler comprises filler core body and wall flow prevention structure provided outside filler core body.
AU WANG T
   DONG W
   FANG M
   TONG G
   GE H
   LUO Z
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 202049838L
AB    NOVELTY - Filler comprises a filler core body and a wall flow prevention structure provided outside the filler core body. The filler core includes a stainless steel corrugated board on the outside and many layers of plastic corrugated boards in the middle. The adjacent plastic corrugated plates are staggered with each other according to the corrugation direction. The surface of the plastic corrugated board has a micron-level porous structure or a micron-level groove texture structure. The plastic corrugated board comprises 40-90 wt.% base material, 5-50 wt.% hydrophilic material, 0-5 wt.% antioxidant, and 5-30 wt.% reinforcing fiber.
   USE - The filler is useful in absorption tower of flue gas carbon dioxide capture.
   ADVANTAGE - The filler has high wetting performance, high film forming rate, high strength, low manufacturing cost and easy transportation and assembly.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The pore size of the micron-level porous structure on the surface of the plastic corrugated board is 30-200 mu m. The width of the micron-level groove texture structure on the surface of the plastic corrugated board is 10-200 mu m. The width of the stainless steel corrugated board and the plastic corrugated board is 100-300 mm, and the thickness is 0.5-2 mm. The matrix material is polypropylene (PP), polyethylene (PE), polyvinyl chloride (PVC), chlorinated polyvinyl chloride resin (CPVC), polytetrafluoroethylene (PTFE) and/or polyvinylidene fluoride (PVDF). The hydrophilic material is maleic anhydride grafted polypropylene (PP-g-MAH) and/or acrylic grafted polypropylene (PP-g-AA). The antioxidant is antioxidant 264 (BHT), antioxidant 1010, antioxidant 1076 and/or antioxidant 1024. The reinforcing fiber is glass fiber, natural fiber, aramid fiber, and/or talc. The specific surface area of the stainless steel corrugated board or plastic corrugated board is 250-500 m2/m3, the corrugation inclination angle is 30-45 degrees , the peak height is 6-12 mm, the wave distance is 9-25 mm and the side length is 8-17 mm. The stainless steel plate and the plastic corrugated plate are fixed by means of perforation-stainless steel rod. The plastic corrugated board is reinforced with nails.
DC A88 (Mechanical engineering and tools e.g. valves, gears and conveyor belts.); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)
MC A08-M09B; A12-S08; A12-W11D; A12-W11F; J01-E02B
IP B01D-053/14; B01D-053/18

PT P
PN CN110860274-A; CN110860274-B
TI Preparation of adsorbed coke used as heavy metal adsorption material by washing, drying and crushing waste pillow pak, mixing with deionized water, stirring, stirring, heating, adding anhydrous ethanol, heating, cooling and filtering.
AU GUO S
   DONG X
   LIANG S
   WANG S
   GU H
   SUN R
AE NANJING INST TECHNOLOGY (NANJ-C)
GA 2020209685
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of adsorbed coke comprises washing, drying and crushing waste pillow pak, mixing with deionized water, stirring at room temperature, adding to reaction kettle, stirring, heating to reaction temperature, adding anhydrous ethanol, heating to second-stage reaction temperature, immediately passing cooling water to cool, and filtering to obtain solid product; and washing with deionized water, dehydrating, drying, mixing with melamine and p-aminobenzenesulfonic acid, grinding, adding to tube furnace, passing through carbon dioxide, heating, and incubating.
   USE - The method is for preparation of adsorbed coke used as heavy metal adsorption material.
   ADVANTAGE - The adsorbed coke is environment-friendly, and has excellent performance, porous structure and large specific surface area.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mas ratio of waste pillow pak and deionized water is 1:3-6. The ratio of anhydrous ethanol and waste pillow pak powder is 3:1-6:1. The ratio of dehydrated solid, melamine and p-aminobenzenesulfonic acid is 2:1:1. Preferred Method: The first heating is done at 200-250 degrees C and at rate of 5-7 degrees C/minute for 120-180 minutes. The second-stage heating is done at 300-350 degrees C and at rate of 10-15 degrees C/minute for 240-360 minutes. The filtering is done using 0.2-0.3 mu m microporous filter film at vacuum condition of 100-200 Pa. The drying is done at 70-80 degrees C for 20-24 hours. The tubular furnace heating is done at 800-900 degrees C, heating rate of 8-10 degrees C/minute and at carbon dioxide flow rate of 60 mL/minute for 90-120 minutes.
DC J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).)

PN KR2020012159-A; KR2139635-B1
TI Manufacture of sintered ore used as raw material            in blast furnace making process, involves mixing iron            ore, binder, and subsidiary material to prepare blended            raw material(s), mixing blended raw materials, and            sintering.
AU JEONG B
AE POSCO (POSC-C)
GA 2020140036
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of sintered ore involves mixing                and assembling iron ore, a binder (B1), and a                subsidiary material to prepare a blended raw                material (M1), mixing and assembling a binder (B2)                containing a raw material capable of generating                heat by an oxidation reaction and a calcium                oxide-containing raw material to prepare a blended                raw material (M2), mixing the blended raw materials                (M1) and (M2) to prepare a blended raw material,                and sintering the blended raw material.
   USE - Manufacture of sintered ore used as raw                material for manufacturing blast furnace.
   ADVANTAGE - The method effectively suppresses or reduces                the amount of gases e.g. nitrogen oxide and carbon                dioxide harmful to the environment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for blended                raw material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: The binder (B1)                  comprises at least one of anthracite coal and                  coke. Preferred Composition: The total blended                  raw material comprises 3-10 wt.% binder (B),                  2.5-4 wt.% binder (A), and 0.15-2 wt.% calcium                  oxide-containing raw material. The blended raw                  material (M2) comprises 5-20 wt.% calcium                  oxide-containing raw material. The raw material                  comprises 30-50 wt.% metal iron, and 15-30 wt.%                  iron oxide.

TI Reducing decarburization time of semi-steel steelmaking converter comprises e.g. mixing semi-steel in converter, adding active lime, high magnesium lime and ferrosilicon, supplying oxygen by oxygen lance and blowing by bottom-blow gas.
AU CHEN L
AE PANGANG GROUP PANZHIHUA IRON & STEEL RES (ANSH-C)
GA 2019A5941U
AB    NOVELTY - Reducing decarburization time of semi-steel steelmaking converter comprises mixing the semi-steel in the converter, then adding active lime, high magnesium lime and ferrosilicon, while supplying oxygen by oxygen lance and using bottom-blow gas for blowing until the end of the blowing to obtain qualified molten steel, and the bottom-blow gases includes nitrogen gas, carbon dioxide and argon.
   USE - The method is useful for reducing decarburization time of semi-steel steelmaking converter.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: can reduce the decarburization time of the semi-steel steelmaking converter by changing and controlling the bottom blowing gas and the bottom blowing mode of the converter.

TI Method for processing battery abnormality of charging cabinet, involves performing battery position changing process, immersing battery in fire-extinguishing liquid, and immersing charging cabinet in fire-extinguishing fluid.
AU HUANG Y
AE SHENZHEN KUAICHEDAO NEW ENERGY DEV CO (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 201999051M
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves performing charging cabinet battery catching fire monitoring process. Battery position changing process is performed. The battery is immersed in fire-extinguishing liquid. The charging cabinet is immersed in fire-extinguishing fluid. Battery surface temperature detecting process is performed. Smoke monitoring process is performed. Carbon dioxide concentration monitoring process is performed on a charging chamber. Oxygen concentration monitoring process is performed on the charging chamber.
   USE - Method for processing battery abnormality of a charging cabinet (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables reducing battery fire catching risk and working difficulties of working personnel and avoiding fire occurring phenomenon and property loss.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating a method for processing battery abnormality of a charging cabinet. '(Drawing includes non-English language text)'

   ADVANTAGE - The pH-sensitive maltodextrin copolymer is decomposed at relatively low pH inflammatory lesion site, and can increase the signal of fluorescent material, while generating carbon dioxide and bubbles, when contraction and expansion are repeated upon contact with ultrasonic waves.

   n = 5-100;and
   at least two of R1-R6 = OH, and the others are H.

   (1) preparation of pH-sensitive maltodextrin copolymer;

Preferred Composition: The pH-sensitive drug carrier comprises drug(s), preferably antiatopic drug, antioxidant, anticancer agent, antiinflammatory agent, anti- apoptotic agent, thrombolytic agent, antiinflammatory drug delivery, analgesic, antiviral agent, and/or antimicrobial agent. The maltodextrin copolymer is decomposed in an acid to release the drug.

AU KIM S G
   GIL C Y
AE CHEONG Y G (CHEO-Individual)
   KIM S G (KIMS-Individual)
   GIL C Y (GILC-Individual)
GA 201974382D
AB    NOVELTY - An artificial wood manufacturing method involves drying coffee waste to a water content of 5% at a drying temperature of 80-100 degrees C to get primary dried coffee waste. The primary dried coffee waste is dried to a water content of 0.1-0.5%, followed by preparing a pellet material by weighing and mixing the second dried coffee waste, adding foaming agent and extruding the pellet material.
   USE - Method for manufacturing artificial wood for eco-friendly building materials.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables manufacturing artificial wood with reduced energy consumption, better application of producing eco-friendly building materials with high thermal efficiency, high functionality, reduced carbon dioxide emissions and high performance windows.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Process: The pellet material is prepared by taking 51-70 wt.% secondary dried coffee waste, and 30-40 wt.% thermoplastic resin selected from polypropylene, polyethylene, and polyethylene terephthalate, 2-6 wt.% acrylic processing aid, 0.5-1 wt.% silicone dispersant, 0.1-1 wt.% rosin powder, and 0.05 wt.% foaming agent.

PN RU2697804-C1
TI METHOD OF PRODUCING 1-(-HYDROXYALKYL)URACILS.
AU NOVAKOV I A
   BABUSHKIN A S
   BRUNILINA L L
   KIRILLOV I A
   NAVROTSKII M B
   ROBINOVICH M D
   YABLOKOV A S
AE UNIV VOLG STATE TECH (UYVL-Non-standard)
GA 201977497Q
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to an improved method of producing 1-(w-hydroxyalkyl)uracils, which can be used in pharmaceutical industry as intermediate products in synthesis of medicinal compounds. Method consists in that uracil is subjected to silylation with excess of hexamethyldisilazane in presence of trimethylchlorosilane, obtained 2,4-bis-((trimethylsilyl)oxy)pyrimidine is alkylated with subsequent cooling and treatment with 5 wt% with aqueous solution of sodium pyrosulphite and tetrahydro silvane. Then organic phase is separated by removal of solvent, evaporation to dryness at low pressure and alkaline hydrolysis of obtained product with boiling 5M solution of potassium hydroxide in 95 wt% ethanol with further removal of solvent, dissolution of residue in water and saturation of mixture with carbon dioxide. Extraction of the end product is carried out with tetrahydrosilvano with removal of water and evaporation of the solvent.
   USE - Chemistry.
   ADVANTAGE - Method increases output of end products to 5772% and simplifies the method by avoiding column chromatography.
1 cl, 5 ex

AE LI T (LITT-Individual)
GA 2019480426

   USE - Method for protecting battery terminal using layered gel.

The oxygen, moisture, carbon dioxide are isolated from silicon terminals for protecting the silicon terminal. The good protective film is formed, and the silicon terminal is protected from oxidative corrosion for a long period of time.

PN CN109338501-A
TI Preparing easy dyeing and cooling fiber comprises            e.g. preparing filler, preparing fiber, and biological            modification.
AU CAO Z
   CAO Q
   LI T
   CAO X
   LI H
AE ANHUI BAOZITOU CLOTHING CO LTD (ANHU-Non-standard)
GA 2019185136
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing easy dyeing and cooling fiber                comprises (1) adding cooling sensitizer to the                peppermint oil, ball milling and ultrasonic                dispersion to obtain mixture of cooling agent,                subjecting mixed cooling agent and coupling agent                to supercritical carbon dioxide reaction to obtain                filler, (2) vacuum drying polyamide 6 and filler,                adding xylitol, premixing, melt spinning the                premix, cooling and shaping to obtain cooling                sensation fiber, and (3) placing the cooling                sensation fiber in enzyme solution, soaking for                10-30 minutes by ultrasonic soaking and drying to                obtain easy dyeing and cooling fiber.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing easy dyeing                and cooling fiber.
   ADVANTAGE - The fiber: ensures water molecules close to                the surface of the fiber and adsorbed to the                activation center, thus improves hydrophilicity and                dyeability of the fiber; and has thermal                conductivity of 0.182 W/m/k.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - TEXTILES AND PAPER - Preferred Components: The cooling sensitizer                  is composed of jade powder, aluminum nitride and                  borneol powder in mass ratio of 1:0.8-1.8:0.3-1.2                  and particle diameter of 400-600 nm. The coupling                  agent is at least one of KH540 or KH550. The                  dyeable cooling sensation fiber comprises 3-12                  wt.%, preferably 8 wt.% peppermint oil, 0.5-1.5                  wt.%, preferably 0.85 wt.% cooling agent, 1.5-4.5                  wt.%, preferably 3 wt.% xylitol, 0.02-0.15 wt.%,                  preferably 0.1 wt.% coupling agent, and polyamide                  6 (balance). Preferred Method: In the method, the                  ball milling treatment rotational speed is                  1500-2500 revolutions/minute and duration is 8-20                  minutes. In the method, the ultrasonic dispersion                  has power of 150-300 W and frequency of 25-40                  kHz, the ultrasonic soaking power is 100-150 W                  and the frequency is 20-30 kHz. In the method,                  the pre-reaction pressure of the supercritical                  carbon dioxide reaction is 3-8 MPa, the reaction                  pressure is 15-30 MPa and the temperature is                  30-45 degrees C. In the method, the melt spinning                  has melt temperature of 260-280 degrees C,                  spinning rate of 4.5-5.5 km/minute, and draw                  ratio of 1.1-1.35. In the method, the enzyme                  solution is an amidase solution at temperature of                  35-45 degrees C and pH of 7.5-8.5.

PN JP2019014190-A; JP7001989-B2
TI Laminated body comprises base material layer and sealant layer, where base material layer comprises polyethylene terephthalate having ethylene glycol as diol unit, and terephthalic acid and isophthalic acid as dicarboxylic acid unit.
AU TANAKA D
   KOMAGATA N
AE DAINIPPON PRINTING CO LTD (NIPQ-C)
   DAINIPPON PRINTING CO LTD (NIPQ-C)
GA 201912149W
AB    NOVELTY - Laminated body (10) comprises a base material layer (11) and sealant layer (12) in this order, where the base material layer comprises polyethylene terephthalate having ethylene glycol as a diol unit and terephthalic acid and isophthalic acid as a dicarboxylic acid unit. The sealant layer has multiple layers, where one of the multiple layers is a barrier resin layer (121).
   USE - Laminated body.
   ADVANTAGE - The laminated body has excellent carbon dioxide reduction effect and superior hygiene as compared with a laminate made of unrecycled polyester.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of laminated body.
   Laminated body (10)
   Base material layer (11)
   Sealant layer (12)
   Barrier resin layer (121)
   First thermoplastic resin layer (122)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The sealant layer includes a first thermoplastic resin layer provided on a surface of the barrier resin layer on a side opposite to the base material layer, and a second thermoplastic resin layer provided on the surface of the barrier resin layer on the base material side layer. The sealant layer includes a third thermoplastic resin layer positioned between the barrier resin layer and first thermoplastic resin layer, and a fourth thermoplastic resin layer positioned between the barrier resin layer and second thermoplastic resin layer. The barrier resin layer contains a saponified product of an ethylene-vinyl acetate copolymer. The barrier resin layer contains polyamide. The barrier resin layer contains polyvinyl alcohol. The content of the isophthalic acid is 0.5 mol.% or more and 5.0 mol.% or less with respect to all dicarboxylic acid units constituting the polyethylene terephthalate. The intrinsic viscosity of the polyethylene terephthalate is 0.58 decilitre/g or more and 0.80 decilitre/ g or less.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

AE FUYANG HONGYE ECOLOGICAL AGRIC TECHNOLOG (FUYA-Non-standard)
GA 2018A08104
AB    NOVELTY - Preservation of cherries involves sorting                cherries to remove cracked fruit, diseased fruit,                deformed fruit, stabbed fruit and stiff fruit,                rinsing with water, gently brushing skin of the                cherries with a soft brush, quickly drying,                sterilizing using 3-5% formaldehyde for 1-2 hours,                soaking cherries in 35-40 mg/kg calcium chloride                solution for 1-2 hours, air-drying cherries,                pre-cooling to 1-3&#176; C for 0.3-0.4 hours,                placing in air-conditioned cold storage environment                at 0-1&#176; C, and controlling carbon dioxide                concentration to 3-5%.
   USE - Preservation of cherries.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple preservation of                cherries with prolonged preservation time.

GA 2018A1447A

TI Preparing strontium carbonate involves adding strontium hydroxide into secondary distilled water at room temperature, uniformly stirring to prepare solution of strontium hydroxide, and heating solid ammonium bicarbonate.
AU XIE Y
   CHEN Z
   WANG J
   ZHONG M
AE CHONGQING DAZU HONGDIE STRONTIUM CO LTD (CHON-Non-standard)
GA 201897182G
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing strontium carbonate involves adding strontium hydroxide into secondary distilled water at room temperature in ratio of 1:8, uniformly stirring to prepare solution of strontium hydroxide. The solid ammonium bicarbonate is heated. The carbon dioxide gas and ammonia gas is generated. The heated product is added into solution of strontium hydroxide obtain strontium hydroxide solution. The dispersant is added into mixed solution, stirring uniformly to obtain reaction liquid. The reaction solution is added to solid-liquid separation to obtain filter cake and filtrate. The filter cake is washed and calcined to obtain strontium carbonate.
   USE - Method for preparing strontium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare strontium carbonate that has high purity, low impurity content, spherical crystal and uniform grain size, and satisfies performance requirements.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Condition: The room temperature is 15-28 degrees C. The flow rate of gas is 60-120 mL/minute. The dropping speed of the dispersant is 8-12 mL/minute. The washing is carried out by secondary distilled water. The number of washing is 4-6. The calcining temperature is 400-600 degrees C for 2 hours. Preferred Components: The dispersing agent is triethylhexylphosphoric acid, sodium lauryl sulfate, methylpentanol, cellulose derivatives, polyacrylamide, guar gum, and fatty acid polyethylene glycol ester.

   KOREA INST SCI & TECHNOLOGY (KSCT-C)
GA 201813589W
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of boron nitride nanotubes, involves injecting a boron-metal catalyst composite and nitrogen precursor into a reaction chamber, producing a decomposition product of the catalyst composite in gaseous state by irradiating the catalyst composite with carbon dioxide laser or a free electron laser, and reacting the decomposition product with the nitrogen precursor.
   USE - Preparation of boron nitride nanotubes used for composite material application, and fiber applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The method economically and efficiently produces single and double-walled boron nitride nanotubes at faster production rate and high production ratio. The boron nitride nanotubes are highly pure, and have reduced/nil defects and excellent strength, heat resistance, corrosion resistance, chemical resistance and thermal conductivity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ELECTRONICS - Preferred Components: The metal catalyst is deposited by a deposition device chosen from deposition device using electron beam, sputtering deposition device, electroplating device, and electroless plating device.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method further involves etching the metal catalyst from the boron nitride nanotubes with an etchant. Preferred Condition: The nitrogen precursor is injected into the reaction chamber at a pressure of 1-300 atm. Preferred Composition: The ratio of single-walled boron nitride nanotubes to double-walled boron nitride nanotubes in the boron nitride nanotubes, is 70-99.9 wt.%. Preferred Components: The boron-metal catalyst composite comprises boron fiber and a metal catalyst deposited onto the boron fiber, or the boron-metal catalyst composite is a mixture comprising boron powder and metal catalyst. The metal catalyst comprises nickel, copper, iron, chromium, cobalt, zinc, aluminum, silicon, scandium, titanium, vanadium, manganese, gallium, germanium, yttrium, zirconium, niobium, molybdenum, technetium, ruthenium, rhodium, palladium, silver, hafnium, tantalum, tungsten, rhenium, osmium, iridium, platinum, gold, bronze, stainless steel, brass, white brass, and their combinations. The boron-metal catalyst composite further comprises a solid precursor comprising nitrogen atom. The etchant is chosen from ammonium persulfate, nickel etchant, copper etchant, iron chloride, nitric acid, hydrochloric acid, and sulfuric acid. Preferred Property: The boron nitride nanotubes have an average internal diameter of 1-10 nm.

PN CN107662924-A
TI Preparing silicon dioxide aerogel comprises e.g.            carrying out hydrolysis by passing polysilicon            production tail gas into water to obtain silica sol,            adjusting pH of silica sol with alkaline solution and            then gelatinizing.
AU WANG R
   YIN B
   FAN X
   WU Z
AE XINTE ENERGY CO LTD (XINT-Non-standard)
GA 201812396S
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing silicon dioxide aerogel comprises                (i) carrying out hydrolysis by passing polysilicon                production tail gas into water to obtain silica                sol, where polysilicon production tail gas is                dichlorosilane, trichlorosilane and silicon                tetrachloride, (ii) adjusting pH of silica sol with                alkaline solution, gelatinizing to obtain silica                gel, (iii) carrying out solvent substitution by                mixing silica gel and replacing water in silica                gel, (iv) carrying out supercritical carbon dioxide                drying or low temperature freezing and drying to                obtain silicon dioxide aerogel.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing silicon                dioxide aerogel.
   ADVANTAGE - The aerogel: has simple preparing process and                low cost; and is environmentally friendly.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (i), further                  comprises concentrating silica sol to obtain                  concentrated silica sol. The concentrated silica                  sol has a concentration of 6-20 wt.%. In step                  (ii), pH of silica sol solution is 8-11. In step                  (ii), temperature of gelation treatment is 50-110                  degrees C, and gelation treatment time is 10-48                  hours. In step (iii), carrying out                  hydrophobilization and modification of silica                  aerogel for 12-30 hours. The method comprises                  carrying out leaching and hydrolysis by passing                  polysilicon production tail gas into water to                  obtain silica sol, where polysilicon production                  tail gas is dichlorosilane, trichlorosilane and                  silicon tetrachloride, connecting stirring tank                  with the leaching tower, adjusting pH of silica                  sol with alkaline solution, carrying out gelation                  treatment to obtain silicone gel, adding solvent                  into stirring tank to perform solvent replacement                  on silica gel, replacing water in the silicone                  gel, connecting dryer to the stirred tank, where                  dryer is supercritical carbon dioxide dryer or                  cryogenic freeze dryer. The evaporator includes                  an evaporator inlet and an evaporator outlet, the                  evaporator inlet is connected to the elution                  tower, the evaporator outlet is connected to the                  stirring tank, the evaporator is used to                  concentrate the silica sol entering the                  evaporator from the elution column to obtain                  concentrated silica sol. The method comprises                  passing hydrolysis of polysilicon production tail                  gas into the first-stage elution tower,                  connecting washing tower with the first level                  washing tower enters to the secondary washing                  tower to generate polycrystalline silicon tail                  gas with water eluting for the second stage                  hydrolysis. The method comprises connecting with                  the liquid seal tank for liquid seal of gas                  discharged by the leaching tower in the liquid                  seal tank and then discharged. The chlorine ion                  detector is connected with the liquid sealing                  tank, and the chlorine ion detector is used for                  detecting the concentration of chloride ions in                  the gas after liquid sealing in the liquid                  sealing tank. The controller is connected to the                  chlorine ion detector, a first valve is provided                  at the entrance of the washing tower for                  producing polysilicon to generate polysilicon                  production tail gas, a second valve is provided                  at the outlet of the liquid seal tank, first                  valve is connected to the controller, the second                  valve is connected to the controller, the                  chlorine ion detector sends the detected                  concentration of chloride ions to the controller,                  when chloride ion concentration detected by the                  chlorine ion detector is lower than the first                  predetermined chloride ion concentration, then                  the controller controls the second valve to open                  for exhausting, or the controller controls the                  second valve to be closed, when the concentration                  of the chloride ions in the chloride ion detected                  by the detection device is higher than the second                  concentration of the preset chlorine ion, the                  first valve is closed to stop to the leaching                  tower is filled with polysilicon production tail                  gas, or controller controls the first valve to                  close. Preferred Components: The hydrophobilizing                  modifier is hexamethyl disilazane, hexamethyl                  disiloxane, hexamethyl disilane, dimethyl                  dimethoxy silane, dimethyl diethoxy silane,                  trimethyl ethoxysilane or trimethyl chlorosilane.                  The first preset chloride ion concentration is                  2-4 parts per minute, and second predetermined                  chlorine ion concentration is 10-15 parts per                  minute.

PN CN115039730-A
TI Performing permanently sealing of carbon dioxide            with carbon dioxide and producing fish at same time            involves realizing by ring-shaped captive fish            equipment set in artificial natural fishery, where            artificial natural fishery is growth of marine            organisms created by inhabitants of marine            organisms.
AU WU M
AE WU M (WUMM-Individual)
GA 2022C0563W
AB    NOVELTY - Performing permanently sealing of carbon                dioxide with carbon dioxide and producing fish at                the same time involves realizing by a ring-shaped                captive fish equipment set in an artificial natural                fishery. The artificial natural fishery is the                growth of marine organisms created by the                inhabitants of marine organisms installed in the                true light layer, and the nutrient supply devices                required for the growth of marine organisms. The                attached habitation object for marine organisms set                in the true light layer is to set a cave structure                composed of small floating bodies on a perforated                plane that can float in water for marine organisms                to inhabit. Cables on the cave structure are                connected to the artificial cement algae reefs                submerged in the seabed, so that the cave structure                is in the true light layer. A tree-like body with a                large contact area with water is arranged on a                perforated surface that can float in water for                marine micro-organisms to attach.
   USE - Method for performing permanently sealing of                carbon dioxide with carbon dioxide and producing                fish at the same time.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to perform permanently                sealing of carbon dioxide with carbon dioxide and                producing fish at the same time, where carbon                dioxide is converted into ocean inert dissolved                organic carbon and stored in seawater for more than                5,000 years, completely eliminating the excessive                greenhouse effect, atmospheric carbon dioxide                concentration return to 280PPm before the                industrial revolution, completely restore the                natural ecology, and realize the natural cycle of                carbon and nitrogen that can fully meet the needs                of human beings.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The nutrient supply device required for the                growth of the marine organisms is on a structural                whole composed of a hollow floating body and a                structural frame. The wave energy collection                devices, wind energy collection devices, air                compressors, water pumps, generators, warning                lights are set up. The transmission rod is                connected to the wave energy collecting floating                body to form a wave energy collecting device. The                wave energy collecting floating body is divided                into upper and lower layers. The upper layer is a                hollow floating body. The lower layer is a                perforated container, which contains the nutrients                required by the primary marine organisms to use                carbon dioxide for photosynthesis. The transmission                rod of the wave energy harvesting device is                connected to the input shaft of the directional                rotation output gearbox. The directional output                shaft of the directional rotation output gearbox                drives the air compressor, water pump and generator                to work through the speed-increasing gearbox. The                wind energy transmission shaft is connected with                the wind energy collection impeller to form a wind                energy collection device. The drive shaft of the                wind energy collection device is connected with the                directional output shaft of the directional                rotation output gear box through the bevel gear to                couple the collected wind energy and wave energy                together. The outlet pipe of the air compressor                leads to the true light layer to provide more                nitrogen sources for the growth of marine                nitrogen-fixing organisms. Multiple air outlets on                the air outlet pipe to disperse the gas, and the                water inlet pipe on the water pump leads to the                nutrient-rich water that is sucked into the deep                ocean. The outlet pipe on the water pump leads to                the true photosphere to provide more growth                nutrients for the primary marine organisms to use                carbon dioxide for photosynthesis. The whole                structure is connected to the artificial cement                algae reef submerged in the sea by cables. The                weight of the artificial cement algae reef makes                the hollow floating body on the whole structure                below the water surface through the cable. The                annular captive fish equipment is composed of                modules annular ring, net, fish feeding device,                horizontal axis wind turbine, vertical axis wind                turbine, wave energy power generation device, water                pumping equipment, air compression device                composition. The ring-shaped captive fish equipment                is set in an artificial natural fishery. The module                refers to the structural whole formed by supporting                the platform away from the water surface on the                floating body of the underwater sub-silo. Rigid                connection between modules forms a solid structural                unit. A non-rigid connector refers to the use of a                stretchable non-rigid material between two adjacent                connectors to tighten the multiple connection                points of the two connectors. The fish feeding                device is a device that is provided with a net-like                fishing device at one end of the pipe, and the                middle end of the pipe is mounted on the bracket                set on the annular ring with a shaft. A telescopic                device is provided between the pipe and the bracket                so that the pipe can rotate around the axis, and                the fishing net is equipped with a fishing light                and a bait release device. After catching the fish                that grow naturally in the artificial natural                fishery, the fish trap can enter the annular circle                through the pipeline to replace the feed to feed                the artificially cultured high value-added fish in                the circle. The horizontal axis wind turbine,                vertical axis wind turbine and wave energy power                generation device are installed on the annular                ring. The water pumping equipment is that water                pumps are connected to the water inlet pipe and the                water outlet pipe and are installed on the annular                ring. The water pump is driven by ocean energy, and                the water inlet pipe of a water pump is leading to                the depth of the ocean in the annular ring to suck                in the eutrophic water that settles to the depths                of the ocean due to fish farming in the upper                layer. The outlet pipe leads to the artificial                natural fishery to provide nutrients for marine                primary organisms. Another type of pump's inlet                pipe is to the ocean depths away from the annular                ring to suck the nutrient-rich water from the ocean                depths. The water in the outlet pipe passes through                containers containing nutrients, and then passes                through pipes with switches to join the main outlet                pipe, and then enters the water diversion pipe to                the artificial natural fishery to provide nutrients                for marine primary organisms. A nutrient inlet is                provided on the container containing nutrients, and                a water inlet pipe of a water pump leads to the                upper layer outside the annular ring to inhale                fresh water. The water outlet pipeline leads to the                annular ring to supplement fresh water for fish                farming in the annular ring. The air compressor is                driven by ocean energy, and the outlet pipe of an                air compressor is the true light layer leading to                the artificial natural fishery to provide more                nitrogen sources for nitrogen-fixing organisms and                another kind of air compressor outlet pipe is to                the fish breeding water in the annular ring to                provide more oxygen for the fish school.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The nutrient supply                  device required for the growth of marine                  organisms in the artificial natural fishery. A                  bamboo tail is provided with a large contact area                  with water for the marine micro-organisms to                  attach on the hollow floating body of the                  nutrient supply device. The wave energy in the                  nutrient supply device required for the growth of                  marine organisms in the artificial natural                  fishery. The wave energy collection floating body                  is divided into upper and lower layers, the upper                  layer is a perforated container, and the lower                  layer is a hollow floating body. The perforated                  container holds the nutrients needed by the                  primary marine organisms to use carbon dioxide                  for photosynthesis. A high-voltage discharge                  device is arranged on the upper perforated                  container, and an air inlet pipe and a water                  inlet pipe are arranged on the high-voltage                  discharge device. The attachment habitats of                  marine organisms in the artificial natural                  fishery are connected with the nutrient supply                  devices required for the growth of marine                  organisms through cables. The many marine                  creatures in the artificial natural fishery are                  the dependent habitats, and is a bamboo tail that                  has a large contact area with water on the rope                  for marine life to attach and inhabit. A floating                  body is provided with a specific gravity lower                  than water on the rope, and the rope is connected                  with the artificial cement algae reef submerged                  in the seabed through the cable, so that the                  bamboo tail is in the true light layer. The water                  inlet pipe is the eutrophic water that leads to                  the ocean depths outside the annular ring and                  sucked into the ocean depths. The water in the                  outlet pipe passes through containers containing                  nutrients, and then passes through pipes with                  switches, respectively, into the main outlet                  pipe, and then into the water diversion pipe,                  leading to the artificial natural fishery to                  provide nutrients for marine primary organisms.                  The container containing nutrients is provided                  with an inlet for nutrients, and a high-voltage                  discharge device is provided on the water inlet                  pipe of one of the containers containing                  nutrients. Air inlet pipes and water inlet pipes                  are provided on the high voltage discharge                  device. The inlet pipe is eutrophic water that                  leads to the ocean depths outside the annular                  ring and sucked into the ocean depths for water                  pumping equipment on the annular captive fish                  equipment. The water outlet pipeline leads to the                  artificial natural fishery to provide nutrients                  for the primary marine organisms, and the output                  pipeline with the synthetic urea device is                  connected with the water outlet pipeline.

PN US2021355243-A1
TI Bio-based material comprises nanocellulose treated with low-temperature plasma, bio-based material is capable of sequestering carbon dioxide from ambient atmosphere, bio-based material is derived from natural and renewable materials.
AU WHITE D H
   CURRY M L
AE UNIV TUSKEGEE (UYTU-Non-standard)
GA 2021D0327E
AB    NOVELTY - Bio-based material comprises nanocellulose treated with low-temperature plasma. The bio-based material is capable of sequestering carbon dioxide from ambient atmosphere.
   USE - Bio-based material.
   ADVANTAGE - The bio-based material improves energy efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following: a filter device, which comprises bio-based material; and#a method for making bio-based material, which involves applying nanocellulose material with low temperature plasma to modify surface of the nanocellulose to provide for surface of the nanocellulose material which is capable of sequestering CO_2 from atmosphere.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The bio-based material is derived entirely from natural and renewable materials. Preferred Conditions: The nanocellulose is treated with low-temperature plasma having electron energies less than 15 electron volt. The nanocellulose is treated with low-temperature plasma for five minutes. The modified nanocellulose surface is created upon nanocellulose which is treated with the low-temperature plasma. The modified nanocellulose surface sequesters carbon dioxide from the ambient atmosphere. The oxygen gas is provided at a flow rate of about 10 parts per minute up to and includes about 50 ppm. The flow rate of the oxygen gas is maintained at a constant rate. The constant rate of oxygen gas is about 40 cm^3/min. Preferred Process: The method involves applying the nanocellulose material with low temperature plasma, applying the low temperature plasma with a tumbler chamber. The method involves providing an oxygen gas of high level of purity with the low temperature plasma.
   EXAMPLE - None given.

PN CN110227360-A; CN110227360-B
TI Method for preparing lignosulfonate doped blend film for use in carbon dioxide separating filed, involves adding polymer matrix and additive to aqueous solution, and fully stirring mixture, and performing solution casting method.
AU WANG Y
   ZHANG X
   LIU C
AE UNIV TAIYUAN TECHNOLOGY (UYTL-C)
GA 201980568B
AB    NOVELTY - A lignosulfonate doped blend film preparing method involves adding polymer matrix and additive to an aqueous solution, and fully stirring the mixture to form a uniform casting solution, and performing a solution casting method to obtain the finished product, where the prepared lignosulfonate-doped blend membrane is a homogeneous dense membrane.
   USE - Method for preparing lignosulfonate doped blend film for use in carbon dioxide separating filed (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparing lignosulfonate doped blend film with better flexibility, excellent comprehensive performance, in simple and eco-friendly manner.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solvent chosen is deionized water. The lignosulfonate comprises commercially available lignosulfonate or an aminated modified lignosulfonate, sodium salt, potassium salt, calcium salt or magnesium salt. Preferred Method: The amination modification method is placing a dried lignosulfonate in a three-necked flask, and stirring deionized water to ensure lignin sulfonate, the mass fraction of the acid salt in the deionized water solution is 2-8 wt.%, and adjusting the pH to 9-12 with an alkali solution, adding ethylenediamine, heating at 60-90 degrees C in a water bath, and adding an aldehyde-containing compound to ensure the amount ratio of the lignosulfonate, ethylenediamine and aldehyde group is 1:0.1-10:2, after mechanical stirring for 6-18 hours, after cooling the reaction liquid at 25 degrees C, precipitating with ethanol, filtering and washing for 3-5 times, and drying in an oven at 50-80 degrees C for 6-24 hours to obtain the finished product.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The additive is polyethylene glycol and lignosulfonate. The crosslinking agent comprises glutaraldehyde, glyoxal, maleic anhydride, borax and gamma -aminopropyltriethoxysilane. The aldehyde group-containing compound comprises formaldehyde, acetaldehyde and glutaraldehyde.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The polymer matrix is polyvinyl alcohol polymer.

TI Preparing stannic sulfide/titanium dioxide            nanocomposite material, comprises e.g. dispersing            titanium dioxide nanosheet in aqueous solution            containing stannic chloride pentahydrate and L-cystine,            and carrying out hydrothermal reaction.
AU SHE H
   ZHOU H
   MA X
AE UNIV NORTHWEST NORMAL (UNWN-C)
   UNIV NORTHWEST NORMAL (UNWN-C)
GA 201892885Q
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing stannic sulfide/titanium dioxide                nanocomposite material comprises dispersing                titanium dioxide nanosheet in aqueous solution                containing stannic chloride pentahydrate and                L-cystine, stirring, dispersing, carrying out                hydrothermal reaction at 120-150 degrees C for                10-12 hours, centrifuging and collecting the grey                product, then washing with ethanol, and drying at                80-100 degrees C for 10-15 hours to obtain final                product.

   SHEN L

   YU R

   WU C

AE UNIV CENT SOUTH (UYCS-C)
GA 201845567H
AB    NOVELTY - Method for extracting guava leaf terpene substance, involves washing guava leaves, drying the guava leaves, crushing the dried guava leaves into powder, adding ethanol to crushed guava leaves in ultrasonic cell crusher for ultrasonic treatment, drying the mixture, subjecting the powder to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, collecting the extract, vacuum-drying the extract, purifying the extract by macroporous resin column, eluting the mixture and concentrating the mixture.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting guava leaf terpene substance (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and rapid to perform and saves energy.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The sieve is 40-60 meshes sieve. The ethanol is 50 vol.% ethanol solution. The ratio of the guava leaf and the ethanol is 1:10-15 g/ml. The temperature of the ultrasonic treatment is 48-52 degrees C, the power of the ultrasonic treatment is 300-350 W, and the time of the ultrasonic treatment is 5-10 minutes. The specific operation of the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction involves adding the dried powder to ethyl acetate as an entrainer, and homogenizing the mixture. The supercritical extraction device is extraction device. The ratio of the powder and ethyl acetate is 1:0.5-2 g/ml. The extraction temperature is 43-50 degrees C, the extraction pressure is 20-25 MPa, and the carbon dioxide flow rate is 18-22 l/hour and the extraction time is 1-2 hour. The macroporous resin column is AB-8 macroporous resin column. Preferred Method: The method involves pretreating the macroporous adsorption resin, and applying resin and sample liquid to the column. The sample liquid is dispersed in water to obtain a triterpenoid enrichment having a concentration of 0.8 mg/ml, and the pH is 6.0. The upper column flow rate is 2 BV/hour.

PT P
PN US2022323902-A1
TI Emission clean-up system enables to perform mixing            blended solution with solubilizer, in water, forming            ionic solid and alkaline liquid which is passed through            wet scrubber, to interface with flue gases formed from            power plant, and remove carbon dioxide and detrimental            gases such as sulfur dioxide.
AU KOLEBER K J
   WOUNDERBERG M P
   BILLER G M
   KUKU L O
AE GLOBAL CARBON EMISSIONS SOLUTIONS LLC (GLOB-Non-standard)
GA 2022C92524
AB    NOVELTY - An exhaust emissions clean-up system enables                to perform a process of mixing a blended solution                with a solubilizer, in water, to form an ionic                solid and an alkaline liquid. The alkaline liquid                is passed through a wet scrubber, to interface with                flue gases generated from a power plant, to remove                carbon dioxide (CO2) and other detrimental gases,                and form a sodium bicarbonate solution.
The enhancement process involves: 1)                initiating chemical reaction; 2) preparing calcium                phosphate solid and sodium hydroxide liquid                solution; 3) preparing sodium bicarbonate solution;                4) forming salt slurry; 5) directing salt slurry                through filter and membrane degausser, to separate                CO2 from slurry; 6) drawing off CO2 from salt                slurry, which is then stored in a pressure tank;                and 7) passing balance salt slurry, in the form of                sodium phosphate solution, through a deionizer, to                remove water, followed by passing sodium phosphate                to storage tank for commercial use and reuse, in                one of the processes.
   USE - Emission clean-up system used for removing                various detrimental gases, such as, sulfur dioxide,                carbon dioxide and nitrogen oxides from fossil fuel                power plants or other industrial power plants                before exhaust gas enters atmosphere.
   ADVANTAGE - The exhaust emissions clean-up system enables                to remove detrimental exhaust gas compositions when                the exhaust gases are exposed to a chemically                produced alkaline liquid cleaning solution, more                specifically to clean exhaust gases from fossil                fuel fired plants and remove the detrimental                exhaust gas compositions. The system also enables                CO2 capture and to reclaim various byproducts from                the used cleaning solutions and sequester captured                CO2.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An exhaust emissions clean-up system enables                to perform a process of mixing a blended solution                with a solubilizer, in water, to form an ionic                solid and an alkaline liquid. The alkaline liquid                is passed through a wet scrubber, to interface with                flue gases generated from a power plant, to remove                carbon dioxide (CO2) and other detrimental gases,                and form a sodium bicarbonate solution. The                enhancement process involves: 1) mixing calcium                hydroxide solution with sodium phosphate solution                in a mixing tank, to initiate a chemical reaction;                2) preparing a calcium phosphate solid and a sodium                hydroxide liquid solution; 3) passing the sodium                hydroxide liquid solution through wet scrubber, to                capture CO2 from power plant exhaust gases, to                prepare a sodium bicarbonate solution; 4) passing                the sodium bicarbonate solution to an acid dosing                tank containing phosphoric acid, to initiate a                chemical reaction, to form a salt slurry; 5)                directing the salt slurry through a filter and                membrane degasser and separate CO2 from slurry; 6)                drawing off CO2 from the salt slurry by vacuum                pump, which is then stored in a pressure tank; and                7) passing the balance salt slurry, in the form of                sodium phosphate solution, through a deionizer, to                remove water, followed by passing sodium phosphate                to storage tank for commercial use and reuse, in                one of the processes.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   1) control system which is a continuous                operating control system used for producing                alkaline liquids that interface with flue gases in                a wet scrubber and remove CO2 and other detrimental                flue gases, and form sodium bicarbonate solution.                The system comprises a storage and supply system                having supply and storage tanks, and processes                operative to produce various blended solutions,                various solubilizers, various ionic solids and acid                solutions, and to produce and store various useful                byproducts; and
   2) process which involves:
   a) mixing sodium carbonate solution or sodium                bicarbonate solution with phosphoric acid in an                acid dosing tank, to initiate a chemical reaction                and form CO2 from the formed salt solution;                b)separating CO2 from the salt solution and storing                in a pressure tank for commercial uses;
   c) separating sodium phosphate from salt                solution; and
   d) storing sodium phosphate for reuse in one                of other processes, and for commercial use.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: 1)The calcium phosphate                  solid prepared in the exhaust emissions clean-up                  system, is passed to the storage tank of the                  system, for commercial use.2) The mixing of sodium carbonate or sodium                  bicarbonate in the exhaust emissions clean-up                  process, involves mixing sodium carbonate with                  the phosphoric acid.Preferred Components: 1) The control system                  comprises an operating program effective to                  provide operating commands to ones of the                  supply/storage tanks, in order to controllably                  direct the desired solution(s) from the selected                  supply tanks, to the associated functional                  tank.2)The operating program is also effective to                  provide operating commands to ones of the                  associated functional tanks to controllably                  direct the associated solution to the associated                  supply/storage tank.

PT P
PN EP3574994-A1; WO2019229040-A1; CN112203760-A; EP3801885-A1; US2021213422-A1; JP2021526452-W
TI Production of catalyst monolith for reforming of            hydrocarbons, involves preparing suspension paste in            liquid diluent of particles of catalyst containing            nickel-magnesium mixed oxide, extruding, forming            precursor, drying, and calcining.
AU LANVER V
   KAUFMANN RECHULSKI M D
   ALTWASSER S
   KAUFMAN R M D
AE BASF SE (BADI-C)
   BASF SE (BADI-C)
   BASF SE (BADI-C)
   BASF SE (BADI-C)
GA 2019A12052
AB    NOVELTY - Production of three-dimensional porous                catalyst monolith involves preparing a suspension                paste in a liquid diluent of particles of a                catalyst (A) comprising at least nickel-magnesium                mixed oxide having an average crystallite size of                less than or equal to 100 nm, preferably less than                or equal to 40 nm and magnesium spinel having an                average crystallite size of less than or equal to                100 nm, preferably less than or equal to 40 nm and                optionally aluminum oxy hydroxide, extruding the                paste through one or more nozzles to form fibers                and depositing the extruded fibers to form a                three-dimensional porous catalyst monolith                precursor, drying the precursor to remove the                liquid diluent, and calcining the precursor. The                catalyst (A) comprises 30 mol% nickel, 8-38 mol%,                preferably 23-35 mol% magnesium, and 50-70 mol%                aluminum, the suspension comprises a binder                material. All the particles in the suspension have                an average particle size of 0.5-500 mm.
   USE - Production of catalyst monolith used for                reforming of hydrocarbons e.g. methane in presence                of carbon dioxide, in water-gas shift reactions and                reverse water-gas shift reactions (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides catalyst monolith having                high strength combined with high surface area or                porosity, and high efficiency for the reaction for                methane and carbon dioxide. The catalyst monolith                is lightweight and has high crushing strength, low                density, high activity and selectivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Production of three-dimensional porous                catalyst monolith involves preparing a suspension                paste in a liquid diluent of particles of a                catalyst (A) comprising at least nickel-magnesium                mixed oxide having an average crystallite size of                less than or equal to 100 nm, preferably less than                or equal to 40 nm and magnesium spinel having an                average crystallite size of less than or equal to                100 nm, preferably less than or equal to 40 nm and                optionally aluminum oxy hydroxide, extruding the                paste through one or more nozzles to form fibers                and depositing the extruded fibers to form a                three-dimensional porous catalyst monolith                precursor, drying the precursor to remove the                liquid diluent, and calcining the precursor. The                catalyst (A) comprises 30 mol% nickel, 8-38 mol%,                preferably 23-35 mol% magnesium, and 50-70 mol%                aluminum, and the intensity of the diffraction                reflection of the catalyst at 2( theta ) of 43.09                degrees is less than or equal to the intensity of                the diffraction reflection at 2( theta ) of 44.82                degrees . The intensity of the diffraction                reflection at 2( theta ) of 43.08 degrees is                preferably less than the intensity of the                reflection at 2( theta ) of 44.72 degrees or of a                hexaaluminate-comprising catalyst (B) comprising                cobalt and at least one metal from barium,                strontium, lanthanum, or of precursors of catalyst                (A) or (B). The suspension comprises a binder                material, and all the particles in the suspension                have an average particle size of 0.5-500 mm.                INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) three-dimensional porous catalyst monolith                of stacked catalyst fibers;
   (2) control system data set, which comprises                several control instructions which when loaded on                an additive production facility prompts the                additive production facility to produce                three-dimensional porous catalyst monolith or                three-dimensional porous catalyst monolith                precursor;
   (3) use of the three-dimensional porous                catalyst monolith of stacked catalyst fiber for                reforming of hydrocarbons, preferably methane in                the presence of carbon dioxide for water-gas-shift                reactions and reverse water-gas-shift reactions;                and
   (4) reformation of hydrocarbon-comprising                compounds, which involves using catalyst at                500-1100 degrees C, preferably 700-980 degrees C,                and pressure of 2-70 bar, preferably 20-40 bar, and                preferably using a feed fluid comprising 20-45                vol.% methane and 20-45 vol.% carbon dioxide, 0-40                vol.% water vapor, and optionally 0-40 vol.%                hydrogen.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: The catalyst (A) has                  surface area of 2-500 m2/g, preferably 5-76 m2/g.                  The nozzles has maximum diameter of less than 5                  mm, preferably less than 1 mm. The monolith has                  porosity of at least 20%, and crushing strength                  of at least 60 N. Preferred Component: The                  starting materials for producing catalyst                  comprise a nickel-comprising salt and a                  hydrotalcite-comprising compound, preferably                  nickel nitrate hexahydrate and hydrotalcite.                  Preferred Process: The method for producing                  catalyst (A) involves (i) contacting fusible                  metal salt and finely divided                  hydrotalcite-comprising starting material, (ii)                  mixing fusible metal salt and finely divided                  hydrotalcite-comprising starting material, (iii)                  thermally treating the mixture to melt the metal                  salt at 30-250 degrees C, preferably at 50-140                  degrees C, (iv) calcining the mixture at                  low-temperature of less than 500 degrees C,                  preferably 250-500 degrees C for 0.1-24 hours,                  (v) molding or shaping, and (vi) calcining at                  high-temperature of more than 500 degrees C,                  preferably 700-1200 degrees C for 0.1-24 hours.                  The method for producing catalyst (B) involves                  (i) producing an aluminum source, preferably a                  finely divided aluminum oxide and/or hydroxide                  modification, (ii) contacting finely divided                  aluminum source with a fusible or soluble                  cobalt-comprising compound and further at least                  one soluble or fusible metal salt chosen from                  barium, strontium, and lanthanum, (iii) mixing                  aluminum source and the dissolved or molten metal                  salts, (iv) drying mixture, (v) calcining the                  mixture at low temperature of less than 550                  degrees C, preferably 250-550 degrees C for                  0.1-24 hours, (vi) molding or shaping, (vii)                  calcining at high-temperature of 800-1300 degrees                  C, preferably 900-1100 degrees C for more than                  half an hour, preferably more than 10 hours. The                  finely divided aluminum oxide source comprising                  boehmite is contacted in the form of dispersible                  primary particles having particle size of less                  than or equal to 500 nm, with a metal salt                  comprising at least soluble or fusible cobalt and                  lanthanum-comprising species. The steps (i)-(iii)                  are carried out in the presence of 0.1-10 wt.%                  seed crystals. The monolith of stacked catalyst                  fibers is three-dimensionally structured by                  depositing the extruded fibers in a regular,                  recurring stacking pattern comprising stacked                  layers of at least 50 wt.% extruded fiber                  deposited parallel and spatially separated from                  each other or in a cobweb pattern, preferably                  deposited as parallel linear strands and                  spatially separated from each other or in                  multiple cob web patterns which the direction of                  the strands in each layer is different from the                  direction in neighboring layers, and obtaining                  porous structure with contact points of strands                  of neighboring layers. Preferred Components: The                  fusible metal salt comprises nickel salt and/or                  cobalt salt, preferably nickel nitrate                  hexahydrate. The binder material is chosen from                  organic or inorganic binders, preferably clays,                  alumina, and silica. Preferred Composition: The                  catalyst (B) comprises 2-15 mol%, preferably 4-8                  mol% cobalt, 2-25 mol%, preferably 4-10 mol%                  barium, strontium, and lanthanum (in total),                  70-90 mol% and 0-50 wt.%, preferably 5-30 wt.%                  oxidic secondary phase preferably chosen from (                  theta )-aluminum oxide, ( alpha )-aluminum oxide,                  lanthanum aluminate, barium aluminate, strontium                  aluminate, cobalt aluminate, lanthanum-stabilized                  aluminum oxides, lanthanum stabilized aluminum                  oxide hydroxide, and has surface area of more                  than 2 m2/g, preferably more than 15 m2/g.                  Preferred Condition: The mixture is dried at                  (-100)-1000 degrees C.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); A32 (Polymer fabrication - such as moulding, extrusion, forming, laminating, spinning.);
