PT P
PN CN109778215-A; CN109778215-B
TI Ternary structure electrode useful for            photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide comprises            FTO conductive glass as a substrate, a            photoelectrocatalytic reaction layer, zinc porphyrin            used as a light absorbing agent, and porphyrin            iron.
AU GOU F
   JIANG X
   QI C
AE UNIV SHAOXING (UNSX-C)
GA 201949180L
AB    NOVELTY - Ternary structure electrode comprises FTO                conductive glass as a substrate, a                photoelectrocatalytic reaction layer deposited on                the surface of the substrate, zinc porphyrin used                as a light absorbing agent, and porphyrin iron or                copper porphyrin used as a catalytic reducing                agent, where the light absorbing agent and the                catalytic reducing agent are adsorbed on the                titanium dioxide. The surface includes a                photoelectrocatalytic reaction layer and a ternary                structure electrode.
   USE - The ternary structure electrode is useful for                photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide                (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) preparing a ternary structure electrode,                comprising (i) preparing photocatalytic reaction                layer mesoporous titanium dioxide structure, (ii)                dispersing the porphyrin zinc light absorber in an                organic solvent of ethanol, acetonitrile,                tetrahydrofuran and/or toluene, and preparing into                0.1-0.5 mmol/l bath dyeing solution A, dispersing                the porphyrin iron or the copper porphyrin copper                catalytic reducing agent in an organic solvent of                ethanol, acetonitrile, tetrahydrofuran and/or                toluene, and preparing into 0.1-0.5 mmol/l bath dye                solution B, dispersing porphyrin zinc light                absorber and the porphyrin iron or porphyrin cobalt                catalytic reducing agent in ethanol, acetonitrile,                tetrahydrofuran and/or toluene mixed organic                solvents, and preparing into mixed bath dye                solution C of 0.1-0.5 mmol/l, where the ratio of                light absorber to catalytic reducing agent is                1:10-10:1, (iii) placing the titanium dioxide                electrode in the prepared pre-dye solution A,                soaking at 25-50 degrees C for 2-24 hours, taking                out the titanium dioxide electrode, washing with                acetonitrile, blowing out unabsorbed metal                porphyrin molecule, drying by cold air in a                nitrogen atmosphere, placing the blow dried                titanium dioxide electrode in the pre-dye solution                B, and immersing at 25-50 degrees C for 2-24 hours,                taking out the two structures of titanium dioxide                electrode that have been adsorbed, washing with                acetonitrile many times, washing away the                unadsorbed metal porphyrin molecules, and blow                drying with cold air under nitrogen atmosphere to                obtain a type I ternary structure electrode,                placing the prepared titanium dioxide electrode in                the pre-dye solution B, soaking at 25-50 degrees C                for 2-24 hours, after immersion, removing the                titanium dioxide electrode, washing with                acetonitrile many times, washing away the                unadsorbed metal porphyrin molecules, and blow                drying under cold air, placing the blow dried                titanium dioxide electrode in the pre-dye solution                A, soaking at 25-50 degrees C for 2-24 hours,                taking out the titanium dioxide electrode which has                been adsorbed, washing and blow drying to obtain a                type II ternary structure electrode, placing the                prepared titanium dioxide electrode in the pre-dye                solution C, soaking at 25-50 degrees C for 2-24                hours, taking out, washing and blow drying to                obtain a type III ternary structure electrode;                and
   (2) use of ternary structure electrode for                photocatalytic reduction of carbon dioxide,                comprising using the electrode as a working                electrode, and a cathode, using a platinum                electrode and a graphite electrode as auxiliary                electrodes, and an anode, using saturated calomel                electrode or a silver chloride silver electrode as                a reference electrode, in the aqueous electrolyte                system, maintaining the electrolyte solution in the                electrolytic cell at 0.05-0.2 mole per liter of the                aqueous solution of                1-aminopropyl-3methyl-imidazolium bromide or 0.1                mole per liter of potassium bicarbonate solution,                the volume is 50-100 ml, the light source is the                simulated sunlight or visible light ( lambda more                than 400 nm) provided by the xenon lamp source, the                light intensity is 100-200 m W/cm2, and prior to                the experiment, the electrolyte solution was                saturated with carbon dioxide, and the applied bias                was -0.5 V to -1.1 V.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The porphyrin zinc                  accounts for 10-80% of the porphyrin adsorbed                  titanium dioxide on the surface; and the                  porphyrin iron or porphyrin copper accounts for                  20-90% of porphyrin adsorbed titanium dioxide on                  the surface. The zinc porphyrin is zinc                  5-(4-carboxyphenylethynyl)-15-hydro-10,20-diphenylporphyrin                  or zinc tetrakis(4-carboxyphenyl)porphyrin; the                  iron porphyrin is iron tetrakis(4-carboxyphenyl)                  porphyrin, iron tetrakis(4-hydroxyphenyl)                  porphyrin or iron tetrakis(4-pyridyl) porphyrin;                  and the copper porphyrin is copper                  tetrakis(4-carboxyphenyl) porphyrin, copper                  tetrakis(4-hydroxyphenyl) porphyrin or copper                  tetrakis(4-pyridyl) porphyrin.

PT P
PN CN109108050-A; CN109108050-B
TI Conversion of sodium-and-fluorine-containing compound in aluminum electrolytic heavy slag involves adding cyanide conversion agent to mixture of carbon material and fluoride raw material, oxidizing, absorbing resulting gas, and drying.
AU LIU E
   LV S
AE UNIV XIANGTAN (UNXT-C)
GA 201904033A
AB    NOVELTY - Conversion of sodium-and-fluorine-containing compound in aluminum electrolytic heavy slag involves calcining the mixed powder material of fluorine-containing mineral and carbon in a high-temperature furnace under air atmosphere at 700-1200 degrees C for 0.5-5 hours, oxidizing the carbon component, burning to obtain fluorine-containing ore, concentrating or crystallizing the dilute solution of the soluble mixture to obtain a concentrated solution or a solid phase mixture, adding 0.1-10 %mass cyanide conversion agent to the mixture (A), high quality carbon material and a fluoride mineral raw material, or concentrated solution or a solid phase mixture, oxidizing the cyanide-containing compound into harmless nitrogen gas, ammonia and carbon dioxide, analyzing cyanide ion content, absorbing the resulting harmless ammonia, carbon dioxide and water vapor, drying, and collecting the reducing gas or directly oxidizing and burning.
   USE - Conversion of sodium-and-fluorine-containing compound in aluminum electrolytic heavy slag.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is environmentally-friendly, and enables simple, efficient and economical conversion of sodium-and-fluorine-containing compound in aluminum electrolytic heavy slag with high productivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Conversion of sodium-and-fluorine-containing compound in aluminum electrolytic heavy slag involves crushing the aluminum electrolytic heavy slag, grinding, sieving, homogenizing to obtain heavy slag powder with particle 200 mu m or less, analyzing and determining the molar amount or quality of sodium and fluorine in the heavy slag powder, analyzing and determining cyanide ions in the heavy slag powder, adding a grinding component to a conversion mill, subjecting the conversion mill to air evacuation or nitrogen replacement, adding the heavy slag powder to the mill, mixing the heavy slag powder with the grinding component in mass ratio of 1:(0.2-10), adding water to the conversion mill, adding 5-80 %mass conversion agent and 0-1 %mass grinding aid to the mill, rotating the conversion mill at rate of 10-1000 rpm, stirring the mixture at 10-110 degrees C, sampling the slurry, analyzing sodium ion content and soluble fluoride ion content, transferring the slurry into a stirring reactor, aging at 60-150 degrees C for 0.5-5 hours, filtering or centrifuging, washing with water to obtain a mixture (A) of a wet fluorine-containing mineral and carbon, which is, an aqueous solid phase and a dilute solution of a soluble mixture, drying or heat-treating the aqueous solid phase at 80-300 degrees C for 1-10 hours, pulverizing to obtain a mixed powder material of a fluorine-containing mineral and carbon, having average particle size of 20 mu m or more, separating and purifying to obtain a high quality carbon material and a fluoride mineral raw material, calcining the mixed powder material of fluorine-containing mineral and carbon in a high-temperature furnace under air atmosphere at 700-1200 degrees C for 0.5-5 hours, oxidizing the carbon component, burning to obtain fluorine-containing ore, concentrating or crystallizing the dilute solution of the soluble mixture to obtain a concentrated solution or a solid phase mixture, adding 0.1-10 %mass cyanide conversion agent to the mixture (A), high quality carbon material and a fluoride mineral raw material, or concentrated solution or a solid phase mixture, oxidizing the cyanide-containing compound into harmless nitrogen gas, ammonia and carbon dioxide, analyzing cyanide ion content, absorbing the resulting harmless ammonia, carbon dioxide and water vapor, drying, and collecting the reducing gas or directly oxidizing and burning. The solid-liquid mass ratio of the heavy slag powder and water is 1:(1-10). An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a sodium-and-fluorine-containing compound conversion system, which consists of a large repair slag crushing device, a milling device, a conversion mill, a stirring reactor, a solid-liquid separation device directly connected to a concentration or crystallization equipment, and a drying or heat-treatment equipment connected to a comminution equipment.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The main element of the slag powder comprises carbon, sodium, fluorine, aluminum, calcium, oxygen, nitrogen, lithium, magnesium, silicon, potassium, and/or iron. The sodium-containing compound includes sodium fluoride, trisodium hexafluoroaluminate, sodium cyanide, and/or sodium ferrocyanide. The fluorine-containing compound includes sodium fluoride, trisodium hexafluoroaluminate, aluminum fluoride, lithium fluoride, calcium fluoride, magnesium fluoride, and potassium fluoride. The conversion agent containing a sodium compound and a fluorine-containing compound includes an oxide, a hydroxide, a sulfate, a nitrate, a carbonate, an acetate, and/or a citrate of calcium, aluminum, magnesium, strontium, barium, lanthanum, and cerium. The cyanide conversion agent includes sodium percarbonate, potassium percarbonate, sodium peroxide, potassium peroxide, calcium oxide, barium oxide, ammonium persulfate, sodium persulfate, potassium persulfate, potassium manganate, oxygen, or ozone. The grinding aid contains a polymeric polyol, a polymeric alcohol amine, triisopropanolamine, ethylene glycol, propylene glycol, glycerol, diethylene glycol, triethanolamine, amide, stearic acid, oleic acid, sodium hexametaphosphate, and/or sodium stearate.
DC E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.); E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.); P43 (Sorting, cleaning, waste disposal (B06, B07, B08, B09).)
MC E05-B03; E05-P; E10-B03B2; E10-C02A; E10-C04H; E10-C04J2U; E10-C04L2; E10-D03D; E10-E04H; E10-E04J4; E11-W; E31-B03A; E31-D02; E31-D03; E31-E; E31-H03; E31-K06; E31-N04D; E31-P06A; E32-B; E33-B; E33-G; E34-B; E34-C02; E34-C03; E34-D; E34-E01; E34-E02A; E35-S; P43-E05

An inner part of the kettle body                cavity is matched with a driving motor (204). An                outer side of the driving motor is connected with a                connecting rod (205). A top end of the connecting                rod is connected with a driving axis (206). An                outer side of the driving axis is connected with a                mounting rod (207). An inner part of the mounting                rod is connected with a buffer spring (208). An                outer side of the buffer spring is connected with a                stirrer (209). An outer side of a driving gear                (203) is connected with a stirring screw propeller                (210). A bottom end of the kettle body cavity is                provided with a discharging port (3).
   USE - Device for accelerating dissolution of solvent                and supercritical carbon dioxide fluid for                extracting and purifying products e.g. biological                product, food and medicine.
   ADVANTAGE - The accelerating solvent and supercritical                carbon dioxide fluid dissolving device provides an                inert environment, avoids generating oxidation,                does not affect the effective component of the                extract, fast extracting speed, non-toxic, and                non-flammable, and does not pollute the environment                and has no solvent residue, nitrate and heavy metal                ion. The pressure protection control system is                installed on the lower part of the protective inner                wall, to protect it.

TI Composite plant volatile oil used in tobacco shred            of tobacco product, comprises plant volatile oils,            where first plant volatile oil is prepared by            subjecting Ophiopogon root, dried lily, thunberg            fritillary bulb and Polygonatum odoratum to            supercritical carbon dioxide extraction.
AU CHEN Y
   LI H
   CHEN M
   FU Q
   LI F
   LIU Y
   JIN Z
   PIAO Y
   CUI C
   TONG Y
   LI B
AE JILIN TOBACCO IND CO LTD (CNTA-C)
GA 2022C2284Q
AB    NOVELTY - Composite plant volatile oil comprises first                plant volatile oil and second plant volatile oil,                where first plant volatile oil is prepared by                subjecting 15-25 pts. wt. Ophiopogonroot, 30-40                pts. wt. dried lily, 25-35 pts. wt. thunberg                fritillary bulb and 5-15 pts. wt. Polygonatum                odoratumto supercritical carbon dioxide extraction,                and second plant volatile oil is prepared by                subjecting 35-50 pts. wt. Momordica grosvenori,                20-30 pts. wt. radix Scutellariae, 5-10 pts. wt.                fructus cnidii and 15-25 pts. wt. semen lepidii to                supercritical carbon dioxide extraction.
   USE - Composite plant volatile oil is used in                tobacco shred of a tobacco product (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The prepared composite plant volatile oil                through the synergistic cooperation of the                components has faint scent and strong fragrance,                good ductility, comfortable mouthfeel and no                residue. The functional spice as the water tobacco                is added into the water tobacco material, is well                coordinated with smoke, endows the water tobacco                with unique faint scent and charm, and creates                cigarette products with unique styles.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) a method for preparing the composite plant                volatile oil, which involves:
   (a) lyophilizing Ophiopogonroot, dried lily,                thunberg fritillary bulb and Polygonatum odoratum,                mixing, and pulverizing to obtain first plant                powder, and lyophilizing Momordica grosvenori,                radix Scutellariae, fructus cnidii and semen                lepidii, mixing, and pulverizing to obtain second                plant powder;
   (b) subjecting the first plant powder and the                second plant powder to supercritical carbon dioxide                extraction to obtain first plant volatile oil and                second plant volatile oil; and
   (c) mixing the first plant volatile oil and                the second plant volatile oil;
   (2) a tobacco shred, which is sprayed with                composite plant volatile oil or the composite plant                volatile oil prepared by the preparation method;                and
   (3) a tobacco product, which comprises tobacco                shred.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The mass ratio of the                  first plant volatile oil to the second plant                  volatile oil is 1-3:1-3. The lyophilization is                  carried out to a water content of 10% or less.                  The crushed mesh number is 20-80 meshes. The                  parameters of supercritical carbon dioxide                  extraction of the first plant powder comprises                  temperature of the extraction kettle of                  40-55&#8451;, extraction pressure is 25-40                  megapascal (MPa), temperature of one separation                  kettle is 45-55&#8451;, pressure is 8-12 MPa,                  and carbon dioxide flow rate is 20-30 L/hour. The                  parameters of supercritical carbon dioxide                  extraction of the second plant powder comprises                  temperature of the extraction kettle of                  35-45&#8451;, extraction pressure is 15-25                  megapascal (MPa), temperature of one separation                  kettle is 40-50&#8451;, pressure is 8-12 MPa,                  and carbon dioxide flow rate is 20-30 L/hour. The                  amount of the composite plant volatile oil added                  is 0.05-0.1 wt.% by mass of the tobacco                  product.Preferred Process: The first plant volatile                  oil is prepared by subjecting 17-23 pts. wt.                  Ophiopogonroot, 32-38 pts. wt. dried lily, 27-33                  pts. wt. thunberg fritillary bulb and 7-13 pts.                  wt. Polygonatum odoratum to supercritical carbon                  dioxide extraction, and second plant volatile oil                  is prepared by subjecting 37-48 pts. wt.                  Momordica grosvenori, 22-28 pts. wt. radix                  Scutellariae, 6-9 pts. wt. fructus cnidii and                  17-23 pts. wt. semen lepidii to supercritical                  carbon dioxide extraction.

PN WO2022092176-A1; TW202222755-A
TI Manufacture of bisphenol used for manufacturing            e.g. regenerated polycarbonate resin, involves            decomposing polycarbonate resin in presence of aromatic            monoalcohol, water and catalyst.
AU UCHIYAMA K
   NAKAMURA M
AE MITSUBISHI CHEM CORP (MITU-C)
GA 202258842E
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture (p1) of bisphenol involves (i)                decomposing a polycarbonate resin in the presence                of aromatic monoalcohol, water and a                catalyst.
   USE - Manufacture of bisphenol e.g.                2,2-bis(4-hydroxyphenyl)propane used for                manufacturing regenerated polycarbonate resin,                epoxy resin and cured epoxy resin. Carbon dioxide                produced as by-product during manufacture of                bisphenol used for manufacturing carbonic acid                diester (all claimed). Uses include but are not                limited to electrodeposition paint for automobile,                heavy-duty anticorrosion paint for ship and bridge,                paint for inner surface coating of beverage can,                laminated board, semiconductor encapsulant,                insulating powder paint, seismic reinforcement of                bridge, concrete reinforcement, flooring of                building, lining of water supply facility,                drainage/water permeable pavement, civil                engineering/construction application, adhesives for                vehicle/aircraft, etc.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables easy and efficient                manufacture of bisphenol under mild and                environmentally-friendly conditions, with small                environmental load. Neutralized wastewater                discharged during manufacture of diphenyl                carbonate, hydrochloric acid wastewater discharged                during recovery of by-produced hydrogen chloride,                and sodium hydroxide wastewater discharged in                detoxification treatment of unliquefied gas                generated during production of carbonyl chloride                can be effectively used. The cured epoxy resin                formed using the bisphenol has improved heat                resistance, crack resistance and low linear                expansion coefficient.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) manufacture (p2) of regenerated                polycarbonate resin, which involves using a                bisphenol raw material containing bisphenol                obtained in the process (p1);
   (2) manufacture (p3) of carbon dioxide, which                involves recovering carbon dioxide produced in the                process (p1);
   (3) manufacture (p4) of carbonic acid diester,                which involves using carbon dioxide;
   (4) manufacture (p5) of epoxy resin, which                involves using the bisphenol; and
   (5) manufacture (p6) of cured epoxy resin,                which involves curing an epoxy resin composition                containing the epoxy resin and a curing                agent.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view explaining                the manufacture of bisphenol. (Drawing includes                non-English language text)
   Dehydrochlorination tank (11)
   Mixing tank (12)
   Neutralization tank (13)
   Water washing tank (14)
   Distillation tanks (15,16)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The alkali metal                  hydroxide is sodium hydroxide or potassium                  hydroxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The catalyst is                  chosen from alkali metal hydroxide, alkali metal                  carbonate, alkylamine, nitrogen-containing                  heterocyclic compound and acid. The alkylamine is                  of formula (I), preferably tertiary amine. The                  acid is chosen from hydrochloric acid, sulfuric                  acid, phosphoric acid and sulfonic acid. The                  nitrogen-containing heterocyclic compound is                  pyridine. The mass ratio of water with respect to                  aromatic monoalcohol is 0.001-10. The aromatic                  monoalcohol is any one chosen from phenol, cresol                  and xylenol.R'a = 1-3C alkyl;andR'b,R'c = H or 1-3C alkyl.Preferred Process: The process (i) involves                  decomposing polycarbonate resin in the presence                  of aromatic monoalcohol, water, catalyst                  comprising nitrogen-containing heterocyclic                  compound and alkali metal chloride, preferably                  sodium chloride at a reaction temperature of 110                  degrees C or less, or decomposing polycarbonate                  resin in the presence of aromatic monoalcohol,                  water, catalyst comprising hydrochloric acid and                  bromophenol, or decomposing polycarbonate resin                  in the presence of aromatic monoalcohol, water,                  catalyst comprising sodium hydroxide and sodium                  chloride and/or carbon tetrachloride. The                  polycarbonate resin is decomposed in a                  slurry-like reaction liquid containing the                  polycarbonate resin, aromatic monoalcohol, water                  and catalyst. The process (p1) involves reacting                  carbonyl chloride with aromatic monoalcohol in                  the presence of nitrogen-containing heterocyclic                  compound to obtain a reaction solution containing                  diaryl carbonate, (i-b1) neutralizing the                  reaction solution with an aqueous alkali metal                  hydroxide solution, preferably aqueous sodium                  hydroxide solution, separating into an oil phase                  containing an aromatic diaryl and an aqueous                  phase containing nitrogen-containing heterocyclic                  compound and alkali metal chloride, preferably                  sodium chloride and removing the aqueous phase as                  neutralized wastewater, (i-b2) washing the oil                  phase with water and (i-b3) recovering diaryl                  carbonate from the oil phase, or reacting                  carbonyl chloride with aromatic monoalcohol in                  the presence of nitrogen-containing heterocyclic                  compound to obtain a reaction solution containing                  diaryl carbonate, (i-c1) supplying hydrogen                  chloride produced as the by-product to an                  absorption tower and absorbing using water or                  dilute hydrochloric acid to obtain concentrated                  hydrochloric acid, (i-c2) distilling concentrated                  hydrochloric acid in a dissipating tower,                  recovering hydrogen chloride gas from the top of                  the tower and recovering hydrochloric acid from                  the bottom of the tower and (ic-3) removing a                  portion of the hydrochloric acid recovered from                  the bottom of the tower as hydrochloric acid                  wastewater to the outside of the system and                  circulating the remaining hydrochloric acid to                  the absorption tower of the process (i-c1), or                  (i-d1) generating carbonyl chloride gas from                  chlorine and carbon monoxide, (i-d2) cooling the                  carbonyl chloride gas to obtain a liquefied                  carbonyl chloride, (i-d3) contacting the                  circulating sodium hydroxide aqueous solution                  with unliquefied gas that was not liquefied in                  the process (i-d2) to decompose the carbonyl                  chloride in the unliquefied gas and discharging                  the carbonyl chloride and (i-d4) removing a                  portion of the circulating sodium hydroxide                  aqueous solution as sodium hydroxide wastewater.                  The process (p4) involves reacting carbon dioxide                  with an aliphatic monoalcohol, or generating                  carbon monoxide using carbon dioxide and coke,                  reacting carbon monoxide with chlorine to obtain                  carbonyl chloride, and reacting carbonyl chloride                  with aromatic monoalcohol.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Process: The process (p2) involves                  using a carbonic acid diester raw material                  containing carbonic acid diester. The process                  (p5) involves reacting epoxy resin with a                  polyvalent hydroxy compound raw material.
   EXAMPLE - In a jacket-type separable flask, 80 g Novarex                M7027BF (RTM: polycarbonate resin), 30 g water, 250                g phenol and 320 g 25 %mass sodium hydroxide                aqueous solution were mixed and stirred at room                temperature to obtain a reaction solution. The                internal temperature was increased to 80 degrees C,                and the reaction was performed for 5 hours while                maintaining at 80 degrees C to obtain a uniform                solution. After adding 200 g toluene to the                obtained uniform solution, 35 %mass hydrochloric                acid was added until the pH of the aqueous phase                became 8.6, and carbon dioxide gas was generated                Stirring was stopped, oil and water were separated,                and the aqueous phase was withdrawn from the flask                to obtain an organic phase (a1). The obtained                organic phase (a1) was transferred to a                distillation tank, and the internal temperature was                gradually increased to 180 degrees C while                observing the amount of distillation, and the                internal pressure was gradually adjusted from                normal pressure to 100 hPa to distill off water,                toluene and phenol. Inside of the flask was                re-pressurized with nitrogen, the internal                temperature was lowered to 80 degrees C, and 200 g                toluene was added to obtain an organic phase (a2).                The obtained organic phase (a2) was washed 5 times                with 50 g desalinated water to obtain an organic                phase (a3). The temperature of the obtained organic                phase (a3) was lowered to 20 degrees C to obtain a                slurry. The obtained slurry was filtered to obtain                a cake. The obtained cake was dried on a rotary                evaporator to obtain 35 g bisphenol A having a                purity of 99.8 %mass. 46 g Bisphenol A, 259 g                epichlorohydrin, 100 g isopropanol, 36 g water,                86.5 g 48.5 %mass sodium hydroxide aqueous solution                and 159 g methyl isobutyl ketone were mixed and                stirred to obtain an epoxy resin having an epoxy                equivalent of 175 g/equivalent measured according                to JISK7236 (2009). 100 Pts. wt. epoxy resin, 94                pts. wt. Rikacid MH-700 (RTM:                4-methylhexahydrophthalic anhydride) and 1 pt. wt.                Curezol 2E4MZ (RTM: imidazole-type curing agent)                were mixed and stirred to obtain an epoxy resin                composition. The cured product formed using the                epoxy resin composition had a linear expansion                coefficient of 61 ppm/ degrees C.

TI Preparing zirconium dioxide modified layered porous vermiculite loaded nickel-based catalyst useful for catalytic synthesis of carbon monoxide and hydrogen using carbon dioxide and methane as raw materials, comprises e.g. adding mixed solution to powder carrier, heating dried powder, and calcining.
AU GUO D
   LI M
   WANG Y
   LV J
   ZHAO Y
   WANG S
   MA X
AE UNIV TIANJIN (UTIJ-C)
GA 202251848B
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing zirconium dioxide modified layered porous vermiculite loaded nickel-based catalyst comprises (1) soaking expanded vermiculite in hydrochloric acid solution under stirring conditions, refluxing for 4-6 hours at 60-90degrees Celsius under vigorous stirring, washing obtained bright yellow precipitate with deionized water, suction filtering, and drying to obtain acid-treated vermiculite powder carrier, (2) adding mixed solution dropwise to the acid-treated vermiculite powder carrier after vacuum drying, air-drying, and drying at 100-120degrees Celsius for 6-10 hours to obtain dry powder, where the mixed solution is aqueous solution containing 50-150 mg/ml nickel nitrate hexahydrate and 10-30 mg/ml zirconium nitrate pentahydrate, and adding 0.5-3 ml of mixed solution for each 1 g of acid-treated vermiculite carrier, and (3) heating dried powder to 500-600degrees Celsius in air atmosphere, and calcining for 3-5 hours to obtain nickel-based catalyst.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for catalytic synthesis of carbon monoxide and hydrogen using carbon dioxide and methane as raw materials (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple operation in the preparation process, low process energy consumption, excellent repeatability. The catalyst has small metal particles, uniform dispersion, strong adsorption and activation ability for reactants, nickel-zirconium interface effect; plays synergistic role; has high resistance to sintering and carbon deposition at high temperature; and has excellent prospect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for Use method of the catalyst, comprising mixing catalyst with quartz sand and placing into fixed bed reactor, introducing methane, carbon dioxide and nitrogen to obtain carbon monoxide and hydrogen under the reaction temperature of 700-800degrees Celsius and the space velocity of 90,000-100,000 ml/hg/cat, where the raw material ratio is methane, carbon dioxide and nitrogen is 1:0.9-1.1:1.9-2.1.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The method further comprises step (4), heating the prepared zirconium dioxide modified layered porous vermiculite-supported nickel-based catalyst to 450-550degrees Celsius in nitrogen atmosphere, and switching the gas to pure hydrogen atmosphere and reducing for 1-3 hours. The method adds 8-12 g of expanded vermiculite to every 200 ml of hydrochloric acid solution.Preferred Method: The loading amount of zirconium dioxide of the catalyst is 0.5-1.5 wt.%, the loading amount of nickel metal is 2.5-7.5 wt.%, and the nickel particle size is 5-7 nm. The hydrochloric acid concentration of the acidified porous vermiculite is preferably 3 mol/l. The water absorption per 1 g of the acid-treated vermiculite carrier before the impregnation is 2.8 ml, and the vacuum drying temperature is 80degrees Celsius. In the roasting process, the heating rate is 2degrees Celsius/minute, the roasting temperature is 550degrees Celsius, and the roasting time in air atmosphere is 4 hours. In the reduction process, the reduction temperature is 500degrees Celsius, the reduction time in pure hydrogen atmosphere is 2 hours, and the flow rate of the introduced hydrogen is 30 ml/minute. In the step (1), the number of revolutions of vigorous stirring is 700-900 revolutions per minute. In the step (2), the vacuum drying temperature is 60-90degrees Celsius, and the time is 3-5 hours. In the step (3), the heating rate is 1-3degrees Celsius/minute. The catalyst is prepared by equal volume impregnation method.

AB    NOVELTY - The device has a bubble dispersed phase                evolution module provided with a bubble dispersing                phase evolution chamber that is provided with                multiple bubble dispersal phase evolution areas.                Two ends of the bubble dispersion phase evolution                area are connected with a bubble generating body                and a bubble outlet pipe. An inner-phase                cooperative plugging visualization module comprises                a main observation chamber. One end of the main                observation chamber is connected with a main                chamber injection tube through a visible injection                channel, and one side of the visible injection                channel is provided with a visible reverse outflow                channel. The visible reverse outflow channel is                connected with a main chamber reverse outflow pipe.                The main observation chamber is connected with a                solid disperse phase evolution module and the                bubble disperse phase evolution module through the                main chamber injection tube and a collecting valve                respectively.

   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                method for simulating carbon dioxide water-based                three-phase foam inner phase cooperative plugging                interaction function.

TI Modeling catalytic cracking two-stage regeneration            system for use in electronic device, involves combining            energy conservation, calculating regenerator dilute            phase area axial temperature distribution.
AU YANG X
   ZHANG R
   WU L
   ZHANG Y
   YAN H
   MENG F
   WANG L
   SUN S
AE SINOPEC ENG GROUP CO LTD (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
GA 2021E65307
AB    NOVELTY - Modeling catalytic cracking two-stage                regeneration system involves generating carbon                monoxide and carbon dioxide in combustion reaction                process of carbon element, generating hydrogen                element during combustion reactor reaction of                hydrogen element in reaction process, then                generated carbon dioxide is continuously oxidized                to generate carbon dioxide; establishing function                of reaction rate of four reactions; establishing                steady state model of regenerator according to                burning reaction dynamics model and catalytic                regeneration system process flow; using normal                differential equation set to establish steady-state                model of regenerator to calculate content of                carbon, carbon dioxide, hydrogen oxide, oxygen and                nitrogen in regenerated flue gas and coke content                in regeneration catalyst; calculating temperature                of dilute phase zone of regenerator; combining                energy conservation to determine axial temperature                distribution of degreasing zone of                regenerator.
   USE - Method for modelling catalytic cracking                two-stage regeneration system used for lightening                heavy oil in modern oil refining industry; and used                in electronic device (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to model regenerator,                kinetic model of catalytic cracking regeneration                process provided, where composition of regeneration                flue gas is predicted and dilute phase temperature                in regenerator is predicted, which in turn helps                operator to optimize regeneration operation, and                reduces risk of occurrence of tail combustion in                regeneration, important significance is possessed                for safety and smooth operation of catalyst                cracking device, where energy conservation is                achieved by combining energy conservation                calculating regenerator dilute-phase area axial                temperature distribution.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) a model establishing device for modelling                method, which comprises model establishing module,                and processing module where model establishing                module establishing burning reaction dynamics                model, and regenerator steady state model;                processing module for performing regenerator model                parameter estimation regenerator taking heat                calculation, and regenerator dilute phase area                temperature calculation;
   (2) a electronic device, which comprises                processor, and memory where memory stored with                computer readable instruction when computer                readable instruction executed by processor of                modelling method.

PN US2021372617-A1; US11421878-B2
TI Method for using an ion transfer membrane            micromixer headend for power generation in a gas            turbine such as two-spool turbofan gas turbine,            involves exhausting pressurized air into a heat            exchange tube in a preheating unit.
AU HABIB M A
   ABDELHAFEZ A
   NEMITALLAH M A
AE UNIV DHAHRAN KING FAHD PETROLEUM & MINER (UYKF-C)
GA 2021D90914
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves exhausting pressurized air                into a heat exchange tube (130) in a preheating                unit (118). The combustion products are transported                to a gas power turbine (140). The electrical power                is generated by expanding the combustion products                into the turbine. The combustion products from the                turbine are exhausted. The exhausted combustion                products are condensed to separate water and carbon                dioxide by a condenser (142). The carbon dioxide is                split into two units by a splitter (193), and one                of the units is sequestered in a carbon dioxide                storage location. The shell surrounding a set of                ceramic ion transfer membrane (ITM) tubes is                equally spaced within a headend.
   USE - Method for using an ion transfer membrane                micromixer headend for power generation in a gas                turbine such as two-spool turbofan gas                turbine.
   ADVANTAGE - The method controls engine emissions while                generating stable flame by using a micromixer                stabilized burner. The method minimizes the                premixing length, so that two or more fuel holes                are evenly spaced around the perimeter of each tube                at the same axial location. The combustion unit is                configured to burn a mixture of fuel, carbon                dioxide and oxygen, where burning the mixture heats                the pressurized air in the air tubes, and the                combustor has a combustion outlet port, an igniter                and a thermocouple, and a headend is connected to                the combustion unit, where the headend includes a                second shell surrounding multiple ceramic ion                transfer membrane (ITM) tubes equally spaced within                the head end, and thus enables to provide clean                combustion in gas turbines in an efficient                manner.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation                of the integrated ITM micromixer burner system for                clean combustion in gas turbines.
   Preheating unit (118)
   Heat exchange tube (130)
   Gas power turbine (140)
   Condenser (142)
   Splitter (193)

PN CN110283254-A
TI Preparing cellulose propylene carbonate involves adding cellulose to dimethyl sulfoxide, and then adding 1,8-diazabicyclo5.4.0undec-7-ene, stirring to obtain fiber-containing solid-liquid mixture, raising temperature.
AU PAN B
   NA H
   ZI L
   ZHU J
   HUANG J
   LIU F
   CHEN X
   WANG X
AE SHANDONG JINZHENG XINKE AGRIC DEV CO LTD (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2019848754
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing cellulose propylene carbonate involves adding cellulose to dimethyl sulfoxide, and then adding 1,8-diazabicyclo(5.4.0)undec-7-ene, stirring to obtain fiber-containing solid-liquid mixture, raising temperature of the solid-liquid mixture obtained to 20-100 degrees C under microwave irradiation, introducing carbon dioxide gas continuously into solid-liquid mixture at pressure of 0.1-5 megapascal during heating, and reaction is carry out for 0.1-1 hour to obtain ionized cellulose solution, mixing bromopropene into ionized cellulose solution obtained, and continuously introducing carbon dioxide at pressure of 0.2-5 megapascal, and heating to 20-100 degrees C for 0.1-1.5 hoursunder microwave irradiation to obtain cellulose propylene carbonate solution. The cellulose propylene carbonate solution obtained is placed in deposition bath for 0.5-3 hours to obtain cellulose propylene carbonate gel.
   USE - Method for preparing cellulose propylene carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare cellulose propylene carbonate that has fast synthetic process, low energy consumption, improves processability and realizes performance control of cellulose propylene carbonate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing cellulose propylene carbonate involves adding cellulose to dimethyl sulfoxide, and then adding 1,8-diazabicyclo(5.4.0)undec-7-ene, stirring to obtain fiber-containing solid-liquid mixture, raising temperature of the solid-liquid mixture obtained to 20-100 degrees C under microwave irradiation, introducing carbon dioxide gas continuously into solid-liquid mixture at pressure of 0.1-5 megapascal during heating, and reaction is carry out for 0.1-1 hour to obtain ionized cellulose solution, mixing bromopropene into ionized cellulose solution obtained, and continuously introducing carbon dioxide at pressure of 0.2-5 megapascal, and heating to 20-100 degrees C for 0.1-1.5 hoursunder microwave irradiation to obtain cellulose propylene carbonate solution. The cellulose propylene carbonate solution obtained is placed in deposition bath for 0.5-3 hours to obtain cellulose propylene carbonate gel. The cellulose propylene carbonate gel obtained is dried in oven to obtain cellulose propylene carbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The deposition liquid is one or more of methanol, ethanol, ethylene glycol, glycerin, acetone, deionized water. Preferred Conditions: The ratio of cellulose to dimethyl sulfoxide of 1:10-100. The addition amount of 1,8-diazabicyclo(5.4.0)undec-7-ene is added in ratio of 1:1-10 to cellulose anhydroglucose unit. The amount of bromopropene added in ratio of 0.5-10:1 to ellulose anhydroglucose unit.

TI Carbon dioxide trans-critical heat across fluid circulating system for use in e.g. aerospace field, has heat return system whose heat side outlet is connected with inlet of high temperature heat source to form fluid circulating system loop.
AU WANG Y
   MA W
   ZHANG Z
   ZHU Q
   LIU X
AE UNIV XIAN JIAOTONG (UYXJ-C)
GA 201965984V
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a high temperature heat source (A) whose outlet is connected to a turbine inlet. An outlet of a steam turbine (B) is connected with a heat release inlet of a heat return system (C). A primary outlet of the heat return system is connected with an exothermic side inlet of a cooling system (D). A heating side outlet of the cooling system is connected with a compressor (E). An inlet and an outlet of the compressor are connected to a heating dual-stage inlet of the heat return system. A heating dual-stage outlet of the heat return system is connected with a heating side inlet of the cooling system. An outlet of a low temperature pump (F) is connected with a heat absorption side inlet of the heat return system. A heat side outlet of the heat return system is connected with an inlet of the high temperature heat source to form a carbon dioxide trans-critical heat across fluid circulating system loop.
   USE - Carbon dioxide trans-critical heat across fluid circulating system for use in aerospace and navigation field.
   ADVANTAGE - The system improves theoretical calculation cycle efficiency to reduce cost and carbon capture rate, operates a turbine inlet section at high pressure and operates residual equipment at low pressure to effectively reduce number of high-voltage equipment and construction cost and effectively improve heat across fluid circulating safety, utilizes liquefied natural gas and space environment as a low-temperature heat source in the cooling system so as to improve working performance and potential rate of the system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of a carbon dioxide trans-critical heat across fluid circulating system.
   High temperature heat source (A)
   Steam turbine (B)
   Heat return system (C)
   Cooling system (D)

   Low temperature pump (F)

PN WO2019115571-A1; DE102017011625-A1; EP3641865-A1; CN111465424-A; US2020353197-A1; EP3641865-B1; CN111465424-B
TI Method for detecting leak in patient gas module,            involves determining statistical similarity measure            between first and second time courses and detecting            leak based on measure of similarity.
AU PETER G
   SCHMID U
   OSTERLOH C
AE DRAEGERWERK & CO AG KGAA (DRAG-C)
   DRAEGERWERK & CO AG KGAA (DRAG-C)
   DRAEGER MFR GMBH & CO KG (DRAG-C)
   DRAEGERWERK & CO AG KGAA (DRAG-C)
   DRAGER MFR GMBH & CO KG (DRAG-C)
GA 2019518452
AB    NOVELTY - The method (10) involves determining (12) a                first time course of a carbon dioxide concentration                in a breathing gas mixture of the patient. A second                time course of a concentration of another gas other                than carbon dioxide in the breathing gas mixture is                determined (14). A statistical similarity measure                is determined (16) between the first and second                time courses. The leak is detected (18) based on                the measure of similarity. The other gas is oxygen,                nitrous oxide or a narcose gas. The similarity                measure corresponds to a phase shift between the                first and the second time course.
   USE - Method for detecting leak in patient gas                module that aspirates and analyzes continuous                sample gas flow from ventilated patient.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides reliable and robust leak                detection. The leaks can reliably be detected                without prior knowledge or without measured values                of preceding respiratory phases. The frequency of                this maneuver, which is associated with the failure                of the gas measurement function, is as small as                possible and is only carried out if there is a                reasonable suspicion of leakage.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a computer program comprising program code                for detecting leak in patient gas module;
   (2) a device for detecting leak in patient gas                module; and
   (3) a respiratory system for a patient.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram of a method                for detecting a leak in a ventilation system for                ventilating a patient. (Drawing includes                non-English language text)
   Method for detecting leak in patient gas                module (10)
   Step for determining a first time course of a                carbon dioxide concentration in a breathing gas                mixture of the patient (12)
   Step for determining second time course of a                concentration of another gas other than carbon                dioxide in the breathing gas mixture (14)
   Step for determining statistical similarity                measure between the first and second time courses                (16)
   Step for detecting leak based on the measure                of similarity (18)

   RIKEN KEIKI KK (RIKN-C)
GA 201923902U
AB    NOVELTY - Gas analysis method for computing concentration of each component gas of gas to be analyzed, which is a mixture gas that is predominantly composed of a methane gas and contains either hydrogen gas and/or carbon dioxide gas, comprises computing concentration of each component gas of gas to be analyzed, by a procedure (1) and a procedure (2), where the procedure (1) includes computing a concentration value Xcarbon dioxide (vol.%) of carbon dioxide gas contained in gas to be analyzed by following equation (1) of given specification, and procedure (2) includes computing a concentration of methane gas and concentration of hydrogen gas, which are contained in gas to be analyzed on a basis of value of particular gas physical property of gas to be analyzed that is computed on basis of refractive index equivalent output value and sonic speed equivalent output value, value of particular gas physical property of each component gas of gas to be analyzed, and volume fraction of each component gas.
   USE - The method is useful for computing a concentration of each component gas of a gas to be analyzed.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is: possible to continually perform, with high accuracy, the analysis of compositions of the gas to be analyzed (the measurement of the concentration of each component gas); and possible to perform gas analysis with high reliability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a gas analyzer for computing a concentration of each component gas of a gas to be analyzed, which is a mixture gas that is predominantly composed of a methane gas and contains either one or both of a hydrogen gas and a carbon dioxide gas, comprising a gas concentration computation mechanism configured to compute the concentration of each component gas of the gas to be analyzed by the gas analysis method (M1).

AU BHATIA A

   REAL M A

AE TECUMSEH PROD CO INC (TECV-C)

GA 2019116399
AB    NOVELTY - A refrigeration system (100) comprises compressor having a housing and electric motor hermetically sealed within the housing; condenser (102); expansion valve (110); evaporator (106); and quantity of working fluid containing electrically conductive ionic liquid and carbon dioxide where working fluid is circulatable through a series of fluid conduits interconnecting the compressor, condenser, evaporator and expansion valve.
   USE - A refrigeration system.
   ADVANTAGE - The refrigeration system, including compressor and cofluid, operates as low-pressure, carbon-dioxide (CO2)-based refrigeration system which realizes the benefits of CO2 as a working fluid without the costs associated with traditional high-pressure liquefied CO2 system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) compressor for use with electrically conductive working fluid comprising a housing having an inlet and outlet, and electric motor which is hermetically sealed within the housing and electrically isolated from interior of the housing; and
   (2) refrigeration system (100) comprising a compressor containing housing having an inlet and outlet, and forming a hermetically sealed chamber which does not admit fluid passage except through inlet and outlet; electric motor which is enclosed within hermetically sealed chamber of housing, has motor windings which are electrically isolated from hermetically sealed chamber; piston assembly which is enclosed within hermetically sealed chamber of housing and drivingly coupled to the motor, is operable to increase a pressure within hermetically sealed chamber from a suction pressure at inlet to discharge pressure at outlet; and greater than or equal to 1 electrical having an external connector portion outside the housing electrically coupled to internal connector portion within hermetically sealed chamber, internal connector portion electrically isolated from hermetically sealed chamber; condenser(102); expansion valve (110); evaporator (106); and quantity of working fluid containing electrically conductive ionic liquid and carbon dioxide and is circulatable through a series of fluid conduits interconnecting the compressor, condenser, evaporator and expansion valve.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the refrigeration system.
   Refrigeration system (100)
   Condenser (102)
   Evaporator (106)
   Expansion valve (110)
   Suction line heat exchanger (112)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Working fluid is accumulated in a sump region at bottom of housing. Quantity of working fluid includes a nonfunctional diluent operable to decrease the viscosity of ionic liquid without altering electrical conductivity of ionic liquid. Quantity of working fluid comprises electrically conductive ionic liquid received within the housing and in contact with the electric motor, carbon dioxide and nonfunctional diluent operable to decrease the viscosity of ionic liquid without altering the electrical conductivity of the ionic liquid. Preferred Conditions: The viscosity of working fluid containing nonfunctional diluent is 30-300 centistoke.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - MECHANICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Devices: Compressor raises a pressure of the quantity of working fluid to less than or equal to 50 bar. Refrigeration system further contains lubrication system having lubrication inlet within the housing of compressor and lubrication outlet in fluid communication with electric motor, such that lubrication system circulates the working fluid around moving parts of electric motor. Lubrication system comprises a suction tube having a lower end submerged in the quantity of working fluid in the sump region, and is operable to draw the ionic liquid of working fluid into contact with moving parts of the compressor. Compressor includes electrical terminal passing through the housing and electrically connected to the electric motor where electrical terminal is electrically isolated from working fluid at interior of the housing. Electric motor of compressor comprises a stator having electrically conductive windings engaged with a winding retainer; rotor received within the stator; and electrically insulative coating on the windings where coating is disposed between the windings and interior of the housing such that windings are electrically isolated from the working fluid. The system further includes a suction-line heat exchanger positioned to receive 1st flow of working fluid from condenser and 2nd flow of working fluid from evaporator, and to discharge 1st flow to expansion valve and 2nd flow to compressor, 1st flow being in heat exchange relationship with 2nd flow while in the suction-line heat exchanger. The system further includes a receiver functionally interposed between suction-line heat exchanger and compressor, and is operable to separate a gaseous flow of carbon dioxide from a liquid flow of ionic liquid, carbon dioxide (CO2) flow valve operably interposed between receiver and compressor and is operable to increase or decrease a volume of flow of carbon dioxide portion of working fluid; and liquid flow valve is operably interposed between receiver and compressor to increase or decrease a volume of flow of ionic liquid portion of working fluid and to measure a nominal flow rate of ionic liquid downstream of liquid flow valve.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Nonfunctional diluent is tetraethylene glycol dimethyl ether.

PN CN108947781-A
TI Preparing erianin, by extracting Dendrobium stem powder, eluting extract with D101 macroporous resin, dissolving content, purifying sample by high pressure silica gel column chromatography and concentrating and crystallizing concentrate.
AU YANG C
AE NANJING ZELANG BIOLOGICAL TECHNOLOGY CO (NJZL-C)
GA 2018A0531L
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing erianin, involves subjecting Dendrobium stem powder to liquid carbon dioxide supercritical extraction for 2-3 hours at the pressure of 5-10MPa and temperature of 60-70 degrees C to obtain extract, uniformly mixing extract and D101 macroporous resin in the chromatography column, then eluting content with 70-80 % ethanol at flow speed of 1-2 BV/hour, collecting and concentrating eluate to obtain crude product, dissolving crude product in methanol, purifying sample by high pressure silica gel column chromatography by acetonitrile-methanol solvent system gradient elution, collecting the erianin fraction, and concentrating erianin fraction under reduced pressure and crystallizing the concentrate with supercritical carbon dioxide antisolvent to obtain erianin crystals.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing erianin.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is suitable for industrial production and increases purity of erianin.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing erianin, involves (a) taking Dendrobium stem as raw material, cutting Dendrobium stem into sections, adding Dendrobium stem sections into high speed tissue masher, crushing dried stem sections at 12000 revolutions per minute (rpm) for 5-7 minutes and sieving crushed content through 60-mesh sieve, (b) subjecting content to liquid carbon dioxide supercritical extraction for 2-3 hours at the pressure of 5-10 MPa and temperature of 60-70 degrees C to obtain extract, (c) uniformly mixing extract and D101 macroporous resin in a weight ratio of 1:(1-3) in the chromatography column, then eluting content with 70-80 % ethanol at flow speed of 1-2 BV/hour, collecting and concentrating eluate to obtain crude product, (d) dissolving crude product in methanol, purifying sample by high pressure silica gel column chromatography by acetonitrile-methanol solvent system gradient elution, collecting the erianin fraction, and concentrating erianin fraction under reduced pressure and (e) crystallizing the concentrate with supercritical carbon dioxide antisolvent to obtain erianin crystals.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: In step (d), the high-pressure silica gel column chromatography has mesh size of 200 mesh sieve, a column inner diameter of 250 mm, and a column diameter ratio of 1:7. The volume ratio of the acetonitrile-methanol is 1:(1-3). In step (e), the supercritical CO2 antisolvent crystallization conditions is pressure 10-20 MPa, temperature 30 degrees C.

PN CN107647236-A
TI Preparing concentrated kudzu root juice, by crushing fresh kudzu roots, injecting carbon dioxide, liquefying, feeding ethylene, adding magnesium chloride and L-cysteine, clarifying, and concentrating mixture.
AU CHEN L
   TANG X
AE JIANGXI YEGE BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018116762
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing concentrated kudzu root juice, involves cleaning fresh kudzu roots, mixing with water, crushing, injecting carbon dioxide into the pulp, adding liquefying enzyme, liquefying, feeding ethylene, centrifuging, adding magnesium chloride and L-cysteine, adding special enzyme, clarifying the mixture, subjecting to membrane filtration using ceramic membrane or organic membrane, vacuum concentrating the mixture to obtain concentrated kudzu juice and natural kudzu flavor and adding the natural kudzu flavor to the concentrated kudzu juice.
   USE - The method is used for preparing concentrated kudzu root juice.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves total flavonoids by more than 87% and essential amino acids retention rate by more than 95%. The prepared concentrated kudzu root juice is rich in nutrients, and has light yellow color and good stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing concentrated kudzu root juice, involves cleaning fresh kudzu roots, mixing with 60-90 degrees C water in a ratio of 1:(1-5), crushing, injecting carbon dioxide into the pulp, after 5-10 minutes adding liquefying enzyme, liquefying the mixture at 96-99 degrees C and pH of 5.6-8 for 1.1-3 hours, stopping injection of carbon dioxide, feeding ethylene for 20-30 minutes, centrifuging the mixture at 1500-3000 revolutions per minute (rpm), adding magnesium chloride and L-cysteine, adding 30,000-60,000 U special enzyme for clarification, the enzyme for clarification is one or more of saccharifying enzyme, pectinase and protease, clarifying the mixture at 56-59 degrees C and a pH of 5.6-6.5 for 0.5-2.5 hours, subjecting to membrane filtration using filter membrane, preferably a ceramic membrane or an organic membrane having pore size of 50-200 nm, concentrating the filtrate using an organic membrane having pore size of 0.1-2 nm, vacuum concentrating the mixture at 20-40 degrees C and (-0.09) to (-0.07) MPa to obtain concentrated kudzu juice and natural kudzu flavor and adding the natural kudzu flavor to the concentrated kudzu juice.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Method: The carbon dioxide is injected at a flow rate of 200-300 ml/minute. The ethylene is fed at a flow rate of 100-150 ml/minute. The liquefying enzyme is 0.1-1 g 20,000-30,000 U/g high temperature alpha -amylase. The centrifugation is performed using three-foot type filter centrifuge or horizontal type screw filter centrifuge with inner surface of drum covered with the filter medium, the filter medium has filter cloth, filter or filter bag and the filter medium has pore size of 20-150 mu m. Amount of the magnesium chloride is 1-5 g/kg. Amount of the L-cysteine is 1.5-3.5 g/kg.

TI Supercritical carbon dioxide goji seed oil extraction system comprises extracting device e.g. valves, carbon dioxide bottle, refrigerator and horizontal pump, and separating device e.g. round bottom flask, condenser pipe and receiving tube.
AU WU K
   WANG C
AE CHENGDU TEXTILE COLLEGE (CHEN-Non-standard)
GA 2018803970
AB    NOVELTY - A supercritical carbon dioxide goji seed oil extraction system comprises separating device and extracting device. The extracting device (100) comprises bus bar, purifier, condenser, main pump, mixer, extracting kettle, first separation kettle, second separation kettle, multiple valves (111), carbon dioxide bottle (112), refrigerator (113) and horizontal pump (114). The bus bar is connected with second separation kettle. The carbon dioxide bottle is connected on bus bar. The first discharging port is provided on bus bar. The condenser and refrigerator are connected. The mixer is connected with horizontal pump. The main pump is provided with second discharging port. The third discharging port is provided on extraction kettle. The fourth discharging port is provided on first separation kettle. The valves are set to second discharging port, fourth discharging port, first discharging port, third discharging port, and fifth discharging port.
   USE - Supercritical carbon dioxide goji seed oil extraction system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system has simple operation. The method is environment-friendly and energy-saving, and has high safety and product yield and purity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A supercritical carbon dioxide goji seed oil extraction system comprises extracting device and separating device. The extracting device (100) comprises orderly connected multiple valves (111), carbon dioxide bottle (112), refrigerator (113), horizontal pump (114) and bus bar, purifier, condenser, main pump, mixer, extracting kettle, first separation kettle and second separation kettle. The bus bar and second separation kettle are connected. The bus bar is connected with carbon dioxide bottle. The bus bar is provided with first discharging port. The condenser is connected with refrigerator. The mixer and horizontal pump are connected. The main pump is provided with second discharging port. The extraction kettle is provided with third discharging port. The first separation kettle is provided with fourth discharging port. The second separation kettle is provided with fifth discharging port. The valves are set to first discharging port, second discharging port, third discharging port, fourth discharging port and fifth discharging port. The separating device comprises round bottom flask connected in turn by rubber plug, distillation head, condenser pipe, receiving tube, receiving bottle, and temperature meter disposed in distillation head. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for supercritical carbon dioxide goji seed oil extraction method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of supercritical carbon dioxide goji seed oil extraction system.

PN WO2018159594-A1; CN110337527-A; JP2019503009-X; JP6917647-B2
TI Recovery of methane gas in electric power generation, involves preheating methane hydrate stratum by injecting heated seawater in which carbon dioxide is dissolved, into injection well, and recovering product by pressure reduction method.
AU MARUYAMA S
   OKAJIMA J
   KOMIYA A
   CHEN L
AE UNIV TOHOKU (TOHO-C)
   UNIV TOHOKU NAT CORP (TOHO-C)
   UNIV TOHOKU (TOHO-C)
GA 2018704378
AB    NOVELTY - Recovery of methane gas from methane hydrate stratum (AA) involves preheating a methane hydrate stratum by injecting heated seawater in which carbon dioxide is dissolved and heated, into several injection wells communicating with the methane hydrate stratum, and recovering methane gas from the several production wells communicating with the methane hydrate stratum by pressure reduction method, which is provided at a position separated from the injection well by a set distance. When the set time period is elapsed, a new injection well is excavated, the heated seawater is injected into the injection well to preheat the methane hydrate stratum, and methane gas is recovered from the preheated methane hydrate stratum using the injection well as production well.
   USE - Recovery of methane gas used as fuel in low-carbon dioxide emission electric power generation (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient and environmentally-friendly recovery of methane gas with improved total output rate and production rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) low-carbon dioxide emission electric power generation method, which involves generating electric power using methane gas as a fuel, recovering carbon dioxide from an exhaust gas generated during electric power generation, dissolving the recovered carbon dioxide in the seawater, heating the seawater in which the carbon dioxide is dissolved using waste heat generated during electric power generation to generate heated seawater, preheating the methane hydrate stratum by injecting the heated seawater into several injection wells communicating with the methane hydrate stratum, recovering methane gas from the several production wells communicating with the methane hydrate stratum, and supplying the recovered methane gas as fuel for electric power generation;
   (2) methane gas recovery system (10), which comprises several injection tubes (11) for injecting the heated seawater into several injection wells, and several recovery tubes (12) for recovering methane gas; and
   (3) low-carbon dioxide emission electric power generation system (1), which comprises an electric power generation system (20) for generating electric power using methane gas as fuel, the methane gas recovery system, and a carbon dioxide recovery system for recovering carbon dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the low-carbon dioxide emission electric power generation system. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Low-carbon dioxide emission electric power generation system (1)
   Methane gas recovery system (10)
   Injection tubes (11)
   Recovery tubes (12)
   Electric power generation system (20)
   Methane hydrate stratum (AA)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Process: The injection of heated seawater into injection well involves injecting the heated seawater into injection well to which pressure which generates fracturing is applied to the methane hydrate stratum. The set distance between the injection well and the production well is 10 m or more. The injection well and the production well are vertical wells. The set period is a periodic period between 2 months to 4 years.

TI Polystyrene continuous multi-layer co-extruded            foam insulation composite pipe comprises carbon dioxide            foamed blended polystyrene insulation layer and            polyethylene and ethylene-vinyl acetate copolymer            protection layer.
AU LIU L
   GU S
   HAN B
   WANG H
AE ZIBO JIELIN PLASTIC TUBE MFR CO LTD (ZIBO-Non-standard)
GA 201860028D
AB    NOVELTY - Polystyrene continuous multi-layer co-extruded                foam insulation composite pipe comprises                co-extruded inner layer of carbon dioxide foamed                blended polystyrene insulation layer and outer                layer of polyethylene and ethylene-vinyl acetate                copolymer protection layer, where the carbon                dioxide foamed blended polystyrene insulation layer                is composed of 80-100 pts. wt. polystyrene or high                impact polystyrene, 1-20 pts. wt. low density                polyethylene, 1-10 pts. wt. nucleating agent, 1-5                pts. wt. antioxidant, 3-9 pts. wt. carbon dioxide,                1-5 pts. wt. ethanol and 1-5 pts. wt. water.
   USE - Used as polystyrene continuous multi-layer                co-extruded foam insulation composite pipe.
   ADVANTAGE - The composite pipe: has high production                efficiency and stable product performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                preparing polystyrene continuous multi-layer                co-extruded foam insulation composite pipe,                comprising either (i) blending a blend of                polystyrene or high-impact polystyrene with                low-density polyethylene, then mixing blend,                nucleating agent and antioxidant by high-speed                mixer, (ii) adding mixed raw materials into                twin-screw extruder for plasticizing and melting,                and then injecting foaming agent composed of carbon                dioxide, ethanol, and water in a supercritical                state, extruding through pipe forming die and core                mold, and carbon dioxide gas forms cells around the                nucleating agent to obtain carbon dioxide foaming                blended polystyrene insulation layer, and (iii)                co-extruding a layer of polyethylene and                ethylene-vinyl acetate copolymer on outer surface                of carbon dioxide foaming blended polystyrene                insulation layer using co-extrusion machine to                obtain polyethylene and ethylene-vinyl acetate                copolymer protection layer or (i) blending a blend                of polystyrene or high-impact polystyrene with                low-density polyethylene, then mixing blend,                nucleating agent and antioxidant by high-speed                mixer, (ii) adding mixed raw materials into                twin-screw extruder for plasticizing and melting,                and then injecting foaming agent composed of carbon                dioxide, ethanol, and water in a supercritical                state, extruding through pipe forming die and core                mold, and carbon dioxide gas forms cells around the                nucleating agent to obtain carbon dioxide foaming                blended polystyrene insulation layer, (iii)                co-extruding a layer of polyethylene and                ethylene-vinyl acetate copolymer on outer surface                of carbon dioxide foaming blended polystyrene                insulation layer using co-extrusion machine to                obtain polyethylene and ethylene-vinyl acetate                copolymer protection layer, and (iv) cooling                polyethylene and ethylene-vinyl acetate copolymer                protection layer and then co-extruding by second                co-extruder to obtain a polyethylene protective                layer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide                  foamed blended polystyrene insulation layer is                  composed of 85-95 pts. wt. polystyrene or high                  impact polystyrene, 8-12 pts. wt. low density                  polyethylene, 4-6 pts. wt. nucleating agent, 2-3                  pts. wt. antioxidant, 5.5-7.5 pts. wt. carbon                  dioxide, 2-3 pts. wt. ethanol and 2-3 pts. wt.                  water. The polyethylene and ethylene-vinyl                  acetate copolymer protection layer is composed of                  50-90 pts. wt. polyethylene resin and 10-50 pts.                  wt. ethylene-vinyl acetate copolymer. The                  polyethylene and ethylene-vinyl acetate copolymer                  protection has a thickness of 0.5-1 mm. The                  polyethylene and the ethylene-vinyl acetate                  copolymer protective layer are also coextruded                  with polyethylene protective layer. The                  polyethylene protective layer has a thickness of                  1-2 mm.
