TI Use of purge gas free of carbon monoxide and            carbon dioxide and including nitrogen, hydrogen and            ammonia directly made from converter of first ammonia            production unit e.g. for reducing catalyst of converter            of second ammonia production unit.
AU GHAFOOR F
   DE GEEST H
   RAMIC A
   VAES F
AE YARA INT ASA (YARI-C)
   YARA INT ASA (YARI-C)
GA 2022C7663A
AB    NOVELTY - Use of a purge gas (7) essentially free of                carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide and comprising                nitrogen, hydrogen and ammonia, and optionally                argon, directly produced from a converter of a                first ammonia production unit (200) comprising an                active and reduced catalyst for reducing the                inactive and oxidized catalyst of a converter of a                second ammonia production unit (100).
   USE - The purge gas is useful: for reducing the                inactive and oxidized catalyst of a converter of a                second ammonia production unit; for revamping a                system comprising at least two ammonia production                units; and in a system for reducing a catalyst in                an ammonia converter in an ammonia production                system comprising at least a first and a second                ammonia production unit (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The purge gas: is essentially free of carbon                monoxide and carbon dioxide and comprising                nitrogen, hydrogen and ammonia, and optionally                argon; and allows the chemical reduction of the                catalyst of an ammonia converter under more                environmentally friendly conditions and at a lower                energy cost.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   revamping (M1) a system comprising at least                two ammonia production units, each unit comprising                an ammonia converter each having an inlet for                receiving a process gas (8) and an outlet for                releasing a purge gas, comprising fluidly                connecting the inlet of the converter of a first                ammonia production unit comprising an inactive and                oxidized catalyst, to the outlet of the converter                of a second ammonia production unit comprising an                active and reduced catalyst, for transporting the                purge gas being produced by the converter of the                second ammonia production unit, to the converter of                the first ammonia production unit;
   reducing (M2) a catalyst in an ammonia                converter in an ammonia production system (9)                comprising at least a first and a second ammonia                production unit, in which each ammonia production                unit comprises at least an ammonia converter                generating a purge gas at a pressure of above                140-90 bar and comprising a low-pressure steam                turbine for operating a gas compressor at a                pressure of 35-40 bar, and a gas compressor                operable at a pressure of 60-90 bar, comprising (a)                feeding low-pressure steam at a temperature of                350-390&#176; C and at a pressure of 35-40 bar                to the low-pressure steam turbine of an ammonia                converter of a first ammonia production unit                comprising an active and reduced catalyst, thus                operating the compressor, and (b) feeding the purge                gas of the second ammonia production unit at a flow                of 9000-16500 Nm3/hour to the compressor of the                converter of the first ammonia production unit                comprising an inactive and oxidized catalyst;                and
   a system for reducing a catalyst in an ammonia                converter in an ammonia production system                comprising at least a first and a second ammonia                production unit, in which each ammonia production                unit comprises an ammonia converter having an inlet                for receiving a process gas and an outlet for                releasing a purge gas, and the ammonia converter                generating the purge gas at a pressure of 140-290                bar and comprising means for receiving low pressure                steam at a temperature of 350-390&#176; C and                at a pressure of 35-40 bar for supplying a                low-pressure steam turbine, a low-pressure steam                turbine for operating a gas compressor at a                pressure of 60-90 bar, and the compressor for                compressing a process gas essentially free of                carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide and comprising                mixtures of hydrogen, nitrogen and ammonia, and                optionally argon, or the purge gas, to a pressure                of 60-90 bar, where the inlet of the converter of a                first ammonia production unit comprising an                inactive and oxidized catalyst i.e. fluidly                connected to the outlet of the converter of a                second ammonia production unit comprising an active                and reduced catalyst.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a schematic view of the                configuration of two ammonia converters.
   7Purge gas
   8Process gas
   9Ammonia production system
   100Second ammonia production unit
   200First ammonia production unit
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the method (M2):                  the purge gas has a flow of 10000-15500 Nm3/hour,                  preferably 12000-13500 Nm3/hour; the first                  ammonia production unit further comprises a front                  end comprising a sulfur removal unit for removing                  sulfur from a feed of natural gas, a primary                  reformer for converting a feed of natural gas                  essentially free of sulfur into a mixture of                  carbon monoxide and hydrogen, in fluid                  communication with the sulfur removal unit,                  optionally, a secondary reformer for increasing                  the conversion of the feed of natural gas                  essentially free of sulfur into a mixture of                  carbon monoxide and hydrogen achieved in the                  primary reformer, in fluid communication with the                  primary reformer, a shift conversion unit for                  converting the mixture of carbon monoxide and                  hydrogen produced in the primary reformer, or                  optionally, in the secondary reformer into a                  hydrogen and carbon dioxide mixture, in fluid                  communication with the primary reformer in the                  absence of the secondary reformer or in fluid                  communication with the secondary reformer in the                  presence of the secondary reformer, a carbon                  dioxide removal unit for separating hydrogen from                  carbon dioxide in the hydrogen and carbon dioxide                  mixture produced in the shift conversion unit, in                  fluid communication with the shift conversion                  unit, and a methanation unit for converting                  remaining amounts of carbon monoxide and carbon                  dioxide in the mixture produced in the shift                  conversion unit into methane, in fluid                  communication with the shift conversion unit, and                  the ammonia converter of the first ammonia                  production unit further comprising a                  high-pressure steam turbine for operating the                  compressor at a high pressure of above 140-290                  bar; and the second ammonia production unit                  comprises all the elements of the first ammonia                  production unit, where the steps (c) to (i) are                  correspondingly performed in the second ammonia                  production unit. The converter of the first                  ammonia production unit further comprises an                  ejector to avoid the overheating or distortion of                  the rotor of the high-pressure steam turbine. The                  second production unit comprises all the elements                  of the first production unit. Preferred Method:                  The method (M2) further comprises (c) removing                  sulfur from a feed of natural gas in a sulfur                  removal unit for producing a feed of natural gas                  essentially free of sulfur, (d) converting the                  feed of natural gas essentially free of sulfur                  obtained in the step (c), using steam, into a                  mixture of carbon monoxide and hydrogen in the                  primary reformer, (e) optionally, increasing the                  conversion of the feed of natural gas essentially                  free in sulfur, using oxygen, into a mixture of                  carbon monoxide and hydrogen achieved in the                  primary reformer in the step (d), in the                  secondary reformer, (f) converting the mixture of                  carbon monoxide and hydrogen obtained in the step                  (d), or optionally in the step (h), into a                  mixture of carbon dioxide and hydrogen in the                  shift conversion unit, (g) feeding the gaseous                  mixture of carbon dioxide and hydrogen generated                  in the step (f) to the carbon dioxide removal                  unit, thus producing hydrogen essentially free in                  carbon dioxide, and (h) feeding the hydrogen                  produced in the step (g) to a methanation unit                  for converting remaining amounts of carbon                  monoxide and carbon dioxide into methane, and (i)                  recovering heat from the steps (f) and (g) and                  optionally, from the step (e), thus producing                  high-pressure steam suitable for being fed to the                  high-pressure steam turbine.

TI Simulating micro-nano supporting agent transmission comprises e.g. processing sandstone into two thin plates with same size, placing on two sides of steel concrete mold, pouring stirred cement, quartz sand, stone, and water, and forming.
AU HE Q
   ZHANG C
   MA C
   CHENG P
   GE Z
   TANG J
   CHEN H
   WANG D
   HU W
AE UNIV CHONGQING (UYCQ-C)
GA 202170845B
AB    NOVELTY - Simulating micro-nano supporting agent transmission comprises e.g. processing the sandstone into two thin plates with the same size, placing on two sides of the steel concrete mold, pouring the stirred cement, quartz sand, stone, water mixture between the two sand rock thin plates, maintaining, forming, dismounting the mold, obtaining the simulated composite layer sample with middle layer as artificial rock core contact the surface between the sandstone thin plate and the artificial rock core, drilling the simulated wellbore on the central position of the sandstone thin plate, adhering the simulated wellbore in the simulated wellbore, performing true triaxial fracturing experiment, controlling the cutting angle of the sandstone thin plate to change the degree of inclination of the soft and weak layer, preparing the composite layer sample, placing the prepared simulated composite layer sample in the stress loading device of the constant temperature water bath system.
   USE - The micro-nano supporting agent transmission is useful for obtaining the influence of complex layer sample soft weak layer and main crack angle on the micro-nano supporting agent transmission characteristic.
   ADVANTAGE - The micro-nano supporting agent transmission: grasp the influence mechanism of the correlation characteristics between the weak bedding surface and the main fracture of the reservoir on the transmission characteristics of the micro and nano supporting agent.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Simulating micro-nano supporting agent transmission comprises (a) processing the sandstone into two thin plates with the same size, placing on two sides of the steel concrete mold, pouring the stirred cement, quartz sand, stone, water mixture between the two sand rock thin plates, maintaining, forming, dismounting the mold, obtaining the simulated composite layer sample with middle layer as artificial rock core contact the surface between the sandstone thin plate and the artificial rock core, drilling the simulated wellbore on the central position of the sandstone thin plate, adhering the simulated wellbore in the simulated wellbore, (b) performing true triaxial fracturing experiment, (1) controlling the cutting angle of the sandstone thin plate to change the degree of inclination of the soft and weak layer, preparing the composite layer sample, placing the prepared simulated composite layer sample in the stress loading device of the constant temperature water bath system, (2) placing the supporting agent for storing and filling, making the volume of the supporting agent to occupy 5-24% of the volume of the tank, (3) starting the jack device, setting the stress loading confining pressure of the simulated multi-layer sample, (4) opening the carbon dioxide gas valve, starting the booster pump, adjusting the frequency to the required displacement through the flow meter, starting the heating pipe and the insulating pipe, converting the carbon dioxide into the supercritical state, pumping the carbon dioxide into the simulated wellbore through the transmission pipeline, forming a high pressure in the simulated multilayer sample to cause the sample to crack, forming a main crack with a certain angle, (5) observing the simulation complex layer sample pressure change curve derived by the computer, judging whether the sample is cracked according to the curve trend, (6) opening the valve between the heater and the supporting agent storage device, making the supercritical carbon dioxide to enter the supporting agent storage device, mixing with the supporting agent to form mixed solution, starting the electric machine, making the mixed liquid flow into the simulated wellbore, providing periodic alternating stress to the booster pump through the computer, (7) using the prepared simulation complex layer sample, forming the angle range of the main crack angle in the sample and the complex layer sample layer surface is 45-90 degrees C, repeating steps , simulating the fracturing process, after finishing the experiment, taking out the simulation complex layer sample, observing the fracturing crack expansion shape and angle, using CT scanning to detect the transmission condition of the micro-nano supporting agent in the crack, and analyzing data.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (a), the cement is Portland cement with a strength grade of 62.5 R. The particle size of quartz sand is 80-120 mesh. The particle size of stone is 4-6 mesh. The mass ratio of cement, quartz sand, stones, and water is 1.0:3.0:4.5. The ratio of the water to cement is 0.5. In step (a), the prepared simulated multi-layer sample is a cube with a side length of 200 mm and a drilling depth of 110 cm. A simulated wellbore that passes through the upper cover of the stress loading system is connected to the transmission pipeline and installed inside. The simulated wellbore is inserted in the center of the simulated multi-layer sample. The length of the simulated wellbore is 90 cm with an open hole section of 20 cm. The proppant is a micro-nano supporting agent, and the particle size is 200-400 mesh fly ash. The particle size of fly ash is 325 mesh, and its sphericity and roundness are both greater than 0.8. The device for the true triaxial fracturing experiment comprises a fracturing fluid tank and a booster pump, which are connected in turn by a pipeline, a supporting agent storage tank and a fracturing simulation component. The supporting agent storage tank is connected in parallel with a pipeline capable of controlling connection and disconnection. The fracturing fluid tank is carbon dioxide gas storage tank. The length of the pipeline between the supporting agent storage tank and the fracturing simulation component is 20-50 cm. The fracturing simulation component comprises a stress loading device. The fracturing simulation component is set in the constant temperature water bath system. The device further comprises a temperature sensor and a pressure sensor arranged on the pipeline. The temperature sensor and the pressure sensor are connected with the data collecting system through the transmission line. The pipeline is further provided with an electric machine set for mixing the fracturing fluid with the propping agent to form a sand mixing liquid. The pipeline is further provided with a heater. The pipeline between the heater and the fracturing simulation component is added with an insulating pipe. The stress loading system consists of a loading cavity with a size of 300 mmx 300 mmx 300 mm, a hand pump with a maximum pressure of 60 MPa and a hydraulic jack. The working size of the water bath in the constant temperature water bath system is 600 mmx 600 mmx 600 mm, and the temperature range is 25-100 degrees C. The pipeline is further provided with a flow meter between the booster pump and the heater for collecting the flow rate of the fracturing fluid pump into the stress loading system. The flow meter is an electromagnetic flow meter. The supporting agent storage device is a cylindrical storage tank with a height of 40 cm and a bottom diameter of 70 cm. The supporting agent inlet is set on the upper part and the outlet and on the lower part. The outlet is connected to the top of the simulated wellbore. The booster pump is a double plunger booster pump with a volume of 266 ml and a maximum pressure provided at 51.7 MPa. The data acquisition system includes a computer and CT analysis equipment. The spatial resolution of the CT analysis equipment is 2.5 LP/mm, the maximum imaging pixel is 4096x 4096, and the size measurement accuracy is 0.02-0.05. A first valve is provided between the fracturing fluid tank and the booster pump. A second valve and a third valve are respectively provided on the main pipeline and the parallel pipeline on both sides of the proppant storage tank. A shut-off valve is arranged between the proppant storage tank and the fracturing simulation component. Preferred Method: In the process, setting the confining pressure of the simulated multi-layer sample to 8, 10, and 12 MPa before and after, left and right, and up and down respectively, the temperature of the constant temperature water bath system and the heater is 45 degrees C, and the flow rate of the flow meter is to 3 m/second, preferably, mixing supporting agent with the fracturing fluid and transferring to the simulated multi-layer sample, controlling the sand ratio to 15%, and setting the displacement to 30 ml/minute. The pressure measurement range of the pressure sensor is 0-34.5 MPa, and the accuracy is 4 kPa, preferably, the temperature measurement range of the temperature sensor is 0-200 degrees , and the accuracy is 0.1 degrees C.

TI Evaluating biological wastewater treatment characteristics comprises e.g. constructing two reaction systems of experimental group and blank group, connecting carbon dioxide adsorption and gas measurement device to each reaction system.
AU HU H
   WANG T
   WU Y
AE ENVIRONMENTAL INNOVATIONS SUZHOU RES INS (ENVI-Non-standard)
GA 202015427U
AB    NOVELTY - Evaluating biological wastewater treatment characteristics comprises e.g. (1) constructing the two reaction systems of the experimental group and blank group, connecting a carbon dioxide adsorption device to each reaction system and connecting gas measurement device at the same time, (ii) reading the data from the gas measurement device, where the oxygen consumption data obtained is the gas volume under standard conditions i.e. converted into oxygen consumption mo by as given in the specification, where mo indicates the mass of oxygen consumed in the reaction system, and is directly displayed by the measuring device, mr indicates the mass of 1 mol O2 is 32 g, Vo represents the volume of oxygen consumed in the reaction bottle under standard conditions in ml, 22.4 indicates that the volume of 1mol ideal gas is 22.4 l under standard conditions, the mass B of oxygen consumed per unit volume in the reaction flask.
   USE - The method is useful for evaluating biological wastewater treatment characteristics.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has controllable process and semi-automatic control system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Evaluating biological wastewater treatment characteristics comprises (1) constructing the two reaction systems of the experimental group and blank group, connecting a carbon dioxide adsorption device to each reaction system and connecting gas measurement device at the same time, (ii) reading the data from the gas measurement device, where the oxygen consumption data obtained is the gas volume under standard conditions i.e. converted into oxygen consumption mo by as given in the specification, where mo indicates the mass of oxygen consumed in the reaction system, and is directly displayed by the measuring device, mr indicates the mass of 1 mol O2 is 32 g, Vo represents the volume of oxygen consumed in the reaction bottle under standard conditions in ml, 22.4 indicates that the volume of 1mol ideal gas is 22.4 l under standard conditions, the mass B of oxygen consumed per unit volume in the reaction flask is where B represents the mass of oxygen consumed per unit volume of the mixed solution in mg/l, V represents the volume of the reaction system in ml, (iii) drawing the characteristic curve of oxygen consumption rate and oxygen consumption by the mass B of oxygen consumed per unit volume, according to the characteristic curve of oxygen consumption rate and oxygen consumption, where each stage of the water sample to be tested treated with activated sludge was evaluated as a bio processable stage and a non-biological-treatment stage, the biologically treatable stage refers to a stage in which the oxygen consumption rate curve is higher than the endogenous respiratory rate curve, the non-biological treatment stage refers to a stage in which the oxygen consumption rate curve is equal to the endogenous respiratory rate curve, collecting water samples at the point in time when the non-biological treatment stage occurs, performing water quality analysis to determine inhibitors and/or refractory organics in the components of the wastewater, the biodegradable stage includes a biodegradable organic matter stage and a biodegradable organic matter stage, the stage of rapid biodegradation of organic matter refers to a stage within 0-5 hours and showing an upward trend, the stage of biodegradable organic matter for 5 hours, the biological oxygen consumption rate is still higher than the endogenous respiration rate, before its significant decline, according to the point at which the biological rapid degradation organic matter stage and the biological easy degradation organic matter stage occur, according to the cumulative oxygen consumption curve, determining the proportion of easy-to-handle organics and processing time An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for device for evaluating biological treatment characteristics of wastewater comprising a carbon dioxide adsorption unit, a reaction unit, a trace gas determination unit, and an oxygen bag are sealed and connected in sequence. The trace gas determination unit is also connected to a data analysis unit.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The inhibitors and/or refractory organics in the components of the wastewater during the non-biological treatment stage can optimize operating conditions, add pre-and post-treatment methods, includes changing carbon sources, nitrogen source, phosphorus source, pre-treatment method for increasing air flotation and hydrolytic acidification. In the preparation step of the device, the two reaction systems of the experimental group and the blank group refer to the waste water and deionized water with a volume of alpha x V, labeled as experimental group and blank group, where V is the volume of the reaction system, the value range of V is 200-400ml, alpha is the dosing ratio of the sample in the reaction system, the value of alpha ranges from 50-70%, add theta x V volume of nutrient solution and (1-( alpha )-( theta )x V volume of activated sludge inoculum to the experimental and blank groups, ( theta ) is the dosing ratio of the nutrient solution in the reaction system. The sludge concentration in the two reaction systems of the experimental group and the blank group is 2-4 g/l. The water temperature in the two reaction systems of the experimental group and the blank group is controlled at 20-30 degrees C. The pH value range of the two reaction systems of the experimental group and the blank group is 6.5-8.5. Preferred Components: The one-way valve is installed between the trace gas measurement unit and the reaction unit, so that the gas can only flow from the trace gas unit to the reaction unit.

PN CN109971707-A
TI Constructing cell library of intracellular functional physiologically active peptides derived from cell body, by cutting umbilical cord, washing, adding medium, identifying isolated mesenchymal stem, culturing and purifying product.
AU ZHOU B
   LI J
   LU Y
   DONG Y
   FAN Y
   YU J
   SHEN L
AE TUS-HEALCARE SCI & TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (TUSH-Non-standard)
GA 201961259H
AB    NOVELTY - Method for constructing a cell library of intracellular functional physiologically active peptides derived from a cell body, involves (a) cutting umbilical cord into small pieces and washing using phosphate buffered saline, adding into Petri dish and preparing into paste, adding medium and mixing, adding into culture flask and adding medium, placing the flask in carbon dioxide incubator, adding medium into flask for 5-9 days, viewing under microscope when the localized cell fusion degree of the culture bottle reaches 90% or more, digesting using trypsin, (b) identifying isolated mesenchymal stem cells, (c) recovering expanded cells by freezing in a water bath at 37 degrees C, placing thawed cell suspension in a centrifuge tube with medium, performing centrifugation, (d) observing cell fusion degree in the culture bottle reaches 85-90%, washing, digesting and centrifuging, (e) culturing collected cells and purifying product.
   USE - The method is useful for constructing a cell library of intracellular functional physiologically active peptides derived from a cell body, which is used for providing antiaging effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for constructing a cell library of intracellular functional physiologically active peptides derived from a cell body, involves (a) cutting umbilical cord into small pieces and washing using phosphate buffered saline containing 2% double-antibody for 2-3 times, adding into Petri dish and preparing into paste, adding medium and mixing, adding into culture flask and adding medium, placing the flask in carbon dioxide incubator, adding medium into flask for 5-9 days, viewing under microscope when the localized cell fusion degree of the culture bottle reaches 90% or more, digesting using trypsin, (b) identifying isolated mesenchymal stem cells washing cells using cell staining buffer or PBS washing solution, centrifuging cell suspension at 1000-2000 rpm for 5-10 minutes, discarding the supernatant, adding staining buffer to make a cell suspension, and adding the cell suspension into the flow tube, blocking cell suspension by Fc receptor, and adding fluorescently labeled antibodies to the cell suspension, placing on ice, incubating in the dark for 30 to 40 minutes, resuspending the cells by adding flow cytometry staining (FAC) buffer or PBS wash, centrifuge the cell suspension at 1000-2000 rpm for 5-10 minutes, discarding the supernatant and add FACs buffer, resuspending the cells in PBS wash solution, then alkaline detection and analysis by flow cytometry, (c) recovering expanded cells by freezing in a water bath at 37 degrees C, placing thawed cell suspension in a centrifuge tube with medium, performing centrifugation at 1000-2000 rpm for 5-10 minutes, adding the medium into the centrifuge tube, washing by centrifugation and removing the supernatant, resuspending the cells by adding the medium, transferring into culture flask and placing in a carbon dioxide incubator for 24-48 hours, aspirating culture medium and adding PBS washing solution for washing, after the washing is completed, aspirating washing liquid and adding trypsin, blowing cell layer and pipetting the cell suspension in a centrifuge tube at 1000-2000 rpm for 5-10 minutes, removing the supernatant, adding the medium, washing by centrifugation, removing supernatant, adding culture, resuspending nutrient base and transferring into a culture flask and placed in a carbon dioxide incubator, (d) observing cell fusion degree in the culture bottle reaches 85-90%, then aspirating the medium, adding PBS washing solution, washing the washing solution and adding the pancreatin after washing, until the macroscopic cells in the adherent cell layer have a large number of cells, adding medium to terminate the digestion, blowing the cell layer and aspirating cell suspension in a centrifuge tube at 1000-2000 rpm for 5-10 minutes, removing supernatant, then adding PBS to wash resuspended liquid and collecting the cells by centrifugation, (e) culturing collected cells into P10 generation and showing fine spindle type, performing centrifugation, collecting cells for 3 times, collecting precipitate by washing, adding deionized water, repeatedly freezing using liquid nitrogen and thawing, and performing low-speed centrifugation, ultrafiltering, mixing with methanol, water and formic acid, evaporating, and purifying product, and freeze-drying product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method involves washing and placing umbilical cord in the culture dish, and removing the umbilical cord from the vascular tissue.
   ACTIVITY - Dermatological; Nootropic.

PN US2018073430-A1; WO2018051251-A1; CA3036311-A1; AU2017329061-A1; IN201937009436-A; KR2019051032-A; BR112019004762-A2; EP3512925-A1; VN64059-A; CN110168058-A; MX2019002888-A1; JP2019537631-W; ID201905267-A; US10989113-B2; CN110168058-B; EP3512925-B1; EA39851-B1; ZA201901493-A; KR2451300-B1
TI Producing power using a combination of a partial            oxidation (POX) system and power production system,            comprises combining solid or liquid fuel and oxygen in            POX reactor, removing and cooling POX stream, and            compressing stream of fuel gas.
AU FORREST B A
   LU X
   ALLAM R J
   FETVEDT J E
   PALMER M R
   ALAN F B
   XIJIA L
   LV X
AE 8 RIVERS CAPITAL LLC (ERIV-C)
   8 RIVERS CAPITAL LLC (ERIV-C)
   8 RIVERS CAPITAL LLC (ERIV-C)
   8 RIVERS CAPITAL LLC (ERIV-C)
GA 201821143N
AB    NOVELTY - Producing power using a combination of a                partial oxidation (POX) system and a power                production system (PPS), comprises: combining a                solid or liquid fuel and oxygen in a POX reactor;                removing from the POX stream; cooling the POX                stream; purifying the POX stream; compressing the                stream of the fuel gas; combusting the stream of                the fuel gas in a PPS combustor; expanding                combustion product stream; passing the expanded PPS                combustion product stream through a PPS recuperator                heat exchanger; removing at least a portion of at                least one impurities; and pressurizing.
   USE - The process is useful for producing power                (claimed) comprising electricity.
   ADVANTAGE - The process: produces high efficiency power                with combustion of fuels while achieving                simultaneous carbon capture; achieves high                efficiency with low cost system, such as using                commercially available POX reactor systems and a                high pressure carbon dioxide working fluid power                cycle; and is cost-effective.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing power using a combination of a                partial oxidation (POX) system and a power                production system (PPS), comprises: combining a                solid or liquid fuel and oxygen in a POX reactor                under conditions to partially oxidize the fuel and                form a POX stream comprising a fuel gas at a first                temperature; removing from the POX stream                comprising the fuel gas at least a portion of any                solid components or gaseous components that do not                form part of the fuel gas; cooling the POX stream                comprising the fuel gas in a POX heat exchanger to                a second, lower temperature; purifying the POX                stream comprising the fuel gas by removing at least                a portion of any liquid water and acid gases and                thus forming a stream of the fuel gas; compressing                the stream of the fuel gas to a pressure of 12 MPa                or greater; combusting the stream of the fuel gas                in a PPS combustor to form a combustion product                stream at a pressure of at least 10 MPa and a                temperature of at least 800 degrees C; expanding                the combustion product stream across a PPS turbine                to generate power and form an expanded PPS                combustion product stream; passing the expanded PPS                combustion product stream through a PPS recuperator                heat exchanger and thus withdrawing heat from the                PPS combustion product stream and forming a cooled                PPS combustion product stream; removing at least a                portion of at least one impurities from the cooled                PPS combustion product stream to form a recycle                carbon dioxide stream; and pressurizing the recycle                carbon dioxide stream in a PPS compressor to form a                compressed recycle carbon dioxide stream. An                INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for power                production unit comprising POX system and a PPS,                comprising POX reactor, POX scrubber unit including                an inlet in fluid communication with an outlet of                the POX reactor, POX heat exchanger including an                inlet in fluid communication with an outlet of the                scrubber unit, POX water separator including an                inlet in fluid communication with an outlet of the                POX heat exchanger, POX acid gas removal system                including an inlet in fluid communication with an                outlet of the water separator, POX compressor                including an inlet in fluid communication with an                outlet of the acid gas removal system, PPS                combustor including an inlet in fluid communication                with an outlet of the POX compressor and configured                to receive a fuel gas from it, PPS turbine                including an inlet in fluid communication with an                outlet of the PPS combustor and configured to                receive a combustion product stream, PPS                recuperator heat exchanger including an inlet in                fluid communication with an outlet of the PPS                turbine, PPS separator including an inlet in fluid                communication with an outlet of the PPS recuperator                heat exchanger and PPS compressor including an                inlet in fluid communication with an outlet of the                PPS separator.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure illustrates combined POX system                and PPS system where the PPS generates power using                a thel gas derived from the partial oxidation of a                liquid or solid hydrocarbon or carbonaceous fuel in                the POX system.
   Crusher (1)
   Partial oxidation reactor (4)
   Air separation unit (6)
   Coal feed stream (21)
   Oxygen stream (56)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The POX reactor is a                  gasifier, fluidized bed reactor or catalyzed                  reactor. The shift reactor is a reverse water gas                  shift reactor. The POX water separator includes                  an outlet in fluid communication with an inlet of                  the POX reactor, the PPS compressor includes an                  outlet in fluid communication with an inlet of                  the POX heat exchanger or the PPS compressor                  includes an outlet in fluid communication with an                  inlet of the PPS recuperator heat exchanger. The                  power production unit further comprises at least                  one of: a solid fuel feeding system, a shift                  reactor, carbonyl sulfide hydrolysis reactor,                  desulfurization unit, activated adsorption unit                  and a catalytic methanation system; closed heat                  transfer loop between the POX heat exchanger and                  the PPS recuperator heat exchanger; at least one                  flow lines for fluid communication between the                  PPS compressor and the POX heat exchanger; at                  least one flow lines for fluid communication                  between the PPS compressor and the PPS                  recuperator heat exchanger; at least one flow                  lines for fluid communication between the PPS                  compressor and the POX reactor.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The process further                  comprises: reducing the temperature of the POX                  stream comprising the fuel gas to a temperature                  that is less than the first temperature and                  greater than the second temperature prior to                  cooling the POX stream comprising the fuel gas in                  the POX heat exchanger; heating the stream of the                  fuel gas after the compressing and before the                  combusting; processing a portion of the POX                  stream comprising the fuel gas in a carbonyl                  sulfide hydrolysis reactor adapted to convert                  carbonyl sulfide to hydrogen sulfide; providing                  one or both of steam and carbon dioxide into the                  POX reactor; passing a portion of the POX stream                  comprising the fuel gas through a shift reactor                  configured to convert a mixture of hydrogen and                  carbon dioxide to a mixture of carbon monoxide                  and water or configured to convert a mixture of                  carbon monoxide and water to a mixture of                  hydrogen and carbon dioxide; mixing a portion of                  the POX stream comprising the fuel gas with a                  portion of the recycle carbon dioxide stream to                  form a mixture, and processing the mixture in the                  shift reactor; passing a portion of the POX                  stream comprising the fuel gas through a warm                  desulfurization unit configured to remove at                  least one of hydrogen sulfide, carbonyl sulfide,                  and heavy metals from the POX stream comprising                  the fuel gas stream; passing at least a portion                  of the POX stream comprising the fuel gas through                  an activated adsorption system configured to                  remove mercury from the POX stream comprising the                  fuel gas; passing at least a portion of the                  stream of the fuel gas through an acid gas                  removal unit configured to separate one or both                  of at least a portion of any hydrogen sulfide and                  at least a portion of any carbon dioxide present                  in the stream of the fuel gas; passing at least a                  portion of the stream of the fuel gas through a                  catalytic methanation system configured to                  convert carbon monoxide and hydrogen to methane                  and water. The step of reducing the temperature                  of the POX stream comprising the fuel gas                  comprises adding a quenching fluid to the POX                  reactor and the quenching fluid comprises one or                  both of water and carbon dioxide. The step of                  heating the stream of the fuel gas comprises                  passing the stream of the fuel gas through the                  PPS recuperator heat exchanger. The POX heat                  exchanger is configured to withdraw heat from the                  POX stream comprising the fuel gas against at                  least one of portion of the compressed recycle                  carbon dioxide stream, stream of pressurized                  water, nitrogen stream, a stream of pressurized                  oxygen and carbon dioxide and stream of the fuel                  gas. At least a portion of the compressed recycle                  carbon dioxide stream is heated in the PPS                  recuperator heat exchanger and passed to the PPS                  combustor or passed to the POX heat exchanger.                  The compressed recycle carbon dioxide stream                  exits the POX heat exchanger and is passed to the                  PPS recuperator heat exchanger. A portion of the                  compressed recycle carbon dioxide stream is                  passed to the POX reactor or is combined with the                  solid or liquid fuel prior to combining the solid                  or liquid fuel with the oxygen in the POX                  reactor. The solid or liquid fuel comprises fuel                  slurry formed of a solid fuel combined with a                  liquid or supercritical carbon dioxide.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).); Q52 (Reaction engines: external combustion; gas turbines; rockets (F01D, F02C, F02K, F02M))

PN CN115043402-A
TI System for preparing concrete using carbon-dioxide            as additive component, has absorption tower uniformly            sprayed with solvent, where connecting end of            absorption tower is provided with regeneration tower            that regenerates carbon dioxide of absorption            tower.
AU MEI L
AE ZHONGGAO FUTAI ENVIRONMENTAL TECHNOLOGY NANTONG              CO LTD (ZHON-Non-standard)
GA 2022C2717N
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a flue gas pre-treatment tower                (1) whose connecting end is provided with an                absorbing component (2). The connecting end of the                absorbing component is connected with a                regenerating device (3). The absorbing component                and the regenerative device are used for dust                removal and desulfurization of flue gas. The                connection end of a compression drying device (4)                is connected to a refrigeration liquefying device                (5) through a transmission pipe. The refrigeration                liquefying device is communicated with an                absorption tower (6) through the transmission pipe.                The absorption tower is uniformly sprayed with a                solvent. The connecting end of the absorption tower                is provided with a regeneration tower (7) that                regenerates the carbon dioxide of the absorption                tower.
   USE - System for preparing concrete using                carbon-dioxide as additive component.
   ADVANTAGE - The system can efficiently and rapidly obtain                the liquid carbon-dioxide with high purity, and has                simple process, low cost, and protects the                environment. The system collects and processes the                flue gas and converts the flue gas into liquid,                adds the obtained carbon-dioxide to the concrete                composition in the form of aggregates, or the                carbon-dioxide during the concrete mixing process.                The system converts carbon-dioxide from gas to                minerals to generate solid carbonate, which can                greatly improve the strength of concrete, greatly                reduce the demand for cement while improving the                strength of concrete, so that the cost of concrete                production is greatly reduced.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a                system for preparing concrete using carbon-dioxide                as additive component.
   1Flue gas pre-treatment tower
   2Absorbing component
   3Regenerating device
   4Compression drying device
   5Refrigeration liquefying device
   6Absorption tower
   7Regeneration tower
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The inner solvent of                  the absorption tower is set as sodium hydroxide.                  The regeneration tower is sprayed with                  regeneration solvent, where the regeneration                  solvent is hydrochloric acid.

PN CN114988360-A
TI Preparing synthesis gas, involves subjecting            natural gas or low-carbon alkane to partial oxidation            reaction, increasing temperature of reaction system to            provide heat for whole reaction, and subjecting            partially oxidized natural gas, oxidation product and            carbon dioxide to reforming reaction.
AU ZHOU Y
   WANG T
   LIANG J
   XU B
AE CHINA CHENGDA ENG CO LTD (CNCE-C)
GA 2022B7841M
AB    NOVELTY - The method for preparing the synthesis gas by                natural gas partial oxidation and carbon dioxide                self-heating reforming involves using carbon                dioxide, oxygen and natural gas or low-carbon                alkane as raw materials, subjecting the natural gas                or the low-carbon alkane to partial oxidation                reaction, increasing the temperature of the                reaction system to provide heat for the whole                reaction, and subjecting the partially oxidized                natural gas, an oxidation product and the carbon                dioxide to reforming reaction under the conditions                of high temperature, heat insulation and no                catalyst. The carbon dioxide is mixed with natural                gas or lower alkane, and then introduced into the                reforming reactor for combustion, and the natural                gas or lower alkane is subjected to partial                oxidation reaction.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing synthesis                gas by partial oxidation of natural gas and carbon                dioxide self-heating type, where the synthesis gas                is used for catalytic reforming technology.
   ADVANTAGE - The method creatively carries out reforming                reaction under the conditions of high temperature,                heat insulation and no catalyst to generate                synthesis gas, and overcomes the problem of carbon                deposit generated by adopting the catalyst to                prepare the synthesis gas. The conversion per pass                of the carbon dioxide reaches 30-60%. The method                has scientific design, ingenious conception and                simple method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of a                reforming synthesis gas system. (Drawing includes                non-English language text).
   1Reforming reactor
   2Waste heat boiler
   3Heat exchanger
   4Heat recovery and carbon dioxide removal                device
   5Combustor
   6Oxygen conveying pipe
   11Natural gas and carbon dioxide mixed gas                conveying pipe
   12Natural gas conveying pipe
   13Carbon dioxide conveying pipe

PN CN114634197-A
TI Absorbing carbon dioxide by utilizing            carbon-fixing agent and brine, comprises e.g. preparing            salt water, dissolving in salt water, full stirring and            activating to completely dissolve carbon fixer, and            passing gas to be treated containing carbon dioxide to            absorb carbon dioxide.
AU JIANG Z
   DI Y
   CHEN Y
   XIA M
   DING Z
   ZHANG X
   PAN Y
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 2022846892
AB    NOVELTY - Absorbing carbon dioxide by utilizing a                carbon-fixing agent and brine, comprises (1)                preparing salt water with a salinity, dissolving                the above carbon fixer in the salt water, full                stirring and activating to completely dissolve the                carbon fixer to obtain an alkaline brine system                rich in calcium (II) and magnesium (II) ion                solutions, and (2) passing the gas to be treated                containing carbon dioxide into the above-mentioned                alkaline brine system to absorb carbon dioxide, and                preparing brine to dissolve the carbon-fixing                agent, and using the brine system after dissolving                the carbon-fixing agent to absorb carbon dioxide,                and the weight percentages of the components of the                carbon-fixing agent are: 0.01-99.99 wt.% calcium                oxide, 0-20 wt.% magnesium oxide, and 0.01-99.99                wt.% calcined dolomite.
   USE - The method is useful for absorbing carbon                dioxide by solid carbon agent and brine, where the                carbon dioxide is discharged by coal power                plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The method uses salt water with a certain                salinity, so it overcomes the problem that the                calcium oxide in the existing technology has low                solubility in fresh water, and the dissolution rate                is slow. Compared with the conventional method, the                high ionic strength in salt water can accelerate                the leaching of calcium ion in the solid carbon                agent, obtaining the accelerating calcium oxide                dissolving rate, increasing the effect of calcium                oxide solubile in water, in the same time, the rate                of salt water leaching calcium ion with high                salinity is more than one time of deionized water.                The method controls the magnesium ion in a certain                range in preparing salt water, so as to overcome                the problem of the calcium carbonate generating                rate is limited.

PN JP2020069428-A
TI Carbon-dioxide storage and release material used for power generating plant, comprises two-dimensional borohydride-containing sheet, and having preset peak spectrum of boron.
AU KONDO T
   FUJINO A
   ISHIBIKI R
   GOTO T
   ITO S
AE UNIV TSUKUBA (UTSK-C)
   TOKYO INST TECHNOLOGY NAT UNIV CORP (TOKD-C)
GA 202037598F
AB    NOVELTY - A carbon-dioxide storage/release material comprises two-dimensional borohydride-containing compound (I) in a sheet, includes a two-dimensional network, and has spectrum with peaks at 187.5-191.2 eV derived from B1's of boron and does not show peak derived from magnesium. The ratio of boron and hydrogen measured by thermal desorption gas analysis, before and after heating, is 6:5-3:1.
   USE - Carbon-dioxide storage/release material used to store carbon dioxide for gas plant or a power generating plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon-dioxide storage/release material has excellent storage characteristics even at normal temperature and pressure, and occlude carbon dioxide by heating at 50 degrees C.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A carbon-dioxide storage/release material comprises two-dimensional borohydride-containing compound of formula: n(HxBy) (I) in a sheet, includes a two-dimensional network, and has spectrum with peaks at 187.5-191.2 eV derived from B1's of boron and does not show peak derived from magnesium. The ratio of boron and hydrogen measured by thermal desorption gas analysis, before and after heating is 6:5-3:1 composed of having two-dimensional network, and having spectrum with peaks at 187.5-191.2 eV derived from B1's of boron and does not show peak derived by magnesium measured by X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy.
   n = 4 or more;and
   x/y = 5/6-1/3.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for production of the carbon dioxide storage/release material, which involves mixing magnesium ion constituting the magnesium diboride of formula: MgB2 (II) and an ion exchange resin coordinated with an ion-exchangeable ion in a polar organic solvent, and heating the obtained carbon dioxide storage and release material precursor at 200-350 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The sheet comprises boron arranged in hexagonal ring which forms mesh-like surface structure in which they are connected to form a network, and hydrogen is randomly bonded to the boron forming the hexagon without having a specific periodic regularity and without aggregating, and two adjacent boron have a site for bonding to the same hydrogen atom. The ion exchange resin including a sulfonyl group. Preferred Properties: The two-dimensional borohydride-containing sheet has a length of at least 100 nm or more in one direction, and having spectrum with peaks at 188 eV derived from B1's of negatively charged boron and does not show peak derived by magnesium measured by X-ray photoelectron spectroscopy, and having peak at 191 eV derived from the sp2 of boron. Preferred Composition: The ratio of boron and hydrogen before and after temperature rise is 1:1 calculated by temperature programmed desorption gas analysis.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic solvent is acetonitrile or methanol.

PT P
PN WO2019071526-A1
TI Method for generating zero-carbon emission fossil fuel power, involves performing oxygen-rich combustion to generate electricity, sending generated electric energy to outside, and injecting carbon dioxide-rich flue gas into layer on-site.
AU PENG S
AE PENG S (PENG-Individual)
GA 201936442J
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves carrying out geological exploration to select a carbon dioxide sequestration layer. A fossil fuel power plant is constructed at a site (EE) above the selected carbon dioxide sequestration layer. Oxygen is pumped to a fossil fuel (BB). Oxygen-rich combustion is performed to generate electricity. The generated electric energy is transmitted to outside. The generated carbon dioxide-rich flue gas is injected into the carbon dioxide sequestration layer on-site. Horizontal distance is determined between two places for about less than 100 km.
   USE - Method for generating zero-carbon emission fossil fuel power for an industrial power plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables setting a site for generation of a carbon dioxide-rich flue gas horizontally close to a site for injection into a carbon dioxide sequestration layer so as to directly inject the carbon-rich flue gas containing 50-95wt.% for carbon dioxide, into the carbon sequestration layer for eliminating the need for a long- distance transportation link between a carbon capture link and a carbon sequestration link, so that carbon capture cost and overall carbon capture and storage (CCS) cost can be reduced, thus achieving zero-carbon emission fossil fuel power generation with better economic benefit.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a system for generating zero-carbon emission fossil fuel power.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic block diagram of a method for generating zero-carbon emission fossil fuel power for an industrial power plant. '(Drawing includes non-English language text)'
   Fossil fuel (BB)
   Site above the selected carbon dioxide sequestration layer (EE)
   Oxygen-enriched combustion power unit (1)
   Fossil fuel input unit (2)
   Power generation and transformation unit (3)

PT P
PN CN109305691-A
TI Dyeing chemical refining comprises e.g. preparing carbon dioxide using calcium carbonate under the condition of high temperature calcining to make it to produce carbon dioxide and calcium oxide and preparing ammonia using the nitrogen gas.
AU ZHANG W
   ZHANG S
   HU S
   TIAN D
AE XUCHANG XINRUIDE CHEM TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (XUCH-Non-standard)
GA 201916081X
AB    NOVELTY - Dyeing chemical refining comprises (1) preparing carbon dioxide using calcium carbonate under the condition of high temperature calcining to make it to produce carbon dioxide and calcium oxide, (2) preparing ammonia using the nitrogen gas and hydrogen under the conditions of high temperature and high voltage mixing, and adding catalyst, reacting to generate ammonia, (3) preparing the ammonia water adding amount of potassium permanganate, removing reducing substance, dropwise adding 98 degrees C distilled water, evaporating ammonia by distilled water, entering absorption bottle filled with distilled water, cooling absorbing bottle by water, (4) introducing carbon dioxide to saturated salt water and saturated aqueous ammonia, reacting to generate sodium bicarbonate and ammonium chloride.
   USE - The method is useful for dyeing chemical refining.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Dyeing chemical refining comprises (1) preparing carbon dioxide using calcium carbonate under the condition of high temperature calcining to make it to produce carbon dioxide and calcium oxide, (2) preparing ammonia using the nitrogen gas and hydrogen under the conditions of high temperature and high voltage mixing, and adding catalyst, reacting to generate ammonia, (3) preparing the ammonia water adding amount of potassium permanganate, removing reducing substance, dropwise adding 98 degrees C distilled water, evaporating ammonia by distilled water, entering absorption bottle filled with distilled water, cooling absorbing bottle by water, (4) introducing carbon dioxide to saturated salt water and saturated aqueous ammonia, reacting to generate sodium bicarbonate and ammonium chloride, (5) separating sodium bicarbonate, heating and calcining the sodium bicarbonate, heating sodium bicarbonate and decomposing to generate sodium carbonate, water and carbon dioxide and carrying out water evaporation by calcining.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (2), the temperature of the high temperature reaction condition is 500 degrees C and the pressure is 20-50 MPa, the catalyst is iron catalyst. In the step (3), adding potassium permanganate is 15kg aqueous ammonia into 10 g potassium permanganate. In the step (4), using generated ammonium chloride can be recycled as a raw material for producing fertilizer. In the step (5) to generate carbon dioxide can be introduced in step (4), the carbon dioxide can be recycled.

PT P
PN CN107794119-A; CN107794119-B
TI Reduction method of cholesterol in lard oil, involves performing tissue experiment based on design improvement and optimization of supercritical carbon dioxide extraction and fractionation device to remove impurities from lard oil.
AU DING B
   DING X
   LI Y
AE GUANGZHOU GOODJAN HEALTH TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
   GUANGZHOU GOODJAN HEALTH TECHNOLOGY CO (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201823417T
AB    NOVELTY - The reduction of cholesterol in lard oil involves performing tissue experiment based on design improvement and optimization of supercritical carbon dioxide extraction and fractionation device to remove impurities from lard oil, loading oil into a feeding groove, removing air in groove, setting the pressure and temperature of the fractionation column, and the mass flow rate of supercritical carbon dioxide and the lard oil, starting a feeding pump, feeding the lard oil to the column, passing supercritical carbon dioxide into the column, and collecting the fractionation liquid.
   USE - Reduction method of cholesterol in lard oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method for reducing cholesterol in lard oil with low operating temperature, and excellent energy conservation effect, emission reduction effect and quality, and which avoids the destruction of beneficial components in the lard oil.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The reduction method of cholesterol in lard oil based on supercritical carbon dioxide fractionation involves performing tissue experiment based on the design improvement and optimization of the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction and fractionation device to remove impurities from the lard oil, loading the lard oil into a feeding groove, removing the air in the feeding groove through a reflux method, orderly setting the pressure of the fractionation column, the temperature of the fractionation column, and the mass flow rate of supercritical carbon dioxide and the lard oil, starting a feeding pump after the pressure and temperature of the fractionation column reach the set value, feeding the lard oil from a feeding tank to the fractionation column, passing supercritical carbon dioxide through an extraction kettle into the fractionation column, where lard oil and supercritical carbon dioxide form two-phase current, and collecting the fractionation liquid in the separation tank every 15 minutes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Components: The separation tank includes two separation tanks. Preferred Conditions: The design pressure of the separation tank (I) is 25 MPa. The design temperature of the separation tank (I) is 0-85 degrees C. The design pressure of the separation tank (II) is 10 MPa. The design temperature of the separation tank (II) is -25 to 75 degrees C.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The pressure of the fractionation column is 30 MPa. The temperature gradient of the fractionation column is 5-10 degrees C. The temperature at a bottom end of the fractionation column is 60 degrees C. The temperature at an upper end of the fractionation column is 70 degrees C. The design pressure of the extraction kettle is 35 MPa and the design temperature is 0-100 degrees C. Preferred Composition: The mass flow rate of supercritical carbon dioxide and the lard oil is 10:1.

AB    NOVELTY - The method comprises introducing the blast                furnace flue gas from the air inlet of the blast                furnace flue gas carbon capture device, forming the                blast furnace flue gas into a vortex rotational                flow through the reactor set in the blast furnace                flue gas carbon capture, collecting the carbon                dioxide rich liquid at the bottom of the blast                furnace flue gas carbon capture device, demisting                the blast furnace flue gas through multi-stage                spraying through the demister set in the blast                furnace flue gas carbon capture device, detecting                real-time flue gas flow through the first flow                meter set in the air inlet, introducing the carbon                dioxide pregnant solution collected at the bottom                of the blast furnace flue gas carbon capture device                into the heating for box heating, and adjusting the                medicine spraying amount of the reverse spraying                device by middle control module, so that the                real-time carbon dioxide flow reaches the standard                carbon dioxide flow range.

TI Air quality reminder method applied to terminal, involves executing air quality reminder operation corresponding to carbon dioxide concentration value of environment in which terminal is located according to relationship between preset carbon dioxide concentration value and reminding mode.
AU ZHENG Z
AE GUANGDONG OPPO MOBILE TELECOM CORP LTD (GDOP-C)
GA 2021C3185S
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves detecting (101) whether the terminal is in a relatively static state. The carbon dioxide concentration value of the environment in which the terminal is located is obtained (102), if the terminal is in a relatively static state. An air quality reminder operation corresponding to the carbon dioxide concentration value of the environment in which the terminal is located is executed (103), according to the preset corresponding relationship between the preset carbon dioxide concentration value and the reminding mode.
   USE - Air quality reminding method applied to terminal (claimed) such as mobile phone, tablet computer and wearable device.
   ADVANTAGE - The method performs an air quality reminder operation corresponding to the carbon dioxide concentration value of the environment in which the terminal is located according to the corresponding relationship between the preset carbon dioxide concentration value and the reminder mode. The method prevents users from being in an environment with high carbon dioxide concentration for a long time without knowing it. The method is convenient for the user to improve the air quality of the current environment, which is beneficial to improve the user's work efficiency and learning efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a reminder device of air quality;
   (2) a terminal for reminding process; and
   (3) a computer-readable storage medium storing computer instructions of reminder method of air quality.

PN WO2020254173-A1; BE1027370-B1; EP3983344-A1
TI Production of predefined volume of mineral water for domestic application, involves mineralizing water, introducing carbon dioxide before or during remineralization, and converting at least one portion of carbon dioxide into bicarbonate.
AU TOB P
AE AQVITA SRL (AQVI-Non-standard)
   AQVITA (AQVI-Non-standard)
   AQVITA SRL (AQVI-Non-standard)
GA 2020C8102N
AB    NOVELTY - Production of predefined volume of mineral water involves mineralizing a predefined volume of water by passing through a removable pod comprising a determined quantity of mineral elements so as to dissolve the mineral elements, introducing carbon dioxide before or during remineralization and performing enzyme catalysis reaction of carbon dioxide and water to convert at least one portion of carbon dioxide introduced into bicarbonate.
   USE - Production of predefined volume of mineral water used for domestic application.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces predefined volume of mineral water by instantaneous process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) domestic apparatus, which has a circuit (2) for circulating water from an inlet (1) to at least one outlet valve (13), an outlet provided with the outlet valve, a circuit passing through a remineralization unit (5) having at least one cavity for inserting a removable pod (18) containing a determined quantity of mineral elements, and cavity crossed by the water circulation circuit and arranged so that the circuit passes through the pod when the latter is inserted into the cavity, and a unit (7) for introducing carbon dioxide upstream or at the level of an insertion cavity of the pod. The inlet is provided with an inlet valve to be connected to a water inlet point. The water circulation circuit comprises the enzyme for catalyzing the reaction of carbon dioxide and water to form carbonic acid and bicarbonate, arranged downstream or at the level of unit (7); and
   (2) disposable drinking water remineralization pod, which comprises a determined amount of mineral elements and enzyme for catalyzing the reaction of carbon dioxide and water to form baking soda.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of the domestic apparatus.
   Inlet (1)
   Circuit (2)
   Remineralization unit (5)
   Carbon dioxide introduction unit (7)
   Outlet valve (13)
   Removable pod (18)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Apparatus: The domestic apparatus further has a demineralization unit upstream of the water-circulation circuit.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: Production of predefined volume of mineral water further involves demineralizing at least one portion of predefined volume of water prior to mineralization and introduction of carbon dioxide to obtain predefined volume of demineralized water. Preferred Property: The mineral elements are in the form of powder having a particle size of 5-200 microns, or rigid capsules. Preferred Components: The mineral elements are provided between two layers of a porous material and the layers are sealed together around the periphery of the pod.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

The heat collection medium in heat                collection pipe circulates at the heat collection                end and the heat exchange end to transfer heat to                the heat storage medium. The heat exchange element is provided to                exchange heat with the heat storage medium after                low-temperature carbon dioxide passes through the                heat exchange element, so that the carbon dioxide                is at a higher evaporation temperature, so that the                entire system has a higher heating                coefficient.

PN WO2020186337-A1; CA3132691-A1; EP3942236-A1; US2022170684-A1
TI Passive refrigeration apparatus for controlled            refrigeration of product, has expansion section that is            adapted to allow vaporization of liquid carbon dioxide            into expansion chamber to create mixture of carbon            dioxide snow.
AU SPAULDING W
   GUNSTONE A
   WELGAN-GUNN L
   ALBER E
   DAVID N
AE CRYOLOGISTICS REFRIGERATION TECHNOLOGIES (CRYO-Non-standard)
   CRYOLOGISTICS REFRIGERATION TECHNOLOGIES (CRYO-Non-standard)
   CRYOLOGISTICS REFRIGERATION TECHNOLOGIES (CRYO-Non-standard)
GA 2020938492
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus has a control valve (5) is                provided in fluid communication with the cylinder                (3) through a fluid pipe (4). A controller (9) is                adapted to activate and deactivate control valve to                control flow of liquid carbon dioxide. A heat                transfer assembly (6) is mounted within the                container, and is provided in fluid communication                with the control valve. The heat transfer assembly                comprises an expansion section in fluid                communication with the control valve, and receives                the flow of liquid carbon dioxide. An expansion                chamber is bounded by heat transfer surface, and                heat transfer surface is provided in thermal                contact with the cargo space (11). The expansion                section is adapted to allow the vaporization of the                liquid carbon dioxide into the expansion chamber to                create a mixture of carbon dioxide snow and carbon                dioxide gas within the expansion chamber, thus                cooling the cargo space through the heat transfer                surface. The carbon dioxide gas is exhausted                through the exhaust fluid line (10).
   USE - Passive refrigeration apparatus for controlled                refrigeration of product such as food and                pharmaceutical.
   ADVANTAGE - The apparatus has easier assembly and provides                a larger filtration area and thus potentially                better snow distribution. The ridges are preferably                arranged to minimize the chance of plugging, and                ensuring a larger unrestricted flow closer to the                orifice. The refrigeration apparatus is provided                with an exhaust manifold between the heat transfer                assembly and the exhaust fluid line, permits                passage of the carbon dioxide gas through, and                which is in thermal contact with the cargo space,                thus facilitating the cooling of the cargo                space.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a cross-sectional view of                the refrigeration system.
   Cylinder (3)
   Fluid pipe (4)
   Control valve (5)
   Heat transfer assembly (6)
   Controller (9)
   Exhaust fluid line (10)
   Cargo space (11)

AU PAPADOPOULOS A
AE PAPADOPOULOS A (PAPA-Individual)
GA 202006757V
AB    NOVELTY - The modified carbon dioxide capture and storage system uses ammonia as a carbon dioxide capture agent binds with the same production process, in same phase without further investments. The other pollutants contained in the flue gas of the coal fired power plants such as sulfur and nitrogen oxides, mercury and hydrochloric acid, and production of other chemical products other than ammonium bicarbonate, such as ammonium sulfate and ammonium nitrate, which add value and which are used usually as fertilizers. The final product of carbon sequestration consists of established fertilizers with high added value, such as ammonium bicarbonate, ammonium sulfate and the ammonium nitrate, which are sold and leave a very significant net profit to the power plants. The simplifications and deletions of components of the existing carbon dioxide capture and storage system with ammonia in coal fired power plants are made, and the drastic reduction in investment costs occurs.
   USE - Modified carbon dioxide capture and storage system in coal fired power plant.
   ADVANTAGE - As the simplifications and deletions of components of the existing carbon dioxide capture and storage system with ammonia in coal fired power plants are made, the drastic reduction in investment costs is achieved. PN US2019366230-A1
TI System for extracting supercritical carbon dioxide to collect desired product from solid matrices, has collector vessel designed to coalesce target compound from target compound laden supercritical carbon dioxide by expansion cooling.
AU CASALS T
   KENNEY S
   MOON E
AE CASALS T (CASA-Individual)
   KENNEY S (KENN-Individual)
   MOON E (MOON-Individual)
GA 2019A23751
AB    NOVELTY - The system has an extraction vessel designed to hold a pre-determined amount of extractable material i.e. botanical material, and positioned to receive supercritical carbon dioxide from a heat exchanger. The extraction vessel is sealed with the extractable material and supercritical carbon dioxide under highly pressurized conditions to produce target compound laden supercritical carbon dioxide through high intensity extraction targeting. A flow regulator device i.e. micro metering expansion valve, releases the pressurized target compound laden supercritical carbon dioxide from the extraction vessel in a controlled manner. A collector vessel is designed to coalesce the target compound from the target compound laden supercritical carbon dioxide by expansion cooling, collecting the target compound and producing carbon dioxide gas.
   USE - System for extracting HIT supercritical fluid i.e. supercritical carbon dioxide, to collect a desired product from solid matrices and the liquid.
   ADVANTAGE - The system is designed such that pressure in collector vessels can be lower, thus improving removal of target compounds from the solution and reducing cost of the vessels. The system does not require a step or contoured feature to be machined in a vessel wall, and requires precise machining of a two-part closure, thus allowing ease of maintenance/reconditioning and replacement of components. The system can precisely control the pressure in an actuator, so that distance in which the valve is opened, can be accurately and quickly adjusted to permit correct flow. The system reduces total extraction time and improves yield efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a method for extracting high intensity targeting (HIT) supercritical fluid.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a HIT supercritical fluid extraction system.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The epoxy compound is                  propylene oxide, 1,2-butylene oxide,                  epichlorohydrin, epibromohydrin, 2-butyloxirane,                  2-phenyloxirane, 2-(but-3-enyl)oxirane,                  2-(allyloxymethyl)oxirane or                  7-oxabicyclo(4.1.0)heptane.
