The                  cycloaddition reaction pressure is 0.1-1 MPa                  (preferably 0.1 MPa), and the reaction                  temperature is 25-80 degrees C (preferably 25                  degrees C). The cycloaddition reaction time is                  1-24 hours.

PT P
PN CN112439392-A
TI Carbon dioxide-responsive chitosan-based copper ion adsorption material prepared by photopolymerization method.
AU HE Y
   LI X
   YAO M
AE UNIV BEIJING CHEM TECHNOLOGY (UYBC-C)
GA 2021243738
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide-responsive chitosan-based copper ion adsorption material prepared by photopolymerization method is claimed. On the basis of retaining a large amount of the original amino groups on the carbon chain of chitosan, graft polymerizable double bonds and hydrophilic groups on the main chain. The material can not only prepare hydrogels by photopolymerization, but also can complex with heavy metal copper ions through the retained amine groups to adsorb copper ions. The material is responsive to carbon dioxide, so as to regulate the complexation and decomplexation of heavy metal copper ions, before carbon dioxide is introduced. The material has a certain adsorption effect on copper ions, and after adsorption, it has a desorption effect on copper ions under the action of carbon dioxide, and the finished material can be reused.
   USE - Used as carbon dioxide-responsive chitosan-based copper ion adsorption material.
   ADVANTAGE - The material: has time and space controllability; prepares adsorbent materials with complicated and fine surface morphology or complicated shapes; and is suitable for applications in various complicated environments.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method specifically comprises modifying chitosan with polymerizable double bonds, uniformly mixing photopolymerization monomer, photoinitiator, crosslinking agent, auxiliary solvent and water according to a certain ratio, ultrasonicating until the solution is uniform and transparent, pouring into the mold, curing under a UV curing lamp with 30-100 mW/cm2 light intensity for 1-20 minutes, and freezing and drying. If the photopolymerization monomer, photoinitiator, and crosslinking agent are water-soluble, there is no need to add a co-solvent, and the polymerizable chitosan, photopolymerization monomer, photoinitiator, crosslinking agent, and water are mixed uniformly in a certain proportion, ultrasonicating until the solution is uniform and transparent, pour it into the mold, and curing with 30-100 mW/cm2 light intensity for 1-20 minutes under an ultraviolet curing lamp, and freezing and drying it. If there are non-water-soluble components in the photopolymerization monomer, photoinitiator, and crosslinking agent, mixing the photopolymerization monomer, photoinitiator, crosslinking agent and cosolvent uniformly, and then mixing with the polymerizable chitosan, adding ultra-pure water together, sonicating until the solution is uniform and transparent, pouring into the mold, curing with 30-100 mW/cm2 light intensity for 1-20 minutes under a UV curing lamp, and freezing and drying. Preferred Components: The initiator is 2-isopropylthioxanthone (ITX), 4-chlorobenzophenone, phenyl bis (2, 4, 6-trimethylbenzoyl) phosphine oxide (photoinitiator 819 ), 2-hydroxy-methylphenylpropane-1-one (1173), 2-hydroxy-4-(2-hydroxyethoxy)-2-methylpropiophenone (2959), photoinitiator TPO, and/or photoinitiator TPO-L. The photopolymerizable monomer is hydroxyethyl acrylate, hydroxyethyl methacrylate, acrylic acid, acrylamide, N-isopropylacrylamide (NIPAM), and/or dimethylaminoethyl methacrylate (DMAEMA). The crosslinking agent is N,N-methylenebisacrylamide, polyethylene glycol diacrylate (PEGDA), tripropylene glycol diacrylate (TPGDA), trimethylolpropane triacrylate, dipentaerythritol pentaacrylate, Tris(2-hydroxyethyl) isocyanate triacrylate, pentaerythritol tetraene acrylate, pentaerythritol pentaacrylate, dipentaerythritol hexaacrylate, trimethylolpropane tetraacrylate, and/or trimethylolpropane triacrylate ester. The co-solvent is ethanol, acetone, dimethylformamide, glycerol, and/or propylene glycol.

PT P
PN KR2157615-B1
TI Production of urea involves reacting diamine compound with carbon dioxide in pyrrolidinone or piperidinone compound as solvent, or reacting primary amine compounds with carbon dioxide in solvent.
AU KIMYONGJIN
   KIM H
AE KOREA INST IND TECHNOLOGY (KTEC-C)
GA 202091149H
AB    NOVELTY - Production of urea (I) or (II) involves reacting a diamine compound (III) with carbon dioxide in a pyrrolidinone or piperidinone compound (IV) as a solvent to obtain compound (I), or reacting primary amine compounds (V) and (VI) with carbon dioxide in compound (IV) as a solvent to obtain compound (II).
   USE - Production of urea.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces urea with yield under mild reaction condition.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Production of urea of formula (I) or (II) involves reacting a diamine compound of formula (III) with carbon dioxide in a pyrrolidinone or piperidinone compound of formula (IV) as a solvent to obtain compound (I), or reacting primary amine compounds of formulae (V) and (VI) with carbon dioxide in compound (IV) as a solvent to obtain compound (II).
   n = 1-4;
   R1,R2,R5-R7 = H or 1-3C alkyl;
   R3,R4 = 1-9C alkyl or 5-7C cycloalkyl;and
   q = 1 or 2.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: Reaction is carried out at 150-250 degrees C and a pressure of 1-20 MPa optionally in presence of a catalyst such as sodium carbonate, potassium carbonate, and/or cesium carbonate. TI Producing catalyst structure useful in gas diffusion electrode for electrochemical carbon dioxide reduction comprises preparing nitrogen-doped carbon nanofiber precursor by electrospinning spinning solution, heat-treating, and dopping.
AU JOO Y C
   KIM J Y
AE UNIV SEOUL NAT R & DB FOUND (USEO-C)
GA 2021407738
AB    NOVELTY - Producing a catalyst structure comprises preparing nitrogen-doped carbon nanofiber precursor by electrospinning spinning solution including copper precursor and nitrogen-containing carbon nanofiber precursor, heat-treating nitrogen-doped carbon nanofiber precursor to prepare nitrogen-doped carbon nanofibers containing copper nanoparticles, performing rapid heat treatment by mixing heteroelement compound with nitrogen-doped carbon nanofibers containing copper nanoparticles for dopping surface of copper nanoparticles with heterogeneous elements in a gaseous state.
   USE - The catalyst structure is useful in gas diffusion electrode for electrochemical carbon dioxide reduction.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst structure has good stability.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The heteroelement compound includes a p-block element of groups 13-17 excluding oxygen. The p-block element is boron, phosphorus, chlorine and fluorine. The copper ions are formed as heteroelements are doped on the surface of the copper nanoparticles. The copper precursor comprises copper acetate, copper nitrate and copper chloride. The nitrogen-containing carbon nanofiber precursor is polyvinylpyrrolidone (PVP), polyaniline (PANi), polypyrrole (Ppy), cyanamide and polybenzimidazole (PBI). Preferred Method: The rapid heat treatment is carried out in an inert gas atmosphere.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN112321616-A; CN112321616-B
TI New supramolecular complex useful in preparing gold nano-particle catalyst for electrocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction.
AU CAO R
   CAO M
   SONG X
AE FUJIAN MATERIAL STRUCTURE INST (CAFU-C)
GA 202115449K
AB    NOVELTY - Supramolecular complex (I) is new.
   USE - (I) is useful in preparing gold nano-particle catalyst for electrocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - (I) utilizes citrulline compound as a protective agent to stabilizes the gold nanoparticle without hindering the active sites on the surface of the nanomaterial.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Supramolecular complex of formula (((H3O)nM)(AuQ4)mx rH2O) (I) is new.
   n = 1-3;
   M = Me10CB(5), CB(6), and CB(8);
   Q = halogen element includes chloro, iodo or bormo;
   m = 1 or 2; and
   r = 3-25.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparation of (I); and
   (2) preparing gold nanoparticle catalyst comprising reducing the raw material containing supramolecular complex to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The preparation of (I), comprises carrying out liquid phase diffusion method of solution I containing cucurbit compound (sic) and the solution II containing gold source to obtain final product. Preferred Components: (I) belongs to the orthorhombic crystal system, monoclinic crystal system or hexagonal crystal system; the unit cell parameter: a is 12-13A, b is 14-30 Angstrom , c is 12-27 Angstrom , Z is 2, 3 or 4, or when the supramolecular complex is of formula ((H3O)(Me10CB(5)))(AuCl4)x xH2O)(II), then (II) belongs to the orthorhombic crystal system, Pnma space group, the unit cell parameters are a is 12.8815(3) Angstrom , b is 14.9476(4) Angstrom , c is 26.9853(7) Angstrom , alpha , beta and gamma is 90 degrees , Z is 4, or when the supramolecular complex is of formula ((H3O)2(CB(6)))(AuCl4)2x yH2O) (III), then (III) belongs to monoclinic crystal system, C12/m1 space group, and then the unit cell parameters are a is 16.1425(9) Angstrom , b is 16.4681(9) Angstrom , c is 12.6844(7) Angstrom , alpha and gamma is 90 degrees , beta is 93.264(2) degrees and Z is 2, or when the supramolecular complex is of formula (((H3O)2(CB(8)))(AuCl4)2x zH2O) (IV), the z value is 3-25; (IV) belongs to the hexagonal crystal system, the R-3 space group, and the unit cell parameters are a is 29.2990(12) Angstrom , b is 29.2990(12) Angstrom , c is 26.3901(14) Angstrom , alpha and beta is 90 degrees , gamma is 120 degrees , and Z is 3. The concentration of the cucurbit compound (sic) in the solution I is 1-10 mg/ml, the concentration of the gold source in the solution II is 0.5-12 mg/ml, the gold source is chloroauric acid, the cucurbit compound (sic) is six-membered ring, eightmembered ring, alkyl substituted five-membered ring; preferably decamethyl five-membered citrulline, six-membered citrulline, and/or eight-membered citrulline, the conditions for the liquid phase diffusion include: adopting an H-shaped tube for liquid phase diffusion, the volume ratio between solution I and solution II is 1:0.5-1.5, the dropping speed of the solution I is 1-3 drops/second, the dripping speed of the solution II is 1-3 drops/second; the time of the liquid phase diffusion is more than 48 hours, the solvent of the solution I comprises water and/or acid; the solvent of the solution II comprises water, preferably, the solvent of the solution I is water, the citrulline compound is five-membered ring substituted by alkyl; or the solvent of the solution I is hydrochloric acid aqueous solution, and the citrulline compound is six-membered citrulline compound, and/or eight-membered citrulline compound. The gold nanoparticle catalyst is Me10CB(5)-Au, CB(6)-Au or CB(8)-Au; the average size of the gold nanoparticle catalyst is 3-5 nm. Preferred Method: The raw materials further include auxiliary agents and reducing agent; the auxiliary agent includes carbon black, the conditions of the reduction include: carrying out ultrasound for 0.5-5 hours, the mass-volume ratio of the supramolecular complex and reducing agent is 3-10:0.48 mg/ml, the reducing agent includes isopropanol; the raw material further includes a solvent, the concentration of the supramolecular complex contained in the raw material is 0.002-0.006 mol/l and the solvent includes water.x = 3-25; andy = 3-25.

PN CN112062947-A; CN112062947-B
TI Preparing caprolactam copolymer by mixing caprolactam with carbon dioxide polymer, heating, adding catalyst and activator, reacting, cooling, adding good solvent to dissolve, then precipitating in poor solvent, centrifuging, and drying.
AU TAO Y
   CHEN J
   WANG X
AE CHANGCHUN APPLIED CHEM INST CHINESE ACAD (CAAC-C)
GA 2020C6448Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing caprolactam copolymer involves: (1) uniformly mixing caprolactam with carbon dioxide polymer, heating to dissolve, adding a catalyst or adding a catalyst and an activator, and then reacting; and (2) stopping the reaction, cooling to room temperature, adding a good solvent to dissolve, then precipitating in a poor solvent, centrifuging to remove the supernatant, and drying.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing caprolactam copolymer.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple and economical preparation of caprolactam copolymer, which has excellent biodegradability and maintains the original excellent thermal and mechanical properties of nylon 6 material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst is an organic acid reagent, a pyridine reagent, a carbene reagent, a guanidine reagent, an amidine reagent, a phosphazene reagent, a urea reagent, an alkali metal, an alkali metal oxide, an alkali metal hydroxide, an alkali metal hydride, an alkali metal alkoxide, a rare earth metal, an alkaline earth metal oxide, an alkaline earth metal hydroxide, an alkaline earth metal hydride and/or an alkaline earth metal alkoxide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The activator comprises an N-acyl caprolactam compound of formula (I). The mass ratio of caprolactam to carbon dioxide polymer is 100:(1-90). The molar ratio of the catalyst to caprolactam is (0.1-50):100. The molar ratio of the activator to caprolactam is (0-10):100. The good solvent is formic acid, m-cresol, trichloromethane, carbon tetrachloride, chlorobenzene, o-dichlorobenzene, N-methylpyrrolidone, N,N-dimethylformamide, N,N-dimethylacetamide, dimethyl sulfoxide, trifluoroethanol, hexafluoroisopropanol, 1-ethyl-3-methylimidazolium bromide, methanol, ethanol and/or benzenediol. The poor solvent is diethyl ether, petroleum ether, n-hexane, cyclohexane, benzene and/or toluene.R = methyl, ethyl, phenyl, 4-trifluoromethylphenyl, 4-tert-butylphenyl, trifluoromethyl or trichloromethyl.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Conditions: The temperature for heating and dissolving step is 80-250 degrees C. The reacting step is carried out at 100-280 degrees C for 0.5-24 hours. The drying step is carried out at 25-150 degrees C for 2-24 hours. Preferred Components: The carbon dioxide polymer is a copolymer product of carbon dioxide, propylene oxide, ethylene oxide, epichlorohydrin, 1-butylene oxide, 2-butylene oxide, epoxy cyclohexane, epoxy cyclopentane, glycidyl methacrylate, methyl glycidyl ether and/or phenyl glycidyl ether. Preferred Properties: The carbon dioxide polymer has a molecular weight of 2000-200000 and a carbonate content of 1-50%.

PN CN110452945-A
TI Producing erythromycin involves using glucose as carbon source and amino acids as nitrogen source, fermenting and cultivating red sugar polysaccharides, and adding glucose, and ammonium sulfate to maintain carbon dioxide release rate.
AU CHU J
   YANG H
   LIAO J
   HONG M
   HANG H
   ZHUANG Y
AE UNIV EAST CHINA SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYEC-C)
GA 201997996Q
AB    NOVELTY - Producing erythromycin involves using glucose as a carbon source and amino acids as a nitrogen source, then fermenting and cultivating red sugar polysaccharides, and has a residual sugar content of less than 3-6 g/L, further adding glucose, and ammonium sulfate at the same time to maintain the carbon dioxide release rate in the fermentation broth at 7-10 mmol/(Lx hour).
   USE - Method for producing erythromycin.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to produce erythromycin in an efficient manner in a suitable fermentation stage and the method is environment-friendly, and requires low culturing cost.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred conditions: The amount of residual sugar is less than 3 to 5 g/L, glucose is added to maintain the amount of residual sugar to 3-5 g/L and in the process after adding ammonium sulfate, the carbon dioxide release rate in the fermentation broth is maintained at 7-8 mmol/(L x hour). The content of glucose is 19-25 g/L, particularly 20-24 g/L during initial fermentation. The pH value of the culture medium is 7 plus minus 0.2. The method further involves separating erythromycin from the fermentation broth after the fermentation is completed. Preferred Composition: The amino acids include 0.4-0.75 g/L alanine, 0.4-0.6 g/L arginine, 0.5-0.7 g/L cysteine, and 0.45-0.65 g/L serine. The medium during the initial fermentation further comprises 0.8-2g/L dipotassium hydrogen phosphate, 1-1.5g/L potassium dihydrogen phosphate 0.4-2g/L, particularly 0.8-1.2g/L magnesium sulfate 0.5-2g/L, particularly 0.8-1.5g/L trisodium citrate 1-3g/L, particularly 1.5-2.5g/L cobalt chloride 0.006-0.015g/L, particularly 0.008-0.01g/L boratex, 0.002-0.01g/L, particularly 0.004-0.008g/L ferric trichloride, 0.004-0.01g/L, particularly 0.005-0.008g/L copper chloride, 0.0001-0.0004g/L, particularly 0.0001-0.0004g/L ammonium molybdate. Preferred Component: The erythromycin is erythromycin A.

PT P
PN CN109629360-A; CN109629360-B
TI Method for reinforcing clay roadbed by solid-carbon bacteria mineralization by cultivating bacteria of genus Streptomyces faecalis, pouring quartz sand into clay, adding bacterial liquid, slaked lime and foamed carbon dioxide, and stirring.
AU YU X
   YU C
   CAI X
   DU T
AE UNIV WENZHOU (UNWZ-C)
   UNIV WENZHOU (UNWZ-C)
GA 201938548E
AB    NOVELTY - Method for reinforcing clay roadbed by solid-carbon bacteria mineralization involves (i) cultivating the bacteria of the genus Streptomyces faecalis to obtain bacterial liquid, (ii) pouring quartz sand into clay constituting roadbed, stirring and mixing, adding bacterial liquid, slaked lime and foamed carbon dioxide to the mixture of clay and quartz sand, and fully stirring, and (iii) leaving still to form carbonized solidification.
   USE - Method for reinforcing clay roadbed by solid-carbon bacteria mineralization.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is environmentally-friendly, convenient and fast to operate, and has excellent reinforcement effect.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of S. faecalis, slaked lime, foamed carbon dioxide and the mixture of clay and quartz sand is 1:0.5:1:2000. Preferred Method: The foamed carbon dioxide in the step (ii) is injected into the roadbed soil on first, third, seventh and fourteen days, and the clean water is sprayed after each injection to wet the soil. After the injection of foamed carbon dioxide on the third day, the surface is flattened by a rolling machine, and a layer of fine bacterial liquid, slaked lime, foamed carbon dioxide, and a mixture of clay and quartz sand are laid according to the surface level of the pavement. After leaving still for four days, the foamed carbon dioxide is injected, the surface is flattened by a rolling machine, and a layer of fine bacterial liquid, slaked lime, foamed carbon dioxide, and a mixture of clay and quartz sand are paved according to the surface level of the pavement. After leaving still for seven days, the foamed carbon dioxide is injected and repeatedly pressed by a roller machine.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); G04 (Miscellaneous compositions - including luminescent and tenebrescent materials, de-icing/de-misting compositions, mastics, heat transfer compositions and aerosol-can filling mixtures (C09H).); Q41 (Road, rail, bridge construction (E01))

PN CN108977839-A; CN108977839-B
TI Synthesis of thiazole-4-carboxylic acid involves adding 4-bromothiazole to quaternary ammonium salt solution to prepare electrolyte solution, passing current to electrolyte to carry out electrocarboxylation reaction, and hydrolyzing.
AU CHEN B
   ZHANG Y
   SONG P
   GAO C
   YU S
   XUE S
   ZHANG M
   PI L
AE UNIV LIAOCHENG (UNLC-C)
   UNIV LIAOCHENG (UNLC-C)
GA 2018A2585D
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesis of thiazole-4-carboxylic acid involves adding 4-bromothiazole to quaternary ammonium salt solution to prepare an electrolyte solution, passing current to electrolyte to carry out an electrocarboxylation reaction in carbon dioxide atmosphere, and hydrolyzing with acetonitrile aqueous solution. The quaternary ammonium salt is tetraethylammonium chloride, tetraethylammonium bromide, tetraethylammonium iodide, tetraethyltetrafluoroborate, tetrabutylammonium chloride, tetrabutylammonium bromide or tetrabutylammonium iodide. The solvent of the electrolyte is N,N-dimethylformamide or acetonitrile.
   USE - Synthesis of thiazole-4-carboxylic acid.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, mild and easy, and utilizes carbon dioxide provide a way to solve greenhouse gases.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The electrocarboxylation is carried out using anode and cathode. The anode is magnesium. The cathode is stainless steel, copper, silver, platinum, nickel, titanium or glassy carbon.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of 4-bromothiazole to quaternary ammonium salt is 1:1. The molar ratio of quaternary ammonium salt to solvent is 1:129. The molar ratio of 4-bromothiazole to amount of electricity is 1:1-5. The volume ratio of acetonitrile to water in acetonitrile aqueous solution is 0.9-1.1:1. Preferred Process: The method further involves injecting thiazole-4-carboxylic acid to mobile phase solution and detecting by HPLC. The mobile phase solution is a mixture of acetonitrile, trifluoroacetic acid and water in a volume ratio of 15:0.1:85.

TI Preparing cyclic carbonate by copper-based catalyst comprises e.g. reacting carbon dioxide and epoxide in conjugated copper porphyrin bipyridyl/conjugated copper porphyrin bipyrrole microporous polymer material.
AU ZHANG X
   CHEN Z
   LI Y
   CAO Y
   WANG Y
AE HENAN INST ENG (HEAN-C)
GA 201830087F
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing cyclic carbonate by copper-based catalyst comprises reacting carbon dioxide and epoxide in conjugated copper porphyrin bipyridyl/conjugated copper porphyrin bipyrrole microporous polymer material as copper-based catalyst and tetra-n-butylammonium bromide as co-catalyst at room temperature for 12-48 hours and pressure without solvent, and catalyzing to obtain final product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing cyclic carbonate by copper-based catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has simple process, low cost, high catalytic activity, mild reaction conditions, and stable structure performance; and is easy to recycle, easy to operate, and suitable for large scale industrial production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The conjugated copper porphyrin bipyridyl microporous polymer material prepared by either carrying out Scholl coupling reaction of copper tetraphenylporphyrin and 2,2-dipyridyl as monomer under anhydrous aluminum chloride catalyst, and the conjugated copper porphyrin bipyridyl microporous polymer material is of formula (I); or carrying out Scholl coupling reaction of copper tetraphenylporphyrin and pyrrole as monomer under anhydrous aluminum chloride catalyst, and the conjugated copper porphyrin bipyridyl microporous polymer material is of formula (II). Preferred Components: The epoxy compound is propylene oxide, butylene oxide, epichlorohydrin, epibromohydrin, styrene oxide, phenyl glycidyl ether or cyclohexene oxide. The addition amount of heterogeneous catalyst active component is 0.2-0.5 mol% of the epoxy compound. The addition amount of co-catalyst is 3.6-7.2 mol% of the epoxide.

The LNG vaporization apparatus cools                to a low temperature, recovers carbon dioxide gas,                and makes it flow out after cooling the pre-cooling                combustion exhaust gas and condensing the saturated                steam. The carbon-dioxide-gas sending apparatus                sends out the recovered carbon dioxide gas that                flows out of the LNG vaporization apparatus.

TI Two-camera power generation system based on            supercritical carbon dioxide, has heat exchanger, power            mechanism, water turbine, generator, water storage            tank, and circulation path.
AU WANG T
   WANG J
AE SHAANXI HANHUA JIUCHUANG NEW ENERGY TECHNOLOGY CO              LTD (SHAA-Non-standard)
GA 2022C83297
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a heat exchanger (1) for                providing heat to the liquid carbon dioxide. A                power mechanism (2) filled with working fluid                inside and is used for rapid gasification, volume                expansion, and production of large amounts of                supercritical carbon dioxide pressure. The working                fluid is pure water or hydraulic oil. The water                turbine (3) is used to achieve work by the working                fluid of the power mechanism. A generator (4) is                used for turning the turbine. The mechanical energy                of water turbine is converted into the electrical                energy of the generator. A water storage tank (14)                is used for replenishing the loss of working fluid                during operation, and the temperature of the                replenishing working fluid. The heat exchanger and                the power mechanism are in a thorough connection.                The power mechanism, the water turbine, and the                water storage tank are connected to form a                circulation passage. The generator is electrically                connected to the water turbine.
   USE - Two-camera power generation system based on                supercritical carbon dioxide and carbon dioxide                utilization technique for large, medium and small                various enterprises.
   ADVANTAGE - The system has wider adaptability, and is                suitable for large, medium and small various                enterprises, and has better promotion. The system                solves the potential safety hazard of the existing                utilization mode, the problem of poor carbon                dioxide sequeebility, providing a high security,                high replicability, small carbon dioxide                seperability, even no need to seal the carbon                dioxide comprehensive utilization method. The water                storage tank and the power mechanism are provided                with a circulating pump for water pressure                compensation when the water pressure is                insufficient. The carbon dioxide is effectively                used, so as to realize the effective reduction of                carbon-di-oxide (CO2) sequegration.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - AN INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                generating method of two-camera generating system                based on supercritical CO.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                two-camera power generation system. (Drawing                includes a non-English language text)

   2Power mechanism

TI Multi-functional culture device capable of remotely monitoring comprises upper cover, fuselage, micro mobile module, bottom case, and control and communication module, upper cover includes upper cover housing and liquid crystal screen.
AU FENG K
   ZHANG K
   ZHANG X
AE FENG K (FENG-Individual)
GA 2019146540
AB    NOVELTY - Multi-functional culture device comprises upper cover (100), fuselage (200), micro mobile module (300), bottom case (400), and control and communication module (500). The upper cover includes upper cover housing and a liquid crystal screen disposed on the upper cover housing. The body includes a body casing, a culture vessel, a temperature and humidity sensor, an oxygen sensor, a carbon dioxide sensor, a humidifier, and a heater, ultraviolet light, alarm and solenoid valve. The fuselage casing is an open structure in which the interior is divided into two upper and lower regions, the culture vessel, the temperature and humidity sensor, the oxygen sensor, the carbon dioxide sensor, and the humidifier, a heater, an ultraviolet lamp, and an alarm are disposed in the upper region. The lower area is provided with a gas injection pipeline connecting the oxygen cylinder and the carbon dioxide gas cylinder with the upper region.
   USE - Used as multi-functional culture device.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Multi-functional culture device comprises upper cover (100), fuselage (200), micro mobile module (300), bottom case (400), and control and communication module (500). The upper cover includes upper cover housing and a liquid crystal screen disposed on the upper cover housing. The body includes a body casing, a culture vessel, a temperature and humidity sensor, an oxygen sensor, a carbon dioxide sensor, a humidifier, and a heater, ultraviolet light, alarm and solenoid valve. The fuselage casing is an open structure in which the interior is divided into two upper and lower regions, the culture vessel, the temperature and humidity sensor, the oxygen sensor, the carbon dioxide sensor, and the humidifier, a heater, an ultraviolet lamp, and an alarm are disposed in the upper region. The lower area is provided with a gas injection pipeline connecting the oxygen cylinder and the carbon dioxide gas cylinder with the upper region, and the gas injection pipeline is provided with a solenoid valve. The microscopic movement module is disposed in a lower region, and the microscopic movement module includes an X-axis motor, X-axis slide rail, X-axis slider, and Y-axis, motor, Y-axis slide, Y-axis slide, Z-axis motor, Z-axis slide, Z-axis slide, microscope and video head. The Y-axis slide rail is fixed on the X-axis slide block, and the Z-axis slide rail is fixed on the Y-axis slide block. The microscope is fixed to a Z-axis slider fixed to the head end of the microscope, the X-axis motor, and the Y-axis motor, when the Z-axis motor rotates, the X-axis slider, the Y-axis slider, and the Z-axis slider are driven on the corresponding X-axis slide rail and the Y-axis slide rail and moving on the Z-axis slide rail to realize that the microscope and the video head can move in three directions of XYZ. The control and communication module is disposed in a lower area, and the control and communication module includes a controller and a communication end. The communication end is respectively connected with the temperature and humidity sensor, the oxygen sensor, the carbon dioxide sensor and the video head, and simultaneously sends the internal parameters of the culture device to the liquid crystal display for display and after being sent to the remote controller, and it is displayed at the terminal. The controller is respectively connected to the liquid crystal panel and the communication terminal according to the control parameters set by the liquid crystal panel and the control set by the terminal transmitted by the remote controller. The parameters control X-axis motor, Y-axis motor, Z-axis motor, humidifier, heater, video head, solenoid valve, ultraviolet lamp and alarm.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the multi-functional culture device.

   Fuselage (200)
   Micro mobile module (300)
   Bottom case (400)
   Control and communication module (500)

TI Deep-portion coal bed gas waste well and            long-stopping well reproducing method, involves            stopping discharging work of deep portion coal bed            methane waste well and long stopping well for plugging            waste well when carbon dioxide concentration in            produced gas is more than specific value.
AU WANG Z
   ZHOU X
   REN H
   GUO Z
   XIANG G
   ZHOU P
   XU H
   WU Z
   LONG C
   ZHAO L
   SUN Z
AE GUIZHOU OIL & GAS EXPLORATION DEV ENG (GUIZ-Non-standard)
GA 2022A2486E
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves arranging a multi-branch                horizontal well (3) with three-opening well body                structures on a ground in deep portion coal bed                methane waste well and long stopping well area                range. A water injection pump rotary valve (10) is                opened for injecting water in a liquid storage tank                (12) into the multi-branch horizontal well through                a water injection pump (11). The water injection                pump rotary valve is closed for opening a liquid                carbon dioxide injection pump rotary valve (13) and                injecting liquid carbon dioxide in a liquid carbon                dioxide storage tank (15) into the multi-branch                horizontal well through a liquid carbon dioxide                injection pump (14). Liquid carbon dioxide is                continuously injected into the multi-branch                horizontal well and started a drainage and                production equipment of a deep portion coal bed gas                waste well and long stopping well (1) for entering                into follow-up carbon dioxide injection                stage.
   USE - Deep-portion coal bed gas waste well and                long-stopping well reproducing method.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables providing the multi-branch                horizontal well and the deep portion coal bed                methane waste well and long stopping well with no                high-seepage channel to prolong service life of                coal bed gas well for 8-10 years, generating                obvious economic and environmental benefits,                increasing coal gas resource recovery rate in well                control range, avoiding premature scrapping of coal                bed methane wells and stop of pumping.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Deep portion coal bed methane waste well and                long stopping well liquid level is gradually                decreased to position in front of liquid carbon                dioxide gas waste well and long-stop well liquid                level. The deep portion coal bed methane waste well                and long stopping well is reproduced. Carbon                dioxide concentration in deep portion coal bed gas                waste well and long stopping well production gas is                periodically monitored after stabilizing deep                portion coal bed methane waste well and long                stopping well daily gas production. Discharging                work of the deep portion coal bed methane waste                well and long stopping well is stopped for plugging                a waste well when the carbon dioxide concentration                in produced gas is more than 20 percentage.

PN WO2021247829-A1; US2021380408-A1
TI Operating a dry reformer system by converting methane and carbon dioxide via reforming catalyst in dry reformer vessel into syngas, discharging the syngas, and increasing concentration of carbon dioxide in the feed.
AU AL-SOLAMI B
   LEE K
AE SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   ARAMCO SERVICES CO (SAOI-C)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
GA 2021E04208
AB    NOVELTY - A dry reformer system (100) is operated by providing feed (118) comprising methane and carbon dioxide to a dry reformer vessel; converting the methane and carbon dioxide via reforming catalyst (106) in the dry reformer vessel (102) into syngas (104) at an operating pressure in the dry reformer vessel of greater than or equal to 20 bar and at an operating temperature in the dry reformer vessel of less than or equal to 900 degrees C, the syngas comprising hydrogen and carbon monoxide; discharging the syngas from the dry reformer vessel; and increasing concentration of carbon dioxide in the feed in response to solid-carbon formation in the dry reformer vessel.
   USE - Operating a dry reformer system (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The concentration of carbon dioxide in the feed is increased to inhibit, suppress, reduce, or prevent coke formation in the dry reformer vessel. To inhibit solid-carbon formation and avoid unwanted deactivation of the reforming catalyst, the method may increase carbon dioxide amount to 66-75 vol.% in the feed to give stable activity of the catalyst and operation durability under dry reforming reaction conditions including elevated pressure. The method may operate at higher pressure without significant coke formation by dynamically varying carbon dioxide content in the feed to the dry reformer in response to detection of presence of coke formation in the dry reformer.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) operating a dry reformer system which involves providing feed comprising methane and carbon dioxide to a dry reformer vessel; converting the methane and the carbon dioxide via reforming catalyst in the dry reformer vessel into syngas at a pressure of greater than or equal to 20 bar and at a temperature of less than or equal to 850 degrees C, the syngas comprising hydrogen and carbon monoxide; discharging the syngas from the dry reformer vessel; and increasing concentration of carbon dioxide in the feed to greater than or equal to 66 vol.% of the feed to inhibit coke formation in the dry reformer vessel; and
   (2) dry reformer system (100) comprising a dry reformer (102) vessel comprising an inlet to receive feed (118) comprising methane and carbon dioxide; a reforming catalyst (106) to convert the methane and the carbon dioxide into syngas (104) at an operating pressure of greater than or equal to 20 bar and an operating temperature of less than or equal to 900 degrees C; and an outlet to discharge the syngas into a discharge conduit, where the syngas comprises hydrogen and carbon monoxide; a flow control valve (120) to control flow rate of carbon dioxide added to the feed; and a control system (110) to increase a flow-rate set point of the flow control valve in response to solid-carbon formation in the dry reformer vessel.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block flow diagram of the dry reformer system.
   Dry reformer system (100)
   Dry reformer (102)
   Syngas (104)
   Catalyst (106)
   Control system (110)
   Feed (118)
   Flow control valve (120)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The feed comprises less than or equal to 1 vol.% oxygen, where additional oxygen is not provided to the dry reformer vessel, the feed comprises less than or equal to 1 mol.% water, and the steam is not provided to the dry reformer vessel. The feed comprises natural gas comprising the methane. Preferred Process: Reforming the methane comprises dry reforming, and where the syngas discharged from the dry reformer vessel comprises a molar ratio of hydrogen to carbon monoxide of 0.4-1.05. The method comprises adding supplemental hydrogen to the syngas discharged from the dry reformer vessel to increase the molar ratio of hydrogen to carbon monoxide in the syngas of 1.5-2.5.

PN WO2021076670-A1; AU2020366355-A1; CA3154766-A1; AU2020366355-A8; EP4045183-A1
TI Preparing rigid porous polymeric carbon sorbent,            comprises mixing polymer with additive to form mixture,            and heating the mixture to form porous polymeric carbon            sorbent via chemical activation.
AU TOUR J M
   ALGOZEEB W
   SAVAS P E
   KITTRELL C
AE UNIV RICE WILLIAM MARSH (RICV-C)
   UNIV RICE WILLIAM MARSH (RICV-C)
   UNIV RICE WILLIAM MARSH (RICV-C)
GA 202141721M
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing (M1) rigid porous polymeric carbon                sorbent, comprises: (a) mixing polymer with                additive to form mixture, where polymer has polymer                chain order comprising long range order and short                range order as detected by powder X-ray diffraction                (XRD), and the additive is activation reagent                comprising metal hydroxide, metal oxalate, metal                acetate and/or metal acetylacetonate; and (b)                heating the mixture to form porous polymeric carbon                sorbent via chemical activation, where (i) the                chemical activation results in a loss of the long                range order and short range order of the polymer as                detected by powder XRD such that the porous                polymeric carbon sorbent material is rigid porous                polymeric carbon sorbent, (ii) rigid porous                polymeric carbon sorbent is operable for capturing                carbon dioxide under pressure of 0.75-5 atmosphere,                and (iii) the rigid polymeric carbon sorbent has                selectivity for capturing carbon dioxide over                nitrogen gas.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing rigid                porous polymeric carbon sorbent.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple to deal with waste                product of this reaction; used can be chemical                recycling of plastics via thermal cracking of                plastic wastes to get oligomers, monomers and                porous sorbent. The carbon sorbent: has high                porosity, electrical conductivity, high capacity                when used as anode or cathode for batteries,                flexibility which changes in storage capacity; and                is excellent carbon dioxide sorbent.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing (M1) rigid porous polymeric carbon                sorbent, comprises: (a) mixing polymer with                additive to form mixture, where polymer has polymer                chain order comprising long range order and short                range order as detected by powder X-ray diffraction                (XRD), and the additive is activation reagent                comprising metal hydroxide, metal oxalate, metal                acetate and/or metal acetylacetonate; and (b)                heating the mixture to form porous polymeric carbon                sorbent via chemical activation, where (i) the                chemical activation results in a loss of the long                range order and short range order of the polymer as                detected by powder XRD such that the porous                polymeric carbon sorbent material is rigid porous                polymeric carbon sorbent, (ii) rigid porous                polymeric carbon sorbent is operable for capturing                carbon dioxide under pressure of 0.75-5 atmosphere,                and (iii) the rigid polymeric carbon sorbent has                selectivity for capturing carbon dioxide over                nitrogen gas at least 40:1 at 0.15 bar of carbon                dioxide in 0.85 bar of nitrogen gas at 23 degrees                C. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) rigid porous polymeric carbon sorbent that                is operable to capture greater than or equal to 15                wt.% carbon dioxide from a post-combustion carbon                dioxide emission outlet gas at 25 degrees C and at                1 atmosphere and the rigid polymeric carbon sorbent                has a selectivity for capturing carbon dioxide over                nitrogen gas at least 40:1 at 0.15 bar of carbon                dioxide in 0.85 bar of nitrogen gas at 23 degrees                C; and
   (2) method (M2) comprising (a) selecting rigid                porous polymeric carbon sorbent having a                selectivity for capturing carbon dioxide over                nitrogen gas at least 40:1 at 0.15 bar of carbon                dioxide in 0.85 bar of nitrogen gas at 23 degrees                C, and (b) utilizing the rigid porous polymeric                carbon sorbent to capture greater than or equal to                15 wt.% carbon dioxide from post-combustion carbon                dioxide emission outlet gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: In the method (m1):                  the polymer comprises high-density polyethylene                  (HDPE), low-density polyethylene (LDPE) and/or                  polypropylene (PP), PVC, nylon, melamine mixed                  with HDPE, melamine mixed with LDPE, melamine                  mixed with PP, melamine-formaldehyde resins,                  polyethylenimine (PEI), PEI mixed with HDPE,                  polyurethanes (PU), polyacrylonitrile, (PAN)                  and/or polyethylene terephthalate (PET), nitrogen                  atoms and waste plastic source; the activation                  reagent is acetate salt of potassium or calcium,                  preferably potassium acetate or calcium acetate;                  weight ratio of the additive and the polymer in                  the mixture is 0.5:1-5:1, preferably 2:1-4:1; and                  the post-combustion carbon dioxide emission                  outlet gas is flue gas. In the rigid porous                  polymeric carbon sorbent: the rigid porous                  polymeric carbon sorbent comprises polymer that                  has no polymeric long range order and no                  polymeric short range order detectable by powder                  X-ray diffraction; the rigid porous polymeric                  carbon sorbent comprises pores having an average                  pore size of 2-100 A, preferably 5-20 A; the                  rigid porous polymeric carbon sorbent is operable                  for releasing the carbon dioxide captured from                  post-combustion carbon dioxide emission outlet                  gas by heating the rigid porous polymeric carbon                  sorbent to at most 110 degrees C at about 1                  atmosphere pressure, preferably at most 75                  degrees C at 1 atmosphere pressure; the rigid                  porous polymeric carbon sorbent is operable for                  capturing greater than or equal to 18 wt.% carbon                  dioxide from post combustion carbon dioxide                  emission outlet gas at 25 degrees C and at 5                  atmosphere, preferably greater than or equal to                  100 wt.% carbon dioxide from post combustion                  carbon dioxide emission outlet gas at 25 degrees                  C at 300 atmosphere; and the rigid porous                  polymeric carbon sorbent comprises nitrogen                  atoms. Preferred Method: In the method (M1): the                  rigid polymeric carbon sorbent has a selectivity                  for capturing carbon dioxide over nitrogen gas at                  least 300:1 in 0.15 bar of carbon dioxide in 0.85                  bar nitrogen gas at 23 degrees C; heating is                  performed at 550-700 degrees C, preferably                  575-600 degrees C; the rigid porous polymeric                  carbon sorbent is operable for capturing carbon                  dioxide from a post-combustion carbon dioxide                  emission outlet gas; the rigid porous polymeric                  carbon sorbent is operable for capturing greater                  than or equal to 15 wt.% carbon dioxide from a                  post-combustion carbon dioxide emission outlet                  gas at 25 degrees C at 1 atmosphere; the rigid                  porous polymeric carbon sorbent is operable for                  releasing the carbon dioxide captured from a                  post-combustion carbon dioxide emission outlet                  gas by heating the rigid porous polymeric carbon                  sorbent to at most 110 degrees C at 1 atmosphere,                  preferably at most 75 degrees C at 1 atmosphere;                  the rigid porous polymeric carbon sorbent is                  operable for capturing more than 18 wt.% carbon                  dioxide from a post-combustion carbon dioxide                  emission outlet gas at 25 degrees C and at most 5                  atmosphere, preferably 100 wt.% carbon dioxide                  from a post-combustion carbon dioxide emission                  outlet gas at 25 degrees C and at most 300                  atmosphere; further comprises controlling pore                  size of the rigid porous polymeric carbon sorbent                  by controlling pressure during the step of                  heating; and heating the mixture is performed at                  a near vacuum pressure of at least 0.01 bars. In                  the rigid porous polymeric carbon sorbent: the                  rigid polymeric carbon sorbent has a selectivity                  for capturing carbon dioxide over nitrogen gas of                  at least 250:1 at 0.15 bar of carbon dioxide in                  0.85 bar of nitrogen gas at 23 degrees C. In the                  method (M2): the post-combustion carbon dioxide                  emission outlet gas is a flue gas; the carbon                  dioxide is captured from post-combustion carbon                  dioxide emission outlet gas at room temperature                  at around 25 degrees C under atmospheric                  pressure; the rigid porous polymeric carbon                  sorbent is utilized to capture greater than or                  equal to 18 wt.% carbon dioxide from                  post-combustion carbon dioxide emission outlet                  gas; the carbon dioxide is captured from                  post-combustion carbon dioxide emission outlet                  gas at a pressure that is at most 5 atmosphere;                  the rigid porous polymeric carbon sorbent is                  utilized to capture greater than or equal to 100                  wt.% carbon dioxide from post-combustion carbon                  dioxide emission outlet gas; the carbon dioxide                  is captured from post-combustion carbon dioxide                  emission outlet gas under pressure that is at                  most 300 atmosphere; further comprises releasing                  carbon dioxide captured from post combustion                  carbon dioxide emission outlet by heating the                  rigid porous polymeric carbon sorbent; the carbon                  dioxide is released by heating the rigid porous                  polymeric carbon sorbent to at most 110 degrees                  C, preferably 75 degrees C; further comprises                  repeating the capture and release of the carbon                  dioxide by the rigid porous polymeric carbon                  sorbent for at least 1000 cycles, preferably at                  least 100000 cycles; the rigid porous polymeric                  carbon sorbent having a selectivity for capturing                  carbon dioxide over nitrogen gas of at least 70:1                  at 0.15 bar of carbon dioxide in 0.85 bar of                  nitrogen gas at 23 degrees C; the rigid porous                  polymeric carbon sorbent comprises nitrogen                  atoms; and the rigid porous polymeric carbon                  sorbent having a selectivity for capturing carbon                  dioxide over nitrogen gas of at least 300:1 at                  least 40:1 at 0.15 bar of carbon dioxide in 0.85                  bar of nitrogen gas at 23 degrees C.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Purification of sewage involves adding carbon dioxide gas responsive polymer to sewage, supplying carbon dioxide gas, adsorbing negatively charged particles or suspended solids in sewage by bicarbonate, supplying nitrogen, and recycling.
AU LIU H
   YANG X
   LI Z
AE UNIV SHAANXI SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYSK-C)
GA 202111805W
AB    NOVELTY - Purification of sewage involves adding carbon dioxide gas responsive polymer to sewage, supplying carbon dioxide gas which responds to tertiary amine group of the polymer to be converted into bicarbonate, adsorbing negatively charged particles or suspended solids in the sewage by bicarbonate, settling negatively charged particles or suspended solids, leaving still, removing supernatant liquid, supplying air or nitrogen, where carbon dioxide gas responds to the polymer to restore its neutral state, releasing adsorbed negatively charged particles or flocculant suspended material, recycling the polymer, and repeating the above process to purify other sewage.
   USE - Purification of sewage.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient and economical purification of sewage.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The small molecule is 3-diethylaminopropylamine or 1-(3-aminopropyl)imidazole.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The polymer is high-molecular weight polymer or small molecule containing carbon dioxide responsive groups grafted with water-soluble natural polymer. The water-soluble natural polymer comprises nanocellulose, starch, sodium alginate and guar gum. The high-molecular weight polymer is homopolymer or copolymer of diethylaminoethyl methacrylate, N,N-dimethylaminoethyl methacrylate and N-amidinododecylacrylamide.

PN WO2020225346-A1; FR3095817-A1
TI New bacterium that naturally oxidizes hydrogen and is genetically modified to overexpress gene coding for lactate dehydrogenase and to inhibit expression of lut operon, for producing lactate from carbon dioxide.
AU BOISART C
   CHABOT N
   DE LA MARE M
AE ENOBRAQ (ENOB-Non-standard)
   ENOBRAQ (ENOB-Non-standard)
GA 2020B0346S
AB    NOVELTY - Bacterium that naturally oxidizes hydrogen and is genetically modified for producing lactate from carbon dioxide, is new, where the bacterium is genetically modified to overexpress at least one gene coding for a lactate dehydrogenase and to inhibit the expression of a lut operon.
   USE - The genetically modified bacterium is useful for producing lactate from carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The genetically modified bacterium allows to suppress the consumption of the lactate produced.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for producing lactate from carbon dioxide, comprising cultivating the modified bacterium in the presence of carbon dioxide as the only carbon source, then recovering the lactate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preparation: No preparation method is given. Preferred Components: The inhibition of the expression of a lut operon comprises: the inhibition of the expression of at least one lutA, lutB or lutC gene of the lut operon; and a deletion of the lut operon. When the bacterium comprises several lut operons, the expression of at least two operons is inhibited. The bacterium is: genetically modified to overexpress at least one gene encoding an endogenous lactate dehydrogenase; genetically modified to overexpress at least one gene encoding an exogenous lactate dehydrogenase, preferably derived from a bacterium (preferred), a fungus, a yeast or a mammal; and also genetically modified to at least partially inhibit at least one pyruvate degradation pathway competing with the lactate synthesis pathway. The expression of at least one gene encoding a lactate ferricytochrome C reductase is at least partially inhibited. The bacterium is Cupriavidus sp., e.g. Cupriavidus necator (preferred), Hydrogenobacter sp., e.g. Hydrogenobacter thermophilus, Rhodococcus sp., e.g. Rhodococcus opacus, or Pseudomonas sp., e.g. Pseudomonas hydrogenothermophila.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Recovery of lithium from used carbon dioxide adsorbent, for use as battery raw material, involves pulverizing adsorbent containing lithium carbonate, zeolite and organics, calcining, dissolving in water to separate zeolite, and evaporating.
AU JEYONG Y
   KIM S
   JOO H
AE UNIV SEOUL NAT R & DB FOUND (USEO-C)
GA 201842187F
AB    NOVELTY - Recovery of lithium from used carbon dioxide adsorbent involves pulverizing used carbon dioxide adsorbent comprising lithium carbonate, zeolite and an organic material, calcining the adsorbent to remove organic material, dissolving the calcined adsorbent in water to separate the zeolite and evaporating water to obtain lithium carbonate powder.
   USE - Recovery of lithium from used carbon dioxide adsorbent, for use as raw material for secondary battery. Uses include but are not limited to hybrid vehicle, electric vehicle, cellular phone, and notebook computer.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient and economical recovery of lithium from used carbon dioxide adsorbent with high lithium recovery rate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: After the separation of zeolite, the resultant adsorbent is subjected to electrolysis in a dissolution tank in which a cation exchange membrane is provided, such that the lithium ions are moved through the cation exchange membrane and combined with hydroxide ion on other side of the dissolution tank to produce lithium hydroxide solution, and water is evaporated to obtain lithium hydroxide powder. Preferred Composition: The concentration of aqueous solution in which calcined adsorbent is dissolved, during the zeolite separation step is 20-25 g/L.

TI Method for separating flow rich in carbon dioxide            by distillation to produce liquid or gaseous carbon            dioxide, involves cooling portion of fraction of flow            liquid in heat exchange unit after being pressurized in            pump.

   LECLERC M
AE AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
   AIR LIQUIDE SA (AIRL-C)
GA 2022D76305
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves cooling a portion (5) of                fraction (3) of flow liquid (1) in a heat exchange                unit (10) after having been pressurized in a pump                (P1). The flow liquid is sent at a head of a column                and at an intermediate level of the column. A part                of the pressurized liquid in the pump is mixed with                the fraction. The flow liquid cooled in the                exchange unit is allowed to enter a hot end of the                exchange unit in gaseous form. The fraction of the                flow liquid forming the product is pressurized in                the pump.
   USE - Method for separating flow rich in carbon                dioxide by distillation to produce liquid or                gaseous carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables carrying out reboiling of a                column by heating and vaporizing bottom liquid and                by injecting the gas in same proportions so as to                guarantee better separation of flow rich in carbon                dioxide, thus ensuring separation conditions close                to a dimensioning case.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of an                apparatus for separating flow rich in carbon                dioxide by distillation to produce liquid carbon                dioxide.

PT P
PN GB2602030-A; WO2022129895-A1
TI Electrochemical cell for use in molecular            catalyst, and electrochemical reduction of carbon            dioxide, comprises cathode, anode, ion-exchange            membrane separating anode and cathode, and gas supply            for providing carbon dioxide gas to cathode, where            cathode is provided by gas diffusion electrode.
AU NERI G
   COWAN A J
   SIRITANARATKUL B
   FORSTER M
AE UNIV LIVERPOOL (UYLI-C)
GA 202281602J
AB    NOVELTY - Electrochemical cell comprises cathode, anode,                ion-exchange membrane separating anode and cathode,                and gas supply for providing carbon dioxide gas to                cathode. The cathode is provided by gas diffusion                electrode comprising gas diffusion layer and                molecular catalyst. The molecular catalyst                comprises a metal and an organic ligand. The metal                comprises manganese and nickel.
   USE - Electrochemical cell for use in molecular                catalyst, and electrochemical reduction of carbon                dioxide (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The electrochemical cell has high activity and                a longer effective catalyst lifetime in order to                make the electrochemical reduction process, and                prolongs the active lifetime of the gas diffusion                electrode, and reduces environmental impact of                chemical feedstock production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   1. a molecular catalyst having                nickel-containing pyrene compound of formula (I);                and
   2. a method of electrochemical reduction of                carbon dioxide, which involves: (a) providing a                cathode and an anode;
   (b)contacting the carbon dioxide in gaseous                form with the molecular catalyst, optionally in the                presence of water; and
   (c) applying an electrical potential between                the cathode and the anode to electrochemically                reducing the carbon dioxide to gaseous product                including carbon monoxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic ligand is                  a nitrogen-containing heterocycle. The molecular                  catalyst is manganese molecular catalyst                  comprising nitrogen-containing heterocycle, and                  the nitrogen-containing heterocycle is                  2,2'-bipyridyl ligand. The nickel molecular                  catalyst includes azamacrocycle, which is cyclam,                  and nickel-containing pyrene compound of formula                  (I). The gas diffusion layer is porous carbon                  material.The molecular catalyst is adhered to the                  gas diffusion layer by a composition comprising a                  fluorocarbon polymer.R4 and R5-H, hydroxy, amino, thiol, chloro,                  fluoro, CF3, CHF2, CH2F, a 1-8C alkyl group,                  optionally forming a ring, a 1-8C alkenyl group,                  aryl group, heteroaryl group, 1-8C alkoxy group,                  1-8C alkylamino group, 1-8C alkylthio groups, or                  a (CO)OR6group or a (CO)NHR6 group, where R6=H, a                  1-8C alkyl group, optionally forming a ring, 1-8C                  alkenyl group or an aryl group;Y=H, a (CH2)nZ group, where n=1-6 and                  Z=polar group selected from -PO3H2, -CO2H,                  -Si(OH)3, -SH, NH2 and OH.
   EXAMPLE - To a solution of 1-pyrenebutanol (1.2 g, 4.3                mmol) in dry dichloromethane (DCM) (15 ml) under an                inert atmosphere, a suspension of pyridinium                dichromate (2.5 mg, 6.65 mmol) in dry DCM (15 ml)                was added rapidly. The resulting suspension was                stirred under argon overnight. The suspension was                then diluted with 600 ml of diethyl ether and                washed with water first followed by brine twice.                The organic phase was dried over magnesium sulfate                (MgSO4), then filtered, and the solvent was                evaporated. The resulting orange oil was purified                using a silica plug eluted with chloroform. Yellow                oil obtained, tha is 4-(pyren-1-yl)butanal: 900 mg,                yield: 75%. 976 mg Boc3cyclam (1.95 mmol), and 800                mg 4-(pyren-1-yl)butanal (2.9 mmol) were added to a                round bottom flask containing 4 A activated                molecular sieve under argon and dissolved in dry                1,2-dichloroethane (DCE) (20 ml). The solution was                stirred for 2 hours under argon at room                temperature. Sodium triacetoxyborohydride (827 mg,                3.9 mmol) was added under an argon blanket and the                solution was stirred for 24 hours. The crude                solution was washed with three aliquots of 2 M                sodium bicarbonate then the organic fraction was                dried over MgSO4, filtered and the solvent was                evaporated to yield a yellow oil. The product was                isolated by flash column chromatography, eluting                first with DCM then with DCM:EtOAc 50:50. Yellow                foam obtained, that is tri-tert-butyl                11-(4-(pyren-1-yl)butyI)-1,4,8,11-tetraazacyclotetradecane-1,4,8-tricarboxylate:                1.16 g, yield: 83%. 1.16 g tri-tert-butyl                11-(4-(pyren-1-yl)butyI)-1,4,8,11-tetraazacyclotetradecane-1,4,8-tricarboxylate                (1.53 mmol) was dissolved in 15 ml DCM, then 7.5 ml                trifluoroacetic acid (TFA) was added dropwise at                room temperature. The solution was stirred at room                temperature until no change was detected in the TLC                (DCM:EtOAC 50:50), ca. 6 hours. The solvent was                rotary evaporated (methanol (MeOH) was continually                added to aid with complete TFA removal). The crude                was purified by passing through an Amberlite IRN-78                (RTM:premier anion resin) twice, eluting with MeOH.                Yellow oil obtained, that is                1-(4-(pyren-1-yl)butyI)-1,4,8,11-tetraazacyclotetradecane:                620 mg, yield 83%.                1-(4-(pyren-1-yl)butyI)-1,4,8,11-tetraazacyclotetradecane                was either reacted with nickel chlorde NiCl2 (1:1)                in ethanol yielding a fine powder of complex of                (Ni(cyclam-pyrene)), where cyclam-pyrene=                1,4,8,11-tetraazacyclotetradecane-1-butyl-pyrene).

PN WO2021161021-A1; AU2021220687-A1; CA3167459-A1
TI Calcination process used in e.g., oxygen            generation involves combusting mineral having metal            carbonate fraction and fuel fraction in presence of            oxygen, water vapor and carbon dioxide, and using            generated heat to drive gas separation.
AU KRUGER T
AE ORIGEN POWER LTD (ORIG-Non-standard)
   ORIGEN POWER LTD (ORIG-Non-standard)
GA 2021957037
AB    NOVELTY - A calcination process involves (a) providing a                mineral comprising a metal carbonate fraction and                fuel fraction, where the fuel fraction has a                calorific value of greater than or equal to 50% of                the energy needed to calcine the metal carbonate                fraction, and combusting the mineral in the                presence of oxygen, water vapor and carbon dioxide,                to generate a metal oxide, water vapor, carbon                dioxide and heat; and (b) using heat generated in                step (a) to (i) drive a gas separation process                which generates high purity oxygen from air, where                oxygen generated in step (b) is used in step (a)                and/or (ii) compress the carbon dioxide generated                in step (a).
   USE - A calcination process used in carbon dioxide                sequestration and oxygen generation (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method results in carbon dioxide being                removed from the atmosphere. The heat energy                generated during calcination can be used to either                separate oxygen from air, so that the oxygen can be                fed back into the system, or used to compress the                gaseous carbon dioxide generated from the                calcination process as portion of a sequestration                process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The fuel fraction of                  mineral has a calorific value which is                  sufficiently high to enable to calcination of the                  entire carbonate fraction. The mineral is oil                  shale or coal shale, preferably where the mineral                  is oil shale from the Green River Formation. The                  metal carbonate fraction of the mineral in step                  (a) comprises a group II metal, or combination of                  group II metals, preferably where the metal                  carbonate in step (a) comprises calcium carbonate                  or magnesium carbonate. The mixture of gasses in                  which the mineral is combusted comprises 0-1                  vol.% nitrogen. The mixture of gasses in which                  the mineral is combusted comprises flue gasses                  recycled from step (a) and oxygen. The oxygen                  and/or carbon dioxide used in the combustion                  reaction of step (a) is greater than or equal to                  90% pure, preferably greater than or equal to 95%                  pure. Preferred Process: The mineral is combusted                  in step (a) in a mixture of gasses comprising                  5-20 vol.% water vapor, 15-25 vol.% oxygen and                  60-75 vol.% carbon dioxide. The                  combustion/calcination reaction of step (a) takes                  place at 800-1350 degrees C, preferably 1000-1150                  degrees C. The gas separation process of step                  (bi) occurs in an Air Separation Unit (ASU), a                  Pressure Swing Adsorption (PSA) System, and a                  Vacuum Swing Absorption (VSA) process. The oxygen                  generation reaction of step (bi) occurs in a                  Pressure Swing Adsorption (PSA) system. The                  process further comprises an additional step of                  sequestering the carbon dioxide generated in step                  (a). The heat generated in step (a) is used to                  both (i) drive a gas separation process which                  generates high purity oxygen from air and (ii)                  compress the carbon dioxide generated in step                  (a), in the process of sequestering the carbon                  dioxide. The process further comprises additional                  step (d) of hydration of metal oxide generated in                  step (a) to produce a metal hydroxide and heat.                  The heat generated during hydration of the metal                  oxide is used to drive the generation of oxygen                  and/or the compression of carbon dioxide in step                  (b).

PT P
PN US2019326591-A1; US11296313-B2
TI Producing water resistant cathodes comprises            mixing cathode powder with solid carbon dioxide to            create a mixture, and heating the mixture to a            temperature and for a time to cause lithium carbonate            to form on the powder.
AU FERIDUN O K
   KRUMDICK G K
   KAHVECIOGLU O
AE UCHICAGO ARGONNE LLC (UYCG-C)
GA 2019882312
AB    NOVELTY - Method (m1) of producing water resistant                cathodes comprises (a) mixing cathode powder with                solid carbon dioxide to create a mixture, and (b)                heating the mixture to a temperature and for a time                sufficient to cause lithium carbonate to form on                the powder.
   USE - The method is useful for producing water                resistant cathodes.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: utilizes the mixing cathode powder                with solid carbon dioxide to create a mixture and                heating the mixture to a temperature; utilizes                simultaneously sublimating solid carbon dioxide and                condensing atmospheric water vapor onto surfaces;                allows the lithium to react with the sublimated                carbon dioxide for a time sufficient to create a                lithium carbonate film on the surface; improves                water stability of cathodes to enable aqueous-based                electrode manufacturing; and is quick and                economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                method (m2) of coating lithium-containing cathode                surfaces comprising simultaneously sublimating                solid carbon dioxide and condensing atmospheric                water vapor onto the surfaces, and allowing the                lithium to react with the sublimated carbon dioxide                for a time sufficient to create a lithium carbonate                film on the surface.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the method (m1), a                  weight ratio of the powder to the carbon dioxide                  is 1:10-1:20. The method (m1) is conducted in                  atmospheric conditions. The mixture in the method                  (m1) is heated to 250-700 degrees C. The powder                  is a NMC type cathode material consisting of                  nickel, cobalt, manganese, lithium and/or oxygen.                  The step of mixing in the method (m1) occurs in                  an atmosphere having of 1-30% humidity. The                  mixing in the method (m1) establishes an                  equilibrium between solid dry ice and gaseous                  water to form carbonic acid. The carbonic acid                  reacts with lithium contained in the powder. The                  reaction in the method (m1) occurs at -30 degrees                  C and 0 degrees C. The mixture in the method (m1)                  is maintained at a pH of 6-11, and is buried                  under additional solid carbon dioxide before                  heating. In the method (m2), a pH of the surface                  is less than 11. The simultaneous step in the                  method (m2) comprises mixing dry ice with cathode                  powder, where a weight percent of dry ice to                  cathode powder of 10:1-20:1. The method (m2)                  further comprises removing excess water from the                  surface, and heating the film. The simultaneous                  step in the method (m2) results in the production                  of carbonic acid. The carbonic acid in the method                  (m2) reacts with the lithium. The condensed water                  in the method (m2) dissolves the lithium prior to                  the creation of the film.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN114539515-A
TI Preparing biodegradable carbon dioxide-based            terpolymer useful for manufacturing disposable medicine            and food packaging material, comprises e.g. adding            lactide, epoxy compound, and tetra-n-butylammonium            chloride to autoclave, reacting, and subjecting to            post-treatment.
AU WANG F
AE KELUN PLASTICS GROUP CO LTD (KELU-Non-standard)
GA 2022748172
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing biodegradable carbon dioxide-based                terpolymer comprises (1) adding lactide, epoxy                compound, cobalt(III) complex of a salen ligand and                tetra-n-butylammonium chloride to autoclave,                passing carbon dioxide with a purity of 99% into                autoclave, and reacting to obtain copolymer, and                (2) dissolving copolymer with acetone to obtain a                mixed solution, subjecting for post-treatment to                obtain a terpolymer of carbon dioxide, lactide and                epoxy compound.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing                biodegradable carbon dioxide-based terpolymer,                which is useful for manufacturing disposable                medicine and food packaging material e.g. medical                appliance, engineering plastic, packaging,                filtering and biomedical material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is simple, economical and easy to                realize industrial production; has small dosage of                catalyst, mild reaction condition; and improves                thermal stability and mechanical strength of the                polylactic acid. The carbon dioxide-based                terpolymer: has easily degradable groups, increases                hydrophilicity and degrades performance of the                polymer; and has wide application prospect in the                field of packaging, filtering and biomedical                material.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                biodegradable carbon dioxide-based terpolymer
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The epoxy compound is                  propylene oxide, 1,2-butylene oxide,                  1,2-dimethyloxirane, 1,2-epoxycyclopentane, and                  epoxy cyclohexane. The lactide is L-lactide,                  D-lactide, or D,L-lactide. The carbon                  dioxide-based terpolymer is terpolymer of carbon                  dioxide, lactide and epoxy compound.Preferred Method: The molar ratio of the                  cobalt(III) complex of a salen ligand to the                  epoxy compound is 1:1000-1:6000. The molar ratio                  of the cobalt(III) complex of a salen ligand to                  tetra-n-butylammonium chloride is 1:1-1:3. The                  molar ratio of the lactide to the epoxy compound                  is 1:3-1:10. In the step (1), before passing                  carbon dioxide with a purity of 99% into                  autoclave, carrying out exhaust treatment, where                  the specific steps are introducing carbon dioxide                  with a purity of 99% into the autoclave to                  replace air in the autoclave for 2-3 times. In                  the step (1), the reaction pressure is 0.5-3 MPa,                  the reaction temperature is 25-60degrees Celsius,                  and the reaction time is 12-36 hours. In the step                  (2), the mass ratio of acetone to the copolymer                  is 1:0.8-1:1.2. In the step (2), the                  post-treatment includes washing mixed solution                  with methanol for 3-4 times, and then vacuum                  drying. The vacuum degree of the vacuum drying is                  0.01-0.3 Pa. The temperature of the vacuum drying                  is 30-50degrees Celsius, and the time of the                  vacuum drying is 20-24 hours.
