PN US2022220002-A1
TI Producing metal oxide, comprises combining metal            halide and oxidant in liquid phase medium under            conditions.
AU SUBRAMANIAM B
   DANBY A M
   CORBIN D R
   SHIFLETT M B
AE UNIV KANSAS (UNIV-C)
GA 202291953F
AB    NOVELTY - Producing a metal oxide involves combining a                metal halide and an oxidant in a liquid phase                medium under conditions to oxidize the metal halide                in the liquid phase medium to produce the metal                oxide.
   USE - Method for producing a metal oxide used in                paints, plastics, paper, and specialty                applications, catalysis, optoelectronics, and                photovoltaics.
   ADVANTAGE - The method dramatically decreases the energy                required for the titanium dioxide oxidation step by                60-80%, lowering the cost of production by 15-25%,                and reducing global carbon dioxide emissions from                titanium dioxide plants by 25-30% (2 million                MT/y).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Metals: The metal halide is a                  metal chloride. The metal of metal halide is                  selected from transition metals, metalloids, and                  combinations, preferably selected from titanium,                  zirconium, tin, niobium, tantalum, tungsten,                  hafnium, geranium, vanadium, indium, antimony,                  molybdenum, rhenium, ruthenium, nickel, iron,                  chromium, manganese, aluminum, and combinations.                  The oxidant is ozone, which is combined with                  oxygen or air. The liquid phase medium comprises                  carbon dioxide.Preferred Conditions: The combining is                  carried out in the presence of supercritical                  carbon dioxide at 32degrees Celsius. The liquid                  phase medium also comprises a directing agent.                  The metal oxide is a fully oxidized metal                  oxide.

PN CN112538150-A; CN112538150-B
TI Preparing high resilience polyurethane foam involves using polymer components comprising block poly(ether-carbonate) polyol and polyether polyol and isocyanates.
AU MIAO Y
   QIAO L
   FU S
   WANG X
   WANG F
AE CHANGCHUN APPLIED CHEM INST CHINESE ACAD (CAAC-C)
GA 202131427C
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing high resilience polyurethane foam, involves using polymer components comprising 5-100 wt.% block poly(ether-carbonate) polyol and 95-0 wt.% polyether polyol and isocyanates.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing high resilience polyurethane foam (claimed) used as seat such as automobiles, airplanes, and high-speed trains.
   ADVANTAGE - The method adopts more uniform components, uses carbon dioxide, and reduces the cost, and the prepared polyurethane foam is more convenient for industrial transportation and storage, and has excellent mechanical properties and low hysteresis rate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The epoxy compound comprises ethylene oxide, propylene oxide, epichlorohydrin, butylene oxide, styrene oxide and/or cyclohexane oxide. The chain transfer agent comprises 1-10C alcohol compounds, hydroxyl-containing polymers with a molecular weight of less than 2000 g/mol, and carboxylic acid compounds. The isocyanate comprises hexamethylene diisocyanate, methyl cyclohexyl diisocyanate, dicyclohexylmethane diisocyanate, toluene diisocyanate, 4,4-diphenyl diisocyanate, P-phenylene diisocyanate, polymethylene polyphenyl polyisocyanate, 3,5-dimethyl 4,4-diphenyl diisocyanate, 2,4-ethylbenzene diisocyanate, 3,3-dimethoxy 4,4-diphenyl diisocyanate, toluene diisocyanate dimer, isophorone diisocyanate, xylylene dimethylene diisocyanate, 1,5-naphthalene diisocyanate and/or tetramethylxylylene diisocyanate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The block poly(ether-carbonate) polyol is prepared by polymerizing an epoxy compound under the action of a catalyst and a chain transfer agent to obtain a polymer product, and continuously copolymerizing the polymerization product with carbon dioxide and epoxy compound at 10-130 degrees C for 1-48 hours under a pressure of 0-15 MPa to obtain block poly(ether-carbonate) polyol. Preferred Properties: The number average molecular weight of the block poly(ether-carbonate) polyol is 600-10000 g/mol. The number average molecular weight of the polyether polyol is 600-10000 g/mol. The functionality of the polyether polyol is 2-8. Preferred Composition: The carbonate content in the block poly(ether-carbonate) polyol is 0-99 wt.%. The functionality of the block poly(ether-carbonate) polyol is 2-8. The mass ratio of the total epoxy compound to the chain transfer agent during the polymerization reaction is (2-50):1. The mass ratio of the catalyst to the total epoxy compound during the polymerization reaction is 1:(300-200000).

TI Preparing monoamine polyoxyethylene ether, by polymerizing N-(tert-butyloxycarboryl)ethanolamine under tin dioxide-platinum oxide-aluminum oxide complex to form polyoxyethylene ether compound and reacting with tert-butyloxycarbonyl group.
AU SONG M
   JIN Y
   YU J
   CHEN T
   JIN L
AE ZHEJIANG HUANGMA TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 2019A04005
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing monoamine-based polyoxyethylene ether, involves polymerizing N-(tert-butyloxycarboryl)ethanolamine under a supported catalyst tin dioxide-platinum oxide-aluminum oxide to obtain a 2-(tert-butyloxycarbonyl-amino)ethanol polyoxyethylene ether, adding an capping reagent, reacting the by-product hydrochloric acid with a tert-butyloxycarbonyl group to obtain isobutene, carbon dioxide and amine salt and adjusting the pH to obtain the finished product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing monoamine-based polyoxyethylene ether.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The capping reagent is chloromethane, chloroethane, allyl chloride or methylallyl chloride. Preferred Method: The method further involves (a) adding N-(tert-butyloxycarboryl)ethanolamine and ethylene oxide in a molar ratio of 1:(1-20) and polymerizing at 140-150 degrees C to obtain 2-(tert-butyloxycarbonyl-amino)ethanol polyoxyethylene ether under the supported catalyst, (b) adding 2-(tert-butyloxycarbonyl-amino)ethanol polyoxyethylene ether and blocking reagent in a molar ratio of 1:(1.10-1.25), reacting, carrying out end-capping reaction at a temperature of 100-130 degrees C and separately recovering the capping reagent and the by-product isobutene, where 2-(tert-butyloxycarbonyl-amino)ethanol polyoxyethylene ether does not require post-treatment, and (c) adjusting the pH value to 8-9 by using 5% sodium carbonate solution, generating sodium chloride and water, dehydrating to crystallize the salt and filtering to obtain the finished product. Preferred Composition: The mass of the supported catalyst tin dioxide-platinum oxide-aluminum oxide is 6-8% based on the total mass of N-(tert-butyloxycarboryl)ethanolamine and ethylene oxide.

TI Preparing plant deodorant comprises e.g. raw material and the raw material pre-processing process, placing the prepared plant raw materials of deodorant is divided into two parts, and respectively carrying out pre-treatment.
AU ZHAO Y
AE HENAN CHUANYU BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (HENA-Non-standard)
GA 201955515P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing plant deodorant comprises e.g. raw material and the raw material pre-processing process: placing the prepared plant raw materials of deodorant is divided into two parts, respectively carrying out pre-treatment, specific process comprises (1) taking 1-2 pts. wt. fructus cannabis, 2-3 pts. wt. turpentine, 3-5 pts. wt. saponin, 4-5 pts. wt. radix sophorae flavescentis, and 5-10 pts. wt. Sanguisorba officinalis, mixing the raw material after hot air-drying treatment, mixing and grinding, and sieving by 150-200 meshes sieve, the grain diameter of sieving 150-200 meshes, placing the screened substance into 15-25 pts. wt. ethyl acetate, sealing and storing; and (2) taking fresh orange peel of 10-12 pts. wt. , 15-18 pts. wt. Aloe, and 15-20 pts. wt. dyers woad leaf, freezing and drying the fresh orange peel, Aloe and dyers woad leaf, freezing and drying the raw material after mixing and grinding, and sieving, and the grain diameter is 150-200 meshes.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing plant deodorant.
   ADVANTAGE - The plant deodorant: has good antibacterial and odor-reducing effects.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing plant deodorant comprises raw material and the raw material pre-processing process: placing the prepared plant raw materials of deodorant is divided into two parts, respectively carrying out pre-treatment, specific process comprises (1) taking 1-2 pts. wt. fructus cannabis, 2-3 pts. wt. turpentine, 3-5 pts. wt. saponin, 4-5 pts. wt. radix sophorae flavescentis, and 5-10 pts. wt. Sanguisorba officinalis, mixing the raw material after hot air-drying treatment, mixing and grinding, and sieving by 150-200 meshes sieve, the grain diameter of sieving 150-200 meshes, placing the screened substance into 15-25 pts. wt. ethyl acetate, sealing and storing at 4 degrees C for 24 hours to obtain mixed solution A; (2) taking fresh orange peel of 10-12 pts. wt. , 15-18 pts. wt. Aloe, and 15-20 pts. wt. dyers woad leaf, freezing and drying the fresh orange peel, Aloe and dyers woad leaf, freezing and drying the raw material after mixing and grinding, and sieving by 150-200-mesh sieve, the grain diameter is 150-200 meshes, screening substance, placing the screened material in 60-80 pts. wt. ultra-pure water to obtain the mixed solution B, (3) the supercritical carbon dioxide (CO2) extraction: extracting method respectively using supercriticalCO2 extraction to the mixed solution A and the mixed solution B to an active ingredient, the specific process is as follows: fixing the extraction kettle in the heating box, connecting two end interface of the extraction kettle, opening the supercritical CO2 fluid extraction device power supply, setting the extracting temperature is 36 degrees C, taking 300 ml mixed solution A in the extraction kettle when the temperature reaches the set pressure value, opening the air inlet switch, regulating the extracting kettle is 30 MPa, when the pressure of the extracting kettle reaches the set value, after keeping for 5 minutes under this condition, opening the gas outlet valve, the pneumatic pump balance of carbon dioxide extraction pressure in the kettle; and keeping constant pressure in the extraction kettle, setting the extraction time is 1 hour, after extracting 1 hour, separating the carbon dioxide into the extracting kettle separating kettle to extract and CO2, a separation temperature in the kettle is 36 degrees C, the pressure is 5 MPa, gaseous CO2 recycled after rectifying column to purify the extract discharged from the lower end of the separating kettle to obtain the fat-soluble extract; and the extraction kettle is fixed in the heating box, connecting two end interface of the extraction kettle, opening the supercritical CO2 fluid extraction device power supply, setting the extracting temperature is 36 degrees C, taking 300 ml mixed solution A in the extraction kettle when the temperature reaches the set pressure value, opening the air inlet switch, regulating the extracting kettle is 30 MPa, when the pressure of the extracting kettle reaches the set value, after keeping for 5 minutes under this condition, opening the gas outlet valve, the pneumatic pump balance of carbon dioxide extraction pressure in the kettle; and keeping constant pressure in the extraction kettle, opening the cosolvent pump, selected as cosolvent with super-pure water, controlling the cosolvent flow speed is 1 ml/minute, setting the extraction time is 1 hour, after the extracting for 1 hour, the carbon dioxide into the extracting kettle in the separating kettle to extract and CO2, a separation temperature in the kettle is 36 degrees C, the pressure is 5 MPa, gaseous CO2 recycled after rectifying column to purify the extract discharged from the lower end of the separating kettle to obtain the water-based extract; the compound emulsion: emulsifying agent obtained in the step (2) extracting fat-soluble extract with water-based extract, and adding 0.1 pts. wt. in the mixture via homogenizer or colloid mill, then performing homogenization treatment to obtain the plant deodorant.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Method: The hot air drying process is as follows: mixing the fructus cannabis, turpentine, saponin, Sanguisorba and radix sophorae flavescentis according to proportion in the step (1), mixing the mixed raw material is paved in the hot wind oven, setting drying temperature is 60 degrees C, the material is taken out every 30min weighing, after raw material weight does not change, namely the completion of drying, the dried raw material, and taking out. The freeze-drying process is as follows: the fresh orange peel, dyers woad leaf and aloe according to proportion in the step (1) mixing, the mixed material at -40 degrees C refrigerator to freeze 24 hours, after freezing the material at the temperature in the low-temperature freeze dryer for freezing and drying, setting the freezing drying process is -50 degrees C, freezing and drying time is 48 hours, after finishing freezing and drying, drying the material, and taking out. Preferred Components: The ultra-pure water purifying apparatus made by ultra-pure water purified from tap water. The emulsifier is disproportionated rosin acid sodium or hexadecyl sodium sulfonate. The plant deodorant is oil-in water emulsion. The active ingredients in the plant deodorant is mainly alkaloid, flavonoid and amino-glycoside.

PN CN111777067-A; CN212356556-U
TI Carbon dioxide generator useful for agriculture, comprises mutual matching shell and methanol burner.
AU LI F
   FENG M
   YANG Y
AE FUJIAN DAWEI ENERGY CO LTD (FUJI-Non-standard)
GA 2020A32118
AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide generator comprises e.g. mutual matching shell and methanol burner, there are many air inlet holes on the shell for outside air to enter, the shell is equipped with a combustion chamber, controller, flame stabilizing sleeve, flue gas outlet, fan, carbon dioxide outlet. The combustion chamber is connected to the methanol burner, and the combustion chamber is arranged in the flame-stabilizing sleeve, upper part of one side of the flame stabilizing sleeve is provided with a flue gas outlet for flue gas to enter the shell, number of ventilation mesh holes are arranged on the outer edge, and the carbon dioxide outlet is arranged on the side wall of the shell to communicate with fan, methanol is taken over from the inlet into the methanol burner for premixed combustion and enters the combustion chamber for complete combustion.
   USE - The generator is useful for agriculture (claimed).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Carbon dioxide generator comprises mutual matching shell and methanol burner, there are many air inlet holes on the shell for outside air to enter, the shell is equipped with a combustion chamber, controller, flame stabilizing sleeve, flue gas outlet, fan, carbon dioxide outlet. The combustion chamber is connected to the methanol burner, and the combustion chamber is arranged in the flame-stabilizing sleeve, upper part of one side of the flame stabilizing sleeve is provided with a flue gas outlet for the flue gas to enter the shell, number of ventilation mesh holes are arranged on the outer edge, and the carbon dioxide outlet is arranged on the side wall of the shell to communicate with the fan, methanol is taken over from the inlet into the methanol burner for premixed combustion and then enters the combustion chamber for complete combustion; high-temperature flue gas containing carbon dioxide produced by combustion enters the flame-stabilizing sleeve, and enters the shell from the flue gas outlet on the flame-stabilizing sleeve, and the outside air enters the air inlet hole on the shell; part of it enters the flame-stabilizing sleeve through the ventilation mesh on the flame-stabilizing sleeve and gradually mixes with high-temperature flue gas; other part is mixed with the flue gas in the shell to reduce the temperature of the flue gas containing carbon dioxide, and gas containing carbon dioxide is sent out from the carbon dioxide outlet through the fan.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The controller is arranged on the upper right side of the housing, and the controller is provided with a control panel for control. The methanol burner includes an inlet connector, a methanol delivery pump, a methanol liquid delivery pipe, a methanol vaporizer, a pilot combustion premixer, a burner, and an ignition device that are matched with each other, where methanol is sucked in from the inlet, and after being pressurized by the methanol transfer pump, methanol liquid transfer pipe enters the methanol vaporizer, and vaporized methanol gas enters the pilot combustion premixer through the methanol gas transfer pipe to be premixed with combustion air; and methanol gas premixed with air is ejected from the burner head and ignited by the ignition device for premixed combustion; and flame produced by the combustion is injected into the combustion chamber for complete combustion. The flame-stabilizing sleeve is in the shape of a round tube, the left end of the flame-stabilizing sleeve is provided with a high-temperature-resistant thermal insulation fiber baffle, and the outside is provided with a high-temperature-resistant thermal insulation layer. A carbon dioxide concentration detector is installed in the greenhouse where the carbon dioxide generator is installed. The carbon dioxide concentration detector is connected with the controller to detect and control the working state of the carbon dioxide generator.

PN EP3673747-A1; US2020205429-A1; CN111374259-A
TI Method for operating controlled atmosphere (CA)            system to regulate atmosphere in cargo storage space,            involves controlling operation of gas exchange module            based on determined respiration parameter value to            target oxygen level setpoint.
AU STAVOVA P
   ZITA J
   VOJIK M
   KOLDA M
AE THERMO KING CORP (THES-C)
   THERMO KING CORP (THES-C)
   THERMO KING CORP (THES-C)
GA 202057947T
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves determining (19) a                respiration parameter value indicative of the rate                of change of oxygen level and/or the rate of change                of carbon dioxide level in the cargo storage space                due to respiration of goods in the cargo storage                space. The gas exchange module is controlled based                on the determined respiration parameter value to                target an oxygen level setpoint and/or a carbon                dioxide level setpoint. The rate of change of                oxygen level and/or a rate of change of carbon                dioxide level is determined by the control module                in the cargo storage space due to respiration of                the goods in the cargo storage space. The                respective respiration parameter value is                determined by the control module from predetermined                respiration parameter values associated with the                rate of change of oxygen level and/or the rate of                change of carbon dioxide level.
   USE - Method for operating controlled atmosphere                (CA) system to regulate atmosphere in cargo storage                space.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide removal module is operated                in a mode in which carbon dioxide is removed from                the cargo storage space at a faster rate when the                goods stored in the cargo storage space are                respiring relatively quickly. Since the                concentration of carbon dioxide in ambient air is                relatively low, the supply of ambient air quickly                reduces the level of carbon dioxide in the cargo                storage space as it displaces interior air.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a controlled atmosphere system for                regulating the atmosphere in a cargo storage space;                and
   (2) a non-transitory computer readable medium                storing program for regulating the atmosphere in a                cargo storage space.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart illustrating a                method of selecting an appropriate gas exchange                module and/or operational mode based on measured                carbon dioxide and oxygen levels.
   Step for measuring carbon dioxide and oxygen                levels (18)
   Step for determining a respiration parameter                value indicative of the rate of change of oxygen                level and/or the rate of change of carbon dioxide                level in the cargo storage space (19)
   Step for selecting approximate gas exchange                module (20)
   Step for operating selected gas exchange                module in selected operational mode (21)

PN CN109593386-A; CN109593386-B
TI Preparing modified heavy calcium carbonate, involves adding heavy calcium carbonate and deionized water, homogenizing, circulating carbon dioxide, performing solid-liquid separation, drying solid phase product by hot air drying, sieving.
AU WANG L
   SONG X
   GUAN C
   CHEN S
   SUN F
   YAN X
AE UNIV SHANGHAI POLYTECHNIC (USSP-C)
GA 2019349967
AB    NOVELTY - The method for preparing modified heavy calcium carbonate, involves adding heavy calcium carbonate and deionized water to premixing tank, and homogenizing, transferring obtained uniformly mixed slurry to gas-solid liquid reaction device, opening carbon dioxide storage tank valve to pressurize the system, closing storage tank valve, circulating carbon dioxide in the reactor, flushing carbon dioxide gas into the bottom, collecting at the top of the reactor for circulation, collecting excess carbon dioxide from carbon dioxide buffer storage tank by pressurized air pump, transferring solid-liquid mixed product to production tank through the bottom of the reaction vessel, pumping the material in the production tank to solid-liquid separation device for solid-liquid separation, where the liquid phase is recyclable process water, and solid phase is product modified heavy calcium carbonate, drying the solid phase product by hot air drying, and sieving.
   USE - Method for preparing modified heavy calcium carbonate used as filler material for resin product (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables simple, reliable and economical preparation of modified heavy calcium carbonate with controlled particle size distribution, improves the filling performance of the inorganic filler, and reduces the production cost of the plastic product.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for preparing modified heavy calcium carbonate, involves (1) adding heavy calcium carbonate and deionized water to premixing tank, and thoroughly homogenizing through the top stirring device, (2) transferring obtained uniformly mixed slurry to gas-solid liquid reaction device through feeding pump, opening carbon dioxide storage tank valve to pressurize the system, closing storage tank valve after the completion of the pressurization, circulating carbon dioxide in the reactor by a pressurized air pump, flushing carbon dioxide gas into the bottom, collecting at the top of the reactor for circulation, collecting excess carbon dioxide from carbon dioxide buffer storage tank by pressurized air pump, transferring solid-liquid mixed product to production tank through the bottom of the reaction vessel, (3) pumping the material in the production tank to solid-liquid separation device for solid-liquid separation, where the liquid phase is recyclable process water, and solid phase is product modified heavy calcium carbonate, (4) drying the solid phase product by hot air drying, and sieving. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for application of modified heavy calcium carbonate used as filler material for resin product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The step (1) comprises 100 pts. wt. calcium carbonate and 400-600 pts. wt. deionized water. Preferred Conditions: The step (2) comprises reacting at pressure of 5-10 kgf/cm2, preferably 8-10 kgf/cm2 for 1-4 hours, preferably 3-4 hours. The step (4) comprises hot air drying at drying temperature of 80-150 degrees C for 2-5 hours. Preferred Process: The step (3) comprises using solid-liquid separation device as an industrial flat-plate centrifuge which performs centrifugal treatment at 1000-1500 rpm for 10-20 minutes. The step (4) comprises sorting sieve with mesh size of 100 mesh, 200 mesh and 600 mesh, removing heavy calcium carbonate by using 100 mesh sieve, passing medium fiber impurities through 200 mesh sieve to retain the undersize, and finally passing through a 600 mesh sieve. The sieve is a modified heavy calcium carbonate product with uniform particle size distribution, and the 200 mesh sieve is used as a return material. The application of modified heavy calcium carbonate involves mixing 100 pts. wt. resin matrix with 15-25 pts. wt. modified heavy calcium carbonate, sequentially adopting a twin-screw extruder, granulating, drying the granulated product, and injection molding using injection molding machine.

PN CN109172893-A
TI Negative-pressure suction pipe comprises filtering device containing adsorbent, where adsorbent is modified pineapple leaf fiber.
AU LV Z
   CHEN Y
   LI N
   LAN D
   XIA X
AE NANCHANG ZHONGSHEN MEDICAL EQUIP CO LTD (NANC-Non-standard)
GA 201909574E
AB    NOVELTY - Negative-pressure suction pipe comprises filtering device containing adsorbent. The adsorbent is modified pineapple leaf fiber.
   USE - Negative-pressure suction pipe.
   ADVANTAGE - The pipe uses electron beam to pre-irradiate pineapple leaf fiber powder, and is modified with acrylic acid, acrylamide and luteolin. The modified pineapple leaf fiber has good capillary effect and adsorption, and good adsorption effect on harmful substances such as carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and surgical smog. The pipe reduces laparoscopic surgery and harm of surgical smoke to medical staff during the process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - TEXTILES AND PAPER - Preferred Method: The modified pineapple leaf fiber comprises crushing pineapple leaf fiber to 100 meshes, and performing electron beam irradiation with electron energy of 3-5 MeV, electronic beam current of 200 mu A and dosage rate of 50 Gy/second; mixing pineapple leaf fiber into mixed solution of acrylic acid and acrylamide, reacting for 12 hours at room temperature, adding luteolin, reacting at room temperature for 12 hours, filtering, and washing filter residue with water for 3 times; and adding filter residue with 6 times 5% sodium hydroxide solution, adding epoxy chloropropane, reacting at 60 degrees C for 4 hours, stirring p-styrene sulfonic acid sodium, filtering, orderly using acetone and water to wash until filtrate is neutral, filtering, and drying at 60 degrees C to constant weight. The mass ratio of luteolin and pineapple leaf fiber is 3:5.
DC J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. PT P
PN WO2022103645-A1; US2022154562-A1
TI Enhancing oil recovery from hydrocarbon bearing            subterranean formation by e.g. injecting dense carbon            dioxide composition and aqueous treatment fluid from            injection well into hydrocarbon bearing subterranean            formation.
AU ALYOUSIF Z
   ALMAJID M M
AE SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
   ARAMCO SERVICES CO (SAOI-C)
   SAUDI ARABIAN OIL CO (SAOI-C)
GA 202268973W
AB    NOVELTY - Enhancing oil recovery from a hydrocarbon                (104) bearing subterranean formation (100),                comprises: withdrawing hydrocarbons from a                production well (110) extending into the                hydrocarbon bearing subterranean formation;                identifying a high permeability streak in the                hydrocarbon bearing subterranean formation;                injecting a dense carbon dioxide composition from                an injection well into the high permeability streak                of the hydrocarbon bearing subterranean formation,                where the dense carbon dioxide composition                comprises dense carbon dioxide and a thickener                soluble in the dense carbon dioxide; injecting an                aqueous treatment fluid from the injection well                into the hydrocarbon bearing subterranean                formation; and the injecting of the aqueous                treatment fluid into the hydrocarbon bearing                subterranean formation drives hydrocarbons in the                hydrocarbon bearing subterranean formation towards                the production well.
   USE - The method is useful for enhancing oil                recovery from a hydrocarbon bearing subterranean                formation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is cost-effective.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Enhancing oil recovery from a hydrocarbon                (104) bearing subterranean formation (100),                comprises: withdrawing hydrocarbons from a                production well extending into the hydrocarbon                bearing subterranean formation; identifying a high                permeability streak in the hydrocarbon bearing                subterranean formation; injecting a dense carbon                dioxide composition from an injection well into the                high permeability streak of the hydrocarbon bearing                subterranean formation, where the dense carbon                dioxide composition comprises dense carbon dioxide                and a thickener soluble in the dense carbon                dioxide, and the thickener comprises a copolymer,                the copolymer being the polymerized reaction                product of monomers that include at least one                alkenyl ether or dialkenyl ether monomer, at least                one acrylate or methacrylate monomer, at least one                structural monomer, and at least one allyl ester                monomer; after injecting the dense carbon dioxide                composition into the high permeability streak,                injecting an aqueous treatment fluid from the                injection well into the hydrocarbon bearing                subterranean formation, where the dense carbon                dioxide composition blocks the high permeability                streak to divert at least a portion of the aqueous                treatment fluid into bypassed regions of the                hydrocarbon bearing subterranean formation during                the injecting of the aqueous treatment fluid into                the hydrocarbon bearing subterranean formation; and                the injecting of the aqueous treatment fluid into                the hydrocarbon bearing subterranean formation                drives hydrocarbons in the hydrocarbon bearing                subterranean formation towards the production                well.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows schematic depicts enhanced                oil recovery of hydrocarbons from a hydrocarbon                bearing subterranean formation comprising one or                more high permeability zones.
   Subterranean formation (100)
   Hydrocarbons (104)
   Production well (110)
   Injection well (120)
   Water flooded region (122)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: Injecting the dense carbon                  dioxide composition comprises isolating a portion                  of the injection well that is in fluid                  communication with the high permeability streak                  from other portions of the injection well before                  injecting the dense carbon dioxide composition                  into the high permeability streak. Isolating the                  portion of the injection well that is in fluid                  communication with the high permeability streak                  from other portions of the injection well                  comprises installing at least one temporary plug                  in the injection well. At least one temporary                  plug are disposed down hole of the portion of the                  injection well in fluid communication with the                  high permeability streak or both up hole and                  downhole of the portion of the injection well in                  fluid communication with the high permeability                  streak. After injecting the dense carbon dioxide                  composition, removing the isolation from the                  portion of the injection well. Preferred                  Components: The dense carbon dioxide is                  supercritical carbon dioxide. The co-solvent                  comprises at least one carbon dioxide compatible                  solvent comprising propylene carbonate and                  dimethyl carbonate and at least one                  oil-dissolving solvent comprising white oil,                  silicon oil, and/or petroleum ether. The                  thickener comprises 45-65 wt.% carbon dioxide                  compatible solvent and 10-15 wt.% oil dissolving                  solvent. The copolymer of the thickener comprises                  an average molecular weight of 500000-800000                  g/mole, a pH of 5-8, and/or a specific gravity of                  0.90-1.10.1 wt.%. Thickener dissolves in                  supercritical carbon dioxide in less than or                  equal to 3 minutes at 25 degrees C. Preferred                  Composition: The dense carbon dioxide                  composition: comprises 0.05-3 wt.% thickener; has                  a viscosity of from 50-150 mP seconds; reduces                  the permeability of carbonate rock by greater                  than or equal to 50%; and is injected at a                  pressure of 900-3000 pounds/inch2, at 20-150                  degrees C, or both. The temporary plugs comprise                  gel plugs.
   EXAMPLE - The examples provide details of solubility of                thickener in dense carbon dioxide and viscosity of                dense carbon dioxide that do not support the                claims.

PT P
PN CN109096050-A; CN109096050-B
TI Producing methanol by using carbon dioxide and            hydrogen comprises e.g. placing carbon dioxide,            hydrogen and hot carrier methanol into reverse water            gas shift adiabatic fixed bed reactor, reacting and            performing gas-liquid separation.
AU CHEN W
   MA L
   SHEN W
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC SHANGHAI RES INST PETROCHEMICAL (SNPC-C)
GA 2019024621
AB    NOVELTY - Producing methanol by using carbon dioxide and                hydrogen comprises e.g. (i) placing carbon dioxide,                hydrogen and hot carrier methanol into reverse                water gas shift adiabatic fixed bed reactor, and                reacting under the action of reverse water gas                shift catalyst to obtain material I, (ii)                performing heat exchange treatment, cooling and                gas-liquid separation of material I obtaining gas                phase containing carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide                and hydrogen material II, then to obtain liquid                phase containing methanol and water material III,                (iii) compressing and pressurizing the material II,                and reacting under the action of a methanol                synthesis catalyst to obtain methanol and water,                performing gas-liquid separation, and then                returning gas phase containing a mixed gas of                carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and hydrogen to the                methanol synthesis reactor to obtain liquid phase                containing methanol and water material IV, and (iv)                placing material III and material IV in methanol                refining device.
   USE - The method is useful for producing methanol by                using carbon dioxide and hydrogen.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is suitable for industrial                production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Producing methanol by using carbon dioxide and                hydrogen comprises (i) placing carbon dioxide,                hydrogen and hot carrier methanol into reverse                water gas shift adiabatic fixed bed reactor, and                reacting under the action of reverse water gas                shift catalyst to obtain material I, (ii)                performing heat exchange treatment, cooling and                gas-liquid separation of material I obtaining gas                phase containing carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide                and hydrogen material II, then to obtain liquid                phase containing methanol and water material III,                (iii) compressing and pressurizing the material II                to enter the methanol synthesis reactor, and                reacting under the action of a methanol synthesis                catalyst to obtain methanol and water, then                discharging methanol synthesis reaction through the                heat exchange, cooling, performing gas-liquid                separation, and then returning gas phase containing                a mixed gas of carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and                hydrogen to the methanol synthesis reactor to                obtain liquid phase containing methanol and water                material IV, and (iv) placing material III and                material IV in methanol refining device, then                taking a part of the obtained fine methanol as                product, and heating a part as heat carrier and                returning to the reverse water gas shift adiabatic                fixed bed reactor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The mass content of                  methanol in the reverse water gas shift adiabatic                  fixed bed reactor is 5-80%. The molar ratio of                  raw material carbon dioxide and hydrogen is 3-4.                  The reverse water gas shift catalyst is                  manganese-based spinel catalyst containing copper                  and manganese. The reverse water gas shift                  reaction is a combination of hydrogen and carbon                  dioxide, and volumetric space velocity is                  6000-16000 h1. The methanol synthesis catalyst is                  copper-based catalyst containing copper, zinc and                  aluminum. Preferred Method: The reaction                  temperature of the reverse water gas shift                  reaction is 550-700 degrees C. The reaction                  pressure of the reverse water gas shift reaction                  is 0-1.5 MPaG. The reaction temperature of the                  methanol synthesis reaction is 200-280 degrees C,                  reaction pressure is 4.5-6.5 MPaG, and volumetric                  space velocity is 3000-10000 h1.

PN CN112430503-A
TI Shampoo fragrance extraction method involves cleaning freshly picked rose petals and licorice, quickly freezing, grinding and subjecting powder to continuous extraction by controlling supercritical carbon dioxide fluid to extract.
AU ZHENG Z
   LIU W
   YANG H
AE ZHENG Z (ZHEN-Individual)
GA 202123204N
AB    NOVELTY - Shampoo fragrance extraction method involves cleaning freshly picked rose petals and licorice, passing to quick-freezing chamber, passing in liquid nitrogen into quick-freezing chamber, freezing at -25 degrees C to -30 degrees C for 15-18 minutes, passing the mixture to grinder, using liquid nitrogen to maintain constant temperature in grinder, grinding at -5 degrees C to -8 degrees C under speed of 600-800 rpm to quickly grind the frozen product into powder, subjecting milled powder to continuous extraction, controlling supercritical carbon dioxide fluid in continuous extraction machine to heat up and extract the frozen milled powder for 15-20 minutes, decompressing supercritical carbon dioxide gas, cooling and separating. The continuous extraction machine comprises base, shell and motor. The upper surface of base is fixedly connected with symmetrically designed support plate provided with semi-circular grooves on both the sides. The shell is fixedly connected in the semicircular groove.
   USE - Shampoo fragrance extraction method.
   ADVANTAGE - The extraction method uses supercritical fluid in extraction tank of device to effectively extract the fragrance in the material and improves the extraction efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Shampoo fragrance extraction method involves cleaning freshly picked rose petals and licorice, passing to quick-freezing chamber, passing in liquid nitrogen into quick-freezing chamber, freezing at -25 degrees C to -30 degrees C for 15-18 minutes, passing the mixture to grinder, using liquid nitrogen to maintain constant temperature in grinder, grinding at -5 degrees C to -8 degrees C under speed of 600-800 rpm to quickly grind the frozen product into powder, subjecting milled powder to continuous extraction, controlling supercritical carbon dioxide fluid in continuous extraction machine to heat up and extract the frozen milled powder for 15-20 minutes, decompressing supercritical carbon dioxide gas, cooling and separating. The continuous extraction machine comprises base, shell and motor. The upper surface of base is fixedly connected with symmetrically designed support plate provided with semi-circular grooves on both the sides. The shell is cylindrical cavity structure and is fixedly connected in the semicircular groove. The inner cavity of the shell is rotatably connected to extraction tank provided with evenly distributed extraction chambers. The extraction chamber is provided with through groove (A) on the side away from the axis of the extraction tank. The through groove (A) is provided with chute (A) away from the rotation direction of the extraction tank, and chute (A) is elastically connected with sealing plate through a spring. The sealing plate has T-shaped design on the side away from the sliding groove (A). The motor is fixedly connected to one side of the shell, and the output shaft of motor penetrates the shell fixedly connected with the extraction tank. The shell is designed with opening on one side close to the base, the support plate is fixedly connected with feed hopper designed in L-shape. The base is provided with discharging slot near the opening of shell. The feeding hopper and the discharging trough are hinged with limiting plate on the side close to the rotation direction of the extraction tank, the sealing plate is elastically connected by spring on one side away from rotation direction of the extraction tank. The sealing plates are all inclined by the outer wall of the extraction tank (4) by the limit compression spring in initial state, and the two sides of the shell are installed with air inlet pipes above the feed groove, air outlet pipe is fixedly connected on both sides of shell above the base. The air inlet pipe and air outlet pipe are designed to extend into the inner cavity of the shell. The two sides of the extraction tank are provided with evenly distributed through grooves (B) corresponding to the inlet pipe and the outlet pipe. The sliding groove (B) is opened on the side wall of through groove (B), connecting pipe is slidably connected in sliding groove (B), the connecting pipe and sliding groove (B) are connected in sliding and sealing manner through sealing bag. The air inlet pipe and air outlet pipe are both close to through groove (B), a sealing cover is elastically sleeved through spring. The connecting pipe has tapered design structure on the side away from the inner cavity of the extraction tank, a sealing ring is fixedly connected to the connecting pipe on the side close to the inner wall of shell, the connecting pipe is sliding and sealing connected with the inner wall of shell, a separating box is installed on the side of the upper surface of the base close to the air outlet pipe, sliding plate is slidably connected to the separating box and the sliding plate is provided with uniformly distributed unidirectional through holes and sliding plate is controlled by electric push rod. The air outlet pipe is in conduction connection with the bottom end of the separation box, and the base is located at the bottom of the separation box and is equipped with booster valve. The booster valve is internally provided with heater and the one end of the booster valve is connected to the upper end of the separation box through a conduit, and one end is connected to the air inlet pipe.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Component: The extraction chamber comprises uniformly distributed interception mesh connected to the side of the extraction chamber away from passage (A) and the interception meshes are all designed in parallel with the passage (A). The interception mesh has conductive design on one side close to the through groove (A). The extraction tank is provided with rotation groove on the axis, the rotation groove and the inner cavity of the shell are connected with each other. The inner cavity of the shell is fixedly connected to the dial rod corresponding to the conduction groove, the dial rod is located in the conduction groove and is fixedly connected to the dial wheel having asterisk-shaped design, uniformly distributed vibration plate is fixed inside the conduction groove, the vibration plates all extend into the extraction chamber, one end of vibration plate located in the extraction chamber connected to the interception mesh through a spring. A throwing box is hinged on one side of the inner cavity of the feeding hopper close to the extraction tank. The side of throwing box is close to the extraction tank and is attached to the extraction tank and elastically connected with the feeding hopper through a spring on the side away from the extraction tank. The arc-shaped plate is fixedly connected to the side of the feeding hopper far away from the extraction tank, the arc-shaped plate and the throwing box rotates in consistent manner. The feeding hopper and the corresponding arc-shaped plate are provided with containing groove. The ejector box compresses the spring, and the arc-shaped plate located in the containing groove, co-interception mesh is hinged at one end of the interception mesh close to through groove (A), several co-interception meshes are connected to each other by guide rod. The co-interception mesh is fixedly connected to the sealing plate by elastic cords on the side close to the through groove (A), when sealing plate is shrinks into sliding groove (A), co-interception mesh is attached to each other under the pulling force of the elastic cord and is perpendicular to through groove (A).

TI Method for quantitative inversion of carbon            emissions using remote sensing data from orbiting            carbon observation satellites, involves calculating            carbon dioxide emission value of power plant based on            linear density of carbon dioxide cross-sectional flux            and optimized wind direction.
AU DENG Z
   LIU Z
   LIN X
AE UNIV TSINGHUA (UYQI-C)
GA 202274895K
AB    NOVELTY - The quantitative inversion method involves                obtaining linear density of carbon dioxide section                flux. A Gaussian plume model is used to optimize                the wind direction to obtain the optimized wind                direction. The carbon dioxide emission value of the                power plant is calculated based on the linear                density of the carbon dioxide cross-sectional flux                and the optimized wind direction. A Gaussian curve                is fitted to calculate the linear density of the                carbon dioxide cross-sectional flux by using carbon                dioxide concentration data provided by the orbiting                carbon observation satellite (OCO-2).
   USE - Method for quantitative inversion of carbon                emissions using remote sensing data from orbiting                carbon observation satellites.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide emission status of the                power plant can be quantitatively monitored, and                fine factory-level carbon emission monitoring and                management can be realized. The method effectively                reduces the error of estimation results caused by                wind direction deviation, and can form an                independent accounting system that does not rely on                factory energy consumption data to calculate carbon                emissions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) a device for quantitative inversion of                carbon emissions using remote sensing data from                orbiting carbon observation satellites;
   (2) a storage medium comprising                computer-executable instructions;
   (3) a computer program product.

PT P
PN US2020206719-A1; US11027256-B2
TI Amine-based carbon dioxide adsorbent having a core-shell structure comprises core containing amine compound and porous support, and shell containing sulfur dioxide-resistant amine compound and being coated with surrounding the core.
AU CHOI M
   CHOI W
   KIM C
AE KAIST (KOAD-C)
   KOREA ADVANCED SCI & TECHNOLOGY INST (KOAD-C)
GA 2020592066
AB    NOVELTY - An amine-based carbon dioxide adsorbent having a core-shell structure comprises core containing amine compound and porous support; and shell containing sulfur dioxide-resistant amine compound and being coated with surrounding the core.
   USE - An amine-based carbon dioxide adsorbent having a core-shell structure.
   ADVANTAGE - The adsorbent exhibits high oxidation resistance because the chelating agent functions to remove a variety of transition metal impurities catalytically acting on amine oxidation. In addition, the sulfur-dioxide-resistant amine layer of the shell selectively adsorbs sulfur dioxide to protect the amine compound of the core and, at the same time, the amine compound of the core selectively adsorbs only carbon dioxide. Sulfur dioxide adsorbed on the shell is readily desorbable at 110 degrees C, obtaining remarkably improved regeneration stability during temperature-swing adsorption (TSA) processes in which sulfur dioxide is present.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) preparation of carbon dioxide adsorbent having a core-shell structure which involves (a) immobilizing an amine compound on a porous support, (b) adding solution prepared by dissolving epoxide in a solvent to the amine compound immobilized on the support, (c) forming a sulfur dioxide-resistant amine layer by reacting the amine compound immobilized on the support with epoxide, and (d) obtaining a carbon dioxide adsorbent containing the sulfur dioxide-resistant amine layer by removing the solvent; and
   (2) adsorbing carbon dioxide using the adsorbent which involves contacting the adsorbent with a carbon-dioxide-containing gas.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: Porous support is selected from silica, alumina, activated carbon, and zeolite. Porous support is present in an amount of 25-95 wt.% with respect to the total weight of the adsorbent. Preferred Properties: Porous support has a porosity of 0.1-5 cc/g.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: Carbon-dioxide-resistant amine compound of the shell comprises a hydroxy-containing carbon chain. Carbon-dioxide-resistant amine compound of the shell is an amine compound of formula -((CH2)m(OH)nNX)y (I). Ratio of nitrogen present as tertiary amine with respect to nitrogen atoms present in the carbon-dioxide-resistant amine compound of the shell is 50-100%. Carbon-dioxide-resistant amine compound of the shell is present in an amount of 0.1-30 wt.% with respect to the total weight of the adsorbent. Amine compound of the core comprises a unit skeletal structure of formulae -((CH2)x-NR)y- (II) and -((CH2)x-NH2) (III). Amine compound of the core is amino-silane selected from polyalkyleneimine including polyethylenimine or polypropylenimine, 3-aminopropyl-trimethoxysilane, trimethoxy(3-methylaminopropylsilane), N,N-dimethylaminopropyl trimethoxysilane, and/or N-(3-trimethoxysilylpropyl)diethylenetriamine. Amine compound of the core is present in an amount of 5-75 wt.% with respect to the total weight of the adsorbent. Molar ratio of nitrogen atom present in the amine compound immobilized on the support to epoxide is 1:0.2-1:1. TI Acid component removal method in high temperature in gasification-power-generation method, involves switching oxygen for modification-catalyst layer temperature rising or flow of air to upstream of carbon-dioxide absorber filled layer.
AU HAMA T
   SUGIMURA E
   SAWADA K
   SHINOKA T
   SHINOOKA T
   BIN L
AE HITACHI ZOSEN CORP (HITF-C)
   HITACHI ZOSEN CORP (HITF-C)
   HITACHI ZOSEN CORP (HITF-C)
   HITACHI SHIP & ENG CO LTD (HITF-C)
GA 2018405237
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves converting the gasification material into a partial combustion gas. The coal powder is collected from the gasification gas. The dust is removed from the coarse-powder structure removal completed gasifying gas. The carbon-dioxide is absorbed and reformed from the dust removal completed gasifying gas. The electric power is produced using the reformed gas. The carbon-dioxide absorption of the carbon-dioxide absorber is carried out by the temperature range of 450-700 degrees C of gas temperature areas. The oxygen for modification-catalyst layer temperature rising or the flow of air is switched to the immediate upstream of the carbon-dioxide absorber filled layer so that the absorber filled layer is heated up to 800-950 degrees C temperature range, and carbon-dioxide is made to leave from the absorber.
   USE - Acid component removal method in high temperature in gasification-power-generation method.
   ADVANTAGE - The high desalination/desulfurization performance can be achieved and the poisoning of a modification catalyst can be prevented. The problem of causing a raise of the pressure loss by clogging of an apparatus can be prevented. carbon-dioxide is made leave from the absorber thus the carbon-dioxide in gasifying gas is efficiently and permanently removable.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an acid component removal apparatus in high temperature in gasification-power-generation system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart of the gasification-power-generation system. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Bag filter (3)
   Power generation equipment (5)
   High temperature boiler (11)
   Cooing tower (12)
   Low temperature boiler (13)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - The cyclone upstream additive is chosen from the group consists of a calcined dolomite, a slaked lime, a sodium aluminate, and sodium bicarbonate. The carbon-dioxide absorber is chosen from the group consists of calcium oxide, calcium hydroxide, and calcined dolomite, and the reformed gas is carried out by sodium hydroxide aqueous solution so that the sodium carbonate which arose is collected and removed.

AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC BEIJING RES INST CHEM IND (SNPC-C)
GA 201838585H
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a desorption tower (17) whose vapor phase outlet is connected to inlet of first-stage pressure swing adsorption regeneration tower (4). The adsorbed components obtained in first-stage pressure swing adsorption regeneration tower are sent to a shallow cool methanol absorption tower (14). The methanol absorbent with carbon dioxide component (16) is sent to desorption tower, and the gas rich in carbon dioxide component (19) is obtained at top of tower through rectification. The methanol absorbent without carbon dioxide component (18) is returned to cool methanol absorption tower.
   USE - System for removing non-methane hydrocarbons and recovering two carbon or more hydrocarbons from coal crushing tail gas in low-temperature methanol washing process.
   ADVANTAGE - The system ensures that the content of non-methane hydrocarbons in the carbon dioxide emitted from the atmosphere reaches the emission requirements, and the fraction of carbon two or more in the exhaust gas is recovered.

   Cool methanol absorption tower (14)
   Methanol absorbent with carbon dioxide component (16)
   Desorption tower (17)
   Methanol absorbent without carbon dioxide component (18)
   Gas rich in carbon dioxide component (19)

PT P
PN WO2022221861-A1
TI Employing waste material to sequester carbon            dioxide, by e.g. contacting magnesium hydroxide with            gas stream, contacting waste material with hydrochloric            acid, recovering mineral ion salts, and reacting            mineral ions salts.
AU JONES J
AE CARBONFREE CHEM HOLDINGS LLC (CARB-Non-standard)
GA 2022D2267V
AB    NOVELTY - Employing (M1) a waste material to sequester                carbon dioxide, comprises: reacting a magnesium                chloride hydrate-containing material with steam to                generate hydrochloric acid and magnesium hydroxide;                contacting the magnesium hydroxide with a gas                stream comprising carbon dioxide to provide a                partially or fully carbonated stream; contacting                waste material with the hydrochloric acid and                optionally water to leach mineral ion salts from                the waste material into a brine or slurry;                recovering the mineral ion salts from the brine or                slurry; and reacting the mineral ions salts with                the partially or fully carbonated stream to                sequester carbon dioxide in the form of mineral ion                carbonate salts.
   USE - The method is useful for employing a waste                material to sequester carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - None given.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                employing (M2) a geological silicate mineral to                sequester carbon dioxide, comprising reacting a                magnesium chloride hydrate-containing material with                steam to generate hydrochloric acid and magnesium                hydroxide, contacting the magnesium hydroxide with                a gas stream comprising carbon dioxide to provide a                partially or fully carbonated stream, contacting                the geological silicate mineral with the                hydrochloric acid and optionally water to leach                mineral ion salts from the geological silicate                mineral into a brine or slurry, recovering the                mineral ion salts from the brine or slurry, and                reacting the mineral ions salts with the partially                or fully carbonated stream to sequester carbon                dioxide in the form of mineral ion carbonate                salts.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step of contacting the                  waste material with the hydrochloric acid: is                  performed at ambient temperature; is performed at                  ambient temperature at ambient pressure; and does                  not involve mechanical agitation or abrasion of                  solids. The step of contacting the waste material                  with the hydrochloric acid further comprises                  recirculating liquids to increase contact between                  the waste material and the hydrochloric acid. The                  method further comprises: transferring the brine                  or slurry to a settling tank; allowing solids in                  the brine or slurry to settle at the bottom of                  the settling tank; transferring the brine or                  slurry to an evaporation pond; allowing liquid in                  the brine or slurry to evaporate. Preferred                  Components: The partially or fully carbonated                  stream comprises magnesium hydroxide compounds of                  formula (Mg(OH)x(HCO)y) (I). The mineral ion                  salts comprise at least one group II metal                  cation, where the group II metal cation is a                  calcium cation, or a magnesium cation. The waste                  material is an industrial waste material. The                  industrial waste material comprises masonry,                  concrete, steel furnace slag, bio-mass fuel                  production slag, or waste coal fly ash. The                  carbon dioxide is a component of a flue gas                  stream. The carbon dioxide is atmospheric carbon                  dioxide. The solar energy and/or naturally                  occurring wind are harnessed to increase the rate                  of evaporation. The non-renewable energy is not                  used to increase the rate of evaporation. No                  energy is provided to the evaporation pond to                  increase the rate of evaporation. The mineral ion                  carbonate salts comprise calcium carbonate. The                  geological silicate mineral comprises olivine,                  forsterite, pyrope, spessartine, grossular,                  andradite, uvarovite, hydrogrossular, norbergite,                  chondrodite, humite, clinohumite, datolite,                  titanite, chloritoid, lawsonite, axinite,                  ilvaite, epidote, zoisite, tanzanite,                  clinozoisite, allanite, dollaseite, vesuvianite,                  paopgoite, tourmaline, osumilite, cordierite,                  sekaninaite, eudialyte, milarite, enstatite,                  pigeonite, diopside, hedenbergite, augite,                  proxferroite, wollastonite, pectolite,                  anthophyllite, cummingtonite, tremolite,                  actinolite, hornblende, glaucophane,                  arfvedsonite, antigorite, chrysotile, lizardite,                  talc, illite, montmorillonite, chlorite,                  vermiculite, sepiolite, palygorskite, biotite,                  phlogopite, margarite, glauconite, oligoclase,                  andesine, labradorite, bytownite, anorthite,                  cancrinite, hauyne, lazurite, erionite,                  chabazite, heulandite, stilbite, scolecite,                  mordenite, and/or clinoenstatite.um of x, and y=2.

GA 2022747055
AB    NOVELTY - The device (100) has oxidation vessel (1)                which is provided with an oxidation electrode (2).                A porous reduction electrode-supported electrolyte                membrane (20) is arranged between the oxidation                vessel and the reduction vessel (4). A porous                reduction electrode-supported electrolyte membrane                is a joined portion that is formed by joining a                porous reduction electrode (5) formed by dispersing                a first electrode membrane in an inside void to a                second electrolyte membrane (6). The second                electrolyte membrane is arranged on the oxidation                vessel side. The porous reduction electrode is                arranged on the reduction vessel side and is                connected to the oxidation electrode through a                conducting wire (7). The reduction reaction of the                carbon dioxide in the reduction vessel is performed                by the action of electrons flowing through the                conducting wire.

   ADVANTAGE - The efficiency of carbon dioxide reduction                reaction in a carbon dioxide gas phase reduction                device is improved. AU EON J S

GA 202271422B
AB    NOVELTY - The system (100) has a tank lorry that stores LNG. A heat exchanger (120) receives the LNG from the tank lorry through an LNG transfer pump. A reformer (130) cracks NG. A carbon dioxide production unit (140) produces a liquefied carbon dioxide (LCO2) and dry ice. A fuel cell unit (150) generates electricity and heat. A smart farm unit (160) receives the heat from the fuel cell unit and uses the heat for heating and cooling. An ultra-low temperature quick-freezing unit (170) rapidly freezes the product produced by the smart farm unit using the refrigerant received from the heat exchanger. The smart farm receives heat from the fuel cell unit to perform cooling. The liquefied carbon dioxide is received from the liquefied carbon dioxide production facility unit.
   USE - Energy-consuming liquefied natural gas (LNG) convergence utilization system.
   ADVANTAGE - The carbon dioxide generated in the reforming process for the utilization of hydrogen resources is captured and recycled in natural gas. The carbon dioxide produces dry ice and liquefied carbon dioxide by acquiring electricity and heat through hydrogen.

TI Synthesizing methanol by carbon dioxide and            methane comprises using argon carrier gas, performing            dielectric barrier discharge to obtain oxygen radical            plasma jets and methyl-hydrogen-based mixed plasma jet            and performing radical reaction.
AU ZHANG X
   HE Z
   WENREN Y
   ZHU Z
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG SCI-TECH (UZST-C)
GA 202184993S
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing methanol by carbon dioxide and                methane comprises (a) using argon as carrier gas,                performing first dielectric barrier discharge to                obtain oxygen radical plasma jets by carbon                dioxide, (b) using argon as a carrier gas,                performing second dielectric barrier discharge to                obtain methyl-hydrogen-based mixed plasma jet by                methane and (c) performing free radical reaction on                the oxygen radical plasma jet and the                methyl-hydrogen mixed plasma jet to obtain methanol                and performing the steps (a) and (b) at the same                time.
   USE - The method is useful for synthesizing methanol                by carbon dioxide and methane.
   ADVANTAGE - The method obtains methanol under normal                temperature and pressure; is simple and suitable                for industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) device for synthesizing methanol by carbon                dioxide and methane; and
   (2) synthesizing methanol using device                comprising connecting the first metal rod and the                second metal rod to a power source, grounding the                first metal wire mesh and the second metal wire                mesh, passing argon and carbon dioxide into the                first insulating pipe through the first air inlet                pipe, passing argon and methane into the second                insulating pipe through the second air inlet pipe,                forming an oxygen radical plasma jet by carbon                dioxide, forming methyl-hydrogen-based mixed plasma                jet by methane when electrifying to carry out                dielectric barrier discharge and allowing the                oxygen radical plasma jet and the                methyl-hydrogen-based mixed plasma jet undergoing                radical reaction to obtain methanol
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation                of the device for synthesizing methanol by carbon                dioxide and methane.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The working voltages                  of the first dielectric barrier discharge and the                  second dielectric barrier discharge are                  independently 25-30kV and the discharge power is                  independently 20-50W. The power supply used is a                  high-voltage radio frequency power supply and has                  a frequency of 13.56 MHz. The flow rate of the                  carbon dioxide and methane are independently                  500-700 ml/minute. In the steps (a) and (b) the                  flow rate of the argon gas is independently 3-4                  l/minute.

AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide viscosity-increasing fluid loss                copolymer comprises structural unit A (I), a                structural unit B (II), and a structural unit C                (III), where where molar ratio of the structural                unit A, the structural unit B and the structural                unit C is 15-60:10-35:10-60.
   USE - Copolymer used as a viscosity-increasing fluid                loss agent in carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The copolymer improves the suspending and                carrying capacity of carbon dioxide as a drilling                fluid and fracturing fluid on cuttings and                proppants, adjusts rheology, increases the                viscosity of the system, and reduces the carbon                dioxide in the reservoir and improve its                displacement efficiency as a displacement                fluid.

   R1, R3, R5, R6 and R7=H or a 1-8C alkyl                group;
   R2=1-8C fluorocarbon group; and
   R4=1-8C alkylene group.
   . An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                method for preparing carbon dioxide                viscosity-increasing fluid loss copolymer, which                involves subjecting ethyl prop-2-enoate derivative                of formula (IV), the allylbenzene derivative of                formula (V), and the methyl prop-2-enoate                derivative of formula (VI) to free radical                polymerization in the presence of a free radical                initiator to obtain a carbon dioxide                viscosity-increasing fluid loss copolymer..
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The weight average                  molecular weight of the carbon dioxide                  viscosity-increasing fluid loss reducing                  copolymer is 100000-600000 g/mol. The molar ratio                  of the structural unit A, the structural unit B                  and the structural unit C is 25-35:20-30:40-60.                  The molar ratio of the ethyl prop-2-enoate                  derivative, the allylbenzene derivative and the                  methyl prop-2-enoate derivative is                  15-60:10-35:10-60. The free radical initiator is                  azobisisobutyronitrile, 4,4'azo (4-cyanovaleric                  acid), azobisisobutylamidine hydrochloride,                  azobisisopropylimidazoline hydrochloride and                  azobisisobutylimidazoline hydrochloride. The free                  radicals initiate dosage is 0.07-0.25mmol based                  on the total molar amount of 1 mol of ethyl                  prop-2-enoate derivative, allylbenzene                  derivative, and methyl prop-2-enoate derivative.                  The conditions of the radical polymerization                  reaction include nert gas protection, the                  temperature of the radical polymerization                  reaction is 50-70 degrees C for 6-36 hours.

PN US10156554-B1
TI System for determining infiltration of building            has infiltration module that determines infiltration of            building based on number of air changes as function of            difference of initial and baseline carbon dioxide            concentrations.
AU HOFF T E
AE CLEAN POWER RES LLC (CLEA-Non-standard)
GA 2018A0052D
AB    NOVELTY - The system has an infiltration module that                determines infiltration of a structure (11) such as                building based on number of air changes as a                function of the difference of the initial carbon                dioxide concentration less the baseline carbon                dioxide concentration over one or more of the                further carbon dioxide concentration at a given                time less the baseline carbon dioxide concentration                and the given time. A conduction module determines                the conduction of building as the difference of                total thermal conductivity less the infiltration of                the building. The at least one improvement to a                shell (12) of the building is performed based on                infiltration and conduction.
   USE - System for determining infiltration of                building through empirical testing using carbon                dioxide concentration monitoring device.
   ADVANTAGE - The equation to calculate annual fuel                consumption and its derivatives is simplified over                the fully parameterized form of the equation used                in energy audit analysis, yet without loss of                accuracy. The carbon dioxide monitoring device                replaces the blower door test component of an                on-site energy audit to baseline infiltration. The                use of device can be incorporated into the                measurement and evaluation portion of utility's                energy efficiency program to verify the                effectiveness of building sealing                initiatives.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for determining infiltration of a building through                empirical testing using a carbon dioxide                concentration monitoring device.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a functional block diagram                of the heating losses and gains relative to a                structure.
   Structure (11)
   Shell (12)
   Gains (13)
   Losses in interior heating (14)
   Occupants (15)
