PT P
PN US2022081375-A1; IN202021040369-A; RU2773213-C1; ZA202106756-A
TI Catalytic oxidative dehydrogenation (ODH) of            alkane and generating hydrogen using integrated process            by e.g. feeding fresh hydrocarbon alkane feed along            with carbon dioxide rich stream to ODH reactor            comprising dehydrogenation catalyst.
AU RAMAKUMAR S S V
   KAPUR G S
   SAU M
   RAVULURI S
   SUBRAMANI S
   THAKUR R M
   DOOSA H B
   NATH V V
AE INDIAN OIL CORP LTD (INOI-C)
   INDIAN OIL CORP LTD (INOI-C)
   INDIAN OIL CORP LTD (INOI-C)
GA 202239718V
AB    NOVELTY - Catalytic oxidative dehydrogenation (ODH) of                alkane and generating hydrogen using integrated                process comprises: e.g. (a) feeding a fresh                hydrocarbon alkane feed (1) along with a carbon                dioxide rich stream (2) to an ODH reactor                comprising dehydrogenation catalyst, where the                alkane feed gets converted into alkenes and                producing an effluent gas comprising hydrogen,                methane, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, water or                unreacted alkanes; (b) separating ODH effluent gas                from the ODH reactor in a gas separation system,                where the gas separation system comprises a                de-propanizer section, a de-butanizer and 4C                splitter section, a 3C splitter section, a                de-methanizer section and a 2C splitter section;                and (c) separating the ODH effluent gases into a                C4+ stream comprising 4C alkanes, 4C olefins and                higher hydrocarbons, a 3C stream (7) comprising                propane and propylene, and an off gas stream e.g.                hydrogen, methane, ethane, ethylene and carbon                monoxide in the de-propanizer section.
   USE - The method is useful for: catalytic oxidative                dehydrogenation of alkane(s) and generating                hydrogen using integrated process (claimed); and                manufacturing olefins e.g. ethylene and propylene                from lower alkanes.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: provides light olefins with high                selectivity; does not require purification system                for carbon dioxide; does not require separate                hydrogen separation system; reduces capital and                operating cost; produces high valued petrochemical                feed stocks e.g. ethylene and propylene by                utilizing existing units in the oil refinery;                utilizes carbon dioxide from existing HGU PSA off                gas thus eliminates requirement of additional                carbon dioxide purification system; provides                potential of producing hydrogen from ODH process                which otherwise would have been lost as water;                increases production of hydrogen from HGU by                recycling carbon monoxide from ODH reaction; and                does not require separate carbon monoxide                mitigation/conversion system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Catalytic oxidative dehydrogenation (ODH) of                alkane(s) and generating hydrogen using integrated                process, comprises: (a) feeding a fresh hydrocarbon                alkane feed (1) along with a carbon dioxide rich                stream (2) to an ODH reactor comprising                dehydrogenation catalyst, where the alkane feed                gets converted into alkenes and producing an                effluent gas comprising hydrogen, methane, carbon                monoxide, carbon dioxide, water or unreacted                alkanes; (b) separating ODH effluent gas from the                ODH reactor in a gas separation system, where the                gas separation system comprises a de-propanizer                section, a de-butanizer and 4C splitter section, a                3C splitter section, a de-methanizer section and a                2C splitter section; (c) separating the ODH                effluent gases into a C4+ stream comprising 4C                alkanes, 4C olefins and higher hydrocarbons, a 3C                stream (7) comprising propane and propylene, and an                off gas stream comprising hydrogen, methane,                ethane, ethylene, carbon monoxide and carbon                dioxide in the de-propanizer section, (d)                separating butanes, butene and heavy hydrocarbons                from the C4+ stream in the de-butanizer and the 4C                splitter section and recycling back the butane to                the ODH reactor for further conversion; (e)                separating the 3C stream into petrochemical grade                propylene and a propane rich stream (4) in a 3C                splitter and recycling back the propane rich stream                to the ODH reactor for further conversion; (f)                routing the off gas stream from the de-propanizer                section to de-methanizer section, where the off gas                stream gets separated into two streams a stream                comprising ethane, ethylene and carbon dioxide and                a stream comprising hydrogen, methane and carbon                monoxide; (g) routing the stream comprising ethane,                ethylene and the carbon dioxide to the 2C-splitter                section, where the stream is separated into                petrochemical grade ethylene and a stream rich in                ethane and carbon dioxide; (h) routing the stream                rich in ethane and carbon dioxide back to the ODH                reactor for recycling of ethane and carbon dioxide;                (i) routing the stream coming from the                de-methanizer section comprising hydrogen, methane                and carbon monoxide to a hydrogen generation unit                (HGU) shift reactor and mixing the stream with                effluent gases coming from a hydrogen generation                unit (HGU) reformer reactor, where the HGU reformer                reactor is fed with methane, naphtha or other                suitable hydrocarbon feed and steam and the                hydrocarbon feed and steam undergo reforming                reaction at elevated temperatures of 750-850degrees                Celsius and produces effluent gas including                hydrogen, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and                un-reacted methane and steam and traces of                nitrogen; (j) converting the carbon monoxide from                the effluent gases and from the stream into                hydrogen and carbon dioxide in presence of steam in                the HGU shift reactor; (k) routing product gases                from the HGU shift reactor comprising hydrogen,                carbon dioxide and rest carbon monoxide, methane,                steam and nitrogen to a pressure swing adsorption                (PSA) section of the HGU shift reactor to separate                pure hydrogen; (l) routing a portion of off gases                from the PSA having 80-95 wt.% carbon dioxide,                0.1-5 wt.% carbon monoxide, 0.1-10 wt.% methane,                0.1-5 wt.% hydrogen and traces of steam and                nitrogen to furnace of the HGU reformer reactor as                fuel; and (m) routing remaining portion of the PSA                off gas to the ODH reactor as a carbon dioxide                source to supplement the recycle carbon dioxide                rich stream coming from the 2C splitter                section.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a schematic view of process                for oxidative dehydrogenation of alkane.
   Fresh hydrocarbon alkane feed (1)
   Carbon dioxide rich stream (2)
   Propane rich stream (4)
   3C Stream (7)
   Off gas stream (8)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The fresh hydrocarbon                  alkane feed comprises ethane, propane, n-butane                  and/or iso-butane. The carbon dioxide rich stream                  is a PSA off gas stream. The conversion of the                  propane to propylene is 20-40 wt.% and                  selectivity of the propylene is 85-95 wt.%. The                  ODH reactor is a fixed bed tubular reactor or a                  fluidized bed reactor and/or a moving bed                  reactor. The diluent (nitrogen) concentration in                  a mixture of the fresh hydrocarbon alkane feed                  and the carbon dioxide rich stream in step (a) is                  0.01-30 vol.%. The molar ratio of the fresh                  hydrocarbon alkane feed to the carbon dioxide fed                  to ODH reactor in step (a) is 0.3-0.8. The gas                  hourly space velocity (GHSV) of a mixture of the                  fresh hydrocarbon alkane feed and the carbon                  dioxide rich stream in step (a) is 200-2500 per                  hour.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The dehydrogenation                  catalyst comprises metal oxides, where the metals                  in the metal oxides comprise VB, VIB, VIII and                  alkali metals group.
   EXAMPLE - Performance of oxidative dehydrogenation (ODH)                catalyst in the presence of hydrogen generation                unit (HGU) pressure swing adsorption (PSA) off-gas                (synthetic mixture comprised of carbon dioxide,                methane and hydrogen). Vanadium based                dehydrogenation catalyst was prepared and used in                the process. The experiments were conducted in a                fixed-bed tubular reactor of 9 mm ID containing                1.618 g catalyst at reaction temperature of                600degrees Celsius as measured by the thermocouple                located in the catalyst bed. Two experiments were                conducted, one using pure propane as feed and pure                carbon dioxide (greater than 99%) as co-feed and                other using pure propane as feed and synthetic PSA                off-gas mixture as co-feed. The molar ratio of                propane to carbon dioxide in the feed mixture was                0.5-0.75, gas hourly space velocity (GHSV) of the                feed gas was 500-1000 per hour and diluent                (nitrogen) concentration in the feed mixture was                5-20 vol.%.

PT P
PN CN114982620-A
TI Energy-saving and emission-reducing large-scale            continuous production system of hydroponic forage            comprises forage production workshop, biogas generator            room, and ventilation corridor connecting forage            production workshop and biogas generator room, and            supporting biogas production system.
AU WAN G
   ZHOU S
   BI Y
   YAO J
   BI J
AE JINFENG ZHIHUI AGRIC TECHNOLOGY TAIAN CO (JINF-Non-standard)
GA 2022B9899M
AB    NOVELTY - Energy-saving and emission-reducing                large-scale continuous production system of                hydroponic forage comprises forage production                workshop (1), biogas generator room (2), and                ventilation corridor (3) connecting the forage                production workshop and biogas generator room, and                supporting biogas production system. The forage                production workshop includes a production area                (10), a harvesting room (20), a container                disinfection room (30), a seed treatment room (40),                and a sowing room (50), and outside the production                workshop, there are also forage loading platforms                (60) and seed storage towers (70). The ventilation                pipe (101) is horizontally arranged on the wall of                the air inlet end of the production area. The                ventilation pipe is provided with a number of                ventilation holes (115). The air inlet of the                ventilation pipe is connected to the air outlet                (205) of the biogas generator room through the                ventilation corridor.
   USE - Energy-saving and emission-reducing                large-scale continuous production system of                hydroponic forage.
   ADVANTAGE - The energy-saving and emission-reducing                large-scale continuous production system of                hydroponic forage has reasonable design and good                practical application value.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                of using an energy-saving and emission-reduction                type large-scale hydroponic forage continuous                production system, which involves: (a) making                loading and unloading manipulator I (118) moves to                the side of the corresponding planting rack when                the pasture to be harvested is on the planting rack                (107), placing the pasture planting tray (108) on                the conveyor belt I (116), using the conveyor belt                I to convey the pasture planting tray to the                harvesting room, pouring out the pasture from the                harvesting room, transferring to the loading                platform, transferring empty planting tray to the                container disinfection room;
   (b) cleaning the used tray, disinfecting,                drying in a container disinfection room for hold                to, soaking the seeds in the seed treatment room,                treating chemically and physically, uniformly                sowing treated seeds on the cleaned, sterilizing,                drying planting trays in a sowing room,                transferring to a conveyor belt II (119) through a                window;
   (c) making the loading and unloading                manipulator II (121) moves to the side of the                required planting frame, placing the tray on the                conveyor belt II at the corresponding position of                the planting frame, making the loading and                unloading manipulator II starts to move to the                other end with specific production cycle,                subjecting the pasture grass from the air outlet                end to the air inlet end to different stages from                seed germination to harvesting, where each layer in                each group of planting frames is sequentially                divided into six different areas with specific                different requirements on illumination, moisture                and nutrition in different stages, the areas are a                germination area, an initial seedling stage, a                growth early stage, a growth middle stage, a growth                later stage and a period to be harvested;
   (d) subjecting pasture grass in different                stages to different moisture, illumination and                nutrition treatments, spraying clear water in the                germination area without supplementing                illumination, spraying clear water at the initial                seedling stage to supplement weak light, spraying                low-concentration nutrient solution in the early                stage of growth, enhancing illumination, increasing                the concentration of the nutrient solution in the                middle growth period, enhancing illumination;
   (e) spraying nutrient solution with the                highest concentration at the later growth stage,                irradiating most intensely, stopping spraying in                the harvest period, continuing to irradiate with                strong light, making                high-carbon-dioxide-concentration gas generated by                a biogas generator (201) enters a ventilation                corridor through an exhaust gas pipeline (202),                passing through a filter and a cooling device, then                enters a ventilation pipe through an air blower,                and enters a production area through a ventilation                hole (115);
   (f) making the air with high carbon dioxide                concentration flows from the air inlet end to the                air outlet end in the flow direction of the                production area, absorbing by photosynthesis of                plants in the process of flowing to the air outlet                end, reducing gradually the carbon dioxide                concentration until the carbon dioxide                concentration is close to or lower than the carbon                dioxide concentration of the atmosphere and is                discharging outdoors through the air outlet                (106);
   (g) arranging turbulent fan (122) on the side                surface of the air inlet end of the planting frame                enables air to be distributed uniformly on one hand                and enables the pasture to be harvested to be kept                in a relatively dry state on the other hand, making                air in the biogas generator room enters the                ventilation corridor through the generator room air                outlet (205) when heat needs to be supplemented for                the production area in cold seasons, and enters the                ventilation pipe together with biogas generator                exhaust gas through filtration, and then enters the                production area, closing air outlet of the biogas                generator room when heat does not need to be                supplemented for a production area in a mild                season, opening an external exhaust fan (204),                exhausting high-temperature air of the biogas                generator room through the external exhaust fan,                opening an air inlet of the ventilation corridor,                external air enters the ventilation corridor and                enters the ventilation pipe together with exhaust                gas of the biogas generator through                filtration;
   (h) starting temperature reducing device to                reduce the temperature of the air in the                ventilation corridor when the temperature needs to                be reduced in a high-temperature season, keeping                the temperature environment suitable for growth in                the production area by the temperature adjusting                means;
   (i) storing feces of a cattle farm, mixing in                a feces temporary storage pool (6), conveying to a                biogas fermentation tank (5) by lifting equipment                or a pipeline for biogas production, conveying the                produced biogas to a biogas storage cabinet (4) by                a pipeline, making supernatant fluid flows to an                oxidation pond (9) to be kept stand and oxidized                for pasture production water after biogas slurry is                precipitated in a biogas slurry precipitation tank                (8), precipitating solid portion after solid-liquid                separation for a certain time and biogas residues                enter a biogas residue treatment room;
   (j) using biogas residue as organic                fertilizers after being treated, making the                produced biogas enters a biogas generator (201)                through a pipeline, using biogas power generation                as energy supply, and using exhaust gas enters a                production area as carbon dioxide supplement.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a plan view of an                energy-saving and emission-reducing large-scale                continuous production system of hydroponic                forage.
   2Biogas generator room
   3Ventilation corridor
   10Production area
   20Harvesting room
   30Container disinfection room
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.); P13 (Plant culture, dairy products (A01G, H, J).); Q46 (Building aids, special structures, ladders (E04G,H, E06C)); X15 (Non-Fossil Fuel Power Generating Systems); X25 (Industrial Electric Equipment)
MC D04-A02; D04-A01P1; D04-A01K2; D04-A01J; D04-A01F3; D04-A01F1; H09-F03; P13-A99; P13-E03; P13-B01; Q46-B07; X15-A10; X15-A02; X25-N01; X25-H03

PT P
PN CN112830821-A
TI Coupling polygeneration of biomass and livestock            and poultry manure by pretreating livestock and poultry            manure and straw, fermenting, desulfurizing,            separating, preparing natural gas, refrigerating and            processing to obtain fertilizer.
AU ZHU J
   WANG Q
   ZHANG Y
   LV Y
   ZHOU H
   QIAO Z
   LU Z
   ZHANG W
   GAO J
   LIU S
AE NAT BIOLOGICAL POWER GROUP CO LTD (NABI-Non-standard)
GA 202160558T
AB    NOVELTY - Method for coupling polygeneration of biomass                and livestock and poultry manure involves (a)                combusting straw, (b) pretreating livestock and                poultry manure and straw, (c) performing anaerobic                fermentation, (d) desulfurizing, (e) performing                solid-liquid separation, (f) preparing natural gas                by removing saturated water vapor, hydrogen sulfide                and fine dust particles in desulfurized biogas,                heating by a constant temperature heater to obtain                compressed biogas and allowing the compressed                biogas to enter the membrane separation system                evenly through the gas separation pipeline to be                separated to obtain bio-natural gas, (g)                refrigerating and (h) pulverizing the solid organic                fertilizer produced in the organic fertilizer                workshop in a material pulverizer, adding ash                generated after straw burning and the pulverized                organic fertilizer to the mixer, granulating using                a granulation mechanism, drying and cooling to                obtain organic carbon fertilizer.
   USE - The method is useful for coupling                polygeneration of biomass and livestock and poultry                manure for producing electricity, heat, cold,                biogas and organic fertilizer.
   ADVANTAGE - The method realizes harmless utilization of                resources.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for coupling polygeneration of biomass                and livestock and poultry manure involves (a)                combusting straw by crushing the straw to 1 cm,                placing into an acidification tank containing an                organic acid from Acetobacter, Gram-negative                Bacillus, glucose oxidizing Bacillus and                Trichoderma for hydrolysis and acidification,                sending to direct-fired straw-fired power                generation water-cooled vibrating grate boiler to                burn to generate electricity and providing steam                and heat for production of bio-natural gas, (b)                pretreating raw material by mixing the livestock                and poultry manure with the returned biogas slurry                in the septic tank for septic homogenization,                allowing the mixed septic gas to flow through the                mechanical grille, then entering the manure                homogenization tank for further homogenization,                performing heated hydrolysis treatment at 50-80                degrees C for 20-100 minutes, then entering into a                fermentation equipment for aerobic fermentation,                fermenting to rapidly decompose within 1-2 days,                releasing heat of 50-60 degrees C, supplying oxygen                into the fermentation equipment through the air                supply system, where the ventilation volume is                0.05-0.2 m3.(minute.m3)-1, fermenting and                decomposing the manure for 5-7 days, pumping the                treated manure from the manure feed tank to the                continuously stirred tank reactor (CSTR) anaerobic                fermentation tank, pouring the straw into silo,                sending to a crusher through a conveyor, sending                the crushed straw to the hydrolysis and                acidification tank through a conveyor, subjecting                the straw to heating and micro-aerobic hydrolysis                in the hydrolysis and acidification tank at 50-70                degrees C for 2-3 days, using the refluxed biogas                slurry for preparation and inoculation and sending                the treated straw into the CSTR anaerobic                fermentation tank through the screw pump, (c)                performing anaerobic fermentation by using a medium                temperature anaerobic fermentation in the CSTR                anaerobic fermentation tank, where the total solid                concentration of the raw materials is 3-15%,                equipping the anaerobic fermentation tank with a                warming device, fermenting at 38 degrees C and 25                revolutions/minute (rpm) for 25.9 days under pH of                7, using a straw generator to cool the circulating                water to provide a heat source for increasing the                temperature of the material in the tank,                discharging the materials after anaerobic                fermentation from the reactor and collecting the                biogas at the top of the reactor, (d) desulfurizing                by desulfurizing the biogas that adopts alkaline                biological desulfurization, purifying the biogas,                controlling the volumetric load of biological                desulfurization at 70-300 mg/(hour.l), influent                S2-concentration at 85-90 mg/l and dissolved oxygen                content of 1.3-2.5 mg/l, reducing hydrogen sulfide                gas content in the biogas to less than 200 ppm,                removing the water in the biogas by a cold-drying                dewatering machine, then entering into                double-membrane dry gas storage tank for temporary                storage, allowing a portion of biogas to enter the                boiler to supply heat for the system and                desulfurizing in fine desulfurization tower to                reduce the hydrogen sulfide in the biogas produced                by anaerobic fermentation to below 15 ppm, and (e)                performing solid-liquid separation by sending the                material after anaerobic fermentation to the                solid-liquid separator through a conveying pump for                solid-liquid separation, removing solid particles,                flowing the separated biogas slurry into a reflux                liquid temporary storage tank, conveying the biogas                residue by belts to the organic fertilizer workshop                for the production of solid organic fertilizer,                allowing the supernatant biogas slurry in the                biogas slurry temporary storage tank to flow into                pretreatment system as the return biogas slurry,                sending the remaining small amount of biogas slurry                to the biogas slurry sedimentation tank through the                biogas slurry pump for precipitation treatment,                then entering the biogas slurry tank for the                production of liquid organic fertilizer, entering                the biogas liquid that overflowed during separation                into an overflow liquid temporary storage tank, and                re-pumping back to the solid-liquid separator                through a screw pump for separation, (f) preparing                natural gas by allowing the desulfurized biogas to                pass through a freeze-dryer, a gas-water separator,                an activated carbon desulfurization tower and a                high-efficiency filter to remove saturated water                vapor, hydrogen sulfide and fine dust particles,                heating by a constant temperature heater to obtain                compressed biogas and allowing the compressed                biogas to enter the membrane separation system                evenly through the gas separation pipeline to be                separated to obtain bio-natural gas, (g)                refrigerating by using a reversible carbon dioxide                adsorbent to absorb carbon dioxide from biogas,                changing the conditions so that the carbon dioxide                adsorbent releases carbon dioxide, through                condensation, entering into a compressor,                controlling temperature and pressurize to above 7                MPa, filling into a steel cylinder to obtain a                liquid carbon dioxide product, then entering into                expansion tank to obtain snowflake-like solid                carbon dioxide after depressurization, temperature                reduction, and expansion, using the pellet                production equipment to squeeze the snow powder                into thin rods, cutting into pellets and squeezing                into finished dry ice and (h) pulverizing the solid                organic fertilizer produced in the organic                fertilizer workshop in a material pulverizer,                adding ash generated after straw burning and the                pulverized organic fertilizer to the mixer,                granulating using a granulation mechanism, drying                and cooling to obtain organic fertilizer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The straw is yellow                  sage straw or silage straw. The solid-liquid                  separator is a screw extruder. Preferred Method:                  The method involves using 6 CSTR anaerobic                  fermentation tanks with a single tank volume of                  8000 m3 and adopting a two-stage series                  connection and same-stage parallel process mode,                  in which there are 3 first-level tanks and 3                  second-level tanks.
   ACTIVITY - Fertilizer.

AE SOUTHWESTERN INST PHYSICS (CNNU-C)
   UNIV CHENGDU TECHNOLOGY POLYTECHNICS (UYCH-Non-standard)
   SOUTHWESTERN INST PHYSICS (CNNU-C)
GA 201938143G
AB    (CN208749434-U)   NOVELTY - The utility model claims a vehicle tail gas processing device, comprising a tail gas processing device shell, the tail gas treatment device housing includes a air intake port connected with the automobile exhaust pipe outlet and an air outlet connected with the external atmosphere, the tail gas process device shell inside is hollow and provided with a damping layer on the cavity inner wall and the cavity is close to the air intake port one end provided with a first adsorption chamber, a rear side of the first adsorption chamber is orderly provided with a reducing chamber, oxidation chamber, an active carbon adsorption chamber, carbon dioxide degradation chamber, carbon dioxide decomposition chamber and carbon absorbing the carbon dioxide decomposition chamber connected to the air outlet of the tail gas processing device shell, suction cavity with carbon dioxide decomposition chamber connection, the utility model complete tail gas treatment, exhaust gas cleaning, stable working state, high processing efficiency, carbon can be recycled.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic structural view of an exhaust gas treatment device for automobile.
   Exhaust gas treatment device casing (1)
   Damping layer (2)
   First adsorption chamber (4)
   Reduction chamber (5)
   Oxidation chamber (6)
   Activated carbon adsorption chamber (7)
   Carbon dioxide degradation chamber (8)
   Carbon dioxide decomposition chamber (9)
   Carbon suction chamber (10)
EA    NOVELTY - The device has an exhaust gas treatment device casing (1) that includes an air inlet that is connected to exhaust pipe outlet of automobile and an air outlet that is connected to the outside atmosphere. The exhaust gas treatment device housing includes a cavity and a damping layer (2) is formed on inner wall of the cavity. A first adsorption chamber (4) is arranged in the cavity near the end of the air inlet. The back side of the first adsorption chamber is provided with a reduction chamber (5), an oxidation chamber (6), an activated carbon adsorption chamber (7), a carbon dioxide degradation chamber (8), a carbon dioxide decomposition chamber (9), and a carbon suction chamber (10). The carbon dioxide decomposition chamber is connected to an air outlet of the exhaust gas treatment device housing, and carbon suction chamber is connected to the carbon dioxide decomposition chamber. The rare earth decomposer has alumina as carrier, cerium as catalyst, and germanium oxide as cocatalyst.
   USE - Exhaust gas treatment device for automobile.
   ADVANTAGE - The exhaust gas is clean, working state is stable, processing and collecting efficiency is high, and carbon is recycled. The exhaust gas treatment device solves the problem of catalyst failure, and has a long service life, and catalyst use cost is remarkably reduced. The automobile exhaust gas treatment device completely treats the exhaust gas, and has high collection efficiency and zero pollution of emissions.

PN CN111848352-A
TI Preparing methanol by reducing carbon dioxide by            coke comprises reducing carbon dioxide using coke to            prepare carbon monoxide and reacting carbon monoxide            with molecular hydrogen.
AU TIAN T
   XIE Y
   WANG B
   WANG Z
   LI Y
   JIANG R
   ZHU M
AE SINOPEC ENERGY SAVING TECHNOLOGY SERVICE CO              LTD (SINO-Non-standard)
GA 2020B2693U
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing methanol by reducing carbon dioxide                by coke comprises reducing carbon dioxide using                coke to prepare carbon monoxide and reacting carbon                monoxide with molecular hydrogen to obtain final                product.
   USE - The method is useful as complementary carbon                source for carbon monoxide and for supplementing                the system energy.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: adopts gasification technology to                convert coke into carbon monoxide reducing gas,                which increases the carbon monoxide gas content in                the reducing gas while reducing the amount of water                vapor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The carbon dioxide is                  obtained by carbon dioxide removed from coal                  gasification shift, carbon dioxide in the process                  of ethylene oxidation to ethylene oxide or                  ethylene glycol, carbon dioxide in the process of                  propylene ammoxidation to acrylonitrile, carbon                  dioxide produced by naphtha hydrogen production                  and/or carbon dioxide captured by flue gas. In                  the reaction of reducing carbon dioxide to carbon                  monoxide by coke: the reaction temperature is                  700-1200 degrees C, preferably 750-1000 degrees                  C, and the reaction space velocity of carbon                  dioxide is 18-200 m3/(hr.m3), preferably                  50-100m3/(hr.m3). In the reaction of producing                  methanol from carbon monoxide and molecular                  hydrogen: the reaction temperature is 220-270                  degrees C, the reaction pressure is 3-6 MPa and                  the reacted hydrogen to carbon ratio is                  2.05-2.15. The method comprises reducing carbon                  dioxide by coke to prepare carbon monoxide and                  reacting in a fixed bed reactor or a fluidized                  bed reactor, preferably a fluidized bed reactor.                  Preferred Components: The particle size of the                  coke is 50-500 mu m, preferably 100-300 mu m, the                  mass fraction of element carbon in the coke is                  greater than 85%, the coke is impregnated with                  potassium carbonate solution, the amount of                  potassium carbonate in potassium carbonate                  solution is 1-10 wt.% coke. The carbon dioxide is                  also doped with molecular oxygen, preferably, the                  molecular oxygen doping amount is 20-80 vol.% of                  the carbon dioxide, preferably 70-80 wt.%. The                  method uses copper-based catalyst in the reaction                  of carbon monoxide and molecular hydrogen to                  produce methanol.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).)

   (3) gas separation device comprising the gas separation membrane;
   (4) gas collection device comprising the gas separation membrane; and
   (5) producing polymer membrane, comprising (a) preparing an amphiphilic comb-type copolymer by adding a polyethylene glycol-based polymer, polydimethylsiloxane-based polymer, and polymerization initiator to organic solvent and copolymerizing by radical polymerization, (b) preparing a mixed solution by mixing a solution of a polyethylene oxide-based polymer and a solution of the amphiphilic comb copolymer, and (c) preparing a polymer film by applying the mixed solution to a substrate and drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic solvent includes N-methyl-2-pyrrolidone, dimethyl sulfoxide, dimethyl acetamide, dimethylformamide and/or tetrahydrofuran (preferred).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The polyethylene glycol-based polymer is poly(ethylene glycol)-behenyl ether methacrylate (PEGBEM) of formula (I). The polydimethylsiloxane-based polymer is monomethacryloxypropyl-terminated poly(dimethyl siloxane. The amphiphilic comb copolymer is siloxane based compound of formula (III). The amphiphilic comb-type copolymer is obtained by polymerizing the polyethylene glycol-based polymer and the polydimethylsiloxane-based polymer in weight ratio of 1:0.5-1:2. The polyethylene oxide-based polymer has weight average molecular weight of 900000-5000000 g/mol, preferably 950000-1500000 g/mol. The amphiphilic comb copolymer has weight average molecular weight of 100000-800000 g/mol, preferably 20000-50000 g/mol. The polymer micelles have average particle diameter of 1-20 nm. The polymer film is a mixture of the polyethylene oxide-based polymer and the amphiphilic comb copolymer in weight ratio of 95:5-40:60. The polymer film exhibits X-ray diffraction analysis (XRD) having first effective peak and a second effective peak at 2 theta angle of 18-20 degrees and 22-24 degrees , respectively, where the intensity ratio of the (first effective peak)/(second effective peak) is 2-2.4. The gas separation membrane separates gases of carbon dioxide, nitrogen and methane. The gas separation membrane has carbon dioxide permeability of 182-240 barrer, carbon dioxide/nitrogen (CO2/N2) selectivity of 30-50 barrer, and carbon dioxide/methane (CO2/CH4) selectivity of 10-15 barrer. The polymerization initiator includes 2,2'-azobis-isobutylnitrile (preferred), 2,2'-azobis-2,4-dimethylvaleronitrile, dimethyl 2,2'-azobis(isobutyrate) and/or 2,2'-azobis(4-methoxyvaleronitrile). The mixed solution is a mixture of the polyethylene oxide-based polymer solution and the amphiphilic comb-type copolymer solution in weight ratio of 95:5-40:60, preferably 90:10-70:30.m = 1-50;p = 10-30;n = 100-250;x = 1-30;y = 5-150; andx:y = 1:1-1:20, where x and y are number of repeats of each repeating unit.Preferred Method: The step of preparing the amphiphilic comb copolymer is radical polymerization at 50-90 degrees C for 10-30 hours, preferably 60-66 degrees C for 16-20 hours.
   EXAMPLE - The examples does not provide specific preparation of the amphiphilic comb-type copolymer and polymer membrane so that do not support the claims.

The high temperature flue gas generated by the waste lead paste smelting can be directly used as heat carrier, or can be used as indirect heat carrier.

PT P
PN US2021300840-A1; US11299442-B2
TI Producing acetylene, ethylene, or both involves combusting fuel stream in combustion zone of supersonic reactor to produce combustion gas effluent stream, pyrolyzing feed stream in pyrolysis zone of the supersonic reactor.
AU SIEDLER N
   LUEBKE C
   WEGERER D
   JAIN P
AE UOP LLC (UNVO-C)
GA 2021B20831
AB    NOVELTY - Producing acetylene, ethylene, or both involves combusting fuel stream in combustion zone of supersonic reactor to produce combustion gas effluent stream, pyrolyzing feed stream in pyrolysis zone of the supersonic reactor in the presence of the combustion gas effluent stream to produce pyrolysis zone effluent stream, quenching pyrolysis zone effluent stream to produce quenched stream. The quenched stream is compressed to form compressed quenched stream. The compressed quenched stream is separated in solvent separation column into product stream and net gas stream. The net gas stream comprises hydrogen, methane, and carbon oxide. The portion of the carbon oxide of the net gas stream is converted in a methanation reactor into methane and produced methanation reactor effluent. The carbon dioxide stream is removed from the methanation reactor effluent in amine scrubber and generated methane containing stream and recycled the methane containing stream to the supersonic reactor.
   USE - Method for producing acetylene, ethylene, or both.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to produce acetylene, ethylene, or both that improves efficiency of the pyrolysis process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following: a method for converting carbon oxide into methane in acetylene or ethylene production process, which involves separating supersonic reactor effluent from supersonic reactor in solvent separation column to provide product stream comprises acetylene, or ethylene, or both and net gas stream comprises hydrogen, methane and carbon oxide, sending first portion of the net gas stream to pressure swing adsorption zone to recover hydrogen to produce hydrogen stream, sending a second portion of the net gas stream to methanation reactor to convert carbon oxide of the second portion of the net gas stream into methane and producing methanation reactor effluent, removing carbon dioxide stream from the methanation reactor effluent in amine scrubber generating methane containing stream and recycling methane containing stream to the supersonic reactor; and#a system for producing acetylene, ethylene, or both, which comprises supersonic reactor, solvent separation column containing solvent and in fluid communication with the supersonic reactor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method involves introducing a separate oxygen containing stream to the combustion zone. The fuel stream supplied to the combustion zone comprises methane or pipeline natural gas. The feed stream to the pyrolysis zone of the supersonic reactor comprises combined methane feed stream. The method involves passing fresh methane containing feed stream to the effluent line of an amine scrubber. The method involves hydrogenating the product stream from the solvent separation column in hydrogenation reactor to generate hydrogenated product stream, compressing hydrogenated product stream in compressor to form compressed hydrogenated product stream, separating the compressed hydrogenated product stream in product fractionation column to provide C2 and/or C3+ hydrocarbon stream and hydrogen stream and recycling the hydrogen stream from the product fractionation column to the hydrogenation reactor. The method involves drying the compressed product stream in a dryer prior to separating the compressed hydrogenated product stream to the product fractionation column. The method involves compressing portion of the net gas stream from the solvent separation column to form compressed net gas stream, adsorbing the compressed net gas stream in a pressure swing adsorption zone to recover an adsorbed hydrogen stream and recycling the adsorbed hydrogen stream to the hydrogenation reactor.
   EXAMPLE - None given.

PT P
PN CN112957273-A; CN112957273-B
TI Culturing stem cell factors used for beauty treatment, involves placing mesenchymal stem cells in serum-free medium, culturing, carrying out subculturing, washing with normal saline, and adding digestive juice.
AU ZHANG R
   XIE H
   ZHANG Q
   XIE J
   ZHANG J
   HAN F
   XU G
   FANG M
AE ZHANG R (ZHAN-Individual)
GA 2021684109
AB    NOVELTY - Culturing stem cell factors involves placing the mesenchymal stem cells in a serum-free medium, culturing at 35-37 degrees C, 5-10% carbon dioxide (CO2) for 7-10 days, carrying out subculturing, and after passage to 4 generations, washing with normal saline for 1-2 times, then adding the digestive juice, digesting and counting, replacing it with induction medium when the cell fusion degree reaches 80-90% and continuing to culture at 35-37 degrees C and 5-10% CO2 for 48-72 hours, filtering the induction medium 3-4 times, combining the filtrates and concentrating them to 1/10-1/5 of the original volume, adding physiological saline containing sodium citrate to make up to the original volume, letting it stand for 1-2 hours.
   USE - Method for culturing stem cell factors used for beauty treatment.
   ADVANTAGE - The method effectively improves the content of the cell factor in the prepared concentrated solution.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Culturing stem cell factors involves placing the mesenchymal stem cells in a serum-free medium, culturing at 35-37 degrees C, 5-10% carbon dioxide (CO2) for 7-10 days, carrying out subculturing, and after passage to 4 generations, washing with normal saline for 1-2 times, then adding the digestive juice, digesting and counting, replacing it with induction medium when the cell fusion degree reaches 80-90% and continuing to culture at 35-37 degrees C and 5-10% CO2 for 48-72 hours, filtering the induction medium 3-4 times, combining the filtrates and concentrating them to 1/10-1/5 of the original volume, adding physiological saline containing sodium citrate to make up to the original volume, letting it stand for 1-2 hours, filtering again, collecting the filtrate and concentrating it to 1/20-1/10 of the original volume, filtering the product with a filter membrane with a molecular weight cut-off of 8-14 kilodalton (KD), collecting the stem cell factor concentrate, and then adding the freeze-drying protective agent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Process: The tissue block containing mesenchymal stem cells is placed in a culture flask, and then serum-free medium is added at 35-37 C, and the incubated at 5-10% CO2, cultivated for 7-10 days. The serum-free medium is discarded when the cell confluence reaches 70-80%, the bottle wall is rinsed with saline, and then 0.25% pancreatin is added to infiltrate the adherent surface of the cells, and incubated at room temperature for 5-10 minutes. The bottle is shaked quickly after the cells become round, and the adherent surface of the cells is pippetted to obtain a cell suspension, centrifuged to discard the supernatant, and then continued subculture at a cell density of 6000-8000 cells/cm2 until the cells reach the fourth generation. Preferred Composition: The induction medium includes 0.5-2 mmol/L hypoxia simulant, 5-10 mmol/L trisodium citrate, 5-10 mmol/L methionine, 10-20 mmol/L glutamine, and Gibco Dulbecco's Modified Eagle Medium (DMEM) basic medium. Preferred Conditions: The concentration of the sodium citrate in the physiological saline in step is 0.01-0.05 mmol/L. The adding amount of the freeze-drying protective agent is 0.1-0.5 wt.% of the volume of the concentrated solution. Preferred Components: The hypoxia mimic is cobalt chloride or deferoxamine. The freeze-drying protective agent is trehalose, mannitol or progesterone.

TI Cleaning device system e.g. self-contained            cleaning device system for in vivo cleaning of lens,            has first button that runs cleaning cycle including            dispensing first fluid and second button runs drying            cycle including dispensing second fluid.

   MADNABEEL A

AB    NOVELTY - The cleaning device system (10) has a cleaning                device (12) which comprises a sheath (14) for                fitting over the shaft of the surgical device. The                heating channel is mounted proximate irrigation                channel. A nozzle (15) is coupled to a distal end                of the sheath. The nozzle having an irrigation port                and drying port. A module housing an irrigation                fluid reservoir, a gas canister and a power source.                A first fluid is dispensed from irrigation fluid                reservoir in the module through the irrigation                channel in the sheath and out the irrigation port                in the nozzle and a second fluid is dispensed from                the gas canister in the module through the drying                channel in the shaft and out the drying port in the                nozzle. A control pad (16) is provided with a first                button and a second button. The first button runs a                cleaning cycle including dispensing the first fluid                and the second button runs a drying cycle including                dispensing the second fluid. The first fluid is                saline and the second fluid is carbon dioxide                gas.
   USE - Cleaning device system e.g. self-contained                cleaning device system for in vivo cleaning of lens                of surgical tool, such as laparoscope and for use                with surgical device.

The                CO2 cartridge can have a reduced size compared to a                CO2 cartridge for a cleaning device using suction                but with suction being driven by the CO2                device.

PT P
PN CN111960443-A
TI Recovering lithium carbonate and            nickel-cobalt-manganese mixed metal oxides from waste            lithium batteries of ternary cathode materials,            comprises dividing waste lithium battery, cutting anode            electrode sheet and heating.
AU ZHOU X
AE ZHEJIANG FUDA TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 2020B81185
AB    NOVELTY - Recovering lithium carbonate and                nickel-cobalt-manganese mixed metal oxides from                waste lithium batteries of ternary cathode                materials, comprises e.g. (i) dividing waste                lithium battery into anode electrode sheet, cathode                electrode sheet, diaphragm, tabs and outer                packaging materials, (ii) cutting the anode                electrode sheet into small pieces, heating,                separating the aluminum current collector from                waste cathode material powder M, (iii) ball milling                the cathode material powder M with water to obtain                slurry, and transferring slurry into reactor, (iv)                filling the above reaction kettle with carbon                dioxide, fully reacting under certain temperature                and pressure, (v) separating solid N and solution                L, (vi) taking solution L to evaporating and                removing water, and (vii) taking solid N and drying                the obtained nickel-cobalt-manganese mixed metal                oxide and carbonate and calcining at high                temperature.
   USE - The method is useful for recovering lithium                carbonate and nickel-cobalt-manganese mixed metal                oxides from waste lithium batteries.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Recovering lithium carbonate and                nickel-cobalt-manganese mixed metal oxides from                waste lithium batteries of ternary cathode                materials, comprises (i) dividing waste lithium                battery into anode electrode sheet, cathode                electrode sheet, diaphragm, tabs and outer                packaging materials, (ii) cutting the anode                electrode sheet obtained in step (i), into small                pieces, and heating in air to obtain anode                electrode material fall off from the anode                electrode sheet, separating the aluminum current                collector from waste cathode material powder M,                (iii) ball milling the cathode material powder M                with water to obtain slurry, and transferring                slurry into reactor, (iv) filling the above                reaction kettle with carbon dioxide, fully reacting                under certain temperature and pressure, (v)                separating solid N and solution L, (vi) taking                solution L to evaporating and removing water to                obtain lithium carbonate, and (vii) taking solid N                and drying the obtained nickel-cobalt-manganese                mixed metal oxide and carbonate and calcining at                high temperature to obtain final product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (i), recovering                  other cathode electrode sheets, separators, tabs                  and outer packaging materials separately. In step                  (ii), the firing temperature of the anode                  electrode in the air is 350-600 degrees C,                  preferably 450-550 degrees C. In step (iv), the                  setting pressure for flushing carbon dioxide into                  reactor is 1-50 atm. In step (iv), the reaction                  temperature in the reactor is 30-220 degrees C.                  In step (iv), the reaction time in the reactor is                  1-20 hours. In step (vii), the baking temperature                  in the process of calcining the                  nickel-cobalt-manganese mixed metal oxide and                  carbonate is 400-900 degrees C. In step (vii),                  the calcining time in the process of baking the                  nickel-cobalt-manganese mixed metal oxide and                  carbonate is 2-20 hours.

PN CN111187959-A; CN111187959-B
TI Metal-rich titanium carbonitride nitride-based            cermet comprises nickel, cobalt, molybdenum carbide,            tungsten carbide and titanium carbon nitrogen            complex.
AU WAN W
   WANG J
   WANG Z
   FAN K
   LI Y
AE UNIV XIHUA (UYXH-C)
GA 202046545C
AB    NOVELTY - Metal-rich titanium carbonitride nitride-based                cermet comprises 10-20 wt.% nickel, 10-20 wt.%                cobalt, 5-10 wt.% molybdenum carbide, 10-20 wt.%                tungsten carbide and remaining amount of titanium                carbon nitrogen complex.
   USE - Used as metal-rich titanium carbonitride                nitride-based cermet.
   ADVANTAGE - The cermet: has simple process, higher                sintering efficiency and high continuity of                gradient layer structure and composition; does not                undergo structural deformation; has good mechanical                properties; and solves the problems of low                sintering efficiency, low gradient layer structure,                low component continuity and product structure                deformation.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Metal-rich titanium carbonitride nitride-based                cermet comprises 10-20 wt.% nickel, 10-20 wt.%                cobalt, 5-10 wt.% molybdenum carbide, 10-20 wt.%                tungsten carbide and remaining amount of titanium                carbon nitrogen complex (TiC0.7N0.3). An                INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing                metal-rich titanium carbonitride nitride-based                cermet comprising (a) weighing nickel, cobalt,                molybdenum carbide, tungsten carbide and titanium                carbon nitrogen complex separately and mixing to                obtain mixed powder, (b) ball milling the mixed                powder at a speed of 60-120 revolutions/minute for                48-72 hours, vacuum drying at 90-120 degrees C for                2-5 hours after filtration, granulating and                pressing after filtering again to obtain ceramic                green compact and (c) sintering the ceramic green                compact at 350-650 degrees C for 2-5 hours at a                time, sintering at 850-1050 degrees C for 2-3 hours                for second time, sintering for third time at                1380-1480 degrees C for 2-3 hours and cooling to                obtain titanium carbonitride-based cermets, where                the vacuum degree is 15-20 Pa during one-time                sintering, nitrogen is introduced during the second                sintering, the nitrogen pressure is 0.5-2 KPa,                mixed gas of carbon dioxide and hydrogen is                introduced during the third sintering, flow rate of                carbon dioxide and hydrogen gas is 1:1 and the                mixed gas pressure is 10-15 MPa.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The metal-rich                  titanium carbonitride nitride-based cermet                  comprises 15 wt.% nickel, 15 wt.% cobalt, 8 wt.%                  molybdenum carbide, 16 wt.% tungsten carbide and                  remaining amount of titanium carbon nitrogen                  complex. Preferred Method: The process of                  preparing metal-rich titanium carbonitride                  nitride-based cermet comprises filtering through                  a 200-400 mesh screen after ball milling, drying                  and filtered through a 600-800 mesh screen. The                  degree of vacuum is 1-5 Pa and the pressure                  during press molding is 200-500 MPa during vacuum                  drying. Preferred Components: The weight ratio of                  nickel to cobalt is 1:1-2. The weight ratio of                  molybdenum carbide and tungsten carbide is 1:1:3.                  The particle size of nickel is 1-2.5 mu m. The                  particle size of cobalt is 1-2.5 mu m. The                  particle size of molybdenum carbide is 1-2 mu m.                  The particle size of tungsten carbide is 1-2 mu                  m. The particle size of titanium carbon nitrogen                  complex is 0.8-2.5 mu m. The ball milling medium                  is No. 120 solvent gasoline and the adding amount                  is 0.8-1.5 ml/g. The grinding ball is a tungsten                  carbide alloy ball with a diameter of 6-8 mm and                  containing 8 wt.% cobalt. The pellet ratio is                  5-15:1 and 5-10 wt.% paraffin wax is added to the                  mixed powder.

AB    NOVELTY - Mouse embryonic stem cell induction culture                medium comprises basic culture medium supplemented                with 2-3 ml N2 cell culture additive, 4-6 ml B27                cell culture additive, 20-30 mg bovine serum                albumin, 4-6 ml non-essential amino acids, 4-6 ml                L-glutamine, 0.5-2 ml beta -mercaptoethanol, 4-5 ml                penicillin-streptomycin, 1 mM PD0325901, 3 mM                CHIR99021, 500-1500 U/ml leukemia inhibitory factor                (LIF) and 5-15 mM retinoic acid (RA), where the                amount of above-mentioned components is for every                500 ml medium. The basic culture medium is                Dulbecco's modified Eagle medium/nutrient mixture                F-12 (DMEM/F12) and Neurobasal (RTM: basal medium                designed for long-term maintenance and maturation                of pure pre-natal and embryonic neuronal cell                populations) medium in a volume ratio of 1:1.
   USE - The mouse embryonic stem cell induction                culture medium or mouse embryonic stem cell                induction culture method are useful for improving                2C-like cells (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The medium improves yield of 2C-like cells                from 0.2% to 3.3% in the control group. The method                increases the proportion of 2C-like cells in mouse                ESCs, experimentally verifies the expression level                and protein level of 2C-like genes in mouse ESCs in                a culture medium that does not contain fetal bovine                serum and provides new ideas for mammalian                totipotent stem cell research.

Preferred                  Cells: The mouse embryonic stem cells are                  selected from the two-cell embryonic gene NELFA                  and MERVL-labeled transgenic cell lines.

PN US2019282923-A1
TI Method for extracting solid materials from liquid, gas or liquid-gas solutions, involves collecting solid material in removable liner by passing solution through fluid stream inlet of extract collection container.
AU OGNIBENE E J
   WEBSTER M
   SULIK A
   STRICKO S
   AUBIN A
   HUDALLA C J
AE WATERS TECHNOLOGIES CORP (WTRS-C)
GA 201980197B
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves securing (101) a first removable liner provided within an extract collection container of a carbon dioxide (CO2)-based extraction system having a fluid stream inlet and a fluid stream outlet. A cap of the extract collection container is closed (103). A first solid material is collected (105) in the first removable liner by passing a first solution through the fluid stream inlet of the extract collection container. The extract collection container is depressurized (107) and removes the removable liner from the extract collection container. A second removable liner is secured and replaced (109) within the extract collection container. A second solid material is collected in the second removable liner by passing a second solution through the fluid stream inlet of the extract collection container.
   USE - Method for extracting solid materials from liquid, gas or liquid-gas solutions.
   ADVANTAGE - The contamination between different fractionation processes using the same collection container is prevented this increases yield. The method allows efficient collection of extracts from an extract collection container without leaving residue behind. The use of a removable liner separates solid and viscous extracts from the collection container and prevents extracts from attaching to the internal surfaces of the collection container provide increased purity of extract and reduced carryover or contamination between fractionation runs.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a carbon dioxide (CO2)-based extraction system for extracting solid materials from liquid, gas or liquid-gas solutions; and
   (2) a hydrocyclone separator for extracting solid materials from liquid, gas or liquid-gas solutions.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart of a method for collecting an extract from CO2-based extraction system.
   Step for securing first removable liner within extract collection container (101)
   Step for closing cap of extract collection container (103)
   Step for collecting first solid material in first removable liner (105)
   Step for depressurizing extract collection container (107)
   Step for replacing and securing second removable liner (109)

Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

TI Method for extracting heavy rare gases such as xenon and krypton from air, involves passing cooled inlet air through adsorption chamber of apparatus configured and arranged to adsorb heavy rare gas from cooled inlet air.
AU HORNBOSTEL M D
   NAGAR A
   PEREA E
AE SRI INT (STRI-C)
   SRI INT (STRI-C)
   STANFORD RES INST INT (STRI-C)
   SRI INT (STRI-C)
GA 2019675198
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves passing (222) inlet air through an airflow path of an apparatus. The carbon dioxide and gaseous water are removed (224) from the inlet air. The inlet air is cooled (226) to a threshold temperature while passing along the airflow path. The cooled inlet air is passed through an adsorption chamber of the apparatus configured and arranged to adsorb (228) heavy rare gas from the cooled inlet air while the cooled inlet air is in a gaseous state. The heavy rare gas is extracted (230) from the adsorption chamber.
   USE - Method for extracting heavy rare gases such as xenon and krypton from air. Can also be used for medical applications including anesthesia as xenon, imaging agent for lung cancer, and protection against brain damage in hypoxia cases, ion thrusters for deep-space missions or micro-thrusters for satellite position control and in fundamental physics experiments.
   ADVANTAGE - The method that harvest heavy rare gases, without the cost of separating the much larger quantities of oxygen, nitrogen, and argon is provided. The reuse of the cooled air reduces the cooling demands on the apparatus. The scalable production of xenon is allowed, thus new applications of xenon that overload the global xenon production capacity is enabled.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a device for extracting heavy rare gases from air.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating a method for extracting heavy rare gases from air.
   Step for passing inlet air through an airflow path of an apparatus (222)
   Step for removing carbon dioxide and gaseous water from the inlet air (224)
   Step for cooling inlet air to a threshold temperature (226)
   Step for adsorbing heavy rare gas from the cooled inlet air (228)
   Step for extracting heavy rare gas from the adsorption chamber (230)

PN WO2019145930-A2; WO2019145930-A3; CA3089935-A1; CN111788747-A; EP3747089-A2; US2021119399-A1; JP2021516452-W
TI Laser system e.g. carbon dioxide laser system, has            ceramic material has series of channel, so as to            generate zig-zag pathway, and folding mirrors that            together constitute laser resonator cavity, when            pathway is filled with gain medium.
AU TURGEMAN S
   SHEFFER E
   REMES M
   LAVI B Z
   BRESTEL M
   TURGMAN S
AE IDEA MACHINE DEV DESIGN & PRODN LTD (IDEA-Non-standard)
   IDEA MACHINE DEV DESIGN & PRODN LTD (IDEA-Non-standard)
   IDEA MACHINE DEV DESIGN & PRODN LTD (IDEA-Non-standard)
   IDEA MACHINE DEV DESIGN & PRODN LTD (IDEA-Non-standard)
GA 201967628L
AB    NOVELTY - The system has housing (11) that is provided                with a first end and a second end, and a bore (10)                having an axis running between first and second                ends. A pair of coaxial metallic electrodes is                arranged within the bore and is provided with an                inner electrode and an outer electrode. The                electrodes are constructed to have a gap between                electrodes and configured to have a radio frequency                (RF) field applied between electrodes. Folding                mirrors are arranged at each end region of the                housing. A ceramic material is provided in the gap                between the coaxial electrodes. The ceramic                material has a series of channel, so as to generate                a zig-zag pathway between mirrors. The folding                mirrors together constitute a laser resonator                cavity, when zig-zag pathway is filled with a gain                medium.
   USE - Laser system e.g. carbon dioxide laser system                and carbon monooxide laser system (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The good mechanical stability of the lasing                cavity within the laser housing can be provided,                and good thermal contact with the cooling channels                in the laser housing can be provided. The good                electrical contact for the RF feed to the outer                electrodes can be provided. The construction of                laser is so compact and is mechanically so robust                that the mirrors can be mounted permanently to the                laser housing without the need to provide any                subsequent alignment adjustment. The good thermal                contact of the outer surface of the channeled                ceramic element with the inner bore of the laser                housing is achieved by second thin soft metal                element and made of silver, which adjusts to the                exact profile of the inner cooled bore, thus                ensuring good thermal contact.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows an exploded view of the                laser cavity housing and its components using                segmented electrode structure.
   Bore (10)
   Housing (11)
   Electrode structure (30)
   Spring element (35)
   End plate (38)

PN CN109867785-A
TI Preparing polyimide aerogel adsorption material            comprises e.g. adding 4,4'-diaminodiphenyl ether into            N-methylpyrrolidone, then adding 1,3-propanediamine,            acetic anhydride and pyridine, aging, vacuum drying and            cooling.
AU ZHU K
   LIU S
   SHEN X
   CUI S
   WANG X
   PENG C
AE UNIV NANJING TECHNOLOGY (UNTY-C)
GA 201954517M
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing polyimide aerogel adsorption                material comprises (i) adding 4,4'-diaminodiphenyl                ether into N-methylpyrrolidone and stirring and                dissolving, (ii) adding 1,3-propanediamine into                above solution, stirring completely and dissolving                to obtain mixed solution, (iii) adding                3,3',4,4'-biphenyltetracarboxylic dianhydride into                mixed solution and stirring at room temperature to                obtain uniform solution, (iv) adding mixed solution                of 1,3,5-tris(4-aminophenoxy)benzene and                N-methylpyrrolidone into above mixed solution,                stirring and mixing, (v) adding acetic anhydride                and pyridine into above mixed solution, and                stirring, (vi) pouring sol into mold, placing                sample at room temperature and aging in the mold,                (vii) adding acetone solution of                N-methylpyrrolidone into gel, replacing the acetone                solution, (viii) taking out the sample replaced by                solution, removing acetone from the gel by carbon                dioxide supercritical drying, vacuum drying in oven                and cooling.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing polyimide                aerogel adsorption material.
   ADVANTAGE - The material: has low thermal conductivity,                excellent mechanical properties and flexible                structure.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing polyimide aerogel adsorption                material comprises (i) adding 4,4'-diaminodiphenyl                ether into N-methylpyrrolidone and stirring and                dissolving, (ii) adding 1,3-propanediamine into                above solution, stirring completely and dissolving                to obtain mixed solution, (iii) adding                3,3',4,4'-biphenyltetracarboxylic dianhydride into                mixed solution and stirring at room temperature to                obtain uniform solution, (iv) adding mixed solution                of 1,3,5-tris(4-aminophenoxy)benzene and                N-methylpyrrolidone into above mixed solution,                stirring and mixing, (v) adding acetic anhydride                and pyridine into above mixed solution, and                stirring until the mixed solution is transparent,                (vi) pouring the sol into mold, placing sample at                room temperature and aging in the mold for certain                period of time, (vii) adding acetone solution of                N-methylpyrrolidone into gel, replacing the acetone                solution once at intervals and then replacing for                multiple times, (viii) taking out the sample                replaced by solution, removing acetone from the gel                by carbon dioxide supercritical drying, vacuum                drying in oven and cooling to obtain final                product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: In step (i), volume                  ratio between N-methylpyrrolidone and                  4,4'-diaminodiphenyl ether is 35.5-80 ml/g. The                  molar ratio between 1,3-propanediamine and                  4,4'-diaminodiphenyl is 1:0.3-3. In step (iii),                  mass ratio between                  3,3',4,4'-biphenyltetracarboxylic dianhydride and                  4,4'-diaminodiphenyl is 1.5-3.1; and stirring                  time is 6-10 hours. In step (iv), mass ratio                  between 1,3,5-tris(4-aminophenoxy)benzene and                  4,4'-diaminodiphenyl is 0.03-0.09; and volume                  ratio between N-methylpyrrolidone and                  4,4'-diaminodiphenyl is 13.3-28 ml/g. In step                  (v), volume ratio between acetic anhydride and                  4,4'-diaminodiphenyl ether is 3.5-7.8 ml/g; and                  volume ratio between pyridine and                  4,4'-diaminodiphenyl ether is 3.5-6.7 ml/g. In                  step (vi), aging time is 18-48 hours. In step                  (vii), volume concentration of                  N-methylpyrrolidone in the acetone solution of                  N-methylpyrrolidone is 50-80 wt.%; each                  replacement time is 18-48 hours, and the number                  of replacements is 4-8 times. In step (viii),                  temperature of vacuum drying oven is 40-80                  degrees C; and the drying time is 18-48 hours. In                  step (viii), density of prepared polyimide                  aerogel adsorbent is 0.08-0.11 g/cm3; compression                  modulus is 2.03-3.26 MPa; and adsorption amount                  of carbon dioxide is 8.2-11.5 cm3/g.
DC A26 (Other condensation polymers including silicone polymers and polyimides (mineral silicates and similar materials would not usually appear in Section A).); A35 (Other processing and general - including vulcanisation, welding of plastics and adhesive processes. Testing.); E13 (Heterocyclics.); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).);
