PN CN109778178-A; CN109778178-B
TI Composite surface treatment device for laser            induced plasma impact cladding layer, comprises laser            cladding device for performing laser cladding workpiece            through first mechanical arm, and laser impact            strengthening device.
AU LU Y
   ZHAO J
   WANG W
   QIAO H
   SUN B
   LI S
   HU T
AE SHENYANG AUTOMATION INST CAS (CAYA-C)
GA 201948234Q
AB    NOVELTY - Composite surface treatment device comprises                laser cladding device for performing laser cladding                workpiece through first mechanical arm, laser                impact strengthening device, and laser cladding                device for carrying out laser impact strengthening                workpiece through second mechanical hand. The                three-axis moving machine tool is used to drive the                operation table of the workpiece, so that the                workpiece is located in the operating range of the                laser cladding device and the laser impact                strengthening device, and the composite surface                treatment of the workpiece is realized.
   USE - Composite surface treatment device for laser                induced plasma impact cladding layer.
   ADVANTAGE - The composite surface treatment device                eliminates stress, and inhibits initiation of                fatigue crack.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for treating composite surface, which                involves:
   (A) setting the process parameters of laser                cladding and the process parameters of laser shock                strengthening;
   (B) controlling the synchronous movement of                the two robot hands according to the processing                path;
   (C) turning on the laser cladding powder                feeding device, the argon gas protection device,                and carbon dioxide laser, controlling the first                robot to move the laser cladding head to the                designated position;
   (D) turning on the laser of the laser shock,                controlling the second robot to move one end of the                output light of the flexible light guiding arm to                the starting position;
   (E) adjusting the relative positions of the                two spots; and
   (F) turning on the two laser sources of carbon                dioxide laser and the laser, and controlling the                movement of the two robots at the same time, so                that after the first robot completes the cladding                and the detected laser shock enhancement                temperature reaches the initial value, the laser                shock enhancement of the set time is started, and                the double is completed, where laser beam induces a                composite surface treatment of the plasma impact                cladding layer.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Condition: The laser impact                  strengthening temperature range is 700-800                  degrees C as laser shock intensification of                  initial temperature. Preferred Process: The                  relative position of the two spots is adjusted to                  mean that the laser spot for controlling the                  laser cladding is tangent to the laser-enhanced                  laser spot. The robot is controlled according to                  the processing trajectory to complete the                  cladding process, and the relationship between                  the temperature and the displacement of the                  cladding laser beam is measured by an infrared                  thermometer, and the position-temperature curve                  is drawn. The position is corresponded to the                  temperature of 800 degrees C which is selected as                  the center position of the laser impact spot, and                  the distance between the center position of the                  laser impact spot and the center position of the                  cladding laser beam is used as the relative                  position of the enhanced spot and the cladding                  spot. The relative position control robot                  controls the relative positions of the spots of                  the two beams to be fixed. The workpiece surface                  consistent comprises magnetic field line                  direction parallel with the surface of the                  workpiece, and the surface of the workpiece is                  located in the magnetic field.

A sound pressure providing unit (220) discharges air in chamber to a sound pressure tank. A pressure regulating portion has an open or close valve (230) which is formed in supply pipeline and discharge pipeline (223). An outer door is formed at outer side end of chamber main portion of door frame and opened toward outside of chamber main portion. PN CN109368933-A
TI Adjustable type artificial wetland system comprises pre-treating system, biochemical treatment system, post-treating system, artificial wetland treatment system and system control.
AU SUN Y
   GU Y
   WANG J
   QIAN Y
   CHENG X
AE SHANGHAI GARDENNING-LANDSCAPING CONSTR (SHCG-C)
GA 2019204234
AB    NOVELTY - An adjustable type artificial wetland system comprises pre-treating system, post-treating system, artificial wetland treatment system, system control and biochemical treatment system. The sewage is adjusted by grid. The grid intercepts large particles of suspended matter and floats in water for ensuring normal operation of subsequent processing facilities and reducing organic load of subsequent processing facilities. The biochemical treatment system regulates effluent from tank into biochemical process and decomposes organic matter into carbon dioxide and water. An anoxic zone is set at biochemical tank front end. An aerobic system divides into two equal systems. The supernatant enters constructed wetland to further process. The exhaust gas is led to exhaust gas treatment device through induced draft fan for processing. The aerobic effluent passes through three juxtaposed artificial subsurface wetland system for purifying sewage.
   USE - Adjustable type artificial wetland system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system has very high organic matter efficiency removal, and saves operation cost of sewage treatment.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An adjustable type artificial wetland system comprises pre-treating system, biochemical treatment system, post-treating system, artificial wetland treatment system and system control. The sewage is adjusted through grid. The grid intercepts large particles of suspended matter and floats in water to ensure normal operation of subsequent processing facilities and reduce organic load of subsequent processing facilities. The biochemical treatment system regulates effluent from tank into biochemical process and decomposes organic matter into water and carbon dioxide. An anoxic zone is set at front end of biochemical tank. An aerobic system is divided into two equal systems. The sewage enters sedimentation tank and precipitates after aerobic treatment. The supernatant enters constructed wetland for further processing. The excess sludge enters sludge tank. The lower sludge enters sludge concentrating device. The exhaust gas is led to exhaust gas treatment device by induced draft fan for processing. The aerobic effluent passes through three juxtaposed artificial subsurface wetland system to purify sewage.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic diagram of adjustable type artificial wetland system (Drawing includes non-English language text).

PN CN108981820-A; JP6584704-B1; JP2020024663-A
TI Home environment detecting device comprises a central processing unit, where the central processing unit is connected with a communication unit, an environmental monitoring system and an automatic control processing operation device.
AU QIU Y
AE SHENZHEN MINGCHUANG AUTOCONTROL TECHNOLO (SHEN-Non-standard)
   SHENZHEN MINGCHUANG AUTOMATIC CONTROL (SHEN-Non-standard)
GA 2018A20459
AB    NOVELTY - Home environment detecting device comprises a central processing unit. The central processing unit is connected with a communication unit, an environmental monitoring system and an automatic control processing operation device. A receiving terminal is further connected to the communication unit. The environmental monitoring system includes a carbon dioxide detector, a carbon monoxide detector, a methane detector, an oxygen content detector, dust content detector, smoke sensor, humidity sensor, temperature sensor, water leakage detecting device and leakage detecting device. The receiving terminal includes a home terminal and a smart terminal. The automatic control processing operation device comprises an automatic water shutoff device, an automatic sprinkler device and an automatic power cut device. The temperature sensor is a thermistor temperature sensor including a thermistor substrate.
   USE - Used as home environment detecting device.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Home environment detecting device comprises a central processing unit. The central processing unit is connected with a communication unit, an environmental monitoring system and an automatic control processing operation device. A receiving terminal is further connected to the communication unit. The environmental monitoring system includes a carbon dioxide detector, a carbon monoxide detector, a methane detector, an oxygen content detector, dust content detector, smoke sensor, humidity sensor, temperature sensor, water leakage detecting device and leakage detecting device. The receiving terminal includes a home terminal and a smart terminal. The automatic control processing operation device comprises an automatic water shutoff device, an automatic sprinkler device and an automatic power cut device. The temperature sensor is a thermistor temperature sensor including a thermistor substrate, the thermistor substrate is a core-shell structured positive temperature coefficient thermal ceramic material based on barium-copper-yttrium-titanium-tungsten-manganese-oxygen. The core structure is (Ba0.985Cu0.012Y0.003) (Ti0.94W0.03Mn0.03)O3, and the shell structure is titanium dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The diagram shows a schematic representation of the home environment detecting device.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The communication unit includes a local area network communication module and a remote network communication module. The local area network communication module is connected to the central processing unit and the home terminal. The remote network communication module is connected to the smart terminal. A first alarm device is further connected to the intelligent terminal, and a second alarm device is further connected to the local area network communication module. In the temperature sensor, the core structure (Ba0.985Cu0.012Y0.003) (Ti0.94W0.03Mn0.03) O3 is composed of barium carbonate, copper carbonate, yttrium oxide, titanium dioxide, tungsten oxide and manganese carbonate powder are formed by ball milling, pre-firing, and ball milling. The core structure has particle diameter of 5-50 mm. Preferred Method: The preparation of the thermistor substrate comprises (i) taking barium carbonate, copper carbonate, yttrium oxide, titanium dioxide, and manganese carbonate powder, mixing the powder uniformly with the wet ball mill to make the fine powder, where the mass ratio of the powder to the zirconium ball is 1:3, the mass ratio to the deionized water is 1:2, ball milling for 10 hours at 350 revolutions/minute, and placing the slurry obtained by ball milling in a dry box, drying at 90 degrees C for 10 hours, pre-firing the dried powder at 1158 degrees C for 3 hours, adding tungsten oxide, performing the second ball milling and drying, and screening to obtain 5 mm powder A, adding deionized water, emulsifier ammonium dodecyl benzene sulfonate, polyoxyethylene octylphenol ether into a reaction vessel equipped with mechanical stirring, reflux condenser, nitrogen protection and thermometer, heating the water bath to 70 degrees C, stirring and dissolving to obtain standby emulsion system, adding the silane coupling agent KH-570 and the powder A into the styrene, performing ultrasonication for 20 minutes, adding the emulsion system, emulsifying for half an hour, adding the initiator ammonium persulfate and the buffer sodium hydrogen carbonate, heating to 87 degrees C for 5 hours, heating to 92 degrees C for 1 hour, demulsifying, washing with hot water, drying to obtain polystyrene/powder A, adding deionized water into the flask, adding sodium dodecyl benzene sulfonate and polyvinylpyrrolidone, adjusting the pH to 2.7 with hydrochloric acid, adding a mixed solution of tetrabutyl titanate and absolute ethanol, stirring, adding a solution of cobalt nitrate pentahydrate and cesium nitrate in anhydrous ethanol, and stirring for 1 hour, adding the PS/powder A and adjusting the pH with ammonia, allowing to stand at 60 degrees C for 24 hours, washing with distilled water and absolute ethanol, drying, obtaining titanium dioxide/polystyrene/powder A, removing the polystyrene, and calcining at 600 degrees C for 5 hours to obtain titanium dioxide/powder A; (ii) adding 7 wt.% polyvinyl alcohol as binder into the prepared titanium dioxide/powder A, and pressing to rectangular shape under pressure of 180 MPa; and (iii) heating to 530 degrees C at 2 degrees C/minute, allowing to stand at 530 degrees C for 1 hour, heating again to 630 degrees C at 3 degrees C/minute, allowing to stand at 630 degrees C for 3 hours, heating to 1360 degrees C at rate of 5 degrees C/minute, allowing to stand at 1360 degrees C for 4 hours, and cooling to room temperature to obtain the thermistor substrate.

PN CN108728398-A
TI Isolating and culturing primary duck liver cells, by injecting Hank's balanced salt solution buffer into male Chongqing duck, perfusing Gey's balanced salt solution buffer, resuspending in complete medium and identifying cell morphology.
AU INVENTOR U
AE JIANGSU CHI SCI CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201887227C
AB    NOVELTY - Isolating and culturing primary duck liver cells, by (a) selecting male Chongqing duck from January to February, and intraperitoneally injecting sodium pentobarbital and heparin, (b) fixing on board with abdomen facing up and cutting abdominal cavity under aseptic conditions, (c) injecting preheated Hank's balanced salt solution (HBSS) buffer with a constant flow pump, (d) perfusing the preheated Gey's balanced salt solution (GBSS) buffer with a constant flow pump, (e) injecting the preheated GBSS mixed enzyme solution with a constant flow pump and (f) shaking the shredded tissue, digesting with GBSS mixed enzyme solution, filtering, centrifuging, removing supernatant, measuring cell activity by trypan blue staining, resuspending in complete medium, inoculating in culture flask, incubating at 37 degrees C and 5% carbon dioxide, identifying cell morphology, performing enzyme-linked immunosorbent assay (ELISA) detection and performing reverse transcription (RT)-PCR detection.
   USE - The method is useful for isolating and culturing primary duck liver cells.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and improves survival rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for isolating and culturing primary duck liver cells, involves (a) selecting male Chongqing duck from January to February, and intraperitoneally injecting sodium pentobarbital and heparin, (b) fixing on board with abdomen facing up and cutting abdominal cavity under aseptic conditions, (c) injecting preheated Hank's balanced salt solution (HBSS) buffer with a constant flow pump, (d) perfusing preheated Gey's balanced salt solution (GBSS) buffer with a constant flow pump, (e) injecting the preheated GBSS mixed enzyme solution with a constant flow pump, (f) shaking the shredded tissue, digesting with GBSS mixed enzyme solution, filtering, centrifuging and removing supernatant, (g) taking cell filtrate and measuring cell activity by trypan blue staining, (h) resuspending in complete medium, inoculating in culture flask, and incubating at 37 degrees C and 5% carbon dioxide, (i) identifying cell morphology, (j) performing enzyme-linked immunosorbent assay (ELISA) detection and (k) performing reverse transcription (RT)-PCR detection.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The sodium pentobarbital is injected in an amount of 30 mg/kg body weight and heparin is injected in an amount of 100 U/kg body weight. The HBSS buffer comprises 1-5% bovine serum albumin (BSA) and has pH of 7.2-7.4. The GBSS mixed enzyme solution (pH 7.2-7.4) comprises 0.1-0.5% type IV collagenase and 0.002-0.006% DNaseI. The complete medium is H-Dulbecco's modified Eagle's medium (DMEM) comprising 10% fetal bovine serum, 100 u/ml penicillin and 100 u/ml streptomycin double antibody and 4-6 mu g/ml insulin and has pH of 7.4.

The vinegar has an acidity of 9%.

PT P
PN RO132625-A2
TI Equipment to prevent falling injuries in critical patients during recovery, has small dimension valves that are provided with adaptors located on outer portion of suit for quick coupling to carbon-dioxide cylinder.
AU CHIOREAN M I
   CHERTES A
   FORGACI I
AE CHIOREAN M I (CHIO-Individual)
   CHERTES A (CHER-Individual)
   FORGACI I (FORG-Individual)
GA 201929113A
AB    NOVELTY - The equipment comprises an overall suit that is compartmented and segmented, and an inert gas cylinder. The segments of the suit are provided with protective elements. The carbon-dioxide (CO2) cylinder is portable with a gas capacity of 4 l, and is rechargeable. The protective elements are provided in the area of the joints and are filled with silicone gel of 15 mm for shock absorption in case of fall. The sandwich-type fabric of the suit is provided with neoprene of 3 mm at inside and material 6000D at outside, and is sealed by plastic. The fabric interior is structured in some cells which communicate to one another by some channels. The channels are made by inserting rectangular tubes of 5 mm/15 mm with rounded corners. The gas passing from one cell to another allows to uniformly dilate by 15 mm. The valves of small dimensions are provided with adaptors located on the outer portion of the suit for quick coupling to the CO2 cylinder.
   USE - Equipment used in hospital, ambulatory or at home to prevent falling injuries in critical patients during recovery period.
   ADVANTAGE - The suit is made of two high-quality durable resilient heat-resistant materials such as materials 6000D and neoprene processed as a sandwich-type fabric, structured with some cells, representing the inflatable portion of the suit compartments. The segments of the suit additionally exhibit protective elements made of neoprene filled with silicone gel in the zones most exposed to injuries, so that the assembly or suit results in an airbag-type equipment adapted for damping shocks in case of patients' fall. The material 6000D used for the outer face of the suit is resistant to wear, chemical agents, UV rays. The neoprene is light and heat-insulating, resistant to weather or various chemical agents.
DC A83 (Clothing, footwear.); P21 (Wearing apparel (A41, A42).)
MC A06-A00E3; A12-V03D; P21-N

PN CN107823252-A
TI Improving astragaloside content, by removing reeds, fibrous roots and rotten roots, ultrasonically cleaning fresh Astragalus roots, cutting softened roots, pre-cooling and drying in far-infrared drying oven and microwave oven.
AU MA Z
   AN Y
   DONG L
   HONG J
   YANG S
   LI X
   CHANG W
   HE Y
   ZHAO Y
   LIU W
AE GANSU HUISEN PHARM IND DEV CO LTD (GANS-Non-standard)
GA 201824449G
AB    NOVELTY - Improving astragaloside content, by in mid-to-late October, harvesting withered plants, excavating, removing reeds, fibrous roots and rotten roots, ultrasonically cleaning fresh Astragalus roots, extracting, introducing saturated steam under negative pressure, cutting the softened Astragalus roots into slices, pre-cooling, drying, placing in a far-infrared drying oven, drying, placing in a microwave oven, drying, packaging in carbon dioxide filled bags using food packaging machine, and storing at 5-10 degrees C.
   USE - The method is useful for improving astragaloside content.
   ADVANTAGE - The method suppresses loss of medicinal ingredients, and prolongs storage time.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Processing method for improving astragaloside content, involves in mid-to-late October, harvesting withered plants having age of 2-3 years, after rain, selecting sunny soil, excavating around 30-40 cm of plants to depth of 70-90 cm, removing reeds, fibrous roots and rotten roots, ultrasonically cleaning fresh Astragalus roots using water at 30-40 degrees C, 20-30 kHz and 120-240 W for 3-5 minutes, placing the cleaned Astragalus roots into vacuum machine, providing air using vacuum pump, extracting for 30-40 minutes, introducing saturated steam under negative pressure, increasing temperature to 75-80 degrees C, moisturizing for 50-60 minutes, cutting the softened Astragalus roots into slices having thickness of 2-4 mm using a rotary slicing machine, placing the Astragalus slices in cooler, pre-cooling to -20 degrees C for 1-2 hours, transferring to vacuum freeze-dryer, drying at -55 degrees C to -50 degrees C and 0.1 kPa for 4-6 hours, placing in a far-infrared drying oven, drying at 55-60 degrees C for 3-4 hours, placing in a microwave oven, drying at 600-800 W for 10-15 minutes, controlling moisture content to 15%, packaging in carbon dioxide filled bags using food packaging machine, the bags are made of polyethylene (PE) and PVC material and storing at 5-10 degrees C.

PT P
PN CN107586946-A; CN107586946-B
TI Comprehensively recovering aluminum, potassium, sodium and silicon from molybdenum tailings useful for preparing cement, comprises taking molybdenum tailings, calcium carbonate and sodium carbonate, mixing, calcining and evaporating.
AU SUN W
   WANG L
   HU Y
   JI B
   LIU R
   KAO C
   ZHANG Y
   GAO J
AE UNIV CENT SOUTH (UYCS-C)
   UNIV CENT SOUTH (UYCS-C)
GA 201806862B
AB    NOVELTY - Comprehensively recovering aluminum, potassium, sodium and silicon from molybdenum tailings comprises (i) taking molybdenum tailings, calcium carbonate and sodium carbonate, blending, mixing molybdenum tailings, calcium carbonate and sodium carbonate and calcining, (ii) adding clinker into water, leaching and separating solid and liquid, controlling, (iii) placing leaching solution containing sodium aluminate and potassium aluminate into the closed container, inletting, reacting, separating out, filtering and (iv) evaporating.
   USE - The low-sodium silicon-rich calcium rich slag is useful for preparing cement (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method utilizes the high-temperature roasting method and solves the environmental pollution problem caused by the molybdenum tailings piling up.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Comprehensively recovering aluminum, potassium, sodium and silicon from molybdenum tailings comprises (i) taking molybdenum tailings, calcium carbonate and sodium carbonate according to the ratio of 100:(100-500):(1.2-2.0), blending molybdenum tailings, calcium carbonate and sodium carbonate, mixing molybdenum tailings, calcium carbonate and sodium carbonate and calcining at 850-950 degrees C for at least 2 hours to obtain clinker and carbon dioxide-containing gas, (ii) adding the clinker obtained in step (i) into water for leaching according to the mass ratio of liquid-solid of less than or equal to 1, and separating solid and liquid to obtain the leaching solution containing sodium aluminate, potassium aluminate and a silicon- calcium residue, controlling the leaching temperature is greater than or equal to 55 degrees C and time is greater than or equal to 0.5 hours, (iii) placing the leaching solution containing sodium aluminate and potassium aluminate obtained in the step (ii) into the closed container, inletting carbon dioxide gas, reacting, separating out aluminum hydroxide, finishing the reaction, filtering to obtain potassium-rich and sodium-rich solution and (iv) evaporating the potassium-rich and sodium-rich solution obtained in the step (iii) to obtain potassium alkali and sodium alkali.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the mass of particles having a particle size of 200 mesh or less accounts for 70-90% of the total mass of the molybdenum tailings. In the (i) the calcium carbonate is provided by limestone, the limestone particle size is less than or equal to 200 meshes or less account for 65% to 75% of the total mass of the limestone. The method comprises blending the molybdenum tailings, calcium carbonate, and sodium carbonate, mixing uniformly, calcining at high temperature at 850-950 degrees C for 2-3 hours to obtain clinker and carbon dioxide containing gas. In the step (ii), the mass ratio of liquid-solid is 1-3:1, adding the clinker obtained in step (i) into water for leaching. In the step (ii), the leaching temperature is 60-85 degrees C and the leaching time is 0.5-1 hours. In the step (iii), the used the carbon dioxide gas comprises carbon dioxide-containing gas produced in step (i). In the step (iii), placing the leaching solution containing sodium aluminate and potassium aluminate obtained in the step (ii) into the closed container, inletting carbon dioxide gas, stirring under the condition of 60-80 degrees C, reacting under the reaction pressure of 1-2MPa, for 2-3 hours, separating out aluminum hydroxide, finishing the reaction, filtering to obtain potassium-rich and sodium-rich solution. The recycling rate of silicon is greater than or equal to to 89%, the recycling rate of the aluminum element is greater than or equal to 89%, the recycling rate of potassium is greater than or equal to 93%, the recovery rate of sodium is greater than or equal to 92%.
DC M25 (Production and refining of metals other than iron - including ore treatment, extraction, working up scrap, obtaining specific metals, control testing methods (C22B).)

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (Claimed): The triphenylmethane                  derivative of formula (I) is prepared by reacting                  4,4',4''-trihydroxytriphenylmethane with an epoxy                  compound in the presence of catalyst A to obtain                  a triphenylmethane polyether compound. The                  polyether compound obtained is subjected to                  condensation reaction with R4COOH in the presence                  of catalyst B to obtain product. Preferred                  Components: The catalyst A is sodium carbonate,                  potassium carbonate, sodium hydroxide, potassium                  hydroxide. The catalyst B is sodium bicarbonate                  and potassium bicarbonate. The catalyst C is                  sodium carbonate, potassium carbonate, sodium                  hydroxide, potassium hydroxide. The catalyst D is                  sodium bicarbonate and potassium bicarbonate.                  Preferred Conditions: The first reaction                  temperature is 100-140&#8451;. The catalyst A                  consumption is 0.5-5% quality of                  4,4',4''-trihydroxytriphenylmethane, and the                  reaction temperature is 180-220&#8451;. The                  amount of the catalyst B is 0.5-3%. The molar                  ratio of the triphenylmethane derivative of                  formula (I) to the auxiliary agent is                  1:(0.01-30). The auxiliary agent is prepared by                  reacting alkylphenol RPhOH with an epoxy compound                  in the presence of catalyst C to obtain a                  polyether compound, which is a phenoxide                  derivative of formula (II). The phenoxide                  derivative is subjected to condensation reaction                  with R9COOH in the presence of catalyst D to                  obtain product. The reaction temperature is                  100-140&#8451;. The catalyst C consumption is                  0.5-5% quality of the alkylphenol. The reaction                  temperature is 180-220&#8451;. The dosage of                  catalyst D is 0.5-3%. The dosage of the                  composition is 0.1-5% mass of carbon dioxide                  under the injection pressure. The formation                  temperature is 20-150&#8451;, the injection                  temperature is 20-80&#8451;, and the                  viscosity of the heavy oil is 10,000-100,000                  mPa.s.Y'=hydrogen atom.

   a1, a2, a3, b1, b2, b3, c1 , c2 and c3=integer                of 0-50.

AU XIANG C
   ATWATER H A
   DIGDAYA I A
AE CALIFORNIA INST OF TECHNOLOGY (CALY-C)
   CALIFORNIA INST OF TECHNOLOGY (CALY-C)
GA 2022637633
AB    NOVELTY - An electrodialyzer (10) comprises one or more multi-compartment cells (12) and each of the cells (12a,12n) containing a saltwater compartment (18) , a base compartment (20) receiving a base solution stream (36) , a bipolar membrane separating the saltwater compartment and base compartment, a catholyte compartment, a monovalent cation exchange membrane (A) separating the catholyte compartment and the saltwater compartment, a cathode (14) contacting the catholyte compartment (24) , an anolyte compartment (26) , a monovalent cation exchange membrane (30) separating the anolyte compartment and the base compartment, an anode (16) contacting the anolyte compartment and one or more intermediate monovalent cation exchange membranes (32) separating the multi-compartment cells.
   USE - Electrodialyzer used for removing carbon dioxide from ocean water (claimed), desalination and generating acid base streams.
   ADVANTAGE - The electrodialyzer is suitable for industrial scale capture and conversion of carbon dioxide from ocean water, and utilizes the cell stack, which is capable of generating acid and base (without salt) or for generation of desalinized ocean water for subsequent processes.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the electrodialyzer.
   Electrodialyzer (10)
   Multi-compartment cells (12)
   Cells (12a,12n)
   Cathode (14)
   Anode (16)
   Saltwater compartment (18)
   Base compartment (20)
   Catholyte compartment (24)
   Anolyte compartment (26)
   Monovalent cation exchange membrane (30)
   Intermediate monovalent cation exchange membrane (32)
   Base stream (36)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The base stream solution is sodium hydroxide. The electrolyte solution includes an one-electron, electrochemically reversible redox couple which is selected from sodium ferricyanide and potassium ferricyanide. The bipolar membrane generates proton and hydroxide ion fluxes through water dissociation reactions at a bipolar membrane interface. The proton flux is provided to the saltwater compartment for converting an input saltwater stream to the saltwater compartment into an output stream of acidified saltwater and the hydroxide ion is provided to the base compartment to increase the base concentration of the base stream received by the base compartment. The electrodialyzer further comprises a compartment (C1), a compartment (C2), an anion exchange membrane separating compartment (C1) and compartment (C2). The anion exchange membrane allows the passage of anions from compartment (C1) to the compartment (C2) and rejects the passage of cations between the compartment (C1) and compartment (C2). The intermediate cation membrane is configured to allow the transfer of monovalent cations from the compartment (C1) to the compartment (C3) of an adjacent cell while rejecting the transfer of anions and multivalent cations from compartment (C1) to compartment (C3).

PN CN112961987-A
TI Method for harmless and resourceful treatment of arsenic-alkali residues, involves mixing sodium arsenate filtrate with soluble iron salt to cause metathesis reaction to obtain iron-arsenic co-precipitation.
AU WANG S
   SU R
   MA X
   JIA Y
   LIN J
AE CHINESE ACAD SCI APPL ECOLOGY INST (CSAE-C)
GA 202171901B
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves subjecting the arsenic alkali residue to the first water leaching to obtain an arsenic alkali leachate and an antimony-containing leaching residue. The arsenic alkali leachate contains sodium arsenate and sodium carbonate. A gas containing carbon dioxide is passed into the arsenic alkali leaching solution for dealkalization to obtain solid sodium bicarbonate and the first sodium arsenate filtrate. The antimony-containing leaching residue is subjected to a second water leaching to obtain a sodium antimonate filter residue and a second sodium arsenate filtrate. The first sodium arsenate filtrate and the second sodium arsenate filtrate are mixed with a soluble iron salt to cause a metathesis reaction to obtain iron-arsenic co-precipitation. The carbon dioxide-containing gas includes air or carbon dioxide gas. The dealkalization is carried out under the condition of stirring, and the stirring speed is 100-200 r/min.
   USE - Method for harmless and resourceful treatment of arsenic-alkali residues.
   ADVANTAGE - The method realizes the high-efficiency stabilization treatment of arsenic and realizes the recovery of sodium antimonate and high-purity sodium bicarbonate. The method has convenient operation, low reaction temperature and saves energy.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram of a method for harmless and resourceful treatment of arsenic-alkali residues. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - The soluble ferric salts comprise ferric sulfate, ferrous sulfate, ferric chloride, ferrous chloride and ferric nitrate.

   OU W
   YUAN J
   XIE Z
AE UNIV GUANGDONG TECHNOLOGY (UGTE-C)
GA 202155004T
AB    NOVELTY - AAS epoxy-modified aqueous polyurethane emulsion comprises 0-60 pts. wt. carbon dioxide copolymer polyol, 23-50 pts. wt. diisocyanate, 1.8-5 pts. wt. 2,2-dimethylolpropionic acid, 0-0.18 pts. wt. catalyst, 0-15 pts. wt. epoxy resin, 1.4-3.5 pts. wt. neutralizer, 1.8-3 pts. wt. sodium ethylenediaminoethanesulfonate, 0-1.8 pts. wt. first chain extender, 0-1.8 pts. wt. second chain extender, and 200 pts. wt. water.
   USE - The emulsion is useful for preparing adhesive film.
   ADVANTAGE - The emulsion: has good alkali resistance, high tensile strength, and good storage stability performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing the AAS epoxy-modified aqueous polyurethane emulsion comprising (1) reacting carbon dioxide copolymer polyol, 2,2-dimethylolpropionic acid, diisocyanate and catalyst to obtain a prepolymer, (2) adding epoxy resin and first chain extender to react with the prepolymer, adding organic solvent to reduce viscosity, adding a neutralizer to react, and continuously adding sodium ethylenediaminoethanesulfonate to react to obtain a modified prepolymer, (3) mixing and emulsifying the modified prepolymer with water to obtain an emulsion, and (4) adding a second chain extender to emulsion to perform a chain extension reaction; and
   (2) adhesive film, adhesive film is formed after AAS epoxy-modified aqueous polyurethane emulsion is formed into film.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The AAS epoxy-modified aqueous polyurethane emulsion comprises 55-60 pts. wt. carbon dioxide copolymer polyol, 23-25 pts. wt. diisocyanate, 1.8-3.5 pts. wt. 2,2-dimethylolpropionic acid, 0.08-0.12 pts. wt. catalyst, 0-15 pts. wt. epoxy resin, 1.4-2.7 pts. wt. neutralizer, 1.8-3 pts. wt. sodium ethylenediaminoethanesulfonate, 0.5-1.5 pts. wt. first chain extender, 1.8 pts. wt. second chain extender, and 200 pts. wt. water. Preferred Components: The epoxy value of the epoxy resin is 0.44. The molecular weight of the carbon dioxide copolymer polyol is 2000-3000, the hydroxy functionality of the carbon dioxide copolymer polyol is 2-5, the carbon dioxide molar content of the carbon dioxide copolymer polyol is 0.1-0.4. The catalyst includes di-n-butyltin laurate or stannous isooctanoate; the neutralizer includes triethylamine or sodium hydroxide; the diisocyanate is isophorone diisocyanate. The first chain extender includes 1,4-butanediol or ethylenediamine; the second chain extender includes 1,4-butanediol or ethylenediamine; the epoxy resin includes bisphenol A type liquid epoxy resin E-44 or bisphenol A type liquid epoxy resin E-51. Preferred Method: In the step (1), the temperature of the reaction is 80-85 degrees C, and the time is 4 hours. In the step (2), the reaction temperature of prepolymer and epoxy resin is 60-80 degrees C, and the reaction time is 2-4 hours; the temperature of adding the neutralizer for the reaction is 20-40 degrees C, and the time is 3-10 minutes; the temperature for adding sodium ethylenediaminoethanesulfonate for reaction is 20-40 degrees C, and the time is 3-30 minutes.

PN CN112609207-A; CN112609207-B
TI Preparation of porous electrocatalyst for reduction of carbon dioxide, involves adding tin powder, selenium powder, choline chloride/urea mixture and hydrazine hydrate to reactor, reacting, cooling, washing, and naturally drying.
AU WANG K
   ZHANG S
AE UNIV TIANJIN TECHNOLOGY (UYTT-C)
GA 2021382188
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing porous electro-catalyst comprises (i) adding tin powder, selenium powder, choline chloride/urea mixture and hydrazine hydrate into the reaction kettle, reacting for 10-24 hours at 100-180 degrees C, naturally cooling, washing the obtained product and naturally drying to obtain the crystal precursor ((CH3N (CH2)2 OH)2(Sne3 O7) H2O (Choline-Sne3 O7), where the molar ratio is choline chloride: urea is 1:1.5-2.5, the molar ratio is urea: hydrazine hydrate is 1:0.5-2, the molar ratio of tin powder: selenium powder is 1:1-5, the molar ratio of tin powder: choline chloride is 1:3-15, and (ii) placing the single crystal Choline-Sne3 O7 precursor obtained in the step (i) in a tubular reaction furnace, heating to 600-800 degrees C at a speed of 1-30 degrees C/minute in the air atmosphere, keeping the temperature for 0-30 minutes, cooling to obtain the porous electro-catalyst and recording as P-tin oxide. The alcohol is preferably ethanol or isopropanol.
   USE - The electrocatalyst is useful as cathode of an electrolytic cell in electrocatalytic carbon dioxide reduction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The product has high yield, large specific surface area, and high catalytic selectivity and stability to carbon dioxide reduction.

PN CN112175038-A
TI Purifying limonin comprises e.g. crushing and screening raw material containing limonin to obtain raw material A, adding raw material A into extraction kettle by supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, and collecting to obtain extract B.
AU LIAO J
AE LEMON QINGYUAN BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (LEMO-Non-standard)
GA 202107656H
AB    NOVELTY - Purifying limonin comprises (i) crushing and screening raw material containing limonin to obtain the raw material A; (ii) adding raw material A to extraction kettle by supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, and collecting to obtain extract B; (iii) adding sodium bicarbonate or sodium carbonate solid substance to extract B, adjusting pH of mixed solution of extract B to 7-8 to obtain mixed solution C; (iv) using methylene dichloride to extract the mixed liquid to obtain extracting liquid D and separating liquid E; (v) using common distillation to remove methylene dichloride in separating liquid E, extracting residual dichloromethane in separating liquid E by air pumping pump vacuum extracion method to obtain purifying liquid F; (vi) adding sulfate solid substance to purifying liquid F, adjusting pH of purifying liquid F to 4-7 to obtain purifying liquid G; (vii) centrifuging purifying liquid G by centrifugal method, filtering to obtain precipitate; and (viii) drying precipitate H.
   USE - The method is useful for purifying limonin.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: can extract the limonin in the raw material by using the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction method combined with the dichloromethane extraction method, also can increases the concentration of the limonin in the extract, thus the later period is convenient to use.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the mesh number after crushing the raw material A is controlled to be 40-80 meshes. In the step (ii), the extracting temperature of the extracting kettle is set as 30-100 degrees C, the extracting pressure is set to 15-20MPa, the separating kettle I temperature is 40-60 degrees C, the pressure is 6-10 MPa, the separating kettle II temperature is 30-50 degrees C, and the pressure is 4-8 MPa. In the step (vi), the sulfate used is magnesium sulfate or calcium sulfate. In the step (vii), the centrifuging speed is 2000-4000 rpm, and the centrifuging temperature is 40-60 degrees C. In the step (viii), the drying method is carried out by freeze-drying method.

TI Internal circulation based energy-saving            purification device for air conditioning system in            building, has switching unit connected with            purification air outlet pipe, exhaust pipe and rear end            of air outlet end of filter adsorption unit.
AU CHEN W
   HUA J
AE SENYAO ENERGY TECHNOLOGY SHANGHAI CO LTD (SENY-Non-standard)
GA 202099345X
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a filter adsorption unit for                adsorbing carbon dioxide. An air heating unit heats                air entering the filter adsorption unit to release                carbon dioxide in the filter adsorption unit. An                air pressurization unit is connected with the                filter adsorption unit, and increases static                pressure of the air entering the filter adsorption                unit. A switching unit is connected with a                purification air outlet pipe, an exhaust pipe and a                rear end of an air outlet end of the filter                adsorption unit and provided with an adjusting                valve.
   USE - Internal circulation based energy-saving                purification device for an air conditioning system                (claimed) in a building.
   ADVANTAGE - The device effectively reduces carbon dioxide                concentration of indoor air, fresh air volume                requirement and energy consumption of an air                conditioning system so as to reduce cold or heat                load of the air-conditioning system. The device                reduces demand for cooling capacity so as to reduce                cost of the air-conditioning system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   an air conditioning system; and
   a method for controlling an air conditioning                system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a front view of an internal                circulation based energy-saving purification device                for an air conditioning system.

AU SI F
   LI P
   CAO Y
   QIAO Z
AE UNIV SOUTHEAST (UYSE-C)
GA 202075931P
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a main compressor whose input                end is connected to a heat exchanger. An output end                of a turbine is connected to a low-pressure side                input end of a high temperature regenerator. An                output end of the high temperature regenerator is                connected to an input end of a low temperature                regenerator. An output end of the low temperature                regenerator is connected to a main compressor. A                carbon dioxide plume geothermal system is provided                with an injection well, a production well and a                separator. The injection well is connected to the                main compressor to inject carbon dioxide output                from a cytochrome c-oxidase Brayton cycle loop. A                separator separates a brine-cytochrome c-oxidase                mixture produced at an outlet of the production                well and enters the cytochrome c-oxidase Brayton                circulation loop for power generation.

GA 202072247D
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a radio frequency feed head for carbon dioxide laser, comprising a copper column, the bottom surface of the copper column is fixedly sleeved with an outer sleeve; one side of the top part of the outer sleeve and the surface of the copper column is movably inserted with an inner sleeve; the top of the inner sleeve is fixedly welded with a limiting sleeve; the top of the outer sleeve is fixedly welded with a sealing ring; and the sealing ring is clamped and connected with the bottom of the limiting sleeve; the circumferential surface of the limiting sleeve and the top of the circumferential surface of the outer sleeve are fixedly welded with a fixing ring; the fixing ring is inserted with a fixing screw through the mounting hole screw thread, The utility model effectively solves the problem that the existing radio frequency feed head for carbon dioxide laser is connected by screw thread in the mounting process, but the threaded connection will press the sealing ring, resulting in the sealing ring is pressed and deformed, so as to cause the problem of sealing effect of the sealing ring is reduced.

PN CN111217693-A
TI Preparing alpha,beta-unsaturated carboxylic acid comprises e.g. dissolving alkenyl boron compound, alkoxide and cuprous halide catalyst in organic solvent, sealing the reaction system under carbon dioxide atmosphere and reacting by stirring.
AU HONG J
   ZHAO Y
   SUN Y
   MO F
AE UNIV PEKING (UYPK-C)
   JIANGSU WEIMING ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 202051456N
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing alpha , beta -unsaturated carboxylic acid (I) comprises dissolving 1 equivalent alkenyl boron compound (II), 1-2 equivalents alkoxide and 0.03-0.05 equivalent cuprous halide catalyst in organic solvent, sealing the reaction system under carbon dioxide atmosphere of 1-10 atm, and reacting by stirring at 25-120 degrees C for 12-36 hours, and adding inorganic acid to obtain final product.
   USE - (I) is useful as intermediate for preparing fine chemical products including fragrance and pesticide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has milder reaction conditions, and high product yield.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing alpha , beta -unsaturated carboxylic acid of formula (I) comprises dissolving 1 equivalent alkenyl boron compound of formula (II), 1-2 equivalents alkoxide and 0.03-0.05 equivalent cuprous halide catalyst in organic solvent, sealing the reaction system under carbon dioxide atmosphere of 1-10 atm, and reacting by stirring at 25-120 degrees C for 12-36 hours, and adding inorganic acid to obtain final product.
   R1, R2 = alkyl, ester group, aryl group, heterocyclic aryl group (optionally substituted with alkyl, phenyl, alkoxy, halogen or halo-substituted alkyl), or H; or
   R1R2 = cycloalkyl or heterocycloalkyl group containing single double bond; and
   B. = boron-containing structure including boric acid, borate or boric acid salt.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The cuprous halide catalyst is cuprous chloride, cuprous bromide or cuprous iodide.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method comprises dissolving (II), cuprous halide and alkoxide in organic solvent, then ventilating, sealing under 1 atm carbon dioxide atmosphere and stirring at 70 degrees C for 24 hours, adding 1 mol/l hydrochloric acid to acidify and extracting with neutral oxygen-containing extractant, washing with brine, collecting the organic phase and concentrating under reduced pressure, and carrying out column chromatography. Preferred Components: The borate is phenylboronic acid pinacol ester, and the boric acid salt is potassium trifluoroborate. (II) is e.g. (E)-hept-1-enylboronic acid, (E)-3-phenylprop-1-enylboronic acid, 1-phenylvinylboronic acid, (E)-4,4,5,5-tetramethyl-2-styryl-1,3,2-dioxaborolane, (E)-2-(4-ethylstyryl)-4,4,5,5-tetramethyl-1,3,2-dioxaborolane, potassium (E)-trifluoro(styryl)borate, (E)-ethyl 3-(4,4,5,5-tetramethyl-1,3,2-dioxaborolan-2-yl)acrylate, 1H-inden-2-ylboronic acid, 2-(3,6-dihydro-2H-pyran-4-yl)-4,4,5,5-tetramethyl-1,3,2-dioxaborolane, 4,4,5,5-tetramethyl-2-(4-phenylcyclohex-1-enyl)-1,3,2-dioxaborolane and 2-(4,4-difluorocyclohex-1-enyl)-4,4,5,5-tetramethyl-1,3,2-dioxaborolane. The concentration of the (II) in the organic solvent is 0.1-2 mol/l, and 0.5-3 mol/l of mineral acid is added for acidification after the reaction. The alkoxide is alkali metal salt of 1-4C alcohol; and the organic solvent is N,N-dimethylacetamide, N,N-dimethylformamide, dimethyl sulfoxide, tetrahydrofuran and/or acetonitrile.

PN CN109611070-A; CN109611070-B
TI Method for breaking polyacrylamide polymer fracturing fluid gel, involves adding energizer to fracturing fluid, adding adjuvant, regulator and degradation agent followed by injecting carbon dioxide in fracturing construction.
AU FAN H
   XUE X
   ZHANG K
   WU J
   LI K
   LIU J
   GUO G
   ZHOU X
   YANG X
AE PETROCHINA CO LTD (CNPC-C)
GA 201935749L
AB    NOVELTY - A polyacrylamide polymer fracturing fluid gel breaking method involves adding energizer to a fracturing fluid with a total mass percentage of 0.011-0.03%, adding an adjuvant at the end of the fracturing process. 0.05-0.15 mass% regulator, 0.01-0.02 mass% catalyst and 0.02-0.05 mass% degradation agent are added in the fracturing fluid system, added with carbon dioxide before the displacement of fluid, and 15-30m3 carbon dioxide is injected in the fracturing construction. The construction is completed, and the well is closed after half an hour, and the first nozzle is used to spray till the pressure reduced to 8-12 MPa, and the second nozzle having a smaller diameter than the first nozzle is used to spray till the pressure is reduced to 3-7 MPa, and the nozzle is blown until the pressure is zero to complete the degradation of the fracturing fluid.
   USE - Method for breaking polyacrylamide polymer fracturing fluid gel.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables breaking polyacrylamide polymer fracturing fluid gel by providing formation damage rate less than 5% and increasing backflow rate more than 10%, which satisfies the reservoir reform of low permeability oil and gas fields so as to ensure the fracturing construction effect.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The energizer is ammonium persulfate, hydrogen peroxide, potassium persulfate or potassium permanganate. Preferred Composition: The regulator is a strong base, which is sodium methoxide, sodium ethoxide, potassium ethoxide, sodium tert-butoxide and/or hydrazine and quaternary ammonium base is mixed with sodium hydroxide to a mass ratio of 3-8:1-2. The catalyst is a mixture of potassium ferrate and ammonium persulfate in a mass ratio of 1:2-9. The degradation agent is a mixture of vitamin C and ferrous sulfate in a mass ratio of 3-5:1-2 and a mixture of nano silica having a total mass to volume ratio of 5-8% is added.

PN CN108516571-A; CN108516571-B
TI Preparing high-purity calcium carbonate involves using high activity calcium oxide and high purity carbon dioxide as raw material, using carbide tower as reaction device, performing reaction, adding calcium oxide and water.
AU JIANG J
   CHEN Z
AE JIAN WUFENG MATERIALS CO LTD (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201873795X
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing high-purity calcium carbonate involves using high activity calcium oxide and high purity carbon dioxide as raw material, using carbide tower as reaction device, performing reaction. The calcium oxide and water is added in ratio 1:3-5, mixed, and sieved to obtain lime milk. The lime milk is placed into slurry tank, and kept for 36-72 hours in slurry pool, and stewed. The lime milk is carried out secondary cyclone separation into slurry adjusting tank. The temperature of the lime milk is adjusted to 15 degrees C. The crystal control agent is added to obtain initial slurry. The initial slurry is added into measuring tank. The initial slurry is pumped into carbonized carbon. The carbonization reaction time is 5-15 minutes. The inflow of carbon dioxide is 30-80 cubic meter/minute, 10-60 cubic meters/minute. The carbonization is carried out in 80-90% silicone carbide. The conversion rate is 5-40 cubic meters/minute. The pH of the slurry is 6.7-7.0.
   USE - Method for preparing high-purity calcium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare high-purity calcium carbonate that has high purity, high yield, high quality and low energy consumption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The obtained final reaction slurry is filtered, washed and dried to obtain high-purity calcium carbonate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Condition: The ratio of calcium oxide and water is 1:3-5. The heating temperature is 50 degrees C. The carbonization reaction temperature is 22-26 degrees C. The nitrogen is introduced at the speed of 50-100 mL/minute. The microwave radiation frequency is 2450 plus minus 50 megahertz. The microwave radiation power is 0.10-1.00 w/g. The microwave radiation time is 30-60 minutes. The microwave radiation temperature is 50-60 degrees C. Preferred Composition: The amount of the crystal form controlling agent added is 0.4-0.8 wt.% of the dry basis of the calcium hydroxide in the milk of lime. Preferred components: The crystalline form control agent is barium chloride and malic acid with ratio of 1:1.

AU WANG J
AE GUANGDONG LIK IND CO LTD (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 201846935D
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a fan provided with a one-way air valve. The analog-to-digital converter is electrically connected with a microprocessor. The microprocessor is electrically connected with a wireless communication module and a drive circuit. The microprocessor is electrically connected with a comparison unit. A drive circuit is electrically connected with a relay circuit. The relay circuit with a series circuit is connected with a heating resistance wire and a blower.

TI Porous carbonaceous monolith structure            manufacturing used for extracting carbon dioxide from a            gas composition, involves introducing precursor            material comprising particles comprising halogenated            polymer having melting point in mold.
AU FINSY V
   DUBOIS E P
AE SOLVAY SA (SOLV-C)
   SOLVAY SA (SOLV-C)
   SOLVAY SA (SOLV-C)
   SOLVAY SA (SOLV-C)
   SOLVAY SA (SOLV-C)
   SOLVAY SA (SOLV-C)
GA 201841102J
AB    NOVELTY - Porous carbonaceous monolith structure                manufacturing involves introducing precursor                material comprising particles comprising a                halogenated polymer having a melting point in a                mold, and forming a shaped body comprising                aggregates of particles of the precursor material                by concurrently applying to the precursor material.                The halogenated polymer is a vinylidene chloride                homopolymer. The shaped body is cooled for                demolding, where the shaped body is introduced in a                furnace by causing the pyrolysis of the halogenated                polymer in the furnace.
   USE - Method for manufacturing porous carbonaceous                monolith structure used for extracting carbon                dioxide from a gas composition by selectively                adsorbing carbon dioxide gas (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method manufactures the porous                carbonaceous monolith structure in a simple and                cost-effective manner.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) a method for manufacturing grooved                carbonaceous monolith, which involves sculpting                carbonaceous monolith structure;
   (2) a method for manufacturing a grooved                carbonaceous monolith, which involves manufacturing                a porous carbonaceous monolith structure and then                sculpting the so-manufactured porous carbonaceous                monolith structure; and
   (3) a method for manufacturing a honeycomb                porous carbonaceous structure, which involves                manufacturing grooved carbonaceous monoliths and                then assembling the so-manufactured grooved                carbonaceous monoliths.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a graphical representation                depicting represents porosimetry curve which                illustrates incremental intrusion volume in                function of the pore size.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Condition: The particles                  comprising the halogenated polymer have a mean                  diameter of 145-400 mu m.

PT P
PN CN107721871-A; CN107721871-B
TI Separating and purifying of konjac ceramide, comprises e.g. preheating konjac ceramide supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extract, carrying out I-stage and II-stage molecular distillation and components flowing out along condensation column.
AU LI H
   MA J
   SHI Q
   JIANG H
   GE F
AE UNIV SUN YAT-SEN (UYSY-C)
   UNIV SUN YAT-SEN (UYSY-C)
GA 2018171783
AB    NOVELTY - Separating and purifying of konjac ceramide, comprises e.g. (i) adding konjac ceramide supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extract into a preheater, and preheating, (ii) feeding the preheated material into molecule distillation system through a feeder, then carrying out I-stage molecular distillation, and (iii) feeding the I-stage molecular distillation material into molecular distillation system, then carrying out II-stage molecular distillation under strict control condition and the components flowing out along condensation column which is high purity konjac ceramide sample.
   USE - The method is useful for separating and purifying of konjac ceramide by molecular distillation.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is reasonable and feasible; has simple operation, no organic reagent residue and high purity; and is suitable for large scale industrial production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Separating and purifying of konjac ceramide by molecular distillation, comprises (i) adding konjac ceramide supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extract into a preheater, and preheating, (ii) feeding the preheated material into molecule distillation system through a feeder, then carrying out I-stage molecular distillation under strict control condition, and (iii) feeding the I-stage molecular distillation material into molecular distillation system through the feeder, then carrying out II-stage molecular distillation under strict control condition and the components flowing out along condensation column which is high purity konjac ceramide sample.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In step (ii), the I-stage molecular distillation conditions are: the vacuum degree is 0.1-10 Pa, the distillation temperature is 160-220 degrees C, the feeding material speed is 1-5 ml/minute, the condensing temperature is 0-10 degrees C, and the scraping film rotating speed is 150-400 revolutions/minute. In step (iii), the II-stage molecular distillation conditions are: the vacuum degree is 20-100 Pa, the distillation temperature is 120-160 degrees C, the feeding material speed is 1-5 ml/minute, the condensing temperature is 10-20 degrees C, and the scraping film rotating speed is 150-350 revolutions/minute. Preferred Components: In step (i), the content of C6-ceramide in the konjac ceramide supercritical carbon dioxide fluid extract is 10-20%. In step (i), the preheating temperature is 60-80 degrees C.

TI Automatic fire-extinguishing power distribution cabinet, has cabinet body rotationally connected with cabinet door and baffle plate, bottle body provided with suction pipe, and cylinder electrically connected with temperature control switch.
AU SHI C
AE CHUZHOU JIBAO ELECTRICAL TECHNOLOGY CO (CHUZ-Non-standard)
GA 201809711W
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims an automatic fire-extinguishing power distribution cabinet, comprising a cabinet body and a cabinet door, the cabinet body is rotatably connected to the cabinet door, the cabinet body is fixedly connected with a baffle plate, the baffle plate is connected with a bottle body, the bottle body left side is connected with the valve; the bottle body is provided with a suction pipe, the valve inlet is connected with the suction pipe on the top of the clapboard is provided with a spray head, the cabinet body is provided with a temperature control switch. when the distribution cabinet body fire, the internal temperature rapidly increased to the temperature control switch set value, the circuit form a passage, the storage battery supplying power to the cylinder, the cylinder starts to run under the pressure valve, the valve is opened. the inner part of the bottle pre-stored carbon dioxide liquid along the pipe is pressed to the connecting tube, the final carbon dioxide liquid sprayed from the nozzle, because it will quickly become gas at normal temperature and normal pressure, carbon dioxide gas will quickly fill the cabinet interior so as to isolate air.

TI Carrying out of chemical reaction e.g. strongly            endothermic reaction, involves using reactor            arrangement in which reaction tubes arranged in reactor            are heated, and passing flammable component(s) through            tubes in reactor, and providing gaseous atmosphere            comprising oxygen during the reaction period.
AU ZELLHUBER M
   HOFSTAETTER M
   KEMPER R

AB    NOVELTY - Carrying out of chemical reaction involves                using a reactor arrangement (100) in which reaction                tubes (2) arranged in a reactor (1) are heated                during a reaction period to a reaction tube                temperature level of 400-1500&#176; C by using                radiation heat provided by one or more electric                heating elements in the reactor, and passing one or                more flammable components through the reaction                tubes during the reaction period, in at least a                portion of the reactor in which the heating                elements are provided, and providing a gaseous                atmosphere during the reaction period or during                portion of the reaction period. The gaseous                atmosphere comprises 500 ppm and 10 vol.%                oxygen.
   USE - Carrying out of chemical reaction e.g.                strongly endothermic reaction in reactor for                performing reverse water gas shift (RWGS) reaction                of carbon dioxide and hydrogen to form carbon                monoxide and water, dehydrogenation of oxygenate                such as reaction of methanol to formaldehyde and                hydrogen, cleavage of ammonia to yield gaseous                nitrogen and hydrogen, dehydrogenation of so-called                liquid organic hydrogen carriers, and reforming of                methanol and glycerol.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is safe, enables carrying out of                chemical reaction capable of using heating element                having excellent durability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for the                reactor arrangement.

AB    NOVELTY - The method involves determining first                measurement period (340) to perform measurements on                first and second signals. First measurement of the                first and second signals is generated within the                first period. Second measurement period is                determined to perform measurements on additional                signals transmitted from a transmitter based on the                first measurement, where the second period is                longer than the first period, and the additional                signals are of same signal type. Third and fourth                signals are received from first and second                transmitters, respectively, where each of the third                and fourth signals is of same type.
   USE - Method for performing measurement based on                dynamic measurement period by a user equipment                (claimed), in a high-speed train. Uses include but                are not limited to mobile phones, computers,                tablets, industrial wireless sensors e.g.                microphones, carbon dioxide sensors, pressure                sensors, humidity sensors, thermometers, motion                sensors, accelerometers, laser scanners, fluid                level sensors, inventory sensors, electric                voltage/current meters and actuators, video                surveillance/monitoring devices e.g. cameras and                video cameras, wearable devices or relaxed                internet-of-things devices.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables relaxing measurement time                period for the signals received from adjacent                remote radio heads, thus improving data                transmission speed, reliability and                availability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   a user equipment
   a computer-readable storage medium comprising                a set of instructions for performing measurement                based on dynamic measurement period.

TI Self-sufficient energy supply for energy            transition, has silent wind turbines that are provided            for producing compressed air and photovoltaic system is            provided for generating electricity such that air            separation unit is provided for production of liquid            nitrogen.
AU 
AE SCHUL D (SCHU-Individual)
GA 2022C7342B
AB    NOVELTY - The self-sufficient energy supply comprises                first silent wind turbines (1) that are provided                for generating electricity. The second silent wind                turbines (2) are provided for producing compressed                air. The photovoltaic system (3) is provided for                generating electricity. An air separation unit (11)                is provided for the production of liquid nitrogen.                Zero-emission drive (12) such as nitrogen-driven                hydraulic unit and one compressed air energy store                (5) are directed into which the compressed air                produced by the wind turbines.
   USE - Self-sufficient energy supply for energy                transition.
   ADVANTAGE - The self-sufficient energy supply ensures that                the air separation unit is supplied with sufficient                regenerative energy in the form of electricity and                compressed air thus the sufficient liquid nitrogen                can be produced in the air separation unit to                operate the zero-emission propulsion system. The                liquid oxygen is produced in the air separation                unit and the carbon dioxide is separated out when                cleaning the pre-cooled compressed air. The silent                wind turbines are used to extract the energy from                the wind such that the silent wind turbines do not                pose any danger to flying animals, can work without                casting shadows and are independent of the wind                direction. The silent wind turbines can achieve a                much higher level of acceptance among the                population than the conventional noisy wind                turbines.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic view of the                self-sufficient energy supply.
   1First silent wind turbine
   2Second silent wind turbine

PN CN115147222-A
TI Point-to-point carbon asset trading method based            on block chain, involves broadcasting carbon asset            volume and transaction price in pre-transaction result            to all nodes in carbon assets trading network.
AU HUANG J
AE CHINA FORESTRY YUNXIN SHANGHAI NETWORK (CHFO-Non-standard)
GA 2022C98189
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves constructing a carbon                asset trading network based on a block chain. A                carbon asset transaction network is added as a node                into the carbon asset Trading network. Carbon                emission quota and carbon emission actual carbon                emission are determined by carbon asset traders on                each side. Carbon asset demand and carbon asset                pre-purchase price or carbon asset margin and                carbon Asset pre-sale price are determined based on                pre-constructed profit maximization model. The                carbon asset volume and transaction price in the                pre-transaction result are broadcasted to all nodes                in the carbon assets trading network. The network                is issued carbon asset demand according to the                broadcast content. Carbon assets trading parties                are performed based on the information issued on                the network.
   USE - Point-to-point carbon asset trading method                based on block chain for emission reduction of                greenhouse gases represented by carbon dioxide, and                used in enterprise.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables realizing point-to-point                transaction of buyer and seller in carbon asset                trading market without need of central mechanism                using intelligent contract to automatically execute                transaction, thus improving transaction efficiency,                prevents transaction price and transaction quota                limit caused by transaction information grasping in                a third party hand, avoids risk of data loss and                leakage, and ensures effectiveness and safety of                the carbon asset transaction to a certain                extent.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                point-to-point carbon asset trading system based on                block chain.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart illustrating                the point-to-point carbon asset trading method.                (Drawing includes non-English language                text).

GA 2022C7009U
AB    NOVELTY - The radar has a signal processing device for                receiving a first optical signal in an initial                optical signal and generating an echo signal. A                second optical switch generates a target optical                signal according to the echo signal and a second                optical signal. A                photoelectric detector converts the target light                signal into a target light.    ZHAO W

AE UNIV ZHENGZHOU (UYZZ-C)
GA 2022C1220P
AB    NOVELTY - Full utilization of red mud soilization,                comprises (a) testing the mass percentages of                sodium oxide, potassium oxide and calcium oxide in                red mud, and testing the mass percentages of silica                in fly ash or clay, (b) calculating the required                amount of silica to generate silicate, converting                into the theoretical amount of fly ash or clay,                adding fly ash or clay to red mud, calculating the                theoretical amount of biomass according to the                chemical reaction of potassium oxide and carbon                dioxide to generate potassium carbonate, adding                biomass to red mud, mixing the red mud and fly ash                or clay and biomass evenly, and calcining the                obtained mixture at 800-900&#8451; for 30-60                minutes, and obtaining red mud clinker, and (d)                mixing the red mud clinker with a mixture of                mountain forest waste and orchard waste, and laying                into fields, mulching and maturing for 5-15 days to                obtain red mud soil.
   USE - The method is useful for full utilization of                red mud soilization.

The obtained red mud clinker alkali alkali is weak,                pH value is less than 8, suitable for soil. The                generation of sodium silicate promotes the particle                agglomeration of Red mud, improves the water                retention rate and ventilation property, and                improves the air permeability and moisture                preservation performance of the Red mud.

AU NANDA B D
   PARIDA D D
   CHATTERJEE S
   ROUT B N
   SINGH V
AE TATA STEEL LTD (TAST-C)
GA 202299441H
AB    NOVELTY - Production of high-carbon ferromanganese                alloy, involves (a) preparing a raw material blend                comprising low-silica containing manganese ore 65                wt.% comprising 46-48 wt.% manganese, 8-9 wt.%                iron, 2.5 wt.% silicon dioxide, 16 wt.% coke                comprising 82 wt.% fixed carbon, 15 wt.% ash, 3                wt.% quartz comprising 98 wt.% silicon dioxide, 9                wt.% dolomite comprising 28 wt.% calcium oxide, 19                wt.% magnesium oxide, 4 wt.% silica, 7 wt.%                high-manganese oxide residue comprising 33 wt.%                manganese oxide, 28 wt.% silicon dioxide, 17 wt.%                aluminum oxide, 10 wt.% calcium oxide, and 6.7 wt.%                manganese oxide, (b) injecting the raw material                blend in a submerged arc furnace, (c) inputting                into the furnace an electrode paste supplying                electrical power for smelting process, and (d)                tapping residue and alloy from the furnace after a                power consumption by the furnace, where the                smelting power inputted is around 2600 KWH/T of                alloy, and the electrode paste containing 84%                furnace is injected in 16KG/T of the alloy.
   USE - The process is useful for production of                high-carbon ferromanganese alloy.
   ADVANTAGE - The invention provides reduction in                consumption of high-silica ore by 50% on a                year-on-year basis, reduction in medium grade ore                consumption by 19%, reduction in carbon consumption                by 6%, and reduction in smelting per ton of alloy.                It provides decrease in phosphorous content of the                alloy due to almost negligible phosphorous in the                slag being fed into the furnace. It also provides                increase in manganese recovery by 0.8% in the                alloy, and decrease in carbon emissions in                ferromanganese production process by less than or                equal to 0.164 T of carbon dioxide per ton.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The size fractions of                  the manganese ore, quartz, high-manganese oxide                  residue, dolomite, and coke are 10-70, 10-50,                  10-60, 10-70 and 8-25 (mm). The process                  parameters include molten metal temperature of                  1290 degrees C, residue temperature of 1390                  degrees C and electrode penetration of 950 mm.                  Preferred Composition: The produced alloy                  comprises 70 wt.% manganese, 0.25 wt.%                  phosphorous, 0.35 wt.% silicon, 6.5 wt.% carbon                  and 22.9 wt.% iron and non-traceable impurities.                  The residue composition comprises 34 wt.%                  manganese oxide, 28 wt.% silicon dioxide, 1.10                  wt.% ferric oxide, 16.5 wt.% aluminum oxide, 9.8                  wt.% calcium oxide and 6.7 wt.% magnesium oxide                  with basicity of 0.59.

   x' = 2.4-2.6;and
   x = 1.4-1.8.

The                  potassium(III) hexacyanocobaltate aqueous                  solution is a saturated aqueous solution. The                  ethanol is anhydrous ethanol. Preferred Process:                  In the process (iii), the crystals are                  precipitated at a temperature range of 0 degrees                  C to -30 degrees C.

TI Method for operating multi-tank cryo-compressed            hydrogen storage system for heavy-duty transportation            utilization, involves heating amount of hydrogen, and            providing hydrogen to cryogenic tank such that hydrogen            heats remainder of portion.
AU MCKLVEEN E J
   JARAMILLO D E
   ROY A
   MORENO-BLANCO J
AE VERNE INC (VERN-Non-standard)
GA 2022904679
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves storing each of portions                in respective a first cryogenic tank (103) and a                second cryogenic tank of a set of cryogenic tanks.                A first portion of the set of portions is withdrawn                from the first cryogenic tank. An amount of                hydrogen of a second portion is withdrawn from the                second cryogenic tank in responsive to the first                cryogenic tank containing a remainder of the first                portion such that the first respective cryogenic                tank is partially depleted. The amount of hydrogen                is heated through a heater external to the set of                cryogenic tanks. The amount of hydrogen is provided                to the first cryogenic tank such that the amount of                hydrogen heats the remainder of the first                portion.
   USE - Method for operating a multi-tank                cryo-compressed hydrogen storage system for                heavy-duty transportation utilization by fuel cell                trucks and marine vessels.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables utilizing a cryo-compressed                hydrogen storage system comprising multiple                cryogenic tanks that are adapted to store a supply                of hydrogen, where hydrogen is divided into                multiple portions, thus mitigating carbon dioxide                emissions resulting from fuel consumption in a                light-duty transportation sector, and preventing                catastrophic climate change outcomes.

   (1) a method for facilitating thermal                management of a cryo-compressed hydrogen storage                system;
   (2) a storage system; and
   (3) a hydrogen storage system.
