PN CN114741961-A
TI Printed circuit board (PCB) type heat exchanger            airfoil fin arrangement structure optimization method,            involves designing airfoil fin arrangement structure by            using optimal compromises solution corresponding to            optimization variable.
AU YANG K
   ZENG L
   WANG J
AE UNIV HUAZHONG SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYHZ-C)
GA 2022A0729R
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining an arrangement                geometric parameter of a wing-shaped fin in a                printed circuit board (PCB) type heat exchanger                (PCHE). The arrangement geometric parameters are                provided with interlaced distance, horizontal                distance and vertical distance. An optimization                variable is determined corresponding to a Pareto                front set by using a multi-target optimization                algorithm. An optimal compromise solution is                obtained from the Pareto front set. An airfoil fin                arrangement structure is designed by using the                optimal compromises solution corresponding to the                optimization variable.
   USE - PCB type heat exchanger airfoil fin                arrangement structure optimization method used in                offshore natural gas and oil gas processing,                floating liquefied natural gas device, high                temperature reactor middle cooler and supercritical                carbon dioxide Brayton circulation heat                exchangers.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficiently obtaining the                PCB printed circuit board type heat exchanger wing                type fin optimal arrangement structure with high                heat exchange performance and low flow resistance,                so that the heat exchangers comprehensive                performance is improved, satisfying working                condition requirement. The method overcomes the                traditional BP neural network by error reverse                propagation to adjust the network connection                weight, so as to easily fall into local optimal                solution, improves prediction precision, and uses                the proxy model to predict the performance                parameter of each generation population, saves                calculation resource, reduces optimization period,                and improves efficiency of the optimization                process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a PCB                type heat exchanger airfoil fin arrangement                structure optimization system.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow diagram illustrating                a PCB type heat exchanger airfoil fin arrangement                structure optimization method. (Drawing includes                non-English language text).

AB    NOVELTY - Degrading soil antibiotics by advanced                oxidation, comprises (i) activating chicken bone                biomass charcoal with oxygen-containing gas, (ii)                soaking chicken bone biomass charcoal activated by                oxygen-containing gas in acid solution for 2-12                hours and filtering out, (iii) adding chicken bone                biomass charcoal after acid leaching into ferrous                chloride solution, then slowly adding sodium                borohydride solution containing sodium hydrosulfite                to ferrous chloride solution, loading surface and                pores of chicken bone biomass charcoal with                sulfided nano-zero valent iron, after soaking,                filtering out particulate matter and drying for                later use, and (iv) mixing activated chicken bone                biomass charcoal loaded with sulfided nano-zero                valent iron and soil contaminated with antibiotics,                then spraying peroxymonosulfate solution into                mixture and stirring evenly.

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The oxygen-containing                  gas is a mixture of water vapor and/or carbon                  dioxide. AB    NOVELTY - The method involves gasifying waste at ultra-high temperature to generate mixed gas. The mixed gas is subjected to hydrogenation reaction under action of a high-selectivity hydrogenation catalyst to produce crude synthesis gas. Hydrogen and carbon dioxide are obtained by water-gas conversion reaction. The hydrogen and liquid organic hydrogen storage carrier are purified to obtain purified hydrogen. Purification reaction is performed on the hydrogen and a liquid organosilane storage carrier under effect of a high-selected hydrogenation catalysts to obtain hydrogen oil stored at room temperature and normal pressure. GA 202271094L
AB    NOVELTY - Water-based flame extinguishing agent                comprises 70 wt.% main agent, 10 wt.% flame                repellant A, 10 wt.% flame repellant B and 10 wt.%                flame retardant. The                flame-repellent A is                (6-oxo-6H-dibenzo-C,E-1,2-oxaphosphine-6-yl)methyl. The flame retardant comprises                10-20 wt.% diethylene glycol butyl ether, 0.1-0.5                wt.% polyfluorene ester and 79.5-89.9 wt.%                water.
   USE - Used as water-based flame extinguishing                agent.
   ADVANTAGE - The flame extinguishing agent: uses                three-dimensional lattice structure to pass through                high-temperature flame; cuts off hydrogen bonds and                hydrogen-oxygen; combines to generate carbon                dioxide and water vapor; combines with hydrogen                bonds to produce non-flammable molecules, which can                be extinguished by changing the molecular structure                of the burning material and the double-effect flame                -extinguishing principle of water film-forming                coverage; and does not reignite.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for                manufacturing the flame extinguishing agent,                comprising pumping the main agent, flame-repellent                agent A, flame-repellent agent B and flame                retardant into the mixing barrel to the pump,                stirring for 60 minutes, and placing the finished                product in the mixing barrel into the static barrel                for storage.

AU TANG L

   ZHANG H
   WANG Q
AE JIANGSU JITRI FUTURE URBAN APPL TECHNOLO (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2022685565
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves obtaining complete tree LiDAR point cloud and image data through unmanned airborne, vehicle and backpack mobile laser radar collecting device. The tree species information of the tree is obtained by using the image information of a single plant tree based on deep learning tree species recognition according to the carbon sink estimation formula based on biological quantity list method. The carbon quantity of the trees is obtained, where V is the accumulation amount of tree, D is the density of tree trunk, R is the ratio of the biological quantity of boot to the biological quantities of tree. Cc is the carbon content in the plant. Tc is a ratio (3/11) of the carbon element molecular weight in the carbon dioxide molecular weight.

TI System for producing chemical compound, such as            ethanol, acetic acid, butanol, butyric acid, acetone,            isopropanol or ethanol, comprises carbon oxide            reduction electrolyzer and gas fermentation reactor to            receive carbon-containing reaction product produced by            carbon oxide reduction electrolyzer.
AU IZETT E
   MISHRA A D
   CORP K L
   BEKKEDAHL T A
   HAINES C S
   HUO Z
   MA S
   CAVE E R
   KUHL K P
   FLANDERS N H
AE OPUS 12 INC (OPUS-Non-standard)
GA 2022598606
AB    NOVELTY - System comprises a carbon oxide reduction                electrolyzer having a membrane electrode assembly,                which comprises one or more ion conductive polymer                layers and a cathode catalyst for facilitating                chemical reduction of the carbon oxide to a                carbon-containing reaction product, and a gas                fermentation reactor configured to receive the                carbon-containing reaction product produced by the                carbon oxide reduction electrolyzer and produce the                one or more chemical compounds.
   USE - System for producing one or more chemical                compound, which comprise ethanol, acetic acid,                butanol, butyric acid, methane, ketone, alkane,                aromatic compound, protein, polyhydroxyalkanoate,                acetone, isopropanol or ethanol (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The system employs a relatively inexpensive                electrolyzer for cost of equipment, does not                produce metal dust and provides a higher conversion                of carbon dioxide to carbon monoxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for producing one or more chemical compounds by                chemically reducing a carbon oxide to a                carbon-containing reaction product via a carbon                oxide reduction electrolyzer comprising a membrane                electrode assembly, which comprises one or more ion                conductive polymer layers and a cathode catalyst                for facilitating the chemically reducing of the                carbon oxide to the carbon-containing reaction                product; conveying the carbon-containing reaction                product to a gas fermentation reactor; and                producing the one or more chemical compounds from                the carbon-containing reaction product in the gas                fermentation reactor.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The carbon oxide                  reduction electrolyzer is configured to produce                  methane, and system is configured to transport                  the methane to the gas fermentation reactor. The                  carbon oxide reduction electrolyzer is configured                  to produce carbon monoxide and hydrogen, and                  system is configured to transport the carbon                  monoxide and the hydrogen to the gas fermentation                  reactor. The gas fermentation reactor is                  configured to produce ethanol, acetic acid,                  butanol, butyric acid, or methane. The gas                  fermentation reactor is configured to produce a                  ketone, a light hydrocarbon, or phenol, where                  system further comprises one or more reactors for                  converting ketone, light hydrocarbon, or phenol                  to a diol. The gas fermentation reactor is                  configured to produce ketone and ketone is                  acetone, where diol is bisphenol A. The gas                  fermentation reactor is configured to produce                  naphtha. The system further comprises one or more                  reactors for converting the naphtha to                  terephthalic acid. The gas fermentation reactor                  is configured to perform aerobic fermentation,                  and system is configured to provide oxygen from                  an anode of carbon oxide reduction electrolyzer                  to the gas fermentation reactor. The gas                  fermentation reactor is configured to produce                  protein, polyhydroxyalkanoate, acetone,                  isopropanol, or ethanol. The gas fermentation                  reactor is configured to produce carbon dioxide                  and system is configured to transport carbon                  dioxide from gas fermentation reactor to carbon                  oxide reduction electrolyzer. The carbon oxide                  reduction electrolyzer and gas fermentation                  reactor are located in a single plant. The system                  is configured to recycle carbon dioxide from the                  gas fermentation reactor to the membrane                  electrode assembly. The system further comprises                  one or more carbon dioxide capture units                  comprising a sorbent for capturing carbon dioxide                  during a first phase of recycling and releasing                  carbon dioxide during a second phase of                  recycling. The system is configured to remove                  oxygen from carbon dioxide recycled from the gas                  fermentation reactor to the membrane electrode                  assembly. The gas fermentation reactor is                  configured to perform biochemical reactions in                  the Wood-Ljungdahl Pathway (WLP). The membrane                  electrode assembly is configured to receive a                  carbon oxide input at a pressure above                  atmospheric pressure. The gas fermentation                  reactor is configured to produce an amine, and                  system further comprises one or more reactors for                  converting the amine to a polyisocyanate. The                  carbon-containing reaction product comprises                  methane, and further transports methane to the                  gas fermentation reactor. The carbon-containing                  reaction product comprises carbon monoxide and                  hydrogen, and further transports carbon monoxide                  and hydrogen to the gas fermentation reactor. The                  chemical compounds comprise an amine, and further                  converts the amine to a polyisocyanate. The one                  or more chemical compounds are produced in the                  gas fermentation reactor by performing aerobic                  fermentation, and providing oxygen from an anode                  of carbon oxide reduction electrolyzer to the gas                  fermentation reactor. The method further involves                  transporting carbon dioxide produced by the gas                  fermentation reactor to membrane electrode                  assembly, and providing carbon oxide to membrane                  electrode assembly at a pressure above                  atmospheric pressure. TI Method for generating building by three-dimensional (3D) printing by concentrating sunlight, involves melting and sintering layer by secondary convergence scanning system, and circulating process for multiple times.
AU DING C
AE DING C (DING-Individual)
GA 202255149T
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves fixing a Fresnel lens or concave mirror on the generation system where sunlight is once converging parallel rays. A Fresnel lens or a concave mirror are used to condense the sunlight for the first time and condensed lights into sunlight with a suitable cross-sectional area is turned to converge parallel rays at one time. The secondary converging scanning system is provided to focus the first converging parallel light into a Z-direction scanning spot in 3D printing after deflection scanning and refocusing. The powdery building materials that need to be melted and sintered are evenly spread in the X and Y directions of 3D printing through the powder spreading system. The powder coating system is provided to lay a layer of 0.5-10 mm of powdery building materials. The process is repeated many times and the powdery building materials are melted and sintered into walls through the secondary convergence scanning system melting and sintering one layer.
   USE - Method for generating building by three-dimensional (3D) printing by concentrating sunlight for heating and melting soil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables realizing zero cost of raw material, energy zero cost and zero discharge of carbon dioxide.

TI Sterilization and drying of food material and seed, involves supplying mixture of high temperature combustion gas and superheated steam to sterilization container, passing object to container, and discharging object from container.
AU NAITO T
   HIRANO Y
   IMAZU H
AE NAITO T (NAIT-Individual)
GA 202257884X
AB    NOVELTY - Sterilizing and drying method, involving (a) an air-fuel mixture supply step of mixing water with high-temperature combustion gas in which hydrocarbon fuel containing LPG or kerosene is burned with air to reduce the oxygen concentration to generate superheated steam, and supplying an air-fuel mixture composed of the high-temperature combustion gas and the superheated steam to a sterilization container at a predetermined temperature, and (b) a sterilization step of passing an object through the sterilization container while supplying the air-fuel mixture into the sterilization container. In the sterilization step, the object is discharged from the sterilization container in a dried state.
   USE - The sterilization and drying method is used for object such as food material and seed (claimed), where the food material include spices such as grains, beans, tea leaves, coffee beans, cacao beans, and Sansho fruits, and the seeds include rice, and soybean.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables effective and rapid sterilization and drying of foodstuffs and seeds, without using expensive infrared generator. The oxidation of foodstuff and seed is prevented by lowering the oxygen concentration of high-temperature combustion gas, and the quality deterioration of the foodstuff or seed caused by oxidation is reduced. The method utilizes infrared rays emitted from high-temperature superheated steam and carbon dioxide gas, foods and seeds are killed by inducing resonance and resonance movement in water and organic molecules that make up microorganisms such as spores and bacteria.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Conditions: In the sterilization step, the object is passed through the sterilization container while naturally falling. In the air-fuel mixture supply step, the temperature of the air-fuel mixture supplied to the sterilizing container is 300-500 degrees C. In the air-fuel mixture supply step, the temperature of the air-fuel mixture is controlled by controlling the amount of water mixed in the high-temperature combustion gas. Preferred Composition: The air-fuel mixture contains a non-condensable gas having a volume ratio of 40-80%, and the oxygen concentration contained in the air-fuel mixture is 5% or less. Preferred Components: The object contains food materials (foodstuff) or seeds.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).); P34 (Sterilising, syringes, electrotherapy (A61L, M, N).); Q76 (Drying (F26))

TI Synthesizing benzylamine that is utilized for preparing e.g. dye, pesticide, pharmaceutical, artificial resin, explosives and anti-corrosion inhibitors, involves hydroamination of benzaldehyde in the presence of catalyst and amination reagent, where catalyst comprises active component and carrier.
AU ZHANG S
   HUANG J
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 202249434V
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing benzylamine involves hydroamination reaction in the presence of a catalyst, where the process utilizes benzaldehyde, an amination reagent and hydrogen are as raw materials, the catalyst comprises an active component and a carrier, the active component comprises composite nano-ruthenium zirconium tin oxide particles, the molar ratio of ruthenium, zirconium and tin is (0.01-10):(1-20):(1-20), the carrier comprises alumina and silica, and the mass ratio of the composite oxide to the carrier is 1-20%.
   USE - Method for synthesizing benzylamine that is utilized for preparing dyes, pesticides, pharmaceuticals, artificial resins, anti-corrosion inhibitors, functional sexual additives, explosives, chemical reagents, carbon dioxide absorbents, curing agents (stabilizers) for rubber and plastics.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and easy to implement, utilizes mild reaction temperature and enables to utilize ruthenium zirconium tin composite oxide catalyst that has fine crystal grains and increases the active center and to increase the interaction of the carrier and the metal composite oxide with the reactants, thus improving the reaction yield.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing the catalyst, which involves:
   a. dissolving ruthenium salt, zirconium salt and tin salt in a mixed solvent of alcohol and water, and mixing to obtain ruthenium zirconium tin mixed solution; and
   b. adding the carrier into the mixed solution for impregnation, drying and calcining to obtain the catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alumina includes alpha- alumina, beta- alumina, gamma- alumina or amorphous alumina. The silica includes silica or mesoporous silica. The ruthenium salt is ruthenium trichloride, tris(triphenylphosphine) ruthenium dichloride or ruthenium acetylacetonate compound. The zirconium salt is zirconium oxychloride or zirconium tetrachloride. The tin salt is tin dichloride, tin tetrachloride, stannous sulfate or stannous nitrate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The amination reagent is liquid ammonia or ammonia gas. The process utilizes alcohol or benzene as solvent, where the alcohol comprises methanol, ethanol, n-propanol, isopropanol, n-butanol, isobutanol, tert-butanol or benzene including toluene, ethylbenzene, xylene or trimethylbenzene.

TI Preparing bulk metal oxide-silica composite            aerogel utilized as e.g. catalyst, involves reacting            monomer raw materials to obtain solution and adding            solution to metal salt precursor solution, followed by            adding aged wet gel into autoclave.
AU ZHI M
   HONG Z
   WU Y
   WANG X
AE UNIV CHUZHOU (UYCH-Non-standard)
GA 202243675S
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing bulk metal oxide-silica composite                aerogel involves dissolving 0.1-3.0 mol/l inorganic                metal salt in anhydrous ethanol to prepare a metal                salt precursor solution, mixing monomer raw                materials of vinyl-containing silane and                mercaptosuccinic acid, irradiating under 365 nm                violet lamp for 0.01-60 hours and reacting to                obtain a product of MVS solution, as gel                accelerator. The MVS solution is added to the metal                salt precursor solution, stirred rapidly, sealed                and placed in an environment of 25-70&#176;C to                obtain a wet gel. The obtained wet gel is immersed                in an aging solution to perform aging treatment at                25-70&#176;C for 2-7 days, where the aging                solution is anhydrous ethanol or tetraethyl                orthosilicate solution. The aged wet gel is added                into an autoclave of a supercritical drying device                to perform supercritical drying, followed by                releasing the gas in the autoclave to obtain block                metal oxide aerogels, where ethanol or carbon                dioxide is used as supercritical medium.
   USE - Method for preparing bulk metal oxide-silica                composite aerogel that is utilized as catalyst,                catalyst supports, aerogel composite insulation                material, gas filter material, and heavy metal ion                adsorbents.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables provides bulk metal                oxide-silica composite aerogel with low density,                high specific surface area, high porosity, low                cost, good environmental stability, using very                easily obtainable raw materials, and avoiding epoxy                compounds, thus reducing pollution to the                environment.

AU SAHMUGANATHAN V
   JITENG G

   APPLIED MATERIALS INC (APMA-C)
   UNIV SINGAPORE NAT (UNUS-C)
GA 202239687Y
AB    NOVELTY - The NAND flash memory method involves                depositing a nanocrystalline diamond layer (208) on                a substrate (200,202). The nanocyte diamond layer                has a thickness, a roughness, a hardness, and a                modulus. A deposition gas comprising a                carbon-containing gas and carbon dioxide is                generated and the deposition gas is activated to                form a plasma. A seed layer is deposited on the                substrate. The substrate is exposed to a hydrogen                plasma to form the nanocytes diamond layer. Another                nanocrystone diamond layer is formed on the former                nanocrystals diamond layer, and has another                thickness and another roughness.
   USE - Method for processing nanocrystalline diamond                hard mask films used in field of electronic device                manufacturing and integrated circuit (IC)                manufacturing.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables reducing cost per bit and                increasing chip density in three-dimensional (3D)                NAND by adding more layers. The method allows the                first nanocrystalline diamond layer to be deposited                on the seed layer with a roughness of less than                about 15 nm, and a memory channel extending from a                top surface of the memory stack to the substrate,                so that the first and second roughness are less                than the first roughness, thus preventing                delamination/peeling at bevel, and improving film                transparency and ease in removing after slit                etching, and hence improving performance and                functionality of the electronic device.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a memory                device.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a cross-sectional view of a                substrate.
   Substrate (200,202)
   Device layers (204)
   Nanocrystalline diamond layer (208)

TI Control method of sticking wall air device based on carbon monoxide/nitrogen oxide online monitoring, involves adjusting boiler hearth internal combustion air amount, so that carbon monoxide concentration of second to-be-optimized detection point is lower than first carbon monoxide threshold value.
AU SONG C
   XU J
   QI X
   YAN G
   YANG P
   GENG K
   ZENG J
   CHANG S
AE WALSN ENERGY TECHNOLOGY LANGFANG CO LTD (WALS-Non-standard)
GA 202229997B
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves setting multiple detecting points on a water-cooling wall. The water cooling wall is provided with multiple air supply ports. Each of the air supply port is blown wind cover multiple detection points. Sample gas is circularly collected at the detection point. The sample gas is filtered and condensing the sample gas to obtain processing sample gas. A first carbon dioxide (CO) threshold and a second CO threshold are set. Determination is made to check whether CO concentration of any one of the detection points is greater than or equal to the first CO threshold. The detection point is marked as a second detection point to be optimized, and adjusting the boiler hearth internal combustion air amount, so that the CO concentration of the second to-be-optimized detection point is lower than the first CO threshold value A1.
   USE - Method for controlling a sticking wall air device based on carbon monoxide/nitrogen oxide (CO/O2) online monitoring in a water cooling wall of a boiler of a coal burning power station.
   ADVANTAGE - The CO/O2 online monitoring of the wall-sticking air device can be performed effectively. The corrosion of the water cooling wall of the boiler can be prevented. The formation of coking on the water-cooling wall can be avoided.

PT P
PN CN113975960-A
TI Odor removing agent for wood processing, comprises tea polyphenol, Aloe vera extract, diatomite, activated carbon, surfactant, camphor powder and citric acid.
AU GUO Y
   SUN Y
AE DONGMING HAOLIN WOOD IND CO LTD (DONG-Non-standard)
GA 202233215E
AB    NOVELTY - Odor removing agent comprises tea polyphenol, Aloe vera extract, diatomite, activated carbon, surfactant, camphor powder and citric acid.
   USE - The odor removing agent is useful for wood processing, where the wood product is wood furniture or wooden adornment made by carving.
   ADVANTAGE - The tea polyphenol has strong antibacterial effect and inhibiting oxidase effect, and can be decomposed with peculiar smell molecule, polymerization, and replacement, changing the original molecular structure, decomposing into water, and carbon dioxide, and effectively removes paint on the surface of the wood product, formaldehyde and on smelling smell. The activated carbon has strong adsorption capacity, can absorb peculiar smell molecules, reaches the effect of removing peculiar smell, and contains organic components such as alkanes, phenols, alkenes and camphor ethers, which can emit aromatic odors.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a preparation method of odor removing agent for wood processing, involving (a) preparing Aloe vera extract by filtering fresh Aloe vera after pulverizing, obtaining filtrate and filter residue after filtering, adding anhydrous alcohol to the filter residue for soaking and extraction, and combining and concentrating the extract and the filtrate, (b) evenly mixing Aloe vera extract and tea polyphenols into deionized water of 500-600 parts mass, (c) adding diatomite, camphor powder and citric acid to the solution in step (b) by mass, heating and stirring in the reactor, and (d) adding activated carbon and surfactant to the reaction kettle, stirring, cooling and drying to obtain an odor removing agent for wood processing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The odor removing agent comprises 7-10 pts. wt. tea polyphenol, 10-16 pts. wt. Aloe vera extract, 1-3 pts. wt. diatomite, 8-12 pts. wt. activated carbon, 1-3 pts. wt. surfactant, 1-3 pts. wt. camphor powder and 2-5 pts. wt. citric acid, preferably 8.5 pts. wt. tea polyphenol, 13 pts. wt. Aloe vera extract, 2 pts. wt. diatomite, 10 pts. wt. activated carbon, 2 pts. wt. surfactant, 2 pts. wt. camphor powder and 3.5 pts. wt. citric acid.Preferred Method: In the process of preparing the Aloe vera extract, 80-120 mesh sieves are used for filtration, and the concentration process includes: putting the combined extract and filtrate into a vacuum concentrator to concentrate under reduced pressure, controlling the pressure at 0.08-0.16 MPa, controlling the temperature at 40-50degrees Celsius, and evaporating the ethanol to obtain the Aloe vera extract. The drying process includes using a rotary dryer for drying at 50-60degrees Celsius for 3-4 hours, and drying the particles to a moisture content of less than 2%-3%.Preferred Conditions: In step (c), the heating temperature is 45-55degrees Celsius, and the electric stirring is performed, and the rotating speed is 200-300 rpm.
DC D22 (Sterilising, bandages, dressing and skin protection agents - including sterilising agents (other than for food), sutures, plaster casts, bioactive prostheses, contact lenses, diapers, animal litter, timber, preservatives, disinfectants, bactericidal detergents, deodorants, insect repellent compounds, moth proofers, sheep dip (A61L).); F09 (Paper-making production of cellulose, chemical treatment of wood - including chipboard and fibre-board (D21).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).)

TI Evaporation system for removing solvent by surfactant, has evaporation kettle, stripping tower, condensate tank and product tank, steam heating device is provided on outer wall of outside of vaporizer, stirrer, generating device, defoamer is arranged on stirring shaft of stirrer.
AU MENG Z
   LIU Y
   LIU B
   LI B
   LI G

AB    NOVELTY - The system has an evaporation kettle (1), a stripping tower (2), a condensate tank (3) and a product tank (4), where a top of the evaporator is connected with a condenser (5) through a pipeline. And another end of a condensing tank is connected to the condensate tank. A bottom of the evaporator is in communication with the stripping tower through the pipeline. The pipeline of the bottom of evaporator and the bottom surface of the strip tower is serially connected with a pump body. The bottoms of the strips tower and the product tank are connected in parallel through the pipe. A steam heating device (6) is provided on the outer wall of the outside of the vaporizer. A stirrer, a generating device, a defoamer is arranged on the stirring shaft of the stirrer.
   USE - Evaporation system for removing solvent from surfactant. Uses include but are not limited to daily chemical, industrial cleaning, oil field chemical flooding, carbon dioxide flooding, nitrogen flooding and steam flooding.
   ADVANTAGE - The system removes solvent of surfactant, which can effectively remove the bubble in the evapsoration tank, and ensures the evapration efficiency of ethanol, isopropanol, butanol and other organic solvent in the solvent.

TI Battery pack with intelligent fixed-point fire            extinguishing function, has temperature sensing nozzle            in any preset position in real-time temperature value            of preset position is deformed when preset deformation            temperature threshold value.
AU LI F
   CHEN Y
   ZHANG Y
   ZHOU X
   JIANG N
   WANG L
   GAO Y
   LIU H
AE ZEPHYR INTELLIGENT SYSTEM SHANGHAI CO (RENE-C)
   ZEPHYR INTELLIGENT SYSTEM CO LTD (RENE-C)
GA 202216766R
AB    NOVELTY - The battery pack comprises a battery pack main                body comprising a shell (100) with hollow cavities                (101), extinguishing agent flow pipeline (10)                comprising branch pipes (20), and temperature                sensing nozzles. The inner unit of each temperature                sensing nozzle is filled with a thermosensitive                element. The liquid outlet of each branch through                pipe is provided with a temperature-sensing nozzle.                The maximum value of a preset deformation                temperature threshold range is less than or equal                to an initial value of the temperature of a battery                monomer generating thermal runaway. The deformation                comprises melting, softening, or embrittling.
   USE - Battery pack for use in an energy storage                system of a vehicle, such as an electric                vehicle(all claimed). Can also be used for a hybrid                vehicle.
   ADVANTAGE - The pack ensures that a lithium ion battery                system in an external action or internal trigger so                as to generate thermal runaway, thus ensuring the                battery pack outside, safety and personnel of eye                is imminent. The pack allows a battery pack                extinguishing tool to consider an inner structure                of the pack, so that uniformity and pertinence of                the fire extinguishing agent in the pack can be                improved.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an                intelligent heat trigger fire extinguishing device                has the fire extinguishing agent comprises                hexafluoropropane, heptafluoropropane,                perfluorohexanone, carbon dioxide, nitrogen, a                helium gas, or argon gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a                battery pack.
   Extinguishing agent flow pipeline (10)
   Branch pipe (20)
   Shell (100)
   Cavity (101)
   Battery module (102)

Preferred Composition: The powdered composition comprising 50-80% waste mines reject, 20-30% activator, 3-6% geo-polymerization binding materials, 0-2% superplasticizer and 0-2% accelerator admixture.

   NIE X
   ZHAO K
   ZHANG J
   HUANG G
AE NANJING KERUN IND TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (NANJ-Non-standard)
   NANJING KERUN NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY CO (NANJ-Non-standard)
   NANJING KERUN IND MEDIA CO LTD (NANJ-Non-standard)
GA 202210765A
AB    NOVELTY - A water-based antirust agent comprises 1.5-2 percent by mass chelating agent, 8-12 percent by mass alkalinity retention agent, 6-8 percent by mass organic acid, 1.5-3 percent by mass amphoteric surfactant, 9-12 percent by mass film forming agent, 4-6 percent by mass corrosion inhibitor, 0.1-0.2 percent by mass bactericide, 0.1-0.2 percent by mass defoaming agent and balance water.
   USE - The water-based antirust agent is used for tempering bearing ring.
   ADVANTAGE - The rust inhibitor is used in the continuous high temperature environment, during the tempering process (160 to 200degrees Celsius, 2 to 3 hours). The anti rust workpiece surface prepared by tempering, using the rust inhibitor, has no rust spots on the surface, after tempering and natural cooling. The agent effectively prevents rust for 3to5 days. After 3to5 days, the surface of the workpiece remains free from yellowing and color rust spots. There is no inorganic salt residue left on the surface of the workpiece. The tempered brightness of the surface is not affected by the formation of tempering spots. The agent also prevents absorption of moisture and avoids the absorption of acid in the air in hot and humid weather, absorption of gases such as sulfur dioxide, carbon dioxide etc form electrochemical corrosion. The surface of the workpiece is not sticky after tempering, thereby avoiding the subsequent shot blasting and sequential bonding of steel balls.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows appearance diagram of workpiece after tempering and heat preservation for 2-3 hours.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The chelating agent contained in the aqueous rust inhibitor, is sodium gluconate and/or sodium glucoheptonate. The alkalinity retention agent is a mixture of triethanolamine and cyclohexyl diethanolamine. The organic acid is straight-chain alkenyl succinic anhydride, dimeric fatty acid and/or long-carbon-chain dibasic acid. The linear alkenyl succinic anhydride is nonenyl succinic anhydride or dodecenyl succinic anhydride. The dimer fatty acid is chain dimer acid with carbon atom number 36 and the number of carbon atoms in the long chain dibasic acid, is 21.The chain dimer acid with carbon atom number 36, is prepared by polycondensation using tall oil fatty acid, cotton oleic acid, soybean oleic acid, cotton seed oil fatty acid or rape oil fatty acid, as raw materials. The dibasic acid with carbon atom number 21, is prepared by Diels-Alder reaction, using tall oil acid, ricinoleic acid or rapeseed oleic acid and acrylic acid.The amphoteric surfactant is N-lauryl-beta-iminodiacetic acid sodium salt. The film forming agent is a mixture comprising polyethylene glycol and acrylic acid-acrylate polymer.The molecular weight of polyethylene glycol is 1500-2000 and the molecular weight of the acrylic acid-acrylate polymer is 60000-80000. The curing temperature is 170-220degrees Celsius. The corrosion inhibitor is p-tert-butyl triethanolamine benzoate and/or p-nitroaniline benzoate.

PN CN113817294-A
TI Preparing degradable epoxy material involves e.g. adding carboxymethyl chitosan into water, mechanically stirring, adding cellulose nanowhisker suspension, stirring, pouring into reaction kettle, stirring and mixing with novolac epoxy resin and curing catalyst, performing casting film forming method.
AU YUAN D
AE GUANGXI ZHISHAN NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 202206861J
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing degradable epoxy material comprises slowly adding 10-50 pts. wt. carboxymethyl chitosan into 500-2500 pts. wt. distilled water at room temperature, mechanically stirring until carboxymethyl chitosan is completely dissolved, adding 2 wt.% cellulose nanowhisker suspension, stirring to mix uniformly, pouring mixed solution into reaction kettle, stirring and mixing with 100 pts. wt. novolac epoxy resin and 1-3 pts. wt. curing catalyst, heating to 60-70degrees Celsius, making amino group on carboxymethyl chitosan react with novolac epoxy resin ring-opening reaction, performing casting film forming method, and drying. The cellulose nanowhisker suspension has 1-10 pts. wt. cellulose nanowhisker with a width of 1-20 nm and a length of 100-2000 nm. The novolac epoxy resin is an aqueous novolac epoxy resin emulsion. The curing catalyst is 2-methylimidazole or 2,4,6-tris(dimethylaminomethyl)phenol.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing degradable epoxy material.
   ADVANTAGE - The degradable epoxy material is safe composition, has high strength and durability when used, utilizes few raw materials, and has mild reaction condition, which is good for saving production cost, easy to grasp, convenient to popularize, and it is expected to realize large-scale production. The chitosan in the product is capable of automatically decomposing under humid conditions rich in oxygen and microorganism, thus generating carbon dioxide and water, so as to damage three-dimensional cross-linked network in the product, degrade the product, and recycle novolac epoxy resin. The product is green and environmentally-friendly.

The method involves inoculating (1-5)x106 cells/ml mononuclear cells into the activation medium and culturing at 37degrees Celsius under 5% carbon dioxide and saturated humidity for 1-5 days.

PN CN113786830-A
TI Preparation method of catalyst for treating carbon black by hydrolyzing and absorbing alkyne-containing waste gas in acetylene device, involves mixing pre-treated modified diatomite carrier with active noble metal salt solution, ultrasonically processing, drying, and calcining.
AU ZHANG H
   LI X
   TIAN Y
   ZHAO W
   ZHOU Y
AE WANHUA CHEM SICHUAN CO LTD (WHCH-C)
   WANHUA CHEM NINGBO CO LTD (WHCH-C)
   WANHUA CHEM GROUP CO LTD (WHCH-C)
GA 2022089061
AB    NOVELTY - A preparation method of catalyst for treating carbon black in acetylene device by hydrolyzing and absorbing alkyne-containing waste gas, involves (S1) adding initiator to acrylamide solution to initiate polymerization, immersing the obtained polyacrylamide in fluorine-containing organic acid silicon ester solution to obtain modified polyacrylamide, (S2) ultrasonically mixing modified polyacrylamide, diatomite and water, extruding, forming, and calcining to obtain pre-treated modified diatomite carrier, and (S3) mixing the pre-treated modified diatomite carrier with active noble metal salt solution, ultrasonically processing, drying and calcining to obtain target modified noble metal catalyst.
   USE - Preparation method of catalyst for treating carbon black by hydrolyzing and absorbing alkyne-containing waste gas in acetylene device (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of catalyst, by economical and environmentally-friendly process. The removal efficiency of waste gas volatile organic compound is above 98%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following: 1.a catalyst for treating carbon black by hydrolyzing and absorbing alkyne-containing waste gas in acetylene device; and 2.a method for purifying carbon black by hydrolysis and absorption of alkyne-containing waste gas in acetylene device, which involves loading modified noble metal catalyst in a catalytic reactor, passing acetylene device carbon black hydrolysis waste gas into a catalytic reactor for reaction, and converting alkyne in waste gas into carbon dioxide and water.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The amount of initiator added in the step (S1) is 2-10 wt.% of the amount of acrylamide. The amount of fluorine-containing organic acid silicon ester is 2-10 wt.% of the mass of polyacrylamide. The alkyne content in the exhaust gas is less than 4000 mg/Nm3. Preferred Components: The acrylamide solution in the step (S1) is an aqueous solution of acrylamide. The fluorine-containing organic acid silicon ester in the step (S1) is butyldimethylsilyl trifluoroacetate and/or trimethylsilyl trifluoroacetate. The fluorine-containing organic acid silicon ester solution in the step (S1) is alcohol solution. The carbon black hydrolysis and absorption waste gas of the acetylene device comprises one or more of acetylene, propyne, diacetylene, vinylacetylene and triacetylene. Preferred Conditions: The polymerization in the step (S1) is performed at 40-100degreesC for 2-24 hours. The immersion time in the step (S1) is 2-24 hours.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The amount of modified polyacrylamide in the step (S2) is 5-10 wt.%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The amount of diatomite is 90-95 wt.%. The adding amount of water in the step (S2) is 60-80 wt.% of the total mass of the modified polyacrylamide and diatomite. The concentration of the active noble metal salt solution is 5-8 wt.%, in terms of metal element content. The active noble metal salt solution in step (S3) is 0.2-0.8 times of the modified diatomite carrier. Preferred Conditions: In the step (S2), the calcination is performed at 400-500degreesC for 3-5 hours in an oxygen atmosphere, and at 500-700degreesC for 3-5 hours. In the step (S3), the ultrasonic process is performed at 80-120 Hz for 2-5 hours, the drying is performed at 100-150degrees Celsius for 4-5 hours, and the calcination is performed in an air atmosphere of 500-700degreesC for 3-5 hours. Preferred Component: The active noble metal salt in the step (S3) is one or more of ruthenium salt, platinum salt and palladium salt, preferably ruthenium nitrate. Preferred Properties: The exhaust gas has space velocity of 5000-15,000 hour-1, and temperature of 250-400degreesC, preferably 280-320degreesC.

TI Method for evaluating ultra-low heat input            weldability of low-carbon micro-alloy steel based on            gas-insulated welding, involves evaluating service            weldability of the metal material through mechanical            property detection of welding joint.
AU LIU S
AE BAOSHAN IRON & STEEL CO LTD (BAOS-C)
GA 2021E7060U
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves selecting a test plate as                a low-carbon micro-alloy steel plate. The flat                welding positions of pair of test plates are butt                welded. One side of front surface is provided with                a first V-shaped groove with a straight edge, and                the other side is provided with a second V-shaped                groove. Root welding and filling cover surface                welding are carried out on the test plate in a                consumable electrode gas shielded welding mode. The                welding protective gas is a mixed gas of argon and                carbon dioxide. Multilayer multi-channel swing-free                straight pull welding is adopted to form an                included angle between welding gun (2) and straight                side of first V-shaped groove along width direction                of groove. A welding joint is obtained, and the                crack tendency of the welding joint is observed and                detected to evaluate the technological weldability                of material. The service weldability of the metal                material is evaluated through the mechanical                property detection of the welding joint.
   USE - Method for evaluating ultra-low heat input                weldability of low-carbon micro-alloy steel based                on gas-insulated welding.
   ADVANTAGE - The process weldability and the use                weldability assessment requirements of the                micro-alloy steel can be met. Universality for                evaluation of the weldability of the low-carbon                micro-alloy steel used in different fields can be                realized.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                inclined welding gun in the groove width                direction.
   Welding gun (2)

PT P
PN CN113747669-A
TI Method for manufacturing through-hole between wiring boards, involves removing residual polyimide resin on hole wall and hole bottom, and plating obtained holes with copper plating by general electroless copper plating method to form desired through holes.
AU ZHOU Y
AE DATONG GONGJU XIAN TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (DATO-Non-standard)
GA 2021E6461A
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves pasting the 10-40 mum copper foil on the 20-100 mum polyimide film to obtain a copper foil/polyimide two-layer film, and placing the copper foil/polyimide two-layer film on the circuit substrate. The copper foil is obtained on the product. The etched copper foil pattern is used as a mask. The carbon dioxide laser beam is used to process the polyimide film to form blind holes. The product is put into a stainless steel tank equipped with a commercial soft etching solution. The stainless steel tank ia placed in an ultrasonic bath with an oscillation frequency of 26 kHz and an output power of 600 W for 2 to 10 min. The residual polyimide resin is removed on the hole wall and hole bottom. The obtained holes are plated with 2-10 mum copper plating by a general electroless copper plating method to form the desired through holes.
   USE - Manufacturing method of through hole between wiring boards.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables performing soft etching under the ultrasonic irradiation after forming the blind hole by laser processing, so as to completely remove the resin remained on the surface of the hole bottom without damaging the insulating resin layer, thus improving electroplating adhesive force of the copper foil bottom surface to form a through hole with reliable height.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a cross-sectional view illustrating a manufacturing method of through-hole between wiring boards. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
DC A85 (Electrical applications.); L03 (Electro-(in)organic - chemical features of conductors, resistors, magnets, capacitors and switches, electric discharge lamps, semiconductor and other materials, batteries, accumulators and thermoelectric devices, including fuel cells, magnetic recording media, radiation emission devices, liquid crystals and basic electric elements. Growing of single crystals of semiconductors and their doping are included, but semiconductor devices, where the manufacture is not claimed are excluded. Electrography, electrophotography, magnetography, electrolysis, electrophoresis, power plant, X-ray and plasma-techniques, ion exchange resins, polyelectrolytes, electroplating, metal electrodeposition, electroforming, anodising, electrolytic cleaning, cathodic protection and electrolytic or electrothermic production or refining of metals are all covered elsewhere (Sections G, J, K and M).); V04 (Printed Circuits and Connectors)
MC A05-J01B; A11-C07; A12-E07A; L03-H04E3; V04-V02; V04-R02G; V04-R02C; V04-R02B

AU CHIBA A
   OH C
   SATO T
AE UNIV TOHOKU (TOHO-C)
   NIHON SOZAI GIKEN KK (NISO-Non-standard)
GA 2021E1501F
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus has an electrode element (11) made of slender metal or an alloy which is rotated around a central axis along longitudinal direction. Plasma is irradiated toward one end of the electrode element by a plasma irradiation unit (12). A cooling unit (21) is provided to cool the side face of the end portion of electrode element. The cooling unit is provided to be cooled by blowing a cooling gas (26) toward the side face of the end portion of the electrode element. The cooling unit is provided with an annular injection port (23) that is provided along the periphery of the side surface of electrode elements on the other side. The cooling gas is composed of argon gas, helium gas, nitrogen gas, and carbon dioxide, or a mixed gas. The plasma irradiation unit includes a plasma irradiation nozzle that irradiates the plasma. The plasma irradiation nozzle is moved in the forward/backward direction relative to one end of the electrode element.
   USE - Powder manufacturing apparatus for manufacturing spherical metal or alloy milling powder by plasma rotating electrode method.
   ADVANTAGE - The particle size of the obtained powder can be made smaller.

PN CN113713846-A; CN113713846-B
TI Preparation and application of high-efficiency            rich amine regeneration mesoporous molecular sieve            catalyst by weighing MCM-41 powder molecular sieve,            adding mixed solvent of benzyl alcohol and toluene, and            heating in an oil bath.
AU LIANG Z
   SUN Q
   GAO H
AE UNIV HUNAN (UYHU-C)
GA 2021E4857R
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation and application of high-efficiency                rich amine regeneration mesoporous molecular sieve                catalyst, i.e. MCM-41 mesoporous molecular sieve                catalyst modified with sulfonic acid group                functional group comprise weighing MCM-41 powder                molecular sieve into round bottom flask, adding                mixed solvent of benzyl alcohol and toluene to form                mixed solution A, heating in an oil bath, stirring                and refluxing to obtain mixture B, centrifuging,                separating solid and liquid, and drying solid to                obtain powder C; adding to round bottom flask,                adding anhydrous chloroform, dropping                chlorosulfonic acid, heating in an oil bath,                stirring and refluxing to obtain a mixed solution                D, centrifuging, separating solid and liquid, and                washing solid with chloroform for 3 times to obtain                solid E; and vacuum drying. The ratio of                chlorosulfonic acid and MCM-41 is 0.2-0.8 mL/g. The                ratio of silicon and aluminum in MCM-41 molecular                sieve is 25.
   USE - The method is for preparation and application                of high-efficiency rich amine regeneration                mesoporous molecular sieve catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple compared with the prior                art, and has cheap and easy to obtain raw                materials. The regeneration performance of                catalytic rich amine solution is much higher than                that of traditional molecular sieve catalysts. The                catalyst is easy to separate and can be recycled,                and has no negative effect on the carbon dioxide                absorption performance of amine solution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The volume ratio of                  benzyl alcohol and toluene is 1:4-1:5.

PN CN113722830-A
TI Modeling simulation method for ablation behavior of C/C composite nozzle of solid rocket engine, involves using horizontal set method to simulate retreat and topography of C/C composite material microscopic surface, and reveal thermal chemical ablation rule of fiber and substrate.
AU LIU J
   WANG J
   LIU Y
   ZENG Q
   PENG C
   SONG X
   GUAN K
AE UNIV SOUTH CHINA TECHNOLOGY (UYSC-C)
GA 2021E43405
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves establishing a geometric model of a microscale C/C composite material. A single fiber is established with a radius of 3.5mum. A matrix layer is established with a thickness of 0.5mum wrapped around the fiber. A C/C composite material microscopic model is composed of a high-temperature gas flow covering surface of the material. A related chemical reaction, dilute substance transfer, and structure equation of horizontal set model are established. Three reaction of carbon and hydrogen peroxide, hydrogen, carbon dioxide are inputted. A reaction activation is determined according to three parameters. A dilute material transfer model is established to calculate a temperature field. A horizontal set method is used to simulate retreat and topography of the C/C composite material microscopic surface, and reveal thermal chemical ablation rule of fiber and substrate.
   USE - Modeling simulation method for ablation behavior of C/C composite nozzle of solid rocket engine.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables analyzing the ablation condition of the C/C composite material nozzle from macroscopic and microcosmic two angles, and has important value for thermal performance research and fine design of the nozzle. The method provides the turbulent flow, fluid heat transfer, particle tracking, dilute substance transfer and chemical reaction of gas.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating the modeling simulation method for ablation behavior of C/C composite nozzle of solid rocket engine (Drawing includes non-English language text).

TI Plasma-calcium peroxide method for synchronously            removing organic matter and phosphate radical comprises            e.g. passing nitrophenol and phosphate mixed solution            between inner and outer medium in plasma reactor, and            dispersing calcium peroxide in nitrophenol and            phosphate to be treated mixed solution.

AE UNIV YANAN (NYNN-C)

AB    NOVELTY - Plasma-calcium peroxide method for                synchronously removing organic matter and phosphate                radical comprises (i) passing mixed solution of                nitrophenol and phosphate between the inner medium                and outer medium in the plasma reactor, where the                plasma reactor comprises an outer medium and an                inner medium, and a high-voltage electrode is                provided outside the outer medium, and (ii) turning                on the power supply, generating plasma between the                outer medium and inner medium, uniformly dispersing                calcium peroxide in the mixed solution of                nitrophenol and phosphate to be treated, where the                nitrophenol and phosphate mixed solution to be                treated exits the outlet below the plasma reaction                through the peristaltic pump which reaches the                inlet above the plasma reactor and enters the                internal medium, and circulating as mentioned                above.

AU PATRICK B
AE PATRICK B (PATR-Individual)
GA 2021B5406A
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing consumable food products with a lower than natural abundance of carbon-14 (14C) content involves performing agricultural growth in a controlled environment with a lower than natural abundance of 14C in carbon dioxide, harvesting of agricultural products from agricultural growth, and extracting consumable products from agricultural products. The edible components of washed consumable products are extracted resulting in extracted edible components, and unit of pre-dry processing of extracted edible components, resulting in processed edible components. The dehydration of processed edible components resulting in dehydrated edible components. An optional unit of merging from one up to a plurality of other ingredients with post-processed dehydrated edible components, resulting in a prepared product, and packaged of prepared product, where agricultural products with reduced 14C are packaged for distribution and consumption.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing consumable food products with a lower than natural abundance of carbon-14C content, where the food products are food or beverage products.

The process is unlike typical food or beverage processing applications because the source of the agricultural products is very specific, to preclude natural 14C contaminants that exist in our food chain. Removing hydration from meal replacement portions and beverages lowers the transportation cost of providing carbon-stable products and benefits to consumers; and when freeze drying is utilized for dehydration as portion of this food and beverage preparation process, a further benefit of extended shelf-life is provided to the consumer.

PN KR2021097876-A; KR2303202-B1
TI Cosmetic composition for e.g. skin whitening, comprises e.g. multilamellar liposome comprising fat-soluble layer with gamma-oryzanol and tocopherol and water-soluble layer with phytic acid, derived from black rice bran supercritical extract.
AU KWONSUNGPIL
   YOU K H
   KIM J A
   DONGCHEOL J
   PARK S J
AE CHUNGDAM CDCJN PHARM LLC (CHUN-Non-standard)
GA 2021944686
AB    NOVELTY - Cosmetic composition comprises a multilamellar liposome comprising a fat-soluble layer and a water-soluble layer as a multi-layer, where the fat-soluble layer contains gamma -oryzanol and tocopherol, which are fat-soluble substances derived from black rice bran supercritical extract, and the water-soluble layer contains phytic acid, and a water-soluble substance derived from the black rice bran supercritical extract are collected.
   USE - The cosmetic composition is useful for skin whitening; relieving skin inflammation, preferably nitric oxide-mediated inflammation; and adsorbing and removing heavy metal ions (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The cosmetic composition is suitable for mass-production at low cost; has improved formulation stability and improved skin permeability; and reduces risk of side effects.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing cosmetic composition comprising (i) carrying out supercritical extraction of black rice bran with carbon dioxide to obtain supercritical extract of black rice bran; (ii) extracting gamma -oryzanol and tocopherol from the black rice bran supercritical extract; (iii) extracting phytic acid by acid hydrolysis of the black rice bran supercritical extract remaining after extracting gamma -oryzanol and tocopherol; (iv) mixing the extracted oryzanol and tocopherol with an organic solvent and then mixing with soybean lecithin; (v) adding phytic acid extracted in the step (iii); (vi) drying under reduced pressure to form a lipid film; and (vii) adding a water-soluble solvent to the resulting lipid film, and homogenizing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Method: In the step (iii), acid hydrolysis is performed using 0.5-2 N hydrogen chloride at 20-90 degrees C for 30-180 minutes.
   ACTIVITY - Dermatological; Antiinflammatory. Test details are described but no results given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.
   EXAMPLE - Phospholipids used to prepare liposomes were lecithin from which unsaturated components were removed by hydrogenation of lipids extracted from soybean, and Emulmetik 950 (RTM: Hydrogenated phosphatidylcholine isolate) (Lucas Meyer) containing 95% or more of phosphatidylcholine was used. Phospholipid and sodium deoxycholate were mixed in a ratio of 9:1, the same amount of ethanol and fat-soluble extract (black rice bran supercritical extract) were added and completely dissolved in a thermostat at 60 degrees C to prepare a transparent sol solution. Hardened at room temperature maintained at 60 degrees C, and 2 N hydrogen chloride was added to water-soluble extract (extracted from the black rice bran supercritical extract foil (degreasing black rice bran) at 90 degrees C for 60 minutes when transparent sol solution was obtained, and was magnetically stirred for 10 minutes or more. After drying completely so that a lipid film was formed inside the flask under reduced pressure at 50 degrees C with a vacuum concentrator, distilled water was added to the lipid film to hydrate, and homogenized at 2000 revolutions/minute for 10 minutes. Liposomes were completed.

AB    NOVELTY - The method involves connecting an electric                motor (12) to an input shaft of a gearbox (15). A                battery (24) is electrically connected to the                electric motor. A heat engine (11), and an engine                interconnection clutch (16) interposed between the                heat engine and the electric motor. The first                resistive torque is applied corresponding to a                negative torque of the electric motor operating in                generator mode to recharge the battery. The second                resistive torque is applied corresponding to a                negative torque of the heat engine by controlling                the engine interconnection clutch. The second                resistive torque is applied as soon as a torque                equal to a sum of a torque previously applied by                the electric motor that is reached when a maximum                recharging capacity of the battery is reached and a                couple of mechanical losses of the heat                engine.

The torque of mechanical losses                of the heat engine depends on the target speed of                the heat engine.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a                computer for managing a deceleration phase of a                motor vehicle.

GA 2021583795
AB    NOVELTY - Calculating oxygen supply in electric arc furnace steelmaking process comprises calculating oxygen demand for combustion and oxidation of carbon, silicon, iron, manganese, phosphorus, and sulfur elements in carbon powder, natural gas, and molten steel through following chemical equation in process of electric arc furnace steelmaking is methane+2oxygen=carbon dioxide+2water, carbon (carbon powder)+1/2oxygen= carbon monoxide, (carbon)+1/2oxygen = carbon monoxide, (silicon)+ oxygen =(silicon dioxide), (iron)+1/2 oxygen =(ferrous oxide), (manganese)+1/2 oxygen=(manganese(II) oxide), (phosphorus)+5/4 oxygen =1/2(phosphorus pentoxide), and (sulfur)+ oxygen= sulfur dioxide, and determining amount of oxygen supply at each stage according to purpose of four stages of arc-starting period, main melting period, final melting period, and oxidation period in electric arc furnace steelmaking process.
   USE - The method is useful for calculating oxygen supply in electric arc furnace steelmaking process.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is more conducive to exert the energy-saving effect of oxygen supply operation in the electric arc furnace smelting process and the realization of a reliable foam slag process; has excellent adaptability to a variety of raw material structures; changes the manual experience operation mode of the traditional electric furnace smelting process; takes complete account of various factors including carbon powder, natural gas and molten steel in the electric arc furnace smelting process including carbon, silicon, iron, manganese, phosphorus, and sulfur etc.; establishes the oxygen supply system at different stages; and realizes the visualized operation of the oxygen supply system for electric arc furnace steelmaking.

TI Secondary battery used in digital device, has gasket that is placed on can whose upper end is clamped on beading portion to form curved portion such that gasket is provided with air bubbles to form closed space.
AU CHUNG J
   KIM S
AE LG CHEM LTD (GLDS-C)
GA 202141769R
AB    NOVELTY - The secondary battery has a top cap portion (30) whose edge is seated in a state in which a gasket (20) is placed on a can in which a beading portion (11) is formed. An upper end of the can is clamped on the beading portion to form a curved portion (12). The gasket has air bubbles (21) which form a closed space.
   USE - Secondary battery used in digital device and transportation unit such as vehicles.
   ADVANTAGE - Since bubbles are formed in the gasket, the elastic deformation can be made larger without changing the material of the gasket. Since sealing is possible by applying a relatively small pressure, the production efficiency can be increased. The clamping process can be performed more stably such that the material with a low elastic compression rate can be manufactured as a gasket. Since the carbon dioxide gas is expanded by applying heat to the mold, the bubble formation can be controlled by controlling the point where heat is applied, the time to which heat is applied, and the gas injection pressure and direction. The air bubbles are more concentrated in a portion contacting the curved portion of the can when clamping is performed so that the elastic compression rate can be increased.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for manufacturing secondary battery.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a cross-sectional view illustrating a state in which air bubbles are formed inside the gasket.

   Curved portion (12)

PN WO2021076763-A1; CA3154791-A1
TI Providing long-shelf life sliced rare, medium rare and medium cooked roast beef by cooking whole beef portions, slicing cooked portions, arranging into trayless gas impermeable pack, flushing with inert gas, and sealing product.
AU DIETZ G J
AE CARGILL INC (CRGI-C)
GA 202141712S
AB    NOVELTY - Method for providing long-shelf life sliced rare, medium rare, and medium cooked roast beef involves (a) (i) providing whole beef portions, (ii) cooking the whole beef portions to an internal temperature of 130-160 degrees F to form cooked roast beef portions, (iii) slicing the cooked roast beef portions to form sliced roast beef having an average thickness of 0.5-10 mm, (iv) arranging the sliced roast beef into a trayless gas impermeable pack formed from flexible film, (v) flushing the trayless gas impermeable pack with an inert gas comprising carbon dioxide, nitrogen, and 0.1-30% carbon monoxide, and (vi) sealing the trayless gas impermeable pack containing the sliced cooked roast beef and the inert gas, or (b) providing whole beef portions, cooking the whole beef portions to provide an internal redness aasterisk/basterisk ratio of 16-22 to form cooked roast beef portions, and performing the above-mentioned steps (iii)-(vi).
   USE - The method is useful for providing long-shelf life sliced rare, medium rare, and medium cooked roast beef (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares roast beef product at a controlled manufacturing facility, provides an additional degree of comfort in security with respect to safe handling of the product without exposure to deli counter condition, which have less rigorous procedures, provides higher quality product with improved flavor and texture, and products color stability, and precisely controls the internal temperature of the cooked whole beef portions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for packed cooked roast beef product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: The whole beef portions are cooked to an internal temperature of 130-140 degrees C, or 140-150 degrees C to form rare cooked roast beef portions. The whole beef portions are cooked to an internal temperature of from about 150-160 degrees F to form medium cooked roast beef portions. The whole beef portions are to provide an internal redness aasterisk/basterisk ratio of 16-22. The flexible film is a plastic film having a thickness of 10-75 mu m. The whole beef portions are treated with a brine solution before cooking. The brine solution comprises sodium phosphates, salt, and vinegar. Preferred Method: The method involves using 0.1-2%, preferably 0.2-0.5% carbon monoxide. The surface of the whole beef portions is macerated before cooking to expose myosin and actin to bind the muscles together. The maceration is performed by using macerator with a series of rotating blades. The macerated whole beef portions are subjected to mechanical myosin and actin extraction by tumbling, massaging, blending, or mixing. The whole beef portions are arranged in a casing to form a beef log before cooking.

The combustion device is arranged in the high-temperature air duct close to the high-temperature air inlet of the boiler, and the direction of the gas jet is the same as that of the air. The oxygen sensor is arranged upstream of the combustion device, near the high temperature air duct of the combustion device. The opening and closing of the combustion device is controlled according to the oxygen content in the measurement channel of the oxygen sensor. The combustion device is turned on when the oxygen content is higher than 3-6%. The combustion device is turned OFF to keep the environment in the channel in a low-oxygen atmosphere when the oxygen content is lower than the value.
   USE - Solid electric heat storage-boiler system with low oxygen heat exchange.
   ADVANTAGE - The partial oxygen in the heat transfer working medium air in the system is converted into carbon dioxide, which reduces the high temperature oxygen corrosion, and strengthens the heat transfer of two forms of radiation and convection. The device and operation cost is reduced. A small amount of fuel gas combustion heat is supplied to the boiler, without causing additional energy consumption.

PN US2021060477-A1
TI Method for removing acid gas from feed gas stream of natural gas including acid gas, methane and ethane involves alternating input of feed gas stream between beds of adsorbent particles comprising zeolite, and venting tail gas stream.
AU THOMPSON J A
AE CHEVRON USA INC (CALI-C)
GA 202124314C
AB    NOVELTY - Method for removing acid gas from a feed gas stream of natural gas including acid gas, methane and ethane involves alternating input of the feed gas stream (101) between at least two beds of adsorbent particles comprising a zeolite of either SSZ-13 or some combination such that the feed gas stream contacts one of the two beds at a given time in an adsorption at feed pressure of 50-1000 psia for predetermined time to adsorb acid gas from feed gas stream, venting a tail gas stream from another of the at least two beds in desorption, and producing a product gas stream (115) containing 2 mol% or less carbon dioxide and 65 mol% or more methane recovered from the feed gas stream and 25 mol% or more ethane recovered from the feed gas. The feed gas stream is input at a feed end of each bed. The product gas stream is removed from a product end of each bed. The tail gas stream is vented from the feed end of each bed.
   USE - Method for removing acid gas from feed gas stream of natural gas including acid gas, methane and ethane.
   ADVANTAGE - The acid gas can be removed completely and stably from feed gas stream.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of two-bed pressure-swing adsorption (PSA) system.
   PSA system (100)
   Feed gas (101)
   Block valve (105)
   Buffer tank (114)
   Product gas (115)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The feed gas stream has flow rate of 1-300 MMSCFD in the adsorption step and the adsorption step occurs at 10-80?oC. Preferred Composition: At least two beds of adsorbent particles comprising zeolite of SSZ-13 or some combination are nine beds of adsorbent particles comprising zeolite SSZ-13. The product gas stream contains 90 mol% or more methane recovered from the feed gas stream and 70 mol% or more ethane recovered from the feed gas stream. The acid gas adsorbed from the feed gas stream comprises carbon dioxide and 0-1000 ppm hydrogen sulfide. The zeolite SSZ-13 has silicon-aluminum ratio of 5-100. The product gas stream contains methane having purity of 95 mol% or more and ethane having purity of 3 mol% or more. The product gas stream contains 50 ppm or less, preferably 4 ppm or less hydrogen sulfide. Preferred Components: The zeolite SSZ-13 has a cation as a framework ion selected from sodium, calcium, potassium, lithium, magnesium and barium, preferably sodium. The acid gas is a gas selected from carbon dioxide, hydrogen sulfide and carbonyl sulfide, and combinations of gases with water. Preferred Method: The method involves following the adsorption step in one of the two beds and simultaneous desorption step in the other of the two beds, equalizing pressure of the two beds through the product end of each of the two beds at the end of the adsorption step and simultaneous desorption step, and repressurizing the bed having just completed the desorption step by sending a slipstream of the product gas stream through the product end of the bed having just completed the desorption step. The method involves following adsorption in bed (1) of nine beds (1)-(9), equalizing in pressure with a bed (5) having a lower pressure than the bed (1) through a line connecting the product ends of the beds (1) and (5), equalizing bed (1) in pressure with a bed (6) having a lower pressure than the bed (1) through a line connecting the product ends of the beds (1) and (6), lowering pressure in the bed (1), passing gas from the bed (1) to bed (8) through a line connecting the product ends of the beds (1) and (8) in a providing purge process such that the bed (8) of the nine beds is purged, equalizing the bed (1) in pressure with the bed (8) having a lower pressure than the bed (1) through a line connecting the product ends of the beds (1) and (8), depressurizing the adsorbent bed (1) to a pressure of 20-1 psia through the feed end of the adsorbent bed (1), purging the bed (1) by providing gas to the bed (1) through the product end of the bed (1) from a bed (4) of the nine beds, equalizing the bed (1) in pressure with the bed (3) having a higher pressure than the bed (1) through a line connecting the product ends of the beds (1) and (3), equalizing the bed (1) with the bed (5) having a higher pressure than the bed (1) through a line connecting the product ends of the beds (1) and (5), equalizing the bed (1) with bed (6) having higher pressure than the bed (1) through a line connecting the product ends of the beds (1) and (6), passing a slipstream of the product gas or a stream of gas from a storage tank through the product end of the bed (1) to repressurize the bed (1) to the adsorption step pressure, and operating the bed (1) in an independent adsorption step for sufficient time for the beds (3) and (5) to be equalized in pressure. The beds (2)-(9) are sequenced to cycle through the adsorption, equalizations, purging, equalization, blowdown, purging, three equalization processes, product-end repressurization step and adsorption. The adsorption, equalization, purging, second equalization, blowdown, purging, third and fourth equalization processes and adsorption step occur in a total cycle time of 400-3600 seconds. The method is performed on an offshore platform. The method has a specific vacuum power consumption of 0-1500 kWhr/MM SCF raw gas. 0-50 vol.% Tail gas is recycled to the feed gas stream to produce product gas stream containing 2 mol% or less carbon dioxide and 90 mol% or more methane in the feed gas stream and 85 mol% or more hydrocarbons in the feed gas stream.
