PN CN112225869-A; CN112225869-B
TI Uniform pore size polyurethane microporous foam comprises components including isocyanate mixture or modified isocyanate mixture, water, catalysts, isocyanate prepolymer, polyether polyol, chain extender and surface tension regulator.
AU WEI Q
   GAO H
   WEI Z
AE GUANGDE XIANGYUAN NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOG (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021091667
AB    NOVELTY - A uniform pore size polyurethane microporous foam comprises components (A)-(D). The component (A) is an isocyanate mixture or a modified isocyanate mixture. The component (B) includes water and a catalyst (b). The component (C) is isocyanate prepolymer. The component (D) includes polyether polyol, a chain extender, a catalyst (d), and a surface tension regulator.
   USE - Uniform pore size polyurethane microporous foam.
   ADVANTAGE - The polyurethane microporous foam has uniform pore size distribution by adopting isocyanate and water, which can be reacted to a preset extent under the action of the catalyst to realize supersaturation in the system and the precipitation of extremely tiny bubbles, so that the reaction is not completed, carbon dioxide can be continuously produced, and the product can be added to the mixed components that do not generate chemical reaction gas to achieve the introduction of nucleus points and the growth of cells on the introduced nucleus points.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the uniform pore size polyurethane microporous foam, which involves: (1) uniformly mixing (A) the components (A) and (B) to form a mixture (AB), and mixing (B) the components (C) and (D) to form a mixture (CD); (2) leaving still the mixture (AB), adding the mixture (AB) to the mixture (CD) and uniformly mixing to form a mixture (ABCD), where the mass ratio of the mixture (AB) to the mixture (CD) is 20-40 wt.%; and (3) coating the mixture (ABCD) on a polyethylene terephthalate film, heating and curing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The chain extender is a small molecular glycol, preferably ethylene glycol, 1,3-propanediol and/or 1,4-butanediol. The catalyst (d) is an organometallic catalyst, preferably bismuth octoate and/or bismuth isooctanoate. The water is deionized water. The catalyst (b) is an amine catalyst, preferably triethyldiamine, triethylamine and/or dimethylethanolamine. The isocyanate or modified isocyanate mixture is toluene diisocyanate, isophorone diisocyanate and/or liquefied diphenylmethane diisocyanate. The mass ratio of the catalyst (b) to water is 0.03-0.07 wt.%. The isocyanate group content in the isocyanate prepolymer is 15-20 wt.%. The mass ratio of the chain extender to the polyether polyol is 1-3 wt.%. The mass ratio of the catalyst (d) to the polyether polyol is 0.04-0.08 wt.%. The mass ratio of the surface tension regulator to the polyether polyol is 0.5-1.5 wt.%.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The mass ratio of the functional groups of the component (A) to the reactive groups of the component (B) is 1:1-1.05:1. The mass ratio of the functional groups of the component (C) to the functional groups of the component (D) is 1:1-1.05:1. The isocyanate prepolymer is a prepolymerized product of excess isocyanate and polyether polyol or polyester polyol. The surface tension modifier is liquid silicone oil, preferably polydimethylsiloxane. The polyether polyol is ethylene oxide (propylene)-type polyether polyol. The polyester polyol is polyethylene glycol adipate diol, poly-1,4-butanediol adipate diol and/or poly-1,3-propanediol adipate diol. Preferred Properties: The viscosity of the isocyanate mixture or modified isocyanate mixture is 20-300 mPa.second at 25 degrees C. The isocyanate prepolymer has a viscosity of 3000-5000 mPa.second at 25 degrees C. The viscosity of component (D) is 600-1000 mPa.second at 25 degrees C. The molecular weight of the polyether polyol is 800-6000. The molecular weight of the polyester polyol is 800-6000. Preferred Conditions: The mixing step (A) is carried out at 6000-10000 rpm for 3-5 seconds. The mixing step (B) is carried out at 6000-10000 rpm for 5-8 seconds. The leaving still time for mixture (AB) is 5-8 seconds, and leaving still time for the mixture (CD) is 4-6 seconds. The mixing step in step (2) is carried out at 8000-10000 rpm for 5-8 seconds. The coating thickness is 0.1-0.5 mm. The heating and curing temperature is 115-120 degrees C, and the time is 15-20 minutes.
DC A94 (Semi-finished materials - fibres, films, foams.); A28 (Condensation polymers in general.)

   LI F
AE CHINA COAL ZHANGJIAKOU COAL MECHANISM CO (CNCO-C)
GA 2020C9917U
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves using the regenerated sand that is combined with the dry method and then the wet method, and the amount of water glass is added. The same molding sand and process ratio are used as the surface sand of the molding process. The model, sand box, chilled iron, positioning pins are prepared according to the modeling process, and the floating sand is blown OFF in the model. The back sand is filled and the tap frequency and tap time are adjusted according to the size of the casting. The sand mold is scraped with a gas needle to pierce the vent holes. The spacing is 200mm in summer and 150mm in winter, evenly distributed, and pierced to 15mm-25mm from the surface of the pattern. A vacuum chamber is entered to harden, the vacuum degree is greater than 85KPa, fill with carbon-dioxide (CO2), the inflation pressure is 0.28MPa-0.32MPa, and the pressure holding time is 60S-120S. The box is turned over and the mold is started after hardening.

PN CN111977997-A; CN111977997-B
TI Controlling reduction and modification of steel slag comprises mixing steel slag, siliceous reducing agent, auxiliary agent powder and water, pressing into block, drying, stage-wise insulation, adjusting basicity and water quenching.
AU LI X
   ZHANG X
   ZANG X
   ZHENG J
   XING X
   WU Y
   WANG W
   RUAN J
   PANG Z
AE UNIV XIAN ARCHITECTURE & TECHNOLOGY (UXAC-C)
GA 2020B9193Q
AB    NOVELTY - Method comprises mixing steel slag, 8-35% siliceous reducing agent, 1-2.3% auxiliary agent powder and water, pressing into a block, drying, where the auxiliary agent can react to generate carbon dioxide during calcination, calcining the dried block under vacuum, insulating in a first stage at 1050 plus minus 50 degrees C, where the siliceous reducing agent fully reacts with calcium ferrite in the steel slag to release calcium oxide, heat insulating in second stage at 1200 plus minus 50 degrees C, so that the siliceous reducing agent fully reacts with iron silicate and other low-valent iron compounds in the steel slag to obtain elemental iron, the iron-containing components are fully reduced, insulating in third stage at 1300 plus minus 50 degrees C, where the siliceous reducing agent fully reacts with the RO phase in the fresh slag, insulating in fourth stage at 1525 plus minus 25 degrees C, adjusting the alkalinity to 1-1.4 after melting, adjusting the basicity and water quenching to obtain reduced ferrosilicon alloy and water quenched slag.
   USE - The water quenched slag is used as a cement admixture (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method: adopts a temperature system that combines direct reduction and melting modification, and fully dissociates iron-containing compounds in steel slag through stepwise reduction and completes the modification, thus realizing efficient and comprehensive utilization of steel slag.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for water quenched slag.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The siliceous reducing agent includes at least one of crystalline silicon waste, ferrosilicon, silicon carbide and silicon powder. The auxiliary agent includes at least one of bituminous coal, anthracite and blue charcoal. The fourth stage calcination and insulation stage comprises adding a modifier after the materials are melted when adjusting the alkalinity, where the modifier is calcium oxide or silica.

   (4) material for handling offshore oil spills comprising oil-absorbing foam; and
   (5) method of dealing with offshore oil spills comprising transporting the oil-absorbing foam or the oil-absorbing foam into the sea level for adsorption or placing it suspension in the sea or placing it on the seabed.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The oil-absorbing foam has density of greater than or equal to 0.023 g/cm3, the porosity of more than 96%, the contact angles of oil and water is 0 degrees and 118 degrees respectively; the organic solvents oil-absorbing foam of 7.9-21.9 g/g, and the polylactide/poly(butylene succinate) blend includes 10-30 (preferably 20) wt.% poly(butylene succinate). Preferred Method: In step (i), the melt blending step is carried out on twin-screw extruder, the melt blending temperature is 180-200 degrees C, preferably 180 degrees C, the screw speed is 10-50 (preferably 30) revolutions/minute and melt blending time is 1-5 (preferably 3) minutes; the step (ii) comprises placing the polylactide/poly(butylene succinate) blend in autoclave at room temperature, filling carbon dioxide with a certain saturation pressure, heating the autoclave to a certain melting temperature and maintaining certain melting time, cooling the autoclave to the foaming temperature and maintaining for a period of saturation, quickly releasing the pressure and immediately cooling the autoclave to room temperature, where the saturation pressure is 9-12 MPa, preferably 10.3 MPa, the melting temperature is 180-200 degrees C, preferably 180 degrees C, and melting time is 1-10 minutes, preferably 5 minutes, the foaming temperature is 105-115 degrees C and saturation time is 30-60 min, preferably 40 minutes.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); E15 (Alicyclics.); H03 (Transportation and storage - only large scale systems are included. PN EP3726473-A1
TI Displaying and processing image captured by optical system used to simulate back-view system of vehicle comprises capturing image, and calculating multiple sizes, positions and fields of view of region of interest of captured image.
AU JOCHMANN T
   HERMANN A
   SCHWALB M
   LINSENMAIER F
   ROETZER I
   EDER O
   MUALLA F
   KAUFFMANN M
   STEDER T
AE SMR PATENTS SARL (SMRP-C)
GA 2020A2544W
AB    NOVELTY - Displaying and processing image captured by optical system used to simulate back-view system of vehicle comprises capturing the image, calculating multiple sizes, positions, and fields of view of a region of interest (ROI) of the captured image, calculating a blur factor for blurring the ROI, manipulating an image gradient of the ROI pixel by pixel using the blur factor to reduce one of brightness or contrast of multiple pixels of the ROI, and displaying the processed image on a display. The ROI comprises multiple regions.
   USE - The method is useful for displaying and processing image captured by optical system used to simulate back-view system of vehicle.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: combines information gained by the use of different sensor systems allows for a more reliable and efficient determination of important parameters, e.g. velocity, and distance; has intuitive result; quickly interprets information presentation that can draw the attention of a driver to a potential dangerous situation; avoids distraction e.g. by smoothly fusing with the original image; increases acceptance and appeal of electronic mirror replacements; reduces adverse effect on aerodynamics, so as to minimize the total carbon dioxide emissions of the vehicle; reduces turbulent flows around the vehicle; and creates predominant laminar flow.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of the back-view display device with a region of interest.
   Rearview display device (700)
   First region (701)
   Second region (702)
   Virtual dashed-line (703)

PN CN111790374-A; CN111790374-B
TI Manganese-zirconium catalyst useful for catalytic oxidation of volatile organic compounds, comprises nano-zirconia as carrier and manganese oxide as active component.
AU CHEN B
   ZHANG Z
   ZHENG J
   QIAO G
AE UNIV XIAMEN (UYXI-C)
GA 2020A4260A
AB    NOVELTY - Manganese-zirconium catalyst comprises nano-zirconia as carrier and manganese oxide as active component.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for catalytic oxidation of volatile organic compounds, such as ethyl acetate and ethyl formate to carbon dioxide and water.
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst oxidizes volatile organic compounds at relatively low temperature, and has excellent high temperature stability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Manganese-zirconium catalyst comprises nano-zirconia as carrier and manganese oxide of formula: MnOx as active component, where catalyst is of formula: yMnOx/ZrO2-h.
   h = catalyst structure comprises Mn-O-Zr bonds;
   y = mass percentage content of MnOx, where the y is present in a value is 5-15% and the balance is zirconia;and
   x = manganese oxide compounds of different valences.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for preparing the catalyst involving (a) adding nano-zirconia balls to a manganese salt solution, immersing and aging the resulting mixture and calcining the resulting dried solid to obtain a manganese oxide of formula MnOx/zirconia catalyst, (b) modifying the obtained catalyst by hydrothermal reaction to obtain a manganese oxide of formula MnOx/zirconia-f catalyst, and (c) subjected the resulted catalyst to high-temperature steaming treatment to obtain the product; and
   (2) use of the catalyst for the catalytic oxidation of volatile organic compounds involving adding the catalyst into a fixed bed reactor, introducing volatile organic compounds gas for the reaction at 150-600 degrees C, a volatile organic compounds gas space velocity of 5000-30000 per hour under normal reaction pressure.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The manganese oxide comprises manganese(IV) oxide, manganese(III) oxide and manganese tetroxide. The manganese salt solution comprises an aqueous solution of manganese nitrate with a mass fraction of 50%. Preferred Property: The nano-zirconia has a size of 200 nm. Preferred Method: The formation of the manganese-oxygen-zirconium bond in the catalyst is obtained from a cleavage of the manganese-oxygen bond after manganese is connected to zirconium-oxygen. The step of immersion and aging is carried out for 8-10 hours. The step of drying is carried out at 60-100 degrees C for 4-6 hours and calcining at 550-600 degrees C for 3-5 hours. The step (b) involves carrying out hydrothermal reaction at 60-200 degrees C for 6-20 hours. The step of high-temperature steam treatment is carried out at 750-900 degrees C for 8-12 hours.
DC E17 (Other aliphatics.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

TI Pressure relief device for vehicle, has driving structure connected with valve plate to drive valve plate to move, and driving structure for controlling valve plate to switch between first state and second state according to air parameter.
AU SHAO B
AE BEIJING AUTOMOTIVE GROUP OFF-ROAD VEHICL (BEIJ-Non-standard)
   BAIC GROUP OFF-ROAD VEHICLE CO LTD (BAIC-C)
GA 2020A4057T
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a valve body formed with a pressure release hole, where a valve plate is movably arranged on the valve body. A driving structure is connected with the valve plate to drive the valve plate to move, where the valve plate is switched between a first state and a second state. The valve plate closes the pressure release hole when the valve plate is obtained in a first state. The valve plate opens the pressure release hole when the valve plate is obtained in a second state. The driving structure controls the valve plate to switch between the first state and the second state according to an air parameter of a vehicle, where the air parameter comprises oxygen content of air in the vehicle, carbon dioxide content, pressure and temperature or humidity.
   USE - Pressure relief device for a vehicle (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The device can control a valve sheet to switch between the closing and opening by the driving structure according to the air parameter in the vehicle, and achieves tight sealing for pre-opening the pressure release hole to form an air passage inside and outside the vehicle, and avoids easy deforming by the valve sheet to generate wrinkles, generating pressure sudden fluctuation when closing a vehicle door, discomfort of a passenger ear film and generating unanticipated leakage and improves better sealing effect.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a side view of the Pressure relief device for a vehicle.

PN GB2582956-A; DE102020107083-A1; CN111810338-A
TI Engine stop-start system for vehicle, has processor which is configured to receive multiple sensor outputs from multiple vehicle sensors and determine whether to start or stop engine of vehicle based on sensor outputs.
AU POUDEL D R
   HESKETH D
   PEDLAR C E
   PAISLEY G D
   PEDLER C E
AE FORD GLOBAL TECHNOLOGIES LLC (FORD-C)
GA 2020A0265R
AB    NOVELTY - The system (10) has a processor (32) that is communicatively coupled to multiple vehicle sensors, and each sensor of multiple sensors produces an output in response to a sensed parameter. The processor is configured to receive multiple sensor outputs from multiple vehicle sensors and determine whether to start or stop an engine of the vehicle based on the sensor outputs. Multiple vehicle sensors includes sensors from the list comprises a brake pedal angle sensor, brake pedal switch (26), brake pressure sensor, and accelerometer. The main portion determines whether to start or stop the engine based on a first control regime when the vehicle is stationary, and based on a second control regime when the vehicle is moving at a predetermined speed.
   USE - Engine stop-start system for vehicle.
   ADVANTAGE - The brake pressure stop threshold increases with the determined road gradient at a rate that increases with increasing road gradient over a range of road gradient values. The vehicle engine stop-start systems automatically switches off the engine of a vehicle under certain conditions to conserve fuel and reduce carbon dioxide emissions from the vehicle.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for operating vehicle engine stop-start system connected to vehicle engine; and
   (2) a non-transitory computer readable medium storing program for operating vehicle engine stop-start system connected to vehicle engine.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the engine stop-start system for vehicle.
   Engine stop-start system (10)
   Brake pedal (12)
   Brake pedal pad (14)

The model has a hexagonal morphology comprising many hexagonal frames for performing micro environment studies in relation with the metastatic bone tumor by mimicking the porous structure of a bone, and comprises a tissue scaffold, where the cancer cells are cultured for observing the tumor formation after it is implanted to the experimental animals.
   USE - As 3D scaffold model.

The PLA is used as a printing material in the 3D printer at a printing temperature of 250 degrees C in its production.

PN CN111575421-A; CN111575421-B
TI Method for adjusting shape of soft melting zone using scanning radar, involves determining optimal operating range, and comparing current state of blast furnace and best operating range to determine adjustment method of soft melting zone.
AU CHEN L
   LIU D
   CHEN W
   ZHENG H
   WANG Q
AE WUHAN STEEL CO LTD (WSGC-C)
GA 2020847590
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves scanning radar that obtains the shape of the material surface to calculate the cutting speed distribution. The soft melting zone distribution pattern is obtained through pattern recognition. The best fit between the cutting speed mode and the soft melting zone distribution mode is determined, and the optimal operating range of the cutting speed and soft melting zone characteristic parameters are determined. The current state of the blast furnace and the best operating range are compared to determine the adjustment method of the soft melting zone. The best fit between the cutting speed mode and the soft melting zone characterization parameter mode are found according to the blast furnace index change, air permeability index, blanking index, melting loss reaction carbon content, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, and heat load as the evaluation criteria. The accumulated zinc in the blast furnace and the moisture in the charge are removed.
   USE - Method for adjusting shape of soft melting zone using scanning radar.
   ADVANTAGE - The precise control of the soft melting zone is realized. The quantitative evaluation and adjustment are realized between the optimization of the cloth and the soft melt zone, and accurately identifies that the material contains water. The transition between the operating states of the blast furnace is greatly stabilized and avoids the abnormal furnace type, which is great significance to the stable production of the blast furnace, energy saving and consumption reduction.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the distribution of various soft melting zones. (Drawing includes non-English language text)

   SONG Y

AE UNIV SHANDONG NORMAL (UYSN-C)
GA 2020848839
AB    NOVELTY - Device comprises a reactor having a first cylinder and a second cylinder nested coaxially. Internal electrodes are connected to the pulse power supply on the axes of the two cylinders. The outer surface of the second cylinder is provided with a grounded outer electrode. The gap between the first cylinder and the second cylinder is filled with catalyst. The outer side of the first cylinder is close to the bottom surface of the reactor with a magnetic stirring plate capable of rotating along the first cylinder. The outer side of the second cylinder is provided with multiple electromagnetic components matched with the magnetic stirring plate. The polarity of the end of the magnetic stirring plate away from the first cylinder is the same as the polarity of the electromagnetic component on the side close to the second cylinder. The internal electrode is an aluminum rod, and the surface of the aluminum rod is provided with multiple protrusions.
   USE - The device is useful for catalyzing and reforming greenhouse gas by plasma.
   ADVANTAGE - The device: fully utilizes the magnetic field to change the movement of charged particles in the discharge air gap; increases the residence time of charged particles in the discharge interval; promotes the reaction of methane and carbon dioxide; and improves the efficiency of greenhouse gas reforming.

The protrusions are all in contact with the aluminum foil on the inner surface of the first cylinder. The device further comprises argon storage devices, carbon dioxide storage devices and methane storage devices. The gas outlet is connected to the soap film flow meter, cold trap, gas collection box, gas pump and gas chromatograph in sequence through pipelines. The catalyst is a nickel-aluminum oxide catalyst, which is fixed by quartz wool.

PN CN111578298-A
TI Converter gas dissipating catalytic combustion based heat exchanging system, has catalytic heat storage module connected with heat exchange module, where flue gas is passed into heat exchange module for realizing heat exchange process.
AU WEI X
   KANG R
   BIN F
   LI B
   YAO Y
   HE J
AE INST MECHANICS CHINESE ACAD SCI (CAMH-C)
GA 2020846942
AB    NOVELTY - The system has low operating cost, high catalytic efficiency and high waste heat recovery rate, and realize simple and safe operation. The system can satisfy national or regional emission standards, and recovers and reuses heat through a heat storage so as to improve waste heat utilization quality, energy saving and emission reduction effect.The system has a gas exchange module, a catalytic heat storage module and a heat exchange module sequentially arranged on a pipeline. The gas exchange module is connected to the catalytic heat storage module, where released gas is passed into the catalytic heat storage module to realize catalytic combustion exothermic reaction in the catalytic heat storage module for converting carbon monoxide into carbon dioxide. The heat storage module stores high-temperature flue gas. The catalytic heat storage module is connected with the heat exchange module, where the flue gas is passed into the heat exchange module for realizing heat exchange process.
   USE - Converter gas dissipating catalytic combustion based heat exchanging system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system has low operating cost, high catalytic efficiency and high waste heat recovery rate, and realize simple and safe operation.    USE - The dietary composition is useful for stabilizing postprandial blood sugar after meals.
   ADVANTAGE - The dietary composition has synergistic effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for preparing the dietary composition, which involves (a) adding wheat germ, barley germ, soybean protein, yam, pearl barley, buckwheat, quinoa and oat fibers to water for extraction, filtering the extract, mixing the filtrate, concentrating the mixture under reduced pressure, and spray-drying to obtain wheat germ powder, barley germ powder, soybean protein powder, yam powder, pearl barley powder, buckwheat powder, quinoa powder, and oat fiber powder, (b) crushing and sieving cinnamon, rhizoma polygonati, turmeric, lily, okra, medlar and cassia seeds, extracting by supercritical carbon dioxide, spray-drying to obtain cinnamon powder, rhizoma polygonati powder, turmeric powder, lily powder, okra powder, medlar seed powder and cassia seed powder, and (c) uniformly mixing the raw materials at a predetermined amount.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Composition: The composition comprises 5 pts. wt. wheat germ powder, 10 pts. wt. barley germ powder, 5 pts. wt. soybean protein powder, 3 pts. wt. yam powder, 3 pts. wt. pearl barley powder, 3 pts. wt. buckwheat powder, 3 pts. wt. quinoa powder, 5 pts. wt. oat fiber powder, 2 pts. wt. cinnamon powder, 2 pts. wt. rhizoma polygonati powder, 2 pts. wt. turmeric powder, 2 pts. wt. lily powder, 3 pts. wt. okra powder, 2 pts. wt. medlar powder and 3 pts. wt. cassia seed powder. Preferred Method: The step (a) involves performing extraction for 2-3 times, each for 60 minutes. The step (b) involves performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction at -10 degrees C to -50 degrees C and 30-50 MPa for 24-72 hours. The step (c) involves spray-drying at 30-50 degrees C.

TI Rubber-plastic labyrinth-type supercritical carbon dioxide micro-foaming injection molding device, has left hand-inclined peduncle whose top surface is matched with inner wall of heating machine barrel, and storage cylinder located outside cooling cylinder.
AU LI Y
AE LI Y (LIYY-Individual)
GA 202072192A
AB    NOVELTY - A rubber-plastic labyrinth supercritical CO2 micro-foaming injection device, comprising a feeding hopper, a heating cylinder, a feeding screw, a labyrinth mixing device, a homogeneous melt conveying block, a spline shaft, a transient heater, a pressure reducing cylinder, a cooling conveying screw block, a cooling cylinder and a storage cylinder. the heating machine barrel, the pressure reducing cylinder and the cooling machine barrel are connected in series to form a whole body. the spline shaft is connected with the feeding screw rod to synchronously rotate; the labyrinth mixing machine, the homogeneous melt conveying block and the cooling conveying screw block are mounted on the spline shaft to rotate along with the same; the labyrinth mixer and the homogeneous melt conveying block are located in the heating cylinder; the instantaneous heater is static before the pressure reducing cylinder; the cooling conveying screw block is located in the cooling cylinder. The device has good mixing effect, the design of the labyrinth mixer makes the material cycle to mix and convey; the mixed material is folded and mixed to form a homogeneous melt. instantaneous heating the instantaneous heating to make it nucleate, and then expanding and decompressing to form micro-bubble body. The double-cylinder combined storage improves the production efficiency by one time. The device has small volume, the structure is relatively simple and easy to process; the machine barrel has no leakage point, and it is safe to operate.

PN WO2020123894-A1; IN202147031192-A; EP3894483-A1; US2022017754-A1; IN399357-B; EP3894483-A4
TI Making carbon black by pyrolyzing unsulfonated            lignin, used in making tires and other high valued            products.
AU CHRISMAN R W
AE CHRISMAN R W (CHRI-Individual)
   CHRISMAN R W (CHRI-Individual)
   NORSE BIOTECH AS (NORS-Non-standard)
   NORSE BIOTECH AS (NORS-Non-standard)
GA 202054305C
AB    NOVELTY - Making (p1) carbon black by pyrolyzing                unsulfonated lignin is claimed.
   USE - The processes are useful for making carbon                black, which is used in making tires and other high                valued products.
   ADVANTAGE - The processes: produce high quality carbon                black that has a complex range of microstructures                that have been developed over the years to fine                tune its performance; can be made more sustainable                by using various biobased hydrocarbons e.g. land                fill gas or bio-oil as the hydrocarbon feedstock                for combustion heating; reduce the energy costs and                carbon dioxide production of the reactor by using                plasma heating for the process; and can be easily                adjusted to higher temperatures to provide a broad                range of carbon black microstructures.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) making (p2) carbon black by (a) exposing                biomass comprising lignin to pressurized water                heated to a temperature above 100 degrees C to                depolymerize the lignin to aromatic monomers and                (b) pyrolyzing the aromatic monomers to form carbon                black; and
   (2) preparing (p3) carbon black, comprising                (a1) separating the lignin polymer from the biomass                using pressurized water at a temperature of 160                degrees C, (b1) separating the hemicellulose which                is mostly converted to monomers from the solids,                (c1) heating the solids to a higher temperature to                liquefy the lignin polymer while still not                dissolving the cellulose, (d1) separating the                melted lignin polymer from the biomass to retain                the cellulose solids, (e1) maintaining the lignin                in pressurized water at a temperature of 170                degrees C for a sufficient time to hydrolyze the                lignin to monomers, (f1) flashing off the water and                drying the monomers, and (g1) feeding the dried                monomers into a high temperature reactor to form                the carbon black.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Process: In process (p1), the                  molecules of lignin monomers are fed into the                  furnace. The process (p2) utilizes furnace as                  shown in figure 1 as given in the specification                  by using the aromatic monomers as the feedstock                  oil. In process (p2), the furnace is heated by a                  plasma process. The step (d1) uses a sintered                  metal frit for the separation. The dried monomers                  from step (f1) are dissolved in alcohol and this                  solution is fed to the high temperature zone of                  the reactor in step (g1). In process (p3), there                  are adjustments in the reactor of temperature,                  feed rate and residence time to provide a range                  of carbon black particle sizes.
   EXAMPLE - Using water as the only reactant, lignin                polymer was separated from biomass using                pressurized water at a temperature of 160 degrees                C. The hemicellulose, which was mainly 5 carbon                sugars, was used for fermentation and was                water-soluble. The hemicellulose was mostly                converted to monomers by hydrolysis and was                separated from the biomass solids by filtration.                The solids were then heated to higher temperatures                (150-160 degrees C) to liquefy the lignin polymer                while still not dissolving the cellulose. The                melted lignin polymer was separated from the                biomass using a sintered metal frit to retain the                cellulose solids. The lignin was then maintained in                pressurized water at a temperature of 170 degrees C                for a sufficient time to hydrolyze the lignin to                monomers. The water was flashed off and the                monomers were dried. The dried monomers were then                melted and fed to the high temperature reactor to                form carbon black.

The air return port and air outlet are connected with air flow channel. The air return port is formed at the bottom of shell and arranged adjacent to window. The fan assembly is arranged in air flow channel to drive the air flow from air outlet and/or air return outlet to air outlet. The fresh air guide plate is arranged at the position of air inlet for opening and closing air inlet. The driving device is connected with air guide plate to adjust opening of air guide plate to open air outlet. The controller controls the opening of fresh air guide plate according to indoor carbon dioxide concentration.

TI Generating hydrogen gas involves e.g. injecting feed gas mixture comprising methane and hydrogen to first chamber of membrane unit, separating hydrogen from gas mixture and venting separated hydrogen via second outlet in second chamber.
AU XU S
   LU Y
AE SP INNOVATION PTE LTD (SPIN-Non-standard)
GA 202043946U
AB    NOVELTY - Generating hydrogen gas, comprises: injecting a feed gas mixture (165) comprising methane and hydrogen to a first chamber of a membrane unit, where the membrane unit has a membrane separating the first chamber from a second chamber; passing hydrogen through the membrane and so separating from the feed gas mixture; venting the separated hydrogen through a second outlet in the second chamber; and venting the separated feed gas mixture through the first outlet in the first chamber.
   USE - The method is useful for generating hydrogen gas, which his used for electrical power generation.
   ADVANTAGE - The system: is capable of generating high-purity hydrogen gas using feed gas mixture at the site of hydrogen utilization, hence reduces hydrogen transportation problems; improves the efficiency of electrical power generation using methane-rich gas; can be implemented in a compact and scalable way which makes the deployment relatively flexible; and reduces carbon dioxide emission.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a hydrogen gas generation system (150) comprising a gas membrane unit having the first chamber arranged to receive the feed gas mixture and the membrane, where the membrane is arranged to allow hydrogen to pass through, while retaining the remaining feed gas mixture, the second chamber includes second outlet for venting the separated hydrogen, the first chamber includes the first outlet for venting the separated feed gas mixture.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The figure shows a flow chart for a separation and delivery system.
   Hydrogen gas generation system (150)
   Feed gas mixture (165)
   Gas compression unit (175)
   Methane stream (185)
   Hydrogen stream (190)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further comprises: compressing the feed gas mixture prior to the injecting step; filtering the feed gas mixture prior to the injecting step; compressing the separated hydrogen after the hydrogen venting step; separating concentrated hydrogen and residual methane from the vented hydrogen using a secondary separation process, venting the concentrated hydrogen, and venting the residual methane, where the secondary separation process includes any one of pressure swing adsorption, cryogenic separation and thermal reforming; combining the vented residual methane with the separated gas feed mixture; after the step of venting the separated feed gas mixture, directing the separated feed gas mixture to power generation plant equipment, and so producing power from the separated feed gas mixture; and directing the produced power to power any one or a combination of the processes including (a) injecting the feed gas mixture to the first chamber of the membrane unit, (b) separating the hydrogen from the feed gas mixture, (c) compressing the feed gas mixture, (d) compressing the separated hydrogen, and (e) separating the concentrated hydrogen and the residual methane from the vented hydrogen. The directing step includes directing the combined residual methane and the separated feed gas mixture to the power generation plant equipment, and so producing power from the combined residual methane and separated feed gas mixture.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INSTRUMENTATION AND TESTING - Preferred Components: The system further comprises: gas compression unit (175) upstream from the gas membrane unit for compressing the feed gas mixture; filter unit upstream from the gas compression unit; a second gas compression unit downstream from the second outlet of the gas membrane unit for compressing the vented hydrogen; a secondary separation unit arranged to separate concentrated hydrogen and residual methane from the vented hydrogen using a secondary separation device, the secondary separation device including any one of a pressure swing adsorption, cryogenic separation and thermal reforming; and power generation plant equipment arranged to generate power from the separated feed gas mixture. The power generation plant equipment is arranged to direct the generated power to any one or a combination of: the gas membrane unit, the gas compression unit, the filter unit, the second gas compression unit and the secondary separation device.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

   AIR LIQUIDE ETUDE & EXPL PROCEDES CLAUDE (AIRL-C)
GA 202043492E
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves compressing air in a main air compressor (15). The compressed air is cooled in the precooling unit. The water and carbon dioxide are removed from the compressed air in a front-end purification unit (30) to form a dry air stream. The pressure of the dry air stream in the booster is boosted to form a boosted air stream. A first stream is withdrawn from the column system. The first stream in the heat exchanger is warmed. The first stream is withdrawn from an intermediate section of the heat exchanger. The first stream in the lost air turbine is expanded to produce an expanded first stream. The expanded first stream in the heat exchanger is warmed. The expanded first stream is withdrawn from a warm end of the heat exchanger. The expanded first stream in a supplemental air compressor is compressed to form a second compressed air. The second compressed air is combined with dry air stream downstream the front-end purification unit.
   USE - Method for improving capacity of existing ASU.
   ADVANTAGE - The user is allowed to increase production of an existing air separation unit without having to alter the capacity of the front-end purification unit.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a process for improving the capacity of an existing ASU.
   Air compressor (15)
   Precooler (20)
   Front-end purification unit (30)
   Drive first booster (40)
   Drive second booster (50)

PN CN111100983-A; CN111100983-B
TI Low-carbon environmental protection and high-efficiency sintering method of sintered fuel, involves directly feeding first fuel into granulator by external mixing to perform granulation of mixture with material to obtain mixture.
AU LI J
   LUO Y
AE WUHAN IRON & STEEL GROUP KUNMING STEEL C (WSGC-C)
GA 2020403569
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves including the mixing of ingredients, granulation, sintering of fabrics, crushing and cooling, and screening of whole grains. The fuel sieving is carried out before mixing the ingredients. The fuel is divided into two portions. The second fuel is loaded into the original fuel bin in the sintering batching room, mixed with the sintered material in the mixer, added with water and wet to obtain the material M1. The first fuel is directly fed into the granulator by external mixing to perform granulation of the mixture with the material M1 to obtain the mixture. The sintered materials are provided with 1-84 wt.% magnetite concentrate, 16-66 wt.% imported fine ore, 1-30 wt.% ordinary low-grade fine ore, and 8.1-20 wt.% quick lime.
   USE - Low-carbon environmental protection and high-efficiency sintering method of sintered fuel.
   ADVANTAGE - The problems of severe crushing and low utilization rate of fine-grained fuel in the sintered anthracite open-circuit crushing process are avoided. The fuel utilization and production efficiency are improved. The solid fuel unit consumption and harmful gas emissions are reduced. The sintering process is simple, and is provided with little change to the traditional sintering process, and strong practicability. The sintering technical indexes of refractory ores are improved, the range of ore use is expanded, and production costs are reduced. The emissions of carbon-dioxide (CO2), sulfur dioxide (SO2) and nitric oxide (NOx) are reduced, thus improving air quality.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flow chart illustrating the sintered fuel graded addition sintering method. (Drawing includes non-English language text)

TI Renewable resin composition used to make product            e.g. film, envelope and straw and for e.g. materials            and parts for automobiles, contains cassava starch,            polybutylene succinate, polybutylene            adipate-co-terephthalate and plasticizer.
AU NHAN H T C
   NHAN C H T
   HWANG J
   HUANG Z
   NHAN CHI H T
AE GREEN WHALE GLOBAL CO LTD (GREE-Non-standard)
   NHAN H T C (NHAN-Individual)
   GREEN WHALE GLOBAL CO LTD (GREE-Non-standard)
   GREEN WHALE GLOBAL CO LTD (GREE-Non-standard)
GA 2020343667
AB    NOVELTY - Renewable resin composition comprises 100 pts.                wt. cassava starch, 100-200 pts. wt. polybutylene                succinate, 20-120 pts. wt. polybutylene                adipate-co-terephthalate, and 10-40 pts. wt. a                plasticizer.
   USE - The composition is useful for: preparing a                product comprising a film, an envelope, a straw, a                container, a tray (all claimed), an industrial                tray, golf goods, a cut blade of wrap aluminum                foil, a confectionery partitioning member or a                special package, where the container is a container                for synthetic detergent, a drug container, a food                tray, a microwave food container, an insect                repellent container, a tray for soap, a lunch box                or a confectionery/candy container, and the                envelope is a cultivation envelope, a flower                cultivation envelope or a rubbish envelope; and                materials and parts for automobiles, civil                engineering, medical use, sports use, and                tree-planting.
   ADVANTAGE - The composition: has a biocontent of 35-70% in                accordance with ASTM D6866 standards; is able to be                easily mass-produced while having price                competitiveness because the cassava starch is used                by replacing potato starch or maize starch; is able                to be composted, and has a low amount of carbon                dioxide emission; is biodegradable and                simultaneously excellent in renewability through                reprocessing; is relatively soft, has high                elasticity and viscosity, and has an effect of                reducing the boiling time due to the low                gelatinization temperature; and has higher swelling                power than that of sticky maize starch in the                conventional composition, hence has high absorption                force, high transparency, and low aging                properties.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The composition                  further comprises an impact reinforcing agent.                  The composition comprises 100 pts. wt. cassava                  starch, 120-180 pts. wt. polybutylene succinate,                  40-100 pts. wt. polybutylene                  adipate-co-terephthalate, and 15-35 pts. wt.                  plasticizer. Preferred Components: The                  plasticizer comprises a polyhydric alcohol, a                  sugar alcohol, an anhydride of a sugar alcohol, a                  urea-based compound, a protein, an acid ester,                  and/or an aliphatic acid polymer, preferably                  glycerin oil.
   EXAMPLE - Pre-resin mixture was prepared by introducing                40 pts. wt. polybutylene succinate and 20 pts. wt.                polybutylene adipate-co-terephthalate into a mixer.                Subsequently, the pre-resin mixture was introduced                into a No. 1 main feeder of a twin-screw extruder,                27.3 pts. wt. cassava starch was introduced into a                No. 2 feeder, and 7.7 pts. wt. glycerin oil as a                plasticizer and 5 pts. wt. coupling agent                (vinyltrimethoxysilane) were introduced into a side                liquid feeder. The resin composition materials                introduced from the feeders were compounded and                extruded by the extruder, and subsequently cooled                in a water bath at 40 degrees C and then dried by a                dryer. A renewable resin composition in a pellet                form was prepared by cutting the dried extrusion                product into a pellet of 2.4-2.5 mm through a                pelletizing machine.

TI Integral non-noble metal catalyst useful for            purified terephthalic acid oxidation tail gas            purification comprises active ingredients, carrier            coating and honeycomb ceramic skeleton.
AU LU Y
   MIAO C
   JIANG J
   ZHANG L
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   SINOPEC SHANGHAI RES INST PETROCHEMICAL (SNPC-C)
   CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP SINOPEC (SNPC-C)
GA 202036698W
AB    NOVELTY - Integral non-noble metal catalyst for purified                terephthalic acid (PTA) oxidation tail gas                purification comprises active ingredients, carrier                coating and honeycomb ceramic skeleton, where the                active component is a deficient perovskite catalyst                AxBO3, where x = 0.6-0.95, where A is at least one                of lanthanum, cerium, bismuth, and B is a                transition metal cobalt, and the carrier coating is                cerium aluminum composition oxide.
   USE - The integral non-noble metal catalyst is                useful for purified terephthalic acid oxidation                tail gas purification (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The integral non-noble metal catalyst: has                high activity and good toxicity resistance, and can                realize high-efficiency catalytic combustion                removal of industrial exhaust gas, namely PTA; and                is economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   (1) preparing the monolithic catalyst,                comprising (i) preparing vacant perovskite catalyst                powder, (ii) preparing carrier composition oxide                coating powder, (iii) pretreating honeycomb                ceramics, (iv) coating of carrier coating, and (v)                performing activity coating of components;                and
   (2) catalytic combustion of PTA oxidation tail                gas, PTA oxidation tail gas is contacted with the                catalyst described in claims 1-9, and a mixture of                nitrogen and oxygen or air is introduced at 200-400                degrees C to volatilize the methyl acetate, xylene                and dibromomethane in the PTA oxidation tail gas                Catalytic combustion of organic compounds generates                carbon dioxide, water, hydrogen bromide and bromine                elements and each gram of catalyst contains                500-25000 ppm of exhaust gas and 5-50 l/hour.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The active                  component-deficient perovskite catalyst AxBO3 has                  x = 0.75-0.86. The A is at least two of                  lanthanum, cerium and boron. The molar ratio of                  the two elements in the A is 9:1-1. Preferred                  Method: The preparation of perovskite catalyst of                  the absence of active component comprises                  precipitating the salt solution of metal A and B                  components and alkaline precipitant to a pH of                  10-11, filtering, washing, drying and roasting.                  The cerium aluminum metal salt solution and the                  alkaline precipitation agent are used to                  precipitate to a pH value of 9-11, and the                  composition oxide coating powder is obtained                  after filtering, washing, drying and roasting and                  pretreatment of the honeycomb ceramics after                  drying the honeycomb ceramic at 100-120 degrees C                  for 4-8 hours, heating at 300-500 degrees C for                  2-5 hours. In the step (iv), coating of the                  carrier coating: carrier composition oxide                  coating powder is dispersed in water to prepare                  the coating solution I, and the honeycomb ceramic                  immersing in the coating liquid I, the immersion                  time is 3-8 hours, take out the residual slurry                  in the blow-dry channel, then dry, calcinate and                  weigh, and repeat the coating process at least 3                  times to the unit volume of the honeycomb ceramic                  skeleton The coating amount is 180-240 g/l, and                  the solid mass ratio in the coating solution I is                  30-50%, the coating of the active component in                  the step (v) disperseing the missing perovskite                  catalyst powder into The coating solution II is                  prepared in water, and the honeycomb ceramics                  loaded with the carrier in step (iv) is immersed                  in the coating solution II for a period of 3 to 8                  hours, and then taken out, and the slurry                  remaining in the pores is dried, then dried and                  calcined After weighing, and repeat the coating                  process at least 3 times, the amount of coating                  on the unit volume of the honeycomb ceramic                  skeleton is 50-100 g/l, and the monolithic                  non-noble metal catalyst is prepared, and the                  solid mass ratio of the coating solution II is                  30-50 degrees C.

The temperature and the                pressure in the reactor are influenced. A removal                stream of the aqueous liquid is withdrawn from the                reactor. The intensive contact of the fragments with                one another can produce a very fine-grained                abrasion, which is primarily formed from cement                stone. The very fine-grained abrasion can be                separated in an advantageous manner by removing a                removal stream from the aqueous liquid, which                contains the abrasion in the form of suspended                particles, from the reactor.

TI Synthetic fuel comprises methanol, aromatic hydrocarbon, refined light oil, solvent oil, condensate, methyl tert-butyl ether, gasoline anti-knock agent and catalyst containing antioxidants and rubber swelling agents.
AU WANG L
AE WANG L (WANG-Individual)
GA 202021364N
AB    NOVELTY - A synthetic fuel comprises methanol, aromatic hydrocarbon, refined 5C light oil, solvent oil, condensate and methyl tert-butyl ether in a weight ratio of 25:30:20:10:8:7, gasoline anti-knock agent and catalyst. The catalyst comprises organometallic corrosion inhibitors, antioxidants, rubber swelling agents, gasoline anti-knock agent comprises anti-knock agent(A) and gasoline anti-knock agent (B).
   USE - Synthetic fuel.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the low fuel consumption automobile methanol synthetic fuel, which involves (1) adding ethanol with purity greater than 99.5% into a dry and clean container, using 22 volt current for 40 minutes to produce hydrogen. The reaction is methanol reacts with water to form hydrogen and carbon dioxide and methanol can increase octane number by 7-8%, mixing methanol, aromatic hydrocarbons, refined 5C light oil, solvent oil, condensate and methyl tert-butyl ether raw materials by electrolysis having weight ratio of 25:30:20:10:8:7 or 15:30:30:10:8:7 into raw material tank to obtain mother liquor, adding organometallic corrosion inhibitor, antioxidant, rubber swelling agent, anti-knock agent (A) or anti-knock agent (B) and the mother liquid to the raw material tank at a weight ratio of 15:8:6:1:10000, mixing the raw materials for physical reconciliation, stirring using circulation pump for 1-2 hours and leaving still for 8-24 hours for sampling and analysis.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The preparation of organometallic corrosion inhibitor involves dissolving methanol, benzotriazole and benzylamine in a weight ratio of 3:5:2 for 8 hours using 200-mesh sieve and filtering the slag. The preparation of antioxidant involves dissolving T501 in methanol at a weight ratio of 1:3 for 2 hours. The preparation of rubber swelling agent involves dissolving methanol, dioctyl phthalate and lauryl alcohol at a weight ratio of 1:1:1 and leaving still for 8 hours. The preparation of anti-knock agent (A) involves dissolving methylcyclopentadiene manganese tricarbonyl, methyl tert-butyl ether, isooctane and n-heptane in a weight ratio of 5:442:290:4 and stirring for 20 minutes. The preparation of antiknock agent (B) involves dissolving tert-amyl methyl ether, dimethyl phosphonic acid, carboxylic acid and methyl naphthalene in a weight ratio of 35:45:15: 5 and stirring for 20 minutes.

TI Method for determining concentration of hydrogen            gas in fluid stream involves measuring response factor,            which is change in electrical resistance across zinc            oxide nanostructured thin film during contacting            relative to prior to contacting.
AU DRMOSH Q A
   YAMANI Z H
   HOSSAIN M K
AE UNIV DHAHRAN KING FAHD PETROLEUM & MINER (UYKF-C)
GA 202017445U
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves contacting the fluid                stream with a hydrogen gas sensor (200) having a                substrate, and a zinc oxide nanostructured thin                film deposited on the substrate. The zinc oxide                nanostructured thin film has a lattice structure                with a weight ratio of low binding energy                superoxide (O2-) ions to medium binding energy                oxygen vacancies in a range of 0.1 to 1.0. A                response factor, which is a change in an electrical                resistance across the zinc oxide nanostructured                thin film is measured during the contacting                relative to prior to the contacting. The                concentration of hydrogen gas in the fluid stream                is determined based on response factor. The fluid                stream includes hydrogen gas and at least one of                ammonia, butane, pentane, butene, pentenem and                carbon dioxide. The zinc oxide nanostructure thin                film does not contain platinum, palladium, nickel,                cobalt, copper, or aluminum. The substrate is glass                substrate or silicon wafer substrate.
   USE - Method for determining concentration of                hydrogen gas in fluid stream.
   ADVANTAGE - Facilitates better gas sensing performance                toward low levels of hydrogen gas since zinc oxide                nanonstructured thin film is fabricated by thermal                oxidation of metallic zinc at ultra-low values of                oxygen partial pressure, with substantially                improved surface roughness and porosity.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                setup for thermal oxidation of a metal thin film                deposited on a substrate at low partial pressure of                oxygen.

AB    NOVELTY - The device (100) has a main structure comprising a suction port formed on one side and a discharge port formed on a side opposite to the suction port. Multiple sensor modules (1020) are mounted on one surface of the main structure. Multiple through-holes are arranged on one surface of the structure so that multiple sensor modules are in fluid communication with the interior of the structure. The sensor includes temperature, humidity, ammonia (NH3), carbon dioxide (CO2), carbon monoxide (CO), hydrogen sulfide (H2S), hydrogen phosphide (PH3), hydrogen arsenide (ASH3), hydrogen chloride (HCl) And a sensor capable of measuring at least one of chlorine (Cl2).
   USE - Air component analysis device for livestock such as cattle, pigs, chickens or ducks.
   ADVANTAGE - The livestock air component analysis device is easily maintained and repaired. The livestock air composition analysis device more accurately measures material components contained in the air. The sensor modules is allowed to be easily attached to or detached from the main structure and prevent the intake port from interfering with the air inside the livestock facility.

PT P
PN CN110586078-A
TI Removing oxygen-containing volatile organic catalyst by dissolving aluminum oxide in aqueous solution, adding acetic acid, ball milling, soaking cordierite carrier in coating obtained, drying to obtain precursor and calcining.
AU HE C
   MA M
   YANG R
AE UNIV XIAN JIAOTONG (UYXJ-C)
GA 2019A77000
AB    NOVELTY - Method for removing oxygen-containing volatile organic catalyst by (i) dissolving 20-30 g gamma -aluminum oxide in 80-100 ml aqueous solution, adding 2-3 g silica sol binder, adding acetic acid to adjust the pH of the solution to 4-6, and performing ball milling for 6-8 hours after uniform stirring to obtain coating, (ii) soaking the cordierite carrier in the coating for 5-10 minutes, removing, blowing out the residual coating in the duct with compressed air, and drying, (iii) repeating the step (ii) twice to obtain aluminum oxide/coriolite sample with a coating amount of 4-6 wt.%, (iv) preparing manganese nitrate solution with a concentration of 0.8-1.2 m, placing the sample in a manganese nitrate solution for 0.5-1 hours, and drying to obtain a precursor of manganese(IV)oxide/aluminum oxide/cordierite monolith catalyst, and (v) calcining the sample obtained in step (iv) at 500-600 degrees C for 5-6 hours to obtain 1-2 wt.% manganese(IV) oxide/aluminum oxide/cordierite monolith catalyst.
   USE - Method for removing oxygen-containing volatile organic catalyst.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is safe, has stable purification of industrial oxygen-containing volatile organic compounds, excellent application prospects, reaction stability, hydrothermal stability, product carbon dioxide selectivity, and activity and can be used for efficient degradation of oxygenated volatile organic compounds (OVOCs) under industrial emission conditions.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The ball milling in step (i) uses ball mill at 1000-2000 radian per hour. The drying process is carried out at 100-120 degrees C for 10-12 hours. The cordierite carrier in step (ii) is a cylindrical carrier with a diameter of 2 cm and height of 5 cm, and 200 cpsi (the number of channels per square inch of cross section).
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

AU WOLAK J J
   WERVE M L
AE SQUAREROOT CORP (SQUA-Non-standard)
GA 201997536P
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a lamp node (200) comprising a LED array light source and a lens. A power source is configured to operate the system at 12 volts, where the lamp node comprises a swarm node controller, a programmable step-up power regulator, an ultrasound distance module, a heat sink, a fan and a temperature sensor. A table node comprises a rotating platform configured for placement of a plant. A plant monitor node comprises a sensor selected from a group consisting of a moisture sensor, a humidity sensor at a root level, a temperature sensor at the root level, a humidity sensor at a canopy level, a temperature sensor at the canopy level, a carbon dioxide sensor, an oxygen sensor and a light lumen detector.
   USE - System for controlling and monitoring growth of a plant in a garden.
   ADVANTAGE - The system adjusts an adjustable beam of high intensity light emitted by the light to promote uniform plant growth by targeting precise areas of the plant based on real-time analysis and feedback performed by a plant monitoring system. The system optimize growth of the plants by developing a database of time-tested growing processes that integrates with a complex real-time plant monitoring and management system so as to save energy consumption, money and time consumption.

   Lamp node components (210)

PN CN110447829-A
TI Seasoning flour product containing composite oil agent comprises wheat flour, short chain inulin, Calendula oil embedment, edible salt, monoglyceride, rapeseed oil, sesame oil, Smallanthus sonchifolius extract and water.
AU WANG X
   LENG Y
   ZHANG Y
   HU X
   DING B
   WANG S
   KE Y
AE UNIV WUHAN POLYTECHNIC (UWHP-C)
GA 201998114L
AB    NOVELTY - Seasoning flour product containing composite oil agent comprises 1100-1300 pts. wt. wheat flour, 40-60 pts. wt. short chain inulin, 13-17 pts. wt. Calendula oil embedment, 38-62 pts. wt. edible salt, 5-7 pts. wt. monoglyceride, 170-204 pts. wt. rapeseed oil, 30-36 pts. wt. sesame oil, 20-24 pts. wt. Smallanthus sonchifolius extract and 330-390 pts. wt. water. The short-chain inulin is a short-chain inulin obtained by extracting jerusalem artichoke with water, filtering, concentrating in vacuum, and evaporating the precipitate to dryness. The Calendula oil embedment is obtained by extracting Calendula with ethanol solution, performing supercritical carbon dioxide extraction, mixing with gamma -cyclodextrin saturated solution, filtering, and drying. The Smallanthus sonchifolius is obtained by extracting Smallanthus sonchifolius fruit with water, and filtering and vacuum concentrating.
   USE - Used as seasoning flour product containing composite oil agent and short chain inulin.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: reduces long-term storage; enhances oil-fixing ability; replaces some of the additives makes the product more natural; and beneficial to human health.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the product, comprising (a) mixing Calendula oil embedant, short-chain inulin, wheat flour and brine to form a surface floc, which is obtained by extrusion and ripening to obtain a semi-finished product, and (b) mixing the semi-finished product with rapeseed oil, sesame oil and Smallanthus sonchifolius, where the brine is a mixture of edible salt, monoglyceride and water.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Composition: The seasoning flour product containing composite oil agent comprises 1150-1250 pts. wt. wheat flour, 45-55 pts. wt. short chain inulin, 14-16 pts. wt. Calendula oil embedded substance, 44-56 pts. wt. edible salt, 5.5-6.5 pts. wt. monoglyceride, 178.5-195.5 pts. wt. rapeseed oil, 31.5-34.5 pts. wt. sesame oil, 21-23 pts. wt. Smallanthus sonchifolius extract and 345-375 pts. wt. water. Preferred Components: The wheat flour has return value of 85-1500 RVU, water solvent retention rate of 64-85% and total starch content of 68-77%. Preferred Method: The short-chain inulin is prepared by mixing jerusalem artichoke with water in a ratio of 1 g:(8-12) ml and activated carbon at 70-90 degrees C for extraction, where the volume ratio of jerusalem artichoke to activated carbon is 1:1-2, filtering and vacuum concentrating to obtain crude inulin, dissolving the crude inulin in water in a material-liquid ratio of 1 g: (6-10) ml, adding an excess of 25-35% ethanol, removing the supernatant and evaporating the precipitate. The Calendula oil embedment is prepared by comprises mixing dried Calendula with 65-75% ethanol at 55-65 degrees C in a material-liquid ratio of 1 g: (25-35) ml and extracting Calendula oil by supercritical carbon dioxide extraction with pressure of 12-18 MPa at 35-45 degrees C for 10-20 minutes, utilizing a microencapsulation technique, thoroughly mixing Calendula oil material with an excess of a saturated solution of gamma -cyclodextrin, cooling the mixture, filtering, and drying. The Smallanthus sonchifolius is obtained by mixing Smallanthus sonchifolius with water in a material-liquid ratio of 1g: (8-12) ml and activated carbon in a closed container at 70-90 degrees C for extraction, where the volume ratio of the Smallanthus sonchifolius to the activated carbon is 1: 1-2, and filtering through suction and concentrating in vacuum.
DC D23 (Oils, fats and waxes - including fatty acids, essential oils, but excluding butter (substitutes) and montan wax (C11B, C).); D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).)
MC D03-H02B2B; D10-A01
IP A23L-027/00; A23L-033/105; A23L-033/12; A23L-033/125; A23L-007/17; C11B-001/10

PN CN110279779-A
TI Topical traditional Chinese medicine useful for e.g. postpartum repair and slimming, comprises hawthorn, semen brassicae, rhizoma alismatis, ebony, adsuki bean, evening primrose, radix stephaniae tetrandrae and silkworm moth.
AU MO X
   REN G
AE JIANGXI ZHANGSHU ZHENGKANG MEDICAL BIOLOGICAL TECHNOLOGY CO (JIAN-Non-standard)
GA 201985646L
AB    NOVELTY - Topical traditional Chinese medicine comprises 3-12 pts. wt. hawthorn, 7-20 pts. wt. semen brassicae, 7-20 pts. wt. rhizoma alismatis, 3-12 pts. wt. ebony, 1-4 pts. wt. adsuki bean, 2-10 pts. wt. evening primrose, 3-12 pts. wt. radix stephaniae tetrandrae, 1-4 pts. wt. silkworm moth, 10-20 pts. wt. prepared rehmanniae root, 10-20 pts. wt. Angelica and 7-20 pts. wt. immature bitter orange.
   USE - The composition is useful for postpartum repair and slimming (claimed), nourishing spleen and qi, promoting urination, activating meridians, treating cold, clearing heat, detoxifying and relaxing bowel.
   ADVANTAGE - The composition has no toxicity and side effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing the topical traditional Chinese medicine, comprising (i) taking hawthorn and evening primrose, crushing, adding water, ultrasonically extracting, filtering to obtain the aqueous extract, vacuum extracting and concentrating to obtain aqueous extract concentrate, (ii) crushing rhizoma alismatis, ebony and prepared rehmanniae root, adding 60-70% ethanol solution, subjecting for high-pressure homogenization extraction, filtering, obtaining an alcohol extract and concentrating under reduced pressure to obtain a concentrated alcohol extract, (iii) adding semen brassicae, Angelica and immature bitter orange into the extracting tank, extracting using supercritical carbon dioxide and separating the extracting oil, (iv) ultra-micro crushing of adsuki bean and silkworm moth to obtain solid powder and (vi) mixing the water extract concentrate, the alcohol extract concentrate, the extract oil and the solid powder, stirring, homogenizing, filtering, and filling the filtrate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - PHARMACEUTICALS - Preferred Composition: The topical traditional Chinese medicine comprises 7 pts. wt. hawthorn, 12 pts. wt. semen brassicae, 12 pts. wt. rhizoma alismatis, 7 pts. wt. ebony, 2 pts. wt. adsuki bean, 5 pts. wt. evening primrose, 7 pts. wt. radix stephaniae tetrandrae, 3 pts. wt. silkworm moth, 15 pts. wt. prepared rehmannia root, 15 pts. wt. Angelica, and 12 pts. wt. immature bitter orange. Preferred Components: The wine alisma is roasted alisma or wheat bran fried rhizoma alismatis. In the step (i), the ratio of material to liquid is 1:5-10. In the step (ii), the ratio of material to liquid is 1:4-7. Preferred Method: In the step (i), the ultrasonically extracting at 50-60 degrees C for 30-50 minutes and the ultrasonic power is 600-800 W. In the step (i), vacuum extracting and concentrating until the relative density is 0.98-1.02. In the step (ii), the high pressure homogenizing and extracting pressure is 12-17 Mpa and the extracting time is 20-30 minutes.
   ACTIVITY - Vasotropic; Uropathic; Antidote; Antipyretic; Virucide.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

PN US2019291314-A1; WO2019183231-A1; CN111712363-A; EP3768486-A1; EP3768486-A4
TI Injection molding method for molding microcellular            foam article, involves introducing physical blowing            agent, injecting mixture into mold cavity of mold and            recovering injection molded microcellular foam article            from mold cavity.
AU DIX S E
   KISHBAUGH L A
   KOSHIBAUG L A
AE TREXEL INC (TREX-Non-standard)
   TREXEL INC (TREX-Non-standard)
   TREXEL INC (TREX-Non-standard)
GA 201982123Y
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves conveying a mixture of a                semi-crystalline polymeric material and a chemical                blowing agent in a downstream direction (17) to a                barrel (18) of an extruder (12). The chemical                blowing agent is decomposable to form carbon                dioxide present in an amount between 0.20 and 3.00                weight percent based on the total weight of the                polymeric material. A physical blowing agent (20)                comprising a nitrogen is introduced into the                mixture and the nitrogen present in an amount                between 0.025 and 1.50 weight percent based on the                total weight of polymeric material. The mixture is                injected into a mold cavity (26) of a mold (14). An                injection molded microcellular foam article is                recovered from the mold cavity.
   USE - Injection molding method for molding                microcellular foam article (claimed) using polymer                foam processing system.
   ADVANTAGE - The production of high quality injection                molded foam articles are enabled, by using certain                amounts of chemical and physical blowing agents                having desirable characteristics like small cell                sizes, thick skins and high elongations. The amount                of nitrogen in the mixture is controlled and                maintained to a specific level, by using a metering                device. The injection molded foam articles are                enabled to use in a variety of applications, due to                its excellent properties and characteristics.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an                injection molded microcellular foam article.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the schematic view of the                polymer foam processing system.
   Extruder (12)
   Mold (14)
   Downstream direction (17)
   Barrel (18)
   Nitrogen (20)
   Cavity (26)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - The chemical blowing agent is selected from                  the group consisting of citric acid, sodium                  bicarbonate, monosodium citrate, calcium                  carbonate and zinc stearate.

PT P
PN CN110236032-A
TI Apple juice sobering beverage used for e.g. reducing concentration ethanol in blood comprises concentrated apple juice, edible sugar, apple cider vinegar, honey, organic acid, sodium organic acid, potassium sorbate and edible flavors.
AU DAI H
AE DAI H (DAIH-Individual)
GA 201982195N
AB    NOVELTY - Apple juice sobering beverage comprises 85-95 pts. wt. concentrated apple juice, 25-35 pts. wt. edible sugar, 10-20 pts. wt. apple cider vinegar, 2-8 pts. wt. honey, 0.8-1.5 pts. wt. organic acid, 0.1-0.7 pt. wt. sodium organic acid, 0.1-0.3 pt. wt. potassium sorbate and 0.3-0.8 pt. wt. edible flavors.
   USE - The apple juice sobering beverage is useful for reducing the concentration of ethanol in the blood, and promoting ethanol removal.
   ADVANTAGE - The apple juice sobering beverage has high organic acid content and strong hangover effect, and esterifies with alcohol to form esters.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing the beverage, involving (a) adding the above-mentioned raw materials, (b) filtering the prepared liquid in a freezing tank using 0.45 mu m cardboard, cooling to 2-4 degrees C, and filtering by a microporous filter having a pore diameter of 0.45 mu m under a filtering pressure of less than or equal to 0.3 MPa, (c) mixing the liquid and carbon dioxide in a mixer, filling in a three-in-one filling machine, sealing, leak-proofing, and filling at less than or equal to 4 degrees C under the pressure of 0.25-0.3 MPa, (d) sterilizing in a warm bottle machine at 70-75 degrees C for 10-15 minutes, (e) packaging, and (f) warehousing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Composition: The apple juice sobering beverage comprises 90 pts. wt. concentrated apple juice, 30 pts. wt. edible sugar, 15 pts. wt. apple cider vinegar, 5 pts. wt. honey, 1 pt. wt. organic acid, 0.4 pt. wt. sodium organic acid, 0.2 pt. wt. potassium sorbate and 0.5 pt. wt. edible flavors. The apple vinegar has concentration of 10-80 wt.%. The concentration of carbon dioxide introduced during the mixing and filling is 1.5-2.5 times the total volume of the liquid. Preferred Components: The edible sugar comprises white granulated sugar, brown sugar, maltose, fructose and/or glucose. Preferred Method: The step (a) involves (i) treating and purifying water by reverse osmosis water treatment equipment, (ii) adding the treated water to a sugar can, heating at 80 degrees C, adding sugar, stirring, dissolving for 30-50 minutes, increasing the temperature at 80-90 degrees C, and heat-preserving for 20-25 minutes to obtain a syrup, (iii) preliminarily dissolving honey, sodium organic acid, potassium sorbate and organic acid in hot water at 90 degrees C or high, adding to the sugar can, mixing with syrup, and heat-preserving for 20-40 minutes, (iv) adding concentrated apple juice and apple cider vinegar to the mixture obtained in step (iii), and heat-preserving at 75-80 degrees C for 10-20 minutes, (v) adding the edible flavor to the mixture obtained in step (iv), stirring for 3-5 minutes, and rapidly cooling to below 45 degrees C, and (vi) making up to the required volume using sterile water. Preferred Conditions: In step (i), the conductivity of the treated water is less than or equal to 10 mu S/cm, and the total number of colonies is not greater than 1 colony forming units/ml.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. TI Process for preparing the synthesis gas by a combined feed, involves subjecting the raw coal to dry fine grinding, then sieved to obtain the pulverized coal of different particle size grades, and transferring slurry in slurry storage tank.
AU HE G
   ZOU T
   LIN Y
   XU H
   ZHENG Y
   DAI A
   LIU J
AE NORTHWEST CHEM IND RES INST CO LTD (NWCH-Non-standard)
GA 2019818176
AB    NOVELTY - The process involves subjecting the raw coal to dry fine grinding, then sieved to obtain the pulverized coal of different particle size grades. The pulverized coal is taken at a certain particle size by a slurry conditioner. The raw coal is wet grinded for adjusting the particle size distribution using the slurry adjusting agent. The slurry is obtained, and transferring the same in the slurry storage tank in reference to the mass concentration of the slurry. A multi-channel gasification nozzle (3) is equipped at the top of the gasification furnace (1). The slag descends into the quenching chamber (2), which is equipped with the gasification furnace. The coal inlet (4) is formed with the gasification furnace, and a crude gas outlet (5) is equipped at the opposite side of the coal inlet. The temperature gas includes carbon monoxide, hydrogen, carbon dioxide and methane.
   USE - Process for preparing the synthesis gas by a combined feed.
   ADVANTAGE - Ensures efficiency in preparing the synthesis gas by a combined feed, such that precision efficiency of the synthesis gas preparing process.

   Coal inlet (4)
