PN CN110226748-A
TI Low-sugar rose stuffing prescription comprises            rose petals, sugar, edible salt, honey, dried fruit,            nuts, agar, high methoxyl pectin, and citric acid.
AU LI J
   LI Z
   ZHOU L
AE UNIV NINGXIA (UNNX-C)
   LI J (LIJJ-Individual)
   NINGXIA JIAHEHUAYU ECOLOGICAL AGRIC CO (NING-Non-standard)
   LI Z (LIZZ-Individual)
   NINGXIA YAXIN LANDSCAPE PLANNING & DESIG (NING-Non-standard)
GA 201981217M
AB    NOVELTY - Low-sugar rose stuffing prescription comprises                300-600g rose petals, 150-1200g sugar, 18-36g                edible salt, 150-300g honey, 90-180g dried fruit,                90-180g nuts, 1.35-1.65 g agar, 3.35-3.65 g high                methoxyl pectin, and 2.85-3.15 g citric acid.
   USE - Low-sugar rose stuffing prescription.
   ADVANTAGE - The low-sugar rose stuffing prescription has                low sugar content and good stability, and is rich                in nutrient.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for preparing low-sugar rose stuffing prescription,                which involves:
   (A) mixing rose petals, sugar and edible salt                according to the ratio, pouring into the blender                for 5-10 minutes or artificially grind for 15                minutes;
   (B) adding honey, dried fruit, nuts, agar,                high methoxyl pectin and citric acid to the mixer                according to the ratio for secondary stirring,                where stirring time is 5-8 minutes;
   (C) pouring stirred mixture into a storage                tank, and placing storage tank in an open air for                full-light fermentation, where temperature is                controlled at 25-30 degrees C, and the interval is                3-4 hours per day for the storage tank, stirring                mixture for 1-2 minutes until no carbon dioxide                bubbles are released;
   (D) placing storage tank continuously in full                sunlight for 20 days, and placing storage tank                after sealing in the post-cooking workshop for                150-180 days; and
   (E) bottling, sterilizing, sealing and                labeling according to the required quantity in                batches and batches to obtain low sugar rose                fillings.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: The fruit comprises                  apple, pear, apricot, peach, raspberry, red                  jujube, medlar and raisin. The nut comprises                  cashew, hazelnut, chestnut, ginkgo, pistachio                  nut, macadamia nut, almond, peanut, sesame,                  walnut, sunflower, pumpkin seed, watermelon seed                  kernel and kernel. The storage tank is glass jar,                  a tile cylinder, and a 304 stainless steel tank.                  The sterilizing method is pasteurization                  sterilization.

AU ZHANG F
   XUE J
   ZHAO Y
   ZHANG D
   WANG J
   LIU B
AE INNER MONGOLIA HAIDAI POWER GENERATION (INNE-Non-standard)

   BASU A
   OGALE S B
AE INDIAN SCI EDUCATION & RES INST (INSC-Non-standard)
GA 201976676R
AB    NOVELTY - A scaffold comprises heteroatom-doped carbon nano-onion film to stabilize high energy density batteries based on lithium metal as anode, where the hetero-atom doping precursor compound for doping carbon nano-onion film is polymeric compounds containing fluorine, nitrogen, boron, sulfur or phosphorus.
   USE - Scaffold for use in fuel cell (claimed), and lithium metal rechargeable batteries, such as lithium ion, lithium sulfur, and lithium air batteries.
   ADVANTAGE - The scaffold can be prepared in stable, easy and cost effective, and stabilizes lithium metal anode in high energy density batteries based on lithium metal as anode, by preventing the dendrite growth.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for the following:
   (1) a method for synthesizing hetero-atom doped carbon nano-onion film, which involves preparing a polymer solution by dissolving doping compound in solvent under continuous magnetic stirring at temperature in the range of 25-30 degrees C for a period of 10-12 hours, coating the solution onto a conducting substrate and drying at temperature in the range of 60-100 degrees C, irradiating the film of doping compound with a carbon dioxide laser for pyrolytic graphitization to obtain the finished product
   (2) a full cell comprising anode and cathode electrodes, separator and solid electrolyte, where the lithium metal stabilized by a scaffold comprises fluorine doped carbon nano-onion film, the fuel cell exhibits capacity in the range of 100-150 mAh g-1 at current density in the range of 0.1-0.25 A g-1.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The heteroatom-doped carbon nano-onion film is fluorine doped carbon nano-onion film. The conducting substrate comprises copper foil, stainless steel, aluminum foil or carbon paper. The cathode comprises lithium titanate, lithium cobalt oxide, lithium iron phosphate or sulfur.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The electrolyte is lithium bis(trifluoromethanesulfonyl)imide in dioxolane:dimethyl ether. The solvent comprises N-methyl-2-pyrrolidone, water, dimethylformamide, ethanol or isopropanol.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The hetero-atom doping precursor compound comprises polymeric compounds containing fluorine as polyvinylidene difluoride, nitrogen as polyacrylonitrile, polyaniline, polypyrole, sulfur such as polyethylenedioxythiophene, polystyrene sulfonate, phosphorus rich compound as phytic acid or boric acid. The doping precursor compound is polyvinylidene difluoride.

PN CN109942255-A
TI Recycled concrete comprises e.g. cement, gravel, fly ash, water reducing agent, water and sand and the preparation of modified recycled aggregate comprises e.g. immersing recycled aggregate raw material in calcium hydroxide solution.
AU QI J
AE BEIJING GUOWANG CONCRETE CO LTD (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 201958707G
AB    NOVELTY - Recycled concrete comprises 265-305 pts. wt. cement, 605-675 pts. wt. gravel, 450-510 pts. wt. modified recycled aggregate, 680-735 pts. wt. sand, 65-90 pts. wt. fly ash, 4-70 pts. wt. water reducing agent and 35-180 pts. wt. water. The preparation of modified recycled aggregate comprises (i) immersing recycled aggregate material in calcium hydroxide solution with mass fraction of 5-10% for 20-30 minutes, taking out to obtain recycled aggregate A with calcium hydroxide solution attached to surface, (ii) reacting recycled aggregate A with carbon dioxide gas for 15-20 minutes to obtain recycled aggregate B, (iii) preparing a sodium fluorosilicate solution with a mass fraction of 0.4-0.6%, adding sodium hydroxide solution with stirring until pH of mixture is 6-6.5, adding kaolin with a mass fraction of 12-16% to mixture, mixing the mixture to obtain reinforcing liquid C and (iv) soaking regenerated aggregate B in reinforcing liquid C for 1-3 hours, taking out and drying to obtain product.
   USE - Used as recycled concrete.
   ADVANTAGE - The concrete improves the impact resistance and splitting strength and reduces the cracking phenomenon.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for preparing a recycled concrete comprising uniformly mixing cement, crushed stone, modified recycled aggregate, sand, water at 20-30 degrees C, adding water reducing agent, fly ash and continuously stirring for 10-20 minutes to obtain recycled concrete.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The preparation of the modified recycled aggregate comprises (i) immersing the recycled aggregate raw material in a calcium hydroxide solution having a mass fraction of 7% for 25 minutes, taking out to obtain a recycled aggregate A having a calcium hydroxide solution attached to the surface, (ii) reacting the recycled aggregate A with carbon dioxide gas for 17 minutes to obtain recycled aggregate B, (iii) preparing a sodium fluorosilicate solution with a mass fraction of 0.5%, adding sodium hydroxide solution with stirring until the pH of the mixture is 6.2, adding kaolin having a mass fraction of 14% to the mixture, and uniformly mixing the mixture is to obtain the reinforcing liquid C and (iv) soaking the regenerated aggregate B in reinforcing liquid C for 2 hours, taking out and drying, and to obtain the modified recycled aggregate. Preferred Composition: The recycled concrete comprises 280 pts. wt. cement, 635 pts. wt. gravel, 485 pts. wt. modified recycled aggregate, 705 pts. wt. sand, 82 pts. wt. fly ash, 5 pts. wt. water reducing agent and 150 pts. wt. water. Preferred Components: The recycled aggregate raw material is waste concrete construction waste, and the recycled aggregate raw material has a particle size of 5-10 mm, an apparent density of 2300-2350 kg/m3, a water absorption rate of 10-12%, a void ratio of 40-45%, mud content 8-12% and crush value average 32%. The cement is P.O42.5. The particle size of the crushed stone is 5-30 mm continuous grade, and the mud content is less than or equal to 5%. The sand has a fineness modulus of 1.6-2.2 and a mud content of less than or equal to 4%. The particle size of the fly ash is less than or equal to 0.1mm. The water reducing agent is a polycarboxylic acid water reducing agent.

PN WO2019116104-A2; WO2019116104-A3; CN111527292-A; US2020300231-A1; US10895248-B2; CN111527292-B
TI Geothermal energy system for Vanadium Redox flow cell or battery, has Catherine wheel comprising wheel arms configured around axle, and condenser receiving vaporized working fluid and separating working fluid back into fluid components.
AU ZHOU K
   HASSAN J
   JAFARI H
AE ZHEJIANG PLANET ROSE NEW ENERGY CO LTD (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
   ZHOU K (ZHOU-Individual)
   ZHOU K (ZHOU-Individual)
   HASSAN J (HASS-Individual)
   JAFARI H (JAFA-Individual)
GA 201951869E
AB    NOVELTY - The system (10) has a Catherine wheel (50) comprising a set of wheel arms (60) configured around an axle (55). An arm conduit extends within the wheel arms to an exhaust end of the wheel arms. A first working fluid i.e. carbon dioxide, flows through the arm conduit and into a turbine (90), and a second working fluid is introduced into the turbine, where the second working fluid is selected from a group consisting of hydrocarbon, propane and Isobutene. The first and second working fluids are mixed and vaporized within the turbine to form a vaporized working fluid that is expelled from the exhaust end to propel the wheel arms and the Catherine wheel about the axle. The first working fluid heats the second working fluid. An electrical power generator (53) is coupled with the Catherine wheel that produces electrical power when the Catherine wheel spins about the axle. A condenser (48) receives the vaporized working fluid and separates the working fluid back into working fluid components.
   USE - Geothermal energy system for a storage device i.e. Vanadium Redox flow cell or battery.
   ADVANTAGE - The system curbs sensible heat generation, lowers compression work, and reduces polytropic index during compression, thus improving efficiency of the system.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a method for producing energy.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of a geothermal energy system.
   Geothermal energy system (10)
   Condenser (48)
   Catherine wheel (50)
   Electrical power generator (53)
   Axle (55)
   Wheel arms (60)
   Turbine (90)

PT P
PN CN109897357-A; CN109897357-B
TI High-stability halogen-free environmentally-friendly flame retardant for plastics, is prepared from raw material comprising modified polyorganosiloxane, fumed silica, modified diatomaceous earth, surface treatment agent, polyethylene wax.
AU LIU Z
AE DONGGUAN HOOLION NEW MATERIAL TECHNOLOGY (DONG-Non-standard)
GA 2019562926
AB    NOVELTY - High-stability halogen-free environmentally-friendly flame retardant is prepared from a raw material comprising 45-80 pts. wt. modified polyorganosiloxane, 20-32 pts. wt. fumed silica, 8-15 pts. wt. modified diatomaceous earth, 2-5 pts. wt. surface treatment agent, and 1-2 pts. wt. polyethylene wax.
   USE - High-stability halogen-free environmentally-friendly flame retardant used for plastics.
   ADVANTAGE - The high-stability halogen-free environmentally-friendly flame retardant, which has good flame retardant and smoke suppressing effects, is safe and reliable, has wide source of raw materials, low cost, does not contain toxic additives, improves the mechanical processing and flame retardant heat resistance of the substrate, has good plasticity, effectively reduce the amount of smoke and inhibit the production of carbon dioxide, and fully meet the environmental requirements of the new flame retardant without halogen and phosphorus.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing a high-stability halogen-free environment-friendly flame retardant for plastics, which involves adding gas phase white carbon and modified diatomaceous earth to the mixer, stirring and mixing at 60-80 degrees C, atomizing the surface treatment agent into a mixture of the fumed silica, stirring the modified diatomaceous earth in a stirred state by using a high-pressure atomizing shower to obtain a premix, adding the modified polyorganosiloxane dropwise to the premix under agitation at 120-160 degrees C, stirring at a constant temperature for 1-3 hours, then adding with polyethylene wax, stirring, mixing well, discharging, and vacuum drying.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Conditions: The modified polyorganosiloxane is prepared by uniformly mixing the amber resin and the polyester resin in a mass ratio of 8-12:1, transferring to a mixer, and heating to 120-140 degrees C, first at 1000-2000 revolutions per minute, and stirring for 30-50 minutes. Then 1.5-2 wt.% of the polyester resin mass is added, and then stirred at 4000-5000 revolutions per minute for 60-75 minutes, and naturally cooled to room temperature to obtain a blend of amber resin and polyester resin. The blend of amber resin and a polyester resin is dissolved in an organic solvent, followed by heating to 130-136 degrees C, and then a catalyst is added. The polyorganosiloxane and a coupling agent are added, and the mixture is reacted at 135 degrees C for 2-4 hours under a pressure of 30-40 megapascal, followed by depressurization and drying to obtain a modified polyorganosiloxane. The polyester resin is polytrimethylene terephthalate, and the peroxide is one of tert-butyl peroxybenzoate, benzoyl peroxide or dodecanoyl peroxide. The blend of the amber resin and the polyester resin is in an organic solvent at a concentration of 0.4-0.6 g/mL, and the polyorganosiloxane is coupled with a coupling agent and amber resin. The mass ratio is 100:0.8-1.2:10. The polyorganosiloxane is one or more of polydiethylsiloxane, polydimethylsiloxane or polymethylsiloxane. The coupling agent is alpha-methacryloyloxymethyltrimethoxysilane. The modified diatomaceous earth is prepared by adding a titanate coupling agent to isopropanol, mixing uniformly, preparing a titanate coupling agent isopropanol solution, and then adding a titanate coupling agent isopropanol solution and polyvinyl alcohol mixing at a mass ratio of 1:0.4 to prepare a premix I, adding acrylic resin to ethanol, stirring and mixing until fully dissolved, to prepare a premixed liquid II, adding diatomaceous earth to the premixed liquid I, and stirring at a high speed of 3000 revolutions per minute for 1 hours at 25 degrees C. The solid is retained by filtration, and then added to the premix II, and stirred at a low speed of 800 revolutions per minute for 2 hours at 35 degrees C. The solid is washed with 10% sulfuric acid solution to remove impurities, then washed to a neutral solution, filterred, vacuum dried at 50-60 degrees C, and then fully grinded to a particle size of over 800 mesh to 95%. The mass ratio of the titanate coupling agent to the isopropanol is 3:8. The mass ratio of the acrylic resin to the ethanol is 1:6. The mass ratio of diatomaceous earth to premix I is 1:3. The surface treatment agent is amino silicone oil, hydroxy silicone oil, diphenyl silicon glycol or methyl ethoxysilane. The surface treatment agent is first heated to 90-110 degrees C, and then transferred to a high pressure atomizing shower.
DC A26 (Other condensation polymers including silicone polymers and polyimides (mineral silicates and similar materials would not usually appear in Section A).); A14 (Polymers of other substituted monoolefins; including PVC, PTFE.); A17 (Polymers of unsubstituted aliphatic monoolefins; including polyethylene.); A23 (Polyamides; polyesters. PN WO2019106097-A1; CN111406112-A; EP3717656-A1; US2020385673-A1; JP2021503916-W
TI Performing fed-batch process for culturing mammalian cells, involves controlling pH using a pH set-point of a first culture stage, by inoculating mammalian cells into culture medium at first pH and culturing cells at the first pH.
AU GREPPMAIR K
   LINK T
   SHAO Z
   LINKE T
AE HOFFMANN LA ROCHE & CO AG F (HOFF-C)
   HOFFMANN LA ROCHE INC (HOFF-C)
   HOFFMANN LA ROCHE & CO AG F (HOFF-C)
   HOFFMANN LA ROCHE INC (HOFF-C)
   HOFFMANN LA ROCHE & CO AG F (HOFF-C)
   HOFFMANN LA ROCHE INC (HOFF-C)
GA 201950418G
AB    NOVELTY - Performing fed-batch process involves controlling the pH using a pH set-point of a first culture stage, by inoculating mammalian cells into a culture medium at first pH and culturing the cells at the first pH, where in the first culture stage the pH set-point is maintained at the first pH. The second culture stage involves culturing the cells at a second pH that is higher than the first pH where in the second culture stage the set-point is maintained at the second pH. The second pH is 0.1 pH units higher than first pH, and the second culture stage has a duration of 6 hours.
   USE - Method for performing fed-batch process for culturing mammalian cells.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to perform fed-batch process using pH up-shift feed medium, or pH-increasing agent forincreasing product titer in a mammalian cell culture, where the product is expressed in the mammalian cells, increasing cell viability in a mammalian cell culture, extending longevity of a mammalian cell culture, reducing lactate accumulation in a mammalian cell culture, reducing ammonium accumulation in a mammalian cell culture, improving pCCh profile in a mammalian cell culture and reducing osmolality in mammalian cell culture, where the pH up-shift feed medium, or pH-increasing agent added to the mammalian cell culture allows to increase the pH from the first pH to the second pH, avoids the accumulation of undesirable metabolites such as lactate, ammonium and carbon dioxide, and minimize detrimental effects on the mammalian cells.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The second culture stage has a duration of 3 days. The temperature of the process is maintained within plus minus 0.5 degrees C, first pH value is 6.5-7.5, or 7.0, second pH is 0.1-0.5 pH units higher than the first pH, or second pH is 0.2 pH units higher than the first pH or the second pH is 7.1, 7.2, 7.3, 7.4, 7.5, or 7.1-7.5. The set-point is increased from the first pH to the second pH either, gradually or instantly. The set-point is increased gradually from the first pH to the second pH, and where the set-point is increased continuously or the set-point is increased in a series of discrete steps. The pH is increased gradually from the first pH to the second pH over a period of 24-72 hours. The fed-batch process involves adding pH up-shift feed medium to the culture medium. The set-point has a dead-band of plus minus 0.05 pH units. The produced fed-batch process product is expressed by the mammalian cells, where optionally the mammalian cells are recombinant cells, and the product is a recombinant protein. The isolating of the product optionally involves preparing a composition comprising the product. Preferred Components: The mammalian cells are chinese hamster ovary (CHO) cells. The product is an antibody, vanucizumab, or emactuzumab.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus has a storage unit (600) for                storing and accumulating quality information of a                workpiece associated with status information of the                workpiece by accumulating the status information. A                measuring unit (510) obtains current status                information of the workpiece in a workpiece                processing path. A quality estimator (700) extracts                a data aggregate corresponding to the current                status information based on data accumulated in the                storage unit and predicts quality of the workpiece                using the data aggregate. PN KR1985666-B1
TI Manufacturing noni syrup involves washing and drying all parts of noni to produce powder, then mixing noni powder, bellflower powder, hemp powder, cinnamon powder and honey to prepare mixture, and aging prepared mixture.
AU OH Y H
AE OH Y H (OHYH-Individual)
GA 201950602W
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacturing noni syrup involves washing and drying all parts of the noni to produce a powder, then mixing the noni powder, the bellflower powder, the hemp powder, the cinnamon powder and the honey to prepare a mixture, and aging the prepared mixture, where washing and drying all parts of the noni to produce a powder involves washing all parts of the noni with a 5-10 wt.% salt water and then a secondary washing with microbubbles water having a particle size of not more than 30 micrometer of not less than 55% in which 0.1-0.5 liter of ethanol is sprayed on a noni surface, sealing with a polymer sheet, aging for 10-12 days in a cool place, and drying for 15 minutes, then mixing with 6-12 wt.% noni powdered, 5-8 wt.% bellflower powder, 5-8 wt.% hemp powder, 1-4 wt.% cinnamon honey and 71-81 wt.% honey, and then aging the prepared mixture is carrying out under conditions of a humidity of 55-60, and aging at a temperature of 20-25 degrees C for 3 days or more.
   USE - Method for manufacturing noni syrup.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to manufacture noni syrup by removing the foreign substance or the microorganism sticking to the noni surface, and removes the disgusting smell of the noni for the aging period with the carbon dioxide generated through the respiration of the noni and the biological activity of the noni is suppressed and the long-term preservation is possible.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Compositions: Manufacturing method of noni syrup further adds the green tea powder processed as the pulse voltage 1-10 kilojoules/kg intensity in the mixture.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN109354871-A
TI High porous high temperature resistant poly(p-phenylene terephthalamide) material comprises poly(p-phenylene terephthalamide), fuming sulfuric acid, sodium carbonate and distilled water useful in e.g. automobiles and high-speed trains.
AU WANG L
   HUANG Q
   LIU Y
   FAN J
   XIA Z
AE UNIV TIANJIN POLYTECHNIC (UNTP-C)
GA 2019191896
AB    NOVELTY - High porous high temperature resistant poly(p-phenylene terephthalamide) material comprises 7-30 pts. wt. poly(p-phenylene terephthalamide), 93-70 pts. wt. fuming sulfuric acid, 2-8 pts. wt. sodium carbonate and 98-92 pts. wt. distilled water.
   USE - The material is useful in automobiles, high-speed trains, high-performance fields including aircraft and spacecraft.
   ADVANTAGE - The material: has three-dimensional porous structure, heat-insulating flame retardant, high temperature resistance, chemical stability and creep resistance. The method is simple and efficient, and is environmentally-friendly.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) preparing High porous high temperature resistant poly(p-phenylene terephthalamide) material comprises (i) dissolving certain mass ratio of poly(p-phenylene terephthalamide) polymer in fuming sulfuric acid, heating at 80 degrees C for dissolution, allowing to stand to obtain poly(p-phenylene terephthalamide)/ fuming sulfuric acid solution mixture, (ii) adding sodium carbonate powder into the water, (iv) quantifying prepared mixture into a mold, injecting aqueous sodium carbonate to produce carbon dioxide and foaming for 4-10 minutes to obtain poly(p-phenylene terephthalamide) porous material, (v) opening the drain port at bottom right of mold to recover waste water from mold, and (vi) taking out obtained porous material, washing with water and drying at room temperature; and
   (2) poly(p-phenylene terephthalamide porous material preparation device comprising liquid storage tank, constant temperature water bath, dissolving device, mold, control valve, liquid recovery device, drain pipe and drain pipe.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The method for preparing poly(p-phenylene terephthalamide porous material preparation device comprises placing dissolving device in constant temperature water bath to obtain solution, heating evenly, accelerating, dissolving, connecting liquid discharge port on lower right side of mold directly to liquid recovery device, reusing the waste liquid, sealing all the joints, equipping mold with automatic sampling device, filling liquid storage tank with mixture of poly(p-phenylene terephthalamide) and fuming sulfuric acid and filling with aqueous sodium carbonate. The method comprises generating large amount of carbon dioxide and foaming poly(p-phenylene terephthalamide) (PPTA) to obtain porous material. Preferred Components: The mold is made of acid-resistant ceramic to prevent corrosion and oxidation by solution and reservoir, drainage and discharge pipes are made of plastic material of Teflon (RTM: Polytetrafluoroethylene).

PT P
PN WO2019017748-A1; KR2019010815-A; KR1993392-B1
TI Production of aromatic compound e.g. benzene,            involves synthesizing aromatic compound from            methane-containing reactant gas in presence of            catalyst, selectively oxidizing and removing            crystalline hard coke, and regenerating catalyst.
AU KIM Y T
   HWANG A
   HAN S J
   TAE K Y
   AHRON H
   JU H S
AE KOREA RES INST CHEM TECHNOLOGY (KRIC-C)
GA 201909605Q
AB    NOVELTY - Production of aromatic compound (JJ) involves                (a) synthesizing an aromatic compound from a                methane-containing reactant gas in the presence of                a methane dehydroaromatization catalyst (CC), (b)                selectively oxidizing and removing crystalline hard                coke generated at ethylene-forming metal active                sites of the catalyst, and regenerating the                catalyst under conditions, where the removal of the                generated coke from polyaromatic compounds formed                at isolated acid sites in the catalyst is                suppressed, and (c) supplying the catalyst                regenerated in the step (b) to the step (a) and                continuously producing the aromatic compound.
   USE - Production of aromatic compound e.g. benzene,                toluene, ethylbenzene, styrene, xylene, and                naphthalene.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and efficiently produces                aromatic compound with high yield from                methane-containing gas by using methane                dehydroaromatization catalyst having increased                activity, improved regeneration efficiency and                excellent stability. The method requires less                energy and is economical since it does not uses                additional heat source for catalyst                regeneration.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) method for regenerating methane                dehydroaromatization catalyst; and
   (2) methane dehydroaromatization catalyst,                which comprises a support providing a point of                acidity capable of causing cyclization of ethylene,                a metal-carbide active component for forming                ethylene supported in the pores of the support, and                0.5-5 wt.% rhenium additionally supported on the                support.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view explaining                the process for producing aromatic compound from                methane. (Drawing includes non-English language                text)
   Carbon monoxide/carbon dioxide (AA)
   Methane (BB)
   Catalyst (CC)
   Reactor (DD)
   Catalyst regenerator (EE)
   Separator (GG)
   Aromatic compounds (JJ)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The support providing                  the acid point is ZSM-5, and the metal carbide                  active component comprises molybdenum. Preferred                  Composition: The catalyst comprises 3-7 wt.%                  molybdenum on ZSM-5.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method further                  involves separating the material discharged from                  a reactor (DD) performing the step (a) into an                  unreacted methane-containing gas and a target                  product in a separator (GG), where the unreacted                  methane-containing gas comprises carbon monoxide                  and/or carbon dioxide (AA). The conversion of                  methane (BB) in the step (a) is 13-25%. The                  unreacted methane-containing gas is used as a                  heat source in ammonia process or petrochemical                  process. The method alternately involves (i)                  synthesizing aromatic compound from                  methane-containing reactant gas in the presence                  of a solid catalyst for methane                  dehydroaromatization, (ii) cooling the solid                  catalyst through heat exchange with the                  methane-containing reactant gas, regenerating the                  solid catalyst by injecting an oxygen-containing                  gas into a catalyst regenerator (EE) without                  supplying an additional heat source, and                  selectively oxidizing and removing crystalline                  hard cokes, (iii) regenerating the catalyst and                  reusing it as the solid catalyst for the step                  (a), (iv) injecting carbon monoxide- and carbon                  dioxide-containing gases that occur during                  catalyst regeneration in the catalyst regenerator                  into the methane-containing reactant gas, where                  the carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide mixed with                  methane in the step (iv) is supplied together                  with the reactant in the step (i) to inhibit                  formation and/or removal of coke during the                  reaction. Preferred Conditions: The catalyst is                  regenerated by carbon oxidation at a temperature                  (T1) lower than the temperature (T2) at which the                  coke is oxidized and removed. The catalyst                  regeneration temperature is 400-500 degrees                  C.
DC E14 (Aromatics - i.e. containing at least one benzene ring.); J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).)

   (C) passing high-pressure carbon dioxide gas from the air inlet, so that the material in the kettle is squeezed out from the discharge hole and cooled into the water tank; and
   (D) placing the obtained material in a pelletizer to obtain pellets of modified polyamide.

TI Negative electrode active material for lithium ion secondary battery comprises carbon which contains silicon oxide particles on a portion or all of its surface.
AU YASUDA A
   OHSAKI Y
   NISHIDA T
   YASUDA Y
   OSAKI Y
   OOSAKI Y
   YASUDAAKIRA
AE HITACHI CHEM CO LTD (HITB-C)

   HITACHI CHEM CO LTD (HITB-C)
   HITACHI CHEM CO LTD (HITB-C)
   SHOWA DENKO MATERIALS CO LTD (HITB-C)
GA 201906192H
AB    NOVELTY - A negative electrode active material comprises carbon which contains silicon oxide particles on a portion or all of its surface, where in the X-ray-diffraction spectrum of the surface of carbon when using CuK alpha ray with 0.15406 nm wavelength as radiation source, ratio (Psi/Psio2) of X-ray diffraction peak intensity (Psi) of 2 theta =27-29 degrees derived from silicon (Si) to X-ray diffraction peak intensity (Psio2) of 2 theta =20-25 degrees derived from silica (SiO2) is 1-2.6, and specific surface area calculated from carbon dioxide adsorption at 273 K is less than or equal to 8.5 m2/g. In the volume cumulative distribution curve obtained by laser diffraction and scattering method, ratio (D10%/D90%) of particle diameter (D10%) when cumulative amount from small particle diameter side is 10% with respect to particle diameter (D90%) when cumulative amount from small particle diameter side is 90% is greater than or equal to 0.1.
   USE - A negative electrode active material for lithium ion secondary battery (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The negative electrode active material can improve the discharge capacity and the high-temperature-storage characteristic of an initial stage of a lithium ion secondary battery.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) negative electrode for lithium ion secondary batteries comprising current collector and negative electrode material layer containing the negative electrode active material provided on the current collector; and
   (2) lithium ion secondary battery comprising positive electrode, the negative electrode, and electrolyte.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: Average value of aspect-ratio represented by ratio (S/L) of short axis to long axis is 0.45-1. In the diffraction peak which belongs to Si(111) in an X-ray-diffraction spectrum, size of microcrystal of silicon computed from the diffraction peak is 1-15 nm. Negative electrode active material has volume average particle diameter of 0.1-20 mu m. Specific surface area computed from nitrogen adsorption at 77 K is 0.1-10 m2/g. Preferred Compositions: Negative electrode active material has 0.1-10 mass% carbon content with respect to total content of carbon and silicon oxide particles. PT P
PN CN109208769-A; CN109208769-B
TI Heat-insulating filling adhesive comprises adhesive containing acrylate, polybutene rubber, and methyl silicone resin, and adhesive containing sodium bicarbonate powder and ethyl orthosilicate, which are coated on different surfaces.
AU YANG S
AE XIAMEN PAITANTE ENVIRONMENTAL PROTECTION (XIAM-Non-standard)
   HANGZHOU ZHENGBO NEW BUILDING MATERIAL C (HANG-Non-standard)
GA 201908028U
AB    NOVELTY - A heat-insulating filling adhesive comprises an adhesive (A) and adhesive (B). The adhesive (A) comprises 1-3 pts. wt. dilute hydrochloric acid, 15-25 pts. wt. acrylate, 10-20 pts. wt. polybutene rubber, 5-12 pts. wt. methyl silicone resin, and 1-2 pts. wt. organic solvent. The adhesive (B) comprises 1-2 pts. wt. sodium bicarbonate powder, 10-15 pts. wt. ethyl orthosilicate, 10-18 pts. wt. acrylate, and 3-5 pts. wt. titanium dioxide powder. The adhesive (A) and adhesive (B) are coated on different surfaces, respectively.
   USE - Applicable to all types of insulation, such as insulation pipes, insulation walls, etc.
   ADVANTAGE - The heat-insulating filling adhesive fills carbon dioxide between different panels while bonding, to greatly reduce thermal conductivity of heat-insulating layer and improve heat-insulating effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method of using the heat-insulating filling adhesive, which involves coating adhesive (A) on a surface to be bonded, leaving still, repeating 1-2 times, coating adhesive (B) to another surface to be bonded without coating to the edge area, leaving still for 5-15 minutes, repeating 1-2 times, bonding the two coated surfaces, pressing by applying pressure from one side of the base plate to the other side, coating a heat-insulating layer on the surface of the adhesive (B)-coated surface, applying a pressure in a range of 2-20 cm from each of the folded edges, which is a position where the adhesive (B) is not applied, and maintaining the pressure for 20-60 minutes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The adhesive (A) comprises 0.1-4 mol/L dilute hydrochloric acid.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The adhesive (A) is coated on a sponge layer. The adhesive (B)-coated surface is provided with a concave structure. After coating adhesive (B), sodium bicarbonate powder is sprayed on the coated surface, and left still for 5-15 minutes. Preferred Property: The sponge layer has a thickness of 1-6 cm.

TI Lychee flavoured sparkling wine fermentation device comprises first fermentation tank, which comprises outer, and inner tank, where outer and inner tank body are spherical and overlapped and outer tank body top is set with outer port.
AU ZHOU Q
AE ZHOU Q (ZHOU-Individual)
GA 201909302T
AB    NOVELTY - Lychee flavored sparkling wine fermentation device comprises first fermentation tank, which comprises outer tank (11), and inner tank (12), where the outer tank and inner tank body have spherical structure and are overlapped. The top of the outer tank body is provided with a outer port (111), and bottom is provided with a first liquid outlet pipe and a outer feeding port is provided with a plug (113) matched with it. A first liquid switch valve is set on the liquid outlet pipe, and the inside of the outer tank body is provided with a liquid level sensor (114) and temperature sensor. The inner top of the tank body is provided with a feeding port, which is provided with a plugging cover matched with the caliber of the inner feed port, which is smaller than the external feeding port. The inner tank body is provided with multiple leakage holes, a pair of loop component, and pair of circulating component, that are symmetrically set at the two opposite side of the outer tank body.
   USE - Lychee flavoured sparkling wine fermentation device.
   ADVANTAGE - The lychee flavored sparkling wine fermentation device allows to realize the circulation of the carbon dioxide gas, which is effectively used, and realizes the automatic pressure relief to ensure the stable pressure in the fermentation tank to avoid blasting and collecting wine, without flocculating substance, and has good foaming effect.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a lychee flavored sparkling wine fermentation device

   Inner tank (12)
   Outer port (111)

The nitrogen oxide content generated by combustion is reduced, emissions are reduced effectively, and the dual effects of energy saving and emission reduction are achieved.

AB    NOVELTY - The battery has an alkali metal ion-                conducting separator electronically separating an                anode and a cathode. The cathode contains a wound roll of cathode active                material having cathode roll length, cathode roll                width and cathode roll thickness.    USE - The packaging material is useful for preserving fruit and vegetable (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The packaging material: is degradable and environmentally-friendly; has good anti-fog property and gas permeability, which can adjust the concentration of oxygen and carbon dioxide in the package; reduces the rate of food metabolism; maintains the food moisture; avoids the phenomena of condensation in the packaging bag; can keep the fruit and vegetable fresh; and can be partially degraded in soil by 80% in 30 days and completely degraded in soil by 90 days.

TI Liner for endoluminal stent or blood vessel, has bladder defining inner lumen for passage of blood, where bladder and fluid are conformed in response to pressure wave of blood in blood vessel, such that pressure within vessel is reduced.
AU FAIZER R
   BAROCAS V
   COLETTI F
   KIZILSKI S B
   DATTA A
   AMILI O
AE UNIV MINNESOTA (MINU-C)
   UNIV MINNESOTA (MINU-C)
GA 201885610V
AB    NOVELTY - The liner has a stent (10) defining a lumen (18) for a passage of blood through a blood vessel. A flexible bladder is arranged within the lumen and filled with a compressible fluid such as air, carbon dioxide, and oxygen. The bladder defines an inner lumen for the passage of blood. The bladder and the fluid are conformed in response to pulsatile pressure wave of the blood in the blood vessel, such that the pressure within the blood vessel is reduced. The bladder comprises a toroid cuff and the liner comprises an elongate elastic tube with axial length at equivalent to the axial length of the stent, where the liner comprises a first ring at a first axial extent and a second ring at a second axial extent.
   USE - Liner for an endoluminal stent or blood vessel utilized in a endovascular procedures for treatment of cardiovascular disease.
   ADVANTAGE - The liner can cause perturbations in blood flow by increasing pulse wave reflections and creating areas of stress both proximal and distal to a rigid span by reduction in pressure to provide better protection for tears and reduce risk of dissections.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for the following:
   (1) a method for deploying a liner for an endoluminal stent graft arranged within a blood vessel
   (2) a liner for a radially-inextensible lumen within a blood vessel.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of a stent graft.
   Stent (10)
   Outer layer (12)
   Support layer (14)
   Lumen (18)

A mixing device is configured to repeatedly apply acceleration to a cell culture container (10) so that the culture medium flows in the culture space. A curved portion is formed on a bottom surface of the curved surface portion. A pushing unit is configured to periodically push the bent portion toward the culture space is formed on the upper portion of the mixing device on which the cell culture container is mounted.
   USE - Cell culture system for monitoring and controlling real-time culture environment.
   ADVANTAGE - The oxygen concentration and the carbon dioxide concentration in the culture space are accurately monitored and changed to monitor and adjust the real-time culture environment so that the internal environment is maintained in the optimal state or changed to the target state. The accumulation and aggregation of cells submerged in the medium by self-weight is completely prevented.

AU RAVIKUMAR P
   KUMAR N
AE HINDUSTAN INST TECHNOLOGY & SCI (HIND-Non-standard)
   P R (PRRR-Individual)
   NITTEN K (NITT-Individual)
GA 201878858D
AB    NOVELTY - A geopolymer based concrete composition comprises coarse aggregate comprising hard broken granite metal of size 4.75-20 mm, fine aggregate comprising river sand, passing 4.75mm retaining 150 micron, fly ash and ground-granulated blast-furnace slag (GGBS) passing 150 microns, sodium hydroxide, sodium silicate, 15.9% sodium oxide, 31.4% silicon dioxide and 52.7% water. The fly ash is activated in alkaline medium as paste and optimizing to obtain mortar using the river sand as fine aggregate and hard broken granite stone as coarse aggregate.
   USE - Geopolymer based concrete composition for development of pavement interlocking block for use in construction application (claimed) and in structural formation such as precast bridge decks, water tanks and retaining walls, civil works and architectural applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The geopolymer based concrete composition is mechanically strong and ensures excellent tensile strength and compressive strength, and reduces the risk of shrinkage. The ground-granulated blast-furnace slag ensures higher durability of structure, reduces the temperature rise and avoids early-age thermal cracking, has excellent workability, and softens the visual impact of large structures. The geopolymer concrete is eco-friendly and an alternative to Portland cement concrete. The concrete reduces the stock of wastes and also reduces carbon dioxide emission and the environmental impact during manufacture of cement. The concrete composition provides excellent protection from corrosion to reinforcement steel and also ensures excellent acid resistance.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CERAMICS AND GLASS - Preferred Composition: The formulation is a mix proportion utilizing the fly ash as a filler material in geopolymer concrete, using river sand as fine aggregate and fly ash as a active material in geopolymer concrete by various proportions of fine aggregate to paste ratio of 1.8, fly ash to alkaline activator ratio of 1.2, sodium metasilicate to sodium hydroxide ratio of 2.0 and water to solid ratio of 0.3187. Preferred Properties: The maximum compressive strength of geopolymer mortar using river sand is 31.74 N/mm2. The compressive strength of geopolymer concrete is 16.07 N/mm2, in the proportion particularly for coarse aggregate to fine aggregate ratio which is 1.86.

GA 201878312X
AB    NOVELTY - The power plant has an isothermal compressor (60) for compressing recycle oil through a turbine (50). A nuclear reactor (30) heats the recycle oil from the isothermal compressor. A heat exchanger (34) is provided with a cooling fan that circulates circulation fluid from the heat exchanger with the nuclear reactor through a recuperator (70). The heat exchanger cools the recycle oil. The isothermal compressor forms an isotherm Brayton cycle and increases whole isotherm Brayton cycle efficiency through process of isothermal compression according to multistage compression process of reiterating multiple times and minimal process. The minimal process includes isobaric cooling process and isentropic compression process. The minimal process is repeated in the isotherm Brayton cycle. The recycle oil is selected among supercritical carbon dioxide, supercritical helium and mixing gas.
   USE - Module nuclear power plant e.g. small size/micro type module nuclear power plant.
   ADVANTAGE - The power plant immediately sets up the module for modularization at a power generation system and passively-safe system and is manufactured after doing carrying process through the minimum construction work so as to produce electricity, satisfy demand about the nuclear power plant for sino-soviet of the country, prevent serious affair among melting of the core and exclude possibility.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a module nuclear power plant operation method.

TI Binary refrigerant mixture used for single or dual evaporator refrigeration system, comprises refrigerant comprising n-butane or isobutane, and refrigerant comprising trans-1,3,3,3-tetrafluoropropene.
AU JUNGE B A
   DEVOS R
AE HAIER US APPLIANCE SOLUTIONS INC (HIER-C)
GA 201869353C
AB    NOVELTY - A binary refrigerant mixture comprises 20-22.5 wt.% refrigerant (r1) and 77.5-80 wt.% refrigerant (r2). The refrigerant (r1) is n-butane or isobutane. The refrigerant (r2) is trans-1,3,3,3-tetrafluoropropene.
   USE - Binary refrigerant mixture is used for single or dual evaporator refrigeration system (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The binary refrigerant mixture has improved energy efficiency. The binary refrigerant mixture is free of carbon dioxide. The refrigeration system is capable of efficient cooling without the use of a high global warming potential hydrofluorocarbon refrigerant such as tetrafluoroethane. The binary refrigerant mixture is less flammable than a hydrocarbon refrigerant such as n-butane or isobutane. The binary refrigerant mixture can be used in refrigeration systems for use with n-butane or isobutane refrigerants without changing their compressor design.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for refrigeration system (10), which comprises the binary refrigerant mixture, a compressor (64) for providing a pressurized flow of the binary refrigerant mixture, a condenser (66) configured to receive and cool the flow of pressurized binary refrigerant mixture, an expansion device (68) in receipt of the pressurized binary refrigerant mixture from the condenser and configured for reducing the pressure of the pressurized binary refrigerant mixture, and an evaporator (70) configured to receive and evaporate at least a portion of the binary refrigerant mixture and provide an inlet refrigerant flow to the compressor.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a single evaporator refrigeration system.
   Refrigeration system (10)
   Compressor (64)
   Condenser (66)
   Expansion device (68)
   Evaporator (70)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - MECHANICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Properties: The compressor is designed for use with n-butane or isobutane. The binary refrigerant mixture enables the n-butane or the isobutene compressor to have a run time of 50-53% when charged with the binary refrigerant mixture. Preferred Arrangement: The refrigeration system further comprises an additional expansion device and an additional evaporator.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The refrigerant (r1) is preferably isobutane. The binary refrigerant is classified as an A2 refrigerant. The binary refrigerant mixture preferably comprises 21-22.5 wt.% refrigerant (r1) and 77.5-79.5 wt.% refrigerant (r2). Preferred Properties: The binary refrigerant mixture has a heat of combustion of less than 19 kJ/kg. The binary refrigerant mixture is less flammable than n-butane or isobutane.

TI Switchgear fire alarm system has reference sensors that perform calibration of reference value of voltage and current sensor, temperature and humidity sensor, flame detection sensor and image sensor in specific section having linear value.
AU SOO J H
AE HYUNTAI CO LTD (HYUN-Non-standard)
GA 201870591D
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a reference value calibration module that performs baseline calibration of voltage and current sensor, temperature and humidity sensor, smoke concentration sensor, carbon dioxide (CO2) concentration sensor, flame detection sensor and image sensor. The reference calibration module stores acquired data acquired by each of reference sensors in ideal situation, performs baseline calibration in specific linear interval, and determines sensitivity of sensor based on assumption that sensitivity of sensor does not change. The long-term drift can be calibrated and drift detected can be used to compensate for drift occurring at sensor at arbitrary CO2 concentration in particular linear section. Each of reference sensors performs calibration of reference value of voltage and current sensor, temperature and humidity sensor, smoke concentration sensor, CO2 concentration sensor, flame detection sensor and image sensor in specific section having a linear value.
   USE - Switchgear fire alarm system.
   ADVANTAGE - The switchgear fire alarm system outputs an alarm before fire occurs in the switchgear. The position of the sensor attached to the switchgear can be freely moved. Baseline calibration is performed to remove the drift generated in the sensor. The switchgear is possible to control, when the fire occurs in the switchgear.

TI Gas shielded welding conductive nozzle for use in gas welding operations, has gas diffuser that has pore for passage of protective gas to form linear and uninterrupted airflow which surrounds welding point to form protective atmosphere.
AU LIU F
   GU S
   KONG L
   SUN Y
AE QINGDAO GUANGFENG AUTOMATION TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (QING-Non-standard)
GA 201868423E
AB    NOVELTY - The welding conductive nozzle has a nozzle main body (100), a gas diffuser (160) disposed at the front end of the nozzle body, and a guide (110) for guiding a welding wire (300) at the front end of the gas diffuser. A ceramic protector (210,220) is disposed in the nozzle body and the gas diffuser. The ceramic protector is provided with a core hole for the passage of the welding wire, and the welding wire passes through the ceramic protector through the core hole and is led out from the guide nozzle. The gas diffuser is provided with a pore for the passage of the protective gas. The protective gas passes through the pore to form a linear and mutually uninterrupted airflow which surrounds the welding point to form a protective atmosphere.
   USE - Gas shielded welding conductive nozzle for use in gas welding operations.
   ADVANTAGE - The welding nozzle utilizes a ceramic protector to automatically center the welding wire, reduce wear, isolate heat conduction, and achieve continuous welding at high temperatures. The gas diffuser increases gas delivery speed, reduces cylinder output pressure, effectively improves carbon dioxide utilization, reduces the emissions, and improves welding quality. The conductive nozzle has a split structure, which can be replaced as needed to reduce the cost of use and maintenance.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the cross-sectional view of the gas shielded welding conductive nozzle.
   Nozzle main body (100)
   Guide (110)
   Gas diffuser (160)
   Ceramic protector (210,220)
   Welding wire (300)

AE MITSUBISHI ELECTRIC ENG CO LTD (MITQ-C)

GA 201865572U
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus has a motion control device (40) for performing power running operation of a rotating electrical machine (20) when an anaerobic threshold (AT) is calculated by conducting a kinesitherapy test on a person to be tested such that a minus assist load is generated in a warm-up period. The motion control device decreases the assist load and increases regenerative load at a constant proportion in a continuous manner from the minus assist load to the regenerative load in a ramp loading period, which follows the warm-up period. The motion control device enables the apparatus to calculate the AT based on time-series data of respiratory metabolism comprising oxygen uptake, carbon dioxide emission and ventilation volume, which is obtained through a measuring instrument (30) during the ramp loading period.
   USE - Apparatus for calculating an AT by conducting a kinesitherapy test on a person to be tested.

TI Air conditioner system, has outdoor unit fixed with indoor unit, and radio frequency sensor for producing power based on communication signal emitted among indoor unit and/or outdoor unit, where system is operated based on produced power.
AU CHULMIN P
AE LG ELECTRONICS INC (GLDS-C)
GA 201864880N
AB    NOVELTY - The system has outdoor unit (100) fixed with an indoor unit (200a) in wireless communication. A radio frequency sensor (300) senses air-conditions and transmits air state data corresponding to the sensed air-conditions. The radio frequency sensor produces electric power based on a communication signal emitted among the indoor unit and/or outdoor unit, where the system is operated based on the produced electric power and the air state data comprises amount of temperature, humidity, atmospheric pressure, dust and data about amount of carbon dioxide and oxygen. The radio frequency sensor collects an electromagnetic wave energy based on the communication signal.
   USE - Air conditioner system.
   ADVANTAGE - The system utilizes the radio frequency sensor in which a position is moved, thus controlling the air-conditions of a desired domain. The system produces operating voltage of the radio frequency sensor using the electromagnetic wave generated in the wireless communication which the indoor unit and the outdoor unit perform, thus implementing radio frequency sensor operating without the separate power source. The system reduces unnecessary operation of the radio frequency sensor, thus improving energy consumption efficiency of the radio frequency sensor and preventing collision of air state data in the indoor unit and the outdoor unit from a predetermined apparatus.

PN CN108373573-A
TI Preparation of medical PVC hose involves filtering and drying mixture prepared using e.g. PVC, N,N-dimethylacetamide, polyallylamine and plasticizer, to obtain dry filter residue, and mixing residue with e.g. chitosan, and extruding.
AU WU Y
   XU Y
   HUANG H
AE WU Y (WUYY-Individual)
GA 2018624715
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of medical PVC hose involves (1) heating and stirring PVC and N,N-dimethylacetamide, in a mass ratio of 1:5-1:8, naturally cooling to room temperature, to obtain solution (A), heating and stirring polyallylamine and N,N-dimethylacetamide, in a mass ratio of 1:6-1:10, adding 0.2-0.4 %mass terephthalaldehyde based on the amount of polyallylamine, naturally cooling to room temperature to obtain solution (B), (2) mixing solution (A) and (B), in a mass ratio of 1.2-1.6:1.0, adding 8-10 %mass plasticizer, and 1-2 %mass dopamine, stirring, supplying mixed gas, degassing by vacuum to obtain a mixture, (3) adding the mixture to deionized water at 40-45 degrees C at a rate of 10-15 mL/minute, ultrasonically processing the mixture for 45-60 minutes, filtering, collecting the filter residue, and drying to obtain dry filter residue, (4) mixing 80-100 pts. wt. dry filter residue, 4-6 pts. wt. chitosan, and 3-8 pts. wt. modified ascorbic acid, kneading, and extrusion molding.
   USE - Preparation of medical PVC hose.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides PVC resin with improved plasticizing property and absorption of the plasticizer, without plasticizer loss and migration.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - In step (2), the solution (A) and (B) are mixed at a temperature of 2-6 degrees C, and the supply of mixed gas comprising carbon dioxide and oxygen, is carried out at a rate of 300-400 mL/minute.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mixed gas comprises carbon dioxide and oxygen, in a volume ratio of 50:1-60:1.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The plasticizer is dioctyl terephthalate, acetyl tri-n-butyl citrate, or dioctyl phthalate. Preferred Process: The modified ascorbic acid is prepared by heating and stirring benzoyl chloride and ascorbic acid, in a mass ratio of 1:1-1:2, under nitrogen gas protection.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Properties: PVC has a polymerization degree of 1000-1200. Chitosan has a degree of deacetylation of 80-85%.

PT P
PN US2018201505-A1; TW607963-B1; CN108328572-A; TW201827333-A; US10710878-B2; CN108328572-B
TI Generating hydrogen used as energy source in hydrogen fuel engine involves adding nitrogen-substituted derivative of alkyldithiolate ruthenium complex as biomimetic hydrogenase photocatalyst into solution and irradiating solution with light.
AU WU T
   LIN J
   PENG C
   CHEN Y
   WU D
   PENG J
AE UNIV NAT CHAO TUNG (UNCT-C)
   UNIV NAT CHAO TUNG (UNCT-C)
   SPRING FOUND NCTU (UNCT-C)
   UNIV NAT CHAO TUNG (UNCT-C)
   SPRING FOUND NCTU (UNCT-C)
GA 2018576935
AB    NOVELTY - A hydrogen is generated by adding nitrogen-substituted derivative of alkyldithiolate ruthenium complex (12) as biomimetic hydrogenase photocatalyst into a solution; adding organic acid reactant into solution; adding P-ligand (13) into the solution; adding electron donor into the solution; and irradiating the solution with light to produce hydrogen.
   USE - Generation of hydrogen used as energy source in hydrogen fuel engine.
   ADVANTAGE - Yield of hydrogen generation is enhanced. Converge solar light by Fresnel lens enhances utilization efficiency to reduce excess cost produced by providing external artificial electric energy or thermal energy to reaction. Economic cycle of hydrogen is improved where generated carbon dioxide may be recycled by carbon capture technique to achieve a purpose of energy saving and carbon reduction (ESCR).
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) hydrogen generation system comprising reaction chamber including container containing solution, and lens converging solar light to irradiate the solution to generate hydrogen where solutes contain nitrogen-substituted derivative of alkyldithiolate ruthenium complex (12) as biomimetic hydrogenase photocatalyst, organic acid reactant, P-ligand (13) and electron donor; and
   (2) solution comprising solutes having nitrogen-substituted derivative of alkyldithiolate ruthenium complex as biomimetic hydrogenase photocatalyst, formic acid (11) as organic acid reactant, tri(o-tolyl)phosphine as P-ligand and triethylamine as electron donor, and solvent which is dimethylformamide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the hydrogen generation reaction.
   Formic acid (11)
   Alkyldithiolate ruthenium complex (12)
   P-ligand (13)
   Solar light of the sun (16)
   Fresnel lens (17)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: Organic acid reactant is formic acid, P-ligand is tri(o-tolyl)phosphine, electron donor is triethylamine, and solvent of solution is dimethylformamide (DMF). Solution contains 1 mu mol nitrogen-substituted derivative of alkyldithiolate ruthenium complex, 3 mu mol of tri(o-tolyl)phosphine, 1 ml dimethylformamide, and 4 ml mixture of formic acid and triethylamine in which the molar ratio of formic acid to triethylamine is 5:2. Preferred Processes: Light is converged by Fresnel lens to irradiate solution, where light is solar light. Preferred Devices: System further includes storage tanks which store formic acid, tri(o-tolyl)phosphine, triethylamine and dimethylformamide, and pumping apparatus having pumping units which transfer formic acid, tri(o-tolyl)phosphine, triethylamine and dimethylformamide from each of corresponded storage tanks to reaction chamber. System includes valve apparatus which discharges hydrogen and carbon dioxide from reaction chamber to gas separation apparatus by controlling valve, gas separation apparatus which separates hydrogen and carbon dioxide, and hydrogen storage tank which stores separated hydrogen. Lens is Fresnel lens.
DC E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Chemical aspects of catalytic exhaust systems for cars are included as well as liquid or gaseous fuels of non-petroleum origin e.g. methanol or ethanol-based fuels. PN BR102016030554-A2
TI Pre-adjusting engine with biofuel combustion of a motor vehicle to the type of fuel to be injected, comprises identifying ethanol content in the fuel to be injected into the engine and identifying ethanol content.
AU TINELI G A
   FRANCO DA SILVA L L
AE BOSCH LTDA ROBERT (BOSC-C)
GA 2020457554
AB    NOVELTY - Pre-adjusting engine with biofuel combustion of a motor vehicle to the type of fuel to be injected, comprises: identifying ethanol content in the fuel to be injected into the engine (ER); identifying ethanol content in the fuel already consumed by the engine (EC); identifying engine temperature; estimating acceptable margin of error of ethanol content in the fuel in relation to the ethanol content of the fuel consumed (EC), such that if the ethanol content in the fuel to be injected (ER) is within from that acceptable margin of error;; identifying which among the error margins the ethanol content of the fuel to be injected (ER) is located; and selecting an adjustment ethanol (EA) content to be used to adjust the air/fuel mixture injected into the engine where if fuel to be injected (ER) is at sea with an acceptable error.
   USE - The method is useful for pre-adjusting engine with bi-fuel combustion of a motor vehicle to the type of fuel to be injected.
   ADVANTAGE - The method exhibits improved robustness, avoids failures in engine start, preferably when there is a change in the ethanol content contained in the fuel, reduces car's energy consumption and carbon dioxide emissions into the atmosphere.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for system for adjusting engine start to biofuel combustion of motor vehicle to type of fuel to be injected.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a flowchart illustrating the method for pre-adjusting engine with bi-fuel combustion of a motor vehicle to the type of fuel to be injected (Drawing includes non-English language text).

AU GERAGHTY S P
AE MONITORING LIFE LLC (MONI-Non-standard)
GA 201852218Q
AB    NOVELTY - The endotracheal tube apparatus (10) has an endotracheal tube (22) that is provided with proximal and distal ends (26,28). The distal connector portion of the proximal end fitting is connected to the endotracheal tube in physical contact with the endotracheal tube, and the proximal end fitting is located adjacent the proximal end of the endotracheal tube proximal to the proximal end of the endotracheal tube. The endotracheal tube is configured to be inserted into a trachea of a human body. A ventilation passageway (52) is mounted within the proximal end fitting and along a length of the endotracheal tube. The proximal end fitting includes a power source which is provided with a battery. The battery is located proximal to the proximal end of the endotracheal tube. The ventilation passageway within the proximal end fitting is open to a ventilation flow through the ventilation passageway while the battery is configured to power a component of the main portion.
   USE - Endotracheal tube apparatus for medical system (Claimed), used on human body.
   ADVANTAGE - The use of fluid inlet openings better aid in obtaining a carbon dioxide sample from the patient, and continues to operate in the event one of the fluid inlet openings becomes irreversibly clogged.

PT P
PN CN108220067-A
TI Brewing wine by selecting jujube, cleaning, cutting, soaking jujube pieces in sugar water, heating, mixing and juicing with peeled lemon, adding white sugar to adjust sugar content, inoculating yeast, fermenting, filling and sterilizing.
AU LI G
   WU J
   XU J
   XIAO C
   JIE Y
AE UNIV SHANXI NORMAL (UYSH-Non-standard)
GA 201855762B
AB    NOVELTY - Brewing wine involves selecting jujube, cleaning, cutting, removing the core, soaking the jujube pieces in 10% sugar water in a mass ratio of 1:1, and selecting fully ripe lemon, cleaning, and peeling; heating the jujube pieces and sugar water together at 90-95 degrees C, maintaining for 8-10 minutes to soften, and mixing and juicing with lemon in a mass ratio of 10:1; adding white sugar to the juice to adjust the sugar content to 22%; inoculating 0.7-1% yeast into the juice, performing main fermentation at 25-28 degrees C for 5-7 days, when the carbon dioxide bubble is reduced, the wine cap begins to sink, and the juice begins to clear, completing the main fermentation, removing residue, performing post fermentation at 20-25 degrees C for 15-20 days, pouring the post fermented wine into a sealed container, and storing and aging at 10-15 degrees C in the dark; and filtering the supernatant using diatomaceous earth filter, filling into a bottle and sealing, and sterilizing at 60-68 degrees C for 10-15 minutes.
   USE - The process is useful for brewing wine.
   ADVANTAGE - The process is capable of brewing wine with high vitamin C content. The brewed wine is golden in color, bright and transparent, has good flavor and a vitamin C preservation rate of more than 85%, and is also rich in flavonoids and triterpenes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: The jujube is chosen from jujube before white mature.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)
MC D05-E

PT P
PN US2018162024-A1; US10232533-B2
TI Manufacture of low density foamed article by placing thermoplastic polyurethane foam beads in mold, combining molten elastomeric ethylene-vinyl acetate copolymer with chemical blowing agent and supercritical fluid, and injecting mixture.
AU BAGHDADI H A
AE NIKE INC (NKEN-C)
GA 201846700P
AB    NOVELTY - A low density foamed article is manufactured by (a) placing amount of thermoplastic polyurethane foam beads in a cavity of a mold and closing the mold, where thermoplastic polyurethane foam beads have density of 0.01-0.1 g/cm3; (b) combining molten elastomeric ethylene-vinyl acetate copolymer with chemical blowing agent and supercritical fluid in extruder connected to the mold to form a mixture, where blowing agent is present in an amount of less than or equal to 15 wt.% based on total polymer weight in the mixture, and supercritical fluid comprises 0.1-5 wt.% supercritical carbon dioxide (CO2) and 0.1-4 wt.% supercritical nitrogen (N2) based on total polymer weight, and injecting mixture into the mold and foaming the mixture to form a foamed article; and (c) injecting the mixture into the mold at injection pressure of 110-207 MPa. Foamed article has density of 0.2-0.3 g/cm3.
   USE - Manufacture of low density foamed article selected from cushion or cushioning element for an article of footwear, protective equipment, or clothing (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The mixture of polymer, blowing agent, and supercritical fluid is injected into a mold containing the beads and form a foamed article containing the beads that has a lower density than can be achieved using supercritical fluid alone or using blowing agents other than supercritical fluids alone. When used as midsole, the molded article provides durability to the footwear, with addition of as little weight as possible while still cushioning to the desired degree.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions Amount of chemical blowing agent is 3-12 wt.% based on molten elastomeric ethylene-vinyl acetate copolymer weight. Supercritical fluid is 0.5-3 wt.% supercritical CO2 or 0.4-2.5 wt.% supercritical N2 based on total polymer weight in the mixture. The mixture further comprises a crosslinker.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Device: The mold comprises a porous tool which has a pore size of 3-20 mu m. The porous tool is a porous metal or metal alloy. The porous tool is an insert that is generally the dimensions of the mold cavity. The porous tool comprises a set of inserts spaced within the mold cavity.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Compositions: Thermoplastic polyurethane foam beads comprise thermoplastic polyurethane with melt flow index of greater than or equal to 160 g/10 minutes, as measured according to ASTM D1238 using 21.6 kg weight and temperature of 190 degrees C. Thermoplastic polyurethane foam beads comprise elastomeric thermoplastic polyurethane with melt flow index of 180-250 g/10 minutes. Thermoplastic polyurethane foam beads comprise elastomeric thermoplastic polyurethane selected from thermoplastic polyester-polyurethanes, polyether-polyurethanes, and/or polycarbonate-polyurethanes, preferably elastomeric thermoplastic polyester-polyurethane or elastomeric thermoplastic polyether-polyurethane. Elastomeric thermoplastic polyether-polyurethane is a reaction product of diphenylmethane diisocyanate. The molten elastomeric ethylene-vinyl acetate copolymer comprises 25-50 wt.% vinyl acetate. Preferred Parameter: Thermoplastic polyurethane foam beads have diameter of 0.5 mm to 1.5 cm.
DC A32 (Polymer fabrication - such as moulding, extrusion, forming, laminating, spinning.); A17 (Polymers of unsubstituted aliphatic monoolefins; including polyethylene.); A23 (Polyamides; polyesters. (including polycarbonates, polyesteramides); alkyds; other unsaturated polymers.); A25 (Polyurethanes; polyethers.); A83 (Clothing, footwear.)

PN US2018144940-A1; WO2018098002-A1; TW201824365-A; CN109983151-A; US10361081-B2; KR2019085020-A; JP2020501346-W; TW757372-B1; JP2022137154-A
TI Method for implanting carbon into substrate using            ion implantation system, involves ionizing carbon oxide            gas source and co-gas comprising phosphine in ion            source chamber to produce carbon ions, and implanting            ions into substrate.
AU COLVIN N
   HSIEH T
   COLVIN N K
AE AXCELIS TECHNOLOGIES INC (AXCE-C)
   AXCELIS TECHNOLOGIES INC (AXCE-C)
   AXCELIS TECH INC (AXCE-C)
   AXCELIS TECHNOLOGIES INC (AXCE-C)
GA 201840439P
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves ionizing a carbon oxide                gas source and a co-gas comprising phosphine in an                ion source chamber to produce carbon ions and                phosphorus oxide. The carbon ions are implanted                into a substrate, where the carbon oxide gas source                comprises carbon suboxide, carbon monoxide, carbon                dioxide, carbon containing gas and oxygen gas                mixture, the ion source chamber comprises multiple                components formed of or coated with lanthanum                tungsten, and the components are selected from a                group consisting of a cathode, cathode shield, a                repeller, a liner and an arc slit.
   USE - Method for implanting carbon into a substrate                using an ion implantation system for doping a                workpiece such as a semiconductor wafer, during                fabrication of an integrated circuit for use in                material modification applications.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables providing improved cathode                electron emission due to lower work function and                decreased formation of tungsten carbide or oxides                on cathode tip, thus reducing cathode electron                emission for carbon implants. The method enables                utilizing lanthanated tungsten alloy in ion implant                systems to mitigate etching and contamination                issues during carbon implantation. The method                enables avoiding damage of the cathode shield so as                to avoid premature failure of the ion source, and                pumping the compounds from the chamber while in                vapor form, thus significantly minimizing oxidation                of the cathode and cathode shield.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                method for implanting carbon ions into a                workpiece.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic block diagram                of an ion implantation system.
   Ion implantation system (100)
   Terminal (102)
   Beamline assembly (104)
   End station (106)
   Ion source (108)

PT P
PN CN107974431-A; CN107974431-B
TI Rapid amplification of natural killer cells comprises e.g. preparing natural killer cell medium, isolating culture of natural killer cells, transferring upper plasma, separating peripheral blood mononuclear cells and incubating.
AU DUAN H
AE BEIJING HUIZHI CHIKANG BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (BEIJ-Non-standard)
   BEIJING HUIZHI CHIKANG BIOTECHNOLOGY CO (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 201835781R
AB    NOVELTY - Rapid amplification of natural killer cells comprises e.g. (i) preparing natural killer cell medium; and (ii) isolating culture of natural killer cells, transferring anticoagulated peripheral blood into a centrifuge tube, centrifuging, transferring upper plasma into another centrifuge tube, using saline for diluting the plasma to obtain diluted blood, separating peripheral blood mononuclear cells from the diluted blood with Ficoll-Paque, resuspending peripheral blood mononuclear cells in complete activation medium, incubating, adding cell expansion medium and continuously cultivating.
   USE - The method is useful for rapid amplification of natural killer cells.
   ADVANTAGE - The method amplifies large number of natural killer cells in a short time, uses small amount of the culture medium and is simple and safe.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Rapid amplification of natural killer cells comprises (i) preparing natural killer cell medium; and (ii) isolating culture of natural killer cells, transferring anticoagulated peripheral blood into a centrifuge tube, centrifuging, transferring upper plasma into another centrifuge tube, using of a physiological volume of 0.5-2 times saline for diluting the plasma to obtain diluted blood, separating peripheral blood mononuclear cells from the diluted blood with Ficoll-Paque, resuspending 3-5 x 107/ml peripheral blood mononuclear cells in 40-60 ml of complete activation medium, incubating in a container at 35-40 degrees C in a 2-8% carbon dioxide incubator for 2-4 days, adding cell expansion medium into the complete activation medium and continuously cultivating for 8-12 days.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The prepared medium in step (i) comprises a complete activation medium and a cell expansion medium, where the complete activation medium comprises 800-1200 U/ml interleukin 12, 800-1200 U/ml interleukin 15, 800-1200 U/ml of interferon- gamma in Dulbecco's modified Eagle (DMEM) medium and the cell expansion medium comprises 800-1200 U/ml interleukin 12 and 800-1200 U/ml interleukin 15 in Roswell park memorial institute (RPMI)1640 medium. The amount of complete activation medium is 40-50 ml and the cell expansion medium is 800-1200 ml. The culture container is a 75 cm3 cell culture flask, and the amount of the cell activation solution is 8-12 ml. The culture concentration of the cells in step (ii) the culture vessel is 1-2 x 106/ml. The cultured cells in step (ii) having a concentration of 0.6-1.2 x 105/ml. Preferred Method: The step (ii) comprises rinsing the culture container with the cell activation solution before resuspending the peripheral blood mononuclear cells, where the cell activation solution comprises 0.5-2 mu g/ml of CD3 monoclonal antibody and 0.5-2 mu g/ml CD16 monoclonal antibody and 40-60 ng/ml stem cell factor with pH 7.2 phosphate-buffered saline.
DC B04 (Natural products and polymers. Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. General compositions.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)
MC B04-F04B1; B04-G04; B04-G21; B04-H02N; B04-H05C; B04-H16; B11-A04A; D05-H08B
