TI Automatic volatile organic compounds sampling            cabin, has mixed air channel whose air outlet is            arranged on top part of cabin body, and fan whose            output power is adjusted such that real-time wind speed            is controlled within preset wind range.
AU HUANG F
AE WUHAN BOFUTONG EXPERIMENTAL INSTALLATION (WUHA-Non-standard)
GA 201832636D
AB    NOVELTY - The cabin has a fresh air inlet system                connected with a fresh air inlet channel. A filter                is connected with a mixed air channel that is fixed                with a sampling cabin body in a parking area. A                humidifier (7), an air conditioner evaporator (8),                an electric heater and a fan (10) are arranged in                the mixed air channel. The fresh air inlet channel                is connected with the mixed air channel. An air                outlet of the mixed air channel is arranged on a                top part of the sampling cabin body. An output                power of the fan is adjusted such that real-time                wind speed is controlled within a preset wind speed                range.
   USE - Automatic volatile organic compounds (VOC)                sampling cabin.
   ADVANTAGE - The cabin can establish the automatic control                system so that the cabin environment can be                automatically adjusted and detected real-time                concentrations of formaldehyde, toluene, carbon                monoxide and carbon dioxide, thus reducing                interference of manual sampling and improving                detection precision. The cabin is provided with a                ventilation baffle plate and a load-bearing                platform that are fixed together to form a return                air passage, so that the air exit from the air                outlet of the mixed air duct circulates from the                top to the bottom part, thus performing uniform                dispersion and accurate sampling data.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram of an                automatic volatile organic compounds sampling                cabin. '(Drawing includes non-English language                text)'
   Humidity sensor (1)
   Humidifier (7)
   Air conditioner evaporator (8)
   Fan (10)
   Speed sensor (12)

PN CN107878183-A; CN207549945-U
TI Automobile engine air inlet quantity adjusting            mechanism has first linkage movable sheet formed with            linkage grooves corresponding to blades, where blades            are fixed to second linkage movable sheet that is            inserted into linkage grooves.
AU ZHANG R
   HE Y
   CHEN D
   SHEN P
AE NINGBO HAIDEXIN AUTOMOTIVE ELECTRICAL APPLIANCES              CO LTD (NING-Non-standard)
   NINGBO HAIDEXIN AUTOMOTIVE ELECTRICAL (NING-Non-standard)
GA 2018294811
AB    NOVELTY - The adjusting mechanism has multiple air inlet                blades (2) placed parallel to each other. Two ends                of the blades are rotatably installed on a grid                frame (1). The frame is provided with a driving                motor (3). An output shaft of the motor is                connected to a torsion bar (4). A first linkage                movable sheet (5) is arranged vertical to the                blades. The torsion bar is provided with a shifting                sheet. The first movable sheet is formed with                multiple linkage grooves (7) corresponding to the                blades. The blades are fixed with a second linkage                movable sheet (8) that is inserted into the linkage                grooves.
   USE - Automobile engine air inlet quantity adjusting                mechanism.
   ADVANTAGE - The adjusting mechanism adjusts the opening                angles of the air inlet blades according to the                engine temperature so as to increase working                efficiency of the engine and fuel-saving and carbon                dioxide emission-reducing performance. The power of                the driving motor is transmitted to the air inlet                blades by the torsion bar and the linkage sheets so                as to make the inlet blades to turn, open or close                and achieve simple transmission effect and high                working reliability. The adjusting mechanism has                reasonable design, simple and compact structure and                convenient adjusting effect.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a perspective view of the                automobile engine air inlet quantity adjusting                mechanism.
   Grid frame (1)
   Air inlet blades (2)
   Driving motor (3)
   Torsion bar (4)
   Linkage movable sheets (5,8)
   Linkage grooves (7)

PN CN107821835-A
TI Feed for preventing mouse obesity, comprises evening primrose oil, brown algae powder, witch hazel powder, malt powder, corn flour, soybean powder, colza cake cake, carrot powder, purslane powder, compound vitamin, minerals, and honey.
AU 
AE FOSHAN JUCHENG BIOCHEMICAL TECHNOLOGY (FOSH-Non-standard)
GA 2018260777
AB    NOVELTY - Feed comprises 0.1-1.5 pts. wt. evening primrose oil, 2-5 pts. wt. brown algae powder, 1-3 pts. wt. witch hazel powder, 4-8 pts. wt. malt powder, 5-25 pts. wt. corn flour, 5-10 pts. wt. soybean powder, 1-10 pts. wt. colza cake cake, 1-5 pts. wt. carrot powder, 2-8 pts. wt. purslane powder, 0.01-0.1 pts. wt. compound vitamin, 0.1-1 pts. wt. minerals, 2-3 pts. wt. honey, and 1-2 pts. wt. probiotics.
   USE - Feed for preventing mouse obesity.
   ADVANTAGE - The feed provides balanced nutrient filaments, which accelerates generation of adenosine monophosphate (AMP) in tissue cells, activates lipolytic enzyme activity, accelerates fat metabolism, decomposes fat and prevents obesity, and is rich is nutrition.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for preparing feed, which involves:
   (A) washing abd cleaning evening primrose seeds, then crushing through 20-40 mesh sieve, then adding into the extraction kettle and subjecting to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction for 3 hours to obtain evening primrose oil;
   (B) crushing soybean and colza cake through a 60-80 mesh sieve, then mixing with carrot powder, and purslane powder to obtain mixed powder; and
   (C) mixing obtained mixed powder, evening primrose oil, compound vitamin, corn flour, witch hazel powder, malt powder, honey, brown algae powder, and probiotics with water under uniform stirring, than transferring them to the crusher for crushing and drying to obtain desired product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Composition: The feed comprises 0.5-1.2 pts. wt., particularly 0.8 pts. wt. evening primrose oil, 2.5-4.5 pts. wt., particularly 3.5 pts. wt. brown algae powder, 1.5-2.5 pts. wt., particularly 2 pts. wt. witch hazel powder, 5-7 pts. wt., particularly 6 pts. wt. malt powder, 10-20 pts. wt., particularly 15 pts. wt. corn flour, 6-8 pts. wt., particularly 7.5 pts. wt. soybean powder, 3-8 pts. wt., particularly 5.5 pts. wt. colza cake cake, 2-4 pts. wt., particularly 3 pts. wt. carrot powder, 4-7 pts. wt., particularly 5 pts. wt. purslane powder, 0.03-0.08 pts. wt., particularly 0.06 pts. wt. compound vitamin, 0.3-0.7 pts. wt., particularly 0.55 pts. wt. minerals, 2.2-2.8 pts. wt., particularly 2.5 pts. wt. honey, and 1.2-1.8 pts. wt., particularly 1.5 pts. wt. probiotics. Preferred Conditions: The carbon dioxide extraction is performed under 32 megapascal pressure, maintaining separation pressure of 6-10 megapascal. The extraction is performed at 45 degrees C, separation temperature is maintained to 35 degrees C, and carbon dioxide flow rate to 80 L/hour.
   ACTIVITY - Anorectic.

Including testing of body fluids (other than blood typing or cell counting), pharmaceuticals or veterinary compounds of unknown structure, testing of microorganisms for pathogenicity, testing of chemicals for mutagenicity or human toxicity and fermentative production of DNA or RNA. PN DE102016213963-A1; WO2018019493-A1; CN109476047-A; EP3490768-A1; US2019168430-A1; US10596738-B2; CN109476047-B
TI Method for manufacturing structural part composite arranged in instrument panel area of motor car, involves molding functional device to base body of structural part composite, and manufacturing composite using external gas pressure method.
AU OSTWALD S
   HEINEMANN O
AE VOLKSWAGEN AG (VOLS-C)
   VOLKSWAGEN AG (VOLS-C)
   VOLKSWAGEN AG (VOLS-C)
GA 2018087270
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves molding a functional device (20) to a base body (10) of a structural part composite (100) during manufacturing the composite, where the functional device is designed as an air bag ascent channel. The composite is manufactured using an external gas pressure method and using polyproplylene. An external gas pressure is applied on a planar region of the base body during manufacturing the composite. A recess section of the functional device is cooled with carbon dioxide point refrigeration during manufacturing the composite, where the refrigeration is applied by molding tools.
   USE - Method for manufacturing a structural part composite that is arranged in an instrument panel area of a motor car and utilized as a part of an instrument panel of a motor car.
   ADVANTAGE - The functional device is molded to the base body of the structural part composite during manufacturing the structural part composite, and the structural part composite is manufactured using the external gas pressure method, thus enabling reduction or avoidance of incidence locations in a molded region of the functional device at the base body.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The air bag ascent channel is designed as a front seat passenger airbag ascent channel. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a structural part composite for a motor car.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of a structural part composite.
   Base body (10)
   Functional device (20)
   Structural part composite (100)

PN EP3276337-A1; FR3054664-A1; US2018039024-A1; FR3054664-B1; US10677988-B2; EP3276337-B1
TI Optical device for concentration sensor for            determining concentration of e.g. glucose in liquid            medium, has micro-resonator comprising elementary            waveguides that are arranged next to each other            according to arrangement in form of loop.
AU BOUTAMI S
   TAUREL B
   TAUREL B T
AE COMMISSARIAT ENERGIE ATOMIQUE (COMS-C)
   COMMISSARIAT ENERGIE ATOMIQUE (COMS-C)
   COMMISSARIAT ENERGIE ATOMIQUE (COMS-C)
GA 201808688L
AB    NOVELTY - The device (100) has a waveguide                micro-resonator (120) for guiding a light beam                according to a closed-loop optical path (20). An                injection and/or extraction waveguide (110) is                optically coupled with the micro-resonator for                injection and/or extraction of the beam. The                micro-resonator comprises a set of elementary                waveguides (121) that are spaced from each other                and arranged next to each other according to an                arrangement in a form of a loop. The waveguides are                made of a single material and exhibit same                dimensions. The waveguides are periodically                distributed according a distribution pitch                (P).
   USE - Optical device for use in a biological or                chemical sensor (claimed) such as gas sensor i.e.                concentration sensor, that is utilized for                determining concentration of carbon dioxide in a                gaseous medium and concentration of a glucose in a                liquid medium in a biological or chemical                environment.
   ADVANTAGE - The waveguide micro-resonator guides the light                beam according to the closed-loop optical path and                comprises the elementary waveguides that are spaced                from each other and arranged next to each other                according to the arrangement in the form of the                loop, thus enabling increasing of sensitivity of                the device to the surrounding environment of the                waveguide micro-resonator.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                biological or chemical sensor.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of an                optical device.
   Distribution pitch (P)
   Closed-loop optical path (20)
   Optical device (100)
   Injection and/or extraction waveguide                (110)
   Waveguide micro-resonator (120)
   Elementary waveguides (121)

PT P
PN CN107604177-A; CN107604177-B
TI Leaching arsenic gold concentrate and its leachate comprises e.g. adding arsenic gold concentrate into reinforcing agent, then grinding, mixing with alkali liquid for pressure leaching and performing solid-liquid separation.
AU CHEN Y
   WANG Y
   YE S
   DING J
   LIU Y
   XIAO L
   QIAN P
AE INST PROCESS ENG CHINESE ACAD SCI (CAGC-C)
GA 201808571B
AB    NOVELTY - Leaching arsenic gold concentrate and its leachate comprises e.g. adding arsenic gold concentrate into reinforcing agent, then grinding, mixing with alkali liquid for pressure leaching and performing solid-liquid separation obtain leachate and leaching residue, then adding lime cream to adjusting pH, and separating solid and liquid to obtain separating liquid I, then adding ferrous salt to adjusting pH and then performing solid-liquid separation to obtain separation liquid II and adding lime cream to adjusting pH and then performing solid-liquid separation.
   USE - The method is useful for leaching arsenic gold concentrate and its leachate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: reduces the energy consumption and avoids generation of intermediate product sulfur element under low temperature.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Leaching arsenic gold concentrate and its leachate comprises (i) adding arsenic gold concentrate into reinforcing agent, then grinding, mixing with alkali liquid for pressure leaching at 25-100 degrees C and then solid-liquid separation obtain leachate and leaching residue, (ii) adding lime cream into leachate obtained in step (i) to adjusting pH, then reacting and separating solid and liquid to obtain separating liquid I, (iii) adding ferrous salt into separation liquid I to adjusting the pH and then performing solid-liquid separation to obtain separation liquid II, (iv) adding lime cream into separation liquid II to adjusting the pH, then reacting and then performing solid-liquid separation to obtain separation liquid III, and (v) introducing carbon dioxide or air into separation liquid III , then reacting and performing solid-liquid separation to obtain separation liquid IV is returned to step (i) for leaching arsenic gold concentrate.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In step (i), the milled arsenic gold concentrate is more than 90 wt.% minerals with the grain size of -0.052 mm. In step (i), the strengthening agent is potassium permanganate, manganese dioxide, hydrogen peroxide, alkyl sulfonate, sodium alkylbenzene sulfonate, lignin sulfonate, fatty acid glyceride, stearic acid, potassium dichromate, persulfate, sodium chlorate, sodium hypochlorite, bromine water and/or iodine; when the strengthening agent is a solid phase; the addition amount of reinforcing agent is 0.01-20 wt.% arsenic gold concentrate; when the reinforcing agent is in the liquid phase, and the liquid-solid ratio reinforcing agent and arsenic gold concentrate is 5-100:1; and the alkaline solution is sodium hydroxide, calcium hydroxide, calcium oxide, potassium hydroxide and/or ammonium hydroxide; the concentration of alkali is 0.5-5 mol/l; and the liquid-solid ratio between alkali and arsenic gold concentrate is 3-10:1. Preferred Method: In step (i), the pressure during leaching is 0.5-3 MPa, and the leaching time is 3-24 hours. The step (ii), comprises adding lime cream to adjusting pH value to less than 12. The step (iii), the ferrous salt is ferrous sulfate, ferrous chloride and/or ferrous nitrate; and in step (iii), adding ferrous salt to adjusting pH value of 8-10. The step (iv), comprises adding lime cream to adjusting pH value to less than 12. The step (v), comprises introducing carbon dioxide or air and reacting for 6-12 hours. The leaching residue obtained in step (i) is used for gold extraction; the solid residues obtained after the solid-liquid separation in the steps (ii), (iii), (iv) and (v) are solidification treatment.
DC A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); M25 (Production and refining of metals other than iron - including ore treatment, extraction, working up scrap, obtaining specific metals, control testing methods (C22B).)
MC A10-E12A; A12-W12F; M25-G03; M25-G20

TI Capsule useful for e.g. promoting blood circulation, comprises herba patriniae, Chinese thorowax, safflower, Angelica, pangolin, peach kernel, radix trichosanthis, Phellodendron, rhubarb, yam, Epimedium, Cistanche salsa and licorice.
AU WU M
AE WU M (WUMM-Individual)
GA 201804920R
AB    NOVELTY - Capsule and its preparation method are claimed. The capsule comprises 13-15% herba patriniae, 5-7% Chinese thorowax, 5-7% safflower, 7-9% Angelica, 6-8% pangolin, 6-8% peach kernel, 4-6% radix trichosanthis, 6-8% Phellodendron, 4-6% rhubarb, 10-12% yam, 8-10% Epimedium, 10-12% Cistanche salsa and 4% licorice.
   USE - The capsule is useful for eliminating palatine tonsils, promoting blood circulation and tissue metabolism, eliminating blood stasis, clearing away heat and toxic materials, eliminating dampness, improving prostate blood circulation, improving dissipation and absorption of glandular tissue.
   ADVANTAGE - The capsule has a total effective rate of 96.6%.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for preparing the capsule, which involves (a) removing impurities from the above-mentioned raw materials, crushing, sieving the crushed raw materials using a sieve of 60 mesh size to obtain mixed powder, (b) using supercritical extraction device, transferring the mixed powder into an extraction kettle of the supercritical carbon dioxide (CO2) extraction device, extracting powder using extraction solvent at a temperature of 30-50 degrees C, and pressure of 15-30 MPa for 4-6 hours, increasing the temperature of separation kettle and reducing the pressure for separating, collecting separated agglomerated product, drying product, crushing dried product and sieving using a sieve of 100 mesh size to obtain extract powder, (c) adding the extract powder into capsule filling machine and preparing into capsule having a mass of 0.5 g, and (d) placing the capsule into UV irradiation for 20 minutes and packaging.
TF ACTIVITY - Vasotropic; Thrombolytic; Anticoagulant; Antidote.

PN CN114258806-A
TI Ventilation system for sunlight greenhouse plant local carbon dioxide concentration control, has fresh air pipe provided with carbon dioxide replenishing port, and carbon dioxide storage tank whose outlet end is connected with port.
AU ZHAO C
   LI A
   QI D
AE UNIV XIAN ARCHITECTURE & TECHNOLOGY (UXAC-C)
GA 202251347C
AB    NOVELTY - The system has multiple blast pipes vertically arranged at an air outlet of a soil-air heat exchange mechanism (1). An upper end of an air supply pipe (3) is connected with air in a sunlight greenhouse (12). A lower end of the blast pipe is extended to soil. An air return pipe (4) is vertically arranged on an air return port of the soil-air exchange mechanism. An outlet end of a carbon dioxide (CO2) storage tank (9) is fixed with a CO2 replenishing port of a fresh air pipe (2). A fan (6) is arranged in the soil air exchange mechanism and located between the air outlet and a return air inlet. The fresh air pipeline is provided with a carbon monoxide replenishing opening that is fixed at an outlet of the fresh air pipe.
   USE - Ventilation system for controlling local carbon dioxide concentration of sunlight greenhouse plant in sunlight greenhouse technology field.
   ADVANTAGE - The system meets the efficient supplement of carbon dioxide in the sunlight greenhouse, effectively promotes the light effect of the plant, and does not affect the carbon dioxide concentration outside the plant planting area, thus ensuring the safety of the worker. The system realizes the fresh air conveying of the air and carbon dioxide supplement, avoiding the waste and leakage of carbon monoxide, and high supplementary efficiency. The air supply device adopts the combination of air supply branch pipe and the air supply strip, several umbrella-shaped air supply strips form a package type air supply structure, ensuring that the supplemented carbon dioxide is fully around the plant blade, realizing effective utilization of carbon-di-oxide leakage, and reducing carbon dioxide discharge.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a ventilation method for controlling local CO concentration of sunlight greenhouse plant.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a sectional view of the ventilation system of sunlight greenhouse plant local CO2 concentration control.

   Air return pipe (4)
   Air supply device (5)
   Fan (6)
   Static pressure box (7)
   Replenishing valve (8)
   Storage tank (9)
   Sunlight greenhouse (12)

PN CN111934171-A; CN212342990-U
TI Detachable carbon dioxide laser tube lens cooling device has second retaining ring which is fixed on outer surface of mounting ring close to carbon dioxide laser tube frame whose end fixed with cylindrical laser tube connecting pipe section.
AU ZHANG L
AE BEIJING LEIHAI LASER TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (BEIJ-Non-standard)
GA 2020B57517
AB    NOVELTY - The device has a cylindrical laser tube connecting pipe section (2) which is fixed at the end of a carbon dioxide laser tube frame (1) and communicating with it. A mounting ring (3) sleeved on the laser tube connecting pipe section, a lens (4) glued to an end of the laser tube connecting pipe section away from the carbon dioxide laser tube frame. A cooling jacket is installed at end of lens. A first retaining ring (20) is fixed on the outer surface of one end of the laser tube connecting pipe section away from carbon dioxide laser tube frame. An external thread (30) is provided on side of the mounting ring. A second retaining ring (31) is fixed on the outer surface of one end of the mounting ring close to carbon dioxide laser tube frame. The end surface of the cooling jacket close to the laser tube connecting pipe section abuts against the end surface of the lens away from the laser tube connecting pipe section. The mounting ring is made of 35chromium molybdenum (CrMoA) material.
   USE - Detachable carbon dioxide laser tube lens cooling device.
   ADVANTAGE - In the detachable carbon dioxide laser tube lens cooling device, a mounting ring is sleeved on the connecting pipe section of the laser tube, and this arrangement can screw one end of the cooling jacket to the mounting ring. The end of the cooling sleeve close to the lens is in close contact with the lens to realize the cooling of the lens by the cooling sleeve and while ensuring that the cooling sleeve can fully cool the lens. The device is convenient to disassemble and install the cooling jacket or replace other related lens devices.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a cross-sectional view of the lens installation at the laser tube connecting tube section of a detachable carbon dioxide laser tube lens cooling device.

PN WO2019121739-A1; LU101346-B1; AU2018391784-A1; CA3085087-A1; KR2020108287-A; EP3728444-A1; US2021115164-A1
TI Water-soluble binder composition for e.g., manufacturing product having collection of matter bound by polymeric binder comprises carbohydrate component and carbon dioxide reaction product of carbon dioxide with nitrogen-containing compound.
AU HAMPSON C
   JONES G
AE KNAUF INSULATION SPRL (KNAF-C)
   KNAUF INSULATION INC (KNAF-C)
   KNAUF INSULATION SPRL (KNAF-C)
   KNAUF INSULATION INC (KNAF-C)
   KNAUF INSULATION SPRL (KNAF-C)
   KNAUF INSULATION INC (KNAF-C)
   KNAUF INSULATION INC (KNAF-C)
   KNAUF INSULATION SPRL (KNAF-C)
GA 201954069R
AB    NOVELTY - A water-soluble, curable, thermosetting binder composition comprises greater than or equal to 1 carbohydrate component and greater than or equal to 1 carbon dioxide reaction product comprising reaction product(s) of carbon dioxide with greater than or equal to 1 nitrogen-containing compound.
   USE - A water-soluble binder composition for binder solution or dispersion and manufacturing product containing collection of matter bound by polymeric binder (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The composition may be easily be sprayed onto the loose assembly or matter and heat treated for curing. In the course of curing, the carbon dioxide bound to components of binder composition is eliminated, thus allowing for proper curing and/or cross-linking reactions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for:
   (1) manufacturing a binder composition which involves (i) providing greater than or equal to 1 carbohydrate component, (ii) providing greater than or equal to 1 nitrogen-containing component, (iii) providing carbon dioxide, (iv) mixing in a solvent the carbohydrate component(s) and nitrogen-containing component(s), and optionally cooling at room temperature, and (v) bubbling carbon dioxide into the mixture; or (i') providing greater than or equal to 1 carbohydrate component, (ii') providing greater than or equal to 1 nitrogen-containing component, (iii') providing carbon dioxide, (iv') reacting the carbon dioxide with the nitrogen-containing component, and (v') mixing in a solvent the carbohydrate component(s) and reaction product of (iv');
   (2) use of carbon dioxide to stabilize a binder composition based on greater than or equal to 1 carbohydrate component and greater than or equal to 1 nitrogen-containing component;
   (3) manufacturing a collection of matter bound by a polymeric binder which involves (i) providing a collection of matter, (ii) providing a binder composition in a solvent to obtain a solution or dispersion, (iii) applying the solution or dispersion to the collection of matter, and (iv) applying energy to the collection of matter containing the solution or dispersion to cure the binder composition; and
   (4) binder solution or dispersion comprising binder composition and crosslinker in solvent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Parameters: The viscosity of an aqueous solution containing 70 wt.% binder composition does not increase greater than or equal to 500 cP when left to stand at 20 degrees C for 12 hours. Preferred Components: The binder composition is capable of reacting with a crosslinker to yield greater than or equal to 1 melanoidins as water-insoluble composition, under relevant curing conditions. The ratio of total carbonyl groups in carbohydrate component(s) to total reactive nitrogen-containing groups in the nitrogen-containing component(s) is 5:1-1:5. The carbohydrate component is selected from monosaccharides, disaccharides, polysaccharides or reaction product. Carbohydrate component is selected from reducing sugars, ribose, arabinose, xylose, lyxose, glucose (dextrose), mannose, galactose, allose, altrose, talose, gulose, idose, fructose, psicose, sorbose, dihydroxyacetone, sucrose and/or tagatose. The nitrogen-containing component is ammonia, inorganic amine or organic amine comprising greater than or equal to 1 prim.-amine group, as well as salts. The nitrogen-containing component is polyamine, preferably a primary polyamine having the formula H2N-Q-NH2, where Q is alkyl, cycloalkyl, heteroalkyl, cycloheteroalkyl, or benzyl, preferably a 6 carbon-containing (6C) alkyl or cyclohexyl, cyclopentyl or cyclobutyl, or benzyl, each of which is optionally substituted with amino, aminoalkyl, hydroxyl, halo, or thiol, where alkyl is selected from 2-24C alkyl, 2-9C alkyl, and 3-7C alkyl. The weight ratio between carbohydrate component and carbon dioxide reaction product with nitrogen-containing component is 0.5:1-30:1. Amount of carbon dioxide (CO2) that is bubbled into the mixture is 0.5-50 wt.%, preferably 1-2 wt.%.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

TI Extracting carbon dioxide from metal carbonate for            e.g. preventing spoilage of foods, involves using            reactor to produce acid, releasing carbon dioxide by            dissolution of metal carbonate in acid, and using            carbon dioxide to improve health, grow rate and yield            of organism.
AU ELLIS L D
   CHIANG Y
   CHIANG M L
AE SUBLIME SYSTEMS INC (SUBL-Non-standard)
GA 2022165545
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting carbon dioxide from metal                carbonates (105) involves using a reactor (102) to                produce at least an acid (103), releasing carbon                dioxide by dissolution of the metal carbonate in                the acid, and using the carbon dioxide to improve a                health, a grow rate, and/or a yield of an                organism.
   USE - Extracting carbon dioxide from metal carbonate                e.g. calcium carbonate for improving health, grow                rate and yield of organism e.g. animal,                photosynthetic bacteria and plant, and as inert gas                for chemical processes, for welding, as lasing                medium, for preventing spoilage of foods and other                air-sensitive materials, to extinguish fires, to                dilute flammable and toxic vapours, as non-reactive                cooling gas, as toxic gas to terminate and subdue                animals, as medical gas, as propellant, as food                additive, as reagent, as component for producing                building materials, as pest control mechanism, as                algae growth promotor, as coral growth promotor, as                oil recovery pressurizing and flow agent, as                cleaning agent, as solvent and as refrigerant (all                claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The extracted carbon dioxide kills pests and                rodents, baits and traps insects such as mosquitos                and bedbugs, stimulates breathing after apnea and                stabilize oxygen/carbon dioxide ratios in blood,                prevents spoilage of food, promotes curing of                cementitious materials through carbonation of                minerals in the cement paste, eliminates pests such                as whiteflies and spidermites, promotes the growth                of algaes, promotes the growth of corals, and                reduces the oil's viscosity and allows to flow more                rapidly through pipes.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) apparatus (101), which comprises a reactor                that is configured to produce at least an acid, a                subreactor (104) configured for the dissolution of                a metal carbonate in the acid, and a unit for                collecting carbon dioxide produced upon dissolution                of the metal carbonate in the acid; and
   (2) system, which comprises a renewable                electricity source, a reactor, and a facility                containing living organisms.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the                system.
   Apparatus (101)
   Reactor (102)
   Acid (103)
   Subreactor (104)
   Metal carbonate (105)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Components: The reactor is an                  electrochemical reactor. The subreactor is a                  region within the reactor. The subreactor is                  separated from the reactor. The apparatus further                  comprises an apparatus for storing the acid, a                  unit for compressing the carbon dioxide, and a                  unit for storing the carbon dioxide. The                  subreactor comprises a pressure vessel configured                  to operate such that the carbon dioxide is                  produced under a pressure is more r than 1 atm.                  The facility is used for farming or biofuel                  production. The facility comprises an indoor                  farm, a greenhouse, or a biofuel production                  facility. The reactor is powered at least in                  portion by the renewable electricity source and                  the reactor produces an acid. The system further                  comprises a unit for dissolving a metal carbonate                  in the acid thereby releasing carbon dioxide, and                  a unit for collecting the carbon dioxide. The                  unit for dissolving the metal carbonate in the                  acid comprises a pressure vessel configured. The                  system is configured to produce carbon dioxide at                  least in portion by the electrochemical reactor                  being at least partially powered by the renewable                  electricity source and the system is configured                  to provide the carbon dioxide to the facility.                  The facility is a medical care facility. The                  system comprises a reactor configured to produce                  at least an acid, a device (D2) configured to                  produce carbon dioxide at least in portion from                  dissolution of a metal carbonate in the acid, a                  collection device configured to collect the                  produced CO2 produced upon dissolution of the                  metal carbonate in the acid and to provide the                  produced carbon dioxide to a storage system                  and/or operating system, an electrochemical                  device configured to electrochemically dissolve a                  metal carbonate to release carbon dioxide, a                  collection device configured to collect the                  released carbon dioxide and provide the collected                  carbon dioxide to a storage system and/or                  operating system, and a renewable power source                  providing power to the electrochemical reactor.                  The storage system and/or operating system is                  configured to enable the produced carbon dioxide                  to be applied to, or used by, another system. The                  device (D2) is included in the electrochemical                  reactor or is separate from the electrochemical                  reactor. The device (D2) comprises a pressure                  vessel pressurized such that the released carbon                  dioxide is released into the interior of the                  pressure vessel under a pressure greater than a                  pressure outside the pressure vessel. The reactor                  is a subreactor. The electrochemical device                  comprises an electrolyzer which is configured to                  electrochemically generate an acid to dissolve                  the metal carbonate located in the electrolyzer,                  and a pressure vessel enclosing the metal                  carbonate located in the electrolyzer and                  configured such that a pressure under which the                  metal carbonate is dissolved is greater within                  the pressure vessel than a pressure outside the                  pressure vessel. The electrolyzer comprises a                  water tank, two electrodes located in the water                  tank and a current or voltage source. The water                  tank is configured to hold water and the metal                  carbonate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The method further                  involves using the algae for food to produce a                  biofuel, using the plant to produce a biofuel,                  powering the electrochemical reactor by                  electricity from a renewable resource, storing                  the acid and/or the carbon dioxide. The method                  involves electrochemically dissolving the metal                  carbonate using the acid to release carbon                  dioxide, using the released carbon dioxide to                  improve health, growth rate and/or yield of the                  organism. The step of electrochemically                  dissolving the metal carbonate to release carbon                  dioxide involves electrochemically generating an                  acid to dissolve the metal carbonate. The step of                  electrochemically generating the acid involves                  electrolyzing water to generate the acid                  comprising hydrogen ions. The method further                  involves electrochemically generating hydroxide                  ions and reacting the hydroxide ions with the                  metal ions to form a metal hydroxide solid.                  Preferred Property: The dissolution of the metal                  carbonate in the acid occurs under pressure is                  more than 1 atm. Preferred Components: The                  renewable resource is solar or wind energy. The                  carbon dioxide is supplied to the facility. The                  metal carbonate is portion of a material                  containing metal carbonate. The material                  containing metal carbonate is a natural material,                  synthesized material or waste material. The other                  system is an agricultural system or aquaculture                  system. The organism comprises an agricultural                  product comprising food for humans or animals.                  The organism is a plant comprising algae. The                  metal carbonate is dissolved under a pressure of                  more than 1 atm. The hydrogen ions react with the                  metal carbonate to generate metal ions and the                  released carbon dioxide. The metal hydroxide                  solid comprises calcium hydroxide solid.

TI Supercritical carbon dioxide extraction method of active ingredient of selenium-enriched full Ganoderma lucidum spore oil comprises e.g. braking and crushing selenium-enriched Ganoderma lucidum fruit body.
AU QIAN L
AE QIAN L (QIAN-Individual)
GA 2020C4170M
AB    NOVELTY - Supercritical carbon dioxide extraction method of active ingredient of selenium-enriched full Ganoderma lucidum spore oil comprises e.g. (i) taking selenium-enriched Ganoderma lucidum fruit body and the selenium-enriched Ganoderma lucidum spores, braking and crushing, and they are infiltrating in edible ethanol for 12-36 hours, (ii) placing the infiltrated selenium-enriched Ganoderma lucidum fruit body and the selenium-enriched Ganoderma lucidum spore mixture into an extraction kettle for extracting, (iii) passing carbon dioxide in a supercritical state into the extraction kettle, controlling the extraction temperature at 30-45 degrees C, and pressure in the extraction kettle at 20-45 Mpa, and extracting for 2-5 hours, (iv) introducing selenium-enriched whole Ganoderma lucidum spore extract containing carbon dioxide fluid flowing out of the extraction kettle into the separation kettle for separating until the carbon dioxide.
   USE - The method is useful for supercritical carbon dioxide extraction of active ingredient of selenium-enriched full Ganoderma lucidum spore oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has good medicinal and nutritional value.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Supercritical carbon dioxide extraction method of active ingredient of selenium-enriched full Ganoderma lucidum spore oil comprises (i) taking selenium-enriched Ganoderma lucidum fruit body and the selenium-enriched Ganoderma lucidum spores, broking and crushing, and they are infiltrating in edible ethanol for 12-36 hours, (ii) placing the infiltrated selenium-enriched Ganoderma lucidum fruit body and the selenium-enriched Ganoderma lucidum spore mixture into an extraction kettle for extracting, (iii) passing carbon dioxide in a supercritical state into the extraction kettle, controlling the extraction temperature at 30-45 degrees C, and pressure in the extraction kettle at 20-45 Mpa, and extracting for 2-5 hours, (iv) introducing selenium-enriched whole Ganoderma lucidum spore extract containing carbon dioxide fluid flowing out of the extraction kettle into the separation kettle for separating until the carbon dioxide after decompression and cooling flows out from the upper part of the separation kettle, and becomes oil to obtain selenium-enriched whole Ganoderma lucidum spore extract in the paste state flows out from the lower part of the separation kettle.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (ii), placing the mixture of selenium-enriched Ganoderma lucidum fruiting bodies and selenium-enriched Ganoderma lucidum spores into the extraction kettle, adding carrier agent to the extraction kettle, where the concentration of the carrier agent is 45-95% of edible ethanol, and the weight ratio of the broken wall particles to the carrier agent is 1:0.3-1. In the step (i), breaking and crushing the selenium-enriched Ganoderma lucidum fruit body and selenium-enriched Ganoderma lucidum spores to 150-400 meshes. Preferred Components: In the step (ii), the selenium-enriched Ganoderma lucidum fruit body and the selenium-enriched Ganoderma lucidum spores are mixed in a weight ratio of 1-25:5.

AU HU Y
   FANG D

TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The step heat exchange is                  a three-stage low-temperature heat exchange. In                  the step (vi), the temperature of the product gas                  after the first-stage low-temperature heat                  exchange is -40 to -50 &#176;C, the                  temperature after secondary low temperature heat                  exchange is -80 to -90&#8451;, the C3+                  component is obtained, and the temperature after                  the three-stage low-temperature heat exchange is                  -100 to -120&#8451;, and the C2 component is                  obtained. The temperature of the                  methane after the secondary low temperature heat                  exchange is -90&#8451; to -100&#8451;,                  and the temperature after the primary low                  temperature heat exchange is -45 to                  -55&#8451;. The methane is sequentially                  subjected to the medium temperature heat                  exchange, the secondary high temperature heat                  exchange and the first high temperature heat                  exchange, and then mixed with the oxygen as a raw                  material gas, the temperature is raised to                  240-270&#8451; after the medium-temperature                  heat exchange, the temperature is raised to                  330-540&#8451; after the secondary high                  temperature heat exchange, and the temperature is                  raised to 710-850&#8451; after the first high                  temperature heat exchange. In the step (ii), the                  feed volume ratio of the methane to the oxygen is                  2-4, and the pressure is 3-5 MPa and the                  temperature of the alkoxylation coupling reaction                  is 700-900&#8451;. In the step (iii), the                  temperature of the catalytic cracking to olefin                  reaction is 600-800&#8451;, and the pressure                  is 3-5 MPa. In the step (iv), the carbon monoxide                  shift reaction includes a carbon monoxide high                  temperature shift reaction and a carbon monoxide                  low temperature shift reaction performed in                  sequence, the temperature of the carbon monoxide                  high-temperature shift reaction is                  300-500&#8451;, and the pressure is 2-4 MPa,                  and the temperature of the carbon monoxide                  low-temperature shift reaction is                  180-250&#8451;, and the pressure is 2-4                  MPa.

PT P
PN US2021367226-A1; US11450845-B2
TI Electrochemical cell e.g. lithium-ion battery,            comprises two electrodes and separator which are held            together by polymer adhesive on separator to maintain            adhesion while portion(s) of electrochemical cell is            exposed to carbon dioxide.
AU BONHOMME F C
   PARK B Y
   ANDERSON H L
AE ENEVATE CORP (ENEV-Non-standard)
GA 2021D4900K
AB    NOVELTY - An electrochemical cell comprises an electrode                (A), an electrode (B), a separator between the                electrodes (A) and (B), an electrolyte in a cell                container, and a solid electrolyte interphase (SEI)                layer formed (130) on the electrode (A) by exposing                (120) at least a portion of the electrochemical                cell to carbon dioxide. The electrode (A) comprises                silicon-dominant electrochemically active material                comprising greater than 50 wt.% silicon. The                electrode (A) is a self-supported film. The                electrodes (A) and (B), and the separator are held                together with a polymer adhesive on the separator                to maintain an adhesion between the electrode (A),                the electrode (B) and separator while at least a                portion of the electrochemical cell is exposed to                carbon dioxide. The SEI layer has a thickness of                1-20 nm.
   USE - Electrochemical cell e.g. lithium-ion                battery.
   ADVANTAGE - The cell has increased cycle life, energy                density, improved safety, low cost, decreased                electrolyte consumption, and more rapid capacity                loss upon cycle compared to batteries containing                graphite anodes. The SEI layer has increased                stability to improve capacity retention and to                reduce fast fading.

The electrode (B) comprises                  nickel-cobalt-aluminum (NCA), lithium-cobalt                  oxide (LCO), lithium-manganese oxide (LMO) or                  nickel-manganese-cobalt (NMC). The electrode (A)                  is a silicon-composite electrochemically active                  film.Preferred Composition: The silicon-dominant                  electrochemically active material preferably                  comprises 70-100 wt.% silicon. PN US2021356680-A1
TI Sequestering carbon dioxide (CO2) involves contacting CO2 containing gaseous stream and aqueous medium to obtain bicarbonate rich product, combining with cation source to obtain solid carbonate composition and product CO2 gas, and injecting gas in subsurface geological location to sequester CO2.
AU BEWERNITZ M A
   CONSTANTZ B R
AE BLUE PLANET SYSTEMS CORP (BLUE-Non-standard)
GA 2021D6906X
AB    NOVELTY - Sequestering (M1) carbon dioxide (CO2), comprises contacting CO2 containing gaseous stream with aqueous medium under conditions sufficient to produce bicarbonate rich product, combining the bicarbonate rich product or component with cation source under conditions sufficient to produce solid carbonate composition and product CO2 gas, and injecting the product CO2 gas into a subsurface geological location to sequester CO2.
   USE - The method is useful for sequestering carbon dioxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) system for sequestering CO2, comprising a source of the CO2 containing gas, a source of aqueous medium, a reactor configured to contact the CO2 containing gas with the aqueous medium under conditions sufficient to produce bicarbonate rich product, and produce a solid carbonate composition and a product CO2 gas from the bicarbonate rich product, and an injector used to inject the product CO2 gas in a subsurface geological location; and
   (2) removing (M2) non-CO2 pollutant from multicomponent gaseous stream, comprising contacting the multicomponent gaseous stream with aqueous medium under conditions sufficient to produce bicarbonate rich product, and subjecting the bircarbonate rich product to carbonate precipitation conditions to remove non-CO2 pollutant from the multicomponent gaseous stream.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the method (M1): the aqueous medium is bicarbonate buffered aqueous medium; the bicarbonate buffered aqueous medium has a pH of 8-10; the CO2 containing gaseous stream is multicomponent gaseous stream; the pCO2 of the multicomponent gaseous stream is higherthan 104 Pa; the bicarbonate rich product comprises droplets of a liquid condensed phase (LCP) in a bulk liquid; the concentration of bicarbonate anions in the LCP droplets is higher than 10000 ppm; the molar ratio of bicarbonate to carbonate anion in the LCP droplets is 1-10; the cation source is source of divalent cations; the divalent cations are alkaline earth metal cations; the divalent alkaline earth metal cations are group consisting of Ca2+ and/or Mg2+; the product CO2 gas comprises substantially pure CO2; the subsurface geological location is a subterranean location; the subterranean location is a fossil fuel reservoir, saline reservoir or a deep ocean location; the commodity is a building material; the building material is aggregate or cement or supplemental cementitious material; and the CO2 containing gas is a flue gas.Preferred Method: The method (M1) further comprises: compressing the product CO2 gas prior to injecting the product CO2 gas in the subsurface geological location; and producing a commodity from the solid carbonate composition. In the method (M1): the CO2 containing gas is contacted with the aqueous medium in presence of LCP promoter, catalyst that mediates conversion of CO2 to bicarbonate; the solid carbonate composition is produced without use of alkalinity source; the method produces mole of purified CO2 for every two moles of CO2 removed from the gaseous stream; and the CO2 containing gas is obtained from industrial plant. In the method (M2), the non-CO2 pollutant is nitrogen oxide (NOx), sulfur oxide (SOx), volatile organic compounds, heavy metals and/or particulate matter.
   EXAMPLE - None given.
DC J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. AU KOBAYASHI K

   JAPAN GORE TEX INC (NIGO-C)
   GORE & ASSOC GK W L (GORE-C)
GA 202142678S
AB    NOVELTY - The battery (1) comprises a housing (2) having                an opening. A positive electrode and a negative                electrode are partially disposed within the                housing, respectively. An electrolyte is disposed                between the positive electrode and the negative                electrode. The electrolyte is configured to release                gas during operation of the battery, in which the                gas is chosen from carbon dioxide, hydrogen, carbon                monoxide, or any combinations. A fluoropolymer                membrane covers the opening of the housing. The                fluoropolymer membrane has a crystallinity of 85%                to 100%. The fluoropolymer membrane has a density                of 2.0-2.2 gram per cubic centimeter. The                fluoropolymer membrane has a carbon dioxide                permeability to moisture permeability ratio of more                than 0.5.

The biological cathode is tightly filled conductive carbon particles as three-dimensional conductive biological carrier. The surface of carrier is enriched with electro-active methane biological film. The cathode liquid is mixed solution containing inorganic carbon source, nitrogen source, mineral substance, vitamin and buffer solution. The anode liquid is waste water containing antibiotic.

   ADVANTAGE - The system realizes fast collecting active methane-producing biological film and obtain high methane yield, and reduces over-potential of cathode reaction to reduce adverse effect of antibiotic emission to environment and realize upgrading of marsh gas.

TI Measuring method for measuring total organiccarbon in water comprises e.g. obtaining sample, acidifying sample, removing carbon dioxide in sample and introducing aeration gas into gas collecting vessel using ultraviolet spectroscopy.
AU XU X
   WANG B
   ZHANG Y
   ZHANG W
AE UNIV NANKAI (UNAN-C)
GA 2020B48739
AB    NOVELTY - Measuring method for measuring total organiccarbon (TOC) in water comprises (i) manufacturing the organic carbon calibration curve; (ii) obtaining the sample, and acidifying the sample, e.g. inorganic carbon in the sample converted into carbon dioxide; (iii) removing the carbon dioxide in the sample by sweeping or aeration; (iv) adding the sample to remove the inorganic carbon into the high temperature combustion tube, measuring by the non-dispersed infrared absorption method, checking the measured value on the organic carbon calibration curve to obtain the corresponding concentration value and recording as a; and (v) introducing the aeration gas into the gas collecting vessel, using ultraviolet spectroscopy to detect the volatile organic matter in the vessel, where the ultraviolet spectrum method is for detecting the volatile organic matter in the vessel and a first reflecting grating is focused on thesecond reflecting grating.
   USE - The method is useful for measuring total organiccarbon in water.

The                denitrifying sulfide removal granules contain                autotrophic denitrifying bacteria and heterotrophic                denitrifying bacteria.
   USE - The method is useful for treating                wastewater.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: removes at least 80% nitrate and                65% sulfide from the wastewater; is carried out in                an upflow anaerobic sludge bed reactor, which                provide good contact between microbes and                substrates, reduces resistance to mass transfer,                minimizes build-up of inhibitory intermediates,                stabilizes environmental conditions and improves                gas production; enables formation of mature                granules that provide a strong and compact                structure, good settling ability, high                concentration of microbes, high ability to retain                biomass in reactor and well withstand against shock                and toxic loadings; and shows the presence of                sulfide that prefers dissimilatory nitrate                reduction to ammonium pathway for nitrate reduction                instead of denitrification pathway for nitrogen gas                production, hence minimizes inhibitory effects on                methanogenesis.

PN CN110699180-A
TI Preparing gloden sunflower essence used as a tobacco fragrance in cigarettes involves taking fresh golden sunflower with complete epidermis, performing dehydration and pulverization, dissolving sunflower oil component in supercritical fluid.
AU JIANG M
   LI X
   GONG X
   WU C
   ZHANG B
   ZHENG L
   PU X
   WU M
   HUANG Z
AE CHANGDE HUAFU BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (CHAN-Non-standard)
GA 2020083964
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing gloden sunflower essence involves taking fresh golden sunflower with complete epidermis and no insects, removing impurities, washing, and draining, and performing dehydration and pulverization of cleaned golden sunflower. The crushed golden sunflower is placed in the barrel of extraction kettle at certain temperature, carbon dioxide gas cylinder is opened, and system is pressurized by a high pressure pump. The entrainer pump is also opened at same time, the entrainer is driven accurately and quantitatively, so that it becomes a mixed fluid with carbon dioxide. The temperature and pressure in the extraction kettle are maintained in the supercritical state of mixed fluid of carbon dioxide and entrainer and allowed to make full contact with material of golden sunflower for a certain period of time. The sunflower oil component is dissolved in supercritical fluid, and the fluid containing sunflower oil is collected from outlet of separation kettle to obtain the desired product.
   USE - Method for preparing gloden sunflower essence used as tobacco essence in cigarettes (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and the equipment used is simple that making the entire preparation process simple and easy to operate. The raw materials used in the preparation process are readily available and inexpensive, which makes the preparation cost lower and facilitates popularization and application.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The dehydration treatment is performed at 60-70 degrees C. The dehydration treatment includes an air-drying treatment that involves uniformly spreading the fresh sunflower seeds to the shade, air-drying in a ventilated place until the surface of the sunflower is dehydrated, and then drying in the sun until the water content of the dried sunflower is 10-12 wt.%. The dehydration treatment also includes a vacuum drying treatment at temperature of 60 plus minus 2 degrees C, a drying vacuum degree of -0.08 plus minus 0.01 megapascal. The pulverization is performed through -20-40 mesh sieve. The supercritical treatment is performed at 35-55 degrees C under pressure of 15-35 megapascal. The flow of carbon dioxide gas through the extraction kettle is 5-15 L/hour, and the extraction is performed in a supercritical state of a mixed fluid of carbon dioxide and an entrainer. The mass ratio of dried Sunflower sunflower powder and entrainer is 1: 0.001-0.1, and the extraction is performed for 1-4 hour. The entrainer is 95% ethanol, propylene glycol, or their mixture.

PN US2019329181-A1
TI Performing aftertreatment catalysis involves exposing engine exhaust to an aftertreatment catalyst system, which comprises catalyst support structure having surface region comprises criteria-pollutant-treating catalyst.
AU RAPPE K G
   LI X S
AE BATTELLE MEMORIAL INST (BATT-C)
GA 2019900731
AB    NOVELTY - Performing aftertreatment catalysis involves exposing engine exhaust to an aftertreatment catalyst system, which comprises catalyst support structure having surface region comprises criteria-pollutant-treating catalyst and a sorbent comprises a eutectic promotor, where sorbent is based on magnesium oxide, on a magnesium oxide sodium carbonate double salt, or on dolomite, and exothermically adsorbing carbon dioxide from the exhaust to the sorbent at a first temperature less than a light-off temperature of the criteria-pollutant-treating catalyst, and increasing temperature at the criteria-pollutant-treating catalyst to a value greater than or equal to the light-off temperature and desorbing carbon dioxide from the sorbent at a second temperature greater than the light-off temperature of the criteria-pollutant-treating catalyst.
   USE - Method for performing aftertreatment catalysis.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to perform aftertreatment catalysis that has catalyst activity, and higher rate of carbon dioxide adsorption.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a aftertreatment catalyst for engine exhaust, which comprises catalyst support structure having a surface region; and
   (2) a method for treating pollutants at low temperature, which involves applying to a surface of a catalyst support structure a criteria-pollutant-treating catalyst and magnesium oxide, on a magnesium oxide sodium carbonate double salt or a dolomite-based sorbent.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The sorbent is at a first layer on a surface of the support structure and the catalyst is at a second layer on the first layer, and where adsorbing comprises adsorbing carbon dioxide to the first layer and increasing temperature comprises transferring heat from the first layer to the second layer. The eutectic promotor has a melting temperature that is less than or equal to 150 degrees C. The ratio of sorbent and the criteria-pollutant-treating catalyst is 1:2 and 4:1. Preferred Process: The method further comprises melting the eutectic promotor, and facilitating adsorbing carbon dioxide. The method involves first applying the sorbent or the catalyst as a first layer on the catalyst support structure and subsequently applying the catalyst or the sorbent, respectively, as a second layer on the first layer. Preferred Components: The eutectic promotor comprises a mixture of two salts which are selected from sodium nitrate, lithium nitrate, potassium nitrate, calcium nitrate, and magnesium nitrate.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC J04 (Chemical/physical processes/apparatus - including catalysis, catalysts (excluding specific e.g. enzymatic or polymerisation catalysts), colloid chemistry, laboratory apparatus and methods, testing, controlling, general encapsulation, detection and sampling (excluding clinical testing) (B01J, L).); Q51 (Internal combustion engines, reciprocating engines, rotary engines (F01, F02B,D,F,G,M,N,P))

PT P
PN CN110172711-A
TI Copper-based three-dimensional self-supporting            electrocatalytic material for electrocatalytic carbon            dioxide reduction is obtained by electrochemically            immobilizing copper/copper(II) oxide catalyst            nanoparticles on carbon material substrate.
AU MA T
   SUN Y
   DENG Z
   LI H
   HUANG Z
   ZHAO Q
   FENG D
   LIU J
AE UNIV LIAONING (UNLN-C)
GA 201975258C
AB    NOVELTY - Copper-based three-dimensional self-supporting                electrocatalytic material is obtained by                electrochemically immobilizing copper/copper(II)                oxide catalyst nanoparticles on a carbon material                substrate.
   USE - Copper-based three-dimensional self-supporting                electrocatalytic material for electrocatalytic                carbon dioxide reduction (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The copper-based three-dimensional                self-supporting electrocatalytic material is                structurally stable, does not require a binder and                a carrier, preparation is simple, the repeatability                is good, and the use of a large amount of                electrolyte containing copper salt is avoided, so                environmental protection and easy large-scale                preparation have broad application prospects in the                field of electrocatalytic carbon dioxide                reduction.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for preparing a copper-based three-dimensional                self-supporting electrocatalytic material, which                involves:
   (A) placing carbon material substrate in a                cage made of copper wire as a working electrode,                using silver/silver chloride as a reference                electrode, and using carbon rod as a counter                electrode;
   (B) taking three-electrode system with 0.1 M                KOH as the electrolyte, performing constant voltage                scan at -0.2 V for 2-4 h; and
   (C) taking out carbon material substrate on                which the Cu/copper(II) oxide catalyst                nanoparticles are immobilized, washing with water,                and drying to obtain a copper-based                three-dimensional self-supporting electrocatalytic                material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - METALLURGY - Preferred Condition: The carbon material                  substrate is carbon cloth, carbon paper, and                  graphene aerogel. The cage made of the copper                  wire is a cage woven from copper having a                  diameter of 0.5-1.0 mm. The carbon material                  substrate is graphene aerogel GA, and the                  obtained copper-based three-dimensional                  self-supporting electrocatalytic material is                  copper/copper(II) oxide@GA and graphene aerogel                  GA is prepared by taking graphene oxide GO                  aqueous solution, adding ethylenediamine and                  sodium borate solution, stirring well, and                  sonicating for 10 min. The reaction is carried                  out in a reaction vessel at 120 degrees C for 14                  h, and the obtained product is washed with an                  ethanol solution, frozen at -10 degrees C, and                  dried at room temperature to obtain a graphene                  aerogel GA. The graphene oxide GO is prepared by                  adding high-purity graphite powder and sodium                  nitrate successively in concentrated sulfuric                  acid, and the mixture is stirred. The potassium                  permanganate is added in portions at a                  temperature below 20 degrees C, and the addition                  is completed in 50-70 min. After the color of the                  mixture changes from purple to grass green, it is                  reacted at 40 degrees C for 0.5-1 h, and the                  mixture becomes brown paste. The heating is                  stopped, and the mixture is allowed to stand at                  room temperature for 72 hours, and the obtained                  product is slowly poured into hot water of 75                  degrees C. After stirring uniformly, 30% of                  hydrogen peroxide is added dropwise until the                  mixture turned bright yellow, centrifuged while                  hot, centrifuged with 50 degrees C distilled                  water, neutralizing excess acid to neutral with                  5% sodium carbonate solution. The distilled water                  is centrifuged until the supernatant is free of                  sulfate, and dried at 60 degrees C to obtain                  graphene oxide GO.
DC E34 (Compounds of Li, Na, K, Rb, Cs, Fr.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).); M11 (Electroplating; electrolytic treatment of or with metals - including electro-deposition of metals, electro-plating apparatus, electro-forming, electro-erosion, spark erosion, anodising (electrophoretically) coating metals and electrolytic cleaning and polishing (C25).)
MC E11-Q02C; E31-N05C; E35-A; J03-B; M11-A03; M11-F; N07-G

PN EP3517178-A1; WO2019145372-A1; WO2019145372-A9; AU2019211008-A1; CN111629790-A; KR2020116119-A; IN202017027845-A; EP3743164-A1; AR114314-A1; BR112020010919-A2; US2020397672-A1; JP2021510686-W; RU2020128172-A; US11426334-B2
TI Use of surface-reacted calcium carbonate in            cosmetic and/or skin care composition as agent for            modifying biomechanical properties of skin, formed by            reacting natural ground or precipitated calcium            carbonate with carbon dioxide and hydronium.
AU BUDDE T
   HECKER A
AE OMYA INT AG (OMYA-C)
   OMYA INT AG (OMYA-C)
   OMYA INT AG (OMYA-C)
GA 2019664291
AB    NOVELTY - Use of surface-reacted calcium carbonate in                cosmetic and/or skin care composition is claimed as                an agent for modifying biomechanical properties of                the skin, where surface-reacted calcium carbonate                has volume median particle size (d50) of 0.1-90 mu                m, and is a reaction product of natural ground                calcium carbonate or precipitated calcium carbonate                with carbon dioxide and greater than or equal to 1                hydronium (H3O+) ion donors, and where carbon                dioxide is formed in situ by H3O+ ion donors                treatment and/or is supplied from an external                source.
   USE - Surface-reacted calcium carbonate for use in                cosmetic and/or skin care composition as an agent                for modifying biomechanical properties of the skin,                i.e. skin firmness, preferably to an increased                firmness of skin, preferably skin of the face                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - Surface-reacted calcium carbonate does not                lead to negative side-effects, and preferably does                not lead to skin irritation, after application on                the skin. The surface-reacted calcium carbonate                increases the skin firmness due to its capability                to mineralize the skin, especially in the form of                calcium ions. Such mineralization may induce the                biosynthesis of collagen fibers, elastins, and/or                glycosaminoglycans, strengthening the extracellular                matrix of the epidermis and/or dermis.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Properties: Surface-reacted                  calcium carbonate has d50 of 0.5-50 mu m,                  preferably 1.5-15 mu m. Surface-reacted calcium                  carbonate has specific surface area of 15-200                  m2/g, preferably 25-160 m2/g, measured using                  nitrogen and Brunauer-Emmett-Teller (BET) method.                  Cosmetic and/or skin care composition has pH of                  8.5, preferably 4-7. Preferred Compositions:                  Natural ground calcium carbonate is selected from                  marble, chalk, and/or limestone, or precipitated                  calcium carbonate is selected from precipitated                  calcium carbonates having aragonitic, vateritic,                  and/or calcitic crystal form. At least one H3O+                  ion donor is selected from hydrochloric acid,                  sulfuric acid, sulfurous acid, phosphoric acid,                  citric acid, oxalic acid, acidic salt, acetic                  acid, and/or formic acid, preferably hydrochloric                  acid, sulfuric acid, sulfurous acid, phosphoric                  acid, oxalic acid, dihydrogen phosphate (H2PO4-),                  being at least partially neutralized by cation                  selected from lithium (Li+), sodium (Na+), and/or                  potassium (K+), hydrogen phosphate (HPO42-),                  being at least partially neutralized by cation                  selected from Li+, Na+, K+, Mg2+, and/or Ca2+,                  most preferably phosphoric acid. Composition                  contains 0.1-50 wt.%, preferably 3-10 wt.%                  surface-reacted calcium carbonate based on total                  weight of the composition.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: Composition further                  comprises water and/or greater than or equal to 1                  oil selected from vegetable oils and their                  esters, alkane coconut ester, plant extracts,                  animal fats, siloxanes, silicones, fatty acids                  and their esters, petrolatum, glycerides, and/or                  their PEGylated derivatives. Composition                  comprises greater than or equal to 1 active agent                  being adsorbed onto and/or absorbed into the                  surface of the surface-reacted calcium carbonate.                  Active agent is selected from pharmaceutically                  active agents, biologically active agents,                  vitamins, disinfecting agents, preservatives,                  flavoring agents, surfactants, oils, fragrances,                  and/or essential oils. Composition further                  comprises greater than or equal to 1 additive                  selected from bleaching agents, thickeners,                  stabilizers, chelating agents, preserving agents,                  wetting agents, emulsifiers, emollients,                  fragrances, colorants, skin tanning compounds,                  antioxidants, minerals, pigments, UV-A, and/or                  UV-B filter. Composition is selected from eye                  make-up product, facial make-up product, lip care                  product, hand care product, and/or skin care                  product.
   ACTIVITY - Dermatological. Test details are described but                no results given.
   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.

PT P
PN CN208991973-U
TI Carbon dioxide laser marking machine comprises machine body, working table, mounting plate provided with lifting hole, supporting column, rotating arm, servo motor, output shaft, and connecting rope.
AU JIANG Q
AE HAIXIANG FUJIAN ENVIRONMENTAL TECHNOLOGY (HAIX-Non-standard)
GA 201955929B
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims marking equipment technology field, it claims a carbon dioxide laser marking machine, comprising a machine body, a workbench, a laser device main body, the bottom of the machine body is provided with a mounting plate; the mounting plate is provided with four lifting hole distributed at intervals uniformly along the periphery of the mounting plate, the lifting hole is provided with a lower end for supporting the post on the ground, the supporting column is provided with a end is propped against lower side on the side wall of the mounting plate and the other end of rotating arm is hinged on the supporting pole. the supporting column is provided with a servo motor, the servo motor is provided with one end fixed on the output shaft of the servo motor and the other end is fixed on the rotating arm far away from the supporting post of one end of the connecting rope. by turning arm drives the machine body to lift, the final can height according to the height of the operator better adjust the worktable is located, which makes the operators with different heights is convenient while operating the laser marking machine for carbon dioxide.
DC V06 (Electromechanical Transducers and Small Machines); X24 (Electric Welding)
MC V06-M06E; V06-M10; X24-D03

PT P
PN CN208887146-U
TI Carbon dioxide heat pump refrigerating system, has temperature sensor fixed on heat exchanger and ice water machine, pressure sensor and temperature sensor connected with controlling system.
AU LI X
AE ZHEJIANG DEYAO NEW ENERGY CO LTD (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 201948526Q
AB    NOVELTY - The utility model claims a carbon dioxide heat pump refrigerant system, comprising orderly connected compressor loop by pipeline, a heat exchanger, an expansion assembly, ice water and a gas-liquid separator, between the expansion assembly and the heat exchanger is provided with a regenerator, the air outlet pipe of the compressor a two connected with the inlet pipe of the heat exchanger inner pipe of air outlet pipe to the regenerator heat exchanger inlet connection, connecting the outlet and the expansion assembly of the inner tube of the heat regenerator, expansion assembly connecting with the inlet of the ice water machine far away one end regenerator. ice water outlet is connected with the inlet of the gas-liquid separator connected, connected outer tube inlet of the gas-liquid separator outlet is connected with the heat regenerator, the air inlet pipe of the outer outlet of the regenerator with the compressor connection; further comprising a control system. temperature sensor is set on the heat exchanger and the ice water and a compressor air inlet pipe and air outlet pipe of the pressure sensor, the pressure sensor and the temperature sensor are connected with the control system; the carbon dioxide heat pump refrigeration system heat energy using efficiency, and can effectively reduce the energy consumption.
DC Q75 (Refrigeration, liquefaction (F25)); X27 (Domestic Electric Appliances\n)
MC Q75-A02A; Q75-E; Q75-T01; Q75-T20; X27-F02B; X27-F02C1; X27-F02C2
