ER

PT P
PN US2019135726-A1; US10647652-B2
TI Making organic acid having at least three carbon            atoms involves reacting formed carbon monoxide with            unsaturated hydrocarbon in presence of catalyst having            multiatom palladium clusters supported on e.g.            graphite, strong acid and phosphine.
AU MASEL R I
   NI Z R
   CHEN Q
   ROSEN B A
AE DIOXIDE MATERIALS INC (DIOX-Non-standard)
GA 201940183W
AB    NOVELTY - Producing organic acid having at least three                carbon atoms, comprises: forming an amount of                carbon monoxide; and reacting the amount of carbon                monoxide with an amount of an unsaturated                hydrocarbon in the presence of (a) a catalyst                comprising multiatom palladium clusters supported                on at least one of (i) a metal oxide, (ii)                activated carbon, (iii) graphite, (iv) graphene,                (v) carbon nanotubes, (vi) fullerenes, (vii) carbon                monoliths, (viii) templated carbon, and (ix)                diamond; (b) a strong acid; and (c) a                phosphine.
   USE - The process is useful for producing organic                acid having at least three carbon atoms preferably                acrylic acid and methyl acrylic acid (claimed) or                propionic acid, and producing olefins comprising                ethylene and propylene and syngas.
   ADVANTAGE - The process: converts formic acid to fuels and                chemicals without the intermediate process of                hydrogenating the formic acid to methanol or                reacting it with ammonia to form formamide; reduces                carbon dioxide present in nature or divert the                amount of carbon dioxide from being discharged into                the environment by the artificial chemical source;                and provides organic acid with high efficiency and                an improved rate and provide the environmental                benefit of effecting a net reduction in the amount                of carbon dioxide greenhouse gas in the                atmosphere.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal oxide                  comprises at least one of alumina, titania,                  zirconia, niobia, silica, magnesia, zinc oxide, a                  zeolite, and a mixture of zeolites. The catalysts                  comprise multiatom palladium clusters where the                  cluster contains more than 100 palladium atoms.                  The catalysts have a palladium dispersion less                  than 90%. The catalysts comprises palladium                  particles with a diameter greater than 1                  nm.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The process further                  comprises initially converting an amount of                  carbon dioxide obtained from a natural source or                  from an artificial chemical source to produce the                  amount of carbon monoxide, thus reducing the                  amount of carbon dioxide present in nature or                  diverting the amount of carbon dioxide from being                  discharged into the environment by the artificial                  chemical source. The reaction temperature is                  50-350 degrees C. The carbon monoxide is formed                  from formic acid, where the formic acid contacts                  an acid catalyst to convert the formic acid to                  carbon monoxide. The acid catalyst temperature is                  different than the temperature of the supported                  palladium catalyst. The acid catalyst temperature                  is at least 100 degrees C preferably at least 130                  degrees C. The converting step comprises                  electrochemically converting carbon dioxide to                  another carbon compound. Preferred Components:                  The phosphine is diphenyl-2-pyridylphosphine and                  the strong acid is trifluoromethanesulfonic acid.                  The unsaturated hydrocarbon is one of acetylene                  and methylacetylene.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.
DC A41 (Monomers and Condensants These are also included in Section E.); E17 (Other aliphatics.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J03 (Electrochemical processes and electrophoresis - including ozone production, brine electrolysis, water electrolysis, production of chemical compounds and non-metallic elements, but excluding batteries or other means of producing power and the treatment of metals (C25B).); 
MC A01-D08; E10-C04G1; E10-C04H; E10-C04K; E11-E03; E11-W; J03-B01; J04-E01; J04-E03; J04-E04; N02-F02; N06-C08; N06-F; N07-C03

PT P
PN JP2019052064-A
TI Manufacture of calcium carbonate used as filler in paper manufacture, involves adding calcium oxide to neutral-amino-acid solution, eluting calcium ion, introducing carbon dioxide gas into solution, and depositing calcium carbonate.
AU AMANO K
   FUJII S
   NAKAGAWA T
   WAKIMOTO Y
AE AISIN SEIKI KK (AISE-C)
GA 201930620F
AB    NOVELTY - Manufacture of calcium carbonate, involves adding calcium oxide to a neutral-amino-acid-containing aqueous solution obtained by mixing two or more kinds of neutral amino acids, and eluting calcium ion, introducing a gas containing carbon dioxide gas into the aqueous solution from which calcium ions are eluted, and depositing calcium carbonate.
   USE - Manufacture of calcium carbonate used as filler in paper manufacture.
   ADVANTAGE - The method improves the elution efficiency of calcium ions and precipitation efficiency of calcium carbonate. The addition of calcium oxide to neutral-amino-acid-containing aqueous solution, improves the neutral amino acid concentration and promotes the chelate reaction with respect to calcium ion. As a result, elution efficiency of calcium ion is increased. Since calcium ion elutes from calcium oxide, when contacted with carbon dioxide gas, a large amount of calcium carbonate can be deposited. Reduction in amount of neutral amino acid is suppressed by the increased precipitation efficiency, and neutral amino acid can be repeatedly used. Calcium carbonate can be manufactured with reduced power consumption by stirring for dissolving calcium oxide in water.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the calcium carbonate manufacturing apparatus.
   Reaction liquid supply unit (A)
   Reaction tank (B)
   Nozzle (D)
   Reaction liquid (W)
   Calcium carbonate manufacturing apparatus (Y)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Condition: During elution, the neutral-amino-acid-containing aqueous solution to which calcium oxide is added is stirred intermittently.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Component: One neutral amino acid is threonine. The neutral amino acids are DL-alanine, threonine, glycine, cysteine, serine, and/or ornithine hydrochloride, preferably 70% or more to less than 100% DL-alanine, threonine and glycine with respect to saturated quantity of each.
DC E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.); F09 (Paper-making production of cellulose, chemical treatment of wood - including chipboard and fibre-board (D21).)
MC E34-D03A; F05-A06D
IP C01F-011/18
PD JP2019052064-A   04 Apr 2019   C01F-011/18   201929   Pages: 17   Japanese
AD JP2019052064-A    JP177856    15 Sep 2017
PI JP177856    15 Sep 2017

PT P
PN JP6487102-B1; JP2020005583-A
TI Container-packed carbonated beverage useful for regulating intestine, suppressing raise in triglyceride and blood glucose level in blood, and lowering cholesterol, comprises indigestible dextrin, water, carbon dioxide and antifoaming agent.
AU KUBOTA H
AE ASAHI INRYO KK (ASAK-C)
GA 201925720N
AB    NOVELTY - Container-packed sugar-free carbonated beverage comprises indigestible dextrin at a concentration of 10-20 g/l, or water, carbon dioxide, antifoaming agent and indigestible dextrin, where gas volume in the sugar-free carbonated beverage is 4-4.8.
   USE - The container-packed carbonated beverage is useful for regulating intestine, suppressing raise in triglyceride and blood glucose levels in blood after meals, and lowering cholesterol.
   ADVANTAGE - The container-packed carbonated beverage is sugar-free and suppressed from degradation odor generated by irradiation by light emitting diode illumination, and has excellent taste, fragrance and flavor.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Container-packed sugar-free carbonated beverage comprises indigestible dextrin at a concentration of 10-20 g/l, or water, carbon dioxide, antifoaming agent and indigestible dextrin, where gas volume in the sugar-free carbonated beverage is 4-4.8. The carbonated beverage satisfies an equation of Y greater than or equal to 0.1867X+1.0667 and Y greater than or equal to 0.28X-0.8, where X is indigestible dextrin concentration (g/l) and Y is gas volume in the beverage. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for method for suppressing degradation odor generated by irradiation by light emitting diode illumination, which involves adjusting content of the raw materials and gas volume amount.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Product: The carbonated beverage is contained in a container in which its appearance is transparent, where the carbonated beverage is irradiated under condition of 5000000 Lux/hour by light emitting diode illumination.

   MECHANISM OF ACTION - None given.

PN CN109180601-A; CN109180601-B
TI Synthesis of 2,4-oxazolidinedione for e.g. pesticide, involves mixing acetylene amide, solvent and organic catalyst in reaction flask, transferring flask to carbon dioxide atmosphere, performing carboxy cyclization reaction, and purifying.
AU ZHOU H
   MU S
   ZHANG R
   ZHANG H
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 201909335U
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesis of 2,4-oxazolidinedione compound (I) involves adding acetylene amide (i) and solvent to the reaction flask, adding organic catalyst, transferring reaction flask to carbon dioxide atmosphere, performing carboxy cyclization reaction at 0.1-0.2 MPa and 25-80 degrees C for 1-6 hours under stirring, removing solvent in vacuum to give crude product, and purifying crude product by column chromatography using dichloromethane as eluent.
   USE - Synthesis of 2,4-oxazolidinedione used in organic synthesis, pesticides and medicine.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, clean and mild, and uses simple organic base instead of metal catalyst. The 2,4-oxazolidinedione has high-functional group tolerance, high conversion and excellent stereoselectivity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Synthesis of 2,4-oxazolidinedione compound of formula (I) involves adding acetylene amide of formula (i) and solvent to the reaction flask, adding organic catalyst, transferring reaction flask to carbon dioxide atmosphere, performing carboxy cyclization reaction at 0.1-0.2 MPa and 25-80 degrees C for 1-6 hours under stirring, removing solvent in vacuum to give crude product, and purifying crude product by column chromatography using dichloromethane as eluent.
   R1 = phenyl, p-fluorophenyl, p-bromophenyl, p-trifluoromethylphenyl, p-nitrophenyl, p-methylphenyl, p-methoxyphenyl or 2-thienyl;and
   R2 = benzyl, p-methoxyphenyl, n-butyl, isopropyl, cyclohexyl, cyclopropyl or 2-propynyl.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The molar ratio of organic catalyst to acetylene amide is 1:20. Preferred Components: The solvent is dichloromethane, N,N-dimethylformamide, dimethyl sulfoxide, acetonitrile or tetrahydrofuran. The organic catalyst is 1,5,7-triazidebicyclo(4.4.0)non-5-ene, 1,8-diazabicyclo(5.4.0)undec-7-ene, 1,5-diazabicyclo(4.3.0)non-5-ene or 7-methyl-1,5,7-triazabicyclo(4.4.0)non-5-ene.

PN CN114190435-A
TI Producing kefir fermented milk by fermenting kefir grains pre-cultured with fruit and vegetable juice, comprises inoculating kefir grains in acidic fruit and vegetable juice for pre-culturing, and inoculating pre-cultured kefir grains into liquid milk.
AU LU J
   ZHANG G
   LONG M
   GUO R
   YU L
AE UNIV HUZHOU (UYOU-C)
GA 202245711A
AB    NOVELTY - Producing kefir fermented milk by fermenting kefir grains pre-cultured with fruit and vegetable juice, comprises (1) inoculating kefir grains in acidic fruit and vegetable juice for pre-culturing; and (2) inoculating pre-cultured kefir grains into liquid milk to ferment to obtain kefir fermented milk.
   USE - The method is useful for producing kefir fermented milk by fermenting kefir grains pre-cultured with fruit and vegetable juice.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has higher acidity, higher viscosity, lower alcohol content, lower carbon dioxide release amount, larger change, and has better anti-bacterial activity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - FOOD - Preferred Components: The acidic fruit and vegetable juice is a natural single fruit and vegetable juice of pH 4-7, a natural fruit and vegetable juice mixture, or a fruit and vegetable juice whose pH is adjusted artificially.Preferred Method: The preculturing comprises (1) pasteurizing acidic fruit and vegetable juice; (2) inoculating kefir grains and culturing; and (3) culturing to obtain pre-cultured kefir particles. The acidic fruit and vegetable juice is inoculated with 3 g kefir grains per 100 ml. The acidic fruit and vegetable juice is incubated at 30degrees Celsius for 12-24 hours after inoculation with kefir grains. The liquid milk is inoculated with 3 g pre-cultured kefir grains per 100 ml. The liquid milk is fermented at 25-30degrees Celsius for 20-36 hours until the pH is 3.5-5. The liquid milk is fermented at 30degrees Celsius to pH 4.5-4.6. The after-ripening is cooling at 4-10degrees Celsius for 12-30 hours.

PN CN113583252-A; CN113583252-B
TI Preparing microporous metal-organic framework            Cu(Qc)2 used in gas adsorption and gas storage,            involves mixing first raw material and second raw            material in organic solvent, and sonicating to obtain            intermediate solution.
AU XI Y
   ZHANG X
   GUO M
   HE G
   LIU H
   NING M
   DAI Y
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY PANJIN IND TECHNO (UYDA-Non-standard)
GA 2021C8493Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing microporous metal-organic framework                Cu(Qc)2 (I) involves mixing first raw material and                second raw material in organic solvent, sonicating                to obtain intermediate solution.
   USE - Method for preparing microporous metal-organic                framework Cu(Qc)2 used in gas adsorption and gas                storage (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare microporous                metal-organic framework by simple and convenient,                and has good carbon dioxide adsorption                capacity.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparing microporous metal-organic framework                Cu(Qc)2 of formula (I) involves mixing first raw                material and second raw material in organic                solvent, sonicating to obtain intermediate                solution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The first raw material                  is zinc oxide. The second raw material is copper                  tetrafluoroborate hexahydrate.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The organic solvent is                  selected from dimethylformamide, ethanol or                  n-butanol. The sulfhydryl polyethylene glycol is                  compound of formula (II). The                  mercaptopolyethylene glycol is                  (11-mercaptoundecyl)hexa(ethylene glycol).A=3-8;B=2-6;and C=H, hydroxyl, methoxy,                  amino, carboxyl.Preferred Process: The preparation of                  microporous metal-organic framework Cu(Qc)2 of                  formula (I) involves mixing first raw material                  and second raw material in an organic solvent,                  sonicating for 10 minutes to obtain intermediate                  solution, mixing the obtained intermediate                  solution with dimethylformamide containing                  quinoline-5-carboxylic acid, performing synthesis                  reaction for 10-16 hours, adding the synergist,                  reacting continuously for 30-100 minutes, washing                  the product after the synthesis reaction with an                  organic solvent for 6-9 times to obtain a Cu(Qc)2                  dispersion, filtering the obtained dispersion                  liquid by suction to obtain purple powder,                  drying, and activating under vacuum at high                  temperature to obtain microporous metal-organic                  framework Cu(Qc)2 of formula (I). The preparation                  of synergist involves taking pts. wt., performing                  in a closed high-pressure reactor, foam nitrogen,                  adding 12-22 pts. wt. 1-allyl-3-vinylimidazole                  nitrate, 5-11 pts. wt. mercaptopolyethylene                  glycol, 0.06-1.5 pts. wt.                  3-amino-4-mercaptoquinoline (CAS:109543-48-8),                  100-120 pts. wt. ethanol, adding 5-11 pts. wt.                  sodium methoxide, raising the temperature,                  stirring to 50-65degrees Celsius, reacting for                  1-4 hours, evaporating, and removing the ethanol                  to obtain the synergist. Preferred Conditions:                  The mass percentage content of the                  quinoline-5-carboxylic acid is 1-10 wt.%. The                  mass percentage content of the synergist is                  0.5-2.3 wt.%. The vacuum drying temperature is                  100-200degrees Celsius, and the time is 6-10                  hours. The microporous metal-organic framework                  Cu(Qc)2 of formula (I) surface area is 180-320                  m2/g, the microporous pore volume is 0.09-0.20                  cm3/g, the diameter of the micropores is 1.0-2.0                  nanometer.

PN CN112794782-A; CN112794782-B
TI New fluorine-containing carboxylic acid compound is prepared by reacting fluorine-containing olefin compound with carbon dioxide in presence of copper catalyst, ligand, base and boron reagent.
AU YU D
   LI J
   YAN S
   WU D
   YE J
   GONG L
   ZENG X
   RAN C
   GUI Y
AE UNIV SICHUAN (USCU-C)
GA 2021567417
AB    NOVELTY - A fluorine-containing carboxylic acid compound (I), is new.
   USE - New fluorine-containing carboxylic acid compound.
   ADVANTAGE - The fluorine-containing carboxylic acid compound can be prepared using wide range of raw materials under mild reaction conditions, without using toxic substances and precious metals. The fluorine-containing carboxylic acid compound opens up an important way for rational and efficient utilization of greenhouse gases.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A fluorine-containing carboxylic acid compound of formula (I), is new.
   R1 = H, halo, methoxy, cyano, alkyl, carbonyl, ester, aryl, heteroaryl and/or their derivatives.
   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for preparation of the compound (I).
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preparation (claimed): Preparation of the compound (I) involves reacting a fluorine-containing olefin compound with carbon dioxide in the presence of a copper catalyst, a ligand, a base and a boron reagent in a solvent at a reaction temperature of 50-100 degrees C for a reaction time of 2-28 hours. Preferred Composition: The fluorine-containing olefin compound is alpha -trifluoromethylstyrene compound of formula (II). The molar ratio of alpha -trifluoromethylstyrene compound with respect to copper catalyst is 40:1-5:1, preferably 10:1. The molar ratio of alpha -trifluoromethylstyrene compound with respect to ligand is 40:1-5:1, preferably 10:1. The molar ratio of alpha -trifluoromethylstyrene compound with respect to base is 1:1-1:6, preferably 1:3. The molar ratio of alpha -trifluoromethylstyrene compound with respect to boron reagent is 1:1-1:5, preferably 1:1.5. The copper catalyst is cuprous thiophene-2-carboxylate, copper acetylacetonate, cuprous acetate, copper acetate, chlorinated cuprous, copper fluoride, copper bromide and/or copper chloride. The ligand is 4,5-bisdiphenylphosphine-9,9-dimethylxanthene, bis((2-diphenylphosphino)phenyl)ether, 4,6-bis(diphenylphosphine)phenazine, bis(diphenylphosphino)ethane and/or bis(diphenylphosphino)butane. The base is potassium fluoride, cesium carbonate, potassium methoxide, sodium methoxide, lithium tert-butoxide and/or potassium tert-butoxide. The boron reagent is pinacol diborate, neopentyl glycol diborate, catechol diborate, bis(2,4-dimethyl-2,4-pentanediol)borate and/or bis(2-methyl-2,4-pentanediol)borate. The solvent is N,N-dimethylformamide or N-methylpyrrolidone. Preferred Process: The reaction is performed in the presence of 1-iodohexane.
   SPECIFIC COMPOUNDS - 15 Compounds (I) are specifically claimed e.g. hexyl 2,2-difluoro-3-(4-phenylphenyl)but-3-enoate of formula (Ia), hexyl 3-(4-chlorophenyl)-2,2-difluorobut-3-enoate, hexyl 3-(4-bromophenyl)-2,2-difluorobut-3-enoate, hexyl 2,2-difluoro-3-(4-methoxyphenyl)but-3-enoate and hexyl 3-(4-tert-butylphenyl)-2,2-difluorobut-3-enoate.

PT P
PN CN110584032-A
TI Deodorizing shellfish by ozone combined with            oxygen-shielding microorganisms involves reducing            bacterial base of raw materials by ozone reducing            bacteria treatment, adding fermentation broth.
AU HE Z
   LIN X
   LIANG Z
   SU H
   LI W
   ZHUANG L
AE FUJIAN ACAD AGRIC SCI AGRIC ENG TECHNOLO (IAMV-C)
GA 2019A7746C
AB    NOVELTY - Deodorizing shellfish by ozone combined with                oxygen-shielding microorganisms involves reducing                bacterial base of raw materials by ozone reducing                bacteria treatment, adding fermentation broth to                processed bacon meat, inoculating lactic acid                bacteria and Saccharomyces cerevisiae with                biological deodorization effect as fermentation                agents, and performing anaerobic microbial                fermentation by filling carbon dioxide or nitrogen                and oxygen.
   USE - Method for deodorizing shellfish by ozone                combined with oxygen-shielding                microorganisms.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to deodorize shellfish by                ozone combined with oxygen-shielding microorganisms                that avoids risk of contamination and corruption of                oysters in the biological deodorization process,                and ensures smooth biological fermentation of the                dominant deodorant species.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Components: The shell meat                  includes fresh meat of scallop, mussel, and clam                  and frozen meat. The lactic acid bacteria are                  facultative anaerobic Lactobacillus paracasei                  subsp. Paracasei RP38 which has biological                  deodorization ability, and has been deposited in                  China Type Culture Collection, and its deposit                  number is CCTCC NO: M2019322, deposited on May 5,                  2019. The Saccharomyces cerevisiae is                  Saccharomyces cerevisiae JJ4, which has ability                  to produce aroma and deodorization, and has been                  deposited in China Type Culture Collection                  Center, deposit number is CCTCC NO: M 2018219,                  and preservation date is April 19, 2018.                  Preferred Conditions: The mass ratio of the                  shellfish to the fermentation broth is 1:0.5-1.                  The ratio of Lactobacillus paracasei subspecies                  RP38 to Saccharomyces cerevisiae JJ4 is 3-5:1.                  Preferred Process: The method involves adding                  saturated ozone water to shellfish in ratio of                  1:1.0-1.5. The pH value of the ozone water is                  adjusted to 4.5-4.6 with citric acid, and                  temperature is adjusted to 5-10 degrees C with                  ice and remove shellfish and drain. The method                  involves preparing fermentation broth, preparing                  sugar water with mass concentration of 1-2% with                  white sugar, adjusting pH to 4.5-4.6 with citric                  acid, and sterilizing and cooling for later use.                  The method involves preparing starter, activating                  Lactobacillus paracasei subspecies RP38,                  inoculating in MRS liquid medium or molasses                  medium, culturing at 25-30 degrees C to bacteria                  volume of more than 1.0x 109 cfu/mL, and then                  centrifuging to obtain fresh lactic acid                  bacteria, directly use as starter or prepare as                  direct-injection bacterial agent, after                  activating Saccharomyces cerevisiae JJ4,                  inoculating it into molasses medium, incubating                  at 25-30 degrees C to bacteria volume of more                  than 1.0x 108cfu/mL, and then centrifuging to                  obtain fresh yeast, which can be directly used as                  fermentation agent. The shellfish and                  fermentation broth treated are contained in                  inflatable and sealed bio-fermenter, and then                  inoculated with compound bacteria containing                  Lactobacillus paracasei subspecies RP38 and                  Saccharomyces cerevisiae JJ4, inoculation amount                  is 1-5%, fill fermenter with carbon dioxide or                  nitrogen oxygen-sealing seal, and perform                  anaerobic microbial fermentation at 25-30 degrees                  C for 3-6 hours to obtain deodorized shellfish                  meat.
DC D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.)

TI Polylactic acid degradable nonwoven fabric            includes preset amount of polylactic acid fibers,            polypropylene resin and calcium carbonate            masterbatch.
AU LIU C
AE DONGGUAN IUIU HOUSEHOLD PROD CO LTD (DONG-Non-standard)
GA 201829916L
AB    NOVELTY - A polylactic acid degradable nonwoven fabric                includes 80-120 pts. wt. polylactic acid fibers,                10-30 pts. wt. polypropylene resin and 40-80 pts.                wt. calcium carbonate masterbatch.
   USE - Polylactic acid degradable nonwoven                fabric.
   ADVANTAGE - The polylactic acid degradable nonwoven fabric                masterbatch has excellent biological degradability,                and can be decomposed into carbon dioxide and water                by the action of microorganism in soil or seawater.                When combusting, the masterbatch does not emits                toxic gas. The cloth made of polylactic acid                degradable nonwoven fabric masterbatch has                excellent hand-feel, drapability and                anti-ultraviolet property, and has low                flammability.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for                preparation of polylactic acid degradable nonwoven                fabric.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The calcium carbonate                  masterbatch is made of heavy calcium carbonate                  with polypropylene PP as carrier. The weight                  ratio of heavy calcium carbonate with respect to                  polypropylene resin is 4-6:1. The heavy calcium                  carbonate is high-grade fatty acid ester-modified                  heavy calcium carbonate having particle size of                  10-50 nm.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The anti-ultraviolet                  agent is mixture containing                  4-benzoyloxy-2,2,6,6-tetramethylpiperidine,                  2-hydroxy-4-n-octyloxybenzophenone and                  2,4,6-tris(2'-n-butoxyphenyl)-1,3,5-triazine in                  the weight ratio of 1.5-2.5:0.5-1.5:1. The                  antioxidant is mixture containing                  tetrakis(methylene-3,5-(di-tert-butyl-4-hydroxy-phenyl)propionate)pentaerythritol                  ester, tris(2,4-di-t-butylphenyl)phosphate and                  n-octadecanol beta                  -(3,5-di-t-butyl-4-hydroxyphenyl)propionate in                  the weight ratio of 1:0.2-0.6:0.4-0.8.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Properties: The polylactic acid                  fiber has density of 1.2-1.3 g/mL, melting point                  of 160-170 degrees C, fineness of 0.5-2 D and                  glass transition temperature of 55-65 degrees C.                  The polypropylene resin is metallocene-catalyzed                  isotactic polypropylene having melt index of 1-10                  g/10 minute. Polypropylene is mixture containing                  copolymerized polypropylene and homopolypropylene                  in the weight ratio of 1-2:1. The polylactic acid                  degradable nonwoven fabric further includes                  0.5-1.5 pts. wt. other additives. The other                  additives are mixture of anti-ultraviolet agent                  and antioxidant in the weight ratio of                  0.8-1.2:1.

PN US2018057439-A1
TI Producing organic acid having at least three carbons, comprises forming carbon monoxide and reacting carbon monoxide with unsaturated hydrocarbon in presence of catalyst comprising palladium, strong acid and phosphine.
AU MASEL R I
   NI Z R
   CHEN Q
   ROSEN B A
AE DIOXIDE MATERIALS INC (DIOX-Non-standard)
GA 201816388M
AB    NOVELTY - Producing an organic acid having at least 3C atoms, comprises: forming a content of carbon monoxide; and reacting the carbon monoxide with a content of an unsaturated hydrocarbon in the presence of (a) a catalyst comprising palladium supported on at least one of a metal oxide, activated carbon, graphite, graphene, carbon nanotubes, fullerenes, carbon monoliths, templated carbon and diamond, (b) a strong acid and (c) a phosphine.
   USE - The process is useful for producing an organic acid having at least 3C atoms.
   ADVANTAGE - The process encompasses the use of carbon dioxide for the removal of calcium from solution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal oxide comprises at least one of alumina, titania, zirconia, niobia, silica, magnesia, zinc oxide, a zeolite and a mixture of zeolites.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The process further comprises initially converting a content of carbon dioxide obtained from a natural source or from an artificial chemical source to produce the content of carbon monoxide, to reduce the content of carbon dioxide present in nature or diverting the content of carbon dioxide from being discharged into the environment by the artificial chemical source. The carbon monoxide is formed from formic acid, which contacts an acid catalyst to convert the formic acid to carbon monoxide, where the acid catalyst temperature is different than the temperature of the supported palladium catalyst, and the acid catalyst temperature is at least 100 degrees C, preferably at least 130 degrees C. The converting step comprises electrochemically converting carbon dioxide to another carbon compound. Preferred Components: The phosphine is diphenyl-2-pyridylphosphine and the strong acid is trifluoromethane sulfonic acid. The unsaturated hydrocarbon is one of acetylene and methylacetylene. The organic acid is one of acrylic acid and methyl acrylic acid. The reaction temperature is 50-350 degrees C.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN CN114163355-A
TI Improving reaction conversion rate of amine and            carbon dioxide comprises preparing bicarbonate            additive, adding bicarbonate additive into diamine            solution containing primary amino to mix to obtain            mixed solution and treating mixed solution using            electro-spray ionization technology to obtain            product.
AU DAI X
   FANG X
   FENG L
   GONG X
   PAN Y
AE UNIV ZHEJIANG (UYZH-C)
GA 202242229B
AB    NOVELTY - Improving reaction conversion rate of amine                and carbon dioxide comprises (i) preparing                bicarbonate additive; (ii) adding the bicarbonate                additive obtained in (i) into diamine solution                containing primary amino to mix uniformly to obtain                mixed solution; and (iii) treating the mixed                solution obtained in (ii) is using electro-spray                ionization technology to obtain a product, namely                carbamic acid.
   USE - The method is useful for improving reaction                conversion rate of amine and carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method proves that the bicarbonate can be                used as a good intermediate of carbon-dioxide                fixation and transformation. The technical solution                is suitable for droplet reaction of amine and                carbon-dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The bicarbonate                  additive is ammonium bicarbonate, sodium                  bicarbonate or potassium bicarbonate. The solvent                  is 5-95 vol.% water or 5-95 vol.% methanol                  aqueous solution. The diamine solution containing                  primary amino is N, N-dimethyl ethylene diamine,                  N, N-dimethyl-1, 3-propylene diamine, N,                  N-dimethyl-1, 3-butanediamine, N-methyl-1,                  3-propane diamine or 1, 4-diaminobutane. The                  concentration of the diamine solution containing                  primary amino group isnot less than 50 micro                  moles/l. The content of the bicarbonate additive                  in the mixed solution is 1-20 mmol/l. The                  conversion rate of the amine is not less                  than78.3%.Preferred Method: In the step (i), weighing                  a certain amount of bicarbonate, preparing                  solvent, adding bicarbonate into the solvent and                  uniformly stirring to obtain the bicarbonate                  additive. In the step (iii), introducing the                  mixed solution obtained by (ii), the injection                  pump sprays the mixed solution at a certain flow                  rate, under the assistance of voltage and                  atomizing gas, forming electrospray droplets,                  where the flow speed is 5-100 l/minute, the                  voltage is 3.5- 6 kV, and the atomizing gas is                  nitrogen and generating bicarbonate decomposition                  and conversion reaction of amine in electric                  spray droplets at 25-350degrees Celsius to obtain                  amino formic acid product.

PN CN113998683-A
TI Method for capturing and utilizing biomass hydrothermal coupled carbon involves performing carbonization pulping and papermaking with pollutants, transporting paper-making waste liquid to carbon dioxide trap, and hydrothermally carbonizing.
AU YANG Y
   LEI J
   CHEN C
   ZHOU Y
   ZHAO G
AE DATANG NORTHEAST ELECTRIC POWER TEST RES (CDAT-C)
GA 202225876D
AB    NOVELTY - Method for capturing and utilizing biomass hydrothermal coupled carbon involves introducing pollutants into biomass pulping and papermaking area of hydrothermal carbonization reactor and performing biomass hydrothermal carbonization pulping and papermaking to obtain paper-making waste liquid, transporting the paper-making waste liquid to carbon dioxide trap for carbon dioxide capture, and transporting the paper-making waste liquid after carbon dioxide capture to biomass hydrothermal carbonization zone of the hydrothermal carbonization reactor for hydrothermal carbonization reaction to obtain high value-added carbon products.
   USE - Method for capturing and utilizing biomass hydrothermal coupled carbon.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is capable of realizing collection of carbon dioxide, utilizing biomass energy, radically solving energy carbon emission problem, and realizing carbon emission reduction and clean energy replacement of gain effect.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The hydrothermal carbonization reaction catalyst is sodium carbonate. The biomass raw material used for the hydrothermal carbonization reaction is one or more of sewage sludge, crop straw, and kitchen waste. The high value-added carbon product is biological carbon, biological oil or activated carbon.

PN CN113278995-A
TI Preparing oxalic acid by carbon dioxide conversion involves passing carbon dioxide into alkali metal hydroxide aqueous solution, absorbing to obtain carbonate or bicarbonate aqueous solution and performing catalytic hydrogenation.
AU ZHANG Y
   LI X
AE INST COAL CHEM CHINESE ACAD SCI (CAXI-C)
GA 202199648S
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing oxalic acid by carbon dioxide conversion involves passing carbon dioxide into alkali metal hydroxide aqueous solution, absorbing to obtain carbonate or bicarbonate aqueous solution, performing catalytic hydrogenation of the obtained carbonate or bicarbonate aqueous solution in the presence of a catalyst to obtain formate, heating, dehydrogenating the obtained formate to obtain oxalate and hydrogen, and electrolyzing the obtained oxalate to obtain oxalic acid and alkali metal hydroxide, where the reaction by-product hydrogen is used as the reaction raw material hydrogen, the alkali metal hydroxide of the reaction by-product is used as the raw material of the alkali metal hydroxide.
   USE - Method for preparing oxalic acid by carbon dioxide conversion.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is cost-effective.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alkali metal hydroxide is selected from lithium hydroxide, sodium hydroxide, potassium hydroxide, rubidium hydroxide, and cesium hydroxide. The active ingredient is selected from copper, platinum, nickel, molybdenum, palladium, iridium, ruthenium, and rhodium. The additive is selected from cobalt or cobalt oxide, lanthanum or lanthanum oxide, magnesium or magnesium oxide, tin or tin oxide, gallium or gallium oxide, The carrier includes gamma -alumina, silicon oxide, activated carbon, carbon microsphere, titanium dioxide, silicon carbide, and resin. Preferred Conditions: The alkali metal hydroxide aqueous solution comprises 0.01-20 mol/L hydroxide. The temperature at which the alkali metal hydroxide aqueous solution absorbs carbon dioxide is 0-90 degrees C. The carbonate or bicarbonate aqueous solution comprises 1-65 wt.% carbonate or bicarbonate. The carbon dioxide is passing into alkali metal hydroxide aqueous solution, absorbing to obtain carbonate or bicarbonate aqueous solution, controlling the hydrogen pressure at 0.1-15.0 megapascal, the reaction temperature at 20-300 degrees C. The molar ratio of hydrogen to carbonate or bicarbonate at 1-500:1. The temperature of the heating dehydrogenation reaction of formate is 390-440 degrees C. The temperature at which oxalic acid and alkali metal hydroxide are generated by the electrolysis of oxalate is 10-120 degrees C. The active component accounts for 0.1-30 wt.% based on the total mass of the catalyst. The auxiliary agent accounts for 0-18 wt.% based on the total mass of the catalyst. The temperature of hydrogen reduction is 80-550 degrees C, and the reduction time is 1-12 hours.

PN CN111454541-A
TI Preparing carbon dioxide extruded board, involves using polystyrene synthetic resin, masterbatch, benzoyl peroxide, decabromodiphenyl ether, ethanol, tetrafluorodichloroethane, calcium stearate, carbon dioxide and epoxy resin.
AU GUO G
AE NINGBO BAOLAI ENERGY SAVING TECHNOLOGY (NING-Non-standard)
GA 202073870C
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing carbon dioxide extruded board, involves using 30-40 pts. wt. polystyrene synthetic resin, 2-5 pts. wt. masterbatch, 1-2 pts. wt. benzoyl peroxide, 5-8 pts. wt. decabromodiphenyl ether, 3-5 pts. wt. ethanol, 2-5 pts. wt. deionized water, 2-3 pts. wt. tetrafluorodichloroethane, 0.5-2 pts. wt. calcium stearate, 8-15 pts. wt. carbon dioxide and 30-42 pts. wt. epoxy resin.
   USE - Method for preparing carbon dioxide extruded board.
   ADVANTAGE - The method provides the extruded board, which effectively reduces the foaming and closed cell rate, has enhanced waterproof and impermeability, and improved energy saving efficiency, does not contain any radioactive substances and toxic gases, does not cause damage to the atmosphere, and is completely lightweight, thermally insulated, energy-saving and environmentally-friendly.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The method involves (i) placing ethanol, deionized water and tetrafluorodichloroethane in a closed stainless steel container, and using a pneumatic stirring device to fully stir and fuse ethanol, deionized water and tetrafluorodichloroethane, (ii) injecting alcohol mixture and supercritical carbon dioxide into a liquid static mixer, and mixing the medium flowing under high pressure through mixing elements to make modified carbon dioxide, (iii) introducing polystyrene synthetic resin, benzoyl peroxide, decabromodiphenyl ether, carbon dioxide and epoxy resin into a mixer, uniformly stirring, heating at 220 degrees C for 1 hour, adding calcium stearate and the masterbatch to the mixer, continuously stirring for half an hour, after the stirring is completed, leaving still for half an hour and introducing into a feeding cavity in an extruder, and (iv) introducing the modified carbon dioxide into the feeding cavity in the extruder to mix with the melt, increasing the pressure in the extruder and extruding the melt out. Preferred Composition: The extruded board comprises 30 pts. wt. polystyrene synthetic resin, 2 pts. wt. masterbatch, 6 pts. wt. benzoyl peroxide, 5 pts. wt. decabromodiphenyl ether, 3 pts. wt. ethanol, 2 pts. wt. deionized water, 2.5 pts. wt. tetrafluorodichloroethane, 1.5 pts. wt. calcium stearate, 10 pts. wt. carbon dioxide and 38 pts. wt. epoxy resin. Preferred Condition: The ratio of alcohol mixture to supercritical carbon dioxide is 1:1.58.

AB    NOVELTY - Preparing M-carbon dioxide-tetraoxygen nano-wire comprises (i) dissolving the M source and the cobalt source in an organic solvent to obtain a reaction liquid A, where the metal element M in the M source is copper or zinc; (ii) heating the reaction solution A until the combustion reaction occurs after the end of the reaction to obtain product B; and (iii) washing and drying the product B to obtain a copper-carbon dioxide- tetraoxygen nanowire or a zinc- carbon dioxide- tetraoxygen nanowire.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing M-carbon dioxide- tetraoxygen nano-wire.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and economical; has short time consumption and mild reaction conditions; and suitable for large scale production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The M source is nitrate, sulfate or chloride salt of the metal M. The cobalt source is nitrate, sulfate or chloride of metal cobalt. The organic solvent is methanol, ethanol, ethylene glycol or glycerin. The molar ratio of metal M to metallic cobalt is 1:2. Preferred Method: In the step (ii), heating reaction liquid A at 200-450 degrees C. In the step (ii), the combustion reaction is completed in 10-20 minutes. In the step (iii), washing the product B with distilled water and organic solvent, and drying the product B at 60-110 degrees C, where the organic solvent is methanol and ethanol.

PN CN111214508-A
TI Extracting volatile oil, by crushing Ardisia crenata root, sieving, dissolving with carbon tetrachloride solution, adding to supercritical carbon dioxide fluid, precipitating, drying, adding absolute ethanol, extracting and freeze-drying.
AU YANG C
AE NANJING ZELANG MEDICAL TECHNOLOGY CO LTD (NJZL-C)
GA 202050536U
AB    NOVELTY - Method for extracting volatile oil, involves (i) crushing Ardisia crenata root and sieving through a 100 mesh sieve, and completely dissolving with 3-5 times carbon tetrachloride solution, (ii) adding the above solution to supercritical carbon dioxide fluid for processing, dissolving solvent in the carbon dioxide stream, precipitating solute, and drying to form nanoparticles, and (iii) adding the nanoparticles to 3-5 times absolute ethanol, uniformly mixing, adding to the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction stream for extraction, collecting the extract, recovering carbon dioxide and freeze-drying.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting volatile oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has high extraction rate, simple process, short extraction time and low production cost, and is suitable for industrial production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The material-to-liquid ratio of carbon tetrachloride added in step (i) is 1:(3-5). The extractant used in step (iii) is absolute ethanol, and the material-to-liquid ratio is 1:(3-5).

PT P
PN US2020070091-A1; IN201821032404-A; EP3620225-A1; IN349362-B; EP3620225-B1
TI Selective capturing of carbon dioxide from gaseous mixture involves capturing carbon dioxide through bio-amine cluster containing cluster stabilizing agent, cluster micelle stabilizing agent, and desorbing through electrodeamination.
AU KUMAR M
   SAHOO P C
   PURI S K
   RAMAKUMAR S S V
AE INDIAN OIL CORP LTD (INOI-C)
   INDIAN OIL CORP LTD (INOI-C)
   INDIAN OIL CORP LTD (INOI-C)
   INDIAN OIL CORP LTD (INOI-C)
GA 2020189765
AB    NOVELTY - Selective capturing of carbon dioxide (CO2) from gaseous mixture involves (a) spraying a bio-amine cluster, (b) capturing (CO2) through bio-amine cluster, (c) desorbing (CO2) through solar assisted electrodeamination. The bio-amine cluster comprises (i) an amine cluster, (ii) a cluster stabilizing agent, (iii) a cluster micelle stabilizing agent, and (iv) 0.05-0.2 wt.% carbonic anhydrase (A') functionalized matrix. The component (A') is obtained from a source chosen from Bacillus thermoleovorans, Pseudomonas fragi, Bacillus stearothermophilus, and Arthrobacter.
   USE - Selective capturing of carbon dioxide from gaseous stream such as natural gas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method effectively captures carbon dioxide, and has excellent overall efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for production of bio-amine cluster, which involves synthesizing amine cluster and synthesizing carbonic anhydrase functionalized matrix.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The synthesis of amine cluster involves synthesizing the quaternary isobutylamine by reacting 2-10C alkanolamine or hindered amine and hydrochloric acid in equimolar concentrations in presence of ATPL as a chelating agent, incubating the obtained reaction solution in ice bath for 30-60 minutes, again incubating the obtained reaction solution at 55-60 degrees C for 80-60 minutes and cooling the reaction solution at room temperature, removing excess water and hydrochloric acid from the obtained reaction solution to attain basic pH by using a rotary evaporator at 20-35 degrees C, crystallizing the solution using an acetone and diethyl ether solvent at a volume ratio of 1:1, separating and drying the solid crystalline layer at room temperature under vacuum, dissolving the solid crystalline substance in deionized water in 30:70 wt.%, adding 0.5 mM cluster stabilizing agent, stirring the solution for 20-40 minutes at room temperature, adding 500 ppm cluster micelle stabilizing agent to the solution, and ultrasonically treating the solution for 2-10 minutes by stirring at room temperature for 15-30 minutes. The synthesis of carbonic anhydrase functionalized matrix involves adding porous matrix to an ethanol solution containing 0.5-2 (volume/volume)% (3-glycidoxypropyl)trimethoxysilane and 0.5-2(volume/volume)% triethoxy-3-(2-imidazolin-1-yl)propylsilane, incubating the suspension at 55-65 degrees C for 80-160 minutes with continuous stirring, filtering and washing porous matrix with deionized water and drying at room temperature to obtain a functionalized matrix, suspending the functionalized matrix in 50 mM sodium phosphate solution containing a thermo-stable carbonic anhydrase, stirring the suspension obtained at 160-240 rpm for 80-160 minutes at room temperature, and filtering and washing matrix with deionized water. The synthesis of a porous matrix involves preparing a solution of Pluronic F-127 (RTM: nonionic polyol surfactant) and cetyltrimethylammonium bromide in deionized water and adding boric acid under stirring condition, incubating the solution with continuous stirring for 10-30 minutes, adding 0.5 M calcium chloride to the solution and incubating with vigorous stirring for 15-45 minutes, adding tetraethylorthosilicate drop wise to the solution and passing CO2 for 10-20 minutes to obtain precipitate, filtering and oven drying the precipitate, and calcining the dried precipitate at 160-240 degrees C for 240-360 minutes. Preferred Conditions: The method is performed at 2-6 degrees C, and utilizing 5 moles 2-10C alkanolamine or hindered amine and hydrochloric acid in equimolar concentration, and 50 ppm ATPL. Preferred Components: The bio-amine cluster is capable of desorption of CO2, and comprises a quaternary isobutylamine with amine-terminated poly(L-lactide) (ATPL) as a chelating agent. The component (ii) is chosen from poly(ethylene glycol) diamine, diaminobutane, urea, 1,6-hexanediamine, diethylenetriamine, N1,N1-dimethylbenzene-1,2-diamine, 1,3-diaminopentane, and ethylenediamine, preferably poly(ethylene glycol)diamine. The component (iii) comprises a long chain alkyl amine or N-based alkyl amines and derivatives, preferably N-(2-((2-aminoethyl)amino)ethyl)-9-octadecenamide, n-benzalkonium chloride, polyoxyethylene alkyl ether, n-alkyltrimethylammonium surfactant, potassium alkanoate, dodecylpyridinium bromide, octylglucoside, sodium dodecyl sulfate, trans-cinnamaldehyde, sodium bis-(2-ethylhexyl)-sulfosuccinate, cetylpyridinium chloride, primary alcohol ethoxylate, polyoxyethylene nonyl phenyl ether, polyethylene glycol ester, linoleate, and dodecylamine, preferably is N-(2-((2-aminoethyl)amino)ethyl)-9-octadecenamide. The carbonic anhydrase functionalized matrix comprises a porous matrix obtained by calcination of precipitates obtained by reacting calcium chloride with tetraethylorthosilicate in presence of Pluronic F-127 (RTM: nonionic polyol surfactant), cetyl trimethylammonium bromide, boric acid and carbon dioxide, and a thermostable enzyme extracted from B.stearothermophilus. The 2-10C alkanolamine or hindered amine is chosen from hydrochloride, sulfate, nitrate salt of isobutyl amine, 2-amino-2-methyl-ipropanol, 2-(2-aminoethylamino)ethanol, 2-amino-2-hydroxymethyl-1,3-propanediol, N-methyldiethanolamine, dimethylmonoethanolamine, diethylmonoethanolamine, triisopropanolamine, triethanolamine, trimethylamine, triethylamine, tripropylamine, tributylamine, dimethylethylamine, dimethylpropylamine, dimethylbutylamine, diethylmethylamine, diethylpropylamine, diethylbutylamine, N,N-diisopropylmethylamine, N-ethyldiisopropylamine, N,N-dimethylethylamine, N,N-diethylbutylamine, 1,2-dimethylpropylamine, N,N-diethylmethylamine, N,N-dimethylisopropylamine, 1,3-dimethylbutylamine, 3,3-dimethylbutylamine, and N,N-dimethylbutylamine. Preferred Properties: The matrix has pH tolerance of 5-13, temperature tolerance of 4-110 degrees C, and salinity tolerance of 0-6%.
DC D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); E19 (Other organic compounds general - organic compounds of unknown or indefinite structure; general mixtures of many types; organic reactions (e.g. nitration, resolution) when applied generally.); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); J01 (Separation - including evaporation, crystallisation, solvent extraction, chromatography, dialysis, osmosis including drying gases and/or vapours, and separation of solids from gases, liquids and other solids. Isotope separation, filter materials (including molecular sieves for separation), and centrifuges (except where used for analysis) (B01D, B03, B04, B07B).); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.)

TI Modified porous membrane material used for            improving separation factor or permeation flux in            liquid membrane separation of carbon dioxide comprises            inorganic porous material and amine-containing            groups.
AU FAN Y
   XU P
   QIU M
   FU K
   CHEN X
   KONG X
   GONG D
AE UNIV NANJING TECHNOLOGY (UNTY-C)
   UNIV NANJING TECHNOLOGY (UNTY-C)
   UNIV NANJING TECHNOLOGY (UNTY-C)
GA 202002646U
AB    NOVELTY - A modified porous membrane material comprises                inorganic porous material and amine-containing                groups modified in the pores of membrane                material.
   USE - The modified porous membrane material is used                for improving separation factor or permeation flux                in liquid membrane separation of carbon dioxide                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The material can avoid the loss of effective                alkaline substances.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS included for:
   (1) preparation of the membrane material                comprising dissolving amine group-containing silane                coupling agent in organic solvent, soaking porous                membrane material to carry out graft reaction,                washing and drying; and
   (2) liquid membrane separation of carbon                dioxide comprising adding modified porous membrane                material to water, and contacting with carbon                dioxide-containing gas mixture to allow carbon                dioxide to pass through the membrane.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The amine group is                  primary amine group, secondary amine group and/or                  tertiary amine group. The inorganic porous                  material is porous alumina, porous titanium                  oxide, porous zirconia and/or porous silica. The                  carbon dioxide-containing gas mixture is carbon                  dioxide, nitrogen, methane, hydrogen, oxygen,                  helium and/or carbon monoxide gas.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The amine                  group-containing silane coupling agent is                  N,N-dimethyl-3-aminopropyltrimethoxysilane and/or                  (3-aminopropyl)trimethoxysilane. TI Preparing silicon dioxide comprises e.g. adding a carbon dioxide capturing agent and water into container, introducing carbon dioxide, mixing with aqueous solution of sodium silicate, heating, performing solid-liquid separation and drying.
AU LI J
   LI M
   WANG Y
   WANG H
   SU Y
   HONG Y
AE UNIV XIAMEN (UYXI-C)
GA 201950061L
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing silicon dioxide comprises (i) adding a carbon dioxide capturing agent and water into container, and then introducing carbon dioxide to obtain homogeneous aqueous solution 1, (ii) mixing an aqueous solution of sodium silicate with the above aqueous solution 1, heating and stirring the reaction to obtain a reaction liquid, and (iii) heating the reaction liquid, then performing solid-liquid separation, and solid phase drying to obtain final product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing silicon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has simple process, and is suitable for industrial production; and utilizes recyclable carbon dioxide capture agent; is energy saving and environmentally friendly.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method further comprises (iv) separating liquid phase by heating or by adding alkaline to obtain a carbon dioxide capturing agent and an aqueous solution, and evaporating the aqueous solution to separate the reaction by-product carbonate. In step (ii), the reaction temperature is 15-200 degrees C, and the reaction time is 130 minutes. In step (iv), the heating temperature is 15-150 degrees C. Preferred Components: In step (i): the carbon dioxide capture agent is an organic substance that is insoluble or slightly soluble in water, preferably sec-butyl n-propylamine, triethylamine, hexylamine, heptylamine, octylamine, n,n-dimethylcyclohexylamine, di-n-propylamine, diisopropylamine, n-ethylbutylamine, di-n-butylamine, diisobutylamine, tripropylamine, tributylamine, N,N-diisopropylmethylamine, N,N-diisopropylethylamine, N,N-dimethylbutylamine, N,N-dimethyloctylamine, cyclohexylamine, cycloheptylamine, cyclooctylamine, N-isopropylcyclohexylamine, dicyclohexylamine, 2,6-dimethylpiperidine, 1-ethylpiperidine, 3,5-dimethylpiperidine, 2,2,6,6-tetramethylpiperidine, N-methylpiperidine, ethylpiperidine, benzylamine, N-methylbenzylamine, ethylbenzylamine, and/or N,N-dimethylbenzylamine. In step (i), the molar ratio between carbon dioxide capturing agent and water is 1:0.2-20. The concentration of the aqueous sodium silicate solution is 0.1-5 mol/l. The volume ratio between aqueous sodium silicate solution and carbon dioxide capture agent is 1:10-20:1. The amount ratio between hydroxy based alkaline and carbon dioxide capturing agent is 1:0.1-10.

   USE - The method is useful for environmentally friendly treating carbon dioxide and producing calcium carbonate.
   ADVANTAGE - The method increases energy efficiency by simplifying the process, produces high value calcium carbonate, and is economical.

   EXAMPLE - Arginine and calcium hydroxide were mixed at ratio of 1:1 to prepare a wet absorbent, and water was added into the prepared wet absorbent to prepare supersaturated solution having concentration of 2.5 M.

TI Preparing cyclic carbonate using iron-based catalyst to convert into carbon dioxide comprises e.g. reacting carbon dioxide and epoxide using conjugated iron porphyrin microcellular polymeric material and tetra-n-butylammonium bromide.
AU ZHANG X
   CHEN Z
   WANG S
   CAO Y
   WANG Y
AE HENAN INST ENG (HEAN-C)
GA 201835143M
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing cyclic carbonate using iron-based catalyst to convert into carbon dioxide comprises reacting carbon dioxide and epoxide as raw materials using conjugated iron porphyrin microcellular polymeric material as a heterogeneous catalyst, tetra-n-butylammonium bromide as co-catalyst at room temperature under normal pressure without solvent for 12-48 hours to obtain final product.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing cyclic carbonate using iron-based catalyst to convert into carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: has low cost, high catalytic activity, stable structural performance; can be reused, mild reaction conditions, fast speed and safe; and is simple, easy to recycle and suitable for large-scale industrial production.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The conjugated iron porphyrin microcellular polymeric material i.e. 2,7,12,17-tetraphenyl-21,22,23,24-tetraazapentacyclo(16.2.1.1(3,6).1(8,11).1(13,16))tetracosa-1,3,5,7,9,11(23),12,14,16,18(21),19-undecaene iron complex of formula (I) is prepared by carrying out Scholl coupling reaction of iron tetraphenylporphyrin as a monomer using an anhydrous aluminum chloride. The conjugated iron porphyrin microporous polymer material can be replaced with a conjugated iron porphyrin bibenzyl amine microporous polymer material i.e. (3-(4-(7,12,17-triphenyl-21,22,23,24-tetraazapentacyclo(16.2.1.1(3,6).1(8,11).1(13,16))tetracosa-1,3(24),4,6,8,10,12,14,16(22),17,19-undecaen-2-yl)phenyl)phenyl)methanamine iron complex of formula (II), and the conjugated iron porphyrin bibenzyl amine microporous polymer material is prepared by carrying out Scholl coupling reaction of iron tetraphenyl porphyrin and benzylamine as monomer using anhydrous aluminum chloride catalyst. Preferred Components: The epoxy compound is propylene oxide, epoxy butane, epoxy chloropropane, epoxy bromopropane, styrene oxide, phenyl glycidyl ether or cyclohexene oxide. The active component of multi-phase catalyst is 0.2-0.5 mol% of the amount of epoxy compound. The amount of co-catalyst is 3.6-7.2 mol% of the epoxy compound.

PT P
PN CN112920939-A
TI Enhancing the production of methane from bio-natural gas fermentation by using fermentation separation coupling integration to enhance carbon dioxide comprises constructing a fermentation circulation system.
AU XUE C
   WU Y
AE UNIV DALIAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDA-C)
GA 2021657416
AB    NOVELTY - Enhancing the production of methane from bio-natural gas fermentation by using fermentation separation coupling integration to enhance carbon dioxide comprises constructing a fermentation circulation system. The fermentation circulation system includes a fermentation reactor and a gas separation membrane system, where the fermentation circulation system passes through the fermentation reactor. Coupled and connected gas separation membrane system, Separate and purify methane and recycle carbon dioxide to the fermentation reactor. The Trichoderma reesei fermentative enzyme solution according to the ratio of straw material to solid-liquid ratio of 1:6-1:10, after pretreating the straw for 3-5 days, it is then mixed with livestock and poultry manure, agricultural product processing waste, food waste and food waste. The one or more of the municipal organic waste is mixed to obtain straw mixed solid waste.
   USE - Method for enhancing the production of methane from bio-natural gas fermentation by using fermentation separation coupling integration to enhance carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to enhance the production of methane from bio-natural gas fermentation by using fermentation separation coupling integration to enhance carbon dioxide, which quickly degrade lignocellulose into fermented monosaccharides, promote the proliferation of carbon dioxide fixation strains and bio-natural gas fermentation strains, and further improve the efficiency of carbon dioxide in-situ fixation and the performance of bio-natural gas fermentation for methane production.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Enhancing the production of methane from bio-natural gas fermentation by using fermentation separation coupling integration to enhance carbon dioxide comprises constructing a fermentation circulation system. The fermentation circulation system includes a fermentation reactor and a gas separation membrane system, where the fermentation circulation system passes through the fermentation reactor. Coupled and connected gas separation membrane system, Separate and purify methane and recycle carbon dioxide to the fermentation reactor. The Trichoderma reesei fermentative enzyme solution according to the ratio of straw material to solid-liquid ratio of 1:6-1:10, after pretreating the straw for 3-5 days, it is then mixed with livestock and poultry manure, agricultural product processing waste, food waste and food waste. The one or more of the municipal organic waste is mixed to obtain straw mixed solid waste. The amount of 5-20 wt.% of the mass of the mixed solid waste of straw is added with the fermentation broth of anaerobic strains that can utilize carbon dioxide to the fermentation reactor to form a mixed-bacteria fermentation system. The gas produced by the fermentation is separated by the gas separation membrane system. The fixed utilization and biological conversion of carbon dioxide is utilizwd in the fermentation process, which reduce emissions and promote methane synthesis, and realize in-situ fixation of carbon dioxide to increase the methane content and output of biological natural gas fermentation.

TI Wind-light-hydrogen-producing-gas supercritical carbon dioxide cycle complementary power generation system for street lamp in residential area park, has electrolytic water hydrogen production part provided with hydrogen compressor hydrogen storage tank and oxygen compressor oxygen storage tank.
AU CHEN Y
   CHEN H
   WEI J
   WANG D
AE UNIV SHANGHAI ELECTRIC POWER (UYSI-C)
GA 202280999D
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a high-temperature regenerator (53) connected with a critical carbon dioxide high temperature turbine. A low temperature regenerator is connected with the high temperature regenerator. A cooler is connected to the low temperature regenerator. An ultra-critical carbon dioxide compressor is connected between the cooler and an electric generator that is coaxially connected with an ultra-high temperature compressor. A low voltage bus is connected through an isolating switch and a high voltage transformer. A wind power generating device (11) and a photovoltaic power generation device (21) supply power to a high voltage bus through the isolation switch. An electrolytic water hydrogen production part is provided with a hydrogen storage tank (33) and an oxygen compressor oxygen storage tank (35).
   USE - Wind-light-hydrogen-producing-gas supercritical carbon dioxide cycle complementary power generation system for a street lamp in a residential area park.
   ADVANTAGE - The system improves wind power and photovoltaic power generation of wind and light abandoning ratio, so that the power grid is removed by electrolytic water hydrogen production to store, thus improving utilization rate of wind power. The system improves efficiency of a gas turbine and reduces power consumption of air compressor. The ultra-critical carbon dioxide circulation part of the smoke exhaust heat to waste heat the generating efficiency. The system injects hydrogen into a combustion chamber of the gas turbine to replace part of gas so as to reduce gas consumption and carbon dioxide emission, and realizes oxygen-enriched combustion.

PN CN112493132-A
TI Improving gas microenvironment of plant tissue culture seedlings by inoculating seedlings in medium, spreading carbon dioxide slow-release particles on medium, culturing, and mixing water-absorbing slow-release material.
AU LIU Z
AE LIU Z (LIUZ-Individual)
GA 202128602S
AB    NOVELTY - Method for improving the gas microenvironment of plant tissue culture seedlings, involves (a) inoculating the seedlings explants in a medium, putting carbon dioxide slow-release particles into the bottle and spreading it on the surface of the medium, hanging a moisture-absorbing bag near the opening of the bottle, and covering it for culturing, and (b) where the carbon dioxide slow-release particles contain a carbon dioxide release agent and a water-absorbing slow-release material, mixing the carbon dioxide release agent and a fully absorbent water-absorbing slow-release material at a weight ratio of (25-50):1, granulating in spherical and in 0.3-0.8 cm in diameter, and putting 2-10 grains per 100 ml based on the volume of the culture flask. The moisture-absorbing bag is filled with filter paper or non-woven fabric with sodium polyacrylate, where the appearance is nearly flat, and the edge of the bag is attached with thin lines, and each bottle is hung with 1-4.
   USE - The method is used for improving the gas microenvironment of plant tissue culture seedlings, and for regulating carbon dioxide and water in the plant tissue culture bottle.
   ADVANTAGE - The method increases the supply of carbon dioxide in the bottle to facilitate the photosynthesis of the bottle seedlings and reduces the humidity in the culture bottle, develops the cuticle of the epidermis of the bottle seedling leaves, and enhances the stomata opening and closing function, which is beneficial to reduce transpiration and improve the survival rate of bottle seedling transplantation. The method reduces the consumption of sucrose in the culture medium, and reduces the pollution rate of the culture medium. The carbon dioxide slow-release particles, moisture-absorbing bag and their application method ensures low cost, simple preparation, convenient application and remarkable effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a culture medium for improving the gas microenvironment of plant tissue culture bottle seedlings, in which spreading the carbon dioxide slow-release particles flatly on the surface of the conventional culture flask culture medium, spreading 2-10 particles per 100 ml based on the volume of the culture flask, hanging a moisture-absorbing bag near the opening of the culture flask, and hanging 1-4 particles per bottle, where the carbon dioxide slow-release particles contain a carbon dioxide release agent and a water-absorbing slow-release material, and the carbon dioxide release agent and the fully-absorbent water-absorbing slow-release material have a weight ratio of (25-50):1, to produce spherical shape, and a diameter of 0.3-0.8 cm, and where the moisture absorption bag is filled with sodium polyacrylate with filter paper or non-woven fabric, and the appearance is nearly flat.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Conditions: The raw materials for the preparation of carbon dioxide slow-release particles are pre-cold sterilized, and the moisture-absorbing bag is conventionally sterilized by dry hot air. Thin lines are attached to the edge of the moisture absorption bag, which is sterilized by dry heat.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The carbon dioxide release agent is a mixture of ammonium aluminum sulfate and sodium bicarbonate in a weight ratio of 1:(10-20).
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The water-absorbent sustained-release material includes superabsorbent resin. The superabsorbent resin includes sodium acrylate-vinyl alcohol copolymer or acrylamide-acrylic acid copolymer.
DC C06 (Biotechnology - including plant genetics and veterinary vaccines.); D16 (Fermentation industry - including fermentation equipment, brewing, yeast production, production of pharmaceuticals and other chemicals by fermentation, microbiology, production of vaccines and antibodies, cell and tissue culture and genetic engineering.); A97 (Miscellaneous goods not specified elsewhere - including papermaking, gramophone records, detergents, food and oil well applications.); P13 (Plant culture, dairy products (A01G, H, J).)

TI Separation of gaseous carbon dioxide or water from            gas mixture using device, involves contacting gas            mixture, evacuating unit comprising adsorber structure            with sorbent material, heating sorbent material, and            cooling adsorber structure.
AU GEBALD C
   REPOND N
   WURZBACHER J A
   TIMOFTE A E
   MESZAROS I
   KEUSCH B
AE CLIMEWORKS AG (CLIM-Non-standard)
   CLIMEWORKS AG (CLIM-Non-standard)
   CLIMEWORKS AG (CLIM-Non-standard)
   CLIMEWORKS AG (CLIM-Non-standard)
GA 2019A6157R
AB    NOVELTY - Separation of gaseous carbon dioxide or water                from gas mixture involves (a) contacting the gas                mixture with sorbent material to allow at least                gaseous carbon dioxide with or without water vapor                to adsorb on the sorbent material under ambient                atmospheric pressure of 0.8-1.2 bar (a') in an                adsorption step, (b) evacuating the unit (1) to a                pressure of 20-400 mbar (a') and heating the                sorbent material in unit (1) to temperature of                50-180 degrees C in a desorption step, and                extracting at least the desorbed gaseous carbon                dioxide and desorbed water vapor or desorbed                gaseous carbon dioxide and water vapor originating                from injected steam from the unit (1) and                separating gaseous carbon dioxide from water vapor                by water condensation downstream of the unit (1),                (c) actively cooling the adsorber structure (2)                with sorbent material in unit (1) to a temperature                more than or equal to ambient atmospheric                temperature and re-pressurization of the unit to                ambient atmospheric pressure condition.
   USE - Separation of gaseous carbon dioxide or water                from gas mixture such as flue gas, exhaust gas,                industrial waste gas, biogas, atmospheric air by                cyclic adsorption/desorption using sorbent material                adsorbing gaseous carbon dioxide, and device                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables efficient separation of                gaseous carbon dioxide or water from gas mixture.                The device is capable of improving the energy                efficiency of a cyclic adsorption and desorption                process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Separation of gaseous carbon dioxide or water                from gas mixture involves (a) contacting the gas                mixture with sorbent material to allow at least                gaseous carbon dioxide with or without water vapor                to adsorb on the sorbent material under ambient                atmospheric pressure of 0.8-1.2 bar (a') in an                adsorption step, (b) evacuating the unit (1) to a                pressure of 20-400 mbar (a') and heating the                sorbent material in unit (1) to temperature of                50-180 degrees C in a desorption step, and                extracting at least the desorbed gaseous carbon                dioxide and desorbed water vapor or desorbed                gaseous carbon dioxide and water vapor originating                from injected steam from the unit (1) and                separating gaseous carbon dioxide from water vapor                by water condensation downstream of the unit (1),                (c) actively cooling the adsorber structure (2)                with sorbent material in unit (1) to a temperature                more than or equal to ambient atmospheric                temperature and re-pressurization of the unit to                ambient atmospheric pressure condition. (i) In the                step (c) at least component of the heat released by                the adsorber structure (2) with the sorbent                material in the unit (1) during cooling is                recovered and stored in a heat storage device (1a)                and used for other process steps in unit (1) or in                at least one different but equivalent unit operated                in the same manner in parallel, and (ii) during                step (b) at least component of at least one of the                sensible and latent heat of gaseous carbon dioxide                and water vapor extracted from the unit (1) is                recovered and stored in a heat storage device (1b)                and used for other process steps in the unit (1) or                in at least one different but equivalent unit                operated in the same manner in parallel, and (iii)                during step (b) at least component of the heat                required for heating the sorbent material in the                unit (1) is supplied from heat recovered in at                least one above step of the unit (1) and/or at                least one different but equivalent unit operated in                the same manner in parallel and at least one of (i)                or (ii) is implemented, as well as (iii). The gas                mixture comprises at least one air, flue gas and                biogas, and gaseous carbon dioxide and water vapor                as well as further gases different from gaseous                carbon dioxide and water vapor. The method using at                least one unit (1) comprises an adsorber structure                (2) with sorbent material. The unit (1) is openable                to flow-through of the gas mixture and for                contacting the gas mixture with the sorbent                material for the adsorption step, evacuable to a                vacuum pressure of 500 mbar (a') or less, and the                adsorber structure (2) located in the unit (1)                which is heatable to a temperature of at least 50                degrees C for the desorption of at least gaseous                carbon dioxide or carbon dioxide and water                vapor.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows schematic view of general                energy management scheme showing possible energy                flows for recovery and usage within one                unit.
   Feed heat transfer fluid (1)
   Adsorber structure (2)
   Cold feed heat transfer fluid (3)
   Heat transfer fluid (4)
   Desorption gas (5)

   An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method                for preparing the catalyst, involving (a)                dissolving palladium salt and copper salt in water,                and mixing to obtain a precursor solution, and (b)                dissolving the reducing agent in water, mixing with                the precursor solution to carry out a co-reduction                reaction, and post-processing to obtain the                catalyst.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the carbon dioxide                  electroreduction catalyst, metal copper is                  embedded in the crystal lattice of metal                  palladium. The molar ratio of the reducing agent to water is                  1:(400-700). The ratio of the amount of the                  reducing agent to the molar amount of the total                  metal in the palladium salt and the copper salt                  is (4.5-7):1.Preferred Method: The step (a) involves                  dissolving the palladium salt and copper salt in                  water, mixing, adding surfactant, and stirring                  completely to dissolve to obtain the precursor                  solution, where the surfactant is polyether F127,                  and the molar ratio of the palladium salt to                  surfactant is 1:(0.05-0.1).
