PN CN109432008-A
TI Preparing nanoliposome comprises cooling carbon dioxide gas, pressurizing, adding e.g. lipid material and encapsulated fat-soluble substance, stirring, heating, adding aqueous solvent, flowing the fluid, releasing fluid and discharging.
AU WANG H
   PAN L
   GU K
   XIE W
   ZHANG P
   LI Z
AE UNIV HENAN TECHNOLOGY (UYHY-C)
GA 201925042W
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing nanoliposome comprises (i) cooling carbon dioxide gas to less than 0 degrees C and pressurizing to 4-6 MPa to obtain liquid carbon dioxide, (ii) adding the lipid material and the encapsulated fat-soluble substance into a high-pressure reaction kettle, injecting liquid carbon dioxide, stirring uniformly, heating to 30 degrees C, (iii) adding an aqueous solvent and an encapsulated water-soluble substance to a absorption tank, opening a high pressure reactor valve, flowing the fluid in the high pressure reaction kettle through the nozzle into the absorbing tank to form atomized fluid, (iv) releasing the fluid in high pressure reaction kettle completely and discharging the absorbed liquid in the absorption tank.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing nanoliposome.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: comprises carbon dioxide, which is non-toxic, volatile and difficult to produce; comprises and ethanol, which can volatilized with carbon dioxide in the absorption tank to the content is not greater than 0.1%.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The lipid material and the encapsulated fat-soluble substance in the step (ii) is carried out by dissolving in absolute ethanol, where the amount of lipid material to absolute ethanol is 1:0-100. Preferred Components: The lipid material comprises soybean lecithin, phosphatidylserine, hydrogenated soybean phospholipid, fatty acid ester or its derivatives or cholesterol. The aqueous solvent comprises phosphate buffer solution or distilled water, where the amount of lipid material and aqueous solvent is 1:100-10000. TI Removal of ammonium perfluorooctanoate from fluoropolymer involves adding obtained solid powder sample and methanol into extraction vessel, extracting using carbon dioxide, reducing temperature and pressure, and collecting product.
AU CHEN J
   HE Q
   LIU Z
AE UNIV SHAANXI NORMAL (UNSN-C)
GA 2018508743
AB    NOVELTY - Removal of ammonium perfluorooctanoate from fluoropolymer using supercritical carbon dioxide involves carrying out sample pretreatment to remove solvent or dispersant from fluoropolymer sample containing ammonium perfluorooctanoate, vacuum drying to obtain solid powder sample, adding solid powder sample and methanol as an entrainer into the extraction vessel, introducing carbon dioxide at a flow rate of 0.1-0.5 m3/hour, preferably 0.3 m3/hour to carry out extraction at 8-20 MPa at 60-100 degrees C for 30-120 minutes, stopping introducing carbon dioxide after the product is extracted, reducing the temperature of the system to room temperature, reducing the pressure to normal pressure, and opening the extraction vessel to collect the product.

   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, environmentally-friendly, has high efficiency, low energy consumption, improved removal efficiency of ammonium perfluorooctanoate from fluoropolymer up to 92.3%, and uses supercritical carbon dioxide which is non-toxic and non-polluting.

PN CN111285628-A; CN111285628-B
TI Performing comprehensive utilization process of low-grade magnesite involves using high-temperature gas from reduction furnace as combustible gas in flash kiln, and mixing gas of temperature-reduced flue gas and oxygen.
AU WANG X
   WU J
   DENG S
AE WANG X (WANG-Individual)
   WU J (WUJJ-Individual)
   DENG S (DENG-Individual)
GA 202056491P
AB    NOVELTY - Performing comprehensive utilization process of low-grade magnesite involves using high-temperature gas from the reduction furnace as combustible gas in the flash kiln. The mixed gas of temperature-reduced flue gas and oxygen from the carbon dioxide storage tank is used as a combustion aid. The low-grade magnesite is roasted, after coarse crushing, fine crushing and sieving in order to obtain light burnt powder and high temperature flue gas, and send high temperature flue gas to the waste heat boiler. The lightly burnt powder is send with magnesium oxide content of (90%, 96%), which is pressed into a spherical shape, to a mid-range kiln for calcination. The light burned powder with magnesium oxide content (85%, 90%) is sent to the soda ash process. The light burning powder with magnesium oxide content (70%, 85%) is transported to the production process of light magnesium carbonate.
   USE - Method for performing comprehensive utilization process of low-grade magnesite.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to perform comprehensive utilization process of low-grade magnesite which has high product quality, very low pollution, very high energy saving degree, effective use of low-grade mineral resources, and huge economic benefits, and can be widely used in the field of minerals.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Performing comprehensive utilization process of low-grade magnesite involves using high-temperature gas from the reduction furnace as combustible gas in the flash kiln. The mixed gas of temperature-reduced flue gas and oxygen from the carbon dioxide storage tank is used as a combustion aid. The low-grade magnesite is roasted, after coarse crushing, fine crushing and sieving in order to obtain light burnt powder and high temperature flue gas, and send high temperature flue gas to the waste heat boiler. The lightly burnt powder is send with magnesium oxide content of (90%, 96%), which is pressed into a spherical shape, to a mid-range kiln for calcination. The light burned powder with magnesium oxide content (85%, 90%) is sent to the soda ash process. The light burning powder with magnesium oxide content (70%, 85%) is transported to the production process of light magnesium carbonate. The light burned powder with magnesium oxide content less than 70 wt.% is transported as waste slag to the wave magnesium board process. The high-temperature gas from the reduction furnace is used as a combustible gas in the mid-range kiln, and the mixed gas of cooling flue gas and oxygen from a carbon dioxide storage tank is used as a combustion aid. The lightly burned powder is calcined with magnesium oxide content of (90%, 96%) and pressing into a spherical shape to obtain high-temperature high-density magnesium oxide balls. The cooling flue gas from the carbon dioxide storage tank is used to pass to cool the high-temperature high-density magnesium oxide ball and output the cooled high-density magnesium oxide ball. The carbon dioxide is generated by the combustion of the heat-absorbing cooling flue gas. The combustible gas and combustion-supporting gas is sent to the reduction furnace in the reduction furnace. The carbon dioxide from the mid-range kiln, the temperature-absorbing flue gas that absorbs heat, and the blue charcoal, steam, and oxygen are injected into the reduction furnace undergo a reduction reaction to generate high-temperature gas. The portion of the high-temperature gas is sent to the mid-range kiln and flash kiln, and portion of the high-temperature gas is sent to the waste heat boiler. The high-temperature gas from the reduction furnace and the high-temperature flue gas from the flash kiln exchange heat with the water in the waste heat boiler. The water in the waste heat boiler is turned into high-pressure steam, and the high-pressure steam drives the generator set to generate electricity, after high-temperature gas exchanges heat with the water in the waste heat boiler, it flowed out from the waste heat boiler, and continued to exchange heat with other media to obtain temperature-reduced gas. The cooling gas is pressurized by the pressurizing machine and sent to the gas storage tank, after the heat exchange between the high-temperature flue gas and the water in the waste heat boiler, it flowed out from the waste heat boiler and continues to exchange heat with other media to obtain the temperature-reduced flue gas. The cooling flue gas is pressurized by the pressurizing machine and then sent to the carbon dioxide gas storage tank. The portion of the cooling flue gas in the carbon dioxide storage tank is also used as the raw gas for the production of soda ash and the production of light magnesium carbonate. The portion of the cooling flue gas is sent to the flash kiln and mid-range kiln, and the other section of the cooling flue gas is supplied to the breeding base.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The particle size of low-grade magnesite is (0 mm, 0.5 mm). Preferred Compositions: The high-temperature gas and the temperature-lowering gas are all mixed gas of carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, hydrogen, and impurity gas, and the volume content of carbon monoxide is 55 wt.%, the volume content of carbon dioxide is 29 wt.%, the volume content of hydrogen is 13 wt.%, and the impurity gas The volume content is 3 wt.%. Preferred Process: The lightly burnt powder with insufficient roasting in the flash kiln falls into the storage bin at the bottom of the flash kiln, and decomposed by its own residual heat for 5 hours and then discharged. The fully roasted light-burning powder and high-temperature flue gas are sent to a high-temperature cyclone separator together. The high-temperature cyclone separates the fully roasted light-burning powder from the high-temperature flue gas to obtain the fully roasted light-burning powder with a particle size greater than 200 mesh. The calcined lightly burned powder that has not been fully entered the intermediate storage tank, and then is decomposed by its own residual heat for 4 hours and then discharged. The density of the high-density magnesia ball is 3.9 tons/cubic meter. The method for comprehensive utilization of low-grade magnesite. The flash kiln The calcined powder is obtained by roasting includes light calcined powder with sufficient calcination and light calcined powder with insufficient calcination.

PT P
PN CN113135815-A
TI Controlling methanol synthesis comprises reacting raw material gas (hydrogen, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide) under the conditions of methanol synthesis to obtain methanol and exhaust gas.
AU LU L
   LIU H
   LEI C
   ZHANG Y
   JIANG X
   FENG C
   ZHAO T
AE CHINA SHENGHUA COAL TO LIQUID CHEM CO (SHGR-C)
   GUONENG YULIN CHEM CO LTD (GUON-Non-standard)
GA 202191370M
AB    NOVELTY - Controlling methanol synthesis comprises reacting the raw material gas under the conditions of methanol synthesis to obtain methanol and exhaust gas, where the raw material gas and exhaust gas each independently comprises hydrogen, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide, and the setting adjustment coefficient A satisfies the following formula: as given in the formula of the specification.
   USE - The method is useful for controlling methanol synthesis.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: accurately adjusts the ratio of hydrogen to carbon oxide in the methanol synthesis process; ensures stable and efficient operation of methanol synthesis; and greatly improves the methanol selection rate.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for methanol synthesis control device.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The real-time flow rate of hydrogen, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide in the raw material gas is independently dependent on the real-time flow rate of raw material gas and real-time component content of hydrogen, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide in the raw material gas. The real-time flow rate of hydrogen in the feed gas Qhydrogen is equal to Qx Chydrogen, where the Q is real-time flow rate of feed gas, and the CH2 is real-time component content of hydrogen in the feed gas. The real-time flow rate of carbon dioxide in the raw material gas Qcarbon dioxide is equal to Qx Ccarbon dioxide, where the Q is real-time flow rate of feed gas, and the Ccarbon dioxide is real-time component content of carbon dioxide in the feed gas. The real-time flow of carbon monoxide in the feed gas Qcarbon monoxide is equal to Qx Ccarbon monoxide, where the Q is the real-time flow rate of feed gas, and the Ccarbon monoxide is the real-time component content of carbon monoxide in the feed gas. The real-time flow of carbon monoxide in the feed gas Qcarbon monoxide is equal to Qx Ccarbon monoxidex alpha 1, where the alpha 1 is first correction factor, i.e. 0.95-1.05. The first correction factor alpha 1 is equal to C standard, carbon monoxide/Ccarbon monoxide, where the C mark, carbon monoxide is the standard real-time component content of carbon monoxide in the feed gas. The real-time flow rate of hydrogen in the feed gas Qhydrogen is equal to Qx Chydrogenx alpha 2, where the alpha 2 is second correction factor, i.e. 0.95-1.05. The second correction factor alpha 2 is equal to (Chydrogen + Ccarbon monoxide-C standard, carbon monoxide)/Chydrogen, where the Chydrogen is real-time component content of hydrogen in the feed gas, the Ccarbon monoxide is the real-time component content of carbon monoxide in the feed gas, C mark, and the carbon monoxide is the standard real-time component content of carbon monoxide in the feed gas. The real-time flow rate of hydrogen, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide in the exhaust gas is independently dependent on the real-time flow rate of exhaust gas and real-time component content of hydrogen, carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide in the exhaust gas. The real-time flow rate of hydrogen in the exhaust gas is Q'hydrogen is equal to Q'x C'hydrogen, where the Q' is the real-time flow of exhaust gas, and the C'hydrogen is the real-time component content of hydrogen in the exhaust gas. The real-time flow of carbon dioxide in the exhaust gas Q'carbon dioxide is equal to Q'x C'carbon dioxide, where the Q' is the real-time flow of exhaust gas, and the C'carbon dioxide is the real-time component content of carbon dioxide in the exhaust gas. The real-time flow of carbon monoxide in exhaust gas Q'carbon monoxide is equal to Q'x C'carbon monoxide, where the Q' is the real-time flow of exhaust gas, and the C'carbon monoxide is the real-time component content of carbon monoide in the exhaust gas.

PN CN112403475-A
TI Preparing a catalyst useful for reforming carbon            dioxide to syngas comprises synthesizing            nickel-iron-lanthanum-calcium-aluminum hydrotalcite            precursor by co-precipitation with nickel and iron as            active phase and lanthanum oxide.
AU SUN Y
   WANG H
   MA C
   DU Y
   ZHANG L
AE SHANGHAI CURUI LOW-CARBON ENERGY TECHNOL (SHAN-Non-standard)
GA 202123703D
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing a catalyst comprises synthesizing                nickel-iron-lanthanum-calcium-aluminum HTLcs                hydrotalcite precursor by co-precipitation method                with nickel and iron as the active phase and                lanthanum oxide as the auxiliary agent, and                performing calcination, nickel-iron/lanthanum                oxide/calcium aluminate catalyst.
   USE - The a catalyst is useful for reforming carbon                dioxide to syngas.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: improves the stability and carbon                deposition resistance of the catalyst by reducing                the crystal grain size of the active component,                enhances the interaction of the carrier metal, and                improves the dispersibility of the metal. The                catalyst has high dispersion, high activity and                good stability.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method specifically                  comprises (1) adding the mixed solution of                  nickel(II) nitrate hexahydrate crystals,                  iron(III) nitrate nonahydrate, lanthanum(III)                  nitrate hexahydrate, calcium nitrate tetrahydrate                  and aluminum nitrate nonahydrate to the solution                  containing in the sodium carbonate solution of                  surfactant, adding sodium hydroxide to the sodium                  carbonate solution for reaction, and continuing                  the reaction, filtering and washing with                  deionized water until the pH of the filtrate is                  neutral, filtering, drying the obtained filter                  cake to obtain                  nickel-iron-lanthanum-calcium-aluminum HTLcs                  hydrotalcite precursor, and (2) placing the                  nickel-iron-lanthanum-calcium-aluminum HTLcs                  hydrotalcite precursor in a muffle furnace, and                  performing calcination to obtain an oxidation                  precursor catalyst, which is denoted as                  nickel-iron/lanthanum oxide/calcium aluminate.                  The reaction temperature is 70 degrees C, and the                  pH value is 9-11, and, the reaction was continued                  at 70 degrees C for 24 hours. In the step (2),                  the calcination temperature is 700 degrees C, and                  the time is 5 hours. in the method, after the                  lanthanum oxide/lanthanum oxide/ calcium                  aluminate is reduced, using in the carbon dioxide                  hydrocarbon reforming reaction to prepare                  synthesis gas. The feed gas for the carbon                  dioxide hydrocarbon reforming reaction includes                  carbon dioxide, methane, ethane and propane, the                  reaction temperature is 800-900 degrees C, and                  the pressure is 0.01-1 MPa. The molar ratio of                  carbon dioxide to the sum of moles of methane,                  ethane and propanein the feed gas is 0.9-1.3:1,                  and the sum of the volume percentages of ethane                  and propane in the feed gas is 0.1-10%. Preferred                  Components: In the step (1), the ratio of the                  molar sum of aluminum, iron and lanthanum ions to                  the molar sum of nickel, calcium, aluminum, iron                  and lanthanum ion is 0.25-0.33:1. The molar ratio                  of iron ions to nickel ions is 0.2-0.6:1. The                  molar ratio of lanthanum ion to nickel ions is                  0.1-0.3:1, and the molar ratio of sodium                  carbonate to the sum of the moles of aluminum,                  iron and lanthanum ions is 4:1. The surfactant is                  tetrapropylammonium hydroxide, and the molar                  ratio of the tetrapropylammonium hydroxide to the                  sum of the moles of nickel, calcium, aluminum,                  iron and lanthanum ion is 0.01-0.05:1. The                  reaction is completed under stirring conditions                  of 400 revolutions per minute. The adding speed                  of the mixed solution of nickel(II) nitrate                  hexahydrate crystals, iron(III) nitrate                  nonahydrate, lanthanum(III) nitrate hexahydrate,                  calcium nitrate tetrahydrate and aluminum nitrate                  is 2.5 ml/minutes. The addition rate of sodium                  hydroxide is 3 ml/minutes. The filter cake                  obtained is dried at 90 degrees C for 2                  hours.

PN CN112226560-A; CN112226560-B
TI Method for reducing content of titanium nitride and/or titanium carbide in slag, involves using raw material containing water and/or carbon dioxide to perform oxidation treatment to slag, and obtaining reacted melt.
AU GUO Y
   WANG Y
   LI D
   PEI Z
   CHEN X
   XU X
   GAO Y
AE CHINA ENFI ENG CORP (CMEG-C)
GA 202109211C
AB    NOVELTY - Reduction of content of titanium nitride and/or titanium carbide in slag comprises smelting vanadium-titanium magnetite in raw material containing water and/or carbon dioxide to obtain reacted melt.
   USE - Method for reducing titanium nitride and/or titanium carbide content in slag.
   ADVANTAGE - The method effectively reduces the content of titanium carbide and/or titanium nitride in the slag, is economical and environmentally-friendly, reduces the base of the titanium carbide and/or titanium nitride content in the slag, avoids the waste of iron, reduces the cost and improves the comprehensive recovery rate of the iron.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The ratio of the mol of the water and/or carbon dioxide to the mol of the titanium carbide and/or titanium nitride in the slag is 2:1-6: 1. The ratio of the mol of the water and/or carbon dioxide to the mol of the titanium carbide and/or titanium nitride is 4:1-6: 1. The limestone is limestone powder or limestone stone. Preferred Method: The raw material is injected into the slag to realize the oxidation treatment, preferably the raw material is sprayed into the middle upper part of the slag. The spraying is carried out by means of top blowing or side blowing. The raw material is smelting flue gas, preferably, the smelting flue gas is the flue gas generated in the smelting process of the vanadium-titanium magnetite. The raw material is water and/or carbon dioxide, adding a slagging agent during the smelting process of the vanadium-titanium magnetite, preferably the slagging agent comprises limestone and limestone is decomposed into calcium oxide and carbon dioxide during the smelting of the vanadium-titanium magnetite, the carbon dioxide serves carbon dioxide for performing the oxidation treatment on the slag. The carbon monoxide and/or hydrogen are generated in the oxidation treatment process, and the used as fuel gas or reducing agent in the process of smelting vanadium titanium magnetite.

PN CN111764156-A; CN111764156-B
TI Preparing polyimide fibers, comprises e.g. soaking polyimide fibers in an aprotic polar solvent, then soaking in a solution containing carbon nanoparticles, processing by supercritical carbon dioxide treatment, and then post-processing.
AU ZHANG Q
   FANG Y
   DONG J
   ZHAO X
AE UNIV DONGHUA (UYDG-C)
GA 2020A20249
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing polyimide fibers, comprises (i) placing polyimide fibers in an aprotic polar solvent for soaking treatment, and (ii) soaking the polyimide fibers in a solution containing carbon nanoparticles, then processing by supercritical carbon dioxide treatment to obtain carbon nanoparticle/polyimide fiber composite material, and then post-processing.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing polyimide fibers.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: improves mechanical properties, heat resistance and dimensional stability of the polyimide fibers; improves the microstructure of the fiber surface; and improves the interface bonding force between the fibers and the resin matrix.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for polyimide fibers prepared by the above method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: In step (i), the polyimide fibers are prepared by adding diamine and dianhydride monomers to an aprotic polar solvent, reacting to obtain polyamic acid solution, then preparing polyamic acid spun yarn by wet spinning or dry jet wet spinning, and thermal cyclizing to obtain polyimide fibers. In step (i), the soaking treatment temperature is 30-100 degrees C, and the soaking treatment time is 2-36 hours. In step (ii): the soaking time is 0.5-24 hours; the temperature of supercritical carbon dioxide treatment is 20-200 degrees C, the pressure is 8-30 MPa, and the treatment time is 0.5-24 hours; and the post-processing is carried out under temperature of 250-500 degrees C and pressure of 0-1 GPa for 1-150 minutes, where the draft multiple is 1-7 times. Preferred Components: The aprotic polar solvent in step (i) is N-methylpyrrolidone and/or dimethylacetamide. The carbon nanoparticles in step (ii) are nanoparticles having surface functionalized with amino or carboxy groups, where the solvent of the carbon nanoparticles solution is acetone, and the concentration of the carbon nanoparticles solution is 1-10 mg/ml. The mass content of the carbon nanoparticles in the carbon nanoparticle/polyimide fiber composite material is 0.01-1%.

TI Composite catalyst used for cathode for electrochemical cell, comprises catalyst layers comprising polycrystalline base made of copper, in which one layer having preferred orientation is positioned on other layer.
AU JI K

AE LG ELECTRONICS INC (GLDS-C)
GA 2019685958
AB    NOVELTY - A composite catalyst comprises a catalyst layer (A) comprising polycrystalline base made of copper having (100) plane in preferred orientation and catalyst layer (B) comprising a polycrystalline layer made of copper positioned on the catalyst layer (A).
   USE - Composite catalyst used for cathode for electrochemical cell for decomposing carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The composite catalyst effectively produces 2C or more hydrocarbons and has excellent carbon dioxide conversion efficiency, productivity and economic efficiency, and the preparation is also simple.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for production of composite catalyst, which involves preparing copper raw material constituting the layer (A), deforming copper raw material, forming catalyst layer (B), and annealing.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The catalyst layer (B) has a dot shaped zero-dimensional structure in the form of wire or needle, one-dimensional structure in the form of thin film or column and two-dimensional structure in the form of island. The complex catalyst comprises at least one element chosen from oxygen, nitrogen or hydrogen. The complex catalyst comprises copper oxide, copper nitride, copper oxynitride, or copper hydride. Preferred Method: The production of composite catalyst involves primary processing step of dynamically recrystallizing the copper raw material and the secondary processing step involves plating, printing and deposition. The machining step (A) is multipass rolling. Preferred Properties: The fraction of (100) plane in copper polycrystalline base is 30% or more. The catalyst layer (B) has a preferred orientation of (111) or (110). The catalyst has a surface structure in which a height difference or a step is formed between the catalyst layers (A, B).

PN CN109569517-A
TI Preparing solid amine adsorbent comprises e.g. dissolving tetraethyl orthosilicate in mixed solution of alcohols, stirring and heating, adding organic amine into above solution, turning on ultrasonic irradiation device, and aging gel.
AU MAO S
   HUANG H
   CHEN X
   JIANG Y
   WANG D
AE CHINA PETROLEUM & CHEM CORP (SNPC-C)
   NCIG RES INST (NCIG-Non-standard)
GA 201933619P
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing solid amine adsorbent comprises (i) dissolving tetraethyl orthosilicate in mixed solution of alcohols, stirring and heating, (ii) adding organic amine into the above solution, and continue mechanical stirring, (iii) turning on the ultrasonic irradiation device, and then adding distilled water, and (iv) maintaining for few hours to form transparent gel, turning off ultrasound and aging gel at room temperature to obtain final product.
   USE - The solid amine adsorbent is useful for producing carbon-dioxide adsorbent.
   ADVANTAGE - The method produces excellent carbon-dioxide adsorbent having large specific surface area, high activity, excellent carbon trapping performance and high recycling frequency.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The ultrasonic frequency is 10-50 kHz. In step (i), the mass ratio between mixture of tetraethyl orthosilicate and alcohol is 0.1-1:8, and the heating temperature is 30-120 degrees C. The step (i) comprises heating mixed solution of tetraethyl orthosilicate and alcohol at 50-80 degrees C. In step (iv), reaction temperature is 30-120 degrees C (preferably 50-80 degrees C), and the reaction time is 1-10 hours. The ultrasonic frequency used is any low frequency ultrasound having frequency of 10-100 kHz; ultrasonic intensity is 0.01-15 W/m-2; ultrasonic irradiation time is 2-12 hours; ultrasonic continuous processing process uses ultrasonic sound intensity of 0.1-2 W/m-2 . Preferred Components: The mixed solution of the alcohol is methanol, ethanol, propanol, and/or ethylene glycol. In step (ii), organic amine is diisopropanolamine (DIPA), isopropylaminoethanol (IPAE), monoethanolamine (MEA), diethanolamine (DEA), methyl monoethanolamine (MMEA), N-methyldiethanolamine (MDEA), 2-amino-2-methyl-1-propanol (AMP), hydroxyethylpiperazine (HEPZ) and/or morpholine (MOR). In step (iii), ultrasonic irradiation device includes ultrasonic generator and ultrasonic transducer; ultrasonic transducer is piezoelectric ceramic ultrasonic transducer or magnetostrictive ultrasonic transducer; and ultrasonic generator is probe or trough ultrasonic processor.

TI Catalyst useful for dry reforming methane comprises nickel nanoparticles supported on a hollow fiber substrate.
AU LIANG X
   SHANG Z
   LI S
AE GAS TECHNOLOGY INST (GASE-C)
   UNIV MISSOURI (UMOR-C)
   LIANG X (LIAN-Individual)
   SHANG Z (SHAN-Individual)
   LI S (LISS-Individual)
   GAS TECHNOLOGY INST (GASE-C)
   UNIV MISSOURI (UMOR-C)
GA 201898657R
AB    NOVELTY - Catalyst comprises nickel nanoparticles supported on a hollow fiber substrate.
   USE - The catalyst is useful for dry reforming methane (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The catalyst: facilitates dry reforming which produces syngas having hydrogen/carbon monoxide ratio of not greater than 0.95, preferably 0.7-0.95 (claimed); exhibits improved catalytic activity and stability; contains increased geometrical surface area; and is economical.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included for:
   (1) reforming methane, comprising contacting methane and carbon dioxide in the presence of the catalyst;
   (2) dry reforming methane, comprising introducing methane and carbon dioxide into a reactor containing a packed bed of many hollow fiber substrate supports carrying nickel nanoparticles; and
   (3) producing the catalyst, comprising depositing nickel nanoparticles onto the hollow fiber substrate support by atomic layer deposition.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The hollow fiber substrate comprises alumina, preferably alpha -alumina. The nickel nanoparticles are 2-6 nm in size. The nickel nanoparticles are deposited onto the hollow fiber substrate by atomic layer deposition. The catalyst further comprises: an overcoat of a promoter to increase catalyst performance in reforming of methane, where the promoter is alumina, cerium(IV) oxide, calcium oxide or lanthanum oxide; and an alumina atomic layer deposition overcoat as a promoter to increase catalyst performance in reforming of methane. The catalyst comprises multiple cycles of alumina atomic layer deposition overcoat. The nickel nanoparticles are: nanoparticles comprising nickel and cobalt bimetallic nanoparticles, nickel and platinum bimetallic nanoparticles, or only nickel nanoparticles; or preferably neat nickel nanoparticles. The alpha -alumina hollow fiber supports carrying nickel nanoparticles include the overcoat of the promoter and an alumina atomic layer deposition overcoat as the promoter. Preferred Method: The method further comprises applying at least one layer of a metal oxide coating over the nickel nanoparticles on the alpha -alumina hollow fiber substrate support, where the metal oxide coating increases catalyst performance in reforming of methane.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

   LI R
AE TIANJIN NAER BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (TIAN-Non-standard)
GA 2018A0494M
AB    NOVELTY - Extraction of plant essential oil comprises selecting berries, roots, stem, or leaves of Pimenta dioica, weighing, and cleaning; crushing plant tissue of P. dioica into 50-70 meshes and sieving; cold-pressing crushed plant tissue 1-2 times and passing through 15-25-mesh sieve; and placing cold-pressed plant tissue in extraction tank, introducing supercritical carbon dioxide (CO2), continuously extracting at certain temperature and pressure, and combining essential oils obtained by cold-pressing and extraction.
   USE - Method for extracting plant essential oil.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has high raw material utilization rate and low production cost. The produced essential oil has high purity.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The method includes crushing plant tissue of P. dioica into 60 meshes; cold-pressing at not greater than 60 degrees C and passing through 20-mesh sieve; and subjecting to supercritical CO2 extraction at 30-40 MPa and 15-25 degrees C for 2-5 hours. The supercritical CO2 flow rate is 20-25 L/minute.

PN CN108485806-A
TI Preparation of refined silkworm pupa oil used e.g. for preventing and treating hyperlipemia by subjecting silkworm pupa oil to supercritical carbon dioxide extraction using entrainer containing ethyl acetate and ofloxacin ethanol solution.
AU CHEN G
   XU T
   WANG X
   ZHANG L
   HU Y
   ZUO C
   XU D
   CAO C
   HU R
   ZHANG X
   YAN F
AE ZHEJIANG ESIGMA BIOTECHNOLOGY CO LTD (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 201871275A
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of refined silkworm pupa oil comprises putting silkworm pupa oil in supercritical carbon dioxide (CO2) device, performing supercritical CO2 extraction using entrainer containing 5-10% ethyl acetate and 0.01-0.02% ofloxacin ethanol solution, collecting raffinate, and obtaining primary refined oil.
   USE - Preparation of refined silkworm pupa oil used for preventing and treating hyperlipemia and non-alcoholic fatty liver and promoting liver regeneration.
   ADVANTAGE - The method has simple manufacturing technique, high automation degree, and little organic solvent residue, is capable of quickly extracting very high content of alpha -polyunsaturated fatty acid linolenic acid, and obtains natural unsaturated fatty acid fragrance.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The supercritical CO2 extraction is conducted at 35-40 degrees C and 30-40 MPa for 0.6-1.0 hour, where separating kettle I has pressure of 10-12 MPa and temperature of 40-45 degrees C and separating kettle II has pressure of 5-7 MPa and temperature of 15-20 degrees C. The ofloxacin comprises 80-90% levofloxacin and 10-20% dextrofloxacin.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Method: The method further comprises gradient freezing and crystallizing by adding primary refined oil with 20-30 mass% carbamide ethanol solution at 1:7-12 mass ratio, uniformly mixing, gradient freezing and crystallizing, filtering, adding the filtrate with hydrochloric acid (HCl) solution, acidifying until pH is 2-3, extracting with petroleum ether, separating the petroleum ether phase, using distilled water to wash the petroleum ether phase to neutral, drying the petroleum ether phase, decompressing and distilling, and recycling the petroleum ether to prepare the polyunsaturated fatty acid of silkworm pupa oil. The gradient freeze drying is conducted at 8-10 degrees C for 2.5-3 hours, 4-5 degrees C for 1.5-2 hours, 1-1.5 degrees C for 0.8-1 hour, - 2 to - 1.5 degrees C for 0.5-0.8 hour, - 4 to - 3.5 degrees C for 0.5-0.8 hour, - 6 to - 5.5 degrees C for 0.5-0.8 hour, - 8 to - 7.5 degrees C for 0.5-0.8 hour and - 12 to - 11.5 degrees C for 0.5-0.8 hour. The method further comprises charging nitrogen and standing the polyunsaturated fatty acid of silkworm pupa oil for 5-8 days to obtain the finished product oil.
   ACTIVITY - Antilipemic; Hepatotropic.
DC B05 (Other organics - aromatics, aliphatic, organo-metallics, compounds whose substituents vary such that they would be classified in several of B01 - B05.); B04 (Natural products and polymers. The control circuit board controls the air bottle to close and an exhaust valve to operate. The machine body is connected with a pressing rod. The electric control trigger unit turns-on the control switch of the air bottle and the control switch of an exhaust valve by controlling the pressing rod.
   USE - Carbon dioxide dissolving degree controllable bubble water machine.
   ADVANTAGE - The machine has simple structure, and adjusts a dissolved carbon dioxide concentration value in the water bottle in an accurate manner, dissolves a multi-gear control corresponding water bottle with different concentrations of carbon dioxide and exhausts redundant carbon dioxide in the air bottle in a rapid manner after adjusting carbon dioxide concentration, and has convenient and rapid operation, and satisfies requirement of different people.

AU ZHAO X

   YAN L

GA 202137204E
AB    NOVELTY - Flexible metal organic frame material                comprises a metal compound (I).
   USE - The organic frame material is useful for                separating mixed gas of acetylene and carbon                dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The organic frame material has high adsorption                ratio of acetylene/carbon dioxide, high adsorption                selectivity and high efficiency, adopts easily                available raw materials, is cost-effective, and                saves energy saving.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Flexible metal organic frame material                comprises a metal compound of formula: (M-L1-L2)n                (I).
   N = positive integer.
   where metal ion, organic ligand (L1) and                organic ligand (L2) are constructed through                coordination bonds. INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included                for the following:
   (1) a method for preparing the flexible metal                organic frame material, involving (a) mixing                deionized aqueous solution containing metal ion,                organic ligand (L2) and sodium hydroxide with                deionized aqueous solution containing organic                ligand (L1), and stirring to react to obtain a blue                powder and (b) using the solvent exchange method to                exchange the blue powder obtained in step (a) in                methanol several times, placing at room temperature                for 1 day, finally reaching 10-7 mbar in a vacuum                state and keeping unchanged, and sieving acetylene                carbon dioxide to obtain the product; and
   (2) a method for separating acetylene and                carbon dioxide mixed gas, involves contacting the                mixed gas of acetylene and carbon dioxide with the                flexible metal-organic frame material to                selectively adsorb acetylene molecules to separate                acetylene from carbon dioxide.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The metal ion is                  copper ion. The organic ligand (L1) is                  2-aminopyrazine. The organic ligand (L2) is                  pyrazine-2,3-dicarboxylic acid. Preferred                  Composition: The ratio of the amount of the metal                  ion, the organic ligand (L1), the organic ligand                  (L2), and sodium hydroxide is 1:12:1:(1.5-3). The                  volume ratio of the deionized aqueous solution                  containing metal ion, organic ligand (L1) and                  sodium hydroxide to the deionized aqueous                  solution containing organic ligand (L2) is                  1:1.

PN US2019150474-A1; WO2019104169-A1; US10729156-B2
TI Purifying exosomes comprises subjecting whey            composition to first, second and third ultrafiltration            to yield first, second and third permeates and            retentates respectively and drying third permeate to            yield exosome powder.
AU PATEL H A
   STEELMAN S
   GNANASAMBANDAM R
   TRIBLE B R
   BURKE N C
AE PURINA ANIMAL NUTRITION LLC (PURI-Non-standard)
   PURINA ANIMAL NUTRITION LLC (PURI-Non-standard)
   PURINA ANIMAL NUTRITION LLC (PURI-Non-standard)
GA 201944904U
AB    NOVELTY - Purifying exosomes comprises subjecting a whey                composition to a first ultrafiltration yielding a                first permeate and a first retentate, subjecting                the first retentate to a second ultrafiltration                yielding a second permeate and a second retentate,                during the second ultrafiltration, treating the                first retentate with carbon dioxide, subjecting the                second retentate to a third ultrafiltration                yielding a third permeate and a third retentate and                drying drying the third permeate to yield an                exosome powder.
   USE - The method is useful for purifying                exosomes.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                system for purifying exosome comprising a first                ultrafiltration apparatus configured to subject a                whey composition to the first ultrafiltration, a                second ultrafiltration apparatus configured to                subject the first retentate to the second                ultrafiltration, a carbon dioxide injector coupled                with the second ultrafiltration apparatus, the                carbon dioxide injector configured to inject carbon                dioxide into the second ultrafiltration apparatus,                a third ultrafiltration apparatus configured to                subject the second retentate to the third                ultrafiltration, where the third permeate comprises                an exosome solution, a second carbon dioxide                injector coupled with the third ultrafiltration                apparatus, the second carbon dioxide injector                configured to inject carbon dioxide into the third                ultrafiltration apparatus and a drying apparatus                configured to dry the third permeate to yield an                exosome powder from the exosome solution.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOLOGY - Preferred Method: The method further                  comprises: during the third ultrafiltration,                  treating the second retentate with carbon                  dioxide; prior to subjecting a whey composition                  to a first ultrafiltration, isolating the whey                  composition from a milk product. The step of                  isolating the whey composition from a milk                  product comprises separating the milk product                  into cream and skim milk, acidifying the skim                  milk and separating the whey composition from the                  acidified skim milk. The first ultrafiltration is                  performed using a first filtration membrane                  configured to retain molecules having a molecular                  weight greater than 10 kDa. The second                  ultrafiltration is performed at 45-180 degrees F,                  a pressure of 60 psi, and a flow rate of 2                  gallons/minute. The third ultrafiltration is                  performed at 110 degrees F, a pressure of 60 psi,                  and a flow rate of about 2 gallons/minute. The                  second ultrafiltration is performed using a                  second filtration membrane configured to retain                  molecules having a molecular weight greater than                  50 kDa. The third ultrafiltration is performed                  using a third filtration membrane configured to                  retain molecules having a molecular weight                  greater than 100 kDa. The step of treating the                  first retentate with carbon dioxide comprises                  injecting the carbon dioxide into a tank                  containing the first retentate. The first                  retentate drops to a pH 5.7 as a result of                  treating the first retentate with carbon dioxide.                  Preferred Components: The milk product comprises                  whole milk or reduced fat milk. The first                  retentate comprises 34-90 wt.% protein. The third                  permeate comprises molecules having a molecular                  weight of 50-100 kDa. The second retentate                  comprises fat, high molecular weight proteins,                  and exosomes. The third permeate comprises a                  concentrated exosome solution, and drying                  comprises spray drying.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PN KR2018053161-A
TI Carbon dioxide separation and recovery system using phase separation of aqueous hydrazine solution, has regeneration tower for discharging separated and regenerated hydrazine aqueous solution and re-supplying to absorption tower.
AU YOU J K
   HONG Y K
AE KOREA INST ENERGY RES (KOER-C)
GA 201842653W
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a precipitator for phase-separating the carbon dioxide absorbing hydrazine aqueous solution supplied through a absorbent discharge line into upper and lower two phases. A first absorbent treatment line recycles the low concentration carbon dioxide absorbing hydrazine aqueous solution. A second absorbent treatment line in which a path is arranged for heat exchange in the heat exchanger with the hot hydrazine aqueous solution recovered in a regeneration tower. A regeneration tower is configured for discharging the separated and regenerated hydrazine aqueous solution and re-supplying it to the absorption tower through the heat exchanger. The ratio of the aqueous hydrazine solution to the second absorbent treatment line in the settler is not more than 50 percentage.
   USE - Carbon dioxide separation and recovery system using phase separation of aqueous hydrazine solution.
   ADVANTAGE - The upper layer aqueous solution that is capable of absorbing more carbon dioxide is recycled to the absorption tower and only the lower aqueous solution is sent to the regeneration tower, and it is possible to improve the overall absorption efficiency.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a method for recovering carbon dioxide.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a conceptual diagram illustrating a carbon dioxide separation and recovery device using a phase separation phenomenon of a hydrazine absorbent.

PN JP2022002836-A
TI Neutralization of improved soil used as e.g. banking material, involves forcibly blowing carbon dioxide gas into to-be-treated soil containing water-oxidized calcium as alkali component, contacting with carbon dioxide, and subjecting to carbonation.
AU INAMOTO Y
AE LEAF AIR KK (LEAF-Non-standard)
GA 2022182727
AB    NOVELTY - The method involves forcibly blowing (S2,S4) carbon dioxide gas supplied from a carbon-dioxide gas generation source to an improved soil containing water oxidation calcium as an alkali component. The improved soil to-be-treated contacts with the carbon dioxide gas, and subjected to carbonation reaction to produce carbonate, neutralizing alkali components contained in the treated improved soil.
   USE - Method for neutralization of improved soil comprising earth and sand improved by cement-based solid material in ground improvement work or found in banking or in agricultural field.
   ADVANTAGE - The neutralization of the improved soil is efficiently and economically enabled in short period of time. The elution of hydroxide ions and alkalinization after the neutralization process are suppressed. The neutralized treated soil is stable for long time period. The surplus soil disposal cost of improved soil such as cement improved soil and lime-improved soil is reduced. The quantity of earth and sand to be newly purchased is reduced by utilizing the neutralized treatment soil. The carbon dioxide is effectively utilized in large quantities.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows the flowchart of an improved soil neutralization method. (Drawing includes non-English language text).
   Crushing step (S1)
   Forced aeration step (S2,S4)
   Carbon dioxide gas generation step (S3)

   HASKOLI ISLANDS (HASK-Non-standard)
   HASKOLI ISLANDS (HASK-Non-standard)
GA 2019875391
AB    NOVELTY - Performing electrolytic reduction of carbon                dioxide (CO2), involves providing an electrolytic                cell comprising reaction chamber/s comprising                anode/s and cathode/s, where cathode/s comprises                catalyst surface/s comprising transition metal                oxide/s selected from hafnium oxide, osmium oxide,                rhodium oxide, chromium oxide, niobium oxide,                manganese oxide,palladium oxide, rhenium oxide,                zirconium oxide, vanadium dioxide and molybdenum                oxide. Electrolyte solution/s is/are placed between                anode/s and cathode/s, so that anode/s and                cathode/s come into contact with the electrolyte                solution, followed with providing CO2 in the                electrolyte solution. Electrical potential is                applied to the electrolytic cell, where CO2                undergoes reduction reaction/s at the cathode to                provide product/s selected from methanol, methane,                methanediol and formic acid.
   USE - Method for performing electrolytic reduction                of carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The method employs certain transition metal                oxide catalysts in the electrochemical reduction of                carbon dioxide that makes possible to produce                high-value products from carbon dioxide under                various conditions, including at ambient room                temperature and atmospheric pressure. The process                can be suitably operated using suitable electrolyte                solutions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for an                electrochemical device for the reduction of carbon                dioxide to reaction product/s selected from                methanol, methanediol, methane and formic acid,                comprises electrochemical cell/s that comprises an                anode and a cathode.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - CHEMICAL ENGINEERING - Preferred Components: The transition metal                  oxide/s is/are selected from rhodium oxide,                  chromium oxide, manganese oxide, molybdenum oxide                  and palladium oxide, more preferably rhodium                  oxide, chromium oxide, manganese oxide,                  molybdenum oxide. The catalyst surface is                  provided as a pure transition metal oxide, and                  comprises two or more transition metal oxides.                  Preferred Conditions: The method is carried out                  under ambient pressure of 1-30 atmosphere,                  preferably 5-20 atmosphere, more preferably 10-20                  atmosphere, at 0-50 degrees C, preferably 10-40                  degrees C, more preferably 20-25 degrees C. The                  catalyst surface comprises surface/s having a                  rutile structure, and at least one surface having                  a (110) facet. An electrode potential that is                  less than about -0.5 Volt, preferably -0.3 to                  -0.1 Volt using a reversible hydrogen electrode                  (RHE) as a reference, is applied to the                  electrolytic cell. Less than 50% moles hydrogen                  are formed compared to CO2 moles that are                  reduced.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN RU2674186-C1
TI Procedure for melting steel in converter involves purging with argon or carbon dioxide.
AU VANZHA G YU
   BOBYLEV G S
   KRASNOV A V
AE SEVERSTAL STOCK CO (SEVE-Non-standard)
GA 201903262X
AB    NOVELTY - Invention relates to metallurgy, in particular to the smelting of steel in a converter with a combined blowing. In the method, purging with argon or carbon dioxide is carried out in a pulsed mode with a frequency of 0.01-1.0 Hz, at the beginning of smelting, the steel is flushed from below with argon until it is consumed in the amount of 0.3-5.0 m3/t of steel, then alternate blowing of steel from below with argon and carbon dioxide is carried out. Intensity of the melt blowing with argon or carbon dioxide from the bottom through bottom tuyeres is set based on the liquid iron consumption for smelting: when the iron consumption is less than 800 kg/t of steel, the intensity of argon and carbon dioxide blowing is set at 12.0-50.0 and 5.0-30 m3/min, respectively, when cast iron consumption is more than or equal to 800 kg/t of steel, the intensity of purging with argon and carbon dioxide is set at 25.0-60.0m3/min, when cast iron consumption is more than 890 kg/t of steel, the intensity of blowing with argon and carbon dioxide is set at 30.0-80.0 m3/min.
   USE - Metallurgy.
   ADVANTAGE - Invention makes it possible to stably receive at the outlet from the converter the nitrogen content in the steel less than 0,003%.
3 cl, 1 tbl
DC M24 (Metallurgy of iron and steel - including manufacture and processing, treatment of steel melts and changing the physical properties of iron and steel, control/testing methods, blast furnaces and converters. Metallurgical coking processes (C21, C10B).)

IP C21C-005/28; C21C-005/35

PT P
PN CN111437738-A; CN111437738-B
TI Preparation of carbon dioxide separation membrane involves mixing azide-functionalized polyimide and N-methylpyrrolidone, degassing, adding e.g. cuprous bromide and methylpyrrolidone, reacting, coating resultant on glass plate, and drying.
AU LIAO J
   MIAO Y
   DUAN W
   MENG Z
   ZHANG P
   XU C
   BAI B
   WU X
   AN X
   XIE X
AE UNIV TAIYUAN TECHNOLOGY (UYTL-C)
GA 2020717832
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of carbon dioxide separation membrane involves adding brominated polyimide into a three-necked flask, adding N-methylpyrrolidone, dissolving, adding sodium azide, heating to 50-100 degrees C, reacting for 24 hours, precipitating reaction product with methanol-water mixed solution, washing the precipitate with distilled water, placing resultant in vacuum-drying oven, drying, obtaining azide-functionalized polyimide, adding azide-functionalized polyimide to Schlenk bottle, adding N-methylpyrrolidone, cooling, degassing, filling with high-purity nitrogen, adding cuprous bromide, pentamethyldiethylenetriamine and alkynyl compound, heating, precipitating reaction product with ether, washing the precipitate with distilled water, vacuum-drying, obtaining click-functionalized polyimide, adding the click-functionalized polyimide to a sample bottle, adding N-methylpyrrolidone, dissolving, filtering, uniformly coating resultant on a glass plate, and drying.
   USE - Preparation of carbon dioxide separation membrane.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of carbon dioxide separation membrane having high gas separation performance.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of carbon dioxide separation membrane involves vacuum-drying 2,5-dimethyl-1,4-phenylenediamine in an oven for 12 hours, adding 4,4'-(hexafluoroisopropylene)diphthalic anhydride to a sublimation furnace at 130-200 degrees C for 12 hours, adding dried 2,5-dimethyl-1,4-phenylenediamine to the furnace under nitrogen atmosphere, adding N,N-dimethylacetamide for dissolving the reaction mixture, placing resultant mixture in ice water bath at less than 5 degrees C, repeating addition of 4,4'-(hexafluoroisopropylene)diphthalic anhydride and N,N-dimethylacetamide, reacting for 48 hours, adding 3-methylpyridine and acetic anhydride, reacting for 48 hours, precipitating resultant product with methanol, washing, placing resultant in a oven, vacuum-drying, obtaining polyimide, adding polyimide to three-necked flask, adding 1,2-dichloroethane, dissolving, adding N-bromosuccinimide and azobisisobutyronitrile, reacting at 50-100 degrees C for 3-6 hours, precipitating, washing precipitate with methanol, vacuum-drying, obtaining brominated polyimide, adding the brominated polyimide into a three-necked flask, adding N-methylpyrrolidone, dissolving, adding sodium azide, heating to 50-100 degrees C, reacting for 24 hours, precipitating reaction product with methanol-water mixed solution, washing the precipitate with distilled water, placing resultant in vacuum-drying oven, drying, obtaining azide-functionalized polyimide, adding azide-functionalized polyimide to Schlenk bottle, adding N-methylpyrrolidone, dissolving, placing resultant in liquid nitrogen, cooling, degassing, filling with high-purity nitrogen, adding cuprous bromide, pentamethyldiethylenetriamine and alkynyl compound, heating at 50-100 degrees C for 24 hours, precipitating reaction product with ether, washing the precipitate with distilled water, vacuum-drying, obtaining click-functionalized polyimide, adding the click-functionalized polyimide to a sample bottle, adding N-methylpyrrolidone, dissolving, filtering, uniformly coating resultant on a glass plate, and drying the glass plate at 80 degrees C.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Composition: The mass-to-volume ratio of 2,5-dimethyl-1,4-phenylenediamine and N,N-dimethylacetamide is 13.6205:140. The mass-to-volume ratio of 4,4'-(hexafluoroisopropylene)diphthalic anhydride and N,N-dimethylacetamide is 44.424:92. The mass ratio of polyimide, 1,2-dichloroethane, N-bromosuccinimide and azobisisobutyronitrile is 10:200:6.5466:0.1745. The mass-to-volume ratio of polyimide and N-methylpyrrolidone is 5:50. The mass-to-volume ratio of sodium azide and brominated polyimide is 2-3. The mass-to-volume ratio of cuprous bromide and pentamethyldiethylenetriamine is 0.206:600. The usage amount of alkynyl compound is 665.6 mu L.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Composition: The mass ratio of click-functionalized polyimide and N-methylpyrrolidone is 1:9.

PT P
PN CN110938481-A
TI Preparation of liquefied natural gas for low-rank coal quality utilization, involves reacting purified gas as methane synthesis material in presence of high-temperature resistant methane catalyst, and performing liquefaction process.
AU ZHANG H
   LV B
   JIN F
   MA Q
   LI J
   PAN J
AE ZHEJIANG TIANLU ENVIRONMENTAL TECHNOLOGY (ZHEJ-Non-standard)
GA 202027535L
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of liquefied natural gas involves preparing low-rank coal to obtain upgraded coal rich gas, upgrading to obtain purified gas containing carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and hydrogen, passing purified gas obtained as methane synthesis material (A) to reactor (R2), reacting in presence of high-temperature resistant methane catalyst (C1) to obtain methane stream (M1), reacting methane stream (M1) in reactor (R3) in presence of high temperature resistant methane catalyst (C2) to obtain methane stream (M2), passing methane stream (M2) through gas-water separation device (G1), separating condensate and gas phase material, reacting gas phase material in reactor (R4) in presence of high temperature methane catalyst (C3) to obtain methane product stream, passing methane product stream through gas-water separation device (G2) to separate original liquefied natural gas stream, and performing liquefaction process, and obtaining 90 vol.% or more methane by cryogenic liquefaction process.
   USE - Preparation of liquefied natural gas for low-rank coal quality utilization.
   ADVANTAGE - The preparation method is economical, and effectively utilizes the volatiles in low rank coal and provides liquefied natural gas with few impurities and high quality. The coupling between the methane synthesis process and other coal processing processes, provides energy for the corresponding process.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of liquefied natural gas involves preparing low-rank coal by gasification reduction process to obtain upgraded coal rich gas, upgrading to obtain purified gas containing carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and hydrogen, adding purge gas to reactor (R1), preheating purge gas to 150-500 degrees C, passing purified gas obtained as methane synthesis material (A) to reactor (R2), reacting carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and hydrogen of methane synthesis material (A) to synthesize methane in presence of high-temperature resistant methane catalyst (C1) to obtain methane stream (M1), converting methane stream (M1) into methane synthesis material (B), passing to reactor (R3), reacting carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and hydrogen of methane synthesis material (B) to synthesize methane in presence of high temperature resistant methane catalyst (C2) to obtain methane stream (M2), passing methane stream (M2) through gas-water separation device (G1), separating condensate and gas phase material whose main component is water, forming gas phase material as methane synthesis material (C), passing methane synthesis material (C) to reactor (R4), reacting carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and hydrogen methane synthesis material (C) to synthesize methane in presence of high temperature methane catalyst (C3) to obtain methane stream (M3), thereby obtaining methane product stream, passing methane product stream through gas-water separation device (G2) to separate original liquefied natural gas stream, feeding original liquefied natural gas stream to perform liquefaction process, and obtaining 90 vol.% or more methane by cryogenic liquefaction process.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred process: The method preferably involves passing methane stream (M3) to the reactor (R5), reacting in the presence of high temperature resistant methane catalyst (C4) to synthesize methane to obtain a methane stream (M4) with 3 vol.% or more carbon monoxide, thereby obtaining the methane product stream, passing methane stream (M3) or methane stream (M4) to the reactor (R6), reacting in the presence of high temperature resistant methane catalyst (C5) to synthesize methane to obtain a methane stream (M5) with less than 1 vol.% carbon monoxide, thereby obtaining the methane product stream. The liquefaction process involves decarbonization process and liquefaction process. The decarburization process involves passing liquefied feed gas to the absorption device, decarbonizing in the upper portion of the absorption device, separating the entrained solution through the demister, passing to the cooling device, cooling to less than 40 degrees C, separating water by the gas-liquid separation device to obtain raw material gas, and performing liquefaction process. The liquefaction process involves passing the feed gas entering to the refrigeration heat exchanger, pre-cooling, passing to the low-pressure rectification device, cooling, returning to the refrigeration heat exchanger, cooling to -156 degrees C to -160 degrees C, passing to a high-pressure rectification device for rectification, distilling the hydrogen component in the feed gas from the top of the tower to form a hydrogen-rich stream, passing rectification gas (S1) containing carbon monoxide, nitrogen and methane from the lower portion of the high-pressure rectification device to the low-pressure rectification tower through throttle reduction, where the carbon monoxide and nitrogen in rectification gas (S1) forms a carbon monoxide-rich stream, which is distilled off from the upper portion of the low-pressure rectification tower, forming rectification gas (S2) from remaining material, and passing out from the lower portion of the low-pressure rectification device. In the absorption device, the carbon dioxide component in the liquefied natural gas raw material is absorbed by the active methyldiethanolamine composite solution. The hydrogen-rich stream flows through the refrigeration heat exchanger and reheats to 20-40 degrees C. Preferred Composition: The ratio of hydrogen and carbon in gas fed to the reactors is (2.9-3.5):1. The amount of carbon dioxide and hydrogen in the gas fed to the reactors is 5 vol.% or more and 50 vol.% or more respectively. Preferred Condition: The reaction in reactors are performed at 200-500 degrees C. The purge gas is introduced into the reactor (R3) and/or (R4), so that when methane is synthesized, the reaction temperature is 200-500 degrees C.
DC H06 (Gaseous and liquid fuels - including pollution control. Combustion improvement additives for liquid fuels are included (C10L).); H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification. Combustion improvement additives for coal, peat and other nonhydrocarbon based fuels are included in this Section together with coal liquefaction and desulphurisation.)

AB    NOVELTY - Converting carbonate material to carbon                monoxide, comprises: transferring heat and at least                one feed stream comprising a carbonate material and                at least one of hydrogen, oxygen, water or a                hydrocarbon into an integrated calcination and                syngas production system (400) that comprises a                syngas generating calciner (SGC) reactor; calcining                the carbonate material to produce a carbon dioxide                product and a solid oxide product; initiating a                syngas production reaction; producing at least one                syngas product from the syngas production reaction,                where the product comprises at least one of a                carbon monoxide product, a water product or a                hydrogen product; and transferring at least one of                the solid oxide product or the syngas product out                of the SGC reactor.
   USE - The method is useful for converting carbonate                material to carbon monoxide.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are also included                for:
   an industrial process comprising performing an                industrial process that comprises at least one of                direct air capture, carbon dioxide capture, a                cement process, a refractory process or a pulp and                paper process, and during performance of the                industrial process, performing a subprocess that                comprises transferring heat and at least one feed                stream comprising a carbonate material and at least                one of hydrogen, oxygen, water or a hydrocarbon                into an integrated calcination and syngas                production system that comprises SGC reactor,                calcining the carbonate material to produce a                carbon dioxide product and a solid oxide product,                initiating a syngas production reaction, producing                at least one syngas product from the syngas                production reaction, and transferring at least one                of the solid oxide product or the syngas product                out of the SGC reactor; and
   an apparatus comprising the SGC reactor that                comprises at least one inlet port arranged to                receive at least one feed stream comprising a                carbonate material and at least one of hydrogen,                oxygen, water or a hydrocarbon into a volume of the                SGC reactor, a heat transfer assembly fluidly                coupled to the SGC reactor to provide heat, a                reaction zone fluidly coupled to the heat transfer                assembly and SGC reactor, where at least a portion                of the heat is transferred to the carbonate                material and reactions occur to produce at least                one product that comprises at least one of a carbon                dioxide product, a syngas product or a solid oxide                product, and at least one outlet port positioned to                discharge at least one of the carbon dioxide                product, the syngas product, the carbonate material                or the solid oxide product, out of the SGC                reactor.

PN CN108409028-A; CN108409028-B
TI Recycling nutrient substances synthesizing microbial protein in sewage, involves coupling anaerobic digestion unit, nanofiltration membrane separation unit and microbial protein production unit to realize low-carbon efficient recovery.
AU XING W
   YAO H
AE UNIV BEIJING JIAOTONG (UBJI-C)
GA 201866824P
AB    NOVELTY - Recycling nutrient substances synthesizing microbial protein involves coupling anaerobic digestion unit, nanofiltration membrane separation unit and microbial protein production unit to realize low-carbon efficient recovery of nutrient resources in sewage. The organic matter in sewage is degraded by using anaerobic digestion unit to generate methane and carbon dioxide gas. The nanofiltration membrane separation unit is intercepted macromolecular harmful substances in effluent of anaerobic digestion unit and transmited small molecular substances of ammonia nitrogen in the microbial protein production unit. The methane and carbon dioxide gas generated by anaerobic digestion unit are introduced, and ammonia nitrogen in the effluent of the nanofiltration membrane separation unit is used as a nutrient to synthesized microbial protein under interaction of the microalgae and methane oxidizing bacteria.
   USE - Method for recycling nutrient substances synthesizing microbial protein in sewage.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to recycling nutrient substances synthesizing microbial protein which is effectively separated anaerobic digestion unit to harmful substances in water in nano-filtration membrane separating unit, and ensures subsequent microbial protein production process which is not affected by contaminants.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - The microalgae is used carbon dioxide as a carbon source and generate oxygen through photosynthesis, where methane oxidizing bacteria is used methane as a carbon source and utilize oxygen which is generated by microalgae to simultaneously generate carbon dioxide. The three units are coupled and separated in a nanofiltration membrane. The unit is effectively isolated the effluent from the anaerobic digestion unit Hazardous materials, and ensured a subsequent microbial protein contaminants from the production process. The anaerobic digestion unit and the carbon dioxide gas produced is methane as a carbon source, and microbial protein production units continue to consumed in entire system with production with use. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for sewage resource recycling and microbial protein synthesis device, which comprises water inlet system, anaerobic digestion unit, air gas supply system, nano-filtration membrane separating unit, adjusting device and microbial protein production unit. The water inlet system main body is composed of water storage tank. The water inlet pump is connected with anaerobic digestion unit which is connected with the water inlet pipeline and filled with suspension sponge support, and top is provided with skirt and connected with air and a gas supply system through an air outlet pipeline, and provided stirring power which is composed of a stirring motor. The stirring blade is used to realize the rotation, and outside is equipped with heating insulating device, and is provided with a water outlet pipeline is connected with the nano-filtration membrane separation unit.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows schematic view of sewage resource recycling and microbial protein synthesis device.
   Water inlet valve (A-2)
   Water pump (A-3)
   Support suspension (B-1)
   Mixer (B-4)
   Stirring blade (B-5)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Process: The gas supply is diffused to external liquid phase of membrane through gas-liquid interface, and membrane module which is installight-emitting diode with different numbers of membrane filaments according to gas supply amount, where 80-160 membrane filaments are installight-emitting diode when gas supply amount is 1 liter per day. The mixing device is used to fully achieve uniform mixing between water, gas and microorganisms. The microbial protein production unit is irradiated by an externally arranged light-emitting diode light source of adjustable light intensity and wavelength, so that the microalgae is utilized carbon dioxide in gas phase as carbon source in liquid phase as a nitrogen source to synthesized microalgae cells as microbial protein products and generate oxygen under photosynthesis. The methane oxidizing bacteria oxidize methane in gas phase by using oxygen generated by microalgae, and utilized liquid. The ammonia nitrogen in phase is used as a nitrogen source to synthesized methane oxidizing bacteria cells as microbial protein products. The carbon dioxide is used again for photosynthesis utilization of microalgae. The ammonia nitrogen is assimilated by microalgae and methane oxidizing bacteria, and microalgae and methane oxidizing bacteria are synthesized simultaneously in the double purification of water. The microbial protein production unit is set to the sequencing mode at beginning of the cycle. The pump is first turned on, after main body of the microbial protein production reactor is fillight-emitting diode with water. The water pump is turned off, and then hydrophobic gas supply membrane module, stirring device and light-emitting diode light source are simultaneously turned on to start synthesizing the microbial cells, where required value are reached absorbance of OD600 which is greater than 0.4. The operation of hydrophobic gas supply membrane module, the stirring device and the light-emitting diode light source are suspended, and after microbial cells are allowed to stand for precipitation, where still precipitation for 30 minutes or more. The central water discharge system of the reactorv is used to excluded effluent, removed most of microbial products from bottom valve of reactor (2/3-3/4 of all cells), left small amount of cells (1/4-1/3 of all cells) as next cycle inoculate microorganisms, after effluent and microbial cells are discharged, and re-opened pump to next cycle.
DC D15 (Chemical or biological treatment of water, industrial waste and sewage - including purification, sterilising or testing water, scale prevention, treatment of sewage sludge, regeneration of active carbon which has been used for water treatment and impregnating water with gas e.g. CO2, but excluding plant and anti-pollution devices (C02).); D13 (Other foodstuffs and treatment - including preservation of food, milk, milk products, butter substitutes, edible oils and fats, non-alcoholic beverages, artificial sweeteners, food additives and animal feed (A23B-L).); E36 (Non-metallic elements, semi-metals (Se, Te, B, Si) and their compounds (except for E35).); H09 (Fuel products not of petroleum origin - excluding coal handling, preparation or mining, but including coking, briquetting, peat processing synthesis, gas production, coal gasification.
