AB    NOVELTY - Carbon dioxide zero discharge catalytic                cracking non-complete regeneration method,                comprises (i) pressurizing the air by the                compressor to obtain the pressurized air; (ii)                allowing the pressurized air enters the air                separating device; (iii) mixing the pure oxygen and                the carbon dioxide after pressurizing the                circulating compressor; (iv) passing the                regenerated flue gas through three-stage cyclone                separator for separating catalyst fine powder; (v)                dividing the regenerated flue gas without catalyst                fine powder into two parts; (vi) mixing regenerated                and the regenerated flue gas stream II by a waste                heat boiler recycling flue gas heat; (vii) removing                sulfide in the low-temperature flue gas through the                desulfurizing tower; (ix) removing the water vapor                in the flue gas by the flue gas after the                desulfurization by the gas-liquid separator to                obtain dry flue gas; (x) allowing the dry flue gas                enters the pressure swing adsorption device for gas                separation, respectively waste gas.
   USE - The method is useful for carbon dioxide zero                discharge catalytic cracking non-complete                regeneration.
   ADVANTAGE - The method reduces carbon emissions from the                source.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Carbon dioxide zero discharge catalytic                cracking non-complete regeneration method,                comprises (i) pressurizing the air by the                compressor to obtain the pressurized air; (ii)                allowing the pressurized air enters the air                separating device, separating to obtain pure oxygen                and nitrogen; (iii) mixing the pure oxygen and the                carbon dioxide after pressurizing the circulating                compressor to obtain the mixed gas; introducing the                mixed gas into the regenerator to perform                non-completely regenerated burning reaction with                the catalyst to be generated, obtaining regenerated                flue gas and regenerated catalyst, returning the                regenerated catalyst to the riser reactor for                cracking reaction; (iv) passing the regenerated                flue gas through three-stage cyclone separator for                separating catalyst fine powder in the flue gas to                obtain regenerated flue gas without catalyst fine                powder; (v) dividing the regenerated flue gas                without catalyst fine powder into two parts, where                one part of the flue gas turbine recycling pressure                energy to obtain the regenerated flue gas stream I,                and the other part through the pressure reducing                pore plate to reduce the pressure to obtain the                regenerated flue gas stream II; (vi) mixing                regenerated and the regenerated flue gas stream II                by a waste heat boiler recycling flue gas heat to                obtain the low temperature flue gas discharged                waste heat the boiler; (vii) removing sulfide in                the low-temperature flue gas through the                desulfurizing tower, obtaining the desulfurized                flue gas and sulfur-containing sewage; (ix)                removing the water vapor in the flue gas by the                flue gas after the desulfurization by the                gas-liquid separator to obtain dry flue gas; (x)                allowing the dry flue gas enters the pressure swing                adsorption device for gas separation, respectively                waste gas, high concentration carbon monoxide and                high concentration carbon dioxide, discharging the                waste gas, using the high concentration carbon                monoxide as chemical raw material or fuel,                pressurizing part of the high concentration carbon                dioxide as circulating carbon dioxide by the carbon                dioxide compressor, allowing the carbon dioxide                pressurized by the circulating compressor enters                into the regenerator for recycling and the other                part is directly used for sealing or driving oil,                where the waste gas comprises steam, oxygen, carbon                monoxide and carbon dioxide, the volume percentage                concentration of carbon monoxide in the waste gas                is 0.1-10 %, the volume percentage concentration of                carbon dioxide in the waste gas is 0.1-10 %, the                volume percentage concentration of the carbon                monoxide in the high-concentration carbon monoxide                is 90-99.9 %, the volume percentage concentration                of the carbon dioxide in the high-concentration                carbon dioxide is 90-99.9 %.

AE UNIV SOUTHWEST PETROLEUM (USWP-C)
   UNIV CHINA PETROLEUM BEIJING (UPEB-C)
   CNPC SAFETY ENVIRONMENTAL TECHNOLOGY RES (CNPC-C)

GA 2021925734
AB    NOVELTY - A temperature/carbon dioxide/pH multiple                responsive emulsifier comprises amino compounds                (I)-(VII).
   USE - Temperature/carbon dioxide/pH multiple                responsive emulsifier used in used in emulsion for                reversible emulsification drilling fluid for                cleaning oil-bearing drill cuttings and/or filter                cake, and recovering waste oil-containing liquid                phase (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The emulsifier can be used in drilling fluid,                which is in a water-insoluble state and cannot be                washed by water. After the temperature is cooled or                stimulated by adding liquid acid or introducing                acid gas, the drilling fluid becomes water-soluble                and easy to be cleaned by water. If heating, alkali                or alkaline gas is continued to be used, the                drilling fluid will return to a water-insoluble                state and cannot be cleaned by water.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - A temperature/carbon dioxide/pH multiple                responsive emulsifier comprises amino compounds of                formulae (I)-(VII).
   R' = H;
   R = hydrophobic carbon chain containing at                least one unsaturated bond, and having odd-numbered                7-17C multi-carbon chain distribution;
   R1 = hydrophobic 17C carbon chain containing                two unsaturated bonds;
   R2 = hydrophobic 17C carbon chain containing                unsaturated bond;
   R'' = CH3;
   R3 = saturated hydrophobic 17C carbon                chain;
   R4 = saturated hydrophobic 19C carbon                chain;
   R5 = hydrophobic 21C carbon chain containing                unsaturated bond;and
   R6 = hydrophobic 21C carbon chain containing                saturated bond.
   INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) emulsion, which comprises 4-5 %mass                temperature/carbon dioxide/pH multiple responsive                emulsifier, and oil phase and water phase in volume                ratio of 5:5-7:3;
   (2) response method of the emulsion containing                emulsifier, which involves dissolving emulsion                containing emulsifier (1)-(13) in water, or when                the emulsion containing emulsifier (1)-(13) is                insoluble in water, adding acid or introducing acid                gas to stimulate the emulsion to dissolve in water,                and/or adding alkali or passing in alkali gas, and                forming substance which is insoluble in water                again. The emulsion containing emulsifier is one of                emulsion containing emulsifier (1) which is                insoluble in water at a temperature of more than 31                degrees C, and soluble in water at a temperature of                31 degrees C or less, emulsion containing                emulsifier (2) which is insoluble in water at a                temperature of more than 30 degrees C, and soluble                in water at a temperature of 30 degrees C or less,                emulsion containing emulsifier (3) which is                insoluble in water at a temperature of more than 29                degrees C, and soluble in water at a temperature of                29 degrees C or less, emulsion containing                emulsifier (4) which is insoluble in water at a                temperature of more than 25 degrees C, and soluble                in water at a temperature of 25 degrees C or less,                emulsion containing emulsifier (5) which is                insoluble in water at a temperature of more than 25                degrees C, and soluble in water at a temperature of                25 degrees C or less, emulsion containing                emulsifier (6) which is insoluble in water at a                temperature of more than 28 degrees C, and soluble                in water at a temperature of 28 degrees C or less,                emulsion containing emulsifier (7) which is                insoluble in water at a temperature of more than 17                degrees C, and soluble in water at a temperature of                17 degrees C or less, emulsion containing                emulsifier (8) which is insoluble in water at a                temperature of more than 20 degrees C, and soluble                in water at a temperature of 20 degrees C or less,                emulsion containing emulsifier (9) which is                insoluble in water at a temperature of more than 20                degrees C, and soluble in water at a temperature of                20 degrees C or less, emulsion containing                emulsifier (10) which is insoluble in water at a                temperature of more than 18 degrees C, and soluble                in water at a temperature of 18 degrees C or less,                emulsion containing emulsifier (11) which is                insoluble in water at a temperature of more than 16                degrees C, and soluble in water at a temperature of                16 degrees C or less, emulsion containing                emulsifier (12) which is insoluble in water at a                temperature of more than 16 degrees C, and soluble                in water at a temperature of 16 degrees C or less,                or emulsion containing emulsifier (13) which is                insoluble in water at a temperature of more than 16                degrees C, and soluble in water at a temperature of                16 degrees C or less;
   (3) reversible emulsification drilling fluid,                which comprises 4-5 %mass emulsifier, 1-2 %mass                oil-based stabilizer, 1-2 %mass organic soil, and                1.6-2.0 g/cm3 weighting agent;
   (4) method for cleaning oil-bearing drill                cuttings and/or filter cake using the reversible                emulsification drilling fluid, which involves                soaking the oil-containing drill cuttings and/or                filter cake of the drilling fluid in water and add                acid liquid or pass acid gas, stirring at 200-300                rpm low-speed stirring, changing the drilling fluid                from a water-insoluble state to a water-soluble                state to achieve the effect of cleaning oily drill                cuttings and/or filter cake, and/or soaking the                oil-bearing drill cuttings of the drilling fluid in                water below the response temperature, or soaking                the oil-bearing drill cuttings of the drilling                fluid in water and cooling to below the response                temperature, stirring at 200-300 rpm low-speed                stirring, and changing the drilling fluid from a                water-insoluble state to a water-soluble state to                achieve the role of cleaning oily drill cuttings;                and
   (5) method for recovering waste oil-containing                liquid phase using the responsive reversible                emulsified drilling fluid, which involves                contacting the waste oil-containing liquid phase                with alkali or alkaline gas or subjecting it to                heating treatment, and turning the oily waste                liquid from a water-soluble state to a                water-insoluble state in the new mud configuration                of the drilling fluid to achieve the effect of                recycling the waste oily liquid phase.

TI Reducing pyrolusite used for feed additive by            spraying mixed solution of formic acid and oxalic acid            to pyrolusite powder, leaching, drying, adding e.g.            reducing auxiliary agent, mixing and grinding,            introducing reducing gas, generating manganese monoxide            hot ore powder, discharging and cooling.
AU YANG W
   MO J
   HU W
   PAN W
   HUANG J
   CHEN X
   ZHAO S
   XU Z
   WU W
   HUANG Y
   LU S
   CHEN P
   LIANG Y
   XIAO H
AE GUANGXI ISKY NEW MATERIALS TECHNOLOGY CO (GUAN-Non-standard)
GA 2021B6986X
AB    NOVELTY - Reducing pyrolusite comprises spraying a                mixture of formic acid and oxalic acid on the                pyrolusite powder, leaching, drying to obtain                mineral powder a, adding reducing auxiliary agent                and reducing coal into mineral powder a, mixing,                grinding to obtain raw material powder b, adding                raw material powder b into rotary kiln, introducing                a high-temperature reducing gas g1 from a carbon                monoxide generator, generating manganese monoxide                hot ore powder c , discharging, cooling to obtain                product manganese monoxide ore powder d and                dividing the tail gas g3 into two air flow, sending                circulating gas g4, fresh hot air and reduced coal                to the carbon monoxide generator to obtain                high-temperature reducing gas g1, removing dust to                obtain the tail gas g6, passing into a absorption                tower equipped with manganese stearate emulsion for                carbon dioxide absorption to produce high-purity                manganese carbonate products and stearic acid                emulsion and recycling to obtain manganese stearate                emulsion
   USE - The pyrolusite is useful for producing                manganese sulfate for preparing battery anode                material, feed additive, agricultural microelement                fertilizer and medicine.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is a clean and environmentally                friendly; ensures that the carbon monoxide                completes the reduction of pyrolusite at a lower                temperature; fully recovers waste heat and absorbs                the produced carbon dioxide, sulfur oxides and                nitrogen oxides to achieve clean and ultra-low                carbon emissions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Reducing pyrolusite comprises (a) spraying a                mixture of formic acid and oxalic acid on the                pyrolusite powder with a particle size of 100-150                mesh, where the spraying volume of the mixed                solution of formic acid and oxalic acid is 1-2                times the amount of pyrolusite, filtering the                pyrolusite after leaching, drying to obtain mineral                powder a, where the mixed liquid of formic acid and                oxalic acid is made by uniformly mixing formic                acid, oxalic acid and water in a ratio of                (2-3):1:(5-7), (b) adding reducing auxiliary agent                and reducing coal into mineral powder a, mixing                evenly, grinding to a particle size of 120-200 mesh                to obtain raw material powder b, where the reducing                auxiliary agent is obtained by mixing calcium                formate, calcium oxalate and calcium stearate in a                ratio of (3-8):(2-3):2, the input amount of                reducing auxiliary agent is 1.0-1.5% of the amount                of mineral powder a, the input amount of the                reduced coal is 0-5% of the amount of mineral                powder a, (c) adding raw material powder b into one                end of a rotary kiln, introducing a                high-temperature reducing gas g1 from a carbon                monoxide generator to 1000-1100degrees Celsius at                the other end of the rotary kiln, where the raw                material powder b and the reducing gas g1 are in                countercurrent contact heat and mass transfer in                the kiln to carry out the reduction reaction at                380-480degrees Celsius for 40-120 minutes,                generating manganese monoxide hot ore powder c at                400-500degrees Celsius, discharging the hot                manganese monoxide ore powder c from the rotary                kiln, cooling to 60degrees Celsius to obtain the                product manganese monoxide ore powder d, allowing                the high-temperature reducing gas g1 undergoing                heat transfer and mass transfer reaction in the                rotary kiln to become hot tail gas g2 with a                temperature of 350-500degrees Celsius, allowing the                hot tail gas g2 undergoing waste heat recovery and                dedusting treatment and forming tail gas g3 at                120-180degrees Celsius, preparing the                high-temperature reducing gas g1 in a carbon                monoxide furnace, heating the furnace to produce                reducing gas under the oxygen content of 14-15%                under oxygen-deficient conditions by adjusting the                feeding ratio of reduced coal and fresh air, where                the high temperature reducing gas g1 including                14-18% carbon monoxide, 65-72% nitrogen, 1-3%                methane, 2-4% hydrogen, 2-3% carbondioxide and 4-6%                oxygen and (d) dividing the tail gas g3 into two                air flow, where the first air flow heats up and                becomes a circulating gas g4 at 500-700degrees                Celsius, sending the circulating gas g4, fresh hot                air and reduced coal to the carbon monoxide                generator of step (c) under-oxygen combustion to                obtain high-temperature reducing gas g1 at                1000-1100degrees Celsius, where the ratio of                circulating gas g4, fresh hot air and reduced coal                is (9-12) m3: (6-7) m3: 3.7 kg, sending the second                air flow, fresh hot air and power coal reduction                coal to a fluidized combustion furnace for                combusting to obtain tail gas g5,where the ratio of                second airflow, fresh hot air and reduced coal is                (3-7)m3:(8-10)m3:1.2kg, removing dust after cooling                tail gas g5 to obtain the tail gas g6 at                60-80degrees Celsius, passing the tail gas g6 into                a absorption tower equipped with manganese stearate                emulsion for carbon dioxide absorption to produce                high-purity manganese carbonate products and                stearic acid emulsion, recycling the stearic acid                emulsion to obtain manganese stearate emulsion and                directly discharging the tail gas g6 after carbon                dioxide absorption treatment, where the manganese                stearate emulsion is obtained by uniformly mixing                manganese stearate, sulfonated kerosene,                isopropanol and water in a ratio of                (1-3):10:2:8.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (a) the                  pyrolusite powder is imported from Australia and                  the raw material comprises 68.33% manganese                  dioxide, 6.84% iron, 9.69% silicon dioxide, 3.77%                  aluminum oxide, 1.11% calcium, 0.53% magnesium,                  1.04% potassium and 0.95% sodium. In the step (b)                  the input amount of reducing auxiliary agent is                  1.5% of the amount of mineral powder a. The input                  amount of the reduced coal is 3% of the amount of                  ore powder a. In the step (c) the hot tail gas g2                  including 5-8% carbon monoxide, 62-70% nitrogen,                  15-20% carbondioxide and 3-5% oxygen. In the step                  (d) the tail gas g6 comprises 67-72% nitrogen,                  14-22% carbon monoxide and 11-17% oxygen.Preferred Method: The step (a) comprises                  obtaining mixed liquid of formic acid and oxalic                  acid by mixing formic acid, oxalic acid and water                  in a ratio of 2:1:7. The step (b) comprises                  obtaining reducing auxiliary agent by mixing                  calcium formate, calcium oxalate and calcium                  stearate in a ratio of 5:2:2. The step (c)                  comprises discharging the manganese monoxide hot                  ore powder c from the rotary kiln, cooling to                  below 60degrees Celsius to obtain the product                  manganese monoxide ore powder d and discharging                  the manganese monoxide hot ore powder c from the                  rotary kiln, entering a heat exchanger 1 and                  cooling below 60degrees Celsius to obtain the                  product manganese monoxide ore powder d. The step                  (c) comprises allowing hot tail gas g2 undergoing                  waste heat recovery and utilization and dust                  removal treatment to become tail gas g3 at                  120-180degrees Celsius, sequentially sending the                  hot tail gas g2 to a waste heat boiler and a dust                  collector 1 for waste heat recovery and                  utilization and dust removal treatment to become                  tail gas g3 at 120-180degrees Celsius. The step                  (d) comprises heating the first airflow through                  the heat exchanger 2 to become circulating air g4                  at 500-700degrees Celsius after the first airflow                  is increased and becomes a circulating gas g4 at                  500-700degrees Celsius.The step (d) comprises                  cooling the exhaust gas g5, and removing dust to                  obtain the exhaust gas g6 at 60-80degrees                  Celsius, sequentially sending the exhaust gas g5                  to the heat exchanger 2, heat exchanger 3 and                  dust collector 2, cooling and obtaining tail gas                  g6 at 60-80degrees Celsius.

PT P
PN CN109174181-A; CN109174181-B
TI Preparation of supported bifunctionalized ionic liquid mesoporous polymer used as catalyst, involves reacting imidazole functionalized mesoporous polymer with 1,2-dibromoethane, acetonitrile, triethylamine and sodium acetate, and filtering.
AU WU H
   MEI Y
   WU P
AE UNIV EAST CHINA NORMAL (UYEN-C)
GA 201909494Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of supported bifunctionalized ionic liquid mesoporous polymer involves mixing imidazole functionalized precursor with polyether and ethanol, stirring, spreading on a glass plate, volatilizing at room temperature for 8-12 hours, drying, and calcining the cured film to obtain an imidazole functionalized mesoporous polymer, and performing ionization of imidazole functionalized mesoporous polymer by mixing the imidazole functionalized mesoporous polymer with 1,2-dibromoethane and acetonitrile in a mass ratio of 4-6:1:35-45, stirring, reacting, filtering, washing the filtrate with acetone and deionized water, drying, mixing the dried product with triethylamine and deionized water in a mass ratio of 5:2-8:35-45, stirring, filtering, washing the filtrate with acetone and deionized water, drying, mixing the dried product with sodium acetate and deionized water in a mass ratio of 5:2-8:35-45, stirring, filtering, washing the filtrate with deionized water, and drying.
   USE - Preparation of environmentally-friendly supported bifunctionalized ionic liquid mesoporous polymer used as catalyst in heterogeneous catalytic synthesis of formamide from carbon dioxide and amine compound (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables preparation of environmentally-friendly supported bifunctionalized ionic liquid mesoporous polymer having high specific surface area and catalytic activity, and improved thermal stability and chemical stability. The mesoporous polymer can catalyze reaction of carbon dioxide with amine compound under mild conditions.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Preparation of supported bifunctionalized ionic liquid mesoporous polymer involves mixing tetrahydrofuran and sodium hydride in a molar ratio of 1-6:1, dripping a mixture comprising an 1H-imidazole of formula (I) and tetrahydrofuran in a molar ratio of 1:8-15 into the obtained mixture, stirring for 40-60 minutes, adding 1-(chloromethyl)-3-methoxybenzene of formula (II) at 70 degrees C, in which molar ratio of compound (II) with respect to compound (I) is 1-5:1, refluxing for 24 hours, reacting, washing with deionized water, extracting with dichloromethane, successively washing the extract with deionized and saturated brine, drying, and purifying by column chromatography to obtain 1-((3-methoxyphenyl)methyl)-1H-imidazole of formula (III), mixing compound (III) and hydrobromic acid in a molar ratio of 1:6-10, refluxing at 120 degrees C for 24 hours to perform demethylation reaction, neutralizing hydrobromic acid with saturated sodium carbonate solution for adjusting the pH to 7.5, washing with water, and filtering to obtain 3-(1H-imidazol-1-ylmethyl)phenol of formula (IV), mixing compound (IV) with a formaldehyde solution having a concentration of 38 wt.% and a sodium hydroxide solution having a concentration of 10 wt.%, stirring at room temperature for 10-20 minutes, adding phenol, stirring at 70-75 degrees C for 1-2 hours, reacting, cooling to room temperature, adjusting the pH of the reaction liquid to 7 using hydrochloric acid, and performing rotary evaporation to obtain an imidazole functionalized precursor, in which the molar ratio of compound (IV), phenol, formaldehyde and sodium hydroxide is 1:8-10:14-18:2-4, mixing the imidazole functionalized precursor with polyether and ethanol in a molar ratio of 1:1:18-25, stirring for 20-50 minutes, spreading on a glass plate, volatilizing at room temperature for 8-12 hours, drying in an oven at 100-120 degrees C for 18-24 hours, and calcining the cured film in a nitrogen tube furnace at 350 degrees C for 3 hours to obtain an imidazole functionalized mesoporous polymer, and performing ionization of imidazole functionalized mesoporous polymer by mixing the imidazole functionalized mesoporous polymer with 1,2-dibromoethane and acetonitrile in a mass ratio of 4-6:1:35-45, stirring at 70-83 degrees C for 12-24 hours, reacting, filtering, washing the filtrate with acetone and deionized water, drying under vacuum at 60-80 degrees C for 12-24 hours, mixing the dried product with triethylamine and deionized water in a mass ratio of 5:2-8:35-45, stirring under reflux at 60-80 degrees C for 12-24 hours, filtering, washing the filtrate with acetone and deionized water, drying under vacuum at 60-80 degrees C for 12-24 hours, mixing the dried product with sodium acetate and deionized water in a mass ratio of 5:2-8:35-45, stirring at room temperature for 12-24 hours, filtering, washing the filtrate with deionized water, and drying under vacuum at 60-80 degrees C for 12-24 hours. An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for use of supported bifunctionalized ionic liquid mesoporous polymer as a catalyst in heterogeneous catalytic synthesis of formamide from carbon dioxide and amine compound. The reaction temperature of the heterogeneous catalytic synthesis is 30-40 degrees C, and the reaction time is 18-24 hours. The molar ratio of the catalyst with respect to amine compound, phenylsilane and acetonitrile is 0.03-0.06:1:2-4:35-45. The amine compound is N-methylaniline, p-(chloromethyl)aniline and/or p-(fluoromethyl)aniline. The pressure of carbon dioxide is 0.5-2 MPa.

PT P
PN CN108192864-A
TI Isolating and culturing human amniotic membrane mesenchymal stem cells by separating amniotic membrane mesenchymal stem cell, enzymolyzing cells with e.g. pancreatin and collagenase, washing cells with dimethyl sulfoxide.
AU ZHANG F
   WANG G
AE GUIZHOU FACTORR CELL BIOTECHNOLOGY CO (GUIZ-Non-standard)
GA 2018514974
AB    NOVELTY - Method for isolating and culturing human amniotic membrane mesenchymal stem cells in vitro by separating amniotic membrane mesenchymal stem cell, transferring placenta to placental treatment tray, preparing three sterile culture dish, cleaning blood stain of amniotic membrane and washing until amnion is translucent or transparent, enzymolyzing with trypsin in a sealed water bath, removing undigested tissue block, adding collagenase II, digesting tissue at constant temperature and low speed, culturing cells in carbon dioxide incubator, removing non adherent cells, washing with physiological saline, filtering cell suspension with cell strainer, leveling filtered cell suspension and n centrifuging cell suspension, discarding supernatant after centrifugation, homogenizing cells, taking precipitate, centrifuging bottom cell cluster with physiological saline, performing centrifugal separation, adding dimethyl sulfoxide (DMSO) and n uniformly distributing cells into three cell cryotubes.
   USE - The method is useful for isolating and culturing human amniotic membrane mesenchymal stem cells in vitro.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple, cost-effective, suitable for large-scale production, increases the stem cell yield and does not contaminate the cells.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Method for isolating and culturing human amniotic membrane mesenchymal stem cells in vitro involves (a) preparing required equipment for separating amniotic membrane mesenchymal stem cell, wiping the placenta collection box with 75% ethanol, then transferring the placenta to the placental treatment tray in the biological safety cabinet for preparing three sterile culture dish, pouring 60 ml physiological saline and 1% streptomycin in each culture dish, (b) using sterilized pointed scissors and hemostats, taking 4-5 g placental amnion tissue along the umbilical cord and placenta junction at 4-5 g, placing into one of the sterile culture dishes, using hemostatic forceps for cleaning the blood stain of the amniotic membrane and washing until the amnion is translucent or transparent, (c) transferring the amniotic membrane treated in step (b) to a 50 ml centrifuge tube, cutting it with tissue scissors to a size of 1-2 mm2, adding the trypsin solution, placing ion a sealed water bath at constant temperature for low-speed digestion, filtering for removing the enzymolyzate for removing the undigested tissue block using a 100 mesh steel sieve, transferring the unscreened undigested tissue block back to the 50 ml centrifuge tube and repeating the digestion process once, (d) transferring the treated tissue from step (c) to new 50 ml centrifuge tube, adding collagenase II, digesting the tissue at constant temperature and low speed until complete digestion, (e) filtering the cell suspension from step (d) using a 100 mu m cell filter, centrifuging the cell suspension, washing the cells using 50 ml physiological saline and precipitating the cells twice, (f) adding fresh medium supplemented with antibiotics to the treated precipitate from step (e) for resuspending the cell pellet, inoculating it into a T175 flask at a high density of 20,000/cm2, placing the flask in a flat position and culturing the cells in carbon dioxide incubator, (g) taking out the culture flask from step (f) after 24 hours, shaking it many times for removing the non adherent cells that are floating, adding 10 ml physiological saline, washing for two times, adding fresh culture medium containing antibiotic, taking 2 ml culture medium, screening for Mycoplasma and then culturing in a carbon dioxide incubator for 48-72 hours, (h) pouring out the culture medium for passaging the cells when the 80-90% of flask bottom is covered with cells, washing the bottom cells of the flask with physiological saline, adding pancreatin to the culture flask for enzymolyzing the cells, terminating the enzymolysis with physiological saline when 60-70% bottom cells are digested, filtering the cell suspension with 100 mu m cell strainer, leveling the filtered cell suspension and then centrifuging the cell suspension, (i) discarding the supernatant after centrifugation from step (h), homogenizing the cells, pipetting 0.5 ml cells for counting, subculturing the cells in T75 flask at 9000-11000/cm2, mixing the cells using cross method and continuing the cell culture in the carbon dioxide incubator, (j) performing second passaging when the cells in the culture flask cover 80~90% of the bottom of the flask, inoculating the passaged cells into the T175 flask in accordance with 2.5x 106, culturing the cells in T175 flask having medium volume 25 ml and repeating the step (h) and (i) for completing the second passaging, (k) freezing the cells when the cells in the T175 flask cover 80-90% of the bottom, removing the supernatant of the culture medium in the flask, adding suitable amount of physiological saline, washing the cells, adding pancreatin for digesting the cells, terminating the enzymolysis with physiological saline when 60-70% bottom cells are digested, filtering the cell suspension with 100 mu m cell strainer, leveling the filtered cell suspension, centrifuging the cell suspension and discarding the supernatant, (l) taking the precipitate from step (k), centrifuging the bottom cell cluster with 3 ml physiological saline, thoroughly washing the cells with 17 ml physiological saline and (m) making the cell suspension sample from step (l) up to 40 ml, performing centrifugal separation, adding 2 ml medium to the cells in the centrifuge tube, adding 2 ml 40% dimethyl sulfoxide (DMSO) and then uniformly distributing the cells into three cell cryotubes.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: In step (c) and (d), the constant temperature low-speed digestion is performed at constant temperature of 37 plus minus 1 degrees C and rotation speed of 70 plus minus 5 revolutions per minute (rpm). In step (c), the trypsin solution mass concentration is 0.125%. The trypsin solution and tissue block are in a ratio of 1:1 and the digestion operation time is 20 minutes. In step (d), 1 %mass collagenase is used for digestion, where 1-3 times collagenase is added to the tissue volume. In step (e), (h), (k) and (m), the centrifugation is performed at 300 G for 10 minutes. In step (f),(g) and (i), the cells are cultured in an incubator at 37 plus minus 0.5 degrees C and the carbon dioxide volume fraction is 5 plus minus 0.2%. In step (h) and (k), 3 ml 0.125% pancreatin is added to the cell and the cells are digested for 2 minutes. In step (m), the medium added to the cells in the centrifuge tube and the frozen medium are pre-cooled in a refrigerator at 4 degrees C. The finished stem cells are subdivided into the cell cryotubes and are stored in liquid nitrogen at -196 degrees C after sealing.

PT P
PN DE102020128989-A1; WO2022096568-A2; WO2022096568-A3
TI Recovering carbon dioxide from atmosphere useful            for hydrocarbon synthesis, preferably methane            synthesis, comprises e.g. passing fibrous support            material loaded with polyethyleneimine alternately            through adsorption zone and desorption zone.
AU ZUBERBUEHLER U
   THALER S
   FEIGL B
   STUERMER B
   BAUMGART F
AE ZENT SONNENENERGIE & WASSERSTOFF-FORSCH (SONN-Non-standard)
GA 2022585919
AB    NOVELTY - Continuously recovering carbon dioxide from                atmosphere containing carbon dioxide, comprises                passing a fibrous support material (1) loaded with                polyethyleneimine alternately through at least one                adsorption zone (A) and at least one desorption                zone (D), where the support material absorbs carbon                dioxide from the atmosphere containing carbon                dioxide at room temperature and normal pressure in                the adsorption zone and in the desorption zone at a                temperature which is higher than room temperature                and/or at a lower carbon dioxide partial pressure                than normal conditions.
   USE - The method in combination with a plant is                useful: for hydrocarbon synthesis, preferably for                methane production (claimed); and in closed air                circuits for air treatment in aircraft and                spacecraft, submarines and buildings; and the                product is useful in the food industry for                producing carbonated drinks or as an extraction                agent.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: is efficient and carried out in a                continuous and energy-conserving manner; does not                require separate cycles for carbon dioxide-loading                and carbon dioxide-release; requires less thermal                energy that can be easily recovered by waste heat                from other processes; simultaneously produces                water; and makes use of waste heat from upstream                and downstream process e.g. electrolysis and                dimethyl ether synthesis.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                device for carrying out the above method,                comprising the fibrous support material loaded with                polyethyleneimine, adsorption zone, and desorption                zone with at least one heating device (4) and/or                includes at least one device for pressure                reduction, and/or a stripping gas feed, where the                fibrous support material loaded with                polyethyleneimine is movably stretched over                multiple of support and/or deflection rollers (2)                arranged in the adsorption zone and/or in the                desorption zone.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation                of recovering carbon dioxide from atmosphere using                the device.
   Support material (1)
   Deflection rollers (2)
   Suction device (3)
   Heating device (4)
   Gas outlets (5)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: At least one desorption                  zone is heated at least in sections at a                  temperature more than 30 degrees C, preferably                  more than 100 degrees C. The reduced carbon                  dioxide partial pressure in the desorption zone                  is achieved through (i) reducing the absolute                  pressure to a maximum of 700 mbar, preferably                  90-250 mbar and/or (ii) supplying a stripping                  gas. The carbon dioxide-containing atmosphere is                  air, which is continuously fed into the                  adsorption zone by natural convection of the                  surrounding air. The support material not only                  releases carbon dioxide but also water in the                  desorption zone. The atmosphere containing carbon                  dioxide is air having a relative humidity of at                  least 20%, in such a manner that the support                  material in the adsorption zone also absorbs                  water from the atmosphere containing carbon                  dioxide in addition to carbon dioxide. Preferred                  Components: At least one desorption zone                  comprises several sections with different                  temperatures, preferably comprises at least three                  sections with different temperatures, where the                  first section has room temperature and an                  absolute pressure of 90-250 mbar, a second                  section is heated at 45-85 degrees C and has an                  absolute pressure of 90-250 mbar, and a last                  section is heated at 95-110 degrees C and has an                  absolute pressure of 90-150 mbar. INSTRUMENTATION                  AND TECHNOLOGY - Preferred Components: The                  adsorption zone is a thermodynamically open                  system, preferably an open space, which allows                  free heat and mass exchange with the                  surroundings. The desorption zone includes at                  least two spatially separate gas outlets (5)                  connected to a suction device (3),                  respectively.
   TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Components: The fibrous support                  material is a fleece or a woven fabric, which                  absorbs 70-700 mg carbon dioxide per 1 g                  polyethyleneimine. The polyethyleneimine is                  branched and has a number-average molecular                  weight of 25000-100000 g/mol.

PT P
PN CN111964846-A
TI System useful for detecting sealing property of work piece by carbon dioxide, comprises sealing box comprising box body and sealing cover set with tooling pressure head, and inflating system for filling carbon dioxide gas into cavity.
AU HUANG J
AE ZHENGZHOU BEST TIMES MECHANICAL & ELECTR (ZHEN-Non-standard)
GA 2020B8080M
AB    NOVELTY - System comprises machine frame, sealing box, inflating system, recovery system, vacuum system and testing system. The sealing box comprises box body, sealing cover, tooling pressure head set on box body and sealing cover, and workpiece fixed in box body. The sealing kit at injecting inlet has sealing joint connected to inflation system, recovery system and vacuum system through injecting pipe. The vacuum system comprises vacuum unit for inner cavity of workpiece and vacuum unit for sealing box. The vacuum unit of inner cavity of workpiece is connected with injecting pipe and is used for vacuuming inner cavity of workpiece. The vacuum unit of sealing box is connected with inner cavity of sealing box, and is used to vacuum sealing box. The inflating system is used to fill carbon dioxide (CO2) gas into cavity to be detected in vacuum state. The detecting system comprises detecting tube, vacuum generator and spectrum detecting unit for detecting CO2 concentration change in sealing box.
   USE - The system is useful for detecting sealing property of work piece by carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The system: utilizes carbon dioxide as medium to detect the tightness of the workpiece; has low carbon dioxide cost, non-toxic, non-flammable carbon dioxide; has low cost of device to detect carbon dioxide content; has responsive and fast detecting speed; reduces the cost and operation steps of testing; improves the detecting efficiency and detecting accuracy; does not effect on the device by the atmospheric pressure; has wide range of use, strong applicability, and simple operation steps; has huge economic and social benefits; and has major innovation in the air tightness testing of workpieces.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic representation of the system for detecting sealing property of work piece by carbon dioxide (Drawing includes non-English language text).

PT P
PN US2020087158-A1; WO2020061039-A1; US10870585-B2; CA3113078-A1
TI Producing calcium carbonate from calcium hydroxide and carbon dioxide by introducing gas stream of carbon dioxide to set of particles comprises solid-state calcium hydroxide to produce particles comprising solid-state calcium carbonate.
AU LAMAR B
   LEIKAM J I
   LEWIS J
AE GRAYMONT PA INC (GRAY-Non-standard)
   GRAYMONT PA INC (GRAY-Non-standard)
GA 202021842Q
AB    NOVELTY - Calcium carbonate is produced from calcium hydroxide and carbon dioxide by providing 1st set of particles comprising solid-state calcium hydroxide, where individual ones of 1st set of particles include (i) specific surface area of greater than or equal to 20 m2/g and (ii) free moisture content of 2-20%; and introducing gas stream comprising carbon dioxide to 1st set of particles to produce 2nd set of particles comprising solid-state calcium carbonate.
   USE - Production of calcium carbonate from calcium hydroxide and carbon dioxide.
   ADVANTAGE - The composition and characteristics of the calcium hydroxide of the 2nd set of particles can enable greater production of calcium carbonate relative to utilizing calcium hydroxide without these characteristics (e.g., traditional lime hydrate). Specific surface area and/or pore volume of the individual calcium hydroxide particles are generally greater than those of traditional lime hydrate, allowing to absorb more carbon dioxide and aiding the production of calcium carbonate. The larger pore size of individual calcium hydroxide particles also aids the production of calcium carbonate by allowing carbon dioxide molecules to travel at least partially through the pores of the calcium hydroxide particles. This is in contrast to conventional methods in which the carbon dioxide molecules engulf entire calcium hydroxide particles, which may result in a shelled effect and lower calcium carbonate production rates.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for system for producing calcium carbonate comprising hydrator configured to add water to a 1st set of particles comprising calcium oxide to produce a 2nd set of particles comprising solid-state calcium hydroxide particles, and a conditioner downstream of the hydrator and positioned to receive the 2nd set of particles and a gas stream comprising carbon dioxide, where conditioner is configured to produce a 3rd set of particles comprising solid-state calcium carbonate from the received 2nd set of particles and gas stream.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Compositions: The 1st and 2nd sets of particles are not components of a liquid slurry or aqueous solution comprising precipitated calcium carbonate. The 2nd set of particles comprises greater than or equal to 95% calcium carbonate. Individual ones of the 1st set of particles have a free moisture content of greater than or equal to 10%. The 2nd set of particles is a component of a slurry comprising not greater than 20 wt.% liquid and greater than or equal to 80 wt.% solids. Preferred Processes: Providing the 1st set of particles comprises providing a slurry including the 1st set of particles, where slurry includes not greater than 20% liquid. Providing the 1st set of particles comprises providing slurry including the 1st set of particles, where slurry including greater than or equal to 80 wt.% solids. The 2nd set of particles and the 3rd set of particles are not produced via precipitation. The 1st set of particles comprises greater than or equal to 90% calcium hydroxide; providing the 1st set of particles comprises providing the 1st set of particles to a conditioner; and introducing the gas stream includes injecting the gas stream to the conditioner, causing the carbon dioxide of the gas stream to directly contact at least a portion of the 1st set of particles. The gas stream is 1st gas stream, and the method further comprises after introducing the 1st gas stream, grinding at least a portion of the 2nd set of particles to produce 3rd set of particles comprising 1st concentration of calcium carbonate, and after grinding, introducing 2nd gas stream comprising carbon dioxide to 3rd set of particles to produce 4th set of particles comprising 2nd concentration of calcium carbonate greater than 1st concentration of calcium carbonate. The method further comprising, while grinding, introducing a 3rd gas stream comprising carbon dioxide to the 2nd set of particles. The gas stream comprising carbon dioxide is received from a lime kiln, and the carbon dioxide comprises greater than or equal to 40% of the gas stream. The 2nd set of particles is exposed to the gas stream in the conditioner for greater than or equal to 15 minutes. Preferred Properties: Individual ones of the 2nd set of particles comprise pore volume of greater than or equal to 0.15 cm3/g; average pore diameter of greater than or equal to 200 Angstrom ; surface area of greater than or equal to 25 m2/g; or free moisture content of greater than or equal to 10%. The 3rd set of particles has a TAPPI brightness of greater than or equal to 95.
DC E33 (Compounds of Be, Mg, Ca, Sr, Ba, Ra, Sc, Y, La, Ac, Al, lanthanides (Rare-earths), Th.)

PT P
PN KR2089257-B1
TI Smart door lock system for switchboard, has exhaust fan that is operated for predetermined time as valve discharges air inside switchboard, such that concentration of carbon dioxide inside switchboard is increased to extinguish fire.
AU CHOI S Y
AE NOTE ESSEX SOLUTION (NOTE-Non-standard)
GA 202023167E
AB    NOVELTY - The system has a lock driving unit (100) that is installed on a switchboard door, for locking / unlocking the door based on an input signal. A detector (200) is arranged for detecting a state information of the switchboard. A control unit (300) is arranged for generating an event signal based on the state information and transmitting the event signal through a communication network. A motion detection sensor module is activated to detect movement around the switchboard through the motion detection sensor module to guide the danger of access to the vicinity of the switchboard by outputting a warning sound or warning message. The fire is extinguished and the exhaust fan is operated for a predetermined period of time as the valve discharges the air inside the switchboard, for increasing the concentration of carbon dioxide inside the switchboard to extinguishing fire when the concentration of carbon dioxide detected by the carbon dioxide sensor exceeds a set value.
   USE - Smart door lock system for switchboard.
   ADVANTAGE - The fire in the switchboard is determined based on the detected temperature, and carbon dioxide is quickly supplied to the switchboard according to the determined result to extinguish the fire. The carbon dioxide sensor is configured to detect and display the carbon dioxide concentration inside the switchboard, and the detected carbon dioxide concentration is configured to determine whether a fire is extinguished or to prevent a user from a safety accident due to carbon dioxide. The switchboard door is enabled to be opened while safety is secured through the authentication of the internal state of the switchboard and door opening and closing.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a block diagram illustrating the schematic configuration of the smart door lock system for switchboard. (Drawing includes non-English language text)
   Lock driving unit (100)
   Detector (200)
   Control unit (300)
   Management server (400)
   User terminal (500)
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INDUSTRIAL STANDARDS - The short-range radio frequency is one selected from Radio Frequency Identification (RFID) or Near Field Communication (NFC).

The carbon dioxide fire-extinguishing lifter includes a carbon dioxide injection port (2), an injection head (3) and a lifting rod (4). The carbon dioxide injection port is connected with a carbon dioxide fire-extinguishing chamber (15) by a hollow structure of the rod. The powder injection port is connected with a dry powder fire-extinguishing chamber (14) by the structure of the rod. A water spraying lifter (12) includes a cylindrical spray head (18) and a nozzle (19). The nozzle is located at a middle part of the spray head and connected with a water storage chamber (13) by the structure of the rod.
   USE - Fire-proof floor.
   ADVANTAGE - The fire-proof floor uses the lifting rod to lift the carbon dioxide fire-extinguishing lifter, the dry powder fire-extinguishing lifter and the water spraying lifter so as to achieve effective fire-extinguishing performance.

PN EP3378968-A1; JP2018154901-A; US2018274109-A1; CN108624905-A; US10208385-B2; JP6672211-B2; EP3378968-B1
TI Carbon dioxide electrolytic device has refresh            control unit which stops supply of electric current            from power supply by reaction control unit and stops            supply of carbon dioxide and electrolytic            solution.
AU KUDO Y
   ONO A
   KITAGAWA R
   YAMAGIWA M
   TSUTSUMI E
   SUGANO Y
   TAMURA J
   MIKOSHIBA S
   KANNO Y
   TAMURA A
   MASAKAZU Y
AE TOSHIBA KK (TOKE-C)
   TOSHIBA KK (TOKE-C)
   TOSHIBA KK (TOKE-C)
   TOSHIBA CORP (TOKE-C)
GA 2018749408
AB    NOVELTY - The device (1) has an electrolysis cell (2)                including a cathode (22) to reduce carbon dioxide                and produces a carbon compound, an anode (11) to                oxidize water or hydroxide ions and produces                oxygen. A power supply (40) is connected to the                anode and the cathode. The reaction control unit is                configured to cause a reduction reaction of the                carbon dioxide in the cathode and an oxidation                reaction of the water or hydroxide ions in the                anode by passing an electric current from the power                supply to the anode and the cathode. The supply                unit includes a gas supply unit to supply a gaseous                substance as portion of the refresh material to one                of the anode and the cathode. A refresh control                unit stops supply of the electric current from the                power supply by the reaction control unit and stops                supply of the carbon dioxide and the electrolytic                solution, and operates the refresh material supply                unit, based on request criteria of a cell output                which is one of output values of the electrolysis                cell.
   USE - Carbon dioxide electrolytic device.
   ADVANTAGE - The storage cost of energy is reduced and a                storage loss is small, when the electric power is                stored in the storage battery. The electrical                continuity between the anode current collector and                the cathode current collector is good while                enhancing mechanical retentivity of the                electrolysis cell to improve reduction reaction                efficiency. The discharge and the introduction of                the anode solution and the cathode solution from                the anode solution tank and the cathode solution                tank are omitted, and thus shorten a time for the                refresh operation is shortened.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is included for a carbon                dioxide electrolytic method.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view                illustrating a carbon dioxide electrolytic                device.
   Carbon dioxide electrolytic device (1)
   Electrolysis cell (2)
   Anode (11)
   Cathode (22)
   Power supply (40)

PT P
PN GB2598907-A; WO2022058045-A1
TI Aqueous liquid sorbent for separating target gas, e.g. carbon dioxide from a mixture of gases in rotating packed bed gas capture system, comprises mixture of amine compounds at particular concentrations having differenet reaction rates with target gas, and different solubility in water.
AU FORTHUN K
   YING J
   TORVANGER O
   EIMER D A
   STRAND A
   EIMER D
AE COMPACT CARBON CAPTURE AS (COMP-Non-standard)
GA 202242192M
AB    NOVELTY - Aqueous liquid sorbent comprises a first amine compound, a second amine compound and water. The sorbent comprises at least 16 wt.% first amine compound. The sorbent comprises at least 51 wt.% total amine compounds. The first amine compound has a reaction rate with the target gas that is greater than the reaction rate of the second amine with the target gas. The second amine compound has a solubility in water that is greater than the solubility of the first amine compound in water.
   USE - Aqueous liquid sorbent for separating target gas, e.g. carbon dioxide from a mixture of gases in gas capture system, preferably, carbon dioxide capture system, post-combustion carbon dioxide capture system, a system for carbon dioxide capture from a mixture of gases comprising hydrogen and carbon dioxide, a rotating packed bed gas capture system, more preferably a rotating packed bed carbon dioxide capture system (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The aqueous liquid sorbent is stable in solution at low temperatures, sufficiently low viscosity, low precipitation and solidification under typical operant conditions of gas capture systems, so the flow of the sorbent is not disrupted and passageways in the system are not blocked. The aqueous liquid sorbent has excellent gas capture rates, particularly carbon dioxide capture rates, high capacity for captured gas, particularly carbon dioxide, low volatility, resistance to thermal degradation, and metal corrosion within the carbon capture system is avoided.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for: i. a gas capture system, which comprises aqueous liquid sorbent; and ii. a method of capturing a target gas from a mixture of gases, which involves bringing the mixture of gases into contact with the aqueous liquid sorbent using a gas capture system or using a rotating packed bed gas capture system.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The target gas is carbon dioxide or hydrogen sulfide, preferably carbon dioxide. The first amine compound is piperazine or a derivative, preferably piperazine, 2-methyl piperazine, N-methyl piperazine, N,N-dimethyl piperazine, hydroxylethylpiperazine, hydroxyisopropylpiperazine and (piperazinyl-1)-2-ethylamine, further preferably piperazine, 2-methyl piperazine, N-methyl piperazine and N,N-dimethyl piperazine, and most preferably piperazine. The second amine compound is an alkanolamine, preferably selected from monoethanolamine, diethanolamine, triethanolamine, N-methylmonoethanolamine, N-methyldiethanolamine, N,N-dimethylmonoethanolamine, N,N-diethylmonoethanolamine, monoisopropanolamine, diisopropanolamine, N-methyldiisopropanolamine, 3-aminopropanol, 2-amino-2-methyl1-propanol, 2-(2-aminoethylamino)ethanol and diglycolamine, preferably, second amine compound is selected from monoethanolamine, diethanolamine, N-methylmonoethanolamine, N-methyldiethanolamine, monoisopropanolamine, diisopropanolamine, 3-aminopropanol, 2-amino-2-methyl-1-propanol, 2-(2-aminoethylamino)ethanol and diglycolamine, more preferably, monoethanolamine or N-methyldiethanolamine, and most preferably, monoethanolamine. Preferred Compositions: The sorbent comprises from 55-90 wt.%, preferably 60-85 wt.%, more preferably 65-80 wt.%, and most preferably 70-75 wt.% total amine compounds. The sorbent comprises 22-60 wt.%, preferably 25-50 wt.%, more preferably 30-45 wt.%, and most preferably 37-42 wt.% first amine compound, and 10-80 wt.%, preferably 15-70 wt.%, more preferably 20-60 wt.%, and most preferably 25-50 wt.% second amine compound. The sorbent comprises the first amine compound and the second amine compound in weight ratio of 9:1 to 1:9, preferably 3:1 to 1:3, more preferably 2:1 to 1:1, and most preferably 3:2 to 5:4. Preferred Conditions: The first amine compound has a solubility in the aqueous liquid sorbent that is greater than the solubility of the first amine compound in water. The first amine compound and the second amine compound are miscible. The first amine compound is soluble in the second amine compound, or vice-versa. The reaction rate of the first amine compound with the target gas and the reaction rate of the second amine with the target gas are both measured at a temperature of 40-60degreesC, more preferably measured at 60degreesC, a target gas loading of 0.2 moles of gas per mole of amine compound, and an amine concentration in water of 40 wt.%. The first amine compound and the second amine compound are stable in solution in the aqueous liquid sorbent at 40degreesC and higher, more preferably 30degreesC and higher, and most preferably at 5degreesC and higher.
   EXAMPLE - An aqueous sorbent solution was prepared using 40 wt.% piperazine, 30 wt.% monoethanolamine (MEA) and 30 wt.% water.

PN JP2020130064-A
TI Carbon dioxide supply apparatus for supplying carbon dioxide to plant planted in e.g. greenhouse, has discharge opening where combustion gas is discharged, so as to pass or strain combustion gas along inside of stem base of plant.
AU ZUSHI Y
   NAKAMOTO T
   MATSUNUMA Y
   NIIMURA S
AE NORITSU KK (NOTS-C)
   SEIWA KK (SEIW-Non-standard)
GA 2020836674
AB    NOVELTY - The apparatus (1) has a heat-source equipment (10) which emits the combustion gas according to combustion of a combustor (102), for cooling carbon dioxide by heat exchange between the heat mediums which flow through a first heat exchanger (103). A thermal radiation unit (20) is arranged besides a cultivation room (2), and is provided with a second heat exchanger (201) through which a heat medium circulates between heat exchangers. A supply duct (310) is provided for supplying the combustion gas discharged from the heat-source equipment to the plant in the cultivation room. The supply duct is provided with a first duct (340) and several second ducts (350) which branches from the first duct, and has a flow passage area smaller than the flow passage area of first duct. Second duct is provided with a discharge opening (351) where the combustion gas is discharged, so as to pass or strain the combustion gas along the inside of the stem base of the plant.
   USE - Carbon dioxide supply apparatus for supplying carbon dioxide to plant planted in cultivation room such as greenhouse and vinyl greenhouse.
   ADVANTAGE - The plant can be warmed when the plant does not receive the damage by heat even if the combustion gas discharged from the discharge opening contacted the plant rapidly, and the combustion gas contacts the plant rapidly. Since emitting amount of the combustion gas from a discharge opening can be lessened, the combustion gas stays at the vicinity of the plant easily. The carbon dioxide supply apparatus which can supply carbon dioxide to the plant in the cultivation room efficiently can be provided.
   DESCRIPTION OF DRAWING(S) - The drawing shows a schematic view of the carbon dioxide supply apparatus. (Drawing includes non-English language text)

   Ducts (340,350)
   Discharge opening (351)

AB    NOVELTY - Preparing metal alloy catalyst involves preparing a carbon support on which palladium nanoparticles are supported, and preparing a precursor solution by dispersing the carbon support on which the palladium nanoparticles are supported in a polyol solvent containing ions of the palladium and other alloy metals. The palladium nanoparticles and the alloying metal are alloyed by irradiating microwaves to the precursor solution.
   USE - Method for preparing metal alloy catalyst used in reducing carbon dioxide for formic acid generation (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to prepare metal alloy catalyst that have improved selectivity for formic acid generation during carbon dioxide reduction and ensures stability of the catalyst.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the following:
   (1) a method for reducing carbon dioxide, which involves preparing a carbon support on which palladium nanoparticles are supported; preparing a precursor solution by dispersing the carbon support on which the palladium nanoparticles are supported in a polyol solvent containing ions of the palladium and other alloy metals; forming a palladium alloy catalyst by alloying the palladium nanoparticles and the alloying metal by irradiating microwaves to the precursor solution; coating the palladium alloy catalyst on the surface of the first electrode; preparing a reactor for accommodating the first electrode, the second electrode, and an electrolyte in which carbon dioxide is dissolved; and electrolyzing carbon dioxide dissolved in the electrolyte by applying an electrical signal to the reactor; and
   (2) a carbon dioxide reduction system, which comprises a reactor containing an electrolyte in which carbon dioxide is dissolved; first and second electrodes partially or completely immersed in the electrolyte; a palladium-silver (PdAg) alloy nanoparticle catalyst coated on the surface of the first electrode and supported on a carbon support; and a power supply for applying an electric signal to the first and second electrodes so that carbon dioxide is reduced to generate formic acid.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The alloying metal is silver (Ag). Preferred Process: The method for preparing the precursor solution comprises dissolving a metal salt containing a cation of the alloy metal in the polyol solvent. The method for electrolyzing the carbon dioxide includes applying a reduction potential to the first electrode to generate formic acid. The carbon dioxide reduction method further comprises the step of selectively dissolving and removing portion of the alloy metal from the palladium alloy catalyst so that the palladium alloy catalyst coated on the surface of the first electrode is activated. Preferred Conditions: The amount of the metal salt contained in the precursor solution is controlled so that the molar ratio of the alloy metal and the palladium is 0.1:3 to 2.8:3. The palladium nanoparticles have diameter of 1-10 nm. The Faraday efficiency of formic acid is the largest among electrolysis products.
   EXAMPLE - No suitable example given.

PT P
PN CN111470992-A
TI Synthesizing glycine involves forming carbon dioxide ammonia solution and hydroxyacetonitrile aqueous solution by ammonia source, carbon source, water entering hydantoin reactor for continuous reaction to obtain hydantoin synthesis liquid.
AU LONG Z
   LI K
   LI N
AE LONG Z (LONG-Individual)
GA 2020745275
AB    NOVELTY - Synthesizing glycine involves forming carbon dioxide ammonia solution and hydroxyacetonitrile aqueous solution by ammonia source, carbon source and water entering the hydantoin reactor for continuous reaction to obtain hydantoin synthesis liquid. The reaction material enters from the bottom of the hydantoin reactor and flows out from the top. The ammonia source is ammonium bicarbonate, ammonium carbonate, liquid ammonia and/or ammonia. The carbon source is ammonium bicarbonate, ammonium carbonate and/or carbon dioxide. The liquid phase obtained after hydantoin synthesis liquid is separated by the reduced pressure flash evaporation and entered the multi-stage series hydrolysis reactor for continuous hydrolysis.
   USE - Method for synthesizing glycine.
   ADVANTAGE - The method enables to synthesize glycine that is environmentally friendly, reduces difficulty of recycling carbon dioxide and ammonia in the system, loss of carbon dioxide and ammonia, increases conversion rate of one-way hydrolysis, shortens hydrolysis time.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - Synthesizing glycine involves forming carbon dioxide ammonia solution and hydroxyacetonitrile aqueous solution by ammonia source, carbon source and water entering the hydantoin reactor for continuous reaction to obtain hydantoin synthesis liquid. The reaction material enters from the bottom of the hydantoin reactor and flows out from the top. The ammonia source is ammonium bicarbonate, ammonium carbonate, liquid ammonia and/or ammonia. The carbon source is ammonium bicarbonate, ammonium carbonate and/or carbon dioxide. The liquid phase obtained after hydantoin synthesis liquid is separated by the reduced pressure flash evaporation and entered the multi-stage series hydrolysis reactor for continuous hydrolysis. The reaction material is entered from the bottom of the hydrolysis reactors of various stages connected in series, and flowed out from upper or top portion of the carbon dioxide generated by hydrolysis which is continuously separated during hydrolysis reaction. The glycine-containing feed liquid from final hydrolysis reactor is lifted out by gas stripper or concentrated and evaporated to remove residual carbon dioxide and ammonia in feed liquid. The obtained liquid phase is refined to obtain glycine product.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The concentration of hydroxyacetonitrile is 15-65 wt.%. The molar ratio of ammonia source, carbon source, water is 1:1.6-22.5:1.6-20:10-80. Preferred Process: The method involves preheating carbon dioxide ammonia solution by preheater, and then mixing with hydroxyacetonitrile aqueous solution in mixer, entering hydantoin reactor for continuous reaction to obtain hydantoin synthesis liquid. The temperature of the carbon dioxide ammonia solution preheated by preheater at 55-100 degrees C. The mixer is static mixer, micro mixer, or other types of tubular mixers. The ratio of feed, carbon dioxide, ammonia is greater than or equal to 1. The operating pressure of step-down flash evaporation separation is 0.2-3.5 megapascal. The method involves generating continuous separation of carbon dioxide by hydrolysis in hydrolysis reaction process refers to gas-liquid separation at the top of one or more hydrolysis reactors. The carbon dioxide is released by hydrolysis, and entered the gas phase to be recycled and reused, and liquid phase is entered the next-stage hydrolysis reactor. The gas-liquid separation space is provided at the top of the hydrolysis reactor for gas-liquid separation, and separate gas-liquid separator is connected in series after top outlet of the hydrolysis reactor for separation. The gas phase obtained by depressurization flash separation of the hydantoin synthesis liquid, gas phase separated from gas-liquid separation of the hydrolysis reaction. The glycine-containing feed liquid are extracted through stripping tower or concentrated and evaporated to remove the residual carbon dioxide and ammonia in feed liquid. The carbon oxide and ammonia recovery system water or recycled process water absorbs above gas phases to obtain the carbon dioxide ammonia solution and then returns for application. The gas phase containing carbon dioxide and ammonia that is taken out by stripper or concentrated and removed by steam. The gas phase separated is absorbed by gas-liquid of the hydrolysis reaction, and finally gas phase separated is absorbed by depressurization and flash evaporation of the hydantoin synthesis liquid.

   GAO J
   LI S
   HE S
AE UNIV GUANGZHOU (UNGZ-C)
GA 2021D42983
AB    NOVELTY - Extracting unsaturated fatty acids in lobster liver comprises (i) frozen lobster liver, (ii) drying lobster liver processed in step (i), (iii) introducing lobster liver processed in step (ii) to the supercritical carbon dioxide extraction equipment for extraction, where the drying treatment is hot-air drying treatment or microwave-assisted hot-air drying treatment.
   USE - The method is useful for extracting unsaturated fatty acids in lobster liver.
   ADVANTAGE - The method compares with the traditional hot-air drying treatment and then the Soxhlet extraction method, and content of polyunsaturated fatty acids in the extracted lipids is significantly increased.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The temperature of said freezing treatment is lower than -10 degrees Celsius. In step (ii), after drying, the moisture content in the lobster liver is less than or equal to to 1 %. The temperature of hot air drying treatment is 60-80degrees Celsius. The time of the hot air drying treatment is 20- 80 minutes. The temperature of said microwave assistant hot air drying treatment is 60-80degrees Celsius.The power of the microwave assistant hot air drying process is 300- 400 W. In step (iii), extraction pressure is between 20- 40 MPa, and extraction temperature is 40-60degrees Celsius. In step (iii), the flow rate of carbon dioxide is 0.3-0.5 kg/hours. In step (iii), the extraction time is 2-5 hours.

   LUO C

GA 2021C26068
AB    NOVELTY - Method for preparing cockroach aggregation pheromone extract from cockroach insect sand and its application, are claimed. The method involves drying cockroach insect sand at a low temperature of 50degreesC for 8 hours, pulverizing, sieving, extracting in a supercritical carbon dioxide (CO2) extraction device, adding carrier agent during extraction, and collecting extract to obtain cockroach aggregation pheromone extract.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing cockroach aggregation pheromone extract, which is useful for preparing cockroach attractant. The cockroach attractant is used in form of granule, pill, tablet and solution (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares cockroach attractant which achieves long-term effect of attracting cockroaches.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - BIOTECHNOLOGY - Preferred Method: The supercritical CO2 extraction is carried out at a pressure of 15-50 MPa, preferably 35 MPa, temperature of 35-60degreesC, preferably 45degreesC, CO2 flow rate of 10-100 l/hour, preferably 50 l/hour, and extraction time of 0.5-3 hours, preferably 2 hours. The cockroach aggregation pheromone extract is capable of preparing a cockroach attractant comprising 1-80 wt.% cockroach aggregation pheromone extract, 5-90 wt.% animal raw material, 5-90 wt.% plant raw material, 1-10 wt.% fatty raw material, 1-10 wt.% sugar raw material, and 0.1-2 wt.% preservative. The animal raw materials are one or a mixture of animal liver, fish meal, shrimp powder, milk powder, goat milk powder, silkworm pupa powder, yellow mealworm powder, earthworm powder, beef powder, and chicken powder. The plant raw materials are one or a mixture of soybean flour, corn flour, flour, rice flour, peanut flour, sweet potato flour, and potato flour. The fat raw material is any one or a mixture of palm oil, soybean oil, peanut oil, sesame oil, rapeseed oil, lard, tallow, butter, and cream. The sugar raw material is any one or a mixture of glucose, fructose, sucrose, maltose, lactose, and honey. TI Enhancing detection of oxygen-containing volatile organic small molecules involves using photoionization-time-of-flight mass spectrometer as detection instrument, using mixture of carbon dioxide and air as diluent for detection.
AU LI H
   WAN N
   HUA L
   JIANG J
   XIE Y
   LI Q
AE CAS DALIAN CHEM & PHYSICAL INST (CACP-C)
GA 202170995R
AB    NOVELTY - Enhancing detection of oxygen-containing volatile organic small molecules involves using photoionization-time-of-flight mass spectrometer as detection instrument. The mixture of carbon dioxide and air is used as the diluent for the detection of oxygen-containing volatile organic small molecules in the sample to be tested.
   USE - Method for enhancing detection of oxygen-containing volatile organic small molecules.
   ADVANTAGE - The method does not need any sample pre-processing, has fast detection speed, realizes high-sensitivity and accurate qualitative analysis of oxygen-containing volatile organic small molecule, is simple, easy to operate, the maximum enhancement times can reach 265 times.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The volume concentration of carbon dioxide in the diluent gas is 5-95%. The concentration of oxygen-containing volatile organic small molecules in the sample gas passed into the photoionization-time-of-flight mass spectrometer is 50-1000 ppbv. The method involves using diluent gas to dilute the sample to be tested online, and then passing the diluted sample to be tested into the injection port of the photoionization-time-of-flight mass spectrometer to obtain the mass spectrum of the sample to be tested. The peak of m/z is 43 in the direct mass spectrometry spectrum is the characteristic peak of acetaldehyde dehydrogenation (M-H)+, and the peak of m/z is 45 is the characteristic peak of acetaldehyde hydrogenation (M-H)+. The peak at m/z is 93 in the direct mass spectrum is the characteristic peak of the two-cluster hydrogenation (2M+H)+ of ethanol. The peak at m/z is 59 is the characteristic peak of the molecular ion dehydrogenation of n-propanol (MH)+, and the peak at m/z is 119 is the two-cluster dehydrogenation of n-propanol (2M-H). The characteristic peak of +, the peak of m/z=121 is the characteristic peak of the two-cluster hydrogenation (2M+H)+ of n-propanol. The peak at m/z is 57 is the characteristic peak of isobutanol molecular ion dehydroxylation (M-OH)+, and the peak at m/z is 73 is the molecular ion dehydrogenation of isobutanol (MH)+. The characteristic peak of m/z is 149 is the characteristic peak of the two-cluster hydrogenation (2M+H)+ of isobutanol. The peak of m/z is 93 in the direct mass spectrum is the characteristic peak of the molecular ion of isoamyl alcohol (M-OH)+. The reference conditions of the photoionization-time-of-flight mass spectrometer includes ionization source VUV Kr lamp, mass spectrometry injection volume is 30ml/minutes, ionization source pressure is 700 Pascal and mass spectrum accumulation time is 30 seconds, and detection method is positive ion mode. Preferred Components: The oxygen-containing volatile organic small molecules in the sample to be tested are formaldehyde, acetaldehyde, glyoxal, propacrolein, methanol, ethanol, n-propanol, isopropanol, butanol, and isoamyl alcohol above.
