PN CN111495425-A; WO2021190541-A1
TI Preparation of polyaniline-gold composite material            useful in carbon dioxide electroreduction, comprises            preparing short chain-shaped polyaniline template,            reacting short-chain shaped polyaniline template with            gold ion.
AU LIU P
   ZHANG Y
   CHENG F
   ZHANG M
   RUAN D
   WANG S
   XIE S
   XIE D
   TAN G
   RUAN R
AE UNIV DONGGUAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDU-C)
   UNIV DONGGUAN TECHNOLOGY (UYDU-C)
GA 2020773112
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation of polyaniline-gold composite                material comprises (i) preparing short chain-shaped                polyaniline template comprising short chain                polyaniline; and (ii) reacting short-chain shaped                polyaniline template with gold ion to obtain                polyaniline-gold composite material.
   USE - The polyaniline-gold composite material is                useful in the carbon dioxide electroreduction                (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The method prepares composite material having                controllable size by in-situ reduction method, and                avoids large agglomeration because the gold loading                amount is suitable and the particles are uniform.                The composite material has good catalytic effect                due to the synergistic effect between the                polyaniline.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for a                polyaniline-gold composite material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: In the step (i), the short                  chain-shaped polyaniline template is prepared by                  cyclic voltammetry; the mixed solution of aniline                  and acid is used as the electrolyte. Acid is                  sulfuric acid, and/or hydrochloric acid. The                  initial potential of the cyclic voltammetry is                  -0.1 to -0.4 V, the stopping potential is                  0.5-1.1V, the starting potential is -0.1 to -0.2                  V, and the stopping potential is 0.9 to 1.0V. In                  the cyclic voltammetry, the scanning ring number                  is 10-40 rings, preferably 15-30 ring; the                  scanning speed is 40-60 mV/s, preferably 50 mV/s.                  The concentration of aniline in the mixed                  solution is 0.05-0.1 mol/l, the acid                  concentration is 0.05-0.1 mol/l; acid is sulfuric                  acid; the mol ratio of aniline to acid is 1:1. In                  step (ii), the reaction time is 5-30 minutes,                  preferably 20-30 minutes. In the step (ii), using                  chloroauric acid solution with concentration of                  0.5-4 mmol/l, preferably 1-2 mmol/l. In the step                  (ii), the reaction temperature is 2-8 degrees C,                  preferably 3-4 degrees C.

PT P
PN CN111218567-A
TI Reducing magnesium at the source of cobalt (nickel) salt comprises taking cobalt hydroxide (nickel) salt, adding solvent and slurry to carbon dioxide and carrying out solid-liquid separation and sectioning magnesium removal filter residue.
AU HU L
   LI L
   WEI S
   CHENG C
   JIANG J
AE QUZHOU HUAYOUGU NEW MATERIALS CO LTD (QUZH-Non-standard)
   HUAYOU COBALT CO LTD (HUAY-Non-standard)
GA 202051437Q
AB    NOVELTY - Reducing magnesium at the source of cobalt (nickel) salt comprises (1) taking cobalt hydroxide (nickel) salt and adding a solvent to prepare a cobalt hydroxide (nickel) hydroxide slurry, (2) adding the slurry to the carbon dioxide is fed into the autoclave for reaction and (3) carrying out solid-liquid separation to obtain a section of magnesium removal filtrate and a section of magnesium removal filter residue.
   USE - The method reducing magnesium at the source of cobalt (nickel) salt.
   ADVANTAGE - The method: improves cobalt (nickel) salt product quality; and reduces the post extraction treatment cost.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: The solvent is water and the mass ratio of water to cobalt hydroxide (nickel) salt is 3-15:1. The mass ratio of the water used for slurrying to the one-stage magnesium removal filter residue is 1-5:1 and the solvent used for leaching is water and the mass ratio of the rinsing water to the one-stage magnesium removal filter residue is 1-3 :1. The reagent used for the heavy metal is magnesium oxide or a sulfide and the molar ratio of the magnesium oxide or the sulfide to the heavy metal is 1-3:1. The sulfide is sodium sulfide or ammonium sulfide. Preferred Method: The method further includes slurring first-stage magnesium removal filter residue with water and after solid-liquid separation again, the second-stage magnesium removal filtrate is obtained and rinsing the solid phase to obtain the second-stage removal magnesium aluminum slag. The method also includes taking a section of the magnesium-removing filtrate and heavy metals. The method comprises (1) taking cobalt (nickel) hydroxide salt, adding a solvent to prepare hydrogen cobalt oxide (nickel) slurry solution, (2) adding the slurry solution to the pressurized reactor and passing carbon dioxide to react, (3) performing solid-liquid separation to obtain a magnesium removal filtrate and one-stage magnesium removal filter residue and (4) slurring first-stage magnesium removal filter residue obtained with water and after solid-liquid separation again, two-stage magnesium removal filtrate is obtained and the solid phase is rinsing to obtain two-stage magnesium removal aluminum residue and (5) taking the one-stage magnesium removal filtrate and the two-stage magnesium removal filtrate optional to precipitate heavy metals.
DC M25 (Production and refining of metals other than iron - including ore treatment, extraction, working up scrap, obtaining specific metals, control testing methods (C22B).)
MC M25-A01; M25-G05; M25-G11; M25-G16

TI Preparing aluminum hydroxide adjuvant used in            immunogenic composition and vaccine, involves treating            aluminum hydroxide adjuvant in carbon dioxide            environment.
AU PAN J
   PAN C
   HAN W
   YAN S
AE CHINA ANIMAL HUSBANDRY IND CO LTD (CNAD-C)
GA 202046612G
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing aluminum hydroxide adjuvant involves                treating aluminum hydroxide adjuvant in a carbon                dioxide environment, where treatment pressure is                1-15 Megapascals (Mpa), and the temperature is                30-121 degrees C.
   USE - Method for preparing aluminum hydroxide                adjuvant used in immunogenic composition and                vaccine (all claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The methid enables to prepare aluminum                hydroxide adjuvant, which has obvious immune effect                and the adjuvant is safe and effective, easy for                industrial production of vaccines, which provides                unique options for the development and application                of vaccines.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - INDEPENDENT CLAIMS are included for the                following:
   (1) an improved aluminum hydroxide                adjuvant;
   (2) an immunogenic composition, which                comprises improved aluminum hydroxide adjuvant and                an antigen; and
   (3) a vaccine, which comprises immunogenic                composition and a pharmaceutically acceptable                excipient.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Conditions: The pressure of the                  treatment is 2-12Mpa and the temperature is                  30-105 degrees C, preferably the pressure of the                  treatment is 7-12Mpa, 30-90 degrees C. The                  treatment time is 10-600 minutes, preferably                  50-360 minutes, more preferably 60-180 minutes.                  The aluminum hydroxide adjuvant is mixed with the                  dispersing aid, and the mixing is performed at                  100-1000 revolution/minute, preferably at 300-700                  revolution/minute. The dispersing aid is one or                  more of disaccharide, polysaccharide, polyol,                  surfactant, amino acid. The disaccharide is                  selected from one or more of sucrose, lactose and                  trehalose, and the polysaccharide is selected                  from hydroxyethyl starch, water-soluble                  cellulose, dextran, mannan and galactosan One or                  more, the polyol is selected from one or more of                  propylene glycol, glycerin, polyethylene glycol,                  the surfactant is selected from polyethylene                  glycol octyl phenyl ether, polyoxyethylene                  dehydration One or more of sorbitol monolaurate,                  polyoxyethylene sorbitan monooleate, and                  ethylphenyl polyethylene glycol. The amino acid                  is one or more of glutamic acid, histidine, and                  arginine. The dispersion aid is the polyol and/or                  the surfactant. The weight ratio of the aluminum                  hydroxide adjuvant to the dispersing aid is                  90:10-99.6:0.4.

PT P
PN CN111154077-A
TI Preparation tetrazine cyclic polydiphenylindole material comprises e.g. performing substitution reaction of 3,6-dichloro-1,2,4,5-tetrazine and 2,3-diphenyl-1H-indole salt, and performing Friedel-Crafts alkylation reaction.
AU CHEN H
   XIAO L
   LI H
AE UNIV XIANGTAN (UNXT-C)
GA 2020442258
AB    NOVELTY - Preparation tetrazine cyclic polydiphenylindole material comprises (i) performing substitution reaction of 3,6-dichloro-1,2,4,5-tetrazine and 2,3-diphenyl-1H-indole salt to obtain 3,6-bis (2,3-diphenyl-1H-indolyl)-1,2,4,5-tetrazine monomer, and then subjecting obtained 3,6-bis (2,3-diphenyl-1H-indolyl)-1,2,4,5-tetrazine monomer to Friedel-Crafts alkylation reaction to obtain final product.
   USE - The material is useful for gas adsorption, preferably carbon dioxide (claimed).
   ADVANTAGE - The material: has excellent physical and chemical stability, high specific surface area, high ratio of micropore structure and carbon dioxide adsorption value.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - ORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Method: The 2,3-diphenyl-1H-indole salt is obtained by reacting 2,3-diphenyl-1H-indole with base, and perofrming friedel-Crafts reaction by reacting 3,6-bis-(2,3-diphenyl-1H-indolyl)-1,2,4,5-tetrazine monomer and crosslinking agent under the action of Lewis acid catalyst. The method specifically comprises (i) dissolving 3,6-dichloro-1,2,4,5-tetrazine in solvent, adding 2,3-diphenyl-1H-indole salt, reacting to obtain 3,6-bis (2,3-diphenyl-1H-indolyl) 1,2,4,5-tetrazine, and (ii) adding cross-linking agent into 3,6-bis (2,3-diphenyl-1H-indolyl) 1,2,4,5-tetrazine, adding Lewis acid catalyst into the reaction system, and reacting to obtain final product. The step (i) comprises dissolving 3,6-dichloro-1,2,4,5-tetrazine in organic solvent, dissolving, adding 2,3-diphenyl-1H-indole salt into the organic solution of 3,6-dichloro-1,2,4,5-tetrazine, reacting and stirring at room temperature for 0.5-5 hours, preferably 2-3 hours, refluxing for 5-12 hours, preferably 6-10 hours, cooling to room temperature, filtering, washing (preferably with organic solvent), drying, passing through the column with petroleum ether/dichloromethane to obtain final product. The step (ii) comprises adding cross-linking agent into 3,6-bis (2,3-diphenyl-1H-indolyl)-1,2,4,5-tetrazine under the protection of nitrogen, then adding Lewis acid catalyst into the reaction system, (a) reacting at 0-30 degrees C for 2-8 hours (preferably 10-20 degrees C for 3-6 hours), (b) reacting at 20-40 degrees C for 6-12 hours (preferably at 30-40 degrees C for 6-10 hours), (c) further reacting at 30-50 degrees C for 8-16 hours (preferably at 40-50 degrees C for 10-14 hours), (d) then reacting at 40-70 degrees C for 8-16 hours (preferably at 50-70 degrees C for 10-14 hours), (e) reacting at 70-90 degrees C for 16-28 hours (preferably at 75-85 degrees C for 18-26 hours), cooling to room temperature and quenching the reaction with dilute hydrochloric acid, filtering, washing with water and ethanol 1-8 times (preferably 2-5 times), extracting with ethanol for 16-36 hours (preferably 18-28 hours), and drying (preferably vacuum drying) to obtain final product. The 2,3-diphenyl-1H-indole salt is prepared adding 2,3-diphenyl-1H-indole at less than 0 degrees C into the base, stirring and reacting to obtain final product. Preferred Components: The base is strong base, preferably potassium hydroxide or sodium hydroxide; the cross-linking agent includes dichloromethane, dichloroethane and/or chloroform; and the Lewis acid catalyst includes iron(III) chloride, zinc chloride, aluminum chloride and boron trifluoride. In step (i), the solvent is organic solvent, preferably acetonitrile, tetrahydrofuran and/or dimethylformamide; the mass ratio between solvent and 3,6-bis (2,3-diphenyl-1H-indolyl) 1,2,4,5-tetrazine is 325:1, preferably 8-12:1; the molar ratio between 3,6-bis (2,3-diphenyl-1H-indolyl) 1,2,4,5-tetrazine and 2,3-diphenyl-1H-indole salt is 1:1-5, preferably 1:2-3. In step (ii), the cross-linking agent includes dichloromethane, dichloroethane and/or chloroform, preferably dichloromethane; the Lewis acid catalyst includes iron(III) chloride, zinc chloride, aluminum chloride and/or boron trifluoride; the mass ratio between 3,6-bis (2,3-diphenyl-1H-indolyl)-1,2,4,5-tetrazine and crosslinking agent is 1:60-180, preferably 1:90-120; and the molar ratio between 3,6-bis (2,3-diphenyl 1H-indolyl)-1,2,4,5-tetrazine and catalyst is 1:4-40, preferably 1:12-28.

TI Preparing porous calcium carbonate plastic filling material comprises e. g. mixing heavy calcium carbonate with deionized water, adding into a pressurized reactor with carbon dioxide recycle for reaction to obtain calcium carbonate slurry.
AU WANG L
   SONG X
   GUO W
   CAO Y
   CHEN S
AE UNIV SHANGHAI POLYTECHNIC (USSP-C)
GA 2019A1461Q
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing porous calcium carbonate plastic filling material comprises (i) mixing heavy calcium carbonate with deionized water, adding into a pressurized reactor with carbon dioxide recycle for reaction to obtain calcium carbonate slurry; and (ii) separating solid-liquid from the calcium carbonate slurry, washing, and drying at high temperature.
   USE - The method is useful for preparing porous calcium carbonate plastic filling material.
   ADVANTAGE - The method is simple and low cost. The product has rich pore, high specific surface area and good reinforcing effect.
   DETAILED DESCRIPTION - An INDEPENDENT CLAIM is also included for porous calcium carbonate plastic filling material is prepared by above mentioned method.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - INORGANIC CHEMISTRY - Preferred Components: In the step (i), the weight ratio of the heavy calcium carbonate to deionized water is 0.1:1-0.2:1. Preferred Method: In the step (i), the reaction pressure of the reactor is 0.5-1 MPa, and the reaction time is 2-4 hours. In the step (ii), the solid-liquid separation is by filtering method, the solids after solid-liquid separation are rinsed with ethanol, the drying temperature is 110-130 degrees C and the drying time is 2-4 hours.

AU RIGAILL J
   RIGAILL F
AE RIGAILL J (RIGA-Individual)

PT P
PN CN107739459-A; CN107739459-B
TI Preparing white carbon black/butylbenzene rubber composite material involves modifying chitin under supercritical carbon dioxide conditions, and adding styrene-butadiene rubber and butadiene rubber into mixer, and internally mixing.
AU SHEN M
   XIA L
   WANG H
   XIN Z
AE UNIV QINGDAO SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYQS-C)
   UNIV QINGDAO SCI & TECHNOLOGY (UYQS-C)
GA 201819620Y
AB    NOVELTY - Preparing white carbon black/butylbenzene rubber composite material involves modifying chitin at 10 MPa at 80-100 degrees C under supercritical carbon dioxide conditions for 10-30 minutes. The styrene-butadiene rubber and butadiene rubber are added into mixer, internal mixed in order to added white carbon, modified chitin, zinc oxide, and stearic acid, mixed uniformly, discharged at 90 degrees C. The rubber material is placed into the open mill, added the accelerating agent and sulfur after completely enveloping the roller, added accelerant and sulfur, passed, placed under the sheet and parked for use.
   USE - Method for preparing white carbon black/butylbenzene rubber composite material.
TF TECHNOLOGY FOCUS - POLYMERS - Preferred Conditions: The styrene-butadiene rubber and the butadiene rubber are mixed for 2-5. minute. The discharging temperature is controlled at 80-100 degrees C. Preferred Composition: The white carbon black/butylbenzene rubber composite material comprises 75 pts. wt. styrene butadiene rubber, 25 pts. wt. butadiene rubber, 40 pts. wt. white carbon black, and 10-20 pts. wt. modified chitin. Preferred Components: The styrene butadiene rubber is SBR1502 and the butadiene rubber is BR2000.
